public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the talk?

options.text: To trace how movement and diffusion on Earth have accelerated over time
To explain how biological evolution eventually produced modern humans
To compare written records with methods of geological dating
To show that technological change has slowed cultural exchange

evidence.quote: Without airplanes, pizza delivery, or the Internet, things moved more slowly across the early earth than they do today.
In short - the pace of travel on earth has really sped up!

private.explanationZh: 全文沿时间顺序比较海洋形成、人类迁徙、作物传播、瘟疫与印刷术传播以及现代航空和数字信息传输，核心是说明地球上移动与传播的速度不断加快，因此选 A。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了从早期地球到现代社会的整条速度变化主线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。人类出现只用于时间线上的过渡，文章没有解释生物进化机制。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。结尾提出如何认识史前地球，但没有展开比较具体的地质测年方法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文强调技术使旅行和信息传播加速，而不是减慢文化交流。

public.prompt: Why does the narrator describe the spread of domesticated wheat?

options.text: To prove that early agriculture first developed near the Pacific
To illustrate how useful practices spread gradually through trade or migration
To argue that beer was the principal cause of wheat domestication
To compare the dispersal of crops with the movement of continents

evidence.quote: In many of these places we were able to develop useful practices like agriculture, but because we were scattered across the globe, the best ideas and tastiest regional foods could only gain ground through trade or human migration.
Wheat, for example, was domesticated around 8500BC to make porridge.

private.explanationZh: 小麦是前一句一般性观点的具体例证：分散在各地的人群需要通过贸易或迁徙传播农业实践和食物。叙述者并不是要确定小麦驯化的唯一动机，而是展示早期传播速度，因此选 B。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文说小麦从新月沃地传播到大西洋和太平洋两侧，并未说农业最早出现在太平洋。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。小麦把“有用实践通过贸易或迁徙扩散”的抽象观点具体化了。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。啤酒只是一种推测，而且原文明确说无论粥还是啤酒都不影响传播例子的作用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文没有把作物传播和大陆漂移进行比较。

public.prompt: Which two developments does the narrator say spread across Europe in less than a century?

options.text: Agriculture and wheat cultivation
Beatlemania and the Hula hoop
The bubonic plague and the printing press
SARS and digital video data

evidence.quote: The bubonic plague and the printing press each spread across Europe in less than a hundred years, bringing death and literacy to the populace before their hair could have even grown 15 meters.

private.explanationZh: 原文直接说明黑死病和印刷术都在不到一百年的时间里传遍欧洲，因此选 C。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。农业和小麦的扩散经历了数千年。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。披头士热和呼啦圈在一两年内风靡世界，不是题干所问的“不到一个世纪传遍欧洲”的组合。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这两个发展与原文中的地点和时间完全对应。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。SARS和数字信息用于说明现代传播更快，并非同一组欧洲历史事件。

public.prompt: Why does the narrator ask several questions near the end of the talk?

options.text: To challenge the reliability of every account of Earth's history
To shift exclusively to predictions about future technology
To summarize the specific travel rates already mentioned
To introduce a broader focus on what we know about Earth and how we know it

evidence.quote: But how do we know this stuff? How do we know what happened on earth before the records of written history? Why do we think the earth is 4,500,000,000 years old? And what on earth is going to happen in the next 45 years?

private.explanationZh: 这些问题把前面的“传播速度史”引向更大的节目主题：我们对地球知道什么、这些知识如何获得，以及未来可能发生什么。它们承担的是引出后续内容的功能，因此选 D。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。提问是为了引出求证方式，并没有否定所有历史叙述。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。问题同时涉及史前历史、地球年龄和未来，并非只谈未来技术。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。提问没有复述各阶段的具体速度。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确对应随后“what we know about the earth and how we know”的节目定位。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-001-q01: 全文按时间推进，对比海洋、生命、农业、疾病和信息传播速度，核心是地球上的移动与扩散不断加速。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-001-q02: 小麦从新月沃地逐步传播到大西洋和太平洋，是有用做法通过贸易或人口迁移缓慢扩散的实例。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-001-q03: 原文明说黑死病和印刷术各自在不到一百年内传遍欧洲。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-001-q04: 结尾连续提问后马上宣布将讲述我们对地球知道什么以及如何知道，作用是引出更广的主题。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly about?

options.text: Why chlorophyll absorbs green light more efficiently than other colors
Why plants are green despite chlorophyll's weak absorption of green light
How plants changed physically as they evolved from bacteria to redwoods
Why black leaves would be unable to carry out photosynthesis

evidence.quote: But wait a second: doesn't the fact that chlorophyll is green mean that it's actually bad at absorbing green light?
In the long run, what we do know is that Chlorophyll, as wasteful as its failure to absorb green light may be, turned out to have other biochemical advantages over the purple competition.

private.explanationZh: 文章从“绿色意味着不吸收绿光”的矛盾出发，讨论古代紫色生物造成的演化假说，并指出叶绿素可能凭借其他生化优势胜出，核心是在解释植物为什么仍然是绿色，因此选 B。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。叶绿素恰恰不擅长吸收绿光。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。该选项同时涵盖文章提出的问题和演化解释。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。从细菌到红杉只是说明植物发生过许多变化，不是全文主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文没有说黑叶不能光合作用，而是追问植物为什么没有发展出黑叶。

public.prompt: Why does the narrator discuss purple archaea and bacteria living in deeper water?

options.text: To demonstrate that modern plants descended directly from purple archaea
To show that red and blue light could not reach organisms in deep water
To present a possible evolutionary setting that favored chlorophyll
To prove that nature deliberately selected green as its preferred color

evidence.quote: so bacteria living there got really good at absorbing everything else but green. And the molecule that did the light-absorbing? Chlorophyll!

private.explanationZh: 紫色古菌吸收绿光、放过红蓝光的设定，为深水细菌形成不同吸光策略提供了可能的演化环境；叙述者借此解释叶绿素为何留下“不吸收绿光”的特征。因此选 C。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有建立现代植物直接起源于紫色古菌的谱系关系。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。紫色古菌让红光和蓝光通过，深水中的可用光正是缺少绿光而非缺少红蓝光。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这是一个明确标为推测的叶绿素演化情境。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文反而提出绿色可能是偶然结果，并未赋予自然主观偏好。

public.prompt: Which claim does the narrator present as established rather than speculative?

options.text: Purple archaea were the direct ancestors of all plants
Black leaves could never survive in natural environments
Green is nature's preferred color for living organisms
Chlorophyll had biochemical advantages over its purple competitors

evidence.quote: In the long run, what we do know is that Chlorophyll, as wasteful as its failure to absorb green light may be, turned out to have other biochemical advantages over the purple competition.

private.explanationZh: 叙述者用“what we do know”明确区分确定内容与前面的推测：虽然叶绿素不吸收绿光看似浪费，但它相对紫色竞争者具有其他生化优势。因此选 D。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。直接祖先关系没有在原文中得到确认。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。黑叶为什么没有出现仍是开放问题，原文未说它绝不可能存活。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。“自然最喜欢绿色”是叙述者提出后否定其确定性的修辞问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原文明确把这一点标为已知结论。

public.prompt: What topic is the narrator most likely to discuss next?

options.text: How chlorophyll's weak green-light absorption may benefit plants
How botanists measure the height of mature redwood trees
Why purple organisms disappeared completely from the oceans
How artificial black leaves could replace natural vegetation

evidence.quote: Or maybe plants were even more clever and turned the weakness of chlorophyll into a strength? But we'll have to leave that story for later...

private.explanationZh: 结尾明确留下悬念：植物也许把叶绿素的弱点转化成优势，并说这个故事以后再讲。因此下一部分最可能解释不吸收绿光怎样反而有利于植物，选 A。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它直接承接结尾未展开的“turn the weakness ... into a strength”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。红杉只作为植物变化很大的例子出现。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有说紫色生物完全消失，也没有预告这一主题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。黑叶是假设性对照，结尾没有提出用人工黑叶替代植物。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-002-q01: 全文围绕叶片为何是绿色展开，重点解释叶绿素不擅长吸收绿光却仍被植物广泛采用的可能原因。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-002-q02: 紫色古菌吸收绿光后，深水中的细菌只能利用剩余光谱，这构成叶绿素可能获得优势的演化情境。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-002-q03: 叙述者用“what we do know”明确指出叶绿素相对紫色竞争者具有其他生化优势，其余说法均属猜测或被否定。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-002-q04: 结尾提出植物或许把叶绿素的弱点变成优势，并说明这个故事留待后续，因此下一部分最可能解释这种益处。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the talk?

options.text: Plants would produce more sugar if their leaves absorbed every wavelength at the surface
The evolution of green photosynthesis was inevitable on every planet
Chlorophyll's overall system benefits outweigh its weak absorption of green light
Purple photosynthesis supplies nearly all of Earth's natural productivity

evidence.quote: However, the fact that almost all plants on earth are still using chlorophyll's recipe must mean there's more to it than meets the eye.

make that outcome inevitable, or did it just turn out that way? Is there a planet somewhere out there in our galaxy dominated by purple plants? We don't know, but what we do know is that here on Earth, chlorophyll's strengths far outweigh its distaste for greens, and for our planet, at least, maybe that's not such a bad thing.

private.explanationZh: 文章逐层说明叶绿素的吸光分布、避免表面能量过载、减少能量运输，以及绿色制糖系统的效率优势，最终得出叶绿素的整体优势远大于不擅长吸收绿光的缺点，因此选 C。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文说表面一次吸收全部绿光可能造成能量过载和类似晒伤的问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。结尾明确说尚不知道绿色结果是必然还是偶然。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它概括了全文对叶绿素“局部弱点、系统优势”的论证。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。占地球自然生产力 99.9% 以上的是绿色光合作用。

public.prompt: Why is it useful for chlorophyll molecules to be distributed in layers throughout a leaf?

options.text: They prevent any green light from entering the leaf
They make each chlorophyll molecule equally efficient at all wavelengths
They concentrate absorbed energy at the leaf's outer surface
They capture much of the green light without absorbing too much energy at once

evidence.quote: For example, even though molecules of chlorophyll are abysmal at absorbing green light, this layered arrangement allows a leaf to capture up to 80% of the green light that hits it. In fact, if a leaf were to absorb all that green light right at the surface, it could be at risk of taking in too much energy too quickly and suffering the botanical equivalent of sunburn.

private.explanationZh: 分层排列给光子多次被吸收的机会，使叶片能捕获多达 80% 的绿光；同时避免能量全部在表面被迅速吸收而造成类似晒伤的损害。因此选 D。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。分层会增加而不是阻止绿光被叶片整体吸收。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。分层不改变单个叶绿素分子的波长吸收效率。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。能量因此被分散，而不是集中在表面。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它同时涵盖提高整体捕获率和避免能量过载两个好处。

public.prompt: Why does the narrator compare green sugar-making machinery with that of purple bacteria?

options.text: To explain why the green system had a competitive advantage
To show that purple bacteria cannot use light to make sugar
To argue that larger cellular machinery always produces more energy
To identify the original organism in which chlorophyll evolved

evidence.quote: That's because the green sugar-making machinery is both smaller (and thus less energy-intensive to build) and at the same time has a more powerful system for using light energy - it can combine the energy captured from two photons of light together to fuel a supersized sugar-synthesis.

private.explanationZh: 与紫色细菌的对比突出绿色系统的两项竞争优势：装置更小、建造耗能更低，同时能合并两个光子的能量进行更强的糖合成。这个比较服务于解释绿色系统为何胜出，因此选 A。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。比较直接支撑后文“green recipe was superior”的结论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文明确说紫色细菌也能制造糖，只是效率不如绿色系统。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。绿色装置的优势之一正是更小，而非越大越好。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。比较功能与效率，不能确定叶绿素最初在哪种生物中演化。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the narrator's question about a planet dominated by purple plants?

options.text: Purple plants have already been detected on a planet in our galaxy
Green photosynthesis may not be the only possible evolutionary outcome
Chlorophyll would function better under the light of another star
All planets with life eventually develop the same photosynthetic system

evidence.quote: make that outcome inevitable, or did it just turn out that way? Is there a planet somewhere out there in our galaxy dominated by purple plants? We don't know, but what we do know is that here on Earth, chlorophyll's strengths far outweigh its distaste for greens, and for our planet, at least, maybe that's not such a bad thing.

private.explanationZh: 叙述者把“绿色是否必然”与“是否存在紫色植物占主导的星球”并列，并明确说不知道。这暗示地球上的绿色优势可能不是所有环境中唯一的演化结果，因此选 B。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文把紫色星球作为未知的可能性，而不是已发现事实。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它是对“inevitable, or did it just turn out that way”的合理推断。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有讨论其他恒星光谱下叶绿素功能更好。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。该绝对说法与叙述者对其他可能结果的开放态度相反。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-003-q01: 全文说明分层吸光、避免能量过载及高效制糖等优势，结论是这些优势远胜叶绿素对绿光吸收弱的缺点。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-003-q02: 分层排列让叶片仍能捕获大部分绿光，同时避免在表面过快吸收全部能量而受到类似晒伤的损害。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-003-q03: 比较显示绿色制糖机器更小、建造耗能更少且能合并两个光子的能量，从而说明其竞争优势。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-003-q04: 叙述者设想由紫色植物主导的星球，并追问绿色结果是否必然，暗示绿色光合作用不是唯一可能的演化结局。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the talk?

options.text: Why Olympus Mons is currently shrinking faster than Mount Everest
How engineers could construct a mountain between New York and Chicago
Why all mountains stop growing once they reach 3,754 meters
How geological processes place practical limits on mountain height on Earth

evidence.quote: Based on the strength of Earth's gravity and the density and strength of rock, in principle you could make a single conical mountain that stretched between New York and Chicago and soared over 45km!

Given all the factors that conspire to limit the height of mountains - sinking into the earth's mantle, fractures, and erosion - I wouldn't bet on our tallest mountains getting too much taller than they already are.

private.explanationZh: 文章先给出岩石强度允许的理论高度，再逐一加入地壳下沉熔化、板块碰撞导致的裂缝和侵蚀等现实约束，说明地球山脉实际高度受到多种地质过程限制。因此选 D。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。奥林匹斯山只是高度对照，原文没有说它正在快速变矮。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。纽约到芝加哥的圆锥山是理论尺度比喻，不是工程方案。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。3,754 米是库克山山顶坍塌后的高度，不是所有山脉的统一上限。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它完整概括理论高度与现实地质限制之间的核心论证。

public.prompt: Why does adding more mass to a mountain eventually limit its height?

options.text: The crust sinks into hotter material, where it softens and melts
The added mass causes Earth's gravity to become permanently stronger
The mountain blocks the movement of every nearby tectonic plate
The mantle rises above the summit and freezes the exposed rock

evidence.quote: If you add more weight above the surface, they sink lower into the earth's hot interior, and when they sink far enough, they soften and basically melt. For our conical mountain, that gives a new height limit of just 15km.

private.explanationZh: 大陆板块漂浮在半固态地幔上；山体增重会让板块沉入更热的内部，沉得足够深时岩石软化甚至熔化，从而把理论高度限制到约 15 千米。因此选 A。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确复述了“增重—下沉—软化熔化—限制高度”的因果链。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。山体增重不会使地球整体重力永久增强。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有说山体会阻止所有附近板块运动。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文是地壳向热的内部下沉，而不是地幔越过山顶并冻结岩石。

public.prompt: Why does the narrator mention the 1991 collapse of Aoraki/Mount Cook's summit?

options.text: To show that New Zealand measures mountain height differently
To illustrate how weakened mountain structures can suddenly lose height
To demonstrate that all tectonic collisions immediately destroy mountains
To compare the rate of erosion on Earth with the rate on Mars

evidence.quote: As well, the powerful collision of two tectonic plates, which creates mountains in the first place, also fractures and cracks the rock, weakening its structure and exposing it to erosion.
For example, 3,764m tall Aoraki/Mount Cook in New Zealand had its top fall off one night in 1991, trimming it down to a 3,754m mountain.

private.explanationZh: 前文说明板块碰撞会使岩石破裂，冻融、风、河流和冰川又会继续削弱支撑。库克山山顶一夜坍塌是这些削弱过程可能导致山体突然变矮的具体实例，因此选 B。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。数字变化来自山顶坍塌，不是测量标准不同。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。该事件把“结构弱化可能造成严重后果”具体化。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有说所有板块碰撞都会立即毁掉山脉。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。例子只发生在地球，没有提供火星侵蚀速率来比较。

public.prompt: What does the narrator imply about Mount Everest's future height?

options.text: It will certainly reach the theoretical 15-kilometer limit
It has stopped being affected by tectonic activity
Its future height depends on competing growth and destructive processes
It will become shorter only if Earth's gravity increases

evidence.quote: Then again, Mount Everest is still growing, 50 years from now, it could be 30 cm taller than it is today... or it could be a good deal shorter.

private.explanationZh: 珠峰仍受构造活动推动而增长，但裂缝、侵蚀和坍塌又可能使它变矮。叙述者同时给出“更高”与“更矮”的可能性，暗示结果取决于相互竞争的过程，因此选 C。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。15 千米是加入地壳下沉后的示意上限，原文并未保证珠峰达到它。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。珠峰仍在增长正说明构造过程仍然作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它解释了为什么叙述者无法确定珠峰未来会更高还是更矮。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。使山体变矮的机制包括侵蚀和坍塌，不需要地球重力增加。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-004-q01: 全文说明地壳下沉软化、岩石破裂和侵蚀等过程如何共同限制地球上山峰的实际高度。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-004-q02: 山体重量增加会让地壳沉入更热的地球内部，沉得足够深时岩石会软化并基本熔化。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-004-q03: 库克山山顶突然坍塌并降低十米，是裂缝与侵蚀削弱支撑后山体可能骤然变矮的具体例证。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-004-q04: 珠峰仍在生长，但未来既可能更高也可能明显变矮，说明其高度取决于构造抬升与破坏过程的竞争。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly explaining?

options.text: How a large-scale circulation pattern creates both equatorial rainforests and subtropical deserts
Why European sailors deliberately avoided trade winds near the equator
How polar ice caps control the location of all dry regions on Earth
Why George Hadley's theory was rejected by later meteorologists

evidence.quote: But why are those parts of our earth barren, and others green?
This giant atmospheric conveyor belt, officially called a Hadley cell, brings us both tropical rainforests and deserts.

private.explanationZh: 文章从地表绿色与荒漠的分布问题出发，用哈德莱环流解释赤道暖湿空气上升降雨形成雨林，以及失去水分后的空气在南北两侧下沉形成荒漠。因此选 A。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时概括哈德莱环流机制及雨林和沙漠两种结果。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。信风推动船只前往赤道，水手困扰的是赤道无风带，并非主动避开信风。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。极地冰盖只在开场景观描述中出现，不是荒漠分布的解释机制。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文说哈德莱的想法最终帮助解释现象，没有说后来被否定。

public.prompt: Why does the narrator describe sailors encountering the doldrums?

options.text: To explain why European trade with the equator ended
To introduce an observed wind pattern that required a scientific explanation
To show that sailors had already discovered the Hadley cell's full mechanism
To argue that ocean currents rather than air movement create deserts

evidence.quote: When sailing from Europe, trade winds pushed ships enthusiastically southwest to the equator, where suddenly, the winds would die. These were the doldrums.

private.explanationZh: 水手体验到信风把船推向赤道、到赤道又突然无风，这个可观察到的谜题引出科学家对风向成因的解释，并为哈德莱模型铺垫。因此选 B。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。无风带带来困难，但原文没有说欧洲与赤道的贸易因此终止。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确说明历史叙事在论证中的引入功能。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。水手观察到现象，完整机制由哈德莱后来提出。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。全文解释的是空气环流，不是用洋流替代空气运动。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what happens to warm, humid air as it rises at the equator?

options.text: It warms further and stores increasing amounts of moisture
It immediately crosses the stratosphere and escapes into space
It cools and releases enough moisture to support rainforests
It becomes dry before any precipitation can occur

evidence.quote: As warm, humid air at the equator rises, it cools, and--since cool air can't hold as much moisture as warm air--it rains. A lot. Enough to make rain-forests.

private.explanationZh: 赤道暖湿空气上升后冷却；冷空气容纳水汽的能力较低，所以大量降雨并支持热带雨林。因此选 C。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。空气在上升过程中冷却，而不是继续升温并储存更多水分。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。空气到达平流层后受其阻挡并向南北展开，没有逃入太空。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它完整对应上升、冷却、降雨和雨林形成的因果链。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。空气先大量降雨，随后在离开赤道时进一步失去水分。

public.prompt: How does the narrator organize the explanation in the talk?

options.text: By listing several deserts and then ranking them by annual rainfall
By comparing competing modern computer models of global climate
By describing polar, temperate, and tropical climates in alphabetical order
By posing a geographic puzzle, tracing a historical wind question, and then applying a circulation mechanism

evidence.quote: But why are those parts of our earth barren, and others green?
Hadley figured that, since the sun warms the Earth most at the equator, air to the north and south must be cooler--and therefore more dense.
As the air leaves the equator, it rains away more moisture, becoming denser and slightly cooler, until finally dry, it sinks, creating the arid bands where many of the world's famous deserts lie.

private.explanationZh: 文章先提出地球为什么有的地方绿色、有的地方荒芜；随后回顾水手与科学家面对的风向谜题，并引入哈德莱的解释；最后沿空气上升、降雨、外流和下沉的环流过程解释雨林与沙漠分布。因此选 D。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文章没有列出或按降水量排名多个沙漠。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文回顾的是历史理论并解释机制，不是比较现代计算机模型。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文章没有按字母顺序描述气候带。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确呈现“现象问题—历史观察—机制应用”的篇章结构。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-005-q01: 全文解释哈德莱环流如何在赤道造成多雨的热带雨林，并在南北两侧造成干燥的沙漠带。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-005-q02: 水手在赤道遇到风突然停歇的现象，引出了科学家需要解释的信风与无风带问题。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-005-q03: 原文指出暖湿空气上升后冷却，因冷空气容纳水汽较少而大量降雨，足以形成雨林。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-005-q04: 叙述先提出地表绿地与荒漠分布之谜，再回顾风向问题，最后用哈德莱环流机制解释两种地带。

public.prompt: What issue does the talk mainly explain about bedbugs?

options.text: How World War II insecticides permanently eliminated bedbugs from wealthy cities
Why bedbugs prefer public places like libraries and subway cars to beds
How bedbug mating behavior explains their spread better than travel does
Why bedbugs have returned as hard-to-control pests in modern life

evidence.quote: Meanwhile, increased global travel has made pretty much any bed in the world just a short flight away, both for human jetsetters and their bedbug stowaways.
The one-two punch of insecticide resistance and global transport has allowed bedbugs go from nightmare to modern reality

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查全文主旨。文章前面先描述臭虫如何吸血、如何令人厌恶，但真正的重点在后半部分：臭虫为什么会重新大规模出现。作者明确把原因概括为全球旅行带来的传播机会，以及杀虫剂抗药性带来的治理困难，因此最准确的总结是它们为何在现代生活中卷土重来而且更难控制。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中说二战后合成杀虫剂曾让美国和欧洲的臭虫消失了半个世纪，但后面紧接着说许多药剂被禁、幸存个体繁殖出抗药性，所以并不是“永久消灭”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。图书馆、地铁、电影院等只是臭虫扩散的例子，不是文章主旨；作者并没有比较它们更偏好哪些地点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。暴力交配只出现在开头作为令人不适的细节，不能解释现代臭虫回潮的核心原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。后文明确指出全球流动和抗药性形成“双重打击”，使臭虫重新成为现代现实中的难题，这正是全文核心。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what does a female bedbug do after reaching exposed skin?

options.text: She lays eggs beneath the skin before returning to the mattress.
She releases chemicals that numb the body while she feeds.
She pierces the skin with her beak and drinks blood.
She waits for several males to arrive before biting.

evidence.quote: she uses her small, sharp beak to pierce your skin and slurp up six times her weight in blood

private.explanationZh: 这是开头的直接细节信息。雌性臭虫爬到裸露皮肤后，会用尖喙刺穿皮肤并吸血，因此正确答案必须对应这个动作过程。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说它把卵产在人体皮肤下面；回到床垫下方之后提到的是交配，不是皮下产卵。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中从未提到麻醉化学物质，这是无根据添加的信息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文直接说它用尖尖的喙刺穿皮肤，并吸食相当于自己体重六倍的血液。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。七个雄虫与它交配发生在它回到藏身处之后，不是在咬人之前。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention central heating?

options.text: To show that heated homes became safer after bedbugs disappeared
To explain why bedbugs could thrive throughout the year in many places
To argue that city growth mattered less than indoor temperature
To suggest that central heating was invented mainly to fight pests

evidence.quote: central heating, which helped bedbugs thrive year-round virtually everywhere

private.explanationZh: 这里问的是作者为什么提到一个例子。中央供暖不是文章的主题，它的作用是解释人类生活环境怎样帮助臭虫全年繁殖、生存并扩大问题规模，所以属于说明因果机制的证据。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者并没有说供暖让住宅更安全；恰恰相反，供暖让臭虫更容易全年存在。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文明确指出中央供暖帮助臭虫几乎在各地都能全年繁盛，这正是提到它的目的。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有比较城市化和温度哪个更重要，而是说两者都让问题恶化。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。中央供暖的发明目的根本不是本文讨论内容，这个选项偏离文意。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the speaker's final comment?

options.text: New legal bans are already reducing modern infestations.
People can avoid bedbugs completely if they are careful travelers.
Bedbugs no longer rely on humans to spread from place to place.
Bedbugs are unlikely to be eliminated easily now.

evidence.quote: Except that in a way, we've brought them back, and this time around, they won't go down so easily.

private.explanationZh: 结尾本来像是要说一句轻松的“别让臭虫咬你”，但作者突然转成悲观总结，说明这次臭虫回归并不是短期、轻松就能压下去的。这里需要从语气和总结句中做合理推断，而不是只看字面细节。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中提到许多化学药剂已被禁用，这并不意味着现代虫害正在因此减少。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。作者强调全球旅行让任何床都可能成为传播地点，并没有说只要小心就能完全避免。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。前文明确提到臭虫会搭乘人类旅行“顺风车”扩散，所以它们仍然依赖人类流动传播。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原文说这一次它们“不会那么容易倒下”，最合理的推断就是现在不容易把它们彻底消灭。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-006-q01: 全文先描述床虱如何吸血，再回顾其长期骚扰人类的历史，最后重点解释其近二十年卷土重来的两个原因：杀虫剂禁用/抗药性，以及全球旅行传播，因此主旨是它们为何重新成为现代社会中难以控制的害虫。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-006-q02: 开头明确说雌性床虱接触裸露皮肤后，会“用尖喙刺穿皮肤并吸食血液”，与c一致。其余选项中的产卵、释放麻醉物质、等待雄虫都未被提到。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-006-q03: 讲者提到人类发明中央供暖后，床虱“几乎在任何地方都能全年繁盛”，这里中央供暖的作用是解释床虱为何能全年存活并扩张，不是在说城市化不重要或供暖是为灭虫而发明。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-006-q04: 结尾说这一次它们“不会那么容易被打倒”，还用“we're kinda screwed”强调局面棘手，因此可推断如今床虱不太可能被轻易消灭。

public.prompt: What central development does the talk focus on in the history of frozen food?

options.text: Why salty ice rooms remained the best freezing technology for centuries
How quick freezing preserved food quality and helped create the modern frozen-food market
Why Arctic fishing traditions replaced artificial freezing methods worldwide
How freezing removes nutrients from food more efficiently than chemicals do

evidence.quote: Birdseye realized that the arctic-frozen foods were tasty because they froze quickly and formed smaller ice crystals that didn't damage the cells.
Combined with clever marketing, this allowed Birdseye to bring arctic winter to the rest of the world and to almost singlehandedly jumpstart the modern market for frozen foods.

private.explanationZh: 全文并不是泛泛介绍“食物可以冷冻”，而是重点说明快速冷冻为什么更好，以及这种方法如何催生现代冷冻食品市场。前一个证据强调快速冷冻能减少细胞损伤、保住口感，后一个证据强调它带来的产业影响，因此 b 最完整地概括了文章重点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。盐冰房间在文中是旧式、效率低的做法，用来衬托后来的改进，不是最终重点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。文章把快速冷冻的品质优势和它对现代冷冻食品市场的推动作用连在一起，这是最准确的主旨概括。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。因纽特人的经验只是启发伯德赛的例子，不是说传统做法取代了现代技术。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。开头恰恰说冷冻能保留大部分营养，而不是更有效地去除营养。

public.prompt: Why does frozen food usually last longer than food in a refrigerator or on a counter?

options.text: Low temperatures slow the chemical and biological processes that break food down.
Freezing removes all liquid from food before microbes can act.
Ice crystals add nutrients back into food cells during storage.
Frozen food is always packaged more tightly than refrigerated food.

evidence.quote: if you cool food a lot, enzymes and bacteria and fungi in the food get too cold to decompose it

private.explanationZh: 这是一道直接细节题。作者解释了冷冻保鲜的科学原因：温度降低会让化学过程和生物过程变慢，所以参与分解的酶、细菌和真菌都不容易继续作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文直接说明低温会让导致分解的过程变慢，因此食物保存更久。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。冷冻不是把所有液体都移走，这把“结冰”误说成“完全脱水”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说冰晶会补回营养，反而说明大冰晶可能损伤细胞。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。作者解释的是温度与分解速度之间的关系，不是包装方式差异。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker describe early freezers as rooms full of salty ice?

options.text: To show that people in cold climates no longer needed artificial freezing
To argue that salty ice improved the taste of thawed food
To prove that freezing was originally invented for fish rather than meat
To contrast slow, cell-damaging freezing with later quick-freezing methods

evidence.quote: A slow freeze gives fluid within cells the time to stack up into big crystals. Since water expands when it freezes, the sharp edges of these crystals poke holes through the walls of the cells, and when the food thaws, the fluid leaks out.

private.explanationZh: 作者提到早期冷冻室，不是为了介绍怀旧历史，而是为了说明旧方法“冻结太慢”会造成什么后果。这个段落的功能是建立问题：慢冻形成大冰晶、刺破细胞，进而为后文快速冷冻的价值做铺垫。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。寒冷地区居民的例子在前文另有用途，这里提盐冰房间不是为了讨论他们是否需要人工冷冻。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有说盐冰让食物更好吃，反而说明慢冻会损害口感与品质。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者没有把冷冻的发明限定在某类食物上，这个说法把范围缩得太窄。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。该段的作用正是通过慢冻造成的大冰晶伤害，反衬后来的快速冷冻为什么更有效。

public.prompt: How does the talk mainly develop?

options.text: It compares Arctic communities and then argues that home freezers are unnecessary.
It begins with modern advertising and then returns to early preservation failures.
It moves from the problem of spoilage to freezing science, then to Birdseye's innovation and its everyday result.
It lists harmful preservatives before focusing only on fish-cooking techniques.

evidence.quote: Food is delicious, not to mention kind of important. But food also goes bad, so humans have invented many ways to preserve it to eat later or far from where it was harvested.
Inspired, Birdseye went on to develop a process to quickly freeze food by pressing small packages between metal plates chilled to 40 below zero.
You probably even have your own freezer, a marvelous device cold enough to quick-freeze almost any food you put in it.

private.explanationZh: 结构题要看全文推进顺序。文章先从食物容易腐坏这个普遍问题切入，再讨论冷冻原理与历史困难，随后写到伯德赛如何发展出快速冷冻方法，最后落到今天家用冷冻室的日常结果。这正是“问题—技术发展—现实应用”的组织方式。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文章并没有以比较北方社群为主线，也没有否定家用冷冻室。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。现代营销只在后段短暂出现，不是开头，更不是全文组织的主轴。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。三个证据分别对应保存问题、技术创新和当代应用，完整体现了全文推进方式。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文章虽然提到某些化学保鲜方法不理想，但并没有把重点放在鱼类烹饪技巧上。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-008-q01: 全文围绕冷冻保鲜的关键突破展开：快速冷冻能减少冰晶对细胞的破坏，从而保持口感质量；Birdseye据此开发工艺并推动现代冷冻食品市场，因此b最概括中心发展。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-008-q02: 文中直接解释：低温会让大多数化学和生物过程变慢，所以酶、细菌和真菌更难分解食物，因此冷冻食品比冷藏或常温食品保存更久。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-008-q03: 提到早期“装满咸冰的房间”后，讲者马上说明慢速冷冻会形成大冰晶并刺破细胞，随后再引出Birdseye的快速冷冻优势，所以这一例子是为了和后来的快速冷冻形成对比。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-008-q04: 结构上先从食物会腐败这一问题切入，再讲冷冻为何能保鲜，然后说明慢冻损伤细胞，接着讲Birdseye如何从北极经验得到启发并推广技术，最后落到现代家用冰箱的日常结果，c准确概括组织方式。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the talk?

options.text: To explain what freezer burn is, why it happens, and how to reduce it
To argue that frost on food always makes it unsafe to eat
To compare sea ice with freezer walls as two unrelated forms of frost
To show that regular frost from the air does more damage than frozen food does

evidence.quote: This is freezer burn, and it happens because ice in the frozen food sublimates - that is, the water changes directly from a solid to a gas rather than first melting and then evaporating.

you can avoid having your food get freezer burn by keeping your freezer consistently cold and preventing air from contacting the food in it - for example, by sealing food in plastic freezer bags without any air pockets.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题需要整合前后信息。前面的证据定义了 freezer burn 的成因，后面的证据说明如何预防，因此全文目的就是系统解释 freezer burn 是什么、为什么会发生，以及该如何减少它。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两个证据一前一后，分别对应定义与预防，合起来正好概括全文。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中明确说 freezer burn 后的食物虽然口感变差，但“仍然可以安全食用”，所以不是强调危险性。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。海冰花只是结尾用来扩展说明升华在自然界也会发生，不是做“两个无关现象”的比较。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。作者并没有比较哪种霜伤害更大，重点始终是解释 freezer burn 的机制。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why do vegetables, meat, and ice cream get freezer burn more easily than flour or nuts?

options.text: They are usually stored in the warmest part of the freezer.
They contain more water that can leave the food and form frost.
Their surfaces melt before they can finish freezing.
They absorb frost that was already floating in the freezer air.

evidence.quote: That's why freezer burn happens less to flour and nuts than to vegetables and meat and ice cream, which contain more water.

private.explanationZh: 这是中段的直接比较信息。作者把不同食物的差别归结为含水量：含水越多，可升华并转移到表面的水分就越多，所以更容易出现 freezer burn。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说这些食物通常放在冷冻室最暖的位置，这不是作者给出的原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文明确指出蔬菜、肉和冰淇淋含有更多水分，因此更容易发生 freezer burn。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者没有说这些食物表面先融化后才冻住，这属于无中生有。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。作者特别区分了普通霜和 freezer burn，后者的霜来自食物内部的水，而不是空气里原本漂浮的霜。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention frost-flowers on sea ice?

options.text: To argue that freezer burn makes natural ice unsafe to touch
To explain where most moisture inside a freezer originally comes from
To show that sublimation can create similar frost effects in nature
To prove that sea ice preserves food better than plastic bags do

evidence.quote: Frost-flowers on sea ice, for example, form when water vapor sublimates from warm ice into air too cold to hold it. Homeless, the vapor deposits back onto the surface as icy lilies - sublime sublimation.

private.explanationZh: 海冰花这个例子出现在结尾，不是转移话题，而是把前面解释过的“升华—再沉积”机制扩展到自然界。作者借此说明 freezer burn 不是冰箱里的孤立现象，而是更普遍的物理过程。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有讨论天然冰是否安全触摸，这与作者的说明重点无关。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。前文提到冷冻室墙上的霜部分来自食物和冰块，但海冰花这个例子不是为了追溯冰箱湿气来源。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。作者明确说 freezer burn 不只发生在冷冻室中，海冰花就是升华产生类似效果的自然例子。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里不是比较哪种保存方式更好，而是在说明同一种物理机制。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about a freezer that often warms up and leaves air pockets around food?

options.text: It will make regular frost disappear before freezer burn starts.
It will keep food moist because extra air blocks sublimation.
It will have little effect if the food is already frozen solid.
It will make freezer burn more likely.

evidence.quote: Sublimation happens more easily when the air is dry and when the ice is close to the melting point of water

you can avoid having your food get freezer burn by keeping your freezer consistently cold and preventing air from contacting the food in it - for example, by sealing food in plastic freezer bags without any air pockets.

private.explanationZh: 这题需要把条件倒过来想。作者先说空气干燥、温度接近融点会让升华更容易发生，接着又说预防办法是保持持续低温并避免空气接触食物。因此，如果冷冻室经常变暖、食物周围还有空气空隙，就会更容易产生 freezer burn。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者没有说普通霜会先消失，更没有说这样能阻止 freezer burn。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文建议避免空气接触食物，说明更多空气不会阻止升华，反而会增加问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。食物已经冻住并不等于万无一失，全文讨论的正是冷冻状态下仍会发生的水分转移。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。题干条件恰好违背作者提出的预防原则，因此最合理的推断就是 freezer burn 更容易发生。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-009-q01: 这段话先定义 freezer burn，再解释其成因是升华，随后说明哪些食物更容易出现、如何预防，并补充自然界中的类似现象，所以主旨就是解释它是什么、为什么发生、以及怎样减少。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-009-q02: 讲者明确说面粉和坚果比蔬菜、肉和冰淇淋更少发生 freezer burn，是因为后者“含有更多水分”，这些水会从食物内部转移到外部形成霜。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-009-q03: 结尾提到海冰上的 frost-flowers，是为了说明 freezer burn 中涉及的升华并不只发生在冰箱里，在自然界也会造成相似的结霜效果。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-009-q04: 文中说升华在空气干燥、冰接近融点时更容易发生，并建议保持冰箱持续寒冷、避免空气接触食物，因此如果冰箱常变暖且食物周围有空气空隙，就更容易发生 freezer burn。

public.prompt: What overall message does the speaker convey about 400 parts per million?

options.text: Scientists have already learned how to control atmospheric carbon dioxide safely.
A round number like 400 ppm matters only because it is easy to remember.
Natural disasters are the main reason carbon dioxide increased after 2013.
Rising atmospheric CO2 is a human-caused global experiment with serious consequences.

evidence.quote: We've used the most effective methods to increase the levels of CO2 in our atmosphere: burning coal, oil and natural gas, chopping down forests, and surprisingly, making cement.
We've seen more and bigger floods, fires, droughts, and storms. Melting ice caps and rising sea levels.

private.explanationZh: 讲话把大气中二氧化碳升高讽刺地称为“世界上最大的失控实验”。前一个证据说明这是由人类活动推动的，后一个证据说明已经出现严重后果，因此全文的核心信息就是：这场人类造成的实验正在带来危险影响。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者不是在宣布已经安全控制住 CO2，恰恰是在警告问题正在恶化。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中承认 400 ppm 在某种意义上是个圆整数，但紧接着说它标志着进入新的阶段，不是“只有好记”而已。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。洪水、火灾、干旱和海平面上升是后果，不是 CO2 上升的主要原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据一说明人为成因，证据二说明严重后果，完整支持这个主旨概括。

public.prompt: Which activities does the speaker identify as major ways humans have increased atmospheric CO2?

options.text: Burning fossil fuels, cutting forests, and making cement
Expanding solar power, recycling metals, and planting crops
Building seawalls, controlling floods, and improving weather forecasts
Freezing food, air travel, and mining helium

evidence.quote: We've used the most effective methods to increase the levels of CO2 in our atmosphere: burning coal, oil and natural gas, chopping down forests, and surprisingly, making cement.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道直接细节题。作者明确列出了几类主要的人类活动：燃烧化石燃料、砍伐森林以及制造水泥。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项完整对应原文列出的三类主要来源。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这些活动没有出现在原文列举中，而且其中一些通常还不是增加 CO2 的典型说法。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这些更像应对后果的措施，不是作者说的排放来源。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文没有把冷冻食物、航空旅行和开采氦放在同一组主要原因中。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker address listeners as participants in an experiment?

options.text: To praise the audience for collecting climate data more carefully than scientists do
To emphasize that ordinary people are helping produce the emissions being discussed
To show that only politicians can change atmospheric chemistry
To suggest that climate change exists only inside laboratories

evidence.quote: After all, it is people -like you and me and major corporations- with access to cars, air conditioning, and supermarkets, who are responsible for the large majority of today's greenhouse gas emissions.
This global experiment can't continue without your participation.

private.explanationZh: 这里的“参与者”是一种带有讽刺意味的说法。作者并不是夸听众在做科学，而是指出日常消费和用能行为本身就在推动温室气体排放，所以听众并非旁观者，而是问题的一部分。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者没有赞扬听众收集数据，重点完全不在科学测量上。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据明确把“你我”都纳入温室气体排放责任之中，这正是称听众为参与者的目的。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者要求大家去告诉朋友、家人和政治人物，说明责任并不只在政治人物。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。全文把现实世界中的排放行为称为实验，意思恰恰是它并不局限于实验室。

public.prompt: What is implied by the final examples about idling a car, leaving on the AC, and wasting food?

options.text: Only people who own cars contribute meaningfully to climate change.
Atmospheric CO2 rises mainly because supermarkets operate inefficiently.
Everyday habits can add to greenhouse gas emissions.
Small personal choices completely determine global climate outcomes by themselves.

evidence.quote: the next time you let your car idle, leave the AC on when you're not home, or forget to eat the food you bought... you're part of something big.

private.explanationZh: 结尾列举的都是非常普通的生活行为，作者的用意是让抽象的气候问题变得具体可感。由此可以推断，日常习惯会积累成温室气体排放，而不是只有大型机构才与这个问题相关。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者同时提到空调和浪费食物，责任并不只限于有车的人。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。超市只是前文举例中的现代生活条件之一，不是作者暗示的主要单一原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。题干中的具体例子都属于日常选择，作者正是借此说明个人习惯会增加排放。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。作者强调个人参与，但没有说个人小行为“单独完全决定”全球气候结果，这个说法过强。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-010-q01: 讲者把大气二氧化碳升到400 ppm称为“世界上最大的失控实验”，并把原因归于人类活动，同时列举洪水、火灾、干旱、风暴等后果，所以核心信息是：这是一场由人类造成且后果严重的全球实验。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-010-q02: 原文点名三类主要来源：燃烧煤、石油和天然气，砍伐森林，以及制造水泥，a与之完全对应。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-010-q03: 把听众称作实验参与者，是为了强调排放并不只是抽象的系统行为，普通人连同企业都在通过日常能源消费参与制造温室气体排放。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-010-q04: 结尾列举汽车怠速、空调空转和浪费食物，都是在说明看似平常的生活习惯也会增加温室气体排放，而不是说只有车主有影响，或个人选择单独决定全部结果。

public.prompt: What main scientific point does the talk develop about Earth's atmosphere?

options.text: How some atmospheric gases escape Earth and how the magnetic field helps limit the loss
Why rockets and large impacts are the ordinary cause of atmospheric escape today
How Earth and Mars lose atmosphere at exactly the same rate
Why humans may soon run out of oxygen because air is leaking into space

evidence.quote: that precious atmosphere of ours is actually leaking, every second, into outer space.
But if our planet didn't have a magnetic field at all, things could be a lot worse.

private.explanationZh: 全文围绕两个核心点展开：第一，地球大气确实会缓慢泄漏到太空；第二，地球磁场在减轻这种流失方面起到重要保护作用。开头提出现象，后文强调没有磁场会糟得多，因此 a 最能概括整篇讲话。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两个证据分别对应“正在泄漏”与“磁场保护”，正好概括全文主线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。大撞击在文中被排除为日常机制，火箭和猫只是用来说明逃逸速度，不是常见原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者提到火星是为了说明差异，而不是说地球和火星的流失速率一样。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。结尾明确说不用担心，因为这个过程极其缓慢，所以并非“很快就会没空气”。

public.prompt: According to the talk, which gases regularly escape Earth's atmosphere today?

options.text: Oxygen and nitrogen
Carbon dioxide and methane
Water vapor and ozone
Hydrogen and helium

evidence.quote: the only gases that regularly escape Earth's atmosphere today are Hydrogen and Helium - the lightest elements in the universe.

private.explanationZh: 这是中段明确给出的事实信息。作者直接说，如今规律性从地球大气逃逸出去的只有最轻的两种元素：氢和氦。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。氧和氮并没有被说成如今会规律性逃逸到太空。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。二氧化碳和甲烷在这篇讲话中并未被列为定期逃逸的气体。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。水蒸气和臭氧也不是作者给出的答案。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原文直接点名 regularly escape today 的就是 Hydrogen and Helium。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention Mars?

options.text: To show where Earth's escaping hydrogen eventually gathers
To illustrate how much worse atmospheric loss can be without a protective magnetic field
To argue that Mars once had stronger gravity than Earth
To explain why solar wind never affects planets with thin atmospheres

evidence.quote: Mars, for example, has no protective magnetic field, and so what little atmosphere it has is constantly buffeted and ripped away by the solar wind.

private.explanationZh: 火星在这里是一个对比例子，不是另起新话题。作者用它来说明：如果没有像地球这样的保护性磁场，太阳风会更持续、更严重地剥离大气，因此这个例子服务于突出磁场的重要性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说地球逃逸的氢最后会聚集到火星上。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。作者明确利用火星说明缺少保护磁场时，大气流失会严重得多。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者没有比较两颗行星过去谁的引力更强。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文意正相反：太阳风会持续影响缺少保护的行星。

public.prompt: How is the talk mainly organized?

options.text: It recounts the history of space travel and ends with a plan to study Mars.
It lists the layers of the atmosphere from bottom to top and then predicts sudden collapse.
It introduces atmospheric leakage, explains several escape routes, contrasts Earth with Mars, and ends by saying the process is very slow.
It focuses on everyday weather first and then shifts to the chemistry of cement.

evidence.quote: that precious atmosphere of ours is actually leaking, every second, into outer space.
Finally, some of Earth's magnetic field lines are weakened and pushed away from the earth by the solar wind, a violent stream of plasma emanating from our sun.
Mars, for example, has no protective magnetic field, and so what little atmosphere it has is constantly buffeted and ripped away by the solar wind.
No need to worry - it'll take a few billion years before we lose all of our hydrogen this way

private.explanationZh: 这是一道整体结构题。文章先提出“地球大气在泄漏”这一现象，再连续解释几种逃逸机制，然后用火星做对照说明缺少磁场的后果，最后以“过程非常慢、暂时不用担心”收束。四段证据正对应这四个结构步骤。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文章没有回顾航天史，也没有以研究火星计划作为结尾。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。全文不是按大气层分层展开，更没有预测会突然崩塌。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。四个证据依次对应引入问题、机制说明、火星对比和缓慢结论，完整反映了文章组织方式。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。天气和水泥并不是这篇讲话的结构主线，这个选项混入了别篇内容。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-011-q01: 整段先提出地球大气正在缓慢泄漏，再解释哪些轻气体会逃逸、通过哪些机制逃逸，并强调磁场能减少这种损失，因此主旨是大气如何逃逸以及磁场怎样起保护作用。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-011-q02: 文中直接说，如今定期逃离地球大气的“只有氢和氦”，因为它们是宇宙中最轻的元素。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-011-q03: 提到火星是为了做对比：火星没有保护性磁场，所以其稀薄大气不断被太阳风冲刷带走，从而说明没有磁场时大气流失会严重得多。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-011-q04: 文章先引入大气泄漏这一现象，接着分几种机制解释氢和氦如何逃逸，再拿火星与地球对比说明磁场的重要性，最后说明虽然每秒都在流失，但总体过程极慢，所以c最符合整体组织。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the talk?

options.text: Why modern cars in Samoa became unsafe after alcohol sales resumed
Why societies came to drive on different sides of the road and how those rules spread
How game theory can predict future car prices in island nations
Why right-handed soldiers were the only cause of modern traffic laws

evidence.quote: There aren't clear and obvious reasons to choose one side over the other, so the origin of driving conventions is a perfect opportunity for the mathematics of game theory and symmetry breaking.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。开头明确提出“为什么有的地方靠右行驶、有的地方靠左行驶”这一问题，并说明这种规则的形成值得追溯其起源。后文分别解释了英国、欧洲、美国等地规则如何形成和传播，所以主旨是不同社会为何形成不同的靠左/靠右行驶传统，以及这些传统如何扩散。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。萨摩亚换侧行驶只是开头的一个引入事例，不是整篇谈话的核心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它概括了全文：先问为何会分左右，再用历史与实际因素解释不同地区的规则及其传播。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中只顺带提到博弈论和对称破缺是一个可讨论的角度，并没有讨论汽车价格预测。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。右手持武器的骑士只是关于左侧通行起源的一种可能解释，不能概括全部现代交通规则。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why did Samoa switch to driving on the left?

options.text: To import cheaper cars from nearby left-driving countries
To copy a transportation law from England
To reduce alcohol-related crashes permanently
To match the driving system used in the United States

evidence.quote: The switch was so Samoans could get cheap "hand-me-down" cars from nearby left-side driving New Zealand and Australia.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。原文直接说明，萨摩亚改为左侧行驶，是为了能够从邻近的新西兰和澳大利亚购买便宜的二手车。这是非常明确的因果关系。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文直接说改制是为了买到来自左侧行驶国家的便宜“二手车”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中提到英国把左侧行驶立法，但没有说萨摩亚是为了模仿英国。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。禁酒三天是为了在切换期间保证安全，不是改制的长期原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。美国是右侧行驶，和萨摩亚改为左侧行驶的原因相反。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention India, South Africa, and Australia?

options.text: To show that only former colonies still drive on the left today
To compare which left-driving countries have the safest roads
To illustrate how England's left-side rule spread through British colonial history
To show that Napoleon forced those countries to adopt left-side driving

evidence.quote: And today, most people who drive on the left side live in countries, like India, South Africa, and Australia, which were once British colonies.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查修辞目的。说话者列举印度、南非和澳大利亚，不是为了介绍这些国家本身，而是为了证明前面“英国把左侧通行写入法律”之后，这一规则随着殖民历史传播到了许多前英国殖民地。例子服务于“传播路径”这一论点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中说“多数”左侧行驶者生活在这些前殖民地国家，并没有说“只有” former colonies 才左侧行驶。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文完全没有比较这些国家的道路安全水平。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。三个国家被举出来，是为了说明英国的左侧通行传统如何扩散到其前殖民地。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。拿破仑影响的是欧洲更多改为右侧行驶的传播，不是这些国家左侧行驶的来源。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the practical reason early American drivers kept their wagons to the right?

options.text: It made it easier to load cargo from the roadside
It allowed horses to rest their left legs more often
It helped Americans imitate French driving customs
It gave right-handed drivers a better view of oncoming traffic while controlling the horses

evidence.quote: As a result, they drove their wagons to the right so they could be closer to the center of the road to see and steer clear of oncoming traffic.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。原文先说右撇子更喜欢站在或骑在马的左侧，以便用右手控制动物；随后说因此他们让马车靠右行驶，这样自己更接近道路中央，便于看见并避开迎面车辆。综合起来可推断，靠右行驶的实用目的在于更好地观察来车并安全操控。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有提到装卸货物这一动机。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文讨论的是驾驶者的视野和控马方式，不是马腿负担。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说美国这样做是为了模仿法国；美国的习惯来自早期马车操控方式。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确整合了“右手控马”和“靠近道路中央观察来车”这两个信息点。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-012-q01: 开头提出萨摩亚改道，中段追溯英法欧洲的左右侧传统，结尾说明美国为何走右侧，整体主旨就是不同社会为何在道路两侧行驶以及这些规则如何传播。其余选项都只抓住局部或把原因说得过于绝对。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-012-q02: 原文明确说萨摩亚改为左侧行驶，是为了从邻近同样左侧行驶的新西兰和澳大利亚获得便宜的二手车。其余选项都没有得到文本支持。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-012-q03: 这三个国家被放在“曾是英国殖民地”这一说明后面，作用是举例说明英国的左侧行驶法律如何通过殖民历史扩散到今天仍靠左行驶的国家。A把范围说成“只有”，过度绝对；B和D都与原文目的不符。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-012-q04: 美国部分说明右撇子赶车人喜欢走在或骑在马匹左侧，以右手控制动物，因此把车赶到道路右边，好让自己更靠近道路中央，从而看清并避开来车。所以可推知其实际好处是便于右撇子控马并观察对向交通。

public.prompt: What is the main idea of the talk?

options.text: Small competitors can survive by occupying niches that dominant rivals largely ignore
Advertising always matters more than product quality in competitive markets
Forests and supermarkets follow completely different competitive rules
Microbreweries usually defeat large brewers by producing the highest sales volume

evidence.quote: many species and businesses that eke out a living in the shadows of giants employ similar strategies to succeed in their respective dog-eat-dog worlds of intense competition for limited resources.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。开头已经点明核心：自然界中的小型物种和商业中的小企业，都会在巨头阴影下采用相似策略求生。后文用蕨类植物和小型酿酒厂展开，说明它们不是正面硬拼，而是利用大公司和大树不擅长或不重视的生态位生存。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了全文关于“小个体如何通过空缺生态位生存”的核心论点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。广告只是大型啤酒商优势的一部分，全文并未声称广告永远比产品质量更重要。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。恰恰相反，全文强调森林和市场在竞争机制上有相似性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文明确说小企业不是靠销量取胜，而是靠细分 niche 生存。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what resources do dominant trees and large brewers mainly capture?

options.text: Water and shelf space
Sunlight and consumers' dollars
Soil nutrients and imported ingredients
Skilled workers and transportation routes

evidence.quote: sunlight in one case and consumers' dollars in the other.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。说话者直接把森林和啤酒行业并列：在森林中最关键的资源是阳光，在啤酒市场中则是消费者的钱。强者先占据最有价值的资源，导致弱者难以用同一策略竞争。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有把这两个资源对应到“大树”和“大啤酒商”的主要竞争目标。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文直接给出这两个资源的对应关系。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。土壤养分和进口原料并不是这里强调的核心资源。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文没有讨论劳动力和运输路线。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention the so-called "empty niches"?

options.text: To argue that dominant competitors never make strategic mistakes
To show that small competitors should always lower their prices
To explain how weaker competitors survive by specializing where the "big guys" are less effective
To suggest that all small firms eventually become as large as the market leaders

evidence.quote: by specializing in conditions the "big guys" are not so good at: the so-called empty niches.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查修辞目的。“empty niches”不是一个孤立术语，而是作者用来解释小型竞争者生存机制的关键概念：它们并不和巨头在同一位置正面竞争，而是专门利用巨头做得不好的条件和剩余资源。这个概念把蕨类和微型酿酒厂两组例子统一起来。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。恰恰因为大公司并非在所有条件下都最强，小竞争者才有生存空间。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中强调的是差异化和专门化，不是降价竞争。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确说明了提出“空缺生态位”的功能，即解释小竞争者如何避开正面冲突而生存。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文从未暗示所有小企业最终都会长成巨头。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about microbreweries that appeal to aficionados?

options.text: They need to spend more on advertising than large brewers do
They can survive only if they imitate mild flavors
They usually capture the largest share of the market
They accept a smaller customer base in exchange for less direct competition

evidence.quote: But where there are resources, there's potential to survive

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。结尾说明，即使资源更少，只要仍有可利用资源，就有生存可能。结合前文关于“aficionados”人数较少但愿意购买强烈、奇特口味啤酒的描述，可以推断微型酿酒厂接受较小市场规模，换取避开与大品牌的直接正面对抗。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文正好说小酿酒厂难以像大品牌那样依靠大规模广告。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。它们的策略是“odd, trendy, and strongly flavored”，不是模仿温和口味。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文明确说按“体量”定义的赢家仍是大树和大公司，小企业不是最大份额获得者。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它是对“资源虽少但足以生存”与“小众爱好者市场”两层信息的合理整合。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-013-q01: 全文把林下蕨类和小型酿酒厂并列，核心论点是小竞争者通过利用大竞争者不擅长或不重视的生态位/市场空隙而生存下来。B、C、D都与全文结论相反或过窄。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-013-q02: 原文直接说森林和啤酒业竞争中，赢家按占有资源多少来定义，对应资源分别是阳光和消费者的钱。其余选项不是文中给出的主要资源。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-013-q03: “empty niches”出现在解释弱势竞争者如何成功的段落中，意思是它们专门利用“大块头”不擅长的条件，从而避开正面竞争并存活。其他选项都歪曲了这一定义。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-013-q04: 文中说微型啤酒厂靠古怪、潮流和重口味吸引少数爱好者，这类人比大众少，但只要那里有资源就能生存。这说明它们接受较小顾客群，以换取较少的直接竞争。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly about?

options.text: Why amniotic fluid becomes dangerous after the second month of development
How fetuses handle bodily waste before birth and why their first poop differs from normal feces
Why newborn babies immediately develop adult digestive bacteria
How the umbilical cord replaces every function of the digestive system

evidence.quote: So most of the potential poop products stay with the mother.
a fetus's large intestine is mostly sterile and devoid of the billions of bacteria that break down our waste and make up as much as 50% of the brown pulp known as feces.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。全文围绕“胎儿在子宫里会不会排尿、排便”展开：前半说明会排尿，后半解释大部分形成粪便的物质由母体处理，而胎儿肠道内形成的是细菌很少的胎便（meconium），所以胎儿不会像出生后的人那样产生普通粪便。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说羊水在第二个月后变得危险，重点也不是危险性。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它完整概括了“胎儿如何处理排泄物”和“为什么第一泡便便不同于普通粪便”这两个核心内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文相反地强调胎儿大肠大多是无菌的，并非立刻具备成人那样的细菌环境。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。脐带确实承担营养和废物转运功能的一部分，但并没有“替代消化系统的一切功能”。

public.prompt: According to the speaker, why is fetal urine "not as gross as it sounds"?

options.text: Because it is sterile and does not contain harmful bacteria
Because the fetus never actually swallows amniotic fluid
Because the fetus digests its own solid waste before birth
Because the urine is immediately converted into nutrients

evidence.quote: For one thing, urine--unlike feces--is sterile, so it doesn't contain bacteria that could make the fetus sick.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。说话者解释说，胎儿喝下含有自己尿液的羊水听起来恶心，但并没有那么糟，因为尿液是无菌的，不含会让胎儿生病的细菌。这里的关键是“sterile”和“doesn't contain bacteria”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文明确给出这一原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文说胎儿会吞咽、也就是会喝下羊水。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说胎儿在出生前把固体废物“消化掉”；相反，后文介绍了胎便的形成。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。尿液不会立刻转化成营养，这不是原文论点。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare a fetus's large intestine with the large intestine of people outside the womb?

options.text: To show that fetuses digest solid food more efficiently than adults do
To argue that fetal intestines produce more bacteria than adult intestines
To show that fetal waste is stored for a longer time than adult waste
To explain why fetal waste becomes meconium rather than ordinary bacteria-rich feces

evidence.quote: a fetus's large intestine is mostly sterile and devoid of the billions of bacteria that break down our waste and make up as much as 50% of the brown pulp known as feces.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查篇章组织。说话者之所以把胎儿的大肠和出生后人的大肠进行对比，是为了解释前面提到的绿色黏稠物为什么不是普通意义上的粪便。普通粪便之所以呈现那种状态，与大量细菌分解废物有关；而胎儿大肠大多无菌，因此形成的是胎便。这个对比是为了解释因果，而不是单纯比较器官。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有比较谁的固体食物消化效率更高。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文说胎儿大肠“mostly sterile”，正好不是细菌更多。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。对比重点不是存储时间，而是细菌环境和废物性质。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确说明了这个对比在论证中的作用：解释为什么形成的是胎便而非普通粪便。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the material in a baby's first dirty diaper?

options.text: It is mostly made of bacteria from the large intestine
It contains no digestive waste at all
It contains waste material but far fewer bacteria than normal feces
It is identical to the mother's digested food

evidence.quote: a fetus's large intestine is mostly sterile and devoid of the billions of bacteria that break down our waste and make up as much as 50% of the brown pulp known as feces.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。原文指出，胎儿的大肠大多无菌，缺少构成普通粪便重要部分的那些细菌；同时前文又说胎儿肠道中确实会产生一种绿色黏稠物。综合可推断，出生后第一次排出的东西确实是废物，但细菌含量远低于普通粪便。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文恰恰强调胎儿大肠缺少大量细菌，所以不可能“主要由细菌构成”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。前文说胎儿肠道里仍会形成一些废物分解后的物质，因此不是“完全没有消化废物”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它综合了“有胎便形成”和“细菌很少”两层信息。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。母亲确实处理了大部分潜在排泄物，但婴儿第一泡便并不等同于母亲消化后的食物。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-014-q01: 讲话依次回答胎儿会不会在子宫里排尿、排便，以及为什么第一泡“大便”和普通粪便不同，主旨正是胎儿出生前如何处理排泄物以及初便为何特殊。其他选项都只覆盖一部分或夸大某个器官功能。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-014-q02: 原文明确说这并没有听起来那么恶心，首先因为尿液与粪便不同，是无菌的，不含会让胎儿生病的细菌。其余选项要么与文本相反，要么并未提到。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-014-q03: 说话者把胎儿大肠与子宫外人的大肠对比，是为了说明胎儿大肠大多无菌，缺少分解废物并构成普通粪便重要部分的细菌，因此形成的是胎便而不是普通细菌丰富的粪便。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-014-q04: 原文说胎儿肠道中仍有一些废物被分解形成胎便，但胎儿大肠大多无菌，因此婴儿第一块脏尿布里的物质含有废物，却比普通粪便少得多的细菌。

public.prompt: What historical development does the talk mainly explain?

options.text: Why North America has always had many species of locusts
How settlers intentionally bred larger and more destructive grasshoppers
How the Rocky Mountain locust went from a major agricultural pest to extinction because farming destroyed its breeding habitat
Why bounty hunting was the most effective historical method for controlling crop pests

evidence.quote: the Rocky Mountain locust, which devastated crops across the Great Plains from 1850 to 1880.
plows, livestock and irrigation excel at destroying locust eggs and crucial locust nymph habitat.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。全文先介绍北美唯一的蝗虫种如何在大平原造成巨大农业灾害，后半再解释这种害虫为什么反而灭绝了：定居者在河谷发展农业，无意中用犁地、放牧和灌溉破坏了蝗卵和若虫栖息地。整篇是在讲一个超级害虫如何被农业“意外消灭”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文明确说北美只发现过一种 locust，而不是“一直有很多种”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。定居者并没有故意培育更大的蝗虫，反而无意间破坏了其繁殖地。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它最完整地概括了“曾经肆虐的大害虫如何因农业开发而灭绝”的主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。赏金和炸毁繁殖地被说成可能更令人满足而非更成功，不是主旨。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why did the locust swarms especially attract the attention of white settlers?

options.text: Because the locusts preferred cultivated crops to prairie grasses
Because the locusts changed color during cold winters
Because the locusts mainly destroyed telegraph equipment
Because the locusts appeared only in towns near Canada

evidence.quote: The fact that the locusts preferred cultivated crops to prairie grasses ensured that their massive swarms caught the attention of white settlers

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。文中明确指出，蝗虫更喜欢吃人工种植的农作物，而不是草原上的草，因此它们的大群出现时会直接威胁定居者的生计，自然就特别引人注意。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文直接给出这一原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。颜色变化是 locust 一般特征之一，但不是这里解释 settlers 特别关注的原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有说它们主要破坏电报设备。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文没有把蝗灾限定在加拿大附近的城镇。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker include one observer's estimate that a swarm was 110 miles wide and 1,800 miles long?

options.text: To prove that telegraphed reports were more accurate than direct observation
To emphasize the enormous scale of the locust outbreak
To show that settlers usually exaggerated the damage done by insects
To compare the swarm's movement to the migration of birds

evidence.quote: he estimated the swarm to be 110 miles wide and 1,800 miles long, roughly twice the size of Colorado.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查修辞目的。说话者引用极其夸张的宽度和长度估计，不是为了讨论测量方法，而是为了让听者直观感受到蝗灾规模之大。再加上“约有两个科罗拉多州那么大”的比较，作用就是强化震撼感。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。电报报告只是估算来源之一，重点不在比较哪种方法更准确。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。数字和州面积对比都服务于突出蝗群规模惊人。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。说话者没有借此质疑 settlers 是否夸大其词。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里没有把蝗群与鸟类迁徙作类比。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker say that agriculture was the Rocky Mountain locust's "kryptonite"?

options.text: Because crops poisoned adult locusts immediately after they fed
Because farmers discovered a chemical that prevented locusts from mating
Because bounty payments encouraged settlers to hunt every remaining swarm
Because ordinary farming practices unintentionally destroyed the eggs and habitat needed for future swarms

evidence.quote: plows, livestock and irrigation excel at destroying locust eggs and crucial locust nymph habitat.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。“kryptonite”比喻致命弱点。原文解释说，农业开发中的犁地、牲畜和灌溉会破坏蝗卵以及若虫生境，因此虽然农业原本是蝗虫取食的对象，却反过来切断了它们完成种群恢复和再度成灾所需的繁殖条件。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说作物会立即毒死成虫。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有提到任何化学方法阻止交配。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。赏金制度被提到，但并不是导致灭绝的核心机制。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确概括了农业为何成为其致命弱点：破坏繁殖所需的卵和栖息地。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-015-q01: 全文先介绍落基山蝗虫如何成灾，再解释它们为何最终灭绝，关键原因是农业开发无意间破坏了其繁殖地和卵，因此主旨对应C。其他选项与文本明显不符或只抓住次要细节。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-015-q02: 原文明确指出，这种蝗虫偏爱人工栽培作物而不是草原草，因此其大规模虫群特别引起白人定居者注意。其余选项不是文中原因。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-015-q03: 说话者给出虫群宽度和长度的惊人数值，是为了突出蝗灾规模极其庞大，让听者直观感受到其严重性，而不是讨论电报准确性或鸟类迁徙。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-015-q04: “农业是它们的氪石”紧接着前文解释：犁地、放牧和灌溉很擅长破坏蝗虫卵和若虫栖息地，导致蝗虫再也达不到群聚转变和成灾所需数量，因此D最符合推断。

public.prompt: How does the talk determine which place on Earth is truly hottest?

options.text: Why official weather stations should be removed from desert regions
How the method of measurement changes which place counts as the hottest, with satellite data pointing to a part of the Lut Desert rather than Death Valley
Why air temperature is always more important than surface temperature
How NASA satellites were designed mainly to help people cook food outdoors

evidence.quote: weather stations measure air temperature in the shade about a meter and a half above the ground.
a nearby portion of the Lut Desert is the winner at 70.7C, or 159.3F.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。全文并不是简单报出一个最热点，而是在说明“怎么测”会影响“哪里最热”的结论。传统气象站测的是离地面一定高度、阴影中的空气温度，而卫星测的是地表辐射温度；因此最终“最热地表”不是常被猜到的死亡谷，而是伊朗卢特沙漠附近的一块区域。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有主张拆除气象站，而是指出其覆盖有限、测量对象不同。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它同时包含了全文两个关键点：测量方法差异，以及由此得出的最热点结论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文并没有说空气温度“总是更重要”，相反强调若讨论地表最热，地表温度更关键。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。烹饪类比只是结尾的形象化表达，不是卫星设计目的。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker say Death Valley is not a complete answer to the question of the hottest place on Earth?

options.text: Because its famous record measures shaded air temperature above the ground rather than the temperature of the sunlit surface itself
Because its weather station was destroyed before the record could be verified
Because it is cooler than Scotland during most of the year
Because NASA satellites cannot detect temperatures in North America

evidence.quote: weather stations measure air temperature in the shade about a meter and a half above the ground.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。说话者指出，死亡谷的著名纪录来自官方气象站，而气象站测的是阴影中、离地约一米半的空气温度；如果问题是“地球表面哪里最热”，那这并不能完全代表在太阳直晒下的地表温度。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确重述了死亡谷纪录的测量对象与“最热地表”问题之间的差别。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说气象站被毁。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。提到苏格兰只是为了说明气象站分布稀疏，不是比较年平均温度。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中明确说卫星每天测量地球温度，并没有排除北美。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention that the globe has only about six official weather stations on average for an area the size of Scotland?

options.text: To argue that Scotland should build more desert research centers
To show that weather stations are most common in the hottest places on Earth
To illustrate how sparse official weather-station coverage is worldwide
To explain why Scotland is a leading candidate for extreme heat waves

evidence.quote: there are only 11,119 official weather stations scattered across the globe, which equates to only 1 station every 13,000 square kilometers, or about six on average for an area the size of Scotland

private.explanationZh: 本题考查修辞目的。这里提到“相当于一个苏格兰大小的区域平均只有约六个官方气象站”，是把抽象的大数字转成听者更容易理解的尺度。它的作用是说明全球气象站覆盖其实非常稀疏，因此难以依靠地面站找到真正最热的地表位置。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有主张苏格兰建设更多研究中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。说话者恰恰强调炎热、偏远地区更少有气象站。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个类比就是为了让听者直观理解气象站分布稀疏。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文还开玩笑说苏格兰并不是热浪的有力候选者。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker end by saying, "No need for your toaster oven, breakfast is served"?

options.text: To suggest that desert travelers commonly cook eggs on the ground
To criticize people who rely on kitchen appliances instead of thermometers
To argue that satellite measurements are mainly useful for household tasks
To help listeners grasp how extreme the recorded surface temperature is by comparing it to cooking heat

evidence.quote: No need for your toaster oven, breakfast is served.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查修辞目的。结尾这句明显是夸张而形象的比喻，承接前面“这个温度比让蛋黄蛋白质凝固所需温度还高一度”的信息。说话者借“烤面包机都不用了，早餐上桌”帮助听者把抽象的 70.7C 具体化，感受到这种地表温度有多极端。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里不是在给出真实旅行建议，而是形象化说明温度之高。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有批评家用电器或温度计使用者。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。卫星用途是测量地表温度，不是服务家务。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确说明了这个幽默收尾的功能：把极端高温转化为可感知的烹饪类比。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-016-q01: 讲话先说明死亡谷的纪录其实是阴凉处空气温度，再说明若问地表真正最热处，应使用卫星测地表温度，最终得到伊朗卢特沙漠附近区域最高。因此主旨是测量方法改变了“最热地点”的答案。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-016-q02: 原文说死亡谷的56.7摄氏度是官方最高纪录，但天气站测的是离地约1.5米、阴影中的空气温度，不是阳光直射下地表本身温度，所以它不是这个问题的完整答案。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-016-q03: 提到“一个苏格兰大小的区域平均只有约六个官方天气站”，是为了让听者理解全球天气站分布多么稀疏，从而说明仅靠天气站难以找出真正最热的地表位置。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-016-q04: 结尾把70.7摄氏度与蛋黄凝固、也就是烹饪温度相比，是一种形象化类比，帮助听者体会该地表温度极端到什么程度，而不是认真建议在那里做饭。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the talk?

options.text: How ocean chemistry controls the formation and dissolution of calcium carbonate shells
Why shallow-water shell-builders are replacing deep-sea organisms
How atmospheric oxygen helps sea creatures breathe at different depths
Why coral-building algae are the only organisms that can make their own shells

evidence.quote: microscopic coccolithophores, coral-building algae, and giant snails engineer their own building material, like magic, by pulling two dissolved chemicals -- calcium and carbonate -- out of the water to form solid shells of, surprise, calcium carbonate.
when carbon dioxide in the atmosphere increases, carbonate in the ocean decreases and shell-building gets harder to do - at least for a moment.

private.explanationZh: 开头说明许多海洋生物靠海水中的钙和碳酸根造壳，后面又解释二氧化碳变化会削弱这种条件，所以核心是在讲海洋化学怎样控制碳酸钙贝壳的形成与溶解。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确，概括了前半段的造壳机制和后半段的溶解、补偿与二氧化碳影响。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，原文没有说浅海生物正在取代深海生物，重点也不是生态竞争。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，氧气只被顺带举例说明气体会溶入海水，不是全文主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，文中列举了多类造壳生物，并未说只有藻类能造壳。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what happens when seawater already contains a high concentration of calcium and carbonate?

options.text: Shells begin dissolving closer to the surface
Shells can sink deeper before they dissolve
Deep water stops dissolving calcium carbonate entirely
Shell-builders no longer need surface water

evidence.quote: if the concentration is high, shells sink deeper before their calcium carbonate dissolves.

private.explanationZh: 文中直接说明，如果海水里钙和碳酸根浓度高，贝壳要下沉到更深处才会开始溶解。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，这对应的是浓度低时溶解深度上移，不是浓度高。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，和原文表述基本同义，是直接信息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，原文说更深才溶解，不是完全不溶解。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，表层仍然是造壳者主要生活区，这里说的是溶解深度变化。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker describe the chemical steps in which dissolved CO2 becomes carbonic acid and then hydrogen carbonate?

options.text: To show that oxygen and carbon dioxide affect shell-building in the same way
To explain why dissolved gases matter only in the deep ocean
To show why extra atmospheric CO2 temporarily reduces the carbonate available for shells
To argue that carbonic acid permanently destroys all shells in the ocean

evidence.quote: If it weren't for the ocean's own balancing act, any incoming carbon dioxide would be bad news for shell builders, because more CO2 means less CO3.

private.explanationZh: 这段化学过程不是为了考公式，而是为了说明为什么大气中二氧化碳增加后，海洋里的碳酸根会减少，从而让造壳暂时更困难。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，文中没有说氧气和二氧化碳对造壳作用相同。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，这一解释针对海表与大气交换后的影响，不限于深海。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确，化学步骤正是为了支撑“更多CO2会减少CO3”的因果链。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，原文强调的是暂时变难以及后续补偿，不是永久毁灭全部贝壳。

public.prompt: What problem does the speaker identify as the more immediate risk?

options.text: Deep-ocean chemistry may stop changing altogether
Surface shell-builders will quickly move into deeper water
All existing shells on the seafloor will dissolve at once
CO2 may rise faster than the ocean can restore favorable conditions for shell-building

evidence.quote: the risk that, for a time, CO2 levels change faster than the ocean can compensate, so that even if it would eventually stabilize and allow shell formation at the surface, it would take centuries to do so.

private.explanationZh: 结尾明确说真正更紧迫的问题不是海洋永远无法恢复，而是二氧化碳变化太快，补偿需要几个世纪，表层造壳生物会在这段时间里承受打击。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，原文没有说深海化学会停止变化。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，讲话者担心的是表层环境恶化，不是生物迅速迁往深海。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，文中把“所有贝壳都开始溶解”说成极端可能，不是更紧迫风险。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确，准确概括了结尾强调的时间尺度问题。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-027-q01: 通篇都在解释海洋化学如何决定碳酸钙贝壳的形成、下沉时的溶解，以及大气二氧化碳变化带来的影响，A最概括全文主旨。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-027-q02: 原文明确说如果海水中钙和碳酸根浓度高，贝壳会在更深处才开始溶解，因此能下沉得更深，B与原文一致。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-027-q03: 讲述CO2先形成碳酸再与碳酸根结合，是为了说明大气中额外CO2会暂时减少海洋里可用于造壳的碳酸根，所以C正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-027-q04: 说话者明确对比后指出，更紧迫的不是所有贝壳都溶解，而是CO2变化速度可能快于海洋补偿速度，导致表层造壳条件长期恶化，所以选D。

public.prompt: What process is mainly explained in the talk?

options.text: How pirate ports used sandy harbors to survive earthquakes
How waterlogged soil can temporarily act like a liquid during strong shaking
Why pocketwatches are useful for dating historic disasters
Why dry ground is more dangerous than wet ground in an earthquake

evidence.quote: Liquefaction happens when waterlogged, loose soil (we call it "sand") turns into quicksand - temporarily.

private.explanationZh: 讲座从牙买加城市下沉的案例引入，随后一直在解释“液化”是什么、为什么发生，以及会造成什么后果。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，海盗港口只是开场案例，不是解释重点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，准确概括了液化的定义和全文主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，怀表只是补充历史细节，不是主题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，原文强调的是饱和松散土在震动下液化，不是干土更危险。

public.prompt: Why can saturated sand usually support weight when it is not being shaken?

options.text: The grains press against one another and extra water can flow away
The water between the grains becomes solid under pressure
Dry layers underneath the sand absorb most of the force
Earthquake vibrations keep the grains evenly packed

evidence.quote: it's fairly stable because the particles get pushed together and rest on each other, while any excess water escapes by flowing away to where the pressure is lower.

private.explanationZh: 在正常情况下，沙粒彼此支撑，多余水分还能流走，因此饱和沙地通常仍表现得像固体。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确，把原文中的两个原因都概括到了。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，水并不会因受压就变成固体。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，文中没有提到下方干层吸收力量。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，恰恰是地震振动会破坏这种稳定，而不是维持它。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention wiggling your toes in beach sand?

options.text: To argue that beach sand is more unstable than city soil
To show that loose dry soils can also behave like liquids
To explain why quicksand forms only near oceans
To give an everyday example of motion causing saturated sand to liquefy

evidence.quote: You can experience liquefaction for yourself in the water-soaked sand near the edge of a beach - it feels pretty solid if you stand still, but if you wiggle your toes, the movement causes the sand to liquefy beneath you, burying your feet.

private.explanationZh: 这个脚趾的例子把大尺度的地震液化缩小到日常体验，帮助听者直观理解“扰动一来，原本稳定的湿沙就会像液体一样”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，例子不是比较海滩和城市谁更危险。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，例子强调的是含水沙子，不是干土。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，液化并不限于海边，地震中的饱和土也会发生。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确，这正是举例的功能。

public.prompt: If a heavy object sinks during earthquake-induced liquefaction, what will most likely happen after the shaking stops?

options.text: It will keep sinking until all the water escapes
It will float back up as the soil separates from the water
It will remain trapped when the ground solidifies again
It will dissolve because the grains lose all friction

evidence.quote: when the shaking stops and the soil particles are no longer suspended in water, the ground solidifies again and anything that sank becomes stuck.

private.explanationZh: 最后一句直接给出结果：震动停止后，颗粒重新沉降，地面恢复固态，已经下陷的东西会被卡住。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，原文说停止震动后会重新固结，不是继续下沉。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，文中没有任何会上浮的描述。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确，和结尾句完全一致。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，物体不是“溶解”，而是被重新固化的土困住。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-028-q01: 全文主要解释液化现象，即含水松散土壤在强烈震动下会暂时像液体一样，因此B最符合主旨。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-028-q02: 原文说未受震动时，砂粒彼此挤压支撑，额外的水还能流走，所以饱和沙地通常还能承重，A正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-028-q03: 提到在海边扭动脚趾，是为了给出一个日常可体验的例子，说明运动会让含水沙子液化，D最准确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-028-q04: 原文最后说震动停止后，土粒不再悬浮，地面重新固化，沉下去的东西会被卡住，因此应选C。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly about?

options.text: Why old theories about bird hibernation remained popular for so long
Why the Arctic tern is no longer considered a marathon migrator
How different tracking methods have improved scientists' understanding of bird migration
How satellites completely replaced all earlier migration research

evidence.quote: The first real clues about where migratory birds actually go during the winter -- hint: it's not the moon or the bottom of a frozen lake -- came around 1900, thanks to a Danish teacher's technique of attaching marked aluminum rings to birds' legs and then re-releasing them.
But recent data from light-level geolocators show that terns actually travel more than twice as far each year, possibly to take advantage of prevailing winds.

private.explanationZh: 讲座按时间顺序介绍了环志、卫星追踪、GPS和光照定位器等方法，并说明这些方法怎样一步步改进了人类对迁徙路线的认识。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，早期错误理论只是引子，不是全文重点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，北极燕鸥只是结尾的一个例子。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确，最能概括全文的组织方式和信息重点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，旧方法并没有被说成毫无价值，只是信息精度不同。

public.prompt: What limitation of bird banding does the speaker emphasize?

options.text: It works only for birds that migrate over land
It cannot show where birds spend the winter
It provides too much detail for long migrations
It reveals only isolated points rather than the full route between them

evidence.quote: But banding can only tell researchers about single points along a bird's migratory path -- not what happens BETWEEN those points.

private.explanationZh: 环志能告诉研究者某只鸟在某个时间点出现在哪里，但无法连续显示两点之间的迁徙过程。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，文中没说环志只适用于陆地迁徙。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，找到越冬地正是环志曾经做到的事之一。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，问题不是信息太多，而是路线信息不连续。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确，准确复述了原文限制。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker bring up the bald eagle in Maryland?

options.text: To introduce satellite tracking as a way to see migrations in greater detail
To show that eagles migrate farther than smaller birds
To explain why American researchers rejected bird banding
To prove that only predators can carry transmitters

evidence.quote: More recently, researchers finally started to get a better view -- a bird's-eye view, in fact -- of these annual migrations, when a bald eagle in Maryland was captured and fitted with a transmitter powerful enough to send signals to a pair of orbiting satellites.

private.explanationZh: 提到那只白头海雕，是为了把话题从环志推进到卫星追踪，说明研究者开始获得更连续、更详细的迁徙信息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确，这只海雕是新方法登场的过渡例子。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，重点不是比较哪种鸟飞得更远。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，文中没有说研究者因此否定环志。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，例子并未证明只有猛禽才能带发射器。

public.prompt: What do recent light-level geolocator data suggest about Arctic terns?

options.text: Their routes are shorter because prevailing winds carry them farther
Their yearly travel distance is much greater than earlier estimates suggested
They no longer make a round-trip between the Arctic and Antarctic
They are the only birds whose migration can be measured accurately

evidence.quote: But recent data from light-level geolocators show that terns actually travel more than twice as far each year, possibly to take advantage of prevailing winds.

private.explanationZh: 以前人们估计北极燕鸥往返约四万公里，而新数据表明它们每年实际飞行距离超过原先估计的两倍。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，原文说“更远”，不是“更短”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，这是对新旧估计差异的合理概括。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，文中没有说它们停止往返两极。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，结尾并未宣称只有这种鸟能被准确测量。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-029-q01: 这段讲述人们如何从早期猜测，到环志、卫星追踪，再到地理定位器，逐步更好理解鸟类迁徙，因此C概括最全面。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-029-q02: 说话者明确指出环志只能在鸟被再次发现时提供地图上的单个点，无法显示这些点之间完整路线，所以D正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-029-q03: 提到马里兰的白头海雕，是为了引出卫星追踪技术开始让研究者更详细地看到年度迁徙过程，因此A正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-029-q04: 文中说新近的光照地理定位数据表明燕鸥每年飞行距离其实是先前估计的两倍多，所以B是合理推断。

public.prompt: What central idea does the talk emphasize?

options.text: Modern hygiene has no effect on children's immune systems
Dogs mainly protect families by providing assistance and security
Farm children avoid allergies because they encounter fewer animals indoors
Exposure to pets may help a child's immune system develop in ways that reduce later allergies

evidence.quote: if your mother lives with a dog or cat while she's pregnant with you, you're about 30% less likely to suffer from allergies as a child.

private.explanationZh: 开头提出核心论点：孕期与猫狗共同生活会改变家庭微生物环境，而孩子日后过敏风险会下降，因此主题是宠物暴露对免疫发育的潜在益处。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，文中说现代卫生改善减少了传染病，但也可能带来另一个问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，陪伴和保护只是引入，不是主要学术内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，卫生假说强调的是更多而不是更少的微生物暴露。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确，最准确概括了整段讨论。

public.prompt: According to the hygiene hypothesis described in the talk, why do Amish farm children tend to have fewer allergies and asthma?

options.text: Their immune systems rarely encounter foreign substances
They inherit stronger "you" proteins from their parents
Early exposure to varied dirt, bacteria, and germs helps their immune systems develop more fully
Living on farms prevents harmful cells from entering the body

evidence.quote: scientists think it's because their immune systems develop more fully thanks to exposure to a wide variety of dirt, bacteria, and germs in fermenting feed, cow manure, and other barnyard delights.

private.explanationZh: 卫生假说在这里的意思是，童年早期接触更多样的微生物，会让免疫系统更充分地学会区分什么该攻击、什么不用过度反应。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，恰恰相反，他们接触到的外来微生物更多。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，文中没有把原因归结为遗传到更强的“自我”蛋白。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确，直接对应原文解释。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，住在农场并不能阻止外来物进入身体，重点是免疫学习。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare immune-system learning to learning foreign languages?

options.text: To suggest that both are easier to develop when exposure happens very early
To show that immune cells memorize words in the same way people do
To argue that adults can reverse allergies by studying new languages
To explain that allergies result from confusing sounds with germs

evidence.quote: our immune systems are best at learning to distinguish harmless foreign substances from harmful ones when exposed to them very early in life.

private.explanationZh: 类比外语学习，是为了强调“关键期”概念：越早接触，系统越容易建立正确区分，不容易把无害物质误判成威胁。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确，抓住了这个类比真正要强调的时间窗口。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，类比不是说免疫细胞会像人一样记单词。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，原文没有提出成年人靠学语言逆转过敏。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，这个说法把类比理解得过于字面。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the speaker's conclusion about pet exposure?

options.text: It can replace the benefits of clean water and sewage treatment
It may influence immune development both before and after birth
It protects children only if they continue living with the same pet
It prevents harmless substances from ever entering the body

evidence.quote: We do know that having a pet around before and after your birth may help keep your immune cells from barking up the wrong tree.

private.explanationZh: 结尾把时间点说得很明确：宠物相关暴露的作用不仅可能发生在出生后，也可能从胎儿期就开始影响免疫系统。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，作者没有否定现代公共卫生措施的重要性。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，这是对“before and after your birth”的直接推断。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，文中没有限定必须是同一只宠物持续存在。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，免疫系统不是阻止一切进入，而是学会不过度反应。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-030-q01: 全文中心是宠物带来的微生物暴露可能帮助儿童免疫系统更好发育，从而降低过敏风险，所以D正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-030-q02: 按照卫生假说，阿米什农场儿童早期接触多样的泥土、细菌和病菌，因此免疫系统发育更充分，C与原文一致。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-030-q03: 把免疫系统学习和学外语相比，是为了说明两者都在生命早期更容易形成有效区分与学习，所以A正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-030-q04: 结尾明确说在子宫中以及出生后有宠物在身边都可能有帮助，因此可推断宠物暴露可能在出生前后都影响免疫发育，选B。

public.prompt: What problem and solution does the talk mainly discuss?

options.text: Why tropical trees spread into cold regions before evolving leaves
How trees evolved ways to avoid freeze damage in their leaves and plumbing
Why maple syrup became an important food in northern climates
How drought alone explains all modern tree diversity

evidence.quote: If you took a tropical tree (whether ancient or modern) and transported it to Siberia or Patagonia in winter, its water would freeze into sharp-edged ice crystals, fatally puncturing the living cells in all of its leaves.
So to survive in cold weather, trees need to avoid two things: bubbles in their pipes and direct damage to living cells due to ice crystals.

private.explanationZh: 前面先指出热带树木在严寒中会因冰晶和管道问题受损，随后总结出树木要解决的两个关键难题，所以主旨是树木如何演化出抗冻机制。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，文中重点不是扩散先后顺序，而是生理适应。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，既概括了问题，也概括了解决方向。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，枫糖浆只是结尾的延伸例子。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，干旱适应只是其中一部分历史背景，不足以概括全文。

public.prompt: Why are air bubbles dangerous in a tree's plumbing system?

options.text: They make leaves absorb too much sugary sap
They freeze faster than the water around them
They interrupt the chain of water molecules needed to pull water upward
They force roots to stop taking in minerals

evidence.quote: Air bubbles break the chain of molecules, essentially shutting off the flow of water.

private.explanationZh: 树木输水依赖水分子之间连续的拉力链，气泡一旦把这条链打断，水就难以上升到树体上部。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，问题在于输水中断，不是叶片吸糖过多。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，文中没有把危险归因于气泡结冰更快。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确，直接对应原文机制。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，原文讨论的是导水链断裂，不是根停止吸矿物质。

public.prompt: How does the talk connect drought adaptation to later survival in cold climates?

options.text: It says drought adaptation made leaf-dropping unnecessary in cold places
It argues wet-tropical trees already had the best pipes for freezing weather
It claims trees moved into cold climates before they evolved narrow pipes
It explains that skinnier pipes first helped with drought and later also helped against freezing-related bubbles

evidence.quote: So the plumbing of ancestral drought-adapted trees was accidentally pre-adapted to the cold as well, well before they began to spread beyond the tropics.

private.explanationZh: 这句话把两段历史连起来：细管道最初是为应对干旱而演化出来的，但结果恰好也降低了寒冷环境中的气泡堵塞风险。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误，落叶是后面谈到的另一种策略，不是这里的结论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，原文说高容量粗管更容易出问题，不是最适合寒冷。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，作者明确说这种预适应发生在走出热带之前。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确，准确概括了“先抗旱、后顺带抗寒”的组织逻辑。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about sugary sap from the end of the talk?

options.text: It is presented as a uniquely cold-useful adaptation, helping explain why maple syrup is associated with northern trees
It evolved mainly so tropical trees could survive monsoon droughts
It has largely been replaced by leaf loss in all cold-climate species
It allows any tropical tree to survive winter without other changes

evidence.quote: As far as we know, sugary sap remains the only adaptation that's only useful in cold places, which is why you have to come to the north if you want to tap into the sweet, sweet, anti-freeze that might just help you survive the winter - we call it maple syrup.

private.explanationZh: 结尾把“含糖树液”定义为真正只在寒冷环境中特别有用的适应，因此也顺势解释了为什么枫糖浆会和北方树种联系在一起。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确，既抓住“只在寒冷环境有用”，也抓住北方枫糖的结论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误，回到热带应对季节性干旱的是落叶策略，不是这里强调的糖液。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误，文中没有说所有寒带树种都用落叶取代糖液。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误，热带树若无其他适应仍会在严寒中受损。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-031-q01: 全文主要讨论树木如何进化出机制，避免叶片冻伤和输水系统受冻起泡，因此B最符合主旨。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-031-q02: 原文解释说输水依赖水分子连续上拉，气泡会打断这条链，从而阻断水流，所以C正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-031-q03: 文章先说细管有助于抗旱，接着说明这种祖先时期的抗旱适应也恰好能减少寒冷环境中的气泡堵塞，所以D准确表达这种连接方式。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-031-q04: 结尾明确说高糖树液似乎仍是只在寒冷地区有用的适应，这也解释了为什么枫糖浆与北方树木联系在一起，因此A正确。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the talk?

options.text: How small disturbances and flowing water cause rivers on plains to develop bends over time
Why mountain streams always carry more sediment than rivers on plains
