How human engineers straighten rivers to prevent flooding
Why rivers stop flowing when they reach soft soil

evidence.quote: Out on the open plains, those stony walls give way to soft soil, allowing rivers much more freedom to shift their banks and set their own ever-changing courses to the sea: courses that almost never run straight.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查全文主旨。开头明确对比了山地河流与平原河流，并指出平原上的河流因为河岸是软土，所以更容易移动河岸、改变流路，几乎不会长期保持笔直，后文就是围绕这种弯曲如何形成与发展展开说明。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了全文核心：平原河流在微小扰动和长期水流作用下逐渐形成曲流。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中确实提到山地溪流与平原河流不同，但重点不是比较谁携带更多泥沙。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。全文讨论的是自然过程，没有讲人类工程如何把河流拉直。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。软土让河流更容易改道和弯曲，不是让河流停止流动。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what happens along the inside bank of a curve when the water there slows down?

options.text: The channel becomes deeper because the current speeds up
Sediment is deposited there and new land gradually builds up
The bank hardens into stone and resists further change
The river cuts a new valley through the mountains

evidence.quote: And since slow-moving water can't carry the sand-sized particles that fast-moving water can, that dirt drops to the bottom and builds up to make the water there shallower and slower, and then keeps accumulating until the edge of the stream becomes new land on the inside bank.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。文中直接说明内侧河岸附近水速变慢后，原本搬运不了的颗粒会沉积下来，逐渐堆高，最后使河流内侧边缘变成新的陆地。

optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文明确说泥沙下沉并不断堆积，最终在内侧河岸形成新陆地。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中说这里的水会变得更浅、更慢，不是更深、更快。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。没有提到河岸变成石头；相反，河道会持续变化。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里讲的是平原曲流内部的沉积，不是切穿山地形成新山谷。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the speaker's explanation of stream width?

options.text: Narrow streams rarely develop meanders at all
Stream width affects only water depth, not the pattern of bends
Wider streams tend to have bends that are spaced farther apart downstream
Wider streams immediately form oxbow lakes

evidence.quote: The wider the stream, the longer it takes the slingshotting current to reach the other side, and the greater the downstream distance to the next curve.

private.explanationZh: 这道题要求根据原文做一步推断。既然河流越宽，水流冲到对岸所需时间越长，而到下一个弯道的下游距离也越大，那么可以推出宽河流的弯道之间通常相隔更远。

optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它是对原文“越宽、下一个弯越远”的直接合理推断。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说窄河流很少曲流，反而说小溪也会像大河一样蜿蜒。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话者明确把宽度与下一个弯的位置联系起来，不只是深度问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。牛轭湖形成还需要弯曲不断扩大并最终切断，不会因为河流宽就立刻出现。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what is likely to happen after a meander loop grows until it runs into itself?

options.text: The river will divide permanently into two equal channels
The river will stop eroding either bank
The river will reverse direction and flow uphill
The river will follow a straighter path and leave an oxbow lake behind

evidence.quote: When that happens, the river follows the straighter path downhill, leaving behind a crescent-shaped remnant called an oxbow lake.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道结尾信息上的预测题。原文已经说明，当河曲不断扩大到“撞上自己”时，河水会改走更直的下坡路线，而原先弯曲的一段被遗留下来，形成牛轭湖。

optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它完整复述了原文描述的后续结果。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说会长期分成两条等宽水道，而是说会改走更直的路线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有说侵蚀会停止，只是说河道切换了路径。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。河流会沿更直的下坡路线流动，不可能倒流上坡。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-047-q01: 全文先对比山地急流和开阔平原河流，再说明微小扰动如何通过侵蚀、沉积和持续偏转把原本较直的河道变成弯曲河道，因此主旨是平原河流为何会逐渐形成弯道。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-047-q02: 原文明确说内侧水流变慢后，较粗颗粒无法继续搬运，会沉到河底并不断堆积，最后让内侧边缘形成新的陆地，所以答案是沉积并逐渐形成新土地。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-047-q03: 讲者说明河道越宽，被甩向对岸的水流到达另一侧所需时间越长，因此下一个弯道会出现在更靠下游的位置，所以可推知较宽的河流弯道间距通常更大。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-047-q04: 结尾直接说明当弯曲不断加剧直到河道绕回并撞上自己时，河流会改走更直的下坡路径，并留下一个新月形残留湖，也就是牛轭湖。

public.prompt: What central point does the talk make about microbes in food?

options.text: Controlled microbial action can both protect food and create flavors people value
Most microbes in food are dangerous and should always be eliminated
People enjoy fermented food mainly because refrigeration is impossible
Microbes matter only in dairy products, not in other foods

evidence.quote: Not only to keep our food safe to eat, but also because the microbes we culture can transform it, almost magically, into awesome deliciousness.
without the help of friendly fermenting microbes, we humans would be terribly uncultured.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查全文主旨，需要整合中后段信息。讲话者一方面强调友好微生物能帮助食物更安全，另一方面强调它们还能创造人类喜爱的味道，甚至深刻影响了饮食文化，因此正确答案必须同时包含“安全”和“风味/文化”两个层面。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了全文的双重核心：微生物既能抑制危险腐败，也能带来人们珍视的发酵风味。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。开头明确说绝大多数微生物其实无害，且后文重点讲的是友好微生物的积极作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有把发酵的意义归结为“因为没有冰箱”，还强调了味道与文化价值。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文举了面包、啤酒、巧克力、泡菜等多种食物，不仅仅是乳制品。

public.prompt: Why do humans generally find rotten food disgusting, according to the talk?

options.text: Because all microbial activity immediately makes food toxic
Because some microbes in spoiled food can be dangerous, so avoiding them is protective
Because rotten food usually contains too much salt for humans to eat
Because people are taught by modern chefs to reject strong smells

evidence.quote: But the other 1% are nasty enough that our ancestors - and the ancestors of various other mammals and birds - evolved a natural repulsion to stuff that might harbor nasty germs.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。原文指出，虽然大多数微生物无害，但仍有一小部分足够危险，因此人类及其他动物演化出对可能带有病菌的腐烂食物的本能厌恶。

optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它对应原文关于危险病菌和演化性厌恶反应的直接解释。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文明确说大约99%的微生物其实无害，不是所有微生物活动都会立刻让食物有毒。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。盐是在后文被用来帮助友好微生物竞争，不是人厌恶腐烂食物的原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话者强调的是进化而来的自然反应，不是现代厨师教育出来的偏好。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention adding salt and ending up with salami instead of dangerous contamination?

options.text: To show that salt removes all microbes from meat
To argue that preserved meat tastes better than bread
To illustrate how people can help harmless microbes outcompete harmful ones
To prove that refrigeration is unnecessary for every kind of food

evidence.quote: But with some micromanagement - adding lots of salt, for instance - we can help harmless, salt-tolerant microbes like Lactobacillus outcompete their dangerous but salt-sensitive relatives.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查例子的修辞功能。说话者举盐和腊肠的例子，不是为了讨论口味偏好，而是为了说明人类可以通过控制环境，让有益微生物抢先占据食物，从而压制危险微生物。

optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个例子正是为了说明“人为干预微生物竞争”这一机制。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文说的是帮助某些微生物胜出，不是把所有微生物都清除掉。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里没有比较腊肠和面包哪种更好吃，重点在安全性与控制腐败。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有得出“所有食物都不需要冷藏”这种宽泛结论。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about people's preferences for fermented foods?

options.text: People everywhere naturally prefer the exact same fermented flavors
Only flavors encountered in adulthood can become desirable
Fermented foods remain popular only when people forget they contain microbes
Repeated exposure can make different microbial flavors appealing in different cultures

evidence.quote: As a result, people around the world have some very different ideas about how to microbe-ify foods - but every culinary culture involves fermentation in one way or another.

private.explanationZh: 这道题要求根据结尾推断。讲话者先说人们接触某些微生物风味越多就越容易喜欢，接着说各地对“如何让食物微生物化”有不同做法，因此可以推断，不同文化会因为长期接触而发展出各自偏爱的发酵风味。

optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它整合了“接触会增强喜爱”与“不同文化有不同做法”两层意思。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文恰恰强调各地想法非常不同，不是完全一样。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话者提到这种接触甚至从羊水中的风味开始，不限于成年以后。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文并未说人们需要“忘记微生物存在”才会喜欢这些食物。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-048-q01: 全文核心观点是：友好微生物一方面能抢先占据食物、压制危险病原体，另一方面还能把食物转化出人们喜爱的风味与质地，所以受控的微生物作用既能保食物安全，也能创造美味。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-048-q02: 原文说人类普遍厌恶腐败食物，是因为其中可能藏有危险病菌，这种反感是进化出的保护机制，并不是因为所有微生物活动都会立刻让食物有毒。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-048-q03: 提到加盐后得到萨拉米而不是危险污染，是为了具体说明人类如何通过控制条件，帮助耐盐的无害微生物压过不耐盐的有害微生物，从而实现受控发酵。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-048-q04: 后文说某些强烈发酵味会随着反复接触而“grow on us”，而且不同文化形成了不同的发酵传统，因此可推知人们会因持续暴露而喜欢上不同的微生物风味。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the talk?

options.text: To encourage viewers to support MinuteEarth on Patreon so the team can keep making videos
To explain the science behind planetary animation software
To announce that MinuteEarth will stop making videos soon
To compare Patreon with several competing video platforms

evidence.quote: We want to keep on making MinuteEarth for you, so we've started a Patreon campaign where you can pledge to support us for each new video we release, which will help us make even more videos and provide you with some cool perks and behind-the-scenes access!

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查主旨。讲话者明确说明团队启动 Patreon 的原因是继续制作 MinuteEarth，并通过观众按视频支持来帮助团队制作更多内容，因此全文核心目的就是发出支持请求。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了讲话者发言的直接目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话里提到动画耗时耗钱，但不是在讲动画软件或科学原理。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话者想继续做视频，而不是宣布停更。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有对比多个平台的功能优劣，重点只是介绍 Patreon 支持方式。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what does Patreon allow supporters to do?

options.text: Vote on which team members should stay on the staff
Choose an amount to contribute per video and set a monthly limit
Download every animation file used in production
Pay once for lifetime access to all future videos

evidence.quote: Patreon lets you decide how much you want to contribute per video, and you can put a monthly limit on your support.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道直接细节题。原文清楚说明 Patreon 的两个机制：支持者可以决定每支视频愿意给多少，并且还能设定每月上限。

optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它完整对应原文列出的两项具体功能。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有提到观众可以决定团队成员去留。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话者提到幕后内容和福利，但没有说会开放全部动画文件下载。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里是按视频支持并可设月上限，不是一次性终身付费。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention Subbable contributions and the cost of animation?

options.text: To complain that the team plans to stop animating future videos
To prove that animation is cheaper than most viewers expect
To show that viewer funding already supports production and explains why more support matters
To argue that only paid viewers should keep watching the channel

evidence.quote: It's only because you watch and share our videos that the channel has been successful, and the monthly contributions that many of you make through Subbable - thank you! - have literally enabled us to pay for production and add two new members to our team - you wouldn't believe how much time (& money) animation takes.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查修辞目的。讲话者提到 Subbable 和动画成本，不是单纯抱怨，而是用已经发生的事实证明观众资助确实能转化为制作经费和团队扩张，从而为新的 Patreon 请求提供理由。

optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个细节的作用就是证明观众支持真实有效，因此继续支持很重要。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲话者没有说要停止做动画，反而是想继续做更多视频。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文说动画很耗时也花钱，不是在说它比大家想象中便宜。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话者没有把观看权限制给付费者，还明确说没钱就留着。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the speaker's comment to viewers whose money is tight?

options.text: The team wants all viewers to give at least a small amount each month
The team will stop releasing videos unless every viewer contributes
Only viewers with financial problems are allowed to keep watching for free
Financial support is optional, and viewers should not feel pressured to give if they cannot afford it

evidence.quote: If money is tight for you - please keep it! But if you have some to spare, please consider supporting us at Patreon.com/MinuteEarth.

private.explanationZh: 这道题要求根据讲话者的态度做推断。既然他说如果手头紧就把钱留着，而有余力的人再考虑支持，就说明资助并非强制，团队并不希望无法负担的人感到压力。

optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确反映了讲话者对经济紧张观众的宽松态度。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲话者明确说手头紧就不要给，不是要求人人都给一点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有把继续更新与“所有人都必须出钱”挂钩。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话者没有设置“只有经济困难者可免费看”的条件，这完全曲解原意。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-049-q01: 整段讲话的主要目的很明确，就是感谢观众并呼吁大家通过Patreon按每个视频支持MinuteEarth，这样团队才能继续并扩大视频制作。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-049-q02: 原文直接说明Patreon让支持者决定每个新视频捐多少，并且可以设置每月上限，因此只有这个选项与讲话内容完全一致。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-049-q03: 提到Subbable捐助和动画成本，是为了说明观众资助过去已经真实支撑了制作和扩编团队，也解释了为什么进一步的支持会直接帮助他们继续做出更多视频。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-049-q04: 讲者明确说如果手头紧，“please keep it!”，这说明资助是自愿的，负担不起的观众不应被施压，因此可推知经济支持并非观看内容的必要条件。

public.prompt: What central claim does the talk make about tectonic plate motion?

options.text: Plates are not moved only by mantle currents; sinking ocean crust can also pull plates and help drive convection
Continents drift only because winds and tides slowly push them across the globe
All tectonic plates move at exactly the same speed as the mantle beneath them
Plate motion stopped once scientists began measuring it from space

evidence.quote: But the plates aren't just passively riding these conveyer-belt-like currents around like a bunch of suitcases at the baggage claim.
So, in some ways, seafloor crust really is more like part of the conveyor belt than something riding on top of it. The continents, on the other hand, are baggage.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查全文核心论点。讲话者先否定“板块只是被地幔洋流被动带着走”的简化说法，结尾又进一步指出海底板块本身就像传送带系统的一部分，说明板块运动并非单一由下方洋流拖动，而与下沉海洋板块的拉力密切相关。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它整合了全文最重要的修正观点：不仅有地幔流，还有海洋板块自身的下沉拉动与对对流的参与。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中完全没有把风和潮汐作为大陆漂移的原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话者专门举例说明有些板块比下方地幔流得更快。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。科学家只是后来能从太空精确测量这种运动，不代表运动因此停止。

public.prompt: What example does the speaker use to show that plates can move faster than the mantle beneath them?

options.text: The African plate moves westward faster than any mountain stream on Earth
The Nazca plate moves eastward at about 10 centimeters per year while the mantle beneath it moves at about five
The Pacific plate stops moving whenever mantle flow slows down
The South American continent moves only when earthquakes occur

evidence.quote: For example, the Nazca plate - a chunk of ocean crust off the west coast of South America - is cruising eastward at about 10cm per year, while the mantle underneath it oozes along at just five.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。讲话者用纳斯卡板块作反例，说明如果板块只是被下方流动带着走，就不该比“传送带”本身更快，但这个板块确实快于其下方地幔。

optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确复述了讲话者给出的数字性例子。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有把非洲板块与山地溪流相比，也没有这个结论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有说太平洋板块会在地幔变慢时停止运动。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话者讨论的是持续的板块运动，不是仅在地震时大陆才移动。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare a sinking plate to a chain sliding off a table?

options.text: To show that tectonic plates are made mostly of metal links
To argue that plate motion is caused by objects falling onto the seafloor
To explain how the sinking edge of a plate can pull the rest of the plate along
To suggest that continents move only when they are disconnected from ocean crust

evidence.quote: As the edge of the seafloor sinks into the mantle, it pulls on the plate behind it, the same way a chain dangling further and further off a table will eventually start to slide.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查类比的作用。链条比喻把抽象的“俯冲板块拉动后方板块”变成容易想象的机械过程，帮助听者理解为什么下沉的前缘会带动整块板块继续前进。

optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个比喻的核心功能就是解释“下沉边缘如何产生拉力”。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。比喻不是在说明板块材质，板块当然不是金属链条。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文讨论的是俯冲导致的拉动，不是海底掉落物体造成运动。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。类比没有谈大陆必须先与海洋地壳脱离才能移动。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the effect of sunken ocean slabs on the mantle?

options.text: They stop mantle rock from moving at all once subduction begins
They heat the mantle so strongly that all nearby plates melt
They prevent any future ocean trenches from forming
They redirect mantle flow downward and contribute to convection beneath the plates

evidence.quote: Sunken slabs of ocean crust block flowing rock from moving further sideways, forcing it to turn downward and sink.

private.explanationZh: 这道题要求根据结尾解释做推断。既然下沉板块会阻挡横向流动并迫使地幔物质向下转向，那么可以推断它们不仅被对流带动，也在重新组织甚至加强这种对流过程。

optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它是对原文“阻挡侧向流动并迫使下沉”的合理概括。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文说的是改变流向，不是让地幔完全停止流动。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说周围板块因此熔化，这属于无根据夸大。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文提到海沟正是这种过程的标志之一，不是说以后无法形成。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-050-q01: 讲话先指出“板块只是被地幔对流带着走”的常见说法并不完整，随后重点说明下沉的海洋板块会拉动后方板块，并且还会帮助驱动地幔对流，所以A最准确概括主旨。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-050-q02: 原文举的具体例子就是纳斯卡板块每年向东约10厘米，而其下方地幔只以约5厘米速度流动，用来证明板块能比下面的地幔移动得更快。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-050-q03: 把下沉板块比作桌边越垂越长、最终把整条链子拖走的链条，是为了形象说明已经沉入地幔的板块前缘会拉着后面的整块板块一起移动。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-050-q04: 结尾解释说下沉的海底板块会阻挡原本横向流动的岩石，迫使其向下转向并下沉，还会产生牵引作用，因此可以推知它们会把地幔流动重新导向下方并促进对流。

public.prompt: What main explanation does the talk give for extremely high plant diversity in some ecosystems?

options.text: Nutrient-poor soils and long, stable periods can prevent domination by fast growers and allow many survival strategies to evolve
The wettest ecosystems always have the most species because water alone determines diversity
Frequent glaciation increases species diversity by constantly renewing rich soils
Plant diversity is highest where large species can grow the fastest

evidence.quote: In fact, both ecosystems owe their enormous diversity, in part, to the fact that their soils have critically low supplies of nitrogen and especially phosphorus, nutrients plants need in order to grow.

However, our high-diversity rainforests and shrublands have spent millions of years beyond the reach of the ice sheets, leaving their residents plenty of undisturbed time to evolve a wide variety of ingenious strategies for surviving nutrient poverty

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查全文主旨，需要整合前后信息。讲话者先提出“贫瘠土壤”这一条件，解释它如何抑制生长最快的优势种；后面又补充“长期稳定、未受冰川清除的时间”这一条件，说明许多物种因此有时间演化出不同生存策略，两者共同解释超高多样性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时包含了全文反复强调的两个关键条件：资源贫乏与长期稳定。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中明确说潮湿并不是唯一决定因素，干燥的灌丛地也可能高度多样。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话者指出冰川往往重置生态系统并形成利于生长而不利于多样性的富矿新土。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文恰恰说快速生长者在肥沃土壤中会排挤其他物种，反而降低多样性。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why do nutrient-rich soils often support fewer plant species?

options.text: Rich soils contain too little phosphorus for any plants to spread quickly
Fast-growing species capture most of the nutrients, water, and sunlight, crowding out slower species
Plants in rich soils avoid using sunlight efficiently
Only large trees can survive in rich soils, so no small plants remain

evidence.quote: The fastest-spreading species soak up most of the extra nutrients, which lets them keep growing super fast, which lets their roots suck up so much water, and their leaves snatch up so much sunlight, that other, slower species actually get LESS of those resources than otherwise.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。原文明确说明，肥沃土壤中的快速生长者会优先吸收额外养分，并进一步抢走水和阳光，结果其他生长较慢的物种反而获得更少资源，最终被挤出。

optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它完整概括了原文描述的资源垄断链条。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里讨论的是养分太多导致优势种垄断，不是磷太少。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文说优势种会“抢走”很多阳光，不是植物不愿高效利用阳光。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有说只有大树能活，而是说快速扩张者压制了其他物种。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare plant communities with businesses in poor and rich countries?

options.text: To argue that economic policy directly determines plant evolution
To prove that poor countries always protect nature better than rich countries
To illustrate by analogy that scarce resources can prevent domination by a few competitors and allow more coexistence
To suggest that plants compete only in places where humans run businesses

evidence.quote: We see this pattern in human society also - there's a far greater number of businesses - mostly small - in poor countries, while fewer bigger companies dominate in rich countries.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查类比的修辞作用。讲话者并不是转去讨论经济学本身，而是借商业世界说明同一个模式：资源丰厚时少数强者容易做大并支配系统；资源匮乏时，反而可能留下更多较小参与者共存。

optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个类比正是为了让听者更直观理解“资源多少与竞争结构”的关系。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲话者没有说经济政策会直接决定植物演化，这超出了类比范围。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有做这种价值判断，只是在说明竞争格局的相似性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。植物竞争是自然现象，类比只是帮助说明，不是说必须有人类商业活动才会竞争。

public.prompt: Besides poor soil, what other major prerequisite for hyperdiversity does the speaker identify?

options.text: Constant volcanic eruptions
Annual flooding by nutrient-rich rivers
Regular clearing by glaciers
Long periods of undisturbed time

evidence.quote: The other major prerequisite for hyperdiversity is time.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道结尾细节题。讲话者在后段明确指出，除了贫瘠土壤之外，超高多样性的另一个关键前提就是时间，也就是长期未被打断的演化积累。

optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原文直接点明“另一个主要前提是时间”。
optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有把火山活动列为普遍前提。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。富营养洪水不符合讲话者的核心论证，反而可能增强优势种。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话者说冰川常常会推平生态系统，因此不是促进超多样性的前提。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-051-q01: 全文解释，高多样性并非来自资源丰富，而是来自贫瘠土壤抑制快速占优物种，再加上长期稳定、未受冰川扰动的时间，让许多不同生存策略得以演化并共存，因此A最完整。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-051-q02: 原文明确说肥沃土壤里，生长最快的植物会吸收大部分养分，并进一步夺取更多水和阳光，结果较慢的物种获得更少资源，最终被排挤出去。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-051-q03: 企业类比是为了帮助听者理解同一逻辑：当资源稀缺时，少数竞争者不容易无限扩张并彻底垄断，于是更多较小参与者能够共存，这对应植物群落中的更高多样性。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-051-q04: 讲者直接说“另一个主要前提是时间”，并解释这些高多样性生态系统长期未被冰川推平，拥有数百万年的不受干扰时间，因此答案是长期稳定、未受扰动的时间。

public.prompt: Which point does the speaker emphasize about people's views of extreme weather?

options.text: They mostly reflect improved public understanding of climate statistics
They are shaped by recent experience, prior beliefs, and media framing
They become accurate only after a severe disaster affects a region
They depend more on political leaders than on weather itself

evidence.quote: Your answer probably depends on the weirdness of the weather you've been experiencing lately.
much of what we hear about the frequency of extreme weather can end up reinforcing whatever we already believe

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题考查对全文核心论点的概括。开头说明人们对天气是否变得更怪的判断，往往受自己最近经历影响；结尾进一步总结，关于极端天气频率的信息常常只是强化人们原有的看法。中间关于暖冬、政治分歧和媒体措辞的内容，都是在支持这一总论点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中确实提到气象站数据，但重点不是公众越来越会读统计，而是主观判断如何被过滤。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个选项同时涵盖了近因经验、既有立场和媒体表达三条主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有说灾害之后判断就一定更准确，只说人们会更容易报告长期变化。
optionRationalesZh.d: 政治身份只是影响因素之一，不能代替全文关于经验和媒体共同作用的主旨。

public.prompt: At the beginning of the talk, what do the flooding and drought examples mainly show?

options.text: Recent dramatic weather can strengthen people's sense that a long-term trend exists
Flooding is easier to remember than drought because it causes visible damage
Residents usually notice climate change before scientists measure it
British and American weather records are often contradictory

evidence.quote: the Brits who reported experiencing flooding were much more likely than their drier compatriots to say they'd noticed wetter and wetter weather over the course of their lives.

And before a withering drought struck the American Midwest in 2012, only 41% of residents thought they detected a long-term uptick in the frequency and intensity of dry spells - but after that one dessicating summer, 66% said they'd been seeing worsening droughts for decades.

private.explanationZh: 这道细节题要求理解两个并列例子的共同作用。无论是英国洪水还是美国干旱，最近发生的强烈天气事件都会让人更容易把眼前体验上升为“早就持续很多年”的长期趋势判断。作者用两种不同天气说明这种心理倾向具有普遍性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两个例子都表明近期极端经历会放大人们对长期趋势的主观判断。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有比较洪水和干旱哪一种更容易被记住。
optionRationalesZh.c: 作者没有说居民比科学测量更早发现变化，后文反而强调客观数据和主观感受可能不一致。
optionRationalesZh.d: 例子讨论的是人的判断变化，不是英美天气记录互相矛盾。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about how Americans judged the warm winter of 2012?

options.text: Their judgments were filtered by beliefs about climate change and by political identity
Most people ignored the winter because it did not affect them directly
People relied mostly on local temperature records rather than on opinion
Political differences disappeared once weather data were adjusted

evidence.quote: those who thought of global climate change as a serious, man-made threat were significantly more likely to report that their local winter weather had been warmer than usual.
there was a political divide, with Democrats more likely than Republicans to rate the winter as unusually toasty

private.explanationZh: 这是推断题。客观数据说明2012年冬天确实偏暖，但作者紧接着指出，不同立场的人对同一冬天会给出不同解释。由此可推断，人们的判断并不只来自天气本身，也会被自己对气候变化的看法和政治身份过滤。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两段证据分别对应气候信念和政治分歧，合起来支持这一推断。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有说大多数人忽视了这个冬天。
optionRationalesZh.c: 恰恰相反，作者讨论的是主观报告如何随立场变化。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文明确说即使调整了全球变暖相关看法，政治分歧依然存在。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention labels such as "polar vortex" and "snowmaggedon"?

options.text: To show that scientists need stricter definitions for winter storms
To argue that unusual weather is mostly invented by reporters
To illustrate how media dramatization can shape public perception of weather
To explain why snowstorms should be named like tropical storms

evidence.quote: different channels compete for our attention by mentioning "extreme weather" far more often than they used to and spicing up not-so-apocalyptic events with terms like 'polar vortex' and 'snowmaggedon.'
With so many sources of information, it's easy for us to choose the ones that align with our broader worldviews, no matter what they are.

private.explanationZh: 这道修辞目的题考查举例的功能。作者提到这些夸张标签，并不是要解释术语本身，而是为了说明媒体会用更戏剧化的表达争夺注意力，从而影响公众如何理解天气事件。后一句关于人们选择符合自身世界观的信息，进一步点明了这种表达的效果。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文不是在讨论科学定义是否严格，而是在谈媒体表达方式。
optionRationalesZh.b: 作者没有说异常天气是媒体虚构的，只说媒体会放大和包装。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。两个证据连起来正好说明媒体戏剧化表达如何影响感知。
optionRationalesZh.d: 命名做法只是材料中的一个例子，不是作者真正要解释的重点。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-052-q01: 开头说人对天气是否更怪异的判断取决于自己最近经历的天气，随后又举出气候信念、党派差异和媒体用词会影响判断，核心就是近期经验、既有信念与媒体框架共同塑造看法。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-052-q02: 洪水和干旱两个例子都显示，一次突出的近期天气事件之后，更多人会说自己早就观察到长期变湿或长期干旱的趋势，说明新近经历会强化他们对长期趋势的感觉。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-052-q03: 讲话明确说，认为全球变暖是严重人为威胁的人更可能觉得当地冬天更暖；即使调整了全球变暖信念，民主党人仍比共和党人更可能这样判断，所以判断受气候信念和政治身份过滤。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-052-q04: 提到“polar vortex”“snowmaggedon”是为了说明媒体为了吸引注意，会更频繁地说“极端天气”并用夸张标签包装并不一定末日般的事件，从而影响公众感知。

public.prompt: What question does the talk primarily answer about the Moon?

options.text: Why spacecraft were needed before people could believe the Moon had a far side
How the Moon became synchronized so that nearly the same side always faces Earth
Why asteroid impacts are the main origin of moons in the solar system
How photographs from Luna 3 changed modern astronomy

evidence.quote: We always see the same old side of the moon because the moon rotates exactly once on its axis each time it orbits Earth.

Whatever the case, it took just 1000 years for the Earth's pull to adjust the moon's spin enough that one rotation of the moon corresponded to one trip around the earth, leaving one side forever locked facing away.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题要求同时抓住现象和成因。开头提出现象：月球每次公转时恰好自转一次，所以我们总看到几乎同一面；后文则解释地球引力如何把月球逐渐调整到这种同步状态。因此全文核心是解释“为什么会这样，以及它是怎样形成的”。

optionRationalesZh.a: Luna 3只是引入话题的背景，不是全文主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它完整概括了月球总朝向地球同一面的原因与形成机制。
optionRationalesZh.c: 撞击起源只在中段短暂出现，不是主要讨论对象。
optionRationalesZh.d: 拍摄和测绘只是结尾补充，不是中心问题。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker imagine a Moon that is not spinning or is spinning twice as fast?

options.text: To contrast hypothetical cases with the Moon's actual synchronized motion
To prove that the Moon once rotated faster than it orbited
To explain why astronomers misread the Moon before the space age
To show why satellites had to map the hidden side

evidence.quote: If it weren't spinning at all, we'd get at least one 360 degree view of its surface with each lap.
If it were spinning twice as fast, we'd also see the moon's entire surface more than once per orbit.
our moon's motions - like the spin and orbit of most other moons in our solar system - are, remarkably, in perfect sync.

private.explanationZh: 这道修辞目的题关注两个假设场景的作用。作者先说如果月球不自转或自转更快，我们会看到完全不同的结果；随后再指出真实情况是自转与公转完全同步。这样的假设不是在讲历史事实，而是在通过对比帮助听者理解潮汐锁定。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两个假设都是为现实状态做对照，让同步这一点更容易理解。
optionRationalesZh.b: 作者后来明确说我们并不知道最初到底哪一个更快，所以这不是这里的目的。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有讨论早期天文学家的误解。
optionRationalesZh.d: 卫星测绘是结尾才出现的结果，不是这两个假设的功能。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what did Earth's gravity do when the Moon's spin and orbit were out of sync?

options.text: It moved the Moon outward until both motions stopped changing
It distorted the Moon and either slowed or sped its rotation depending on the mismatch
It kept the Moon from developing any bulges at all
It made the Moon orbit in a perfect circle instead of an ellipse

evidence.quote: Earth's gravity deformed the moon into a slight oval, with one of its bulges facing Earth.

if it was spinning more than once per orbit, earth would pull at a slight angle against the moon's direction of rotation, slowing its spin; if the moon was spinning less than once per orbit, Earth would have pulled the other way, speeding its rotation.

private.explanationZh: 这道细节题考查中段机制。地球引力先把月球拉成略微椭圆的形状，又会在自转和公转不同步时，通过拉扯鼓包来改变月球自转速度。也就是说，引力既造成形变，也会根据失配方向来减速或加速自转。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文重点不是把月球向外拉远，而是通过引力扭矩调整自转。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个选项完整概括了形变和调速两个作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中恰恰说月球形成了鼓包。
optionRationalesZh.d: 椭圆轨道在后文解释可见范围时才出现，这里不是地球引力对失配的主要作用。

public.prompt: What explains why people on Earth can see slightly more than half of the Moon?

options.text: The far side reflects sunlight more strongly during some seasons
The Moon's surface shifts because its crust is still cooling
Variations in the Moon's orbit and tilt reveal small areas beyond the average edge
Earth's atmosphere bends moonlight around the lunar horizon

evidence.quote: We do end up seeing slightly more than that one side, because the moon's elliptical orbit gives us peeks beyond its average eastern and western horizons, and its tilted axis causes "moon-seasons" revealing more of the lunar north or south poles.
But those glimpses only add up to an extra 9%, leaving 41% of the moon hidden from earth.

private.explanationZh: 这道细节题对应结尾补充说明。虽然月球总体上被潮汐锁定，但椭圆轨道会让我们偶尔看到平均东西边缘之外的一点区域，倾斜轴线也会让南北极多露出一些。正因为这些小幅变化，我们能看到略多于一半的月面。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有提到远月面反光更强。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有说月壳冷却导致可见范围变化。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。轨道椭圆和轴线倾斜正是作者给出的两个原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 大气折射并不是这里的解释。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-053-q01: 整段主要解释为什么我们总看到月球同一面，即月球自转与绕地公转同步，以及这种同步是怎样在地球引力作用下形成的。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-053-q02: 讲话先设想月球完全不转或转得更快时我们会看到什么，再对比现实中月球每绕地一周恰好自转一周，从而突出其同步运动的特殊性。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-053-q03: 中段明确说地球引力把月球拉成略微椭圆的形状；如果月球转得比每轨一周更快，就会减慢其自转；如果更慢，就会加快其自转，因此答案是既造成形变又按失配方向减速或加速。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-053-q04: 结尾说我们能多看到一点，是因为月球椭圆轨道让我们偶尔看到平均东西边界之外，倾斜的自转轴又让我们多看到南北极一些区域，所以是轨道和倾角变化造成的。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the talk about wildlife in cities?

options.text: Why city governments often fail to protect native species effectively
How urban weeds spread from one continent to another through trade
Why cities should be considered ideal environments for biodiversity overall
How some species survive or adapt to urban environments despite city pressures

evidence.quote: Some lucky species happen to be naturally suited to cities.
they aren't biological dead zones either - they're more like accidental laboratories where the limits of life's adjustability are being tested and tasted.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题要求概括作者如何看待城市与野生生物的关系。全文不是简单说城市有害，也不是说城市对生物多样性全是好事，而是解释在城市压力下，一些物种如何凭借先天适配、行为调整和遗传变化继续生存甚至繁盛。结尾把城市比作“意外实验室”，进一步概括了这一主题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有把重点放在政府政策失败上。
optionRationalesZh.b: 作者提到城市杂草，但不是在讨论全球贸易传播。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文明确说城市不是生物多样性的福地，所以这个选项过度夸大。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确概括了全文从先天适合到进化适应的核心内容。

public.prompt: What do the examples of ivy, pigeons, and raccoons illustrate?

options.text: Urban animals must learn new behaviors before they can find food
Some species already possess traits that make city environments workable for them
Most city wildlife depends on direct human protection for survival
Only plant species can take advantage of man-made structures

evidence.quote: English ivy and rock pigeons climb and roost on vertical structures like trees and cliffs anyway, so brick walls and sky-high ledges are fine substitutes.
omnivorous raccoons thrive on an endless buffet of everything from corn chips to cockroaches, helping them live ten times more densely in cities than in woodland habitats .

private.explanationZh: 这道细节题考查前段举例的共同含义。常春藤和鸽子本来就适合利用垂直结构，所以墙面和高处边缘能替代自然栖息点；浣熊则因食性广而能利用城市中的丰富食物。三个例子共同说明，有些物种并不需要先发生重大变化，原本的特征就让它们适合城市。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中这几个例子强调的是先天适合，不是先学会新行为。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它概括了结构利用和杂食性这两类已有优势。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有说这些物种主要依赖人类保护。
optionRationalesZh.d: 鸽子是动物，已经能排除“只有植物”这一说法。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about why some urban evolution happens quickly?

options.text: Cities directly cause organisms to choose new genes for themselves
Urban settings always create stronger mutations than rural ones do
Sudden pressures can rapidly favor rare helpful traits, especially in fast breeders
Only species exposed to industrial toxins can evolve successfully in cities

evidence.quote: when a big challenge springs up suddenly , an uncommon trait that helps individuals cope can become the genetic norm surprisingly fast - especially in speedy breeders - because those without it may not get a chance to reproduce.
it took just just six decades for 99 percent of the local tomcod to evolve a protective mutation that blocks the toxin from entering their cells.

private.explanationZh: 这是推断题。作者先给出一般原理：当强烈挑战突然出现时，少见但有利的特征会被迅速筛选出来，尤其在繁殖快的物种中更明显；随后再用tomcod的例子说明这一过程能在相对短时间内发生。因此可以推断，城市中的快速进化主要来自强选择压力，而不是进化规律本身改变。

optionRationalesZh.a: 基因变化不是生物主动选择的结果，这与原文的进化机制不符。
optionRationalesZh.b: 作者没有说城市会制造更强的突变，只说会更快筛选有利特征。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个选项把一般机制和具体例子准确结合起来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 工业毒素只是一个例子，后文还有城市杂草等其他适应情形。

public.prompt: How does the speaker organize the discussion of city life and biodiversity?

options.text: By beginning with biodiversity losses, then moving to several ways species persist or adapt in cities
By comparing three cities and then ranking their conservation policies
By presenting genetic theory first and rejecting field examples at the end
By arguing that cities help all species equally and then listing minor exceptions

evidence.quote: Since the mid-1800's, Adelaide's mammal diversity has fallen by more than a quarter, New York City has lost nearly half of its native plants, and at least 25 species of butterflies have disappeared from Rome.
Natural flexibility can also help animals cope with the stresses of metropolitan living: coyotes that colonize cities often become more nocturnal to minimize their encounters with humans.
But over several generations, genetic change can help some populations evolve into city slickers.
This doesn't mean that cities are a boon for biodiversity , but they aren't biological dead zones either

private.explanationZh: 这道结构题考查全文推进方式。作者先用多个城市的损失现象建立“城市会伤害生物多样性”的背景，然后转入主体，依次说明先天适配、行为灵活性和遗传进化如何帮助部分物种在城市坚持下来。结尾再收束到一个平衡判断：城市不是福地，但也不是完全的生物死区。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项准确反映了从问题背景到适应机制再到总结判断的组织顺序。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有评估城市政策，只是借几个城市举例说明损失。
optionRationalesZh.c: 遗传变化不是开头，作者也没有否定前面的例子。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文并没有说城市对所有物种都有利，反而强调很多物种无法轻易适应。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-054-q01: 开头先说城市让很多物种减少，随后重点转到仍能生存甚至繁盛的物种，并解释它们如何依靠原有特征、行为灵活性或进化适应城市压力。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-054-q02: 常春藤、鸽子和浣熊的例子都在说明，有些物种本来就具备适合城市的特征，例如会利用垂直结构或杂食，因此城市环境对它们是可行的。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-054-q03: 讲话说当巨大挑战突然出现时，能帮助个体应对的罕见特征会很快变成基因常态，尤其是在繁殖快的物种中，因为没有该特征的个体可能来不及繁殖。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-054-q04: 结构上先用阿德莱德、纽约、罗马说明城市中的生物多样性损失，然后转入几种物种为何能在城市中存活或适应，包括天然适配、行为调整和快速进化。

public.prompt: What central issue does the speaker explore in this talk?

options.text: Why decomposers become active only a few minutes after death
How scavengers avoid or survive the dangers of feeding on dead animals
Why humans are worse predators than scavengers in most ecosystems
How fresh meat differs chemically from rotten carrion

evidence.quote: many scavengers get shoulder-deep in dead meat without seeming to suffer - and we're just beginning to understand how they do it.
perhaps we can scavenge some of these scavengers' tricks

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题聚焦全文想解决的核心问题。开头提出谜题：很多食腐动物深入腐肉却似乎不会生病；结尾则说人类也许还能借鉴它们的办法。中间所有内容，无论是挑选食物、抗菌处理、强胃酸还是免疫机制，都是围绕“它们如何降低风险并活下来”展开的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 分解者在几分钟内开始活动只是背景，用来说明风险来源。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它概括了全文讨论的各种防御与适应机制。
optionRationalesZh.c: 作者没有比较人类和食腐动物谁是更差的捕食者。
optionRationalesZh.d: 化学差异不是全文主线，重点是生物如何应对危险。

public.prompt: What is one early way some scavengers reduce the risk of eating carrion?

options.text: They heat carcasses before feeding on them
They avoid all meat that shows any sign of decay
They use quality control by rejecting some diseased carcasses or preferring fresher meat
They rely on plant foods until their immune systems mature

evidence.quote: Quality control is one of their earliest lines of defense.
Wolves and foxes have been known to pass up diseased reindeer carcasses in favor of prey killed by other predators, although it's not yet clear how they can tell the difference.
spotted hyenas prefer to dine on fresh meat, minimizing their contact with microbes.

private.explanationZh: 这道细节题考查作者在前半段提出的第一层防线。所谓quality control，就是尽量减少接触最危险的腐肉：狼和狐狸会避开病死尸体，斑点鬣狗则偏爱更新鲜的肉。作者借此说明，许多食腐动物并不是毫无选择地乱吃，而是先在源头上降低感染风险。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有提到它们会先加热尸体。
optionRationalesZh.b: 有些食腐动物会主动寻找腐肉，因此“避开所有开始腐败的肉”过于绝对。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个选项准确概括了拒绝病尸体和偏好鲜肉两种做法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有说它们主要依赖植物性食物。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about how vultures build resistance to toxins?

options.text: They are born fully protected against every pathogen found in carrion
Their resistance comes only from choosing less dangerous carcasses
Group grooming is the only reason their immunity increases over time
Repeated exposure seems to strengthen immunity even though the first survival step is unclear

evidence.quote: Bearded vultures assault microbes with stomach acid that's ten times more acidic than our own, and strong enough to corrode steel.
with each encounter, their immune systems churn out more and more antibodies, building resistance to the toxins.

private.explanationZh: 这是推断题。原文先说秃鹫有极强胃酸，但又指出有些病原体仍能通过；随后说明每次接触后，它们的免疫系统会产生越来越多抗体。由此可推断，秃鹫的抗毒能力至少部分是通过反复暴露逐渐建立的，而不是一开始就完全具备。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有说它们天生就对所有病原体完全免疫。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这更像狼、狐狸和鬣狗前面的做法，不足以解释秃鹫后段的抗体机制。
optionRationalesZh.c: 群体接触建立免疫说的是鬣狗和狮子，不是秃鹫这里的重点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它同时保留了“首轮生存机制未明”和“反复暴露增强抗体”两个关键信息。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker bring up human vaccination near the end?

options.text: To show that vaccines were invented by copying hyenas directly
To prove that humans are already better than scavengers at defeating anthrax
To explain why scavengers should be used in medical experiments
To connect scavenger immunity with a familiar human example of controlled exposure

evidence.quote: Hyenas and lions, for instance, likely pass small doses of germs around as they groom, eat, and compete with each other, which may help build up group-wide immunity to toxins like anthrax.
we humans have established our own herd immunity through controlled exposure to diseases like meningitis and smallpox - we call it vaccination.

private.explanationZh: 这道修辞目的题考查类比的作用。作者先说鬣狗和狮子可能通过小剂量接触病菌建立群体免疫，再把这一思路和人类疫苗联系起来。目的不是讲疫苗史，而是让听者用熟悉的人类例子理解食腐动物身上的免疫逻辑。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有说疫苗发明直接来自对鬣狗的观察。
optionRationalesZh.b: 作者恰恰说人类还没有战胜某些毒素，因此不是为了证明人类更强。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有讨论是否要把食腐动物用于实验。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。人类疫苗在这里是帮助理解“受控暴露建立免疫”的类比。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-055-q01: 全文围绕一个中心问题展开：腐肉中有危险微生物和毒素，但食腐动物为何还能吃腐肉而不明显生病，也就是它们如何避免或挺过这些风险。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-055-q02: 开头就说“quality control”是早期防线之一，狼和狐狸会避开生病的驯鹿尸体，鬣狗也更偏好新鲜肉，这都属于通过挑选尸体或偏好更新鲜肉来降低风险。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-055-q03: 讲话明确说我们还不确定秃鹫第一次接触毒素时如何幸存，但知道每次接触后其免疫系统都会产生更多抗体、建立抵抗力，所以可推断重复暴露会增强免疫。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-055-q04: 提到人类疫苗是为了用听众熟悉的例子说明“受控暴露后形成免疫”这一原理，与前文鬣狗、狮子等通过小剂量病菌暴露建立群体免疫的描述形成对应。

public.prompt: What does the talk mainly explain about clouds?

options.text: Why people once needed balloons and airplanes to study cloud layers
How cloud names became important in weather forecasting
How clouds form and why the same process can fuel powerful storms
Why liquid water is harder to transport through the atmosphere than ice

evidence.quote: our atmosphere manages to pump out one cloud after another all over the world
the energy released can produce stratosphere-high pillars of cloud with violent updrafts, fierce electrical storms, and grapefruit-sized hailstones.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题要求把全文前后两部分连起来看。前面重点解释云是怎样形成的，后面说明凝结释放的能量如何让同一套过程发展成强上升气流、雷暴和大冰雹。因此主旨不是单纯介绍云的外观，而是解释形成机制及其可能带来的强天气结果。

optionRationalesZh.a: 热气球和飞机只是开头的反衬，不是全文重点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有讨论云名在天气预报中的重要性。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它同时概括了成云过程和强风暴形成。
optionRationalesZh.d: 作者不是在比较液态水和冰的运输难度。

public.prompt: Why, according to the speaker, can moist air rise especially easily?

options.text: Because liquid droplets weigh less than nitrogen molecules
Because water vapor is lighter than the nitrogen and oxygen it replaces in air
Because falling air pressure adds heat directly to every water molecule
Because sunlight always warms humid air more than dry air

evidence.quote: the more water vapor it collects before lift-off, the lighter it gets.
Basic physics dictates that a given volume of gas has the same number of molecules regardless of what those molecules are.
And water is made of H plus H plus O, which is lighter than both two Ns and two Os.

private.explanationZh: 这道细节题考查中段的物理解释。作者指出，在相同体积下，气体分子数量相同，而水蒸气分子比构成空气主体的氮气和氧气分子更轻。因此潮湿空气中的水蒸气越多，整体就越轻，也就更容易上升。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这里讨论的是水蒸气分子，不是液态小水滴。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。三个证据连起来正是这一逻辑。
optionRationalesZh.c: 气压下降会让空气团膨胀降温，不是给每个分子直接加热。
optionRationalesZh.d: 太阳加热是起点之一，但这里强调的关键原因是分子质量差异。

public.prompt: How does the speaker explain the formation of a cumulus cloud?

options.text: By tracing a sequence from evaporation to rising air, cooling, condensation, and upward growth
By comparing cloud formation over oceans, forests, and cities
By listing famous storms and then working backward to their causes
By arguing that clouds form only when humans disperse water high in the sky

evidence.quote: Cumulus clouds, for example get their start when solar energy evaporates water from oceans, plants, and soil by breaking the bonds that hold water molecules together.
As the invisible balloon goes up, the falling pressure outside allows it to keep ballooning, which spreads out its internal heat and lowers its temperature.
Eventually, the air at the top cools enough for the water vapor there to condense into droplets
creating a flat-bottomed cloud that appears to grow upward out of nothing.

private.explanationZh: 这道结构题要求辨认作者解释成云过程的顺序。作者先从蒸发讲起，再说暖湿空气上升、受压降低而膨胀降温，接着水蒸气凝结成水滴，最后云底固定而云体继续向上发展。整段明显是在按因果和时间顺序展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项准确概括了作者的步骤式讲解。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有做不同地表环境之间的系统比较。
optionRationalesZh.c: 作者不是先列风暴案例再倒推原因，而是先解释基本机制。
optionRationalesZh.d: 开头提到人工造云很难，正是为了反衬自然过程，而不是说云只能靠人类形成。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare a small cumulus cloud to about 270 tons of TNT?

options.text: To suggest that cloud formation is caused by atmospheric explosions
To explain why hail is chemically similar to explosives
To argue that hot-air balloons are unsafe near any cloud at all
To make the scale of condensation energy vivid to the listener

evidence.quote: the total energy released from condensation is huge - equivalent to about 270 tons of TNT.
And if the supply of water vapor is much larger, the energy released can produce stratosphere-high pillars of cloud with violent updrafts, fierce electrical storms, and grapefruit-sized hailstones.

private.explanationZh: 这道修辞目的题考查数量类比的作用。作者把凝结释放的能量类比成270吨TNT，不是说云和炸药有关，而是为了让听者直观感受到这种能量到底有多大。后一句紧接着说明，正是这种巨大能量在水汽更多时可以推动强烈风暴发展。

optionRationalesZh.a: 作者没有说云是由爆炸造成的，这误解了类比。
optionRationalesZh.b: TNT只是能量规模的参照，不是在谈化学成分。
optionRationalesZh.c: 结尾关于热气球只是轻松收束，不是这个类比的主要功能。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。类比的核心就是把抽象的能量规模变得具体可感。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-056-q01: 整段先解释云如何由蒸发、上升、冷却和凝结形成，结尾又说明凝结释放的巨大能量如何推动上升气流并在水汽充足时发展成强烈风暴，因此主旨是云的形成及其如何为强天气提供能量。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-056-q02: 讲话解释说同体积气体分子数相同，而水汽由较轻的H和O组成，比它所替代的主要大气成分氮气和氧气更轻，所以温暖潮湿空气比温暖干燥空气更有浮力。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-056-q03: 讲解顺序很清楚：太阳蒸发地表水分，暖湿空气上升，外部压强降低使其膨胀降温，顶部水汽凝结成云滴，随后更多水汽在同一高度凝结，云体向上发展。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-056-q04: 把小积云释放的能量比作约270吨TNT，是为了把凝结释放能量的规模形象化，让听者直观感到即便小云团也蕴含巨大能量，而不是说云是爆炸造成的。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the talk?

options.text: To explain why pandas are the rarest endangered species on Earth
To argue that conservation should often use rational triage criteria instead of cuteness
To describe how emergency-room workers rescue endangered animals
To show that most conservation money already protects all threatened species equally

evidence.quote: a medical triage-like approach could help us decide which species to save first.
Should we really let appearances decide who lives and who dies, or should we take a more rational approach?

private.explanationZh: 开头提出“医疗分诊式”的保护思路，结尾又追问是否应让外表决定物种存亡，说明全文核心是在主张保护决策应更多依据理性分诊标准，而不是仅凭熊猫这类可爱形象。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中明确说熊猫并不像爪哇犀牛那样稀有，所以这不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确项，既概括了分诊式思路，也抓住了结尾对“外表决定生死”的反思。
optionRationalesZh.c: 急诊人员只是类比，用来引出分诊概念，不是全文主要内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中恰好说很多以明星物种为门面的活动主要只救它们自己，并非平均保护所有物种。

public.prompt: According to the talk, which organisms are given as examples of species that are vital to entire ecosystems?

options.text: Javan rhinos and giant pandas
Maud Island frogs and pygmy hog-sucking lice
Mangroves and otters
Bamboo forests and kelp

evidence.quote: focus on species vital to entire ecosystems, like mangroves, whose groves support over a thousand species or otters, whose urchin-eating keeps kelp forests healthy.

private.explanationZh: 讲话者在介绍“先救总统”式优先级时，直接举出红树林和水獭，说明这类物种之所以重要，是因为它们关系到整个生态系统中许多其他物种的存续。

optionRationalesZh.a: 爪哇犀牛被用来说明“最危急”的物种，熊猫后来被讨论为高人气但未必最优先，不是这里的答案。
optionRationalesZh.b: 毛德岛青蛙用于说明较便宜、较容易救的案例；猪吸虱则出现在后文作为没人宣传的“丑小鸭”物种。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确项，原文明确说红树林和水獭是对整个生态系统至关重要的例子。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文提到的是红树林与海带森林相关，但真正被列为物种例子的是红树林和水獭，不是竹林与海带本身。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention that pandas are the “literal faces of conservation”?

options.text: To explain why pandas attract funding even when they are not the strongest triage candidates
To prove that panda campaigns mainly save unattractive species
To show that pandas are more ecologically important than mangroves and otters
To argue that emotional appeals should replace rational conservation planning

evidence.quote: As the literal faces of conservation, pandas pull in big bucks for wildlife protection.
the majority of campaigns fronted by pandas or other charismatic creatures are solely devoted to saving their symbolic species.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到熊猫是保护事业的“门面”，是为了说明它们虽然未必最稀有、也未必最关键，却因为形象讨喜而更能吸引公众捐款和注意力，这正好解释了现实决策为何常偏离理性分诊。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确项，熊猫作为“形象代言”带来资金，正是说服力所在。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文说WWF资金会分给一些项目，但多数以明星物种为门面的活动主要还是救它们自己，因此这项说反了。
optionRationalesZh.c: 讲话者明确说熊猫不像水獭和红树林那样对生态系统关键，所以此项与原文矛盾。
optionRationalesZh.d: 全文是在质疑单靠情感和外表作决定，而不是主张用情感取代理性。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about many less charismatic species?

options.text: They usually cost more to protect than pandas
They rarely matter to the ecosystems around them
They receive more public attention than famous animals
They may fit conservation triage well even though people overlook them

evidence.quote: underdog species like these are often ideal triage candidates: they can be simpler to resuscitate, less expensive to protect, and vital to their ecosystems - their only flaw is inferior cuteness.

private.explanationZh: 结尾指出这些“弱势物种”往往更容易救、成本更低、对生态系统也重要，只是“不够可爱”。因此可以推断，它们常常更符合分诊优先标准，只是公众忽视了它们。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文恰恰说这些物种往往更便宜、更容易保护，不是更贵。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文说它们常常“vital to their ecosystems”，所以此项与证据相反。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这些物种的问题正是缺乏关注，而不是比明星物种更受关注。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确项，推断自“更容易复苏、成本更低、对生态系统重要”这一连串条件。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-057-q01: 通篇先介绍物种保护资源有限，再类比医疗分诊，随后对比犀牛、红树林、水獭、熊猫等案例，结尾明确追问是否应采取更理性的方式，因此主旨是主张保护决策应更多依据理性分诊标准而非可爱程度。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-057-q02: 原文在介绍“对整个生态系统至关重要”的物种时，明确举了红树林和水獭两个例子；其余选项中的物种在文中承担的是别的角色或并未被这样表述。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-057-q03: 说熊猫是“保护事业的门面”紧接着说明它们能吸引大量资金，但前文又说它们既不最稀有，也未必最关键，说明此处是为了解释熊猫为何即使不是最优分诊对象仍能得到大量资助。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-057-q04: 结尾明确说这些“弱势”物种往往更容易救活、成本更低、且对生态系统重要，唯一缺点只是不可爱，因此可推知它们常常是很适合分诊保护、却被公众忽视的对象。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the talk?

options.text: Why tiny airborne impurities are necessary for raindrops to form
How lightning turns water vapor into solid ice
Why the dew point prevents water molecules from sticking together
How rainclouds remove dirt from the atmosphere

evidence.quote: without these microscopic pieces of dirt, there would be no rain, because water vapor can't condense into droplets on its own.
it's these little pieces of dirt, surrounded by water, that make life possible on our big piece of dirt surrounded by water.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接说没有这些微小杂质就不会下雨，结尾又总结正是这些被水包围的小尘粒让生命成为可能，所以全文主旨是解释空气中的微粒为何是雨滴形成的必要条件。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确项，既概括了雨滴形成机制，也抓住了“微小杂质不可缺少”的中心思想。
optionRationalesZh.b: 全文没有讨论闪电把水汽变成冰这一过程。
optionRationalesZh.c: 露点被提到是水团开始增长的条件之一，但讲话者的重点不是说露点阻止分子黏在一起。
optionRationalesZh.d: 讲话者关心的是尘粒如何帮助形成雨滴，而不是雨云如何清除大气中的灰尘。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why do very small water clusters tend to shrink instead of grow?

options.text: They are too cold to absorb more vapor
Their curved surfaces leave outer molecules with too few neighbors to hold them
They are pushed apart by dirt particles
Their electric charges repel nearby water molecules

evidence.quote: If it's too small, its surface is so curved that the molecules on the outside have few neighbors to bond to, which makes them easy to break off

private.explanationZh: 讲话者解释，小水团表面弯曲得太厉害，外侧分子能形成的键太少，因此更容易脱落，结果就是整体上更容易缩小而不是继续长大。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有说问题在于它们“太冷”，而是说表面曲率导致外层分子不稳定。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确项，完整对应了“曲率大、邻居少、容易断开”的因果链。
optionRationalesZh.c: 尘粒在后文是帮助凝结的，不是把小水团推散。
optionRationalesZh.d: 全文没有把这里的困难解释为电荷排斥。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker say that you would need “a golf ball of air ten million miles across”?

options.text: To compare the size of raindrops with planets
To show that dew-point air contains almost no water molecules
To suggest that clouds can form only in outer space
To emphasize how unlikely it is for water clusters to reach critical size on their own

evidence.quote: And you'd need a golf ball of air ten million miles across to find one 50-molecule cluster!
Which, basically, means that clusters of water molecules never get to 150 million on their own.

private.explanationZh: 这个夸张的尺度不是为了给出真实空间场景，而是为了让听者直观感受到：如果没有尘粒作为起点，水分子团几乎不可能自行长到临界尺寸。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这里不是在比较雨滴和行星大小，而是在强调概率极低。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中说露点时仍有大量小水团，所以并不是“几乎没有水分子”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 讲话者没有说云只能在太空形成，这显然误解了例子的作用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确项，后一句马上总结说水分子团根本无法自己长到1.5亿个分子那么大。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the “dirt” in the atmosphere?

options.text: It matters only after raindrops are already fully formed
It prevents water vapor from cooling to the dew point
It provides a starting surface that lets droplets grow and ultimately supports life
It is useful mainly because it heats the atmosphere

evidence.quote: they can start off at that critical size by condensing onto one of the gadjillions of little pieces of dirt floating in our atmosphere, and then grow and grow until they're a droplet in a raincloud.
it's these little pieces of dirt, surrounded by water, that make life possible on our big piece of dirt surrounded by water.

private.explanationZh: 从结尾可推断，这些微粒的重要性不只是“脏东西存在着”，而是它们给水汽提供了起始附着面，使液滴得以增长，进一步形成降雨并支持地球生命。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文强调它们在雨滴形成之前就已经起关键作用。
optionRationalesZh.b: 露点是空气冷却后的状态，文中没有说尘粒会阻止空气达到露点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确项，综合了“在尘粒上凝结”与“让生命成为可能”两层意思。
optionRationalesZh.d: 尘粒的作用是作为凝结核，而不是主要通过加热大气。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-058-q01: 开头直接提出每个雨滴里都有微小杂质，并说明没有这些杂质就没有雨；后文都在解释小颗粒为何是液滴形成的必要起点，所以主旨是空气中的微小杂质为何对降雨形成必不可少。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-058-q02: 原文明确解释小水团表面弯曲度太大，外侧分子邻居太少，容易被打掉，因此失去分子的概率大于获得分子的概率，所以会倾向于缩小而非长大。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-058-q03: “需要一个横跨一千万英里的高尔夫球大小空气团”这一夸张数字，是接在关于50分子团簇极难出现的说明之后，用来强调水分子团簇靠自身长到关键尺寸几乎不可能。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-058-q04: 结尾说水汽可以先凝结在大量漂浮的微小尘粒上，再不断长成雨云中的液滴，并进一步说正是这些被水包围的小尘粒让地球生命成为可能，因此可推知尘粒提供了液滴生长的起始表面。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly about?

options.text: Why the Moon receives more sunlight than Earth
How Earth's atmosphere keeps the planet warmer and more stable through greenhouse absorption
Why nitrogen and oxygen are the strongest greenhouse gases
How fast planetary rotation creates infrared radiation

evidence.quote: What protects us is our atmosphere.
At the same time, it traps the infrared radiation - aka heat - radiating out from Earth's sun-warmed surface, keeping us from freezing solid at night.

private.explanationZh: 讲话先指出真正保护地球的是大气层，随后解释大气如何吸收并滞留来自地表的红外辐射，因此主旨是在说明大气，尤其是温室吸收过程，如何让地球保持较温暖且较稳定。

optionRationalesZh.a: 开头提月球只是引入对比，主旨并不是解释月球为何受更多阳光照射。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确项，准确概括了“保护地球的是大气层”以及“滞留红外热量”的核心机制。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中明确说氮气和氧气大多不能有效吸收红外，所以此项与原文相反。
optionRationalesZh.d: 讲话者还特别说地球转得快并不是主要原因，因此此项错误。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why can carbon dioxide and methane absorb infrared radiation?

options.text: They permanently carry a full electric charge
They reflect infrared rays directly back into space
Their collisions and vibrations often make them electrically lopsided
They are more common in the atmosphere than nitrogen and oxygen

evidence.quote: both carbon dioxide and methane spend most of their time "shaking it" in electrically-lopsided ways, allowing them to absorb infrared rays and help insulate the earth.

private.explanationZh: 原文说明二氧化碳和甲烷表面上看似对称，但分子一直在碰撞、转动和振动，这些运动常使它们呈现电性不对称，从而能吸收红外线。

optionRationalesZh.a: 讲话者并没有说它们一直带完整电荷，而是说它们会暂时呈现电性偏斜。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文讨论的是吸收红外，而不是直接把红外反射回太空。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确项，完整对应了“碰撞与振动导致电性不对称”的解释。
optionRationalesZh.d: 它们并不比氮氧更常见；相反，文中强调大部分大气是氮和氧。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker begin by comparing Earth with the Moon?

options.text: To highlight how strongly an atmosphere affects surface temperatures
To show that long days are the main reason Earth stays warm
To prove that visible light matters more than infrared radiation
To argue that the Moon once had the same climate as Earth

evidence.quote: our planet's relatively fast rotation isn't what spares us from those loony temperatures. What protects us is our atmosphere.

private.explanationZh: 月球与地球距离太阳差不多，却出现极端高低温。这个对比的功能是突出：决定地表温度是否宜居的关键，不是距离太阳的微小差别，而是有没有能发挥保温作用的大气层。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确项，月球对比是为突出大气层对温度调节的重要性。
optionRationalesZh.b: 讲话者明确否定“地球转得快”是主要原因，所以此项错误。
optionRationalesZh.c: 开头对比并不是为了比较可见光和红外的重要性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文没有提出月球曾拥有与地球相同气候的论点。

public.prompt: What do the ice-record examples suggest about atmospheric carbon dioxide?

options.text: Its effects are too small to influence climate very much
It mattered only in ancient climates, not in the present
It changes temperature only when most of the atmosphere is nitrogen
Even relatively small changes in it can produce large temperature effects

evidence.quote: ice records from our own coldest climate show that small, natural variations in atmospheric carbon dioxide produce relatively big changes in temperature.
They also show that, compared to the last 800,000 years, the game today is much, much harder.

private.explanationZh: 冰芯记录表明，大气中二氧化碳的自然小幅波动也会引起相对较大的温度变化，接着又说今天这场“辐射弹珠游戏”更难了，因此可推断二氧化碳变化虽小，气候后果却可能很大。

optionRationalesZh.a: 证据恰好说明小变化也能带来大影响，因此此项相反。
optionRationalesZh.b: 结尾马上把古气候证据连接到“今天更严重”的状况，所以不能说只与古代有关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有把效应归因于氮气占多数这个条件。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确项，是对冰芯记录含义的合理概括。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-059-q01: 全文先用月球与地球温度对比引出大气层的重要性，再解释哪些气体如何吸收红外辐射并把热量“弹回”地表，核心就是地球大气如何通过温室吸收让地球更温暖且温度更稳定。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-059-q02: 原文先说二氧化碳和甲烷静态看似不偏电，但实际分子不断碰撞、转动和振动，且常以电荷分布不对称的方式“摇摆”，因此能够吸收红外线。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-059-q03: 开头比较地球和月球虽然离太阳距离差不多，却有截然不同的温度环境，随后立刻指出保护地球的是大气层，因此这段比较的作用是凸显大气层对地表温度的强大影响。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-059-q04: 文中明确说冰芯记录显示，大气中二氧化碳的小幅自然变化会带来相对较大的温度变化，因此可推知即使二氧化碳变化不大，也能产生显著气候效应。

public.prompt: What is the speaker mainly arguing?

options.text: Humans and chimpanzees are genetically identical except for one chromosome pair
Counting single-letter mutations is the best way to measure species similarity
The claim that humans are “99% chimp” is misleading, although genome comparison is still useful
Genome sequencing has replaced all earlier evidence about evolution

evidence.quote: But in reality, the human and chimp scrolls don't sync up so easily.

the researchers simply excluded all the large mismatched sections - a whopping 1.3 billion letters of DNA - and performed a letter-by-letter comparison on the remaining 2.4 billion, which turned out to be 98.77% identical.
But that doesn't mean we can't learn anything by comparing their genomes.

private.explanationZh: 讲话者先指出人类与黑猩猩基因组并没有那么容易一一对齐，又解释99%这个数字是通过排除大量不匹配片段得出的，随后补充说比较基因组仍然能提供信息。因此中心论点不是简单否定比较，而是说“99%黑猩猩”这种说法过于误导，但基因组比较依然有价值。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文强调人类和黑猩猩之间还有大片不匹配区域，绝非只差一个染色体对。
optionRationalesZh.b: 讲话者认为只数单个字母变化并不能充分说明相似性，所以此项错误。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确项，完整概括了前半段对99%说法的质疑和后半段对比较价值的保留。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文说测序帮助修正更早画出的进化树，不是完全取代早期证据。

public.prompt: What did the researchers do with the large mismatched sections when they calculated the 98.77% figure?

options.text: They left those sections out of the letter-by-letter comparison
They counted each mismatch as a single change
They replaced chimp DNA with human DNA before comparing
They averaged them with banana and dog genomes

evidence.quote: the researchers simply excluded all the large mismatched sections - a whopping 1.3 billion letters of DNA - and performed a letter-by-letter comparison on the remaining 2.4 billion, which turned out to be 98.77% identical.

private.explanationZh: 原文明确说研究者“simply excluded”那些大片不匹配区域，然后才对剩余部分逐字母比较，所以98.77%这个结果是在排除后得出的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确项，直接对应“excluded all the large mismatched sections”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 讲话者提出“算一个还是几千个”只是说明难题，不是研究者最终采用的方法。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有任何“替换DNA再比较”的做法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 香蕉和狗只在开头作为流行说法示例，和98.77%的计算无关。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker ask whether a duplicated genetic paragraph should count as “thousands of changes, or just one”?

options.text: To show that duplicated DNA always creates major physical differences
To illustrate why comparing large genomic changes is harder than counting single-letter differences
To argue that humans have more DNA paragraphs than chimpanzees
To suggest that genome scientists made a simple arithmetic mistake

evidence.quote: if a genetic paragraph - thousands of letters long - appears twice in a human scroll, but only once in its chimp counterpart, should the second copy count as thousands of changes, or just one?

private.explanationZh: 这个提问不是让听者真的计算，而是用具体例子说明：大片段的重复、缺失、倒置等变化很难像单个字母替换那样直接计数，因此“相似度百分比”背后有方法学难题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文后面还说少量突变有时影响很大，有时很多突变影响很小，所以这里不是断言重复一定造成大变化。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确项，问题本身就是为了展示大尺度基因组变化难以简单计数。
optionRationalesZh.c: 讲话者没有借此主张人类一定拥有更多“段落”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这里强调的是概念上的比较难题，不是科学家算错了加减法。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about comparing genomes from the end of the talk?

options.text: It is useful only when two genomes line up perfectly
It tells scientists exactly how similar organisms look and behave
It matters less now that evolutionary trees existed before sequencing
It can clarify evolutionary relationships even when a simple percentage comparison is misleading

evidence.quote: DNA contains a record of the evolutionary relationships between all organisms.
by reading closely, we've been able to glean enough information to refine the evolutionary trees we started drawing long before genome sequencing was around.

private.explanationZh: 结尾说DNA保存着生物进化关系的记录，虽然这份记录并不整齐，但仔细阅读后仍足以帮助人们修正进化树。因此可以推断，基因组比较的价值在于澄清演化关系，而不只是给出一个表面百分比。

optionRationalesZh.a: 结尾强调即使记录“garbled”，比较仍然有用，所以不需要完美对齐。
optionRationalesZh.b: 前文明确说突变数量并不能充分说明外形或行为有多相似，因此此项过度。
optionRationalesZh.c: 早期已有进化树并不削弱测序价值，原文说测序帮助“refine”这些进化树。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确项，准确概括了结尾保留的科学价值。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-060-q01: 前半部分集中说明“人类和黑猩猩99%相同”这一说法为何会误导，因为大量不匹配片段被排除；后半部分又强调比较基因组依然能帮助理解进化关系，所以主旨正是该比例说法有误导性但基因组比较仍有价值。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-060-q02: 原文直接说研究者“干脆排除了所有大型不匹配片段”，然后只对剩余的24亿个字母做逐字比较，得到98.77%的结果，因此答案是把那些部分排除在比较之外。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-060-q03: 提出“重复的一段该算几千处变化还是一处”这一问题，是在说明大型结构差异不像单字母变化那样容易统计，目的是展示比较大尺度基因组变化的困难。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-060-q04: 结尾说仅靠变化数量不能充分说明两个生物有多像，但DNA仍保存着进化关系记录，科学家借此改进进化树，因此可推知即便简单百分比会误导，基因组比较仍能澄清进化关系。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the talk?

options.text: Why land absorbs more heat than the oceans
How heat stored in the oceans helps drive more extreme weather
Why greenhouse gases no longer affect rainfall
How hurricanes and droughts are unrelated weather events

evidence.quote: heat stored in the oceans has a big impact on the weather.
it's not just a lot of hot air. It's also a bunch of warm water.

private.explanationZh: 讲话者先把重点转向海洋储热对天气的巨大影响，结尾又总结说极端天气不只是“热空气”，更是“温暖的海水”造成的，因此主旨是说明海洋吸收的热量如何推动更极端的天气。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中只说少量额外能量被陆地吸收，主旨并不是比较陆地和海洋谁吸热更多。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确项，完整抓住“海洋储热”与“极端天气”之间的核心联系。
optionRationalesZh.c: 讲话者恰恰解释了增温如何增加水汽并影响降雨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 全文是在建立它们之间的联系，而不是说它们彼此无关。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what does each degree of air-temperature increase do?

options.text: It lowers the dew point by about 7 percent
It reduces ocean evaporation by about 7 percent
It raises the air’s capacity to hold water vapor by about 7 percent
It makes storms move about 7 percent faster

evidence.quote: every degree increase in air temperature raises the air's capacity to hold water vapor by about 7 percent.

private.explanationZh: 原文直接给出这个数量关系：气温每升高一度，空气容纳水汽的能力大约增加7%。这是后面解释强降雨的重要基础。

optionRationalesZh.a: 讲话者没有说露点下降7%，这里是对含水能力的描述。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文说海表变暖会让更多水蒸发，不是减少蒸发。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确项，和原句完全对应。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有给出“风暴移动速度增加7%”这种说法。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare the extra ocean heat to “an atomic bomb exploding every second for the last 100 years”?

options.text: To emphasize how enormous the added ocean energy is
To argue that bombs are the main cause of climate change
To show that ocean heat disappears quickly
To explain why land surfaces stay cooler than the sea

evidence.quote: That increase in ocean heat content is the energy equivalent of an atomic bomb exploding every second for the last 100 years.

private.explanationZh: 这个比喻的作用是把抽象的海洋热含量转换成震撼、可感知的规模感，让听者意识到所谓“只有0.8度升温”背后隐藏着极其巨大的额外能量。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确项，比喻的核心就是强调能量规模大得惊人。
optionRationalesZh.b: 讲话者并没有把炸弹当作气候变化成因，这只是数量级类比。
optionRationalesZh.c: 该比喻说明的是热量巨大，不是热量迅速消失。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这里没有比较陆地为何更凉，而是在解释海洋储热规模。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about major storms from the end of the talk?

options.text: Scientists are certain that warmer oceans already create more storms every year
Storms become weaker because warm water spreads their energy out
Only snowstorms are affected by ocean temperatures
Warmer oceans may not guarantee more storms, but they can make storms more destructive

evidence.quote: We don't yet know for sure whether warming oceans are producing a greater number of these storms
more heat in the oceans gives these kinds of storms significantly more energy and destructive power when they do happen.

private.explanationZh: 讲话者明确保留了一个不确定点：是否会出现更多风暴还不能确定；但他同时强调，海洋里更多的热量会在风暴发生时赋予它们更大的能量和破坏力。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文专门说“还不能确定”是否会产生更多风暴，因此不能说科学家已经确定。
optionRationalesZh.b: 证据说明的是更强的能量和破坏力，不是更弱。
optionRationalesZh.c: 结尾讨论的是“大风暴和洪水”，并不限于雪暴。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确项，准确整合了“数量未定”和“破坏更强”两个信息点。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-061-q01: 讲话先指出表面气温升高本身只占少量额外能量，随后重点说明绝大多数热量进入海洋，而变暖海洋如何增强蒸发、降水、干旱和风暴，因此主旨是海洋储热如何推动更极端的天气。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-061-q02: 原文明确说气温每升高1度，空气容纳水汽的能力约增加7%，这是直接陈述，没有歧义。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-061-q03: 把海洋多吸收的热量比作“过去100年里每秒一颗原子弹爆炸”，显然是为了把抽象能量规模具体化，强调新增海洋热量极其巨大。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-061-q04: 结尾明确说目前还不能确定变暖海洋是否会产生更多风暴，但基本物理表明更多海洋热量会在风暴发生时赋予其更大能量和破坏力，因此可推知并非一定更多，但可能更强、更具破坏性。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly explaining about why leaves change color in autumn?

options.text: Why deciduous leaves change color in autumn as trees recover nutrients before dropping them
How evergreen trees keep their leaves active through winter
Why soil minerals directly decide whether leaves become red or yellow
How fallen leaves mainly serve as food for forest animals

evidence.quote: So each fall, deciduous trees recycle their leaves before dropping them.

private.explanationZh: 讲座的核心不是单纯描述叶子会变黄或变红，而是解释变色背后的原因：落叶树在秋天拆解并回收叶片中的营养，叶绿素因此被分解，原本被遮住的颜色才显现出来。所以正确概括必须同时包含“变色”和“回收养分后再落叶”这两层意思。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了全文的因果链：树木先为回收养分而处理叶片，叶绿素消失后秋叶颜色才显现。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。全文讨论的是落叶树秋季落叶与变色机制，不是常绿树如何越冬。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说土壤矿物质直接决定红叶或黄叶的颜色，重点是色素显露和新色素形成。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。落叶被动物利用并不是本讲的主要内容，也不能概括全文。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why can energized oxygen molecules become a problem during leaf disassembly?

options.text: They stop yellow and orange pigments from appearing in the leaf
They damage leaf tissues that are trying to send nutrients back into the tree
They cause the leaf's water supply to freeze too early
They make chlorophyll absorb less sunlight than normal

evidence.quote: These molecules wreak havoc, damaging the parts of the leaf that are in the process of recovering and transporting nutrients back to the rest of the tree.

private.explanationZh: 文中明确说，被激发的氧分子会“造成破坏”，伤害那些正在回收并运输养分的叶片部分。也就是说，它们之所以危险，是因为会干扰树木把氮、磷等重要养分重新收回体内的过程。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。黄橙色出现是因为绿色叶绿素被去除后原有色素不再被遮盖，不是因为氧分子阻止其出现。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文直接指出这些氧分子会损害正在回收和运输养分的组织。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有提到结冰或供水问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。问题不在于叶绿素吸光减少，而在于吸收到的能量无法用于光合作用，转而激发氧分子。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention that some trees build new red or purple pigments in autumn?

options.text: To argue that red leaves recover fewer nutrients than yellow leaves do
To show that red pigments replace all photosynthetic pigments throughout the year
To explain an added protective strategy that also makes some fall leaves look red
To suggest that unusual leaf colors come directly from the soil

evidence.quote: These new pigments tend to be red or purple in color, so trees that use them have red leaves in the fall

private.explanationZh: 这里的举例作用是补充前面的机制说明：有些树不仅分解叶绿素，还会额外制造遮光的新色素来保护叶片拆解过程，因此叶子会呈现红色或紫红色。也就是说，这句话是在解释“为什么有些树会变红”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有比较红叶和黄叶回收养分效率的高低。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说这些色素全年都取代其他光合作用色素，只说它们在秋季作为额外保护出现。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。红紫色新色素既有保护作用，也解释了部分树木秋天变红的现象。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中把颜色变化归因于叶内色素变化，不是土壤直接造成。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about a deciduous tree that could not recover nutrients from its old leaves?

options.text: It would no longer need to shed its leaves each year
It would continue photosynthesizing normally all winter
It would become less dependent on chlorophyll the next summer
It would have fewer stored materials available for growing new leaves in spring

evidence.quote: these exquisite gold and russet displays help deciduous trees recover as much as 50% of the nitrogen and phosphorus from their old leaves to help grow fresh new green ones in the spring.

private.explanationZh: 原文说秋叶展示帮助树木回收多达一半的氮和磷，用来在春天长出新叶。由此可推知，如果无法回收这些养分，树木在来年春天可用于新叶生长的储备就会减少。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说养分回收失败会让树木不再落叶，落叶本身仍与越冬成本有关。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说它会冬季继续正常光合作用，反而说落叶是为了避免高昂的越冬成本。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。是否依赖叶绿素与能否回收养分不是文中推出的关系。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这是根据“回收养分有助于春天长新叶”直接推出的结果。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-067-q01: 全文先否定“只是露出原有黄色橙色素”这类表层解释，随后集中说明真正原因是落叶树在秋天分解并回收叶片中的氮、磷等养分，叶色变化是这一回收过程中的结果。其余选项都偏离主题或引入文中未讨论对象。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-067-q02: 文中明确说，叶片拆解开始后，失去正常光合作用的叶绿素把多余能量传给氧分子，使其变得非常活泼，并“破坏正在回收和运输养分的叶片部分”。因此问题在于它们会伤害正在把养分送回树体的组织。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-067-q03: 说话者提到一些树会新建红色或紫色色素，是为了说明它们在秋季还有一层额外防护：先给叶绿素遮光，减少破坏；同时这也解释了为什么有些叶子会变红。其他选项不是原文意思，就是过度推断。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-067-q04: 原文说树木回收叶中养分并把它们储存在枝条里，等到来年春天再用来长新叶；最后还说能回收多达50%的氮和磷。由此可推知，如果不能回收旧叶养分，春天可用于长新叶的储备材料会更少。

public.prompt: What challenge and response is the talk mainly about?

options.text: How cities can become more resilient to rising seas and stronger storms
Why medieval European invasions shaped the location of modern cities
How Dutch storm barriers alone can solve future sea-level rise
Why wave power is already replacing most urban energy systems

evidence.quote: we still need to gather more information and develop more solutions to make them as resilient as possible.

private.explanationZh: 结尾总结明确指出，城市还需要更多信息和更多解决方案，以尽可能增强韧性。整篇讲座围绕的正是这个主题：面对海平面上升和更强风暴，城市应如何通过工程、自然和数据等多种方式提高抗灾能力。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了全文关于城市如何应对海平面上升和风暴威胁的核心内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。开头提到入侵者只是背景引入，重点不是城市选址或军事史。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中明确说连荷兰的努力都不够，说明不是单靠荷兰式屏障就能解决。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。波浪发电只是一个可能性例子，不是全文主旨，更不是已经普遍替代。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why will Dutch water defenses probably not be enough in the future?

options.text: The Netherlands plans to remove its existing dikes and locks
Sea levels are expected to keep rising dramatically over the coming centuries
European settlements no longer live near major waterways
Storm surge barriers can only protect cities from invaders

evidence.quote: with sea levels projected to rise by as much as a meter in the next 90 years, and three more meters in the following century, it's clear that even the efforts of the Dutch won't be enough.

private.explanationZh: 题干问的是“为什么不够”，原文直接给出原因：未来九十年海平面可能上升一米，之后一个世纪还可能再升三米。在这种趋势下，即使荷兰已经很擅长防水，也难以完全应对。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有提到荷兰要拆除现有防御设施。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文把海平面持续大幅上升作为荷兰防御不足的直接原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中恰恰说许多城市位于海岸和主要水道附近。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。防风暴潮屏障是为了抵御海水和风暴，不是防御入侵者。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention thirsty concrete that can soak up water quickly?

options.text: To explain why British cities no longer need evacuation plans
To show that concrete is replacing all natural storm defenses
To give an example of creative flood-adaptation ideas beyond simply blocking water out
To argue that water pumps are more effective than Dutch barriers

evidence.quote: Inventors in the U.K. have created thirsty concrete capable of soaking up 600 liters of water per square meter EVERY minute and funneling it away.

private.explanationZh: 这一例子出现在“也许根本无法把所有水都挡在外面，所以人们开始有创意”之后。它的修辞作用是用一个具体发明说明：城市正在探索不止一种适应洪水的方法，而不只是继续加高堤坝。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说英国城市因此不再需要疏散规划。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中并未说混凝土会取代所有自然防御，后面反而强调自然与工程结合。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。该例子是为了展示新的适应思路，说明应对城市洪水不只靠阻挡海水。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里不是比较水泵与屏障的优劣，而是在举创新方案的例子。

public.prompt: What does the speaker imply by saying that urban ideas need to flow out faster than the water can flow in?

options.text: Flooding is useful because it encourages creativity in cities
Urban rivers should be redirected away from coastal neighborhoods
Engineers should focus only on water pumps rather than data
Cities must put new resilience ideas into action quickly

evidence.quote: they just need to flow out faster than the water can flow in.

private.explanationZh: 这是一个比喻。前面说城市是创新热点，后面补充说这些想法必须比洪水来得更快地“流出来”，暗含的意思就是：有想法还不够，必须尽快把方案开发并落实，否则灾害速度会超过应对速度。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者并非认为洪水“有益”，而是强调应对洪水的紧迫性。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有主张把城市河流改道。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中提到数据、自然系统、工程设施等多种方案，不是只关注水泵。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这是对比喻含义的准确转述，强调方案实施速度的重要性。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-068-q01: 开头回顾城市防水历史，随后指出海平面上升和更强风暴使全球城市面临风险，接着列举多种创新适应办法，结尾强调提升韧性。因此主旨是城市如何应对上升海平面和更强风暴、变得更有韧性。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-068-q02: 文中直接说未来90年海平面可能上升多达1米，再往后一个世纪还会再升3米，因此“即使荷兰人的努力也不够”。所以关键原因是未来几个世纪海平面仍将显著上升。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-068-q03: 在说“单靠把所有水挡在外面可能并不可行”之后，讲话者举出英国发明的吸水混凝土，明显是把它当作一种富有创意的适应案例，说明除了修屏障之外，人们还在寻找别的应对办法。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-068-q04: 最后一句把“创意流动”与“洪水流入”对比，意思不是字面上的水流，而是强调城市虽然有很多好点子，但必须更快把这些提升韧性的想法落实出来，否则来不及应对风险。

public.prompt: What central idea about inheritance does the talk emphasize?

options.text: How learned experiences may influence later generations through epigenetic changes
Why DNA sequences never respond to environmental conditions
How young rats copy fear only by directly observing their parents
Why scientists fully understand human inheritance patterns already

evidence.quote: What is surprising is that the rats' pups - and their pups' pups - were also startled by the smell of acetophenone and had the same extra neurons as their fathers

private.explanationZh: 这段现象正是全文要解释的核心：父代获得的反应，为什么会在未经历同样刺激的后代身上再次出现。后文说明这与DNA序列本身未必改变有关，而与表观遗传调控方式有关，因此主旨是“经验如何通过表观遗传影响后代”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了全文围绕表观遗传继承展开的解释。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中并不是说DNA序列“永不”受环境影响，而是区分了DNA序列和基因读取方式。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文特别强调后代在未接触父亲和气味的情况下仍有反应，所以不是观察学习。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文末明确说在人类中还远远没有完全弄清楚这些联系。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what are epigenetic tags?

options.text: Special DNA sequences that permanently replace damaged genes
Chemical switches attached to DNA that regulate how strongly genes are used
Extra neurons that appear in the nose after fear training
Proteins that erase all inherited differences between cells

evidence.quote: Chemical switches attached to the DNA turn genes on and off or up and down, telling the machinery which proteins to produce and in what quantities.

private.explanationZh: 原文直接定义了表观遗传标签：附着在DNA上的化学开关，它们决定基因是开启、关闭，还是表达得更强或更弱，从而影响细胞制造哪些蛋白质、制造多少。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说它们会替换受损基因，重点是调控读取方式而非改写序列。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这几乎就是原文定义的同义改写。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。额外神经元是实验观察到的结果，不是表观遗传标签本身。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。表观遗传标签正是造成不同细胞行为差异的原因之一，不是消除差异。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention the famine study from Sweden?

options.text: To recommend food deprivation as a way to improve future generations
To prove that scientists know exactly which human epigenetic switches were changed
To suggest that unusual inheritance patterns may extend beyond laboratory rats
To show that famine changes human DNA sequences in the same way as mutations

evidence.quote: Rodents aren't the only creatures demonstrating this weird type of inheritance.

private.explanationZh: 在介绍完老鼠实验后，讲者转到瑞典的案例，是为了把论证范围从啮齿动物扩展到人类观察研究，说明这种“后天经历影响后代”的现象并非只出现在实验鼠身上。不过他随后也马上强调，人类中的具体机制仍不清楚。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲者明确否定这种做法，说这并不意味着应该让孩子挨饿。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文明确说科学家还不知道瑞典饥荒究竟触发了哪些具体开关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个案例的功能是把同类现象推广到老鼠之外。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中强调的是表观遗传层面的开关变化，而不是DNA序列突变。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the speaker's final remark about being grateful humans do not live in a laboratory?

options.text: Scientists should stop studying inheritance in animals altogether
Laboratory animals enjoy better health outcomes than humans do
Only laboratory environments can produce any epigenetic changes
Human lives are less controlled for research, which makes studies harder but is still preferable

evidence.quote: it's mostly because we humans don't live in the well-controlled environment of a laboratory. And for that, we should be grateful.

private.explanationZh: 这句话的潜台词是：人类环境复杂多变，所以研究起来不像实验室动物那样容易控制变量，因此科学结论更难建立；但从生活角度看，没有人愿意像实验动物那样被严格控制，所以这种“研究上的麻烦”本身也是值得庆幸的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有否定动物研究，恰恰是通过动物研究获得了重要线索。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有比较实验动物和人类谁更健康。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者没有说只有实验室才会产生表观遗传变化，只是说实验室更便于研究。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它完整表达了“研究更难，但生活上更好”的双重含义。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-069-q01: 全文围绕“学来的经历为何似乎能影响后代”展开：先讲受惊老鼠及其后代都怕同一种气味，再解释这不是DNA序列变化，而是表观遗传层面的改变。因此核心观点是后天经历可能通过表观遗传变化影响后代。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-069-q02: 原文明确定义：epigenetic tags 是“attached to the DNA”的 chemical switches，会把基因开大、关小、打开或关闭，告诉细胞机器该产生哪些蛋白、产多少。因此b与原文完全对应。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-069-q03: 在讲完老鼠后，讲话者说“Rodents aren't the only creatures...”并举出瑞典饥荒研究，作用就是把这种奇特继承现象从实验鼠扩展到人类观察案例，说明它可能不只存在于实验室老鼠身上。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-069-q04: 结尾先说人类研究难，是因为人类不像实验室那样处在高度受控环境中；接着又说对此我们应当感激。可推知：人类生活缺少实验室式控制，虽然让研究更困难，但从生活角度看这仍然更可取。

public.prompt: What overall message does the talk give about climate change?

options.text: Invisible greenhouse gases are already causing harmful effects around the world and people need to respond
Most local weather problems are unrelated to long-term climate change
Climate change mainly affects only coastal cities with sea walls
The best climate solution is to install more gas alarms in homes

evidence.quote: Carbon dioxide, methane and nitrous oxide are also invisible gases, but without un-ignorable alarms in place, they've been allowed to cause rapid and dangerous changes to the climate, oceans and ecosystems on earth.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接点明核心论点：几种看不见的温室气体已经在地球的气候、海洋和生态系统中造成快速而危险的变化。后面列举世界各地的具体影响，并在结尾呼吁行动，因此正确主旨必须同时包含“全球危害”和“需要回应”两层。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了全文的全球影响展示和行动呼吁。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。全文恰恰是在把各地问题与气候变化联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中举了干旱、野火、季风、冰雪减少等多种内陆与沿海影响，不限于海岸城市。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。家庭报警器只是开头的类比，不是作者提出的主要解决方案。

public.prompt: According to Alex, what climate-related problem is affecting Cambodia?

options.text: Record snowfall is disrupting winter tourism
Unpredictable monsoons are hurting rice farmers and fishers
Storm surges are threatening the electricity from the Guri Reservoir
The city is still recovering from Hurricane Sandy

evidence.quote: increasingly unpredictable monsoons threaten the livelihoods of the rice farmers and fishers who make up half the population.

private.explanationZh: Alex谈到柬埔寨时，明确指出越来越难以预测的季风正在威胁稻农和渔民的生计。这是该地区气候变化影响的具体表现，也是题干要求回忆的细节信息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这对应的是明尼苏达冬季运动和旅游的问题，不是柬埔寨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确复述了Alex关于柬埔寨的描述。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Guri Reservoir和电力风险是委内瑞拉的例子。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。飓风桑迪后的恢复是纽约市的情况。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker say that listeners may have their own climate-change story?

options.text: To argue that personal stories are more scientific than climate data
To show that the earlier examples were probably exaggerated
To connect the global examples to the audience's personal experience before urging action
To suggest that only people directly harmed by storms should respond

evidence.quote: Some of these experiences might sound familiar to you, or perhaps you have your own story about what climate change looks like where you live.

private.explanationZh: 这句话承接前面来自世界各地的案例，作用是把“他们的故事”转化为“你的故事”，让听众意识到气候变化并非遥远议题，而可能与自己所在地区直接相关。紧接着作者就提出“如果要行动，就得现在行动”，所以这里明显是在为行动呼吁做铺垫。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有把个人故事说成比科学数据更科学。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。作者不是削弱前文例子，而是在扩大其相关性。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确说明了这句话如何把全球案例转接到听众自身，并服务于后续劝说。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。作者呼吁更广泛的公众参与，不是只限于直接受灾者。

public.prompt: What does the speaker imply by saying we need to make ourselves as visible as possible?

options.text: Only political leaders are capable of understanding climate science
People should focus on making greenhouse gases visible with new sensors
Citizens need to move into the public eye by relocating to capitals
Public pressure must be noticeable enough for leaders to see support for emissions limits

evidence.quote: Since these gases are invisible, we need to make ourselves as visible as possible so the leaders of Earth can see that we, its citizens, want our nations to agree to a legally binding limit on greenhouse gas emissions.

private.explanationZh: 这里的“visible”不是字面上让身体更显眼，而是指公众声音和政治诉求要足够明显，让领导者看见公民要求对温室气体排放设定具有法律约束力的限制。也就是说，作者是在强调集体表达和施压的重要性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者强调的是公民要被看见，而不是说只有领导人懂科学。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。句子不是在讨论传感器技术，而是在讨论公众行动。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有建议搬到首都或通过地理迁移来提高可见度。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确转述了“让领导者看见公民诉求”的含义。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-070-q01: 开头把温室气体比作没有警报的危险隐形气体，中间由团队成员举出世界各地已发生的具体影响，结尾号召公众采取行动、推动减排。整体信息就是：这些看不见的气体已造成全球危害，人们需要回应。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-070-q02: Alex 明确说，在柬埔寨，“increasingly unpredictable monsoons”正在威胁稻农和渔民生计，今年雨来得晚还损害幼苗并让渔获量减半。所以正确答案是不可预测的季风正在伤害稻农和渔民。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-070-q03: 在列举多个地区案例之后，讲话者说听众也许也有自己的气候变化故事，紧接着就提出“如果我们要行动，就得现在行动”，并要求大家联系代表、企业、亲友。可见这句话的作用是把全球案例与听众个人经验连接起来，再推动行动。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-070-q04: 上下文先说这些气体看不见，所以人们要“as visible as possible”，好让地球领导者看见公民希望各国同意有法律约束力的排放限制。意思是公众压力必须足够显眼、足够被注意到，领导人才能看见并回应这种支持。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare river mouths to innie and outie belly buttons at the beginning of the talk?

options.text: To introduce two contrasting coastal shapes in a memorable way
To argue that river mouths and human bodies develop for the same reason
To show that most river mouths are biologically formed structures
To explain why people prefer deltas to estuaries

evidence.quote: Rivers also have innies and outies - not belly buttons, but mouths: where rivers flow into the sea, the land either pokes out or bends inward.

private.explanationZh: 开头这个比喻的作用不是讨论人体，而是把复杂的海岸地形差异用“innie / outie”这种听众容易想象的对比快速引出来。这样后文就能顺势解释：为什么有的河口向海突出，有的则向内凹入。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确说明了这个比喻的引入作用：形象地区分两种河口形态。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。作者并没有说河口与人体因为同一机制形成，只是借用类比帮助理解。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里没有把河口说成生物结构。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。作者不是在谈人们偏好哪种地貌，而是在解释成因。

public.prompt: According to the talk, under what conditions can a river mouth start building new land into the sea?

options.text: When sea level falls by more than 120 meters in a single season
When the river delivers enough sediment and the ocean is calm enough
When the river stops splitting into multiple channels
When coastal cities remove all dams and levees upstream
