public.prompt: What can be inferred from the talk's conclusion about modern population growth in India and China?

options.text: It created large populations there even though both countries started small.
It mattered less than temporary historical events such as wars and disasters.
It magnified an advantage created by large, fertile lands that had already supported many people.
It affected India strongly but had little effect on China.

evidence.quote: the large and fertile lands of India and China were able to sustain lots of people, such that when the era of modern population growth came around, they had a head start.

private.explanationZh: 结论部分把因果链收束起来：先有广阔而肥沃的土地长期养活了很多人，后有现代人口增长时代到来，把这个已有的人口优势进一步放大。所以最合理的推断是 C，而不是说现代增长凭空创造了巨大人口。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文强调的恰恰是两国并不是“小起点”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲者承认历史上有其他事件影响人口，但结论仍把重点放在土地条件和起点优势上。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确整合了“先有高起点，后被现代增长放大”这一结论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲座一直把印度和中国并列讨论，没有说其中一国几乎不受影响。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-131-q01: 讲话人明确说，印度和中国近300年的增长率其实和世界其他地区差不多，它们今天人口特别多，主要是因为在现代快速增长开始前它们的人口基数就已经很大。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-131-q02: 提到巴基斯坦和孟加拉国，是为了说明即便同样耕地丰富、人口密集，如果国土面积更小，也容纳不了像印度和中国那样多的人口。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-131-q03: 银行账户的比喻用来说明：如果两个对象按差不多的比例增长，起点更大的那个在绝对数量上会增加得更多，对应人口基数大的国家后来总人口也会更高。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-131-q04: 结论部分说印度和中国广阔且肥沃的土地长期养活了大量人口，所以当现代人口增长时代到来时，它们已经有了明显领先优势；现代增长是把这种既有优势进一步放大。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the talk?

options.text: To describe how rhythmic circuits control body movements in different animals
To explain why brain waves may be essential for memory and coordination in the brain
To show that beta and delta rhythms can fully diagnose unhealthy brains
To argue that artificial brain circuits already reproduce natural neural rhythms

evidence.quote: All over our brains all the time, millions of neurons are syncing up - sometimes for just a fraction of a second - and generating electrical ups and downs that we can record as brain waves.

Or maybe the rhythms in our brains are even more important than that - maybe their main function is to keep time for the brain - to keep all the parts sufficiently synchronized so that they can work together.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查全文主旨。讲者先说明大脑里普遍存在脑电波，然后讨论这些节律可能承担的功能，例如帮助记忆形成、让不同脑区保持同步并协同工作，所以核心是在解释脑电波为什么重要，而不是单纯介绍动物运动或睡眠分类。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这个选项只概括了开头用来引入话题的一个对比点。讲者很快把重点转到大脑中的节律，而不是持续讲动物运动控制。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确答案。全文围绕脑电波可能在记忆和脑区协同中的作用展开，准确概括了主要内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中只是举例说清醒时常见 beta rhythm、深睡时有 delta rhythm，并没有说这些模式足以完整诊断不健康的大脑。
optionRationalesZh.d: 结尾反而说科学家还没能建成人工脑回路，所以这个说法与原文相反。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what do certain sleep rhythms do?

options.text: Reactivate earlier neural patterns so lasting memories can form
Slow the brain down until beta rhythms disappear completely
Prevent neurons from firing together during deep sleep
Replace waking experiences with new unconscious signals

evidence.quote: During one stage of sleep, short, powerful rhythms deep in the brain trigger the same sequences of neurons that were active before sleep, creating lasting connections between them.
We've found that, without these rhythms, certain kinds of memories can't form.

private.explanationZh: 原文明确说，在某个睡眠阶段，强而短的节律会重新触发睡前活跃过的神经元序列，并在它们之间建立持久连接；没有这些节律，某些记忆就无法形成。因此答案是它们帮助记忆巩固。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确答案，完整对应“重新触发同样的神经元序列”和“形成持久连接”这两个关键信息。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有说这些节律的作用是让 beta rhythm 完全消失，beta 只是清醒状态的一个例子。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文恰恰说这些节律会让特定神经元序列再次被触发，而不是阻止它们一起活动。
optionRationalesZh.d: 讲者讨论的是记忆巩固，不是用新的无意识信号替换清醒经历。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker begin by discussing neural circuits that control movement?

options.text: To argue that movement is the only behavior generated by rhythmic neural activity
To show that animal motion is more complex than scientists once believed
To introduce rhythm as a basic neural feature before applying the idea to brain waves
To prove that coordinated motion and long-term memory rely on the same brain region

evidence.quote: Perhaps more surprising is that crazy complex neural circuits that aren't dedicated to movement also have rhythm:

private.explanationZh: 讲者先提运动回路，是为了先让听者接受“神经系统会产生节律”这个基础概念。随后用“更令人惊讶的是”转向并不专门负责运动的复杂脑回路，说明脑电波也是这种节律现象的一部分。这是典型的由熟悉现象引入更复杂主题的铺垫。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文明确说不只是控制运动的回路有节律，复杂的大脑回路也有，所以“only”错误。
optionRationalesZh.b: 开头并不是在回顾科学史或修正对动物运动复杂性的看法，而是在搭建论述框架。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确答案。运动回路的例子起的是引入作用，让后面关于脑电波的讨论更容易理解。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有说运动协调和长期记忆依赖同一个脑区，重点是它们都和节律有关。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the speaker's conclusion about neural rhythms?

options.text: They are mainly interesting because they make artificial brains easy to build
They are a temporary feature that evolved only in humans
They matter less than scientists once thought because they are fleeting
They are presented as a fundamental part of how nervous systems operate

evidence.quote: generating rhythms is sort of what nervous systems evolved to do in the first place - we really can't get anywhere without them.

private.explanationZh: 结尾这句话把神经节律提升到“神经系统本来就进化出来要做的事情”这一层级，意思不是它们只是偶然出现的附带现象，而是神经系统运作的基础机制之一。所以可以推断讲者把它们视为根本性的功能。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文说科学家还没能建成人工脑回路，因此不是“容易构建”的意思。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中谈的是 nervous systems 的演化功能，没有把这种节律限定为人类独有。
optionRationalesZh.c: 虽然节律是 fleeting 的，但讲者并没有因此淡化它们的重要性，反而强调“离不开它们”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确答案，准确概括了结尾对神经节律地位的推断。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-137-q01: 全文先说明大脑也会产生节律，再重点解释这些脑波可能与长期记忆形成、脑区同步和协同工作有关，所以主旨是说明脑波为何对记忆与协调可能至关重要。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-137-q02: 文中明确说睡眠中一种强而短的节律会触发睡前活跃过的同一组神经元序列，从而在它们之间形成持久连接，没有这些节律某些记忆就无法形成。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-137-q03: 开头先从控制运动的简单神经回路也具有节律说起，是为了先建立“节律是神经系统基本特征”这一概念，再转到更复杂的脑回路和脑波。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-137-q04: 结尾说产生节律本来就是神经系统进化出来要做的事，没有节律我们哪里也去不了，因此可推知说话者把神经节律视为神经系统运作的基础部分。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the talk?

options.text: Explaining how different animals became gigantic by overcoming different size limits
Comparing why insects and whales both became smaller over time
Describing why dinosaurs were the first animals to breathe air
Arguing that food supply matters more than physics in every case

evidence.quote: Every once in awhile, though, something crazy happens that allows an animal to get truly gigantic.

With this new krillion calorie diet, together with their air-breathing lungs and water-supported bodies, blue whales quickly tripled in size to become not just gigantic but truly the largest animals to have ever lived.

private.explanationZh: 全文围绕一个总问题展开：为什么有些动物会变得特别巨大。讲者依次用节肢动物、恐龙和蓝鲸举例，说明它们分别通过更高氧气、减轻骨骼重量、以及丰富食物加上既有优势等方式突破了不同的体型限制。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确答案，准确概括了全文通过多个案例解释“巨型化”条件和限制的结构。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中讲的是为什么一些动物变大，不是比较它们为什么变小。
optionRationalesZh.c: 讲者没有说恐龙是最早呼吸空气的动物，呼吸空气只是后面鲸类案例中的一个优势。
optionRationalesZh.d: 食物供应只是在蓝鲸部分特别关键，全文也反复强调氧气扩散和 square-cube law 这样的物理限制。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why were ancient arthropods able to grow much larger than usual?

options.text: Their exoskeletons became lighter after they moved into water
Higher atmospheric oxygen let enough oxygen diffuse through larger bodies
They evolved lungs that pulled in oxygen more efficiently
Colder climates reduced the energy cost of maintaining their tissues

evidence.quote: But about 300 million years ago, Earth's atmospheric oxygen levels spiked. With more oxygen in the air, arthropods' bodies could grow way bigger

private.explanationZh: 这道题对应节肢动物案例。原文先解释它们平时体型受限，是因为氧气只能扩散这么远；随后说大约三亿年前大气氧含量升高，因此更多氧气可以进入更大的身体内部，于是体型才得以显著增大。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有说它们通过进入水中或减轻外骨骼来变大，这属于鲸类部分的思路。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确答案，直接对应大气氧含量上升使更大身体也能获得足够氧气。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文明确说节肢动物是通过外骨骼“sponging up air”来呼吸，不是进化出肺。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有把巨型化原因归结为气候变冷或能量消耗下降。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker discuss the square-cube law before describing dinosaurs' air sacs?

options.text: To show that dinosaurs solved a breathing problem that arthropods never faced
To explain why long necks made dinosaurs weaker than other land animals
To argue that dinosaurs depended on atmospheric change for their large size
To establish the size constraint that the air-sac system helped dinosaurs overcome

evidence.quote: but at some point they hit a limit due to the so-called square cube law:
a system of air pockets and air sacs throughout their skeletons that allowed them to get bigger without getting heavier

private.explanationZh: 先讲 square-cube law，是为了先交代恐龙继续长大会遇到的根本物理难题：体重增长快于支撑能力。接着再引出空气囊和气腔系统，说明这项适应为什么重要，因为它正好帮助恐龙在不同比增重的情况下继续变大。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这里的重点不是呼吸方式差异，而是体型扩大带来的重量与支撑问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 讲者提到长而轻的脖子是这种适应带来的额外优势，不是说长脖子让它们更弱。
optionRationalesZh.c: 大气氧上升是前面节肢动物的案例，不是恐龙巨大化的关键解释。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确答案。square-cube law 提供问题背景，air sacs 则是解决该限制的突破。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about why blue whales became exceptionally large?

options.text: Water alone removed every important limit on body size
Their size increase happened mainly because fish in the ocean got smaller
They combined earlier advantages with a new food source dense enough to support further growth
They were the first mammals to return to the ocean after living on land

evidence.quote: But these giant creatures just didn't have enough food to get any giant-er than that.

With this new krillion calorie diet, together with their air-breathing lungs and water-supported bodies, blue whales quickly tripled in size to become not just gigantic but truly the largest animals to have ever lived.

private.explanationZh: 这题需要整合前后信息。讲者先说这些大型海洋哺乳动物原本受限于食物不足，后来海流变化带来高营养环境和大量浮游生物，于是它们在已有“肺呼吸+水体浮力支撑”优势的基础上，终于能够继续增大。因此关键不是单一因素，而是旧优势与新食物条件结合。

optionRationalesZh.a: 回到水中确实解决了一部分重量问题，但原文明确说它们后来仍然受限于食物不足，所以“water alone”不对。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有说鱼变小导致鲸类变大，真正的关键是营养上涌和浮游生物聚集。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确答案，准确概括了原文强调的组合条件。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文没有讨论它们是否是第一批重返海洋的哺乳动物，这不是推断重点。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-138-q01: 整段依次讲节肢动物、恐龙和鲸类如何分别突破氧气扩散、平方立方定律和食物限制等约束而变得巨大，主线就是不同动物如何跨越不同体型上限。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-138-q02: 文中直接说明约三亿年前大气中的氧含量上升，空气中更多氧气使节肢动物即使身体更大，氧气也仍能扩散到体内更深处。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-138-q03: 先解释平方立方定律，是为了说明陆地大型动物会因体重增长快于支撑强度而受限；随后才指出恐龙的气囊和空腔骨骼系统帮助它们在不过度增重的情况下继续变大。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-138-q04: 文中说鲸类先靠回到水中减轻重量、保留肺部呼吸富氧空气而长得很大，但真正进一步暴涨体型是因为洋流变化带来高营养并形成大量浮游生物，所以是旧优势加上新食物来源共同支持了更大体型。

public.prompt: What does the talk mainly explain?

options.text: Why brain size changes during growth within a single wild species
Why farmed fish and wild mammals react differently to fear
Why domesticated animals tend to have smaller brains than their wild relatives
Why body size is a poor predictor of brain size in all animals

evidence.quote: there's a less obvious physical change that virtually all domesticated animals share: shrunken brains.
In short, domesticated animals have formed long-term partnerships with humanity by literally losing their minds.

private.explanationZh: 全文从“驯化动物普遍脑变小”这一现象出发，接着解释它为什么会发生：驯化偏向选择更温顺、惊恐反应更弱的动物，而这类差异又与某些脑区缩小有关。结尾则用一句总结性的话收束这一解释，所以主旨就是说明驯化动物为什么比野生近亲脑更小。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中提到同一物种个体间体型和脑大小通常相关，但那只是背景，不是全文主要解释对象。
optionRationalesZh.b: 虽然举到 farmed trout，但并不是在比较鱼类和哺乳动物对恐惧的不同反应。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确答案，既点出现象，也概括了全文的解释方向。
optionRationalesZh.d: 讲者实际上说脑大小与体型通常高度相关，所以这个选项与原文相反。

public.prompt: According to the talk, when wolves and dogs are the same body size, what is true?

options.text: The wolves still have larger brains
The dogs usually have larger panic buttons
Their brain sizes are about the same
The dogs have brains that shrink only during adulthood

evidence.quote: But when you compare wolves and dogs, between individuals of the same size, the wolves have bigger brains, no matter what the body size is.

private.explanationZh: 这是文中的直接比较。讲者先说一般情况下体型和脑大小紧密相关，但接着指出在狼和狗的对比中，即使个体体型相同，狼的脑仍然更大，说明驯化带来了额外的脑缩小效应。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确答案，和原句完全一致。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有在这一句里比较 panic buttons 的大小，而且后文说更温顺的动物往往 panic button 更小。
optionRationalesZh.c: 讲者明确说 same size 的情况下狼脑更大，因此“不多”不对。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有说狗的脑只会在成年后缩小，这属于无根据添加。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker describe certain brain regions as the brain's "panic button"?

options.text: To suggest that domesticated animals never experience fear
To clarify which kind of brain function shrank and how that relates to tameness
To argue that brain shrinkage happened evenly across the entire brain
To show that animals with larger brains are always more aggressive

evidence.quote: What's more, across different domesticated animals, a disproportionate amount of the shrinkage happened in parts of the brain that monitor information from the outside world and tell animals when and how to freak out - sort of like the brain's panic button.
We know that, in general, animals with bigger panic buttons have a more sensitive fight or flight response, and animals with smaller panic buttons are naturally tamer.

private.explanationZh: “panic button”是一个帮助理解的比喻。讲者不是在进行神经解剖学命名，而是想让听者明白：缩小得特别明显的是那些处理外界刺激并触发惊恐/战逃反应的脑区，而这正好能解释为什么被驯化的动物会更温顺。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中说的是反应更不敏感、天生更温顺，不是完全没有恐惧。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确答案。这个比喻把“哪些脑区变小”与“为什么更容易被驯化”连了起来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文正好强调 shrinkage 是 disproportionate，也就是并非整个大脑均匀缩小。
optionRationalesZh.d: 讲者并没有提出“脑越大就一定越具攻击性”这种绝对结论。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the domestication process described in the talk?

options.text: Humans first selected animals with unusually large bodies and later reduced their brains
Brain shrinkage happened before people began choosing which animals to breed
Wild animals became tame mainly because they had less contact with the outside world
Human selection favored already tamer animals and then breeding pushed that trend further

evidence.quote: So those are the ones we probably would have tried to domesticate.
And, by breeding the tamest animals with each other, we shrunk their panic buttons - and therefore their brains - even further.

private.explanationZh: 这题需要把两步过程连起来理解。第一步，人类更可能一开始就挑选那些天生更温顺、panic button 更小的动物去驯化；第二步，再让这些最温顺的个体相互繁殖，于是这种特征被进一步强化，脑也继续缩小。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文把重点放在温顺程度和 panic button 上，不是先按体型筛选。
optionRationalesZh.b: 讲者的逻辑是人类选择和后续繁殖共同推动脑缩小，不是脑先无缘无故缩小再被选择。
optionRationalesZh.c: “监测外界信息的脑区变小”并不等于它们因为接触外界更少才变得温顺，这是因果倒置。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确答案，完整反映了“先挑更温顺的，再通过繁殖进一步强化”的推断。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-139-q01: 全文核心在解释一个共同现象：几乎所有被驯化动物的大脑都比其野生近亲更小，并进一步说明这与驯化过程中对温顺性的选择有关。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-139-q02: 原文明确说，当比较体型相同的狼和狗时，不论身体大小如何，狼的大脑都更大。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-139-q03: “panic button”是对那些处理外界信息并决定何时如何惊慌的脑区的通俗说明，用来帮助听者理解到底是哪些功能区域缩小了，以及这为什么会与更温顺有关。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-139-q04: 文中先说天生“panic button”较小、也就是更温顺的动物更可能被人类尝试驯化，随后又说人类继续繁殖最温顺的个体，使这种趋势进一步加强，所以应选人类先选择较温顺者，再通过繁殖强化这一特征。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the talk?

options.text: Courts no longer allow natural disasters to be mentioned in contracts
The "act of god" defense is becoming harder to use as prediction improves and human influence becomes clearer
Insurance companies created the phrase "act of god" to avoid paying for floods
Most environmental laws now define every disaster as human-caused

evidence.quote: But it's getting harder and harder to meet these criteria, starting with the reasonable precautions.
And the second criteria is also harder to meet because we're finding that an increasing number of natural disasters do have human fingerprints on them.

private.explanationZh: 全文主旨不是定义术语本身，而是说明为什么“act of god”这种法律抗辩越来越难成立。讲者围绕两条标准展开：一方面，预测能力提升让“合理预防措施”的要求提高；另一方面，越来越多灾害带有人类影响痕迹，因此第二条标准也更难满足。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文说今天在合同和保险条款中仍然能看到这个术语，不是已经不允许提及。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确答案，完整概括了讲者提出的两个主要原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中说法院自 1500 年代起就在使用该术语，并没有说是保险公司创造的。
optionRationalesZh.d: 讲者说越来越多自然灾害带有人类 fingerprints，但并没有说“每一个”灾害都被法律认定为人类造成。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what must someone prove to use the "act of god" defense?

options.text: That the event had no economic consequences and no human victims
That a religious authority classified the disaster as unprecedented
That reasonable precautions would not have prevented the damage and that no human influence was involved
That the event was larger than any previous flood, drought, or wildfire

evidence.quote: To use the "act of god" defense, you don't have to prove that a god actually caused the event, you just have to prove two things: one, that taking reasonable precautions couldn't have prevented the damage, and two, that the damage was caused by a natural force with no human influence.

private.explanationZh: 这是文中的直接定义性细节。讲者明确列出两项要求：其一，采取合理预防措施也无法避免损害；其二，损害来自没有人类影响的自然力量。二者缺一不可。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中从未要求必须没有经济损失或人员伤亡，这不是法律标准的一部分。
optionRationalesZh.b: 讲者特地强调不需要证明真有某个神造成事件，更不需要宗教权威认证。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确答案，准确复述了原文列出的两项标准。
optionRationalesZh.d: 是否“史上最大”并不是讲者给出的条件，关键在可预防性和人类影响。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker return to the 1903 butter case later in the talk?

options.text: To illustrate how limited forecasting once made the defense easier to sustain
To show that rail companies still win most climate-related lawsuits
To argue that butter losses are treated differently from other cargo losses
To prove that Kansas floods were entirely unrelated to natural forces

evidence.quote: For instance, back in 1903, the railway had put the butter car on what seemed like high enough ground to keep it safe, but they had little way of knowing how severe the flood would be.

private.explanationZh: 讲者重新提起 butter case，不是为了继续讲案件细节，而是为了把抽象标准落到具体历史情境中：在 1903 年，当时对洪水严重程度的预测能力很有限，所以铁路公司看似已经采取了合理措施，这就说明那时这种抗辩更容易成立。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确答案，准确说明了这个例子在论证中的功能。
optionRationalesZh.b: 讲者没有借此统计今天铁路公司是否常胜，只是在说明预测能力变化。
optionRationalesZh.c: 案件中的 butter 只是一个具体货物例子，并没有被说成法律上享有特殊待遇。
optionRationalesZh.d: 讲者不是要否认洪水是自然力量，而是在讨论当时人们对风险的可预见性。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about future "Act of God" lawsuits?

options.text: They will disappear because disasters are becoming less destructive
They will focus only on ocean warming rather than floods or wildfires
They will succeed more often because courts now accept broader definitions of nature
They may appear more often, but winning with the defense could become less likely

evidence.quote: we may actually be seeing more Act of God cases pop up in court in the future.
the success of the act of god defense may soon be relegated fully to the margarines.

private.explanationZh: 这题要整合结尾两层意思：一方面，自然灾害更频繁、破坏更大，相关案件可能会更多；另一方面，因为人类影响更容易被识别、预测技术更强，所以即便案件增加，这种抗辩本身未必更容易胜诉，反而可能更难成功。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文说灾害更常发生、破坏更大，不是更少、更轻。
optionRationalesZh.b: 讲者举了海洋变暖、洪水、干旱、野火等多类例子，没有说未来只会集中在单一主题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 结尾恰恰说 defense 的成功可能被边缘化，而不是更容易成功。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确答案，准确概括了“案件可能更多，但抗辩更难赢”的双重趋势。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-140-q01: 全篇论证“act of god”抗辩越来越难成立，原因有两个：人类对灾害的预测能力提高了，且越来越多灾害被发现带有人类活动痕迹。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-140-q02: 说话者明确列出两项条件：合理预防措施即使采取了也无法防止损害，而且损害必须由没有人类影响的自然力量造成。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-140-q03: 后面再次提到1903年黄油案，是为了具体说明过去对洪水规模了解有限，所以当时铁路公司把车放在看似足够高的位置就可能被视为合理；如今预测更强，这种辩护标准已提高。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-140-q04: 文末说自然灾害更常见、破坏更大，所以相关案件未来可能更多；但由于我们更能预测这些事件，也更能识别人类责任，这种抗辩要获胜反而可能更难。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly about?

options.text: Explaining why indirect migration routes can help birds travel more efficiently
Describing how all birds avoid strong winds by flying at night
Comparing bird migration with airplane traffic over Israel
Arguing that bird migration is becoming more random over time

evidence.quote: But instead, they often zig and zag along the way - adding an extra thousand miles or more to their journeys, which actually helps them get to their destinations faster.

Winds on Earth regularly blow in specific directions, and when birds fly with the wind at least partially at their backs, they can greatly increase their flying speed compared with birds flying against the wind.

private.explanationZh: 全文的中心不是单纯描述鸟类会迁徙，而是解释为什么它们常常不走直线，反而 zig and zag。讲者随后给出两类原因：顺风可以让飞行更快，而贴近陆地也能帮助不同鸟类利用热气流或避开海上风险。因此主旨是解释绕路为何反而更高效、更可行。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确答案，准确概括了“看似绕路，实际上更快或更安全”的核心解释。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有说所有鸟都靠夜飞避开强风，重点是借助有利风向和陆地条件。
optionRationalesZh.c: 飞机交通只在赞助段里一带而过，不是全文主要内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 讲者强调这些路线受风向和地形约束，并非越来越随机。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why do many small birds avoid flying over open water?

options.text: They need thermals that form only above oceans
They may be unable to survive storms far from land
They lose their sense of direction when no mountains are visible
They cannot fly as fast with tailwinds over water

evidence.quote: Small birds generally avoid water because they're too small to survive storms on the open seas.

private.explanationZh: 这是原文直接给出的原因。小型鸟类不是不会飞越海面，而是体型太小，在开阔海域遭遇风暴时生存能力不足，所以通常尽量避开长距离海上路线。

optionRationalesZh.a: thermals 是后面解释大型鸟类为何偏向陆地时提到的，不是小型鸟类避水的原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确答案，和原文表述一致。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有提到通过看山脉来导航，因此这不是依据。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文没有说尾风在海上对小鸟无效，真正问题是海上风暴生存风险。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker explain how thermals work?

options.text: To show why some birds rest on the ocean before continuing migration
To compare the flight of large birds with the swimming of marine animals
To explain why larger birds stay over land and follow routes shaped by rising air
To argue that uneven ground heating slows migration more than headwinds do

evidence.quote: Larger birds, on the other hand, stay above land because their wings are big enough compared to their bodies that they're able to get lift, without flapping, from huge columns of rising air called thermals, which form from uneven heating of the ground.
A bird in a thermal can simply rise with the hot air and then glide down to the next thermal.

private.explanationZh: 讲者详细解释 thermals 的作用，是为了说明大型鸟类为什么偏爱走陆地上空的路线。因为它们可以借助上升热气流获得升力、减少拍翅，从而长距离滑翔。这一解释直接支撑了前一句“larger birds stay above land”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有说它们会在海上休息，恰恰强调大型鸟类为了利用 thermals 更倾向于留在陆地上空。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这里没有拿鸟类飞行和海洋动物游泳做类比，重点是空气动力学条件。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确答案，thermals 的说明服务于解释大型鸟类路线选择。
optionRationalesZh.d: 地面受热不均是在说明热气流如何形成，不是在比较哪种因素更“拖慢”迁徙。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about migration hot spots?

options.text: They form mainly where birds stop to wait for winter temperatures to rise
They result from many birds being funneled toward a limited number of useful crossing points
They are created when local people feed migrant flocks on the ground
They appear only in places where small and large birds use identical flight strategies

evidence.quote: Sticking to land during their long migrations brings birds from all over the world together at a few key straits and land bridges along their routes.
these hot spots fill with flocks of squawking foreigners, both in the sky and on the ground below.

private.explanationZh: 这里需要把“few key straits and land bridges”与后面的“hot spots fill with flocks”连起来理解。许多鸟因为风向和陆地条件而不得不经过少数关键通道，所以这些地方就会出现高度聚集的迁徙热点。这是由路线汇聚推出来的结果。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有说这些热点主要是鸟在等待气温回升的地方，而是迁徙路线上的汇聚点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确答案，准确概括了有限通道把大量鸟类“漏斗式”集中到一起的逻辑。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有提到人类喂食导致热点形成，这是无关信息。
optionRationalesZh.d: 小鸟和大鸟迁徙策略并不完全相同，但它们仍可能在关键地峡或陆桥附近汇合，所以“only”错误。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-141-q01: 全文主要解释鸟类为什么不直飞而是曲折迁徙，因为借助顺风、避开开阔水域并利用陆地热气流，这些看似绕路的路线反而更高效。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-141-q02: 文中直接说小型鸟类通常避开水面，因为它们太小，无法在远离陆地的海上风暴中存活。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-141-q03: 解释热气流的作用，是为了说明大型鸟类为什么偏向留在陆地上空飞行：它们可以利用陆地受热不均形成的上升气流获得升力，从而影响迁徙路线。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-141-q04: 前文说许多鸟为了顺风和依赖陆地条件，会集中经过少数海峡与陆桥；后文又说这些热点每年会聚满鸟群，因此可推知热点是大量迁徙鸟被“汇聚”到有限有效通道的结果。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the talk?

options.text: How Yukon squirrels changed the number of cones produced by spruce trees.
Why some trees produce seeds together in huge bursts and what advantages that strategy can provide.
Why white spruce trees depend entirely on squirrels to spread their seeds.
How climate change permanently increased cone production in 2010.

evidence.quote: This wasn't a freak event - it's a phenomenon called masting, in which all the plants of a particular species in an area go totally nuts in a big baby-making bonanza.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查主旨。讲者先用2010年白云杉大量结球果的现象引出“masting”这一概念，随后整段都在解释树木为什么会同步大量繁殖，以及这样做在授粉和避免种子被吃光方面有什么好处。因此正确答案必须同时概括“同步大量结果”与“这样做的原因/优势”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。松鼠只是开头引入现象时出现的背景角色，并没有“改变”云杉产球果的数量。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。它准确概括了全文核心：树木为何会同步大规模结实，以及这种策略可能带来的繁殖优势。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。文中提到很多树依赖风传播花粉，而不是完全依赖松鼠传播种子。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。2010年只是一个例子，讲者没有说气候变化永久性提高了产量。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about trees that rely on wind pollination?

options.text: They reproduce best when neighboring trees release pollen and flowers at the same time.
They can reproduce successfully even if only one tree flowers in a region.
They save energy by producing fewer flowers during mast years.
They use squirrels to carry pollen between distant trees.

evidence.quote: For species that rely on the wind to carry male pollen from one tree to the female flowers of another, the best way for any one tree to make lots of babies is for ALL the trees to make lots of pollen and lots of flowers.

private.explanationZh: 这道题要求根据原文推断。原文明确说，对于依赖风传播花粉的树种，任何一棵树想成功繁殖，最好的办法是所有树都同时产生大量花粉和花。这说明同步开花、同步释放花粉能提高繁殖成功率。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。它是对原文“ALL the trees”同时产生大量花粉和花的直接合理转述。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。原文强调的是区域内多棵树一起高产，而不是单棵树单独开花也能同样成功。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。文中说大规模繁殖很耗能，并不是说在mast year里会减少开花。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。花粉传播方式是风，不是松鼠。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker discuss squirrels eating acorns?

options.text: To show that squirrels directly control when oak trees enter mast years.
To provide an example of how irregular seed production can overwhelm seed predators.
To argue that acorns are nutritionally better than spruce cones.
To explain why squirrels migrate when oak trees stop producing seeds.

evidence.quote: if the oaks keep acorn production low most years, the squirrel population will also stay low, and then, during so-called mast years, those few squirrels can stuff themselves silly without putting a dent in the acorn supply, leaving plenty behind to sprout and grow.

private.explanationZh: 讲者提到松鼠吃橡子，不是为了转换成“松鼠专题”，而是为了举一个非常具体的例子，说明树木如果平时少产种子、偶尔暴增，就能让以种子为食的动物数量维持在较低水平。这样到了丰产年，捕食者再怎么吃，也吃不完全部种子，剩下的就有机会发芽成长。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。文中没有说松鼠决定橡树何时进入mast year，决定因素在树木自身策略。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。这个例子正是用来说明“捕食者饱和”机制：偶发高产会让种子捕食者吃不尽。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。讲者没有比较橡子和云杉球果的营养价值。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。文中没有讨论松鼠迁徙，重点在松鼠数量与种子供给之间的关系。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what could masting help some trees do?

options.text: Grow taller than neighboring species.
Survive warmer spring temperatures.
Extend the survival of their species by leaving more seeds to grow.
Store extra water underground for future years.

evidence.quote: So for some trees, masting could be a way to extend the life of their species, without becoming feces.

private.explanationZh: 这是结尾处的直接信息。讲者总结说，对某些树来说，masting能够帮助它们延续物种，因为更多种子能逃过捕食并成长为新树。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。文中讨论的是繁殖成功和物种延续，不是树高竞争。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。温暖春天气温在文中只是可能的天气线索，不是masting带来的结果。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。它准确概括了结尾句子的意思：通过让更多种子存活，树木得以延续其物种。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。文中没有提到储水。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-152-q01: 开头先提出2010年云杉大量结球果的现象，后文主要解释这种同时大量结果的masting现象，以及它可能带来的授粉和防种子捕食者等优势。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-152-q02: 原文明确说，对依赖风传播花粉的树种来说，任何一棵树想多繁殖，最佳方式就是所有树同时产生大量花粉和花，因此应选同步发生。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-152-q03: 讲松鼠吃橡子是为了举例说明：树木在平年少产、丰年暴产，可以让少量捕食者吃不完，从而留下更多种子发芽。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-152-q04: 结尾明确说，对一些树来说，masting可以让更多种子留下来发芽生长，从而延续物种，而不是长高、耐热或储水。

public.prompt: What central question about extreme old age does the talk mainly address?

options.text: Why the risk of death may stop rising at extreme old age and what that suggests about human lifespan.
Why childhood diseases are now the main limit on human longevity.
How parents can prevent harmful genes from being passed to their children.
Why most centenarians are expected to live beyond 127.

evidence.quote: Eventually, the force field wears off entirely, so while people's chances of dying at that age are really high, they don't go up any further because there's no protection left to lose.

private.explanationZh: 全文围绕一个核心问题展开：为什么人在极高龄时死亡概率可能不再继续上升，以及如果真是这样，这对人类寿命上限意味着什么。讲者先给出不同年龄的死亡概率，再用自然选择的解释说明曲线为何可能趋平，最后讨论这对刷新长寿纪录的影响。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。它完整概括了讲座的两个主轴：死亡率曲线为何趋平，以及这对寿命极限的含义。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。童年疾病只是为了说明自然选择如何清除早发致命基因，不是全文主题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。亲代与子代关系只是论证中的一环，不是讲座主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。讲者并没有说“大多数”百岁老人会活过127，恰恰相反，那只是极少数人的概率事件。

public.prompt: According to the speaker, what is a 100-year-old's chance of dying in the next year?

evidence.quote: a 100-year-old has a 50% chance.

private.explanationZh: 这是开头列举年龄与死亡概率时给出的具体数字信息。讲者明确说，一位100岁老人下一年死亡的概率是50%。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。5%对应的是80岁老人，而不是100岁老人。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。文中没有把100岁老人的下一年死亡概率说成10%。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。原文直接给出100岁老人下一年死亡概率为50%。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。80%不是讲者提供的数字。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about harmful mutations that strike very late in life?

options.text: Natural selection removes them especially efficiently because older adults are weaker.
They are less likely to be removed because they usually act after reproduction and child-rearing.
They disappear quickly because centenarians rarely pass genes to children.
They become harmless once a person reaches adulthood.

evidence.quote: In short, natural selection's ability to eliminate harmful genes gets lower and lower as the age at which those genes strike gets higher and higher.

private.explanationZh: 讲者的推理链条是：越早发作的致命基因，越容易在自然选择中被淘汰；越晚发作，对生殖和抚养后代的影响越小，因此越不容易被清除。题目问“可以推断什么”，正确答案应当抓住这种晚发基因更容易保留下来的逻辑。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。原文说的是随着发作年龄升高，自然选择清除这些基因的能力下降，而不是更有效。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。它准确概括了原文逻辑：晚发作意味着通常已完成繁殖和抚养，因此被淘汰的压力更小。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。文中没有说这类基因会“很快消失”，结论恰好相反。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。成年后发作并不等于无害，原文讨论的是基因能否被自然选择清除，不是危害是否消失。

public.prompt: What does the speaker suggest about the current longevity record when the whole living population is considered?

options.text: It will remain unbroken because death rates after 100 are too high.
Only current centenarians have a realistic chance to break it.
A small number of people are likely to exceed it.
Anyone who reaches 100 will probably live past 110.

evidence.quote: odds are that 15 people will beat that record

private.explanationZh: 结尾处讲者把问题转成“人数游戏”。如果死亡率在极高龄阶段大致稳定，那么在总体人口足够大的情况下，虽然单个人突破纪录很罕见，但仍然会有少数人有机会做到。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。讲者并没有说纪录一定不会被打破，反而说按全球人口算，很可能会有人超过它。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。讲者明确区分了“当前百岁老人”与“今天活着的全部人口”，后者才带来更大的概率。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。它准确转述了“可能有15个人打破纪录”这一核心结论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。原文没有说活到100岁就“很可能”超过110岁，反而强调人数会快速减少。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-153-q01: 全文围绕一个核心问题展开：为什么极高龄时死亡概率可能不再继续上升，以及这对人类寿命上限意味着什么。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-153-q02: 开头直接给出数字：100岁的人下一年死亡概率是50%。这是明确细节信息。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-153-q03: 原文论证自然选择随着致死年龄升高而越来越难清除有害基因，因为它们往往发生在生育和抚养后期甚至之后，所以更可能保留下来。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-153-q04: 结尾说如果把今天地球上全部人口都算进去，概率上会有少数人打破当前寿命纪录，因此应选“小数人可能超过纪录”。

public.prompt: What is the main idea of the talk?

options.text: Apex predators and mesopredators usually have identical effects on food webs.
Human pressure on apex predators can allow mesopredators to expand and disrupt ecosystems.
Mesopredators became dominant only because prey animals disappeared.
Reintroducing wolves has solved predator-related problems worldwide.

evidence.quote: These so-called hunters are the mesopredators. They're replacing apex predators all over the world, and they do things differently.

private.explanationZh: 全文主线非常清楚：人类削弱或消灭顶级捕食者之后，中型捕食者开始上位，而它们的扩张会引发一连串生态后果。后文举出多个例子和黄石公园的案例，都是为了展开这一主旨。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。讲者强调的是两者“做事方式不同”，并非影响相同。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。它准确概括了人类打压顶级捕食者、中级捕食者扩张、生态系统受扰动这条主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。文中没有说它们上位仅仅因为猎物消失，关键是顶级捕食者减少以及人类环境提供机会。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。黄石案例只是一个局部例子，不能扩大成“全球都已解决”。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about why mesopredators often succeed near humans?

options.text: They depend on humans to protect them from all other species.
They benefit both from reduced pressure by apex predators and from food opportunities around people.
They reproduce only in cities and suburbs.
They avoid human waste and prefer untouched habitats.

evidence.quote: Living near humans is rich (ooh) We're sitting here eating up trash (and rats!)

private.explanationZh: 这道题需要把中段多条信息整合起来理解。前面说过顶级捕食者减少后，中级捕食者少了竞争和被捕食压力；这里又补充说，靠近人类生活还能获得垃圾、老鼠等额外资源。所以它们成功并不是单一原因，而是两方面叠加。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。文中没有说人类会主动保护所有中级捕食者，只说人类活动造成了有利条件。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。它整合了“顶级捕食者减少”和“人类环境提供食物资源”两层原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。文中没有把其繁殖地点限定为城市或郊区。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。原文恰恰说它们会利用人类环境中的垃圾和老鼠。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention Yellowstone?

options.text: To show that removing coyotes always increases wolf populations.
To provide an example of how bringing back an apex predator can help restore ecological balance.
To argue that every ecosystem should contain the same predator species.
To explain where mesopredators first evolved.

evidence.quote: Reintroducing wolves to Yellowstone helped restore the whole ecosystem.

private.explanationZh: 黄石公园的例子在篇章中的作用非常明确：前文一直在讲顶级捕食者消失会放大中级捕食者并扰乱生态；这里则给出一个反向案例，说明重新引入顶级捕食者后，生态网络能够得到修复，因此是用作“恢复平衡”的实例。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。文中不是在讨论“移除郊狼必然增加狼”，而是在讲引入狼之后的系统性恢复。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。它准确说明了黄石案例的修辞功能：举例证明重新引入顶级捕食者可能带来生态恢复。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。讲者没有主张所有生态系统都必须拥有同一种捕食者。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。黄石案例不是在讲中级捕食者的起源。

public.prompt: What does the speaker suggest is likely in many places going forward?

options.text: Mesopredators will continue rising because many people do not want large apex predators nearby.
Apex predators will quickly recover on their own without human help.
Farmers will stop hunting predators that threaten livestock.
Food chains will become simpler because mesopredators will disappear.

evidence.quote: But most people don't want a wolf in their backyard - or a lion, tiger or bear - so mesopredators will probably continue to rise to the top of the charts.

private.explanationZh: 结尾处讲者给出的是面向未来的判断。虽然重新引入顶级捕食者在某些地方有效，但多数人并不愿意让狼、狮子、老虎或熊出现在自己附近，因此在很多人类主导的环境里，中级捕食者继续占上风是更可能的趋势。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。它直接转述了讲者的结论：由于人们不愿大型捕食者靠近，中级捕食者还会继续上升。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。讲者没有说顶级捕食者会自行快速恢复。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。文中没有预测农民会停止猎杀威胁牲畜的捕食者。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。讲者预测的是中级捕食者继续上升，而不是消失。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-154-q01: 主旨是：人类使顶级捕食者衰退，给中型捕食者腾出空间，后者上升后会引发营养级联并扰乱生态系统。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-154-q02: 文中同时给出两层原因：顶级捕食者减少后中型捕食者被“释放”，而且靠近人类还能吃垃圾和老鼠等资源，所以它们常在有人类的地方成功。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-154-q03: 提到黄石公园是作为实例，说明重新引入狼这种顶级捕食者后，可以压制中型捕食者并帮助整个生态系统恢复平衡。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-154-q04: 结尾明确说，多数人不希望狼、狮、虎、熊出现在自家后院，因此中型捕食者未来很可能继续上升到食物网顶端。

public.prompt: What point does the talk mainly make?

options.text: Similar-looking words and animals usually come from the same ancestors.
A few surface similarities are enough to reconstruct family trees accurately.
Superficial similarities can mislead us about relationships, so we need multiple lines of evidence.
Language evolution is much easier to study than biological evolution.

evidence.quote: So, It turns out, if you are trying to determine how two things are related, you can't just use one or two data points, even if they're VERY pointy.

private.explanationZh: 结尾句就是全文主旨的总结。前面无论是词语例子还是动物例子，都是为了说明“表面相似”并不足以证明真正的亲缘或历史关系。要判断两者如何相关，必须看不止一两个线索。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。全文恰恰在反驳“看起来像就多半同源”的想法。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。讲者明确说不能只用一两个数据点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。它准确概括了文章的警示：表面相似可能误导，判断关系需要更多证据。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。文章没有比较哪一个领域“更容易”研究。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare "emoticon" and "emoji"?

options.text: To show that two similar-looking terms can have separate origins.
To argue that English borrowed most of its technology words from Japanese.
To prove that modern internet language evolves faster than biology.
To show that pronunciation is a better guide than meaning when tracing origins.

evidence.quote: But "emoticon" is a combination of the English words "emotion" and "icon", while the word "emoji" combines the Japanese words for picture and character; the two words don't hint at any hidden relationship between English and Japanese.

private.explanationZh: 这里的“emoticon”和“emoji”是讲者用来打破直觉的第一个例子。它们看起来、听起来都很像，意义也接近，但来源并不相同，因此这个例子用来说明“相似不等于同源”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。这个例子正是为了说明两个相似的词也可能来自完全不同的语言来源。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。讲者没有讨论英语是否大量借用日语科技词汇。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。例子只是说明词源判断会被表面相似误导，不是在比较演化速度。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。讲者没有说发音比意义更可靠，重点是不能凭单一表面特征下结论。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the bird example?

options.text: Carrion-eating birds always come from the same evolutionary branch.
DNA evidence is useful mainly when species look completely different.
Similar ecological pressures can produce similar traits in unrelated species.
Bald-headed birds are more closely related to penguins than to flamingos.

evidence.quote: For example, biologists long thought that these two birds must be sibling species, but recent DNA testing suggests that they're not even on the same evolutionary branch, and may be less related than penguins and flamingos; they evolved their bald headedness independently - probably because they both pursued the same carrion-eating lifestyle.

private.explanationZh: 鸟类例子说明：即使两个物种在外形上非常相似，这种相似也可能是独立演化出来的，而不是来自近亲关系。讲者进一步指出，它们都吃腐肉，因此相似的生态压力可能让不太相关的物种发展出相似特征。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。文中并没有说吃腐肉的鸟“总是”来自同一演化支系。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。DNA证据在这里之所以重要，是因为外表相似会误导，而不是只在完全不同的物种之间才有用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。它准确概括了例子的推论：相似生态位可能导致不相关物种独立演化出相似特征。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。文中说这两只鸟彼此未必同支系，并拿企鹅和火烈鸟作比较，但没有得出本选项这种关系判断。

public.prompt: How is the talk mainly organized?

options.text: It lists random examples of unusual words before defining evolution.
It compares how life and language change, gives misleading-similarity examples from both, and ends with a caution about using too little evidence.
It argues that language evidence is reliable but biological evidence is not.
It focuses on internet slang first and then shifts to a sponsor message about websites.

evidence.quote: Life and language evolve in many of the same ways: a group splits, and over time, small changes occur in genetic traits and particular words.
So, It turns out, if you are trying to determine how two things are related, you can't just use one or two data points, even if they're VERY pointy.

private.explanationZh: 篇章结构是先搭起“生命演化”和“语言演化”的类比框架，然后分别举出词语和生物中的误导性相似案例，最后回到一个总的结论：不能只看少数表面证据。这种从总体类比到案例展开、再到总结警示的组织方式最符合全文。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。文章一开始就先定义和比较演化方式，并不是先随机列例子。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。它完整概括了文章的推进顺序：先类比，再举双领域例子，最后给出方法论警示。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。讲者没有说语言证据可靠而生物证据不可靠；两者都可能被表面相似误导。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。网站赞助部分只是结尾广告，不是全文的主要组织逻辑。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-155-q01: 全文反复论证表面相似性会误导我们判断亲缘关系，因此不能只看一两个相似点，而要依靠多方面证据。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-155-q02: “emoticon”和“emoji”被拿来做例子，是为了说明两个看起来、听起来都相似的词，其实来源可以完全不同。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-155-q03: 鸟类例子说明，即使两个物种不近亲，只要面临相似生态压力，比如食腐生活方式，也可能独立演化出相似外形特征。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-155-q04: 结构上先说生命和语言都能演化、可据此追溯来源，再分别举语言和生物中的误导性相似例子，最后总结不能只凭少量数据判断关系。

public.prompt: What aspect of Earth's surface is the talk mainly explaining?

options.text: Why Earth's surface has two common elevation levels instead of one smooth middle range.
Why Mount Everest and the Mariana Trench are gradually becoming the same height.
How Venus developed deeper oceans than Earth.
Why sea level on Earth stays constant over time.

evidence.quote: Earth's surface has two sort of levels to it. The main reason is that Earth's outer layer, the crust, is made up of two different materials: it's all rock, but the rock that makes up the seafloor is denser than the rock that makes up the continents.

private.explanationZh: 全文围绕“为什么地球表面的高度分布是双峰的”展开。讲者先指出直觉上我们可能以为地球高度分布应当像单峰曲线，再说明真实情况是两个常见高度层级，并把原因归结为海洋地壳与大陆地壳的密度和厚度差异。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。它准确概括了讲座所解释的核心问题：地球为何呈现两个常见高度层级。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。珠峰和马里亚纳海沟只是极端点的引入，不是全文主旨。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。文中用金星作对比，但没有讨论金星如何形成更深海洋。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。文章重点不是海平面是否恒定，而是全球地表高度分布。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what usually happens when an oceanic plate collides with a continental plate?

options.text: The continental plate sinks because it is denser.
The oceanic plate sinks back into the planet.
Both plates stay at the same height and stop moving.
Both plates rise equally and form a mountain range.

evidence.quote: when the two plates collide, the oceanic one sinks back down into the planet.

private.explanationZh: 这是板块碰撞部分的直接信息。讲者明确说，海洋板块比大陆板块更致密，因此两者相撞时，下沉回地球内部的是海洋板块。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。更致密的是海洋板块，不是大陆板块。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。原文直接说明海洋板块会下沉回地球内部。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。文中没有说两块板块会停住不动。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。双方都上升形成山脉对应的是大陆板块与大陆板块相撞，不是海洋板块撞大陆板块。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare Earth with Venus?

options.text: To show that a single-hump elevation pattern is possible and highlight how unusual Earth's pattern is.
To argue that Venus once had continents just like Earth's.
To prove that Venus has more land above sea level than Earth.
To explain that Earth and Venus have identical crust densities.

evidence.quote: And if we were talking about our sister planet Venus, we'd be right - this is what the elevation-distribution curve looks like for Venus.

private.explanationZh: 金星在这里的作用是“对照组”。讲者先说如果按照直觉画一个普通单峰分布，那放在金星上反而是对的；接着再指出地球并不是这样。这个比较就是为了突出地球双峰分布的特殊性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。比较金星的目的正是说明单峰分布并非不可能，从而反衬地球的双峰分布更特殊。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。文中没有讨论金星是否曾经有和地球一样的大陆。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。金星没有被拿来讨论“海平面以上陆地面积”这个问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。讲者没有说两颗行星地壳密度相同。

public.prompt: What can be inferred if Earth's surface followed a more Venus-like elevation distribution?

options.text: Most ocean floor would rise above the continents.
Land animals would have much more space than they do now.
Only a small portion of the planet would remain above sea level.
Mountain ranges would disappear because plate collisions would stop.

evidence.quote: if our planet's surface was more like Venus's, with it's so-called normal distribution, only about 5% of it - an area a little smaller than Africa - would be above sea level, leaving not much space for landlubbers like us.

private.explanationZh: 这是结尾的推论题。讲者说如果地球像金星那样呈现所谓“正常”的单峰高度分布，那么海平面以上的面积只会剩下大约5%。这意味着陆地会非常少。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。文中没有说海底会整体升到大陆之上。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。讲者明确说那样的话留给陆生生物的空间会更少，不是更多。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。原文直接说明只有大约5%的地表会在海平面以上，因此高于海平面的部分会很小。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。这里讨论的是高度分布结果，不是板块碰撞会不会停止。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-156-q01: 整段讲的是地球地表高度分布为何不是单峰，而是大陆和海底分别形成两个常见高度层次。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-156-q02: 原文明说海洋板块密度更大，所以与大陆板块碰撞时，海洋板块会下沉回地球内部。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-156-q03: 比较金星是为了说明单峰式高度分布是可能存在的，从而突出地球这种双峰分布有多特别。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-156-q04: 结尾明确推论：如果地球像金星那样更接近正态单峰分布，那么高于海平面的部分只会占很小一部分。

public.prompt: What is the main explanation offered in the talk for penguins living near the equator?

options.text: Why Antarctic penguins are losing food because algae are disappearing.
How Galapagos penguins migrated from the Arctic into tropical waters.
How a cold ocean current lets penguins live and feed near the equator.
Why most tropical islands have dense populations of penguins.

evidence.quote: But over 4000 miles north, in the tropical Galapagos Islands, you can also find penguins, because of something called the Cromwell Current.
The current helps the penguins stay cool, but even more importantly, it helps them eat.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查主旨概括。开头先提出“赤道附近也有企鹅”这个反常现象，随后说明原因是Cromwell洋流，而且这个洋流不仅让企鹅保持凉爽，还支持它们获取食物，所以全文核心就是冷洋流如何让企鹅在赤道附近生存。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有讨论南极企鹅因藻类减少而挨饿，重点不是南极生态危机。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有说它们从北极迁来，后文反而强调企鹅起源于南方。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它同时概括了冷却作用和食物链支持，最符合全文主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中说加拉帕戈斯是特殊例外，不是说多数热带岛屿都有大量企鹅。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare the ocean to salad dressing?

options.text: To show that ocean layers can stay separated until something mixes them.
To argue that nutrients are heavier than all other materials in seawater.
To suggest that penguins depend more on oil than on algae for food.
To explain that vinegar-like currents are unique to the Galapagos.

evidence.quote: Essentially, the ocean is like a salad dressing, where the denser vinegar stays trapped below the oil until you shake it up.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查修辞目的。说话者用沙拉酱分层来类比海洋分层，是为了把“营养在下、阳光在上、平时不易混合”这一抽象机制讲得更直观。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个比喻的重点就是说明上下两层会分开，只有被搅动时才会混合。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。比喻不是在精确比较各种物质重量，而是在说明分层现象。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。oil和vinegar只是类比材料，不是在讨论企鹅真正吃什么。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有提出什么“像醋一样的洋流”类别，这只是帮助理解的比喻。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why are there no penguins in the world's cold northern oceans?

options.text: Northern oceans contain too few nutrients for any penguin species.
Penguins evolved in the Southern Hemisphere, and warm nutrient-poor water blocks northward spread.
Northern predators eliminated the birds before they could establish colonies.
The Cromwell Current carries penguins back toward Antarctica each year.

evidence.quote: there's simply too much warm, nutrient-poor water between them and the Arctic.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查细节。原文解释说企鹅虽然更常见于寒冷地区，但它们起源于南方，而通往北极之间又隔着大片温暖、贫营养的海水，所以无法自然扩散到北方冷海。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说北方海洋对所有企鹅都缺乏营养。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确整合了演化起源和迁移障碍两个原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文完全没有提到北方捕食者消灭企鹅。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。Cromwell洋流解释的是加拉帕戈斯环境，不是把企鹅送回南极。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the Galapagos penguins?

options.text: They mainly live there because the islands have no competing seabirds.
They can tolerate tropical heat without any help from ocean conditions.
They regularly migrate between Isabela Island and the Arctic.
They survive there because local ocean mixing creates an unusual cold, food-rich habitat.

evidence.quote: The current helps the penguins stay cool, but even more importantly, it helps them eat.
just a hair or a feather above the equator.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查推断。前文说明洋流既让企鹅降温，又通过促进食物链帮助它们生存；后文又强调这些企鹅几乎生活在赤道上。由此可推断，它们能在那里存在，是因为局部海洋混合制造了一个反常的冷而且食物丰富的栖息环境。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有提供关于海鸟竞争是否存在的信息。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文恰恰强调它们需要冷水洋流帮助降温和觅食。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说它们会在赤道和北极之间迁徙。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个选项把前后的关键信息整合成合理推断。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-157-q01: 开头先提出赤道附近也有企鹅，随后全文解释克伦威尔寒流如何上升、带来降温与食物，因此主旨是寒流让企鹅能在赤道附近生存并觅食。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-157-q02: “沙拉酱”类比紧接在“表层有阳光、深层有营养、两层不易混合”之后，用来说明海水分层会保持分离，必须靠风或洋流把它们搅混。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-157-q03: 原文明确说企鹅“evolved in the South”，而且它们与北极之间隔着太多温暖、营养贫乏的海水，所以无法扩散到北方寒冷海域。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-157-q04: 文中说明当地寒流上涌既让环境更冷，又把营养带到有阳光的表层，使食物链繁盛，因此可推断加拉帕戈斯企鹅依赖这种异常的寒冷且食物丰富的栖息地。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly about?

options.text: Why eating feces can help some animals gain nutrients or microbes.
Why humans should imitate animals by eating more feces.
How predators force animals to hide their waste.
Why only baby animals can safely digest feces.

evidence.quote: But for lots of animals, feces is a regular part of the menu.
And because the digestive process doesn't usually manage to suck ALL the nutrients out of food - poop is nutritious.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查主旨。全文围绕“为什么很多动物会吃粪便”展开，先解释粪便未必总是危险，再说明其中还留有营养，后面又举出多种动物通过吃粪便获取营养或微生物的例子，所以A最完整。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了全文的核心逻辑：回收营养和获得有用微生物。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有主张人类应该模仿这种行为。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。全文重点不是如何避开捕食者。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中举的例子并不限于幼体动物。

public.prompt: Why do rabbits eat the soft clusters they produce after a plant meal?

options.text: To hide their scent from predators before they move on.
To dry the food before swallowing it a second time.
To recover nutrients and return helpful microbes to their guts.
To share partially digested food with their young.

evidence.quote: Then they gobble the whole package back up in order to recover the nutrients and bring the microbes back into their guts.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查细节。说话者明确指出，兔子把第一次排出的软团吃回去，是为了重新吸收营养，并把负责消化的微生物重新带回肠道。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有把这种行为解释为躲避捕食者。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有提到先把食物晾干这一过程。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个选项准确转述了原句信息。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文讲的是兔子自己再次消化，不是喂给幼崽。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention gorillas and southern cassowaries?

options.text: To show that only large animals can survive on feces.
To give examples of animals that re-eat waste to get nutrients left behind.
To contrast herbivores that avoid poop with carnivores that seek it out.
To prove that gut bacteria are unimportant in digestion.

evidence.quote: For example, when gorillas feed on the hard seeds of the Dialium tree, their gut bacteria soften the tough seed but don't extract many nutrients.

And when the southern cassowary eats cassowary plums, the fruits are so big and the bird's digestive tract is so short that the cassowary poops out whole chunks of the fruit; it then turns around and picks them out to eat - and digest - again.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查举例目的。大猩猩和食火鸡这两个例子都在说明：第一次消化没有把价值完全提取出来时，有些动物会再次吃回排出的东西，以回收剩余营养。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。后文还讲到兔子和考拉，说明这不是“大型动物专属”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两个例子共同支持“再次摄食以回收营养”这一观点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有建立草食与肉食在这方面的对比。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文多次强调微生物在消化中的重要作用。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about pap for baby koalas?

options.text: It mainly protects them from predators while they stay in the pouch.
It replaces eucalyptus leaves throughout the koala's life.
It is produced only when the mother cannot find enough leaves.
It prepares them for a future eucalyptus diet by providing needed microbes.

evidence.quote: Pap both supplies nutrients AND gives the baby the microbes it needs to digest its future food.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查推断。原文说pap不仅提供营养，还让幼崽获得将来消化食物所需的微生物，因此可以推断，它的关键作用是帮助小考拉为以后吃桉树叶做准备。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有把pap的主要作用说成防御捕食者。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文强调这是为未来饮食做准备，不是终身替代食物。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有把pap的产生归因于叶子不够。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确概括了pap在饮食过渡中的功能。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-158-q01: 整段从“很多动物会吃粪便”展开，后面分别讲营养回收、微生物获取和多个动物例子，因此主旨是某些动物为什么靠吃粪便获得营养或微生物。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-158-q02: 兔子那段直接说它们把含有部分消化食物和微生物的软团再吃回去，“in order to recover the nutrients and bring the microbes back into their guts”。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-158-q03: 提到大猩猩和食火鸡，都是为了举例说明一些动物会重新吃自己排出的东西，以继续提取先前没吸收完的营养。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-158-q04: 文中说幼年考拉出生时没有分解桉树叶所需的特殊细菌，而 pap 能提供这些微生物并帮助未来消化该食物，所以可推断它是在为将来的桉树叶饮食做准备。

public.prompt: What issue does the talk mainly discuss?

options.text: How Italians discovered that swamps directly create malaria parasites.
Why malaria used to occur widely and why it remains especially difficult to eliminate in Africa.
Why mosquito bites no longer matter outside tropical regions.
How bed nets completely solved malaria in all climates.

evidence.quote: But both the parasite that causes malaria, and the mosquitoes that transmit it, can survive over large swathes of the planet, and until recently, malaria regularly killed people all over the world.
But in Africa, malaria has been especially hard to fight.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查主旨。全文先打破“疟疾只是热带病”的常见印象，说明它过去曾广泛出现在全球；随后再解释为什么它在温带更容易清除，而在非洲尤其难以根除，所以B最能概括全文。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。意大利词源只是开头的历史例子，不是全文中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它同时覆盖了历史分布和现实防治难点两条主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说热带以外的蚊虫叮咬已经无关紧要。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文明确说非洲能否彻底消灭疟疾仍不确定。

public.prompt: According to the talk, how does cooler weather reduce malaria transmission?

options.text: It prevents mosquitoes from breeding in any wet area.
It makes people stay indoors all day and avoid mosquitoes.
It slows the parasite's development, so fewer mosquitoes become infectious.
It forces mosquitoes to bite animals instead of humans.

evidence.quote: The cooler the weather, the more slowly it develops, and under about 15 degrees Celsius, it can't reach its infectious stage at all.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查细节。说话者指出，寄生虫在蚊子体内需要时间发育到可传染阶段；天气越冷，这个过程越慢，低于约15摄氏度时甚至无法完成，因此具有传染性的蚊子更少。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说低温会让所有蚊子都无法繁殖。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。待在室内是财富增长带来的另一因素，不是低温的直接机制。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确概括了低温通过拖慢寄生虫发育来减少传播。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。咬人还是咬动物是蚊种行为差异，不是温度直接造成的。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention the Italian name "mala-aria"?

options.text: To show that people once misunderstood malaria and that the disease was known outside the tropics.
To argue that Europe still has the highest malaria death rate.
To explain how scientists first bred malaria-resistant mosquitoes.
To prove that swamp air is more dangerous than mosquito bites.

evidence.quote: In fact, the disease got its name relatively far from the tropics - Italians called it "mala-aria," or "bad air," because they thought it was caused by noxious fumes emanating from swamps.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查举例目的。提到意大利语词源，一方面说明疟疾并不只和热带相关，欧洲历史上也很熟悉它；另一方面也说明过去人们误以为它来自沼泽里的坏空气。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确抓住了词源例子的两个功能：历史分布和旧误解。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中说欧洲现在已经没有疟疾了。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。词源与培育抗疟蚊子无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。说话者是在回顾旧观念，不是在证明坏空气更危险。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the discussion of bed nets and African mosquitoes?

options.text: Bed nets matter little because most malaria transmission happens indoors.
Africa's main malaria mosquito makes control harder even when prevention improves.
Once wealth rises, malaria automatically disappears in every region.
Malaria in Africa is now caused mainly by climate change rather than mosquitoes.

evidence.quote: And that's largely because the main mosquito that transmits malaria in Africa loves humans - it bites our species four times more often than it bites other animals, whereas malaria-carrying mosquitos outside of Africa bite other animals more often than they bite us.
But, thanks in part to a huge increase in the number of people using bednets, that's just half as many people as malaria killed in Africa 15 years ago.
But, whether or not we'll be able to fully eliminate malaria in Africa, well, that is still up in the air.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查推断。原文说蚊帐使用增加后死亡人数下降，说明防治有效；但同时非洲主要传播蚊特别偏爱咬人，因此是否能彻底清除仍不确定。综合可推断，防治虽有进展，但非洲的主要蚊种特征仍让控制工作非常困难。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中把蚊帐当作有效措施，不是说它几乎没用。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它最准确地整合了“已有改善”与“仍难根除”两层信息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有说财富上升就会自动消灭所有地区的疟疾。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。全文重点始终是寄生虫和传播蚊，而不是把当前问题主要归因于气候变化。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-159-q01: 前半段说明疟疾过去遍布许多地区、后来为何在温带更易清除，后半段解释为何它在非洲尤其难以消灭，所以 b 最完整概括主旨。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-159-q02: 原文明确说气温越低，寄生虫在蚊子体内发育越慢；低于约15摄氏度时甚至无法发育到具有传染性的阶段，因此带传染性寄生虫的蚊子更少。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-159-q03: 说到意大利语“mala-aria”是为了表明人们曾误以为疟疾来自“坏空气”，同时也说明这种疾病历史上并不只见于热带。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-159-q04: 文中一方面说蚊帐让死亡人数减半，另一方面强调非洲主要传播蚊更偏爱叮咬人类，所以即使防治改善，控制和彻底消灭仍然更困难。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the talk?

options.text: The talk argues that honey production matters more than crop pollination.
The talk shows that pesticides are the only threat facing bees.
The talk explains why commercial bees are declining and how propolis-friendly hives can help protect them.
The talk explains why wild bees should replace all commercial hives.

evidence.quote: Unfortunately, when we bring so many bees together, parasites and diseases easily spread from one bee colony to another.
And we can also help bees fight parasites with a chemical weapon they already have in their own arsenal - propolis.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查主旨。前半段讲商业蜜蜂因疾病、寄生虫、单一饮食和农药而受威胁，后半段重点介绍蜂胶如何帮助蜜蜂抵御感染，所以全文核心是“问题是什么，以及蜂胶怎样成为解决办法之一”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文强调授粉和粮食供应的重要性，不是在贬低授粉。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。农药只是问题之一，不是唯一威胁。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它同时概括了蜜蜂困境和文章重点提出的蜂胶方案。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文没有主张让野蜂完全替代商业蜂。

public.prompt: Why do almond farmers bring in bees from around the country each spring?

options.text: Local bees cannot pollinate all the flowers during the short bloom.
The transported bees make almond trees produce sweeter nectar.
California law requires outside bees for all almond orchards.
Beekeepers need warmer weather before their bees can make honey.

evidence.quote: Each spring in California, so many almond trees put out flowers at the same time that local bees can't pollinate them all.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查细节。开头直接说明，由于杏仁树在短时间内同时大量开花，本地蜜蜂数量不够，所以农民才要从外地调来更多蜂群完成授粉。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项正是原文给出的直接原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说外来蜜蜂会让花蜜更甜。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有提到任何法律规定。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。运输蜂群是为授粉，不是为等天气适合产蜜。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker explain how bees make propolis from tree resins and wax?

options.text: To prove that bees prefer tree resin to flower nectar as food.
To argue that wax alone is responsible for most hive sanitation.
To suggest that commercial hives fail because they contain no propolis at all.
To show why propolis can function as a natural antibiotic defense in the hive.

evidence.quote: To make propolis, bees visit trees and collect resins rich in flavonoids and aromatic acids, which are toxic to many organisms.
Then they mix the resins with wax, making a sticky antibiotic glue.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查解释机制的目的。说话者说明蜂胶来自含有有毒化学物质的树脂，并与蜡混合成“抗生素胶”，目的是让听者理解蜂胶为什么能成为蜂巢中的天然防御武器。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里不是在比较蜜蜂对食物的偏好。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文强调的是树脂中的化学成分与蜡结合后的蜂胶，不是只有蜡起作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中说商业蜂箱里也有蜂胶，只是使用方式不同。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确说出了这一机制说明的真正用途。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about roughening commercial hive walls?

options.text: It discourages bees from sealing gaps and pushes them outdoors.
It helps bees treat the hive more like a natural nest and spread more protective propolis.
It replaces the need for disease-resistant breeding programs.
It mainly increases honey output by giving bees more storage space.

evidence.quote: Honeybees in the wild smear this substance inside their nests, which sanitizes them and keeps intruders out.
But, if you build a hive out of rough, unfinished lumber or attach plastic pieces filled with holes to the walls, bees spread way more propolis and their larvae suffer way fewer infections.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查推断。前文说野外蜜蜂会把蜂胶涂在巢内，后文又说把人工蜂箱做得粗糙一些后，蜜蜂会涂更多蜂胶且幼虫感染更少。由此可推断，粗糙内壁让蜂箱更像自然巢穴，从而激发更强的蜂胶防御行为。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说粗糙化会把蜜蜂赶出蜂箱。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它合理连接了野生巢穴行为与人工蜂箱实验结果。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中把抗病育种列为另一条路径，并未说可被完全取代。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里关注的是减少感染，不是扩大储蜜空间。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-160-q01: 全文先讲商业蜂数量下降的多重原因，再重点介绍蜂胶和粗糙蜂箱内壁如何减少感染，因此主旨是商业蜂为何衰退以及这种做法如何帮助保护它们。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-160-q02: 开头明确说加州杏仁树会在同一时间大量开花，地方上的蜜蜂“不够给它们全部授粉”，所以农民才从全国调来蜜蜂。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-160-q03: 讲蜂胶的原料和形成过程，是为了说明它含有对许多生物有毒的成分，能成为“sticky antibiotic glue”，即蜂巢中的天然抗菌防御。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-160-q04: 文中说商业蜂箱内壁太光滑，蜜蜂通常不把蜂胶涂满内壁；若改用粗糙木材或带孔塑料片，蜜蜂就会涂更多蜂胶、幼虫感染更少，因此可推断粗糙化让蜂箱更像自然巢穴。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the video?

options.text: To answer common viewer questions and explain the people and work behind MinuteEarth.
To present a scientific lecture about animal waste and digestion.
To announce that MinuteEarth will stop making videos.
To compare MinuteEarth with other science channels through formal experiments.

evidence.quote: We recently reached 2 million subscribers, and as a way to say thanks for watching, we decided to make a video answering your most common questions.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查主旨。开头直接说明，这支视频是为了感谢观众并回答大家最常见的问题。后面的内容也确实围绕团队、制作、偏好和支持方式展开，所以A最准确。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它直接对应视频开头给出的目的说明。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。虽然提到poop videos，但这不是一场正式科学讲座。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有宣布频道停止更新，只是个别成员提到自己会转向别的项目。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。视频形式是问答，不是做频道之间的正式比较实验。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker spend time explaining the channel's many poop videos?

options.text: To justify why the team repeats the same joke instead of making science content.
To show that science can reveal complexity even in ordinary, unpleasant subjects.
To argue that viewers should stop asking about excrement in the comments.
To introduce a new series devoted entirely to human digestion.

evidence.quote: one of the things that makes science so cool is that it gives us a way to discover how even something as seemingly mundane as a pile of crap is the result of complex, kind of beautiful processes, and there are whole worlds in there when we stop to look.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查修辞目的。说话者借“为什么总讲poop”这个问题，实际上是在强调科学的价值：它能让人看见日常甚至令人不适的事物背后也有复杂而美的过程。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲话者不是在说“只是重复玩笑”，而是在说明科学内容的意义。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个选项最准确概括了这一段的解释目的。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有抱怨观众提出这类问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有宣布新的专门系列。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the section about team members' jobs, hobbies, and other projects?

options.text: Most team members work only on MinuteEarth and avoid outside interests.
The channel hires new members mainly for their music abilities.
The team is made up of people with varied roles, and some are shifting attention to other work.
The team plans to replace science topics with sports and travel content.

evidence.quote: And we're working on a bunch of other projects, like Paradigms and MinuteLabs and Bodypedia on Japan's NHK!
Going forward, I'm actually only going to be working on those projects, rather than on normal MinuteEarth videos, so if you don't hear my voice on the channel much anymore, that's why.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查推断。中段列出成员的其他工作、爱好和项目，接着又有人说明自己以后会把更多时间放在这些项目上。由此可推断，团队成员背景很多元，而且并不是所有人都只做MinuteEarth，有些人正在把重心转向别的工作。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文恰恰强调很多成员有其他工作和兴趣。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。虽然有人做音乐，但没有说招人主要看音乐能力。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它综合了“成员多元”和“有人转向其他项目”两层信息。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有说频道要改做体育或旅行内容。

public.prompt: According to the speaker, how can viewers support MinuteEarth?

options.text: By sending the team extinct-animal suggestions only.
By translating every episode into multiple languages before it is released.
By voting on which team member should stop working on side projects.
By watching and sharing the videos, becoming Patreon patrons, or sponsoring videos directly.

evidence.quote: First, you can support us by continuing to watch MinuteEarth and sharing it with your friends and family.
You can support us financially by becoming a Patreon patron or, if you run a company or a foundation you can actually sponsor videos directly.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查细节。结尾部分明确给出支持方式：继续观看并分享视频，以及通过Patreon或公司、基金会直接赞助来提供经济支持。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。关于灭绝动物的问题出现在别的问答里，不是支持频道的方法。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有要求观众在发布前翻译每一集。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有把给成员投票当成支持方式。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个选项完整对应了结尾列出的支持渠道。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-161-q01: 视频一开始就说为了庆祝两百万订阅，要回答观众最常见的问题；后面内容也都围绕团队成员、频道运作和支持方式展开。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-161-q02: 讲话者解释做很多“poop videos”并不是单纯重复笑话，而是因为科学能揭示连粪便这类看似普通又令人不适的事物背后也有复杂而有趣的过程。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-161-q03: 那一段列出成员的不同背景、兴趣和其他项目，并且明确有人以后会更多转向别的项目，所以可推断团队角色多样，而且部分成员的重心在变化。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-161-q04: 结尾直接说观众可以继续观看并分享视频，也可以成为 Patreon 赞助者，或者如果是公司/基金会则直接赞助视频。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly about?

options.text: Why blocked appendixes can create global differences in appendicitis rates
How diet-related changes in stool formation may help explain appendicitis patterns across places and over time
Why summer heat directly infects the appendix with dangerous bacteria
How surgeons decide when an inflamed appendix must be removed

evidence.quote: people are much more likely to get appendicitis in certain places than others.

We think that that's because an increase in wealth meant that people in those countries started eating less fiber and more processed foods and sugars; which may have caused people in these places to have weirder poops - and thus more frequent fecaliths.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查全文主旨。说话者先说明粪石堵塞阑尾会引发阑尾炎，随后重点解释不同地区和不同时期的发病差异为什么可能与饮食变化有关，所以全文核心是饮食如何通过影响粪便形成来改变阑尾炎模式。

optionRationalesZh.a: 该项只概括了疾病机制和地区差异，没有覆盖全文反复强调的饮食因素。
optionRationalesZh.b: 该项准确概括全文重点：饮食改变了粪便和粪石形成，从而可能解释阑尾炎在不同地方和时期的变化。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文说夏天吃更多糖似乎支持饮食假说，并没有说高温本身直接导致感染。
optionRationalesZh.d: 演讲只顺带提到需要尽快治疗，并没有讨论医生如何决定手术。

public.prompt: According to the speaker, why does the higher summer rate of appendicitis support the diet hypothesis?

options.text: People tend to drink less water in summer, which hardens stool immediately.
Hot weather increases bacterial growth inside the appendix itself.
People usually eat more sugar in warm weather, which seems to produce more stool rocks.
Doctors are more likely to diagnose appendicitis during summer vacations.

evidence.quote: appendicitis was much more common in the summer; it turns out that we eat a lot more sugar when it's warm outside, which seems to cause even more stool rocks.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。原文直接把夏季阑尾炎更常见与天气暖时人们吃更多糖联系起来，并说这似乎会产生更多粪石，因此被当作支持饮食假说的证据。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有把少喝水作为这里的关键原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有说高温直接增加阑尾内细菌，而是强调糖和粪石形成。
optionRationalesZh.c: 该项与原文完全对应，是说话者给出的直接解释。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文没有提到诊断习惯或假期安排。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention South Korea, Iran, and Chile?

options.text: To show that countries becoming wealthier may begin to show the same appendicitis pattern seen earlier in the United States
To argue that appendicitis is caused mainly by genetic traits shared across those populations
To prove that processed foods reduce appendicitis as long as fiber intake stays stable
To contrast countries with high sugar intake against countries with unusually cold climates

evidence.quote: And in countries like South Korea, Iran and and Chile that are just getting wealthier, today we're seeing increases in appendicitis rates that look a lot like those that happened in the US earlier in the 20th century.
Diets in those countries have also gotten rid of a lot of fiber and added a lot of processed food.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查举例目的。说话者提到这些国家，是为了把它们与美国早期经历进行类比，说明当国家变得更富裕、饮食更少纤维且更多加工食品时，也可能出现类似的阑尾炎上升趋势。

optionRationalesZh.a: 该项准确说明了举例的论证作用：用新近富裕国家的变化来支持前面的饮食假说。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有把这些国家当作遗传学证据，重点在饮食与财富变化。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文恰恰把加工食品增多与发病上升联系起来，而不是减少发病。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这里不是气候对比，核心是富裕化和饮食变化。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the recent drop in appendicitis rates in the United States?

options.text: Doctors have proven that sugar no longer affects stool formation in the United States.
The drop happened mainly because wealth in the United States has decreased over the last 40 years.
Researchers now think appendicitis is unrelated to diet anywhere in the world.
The decrease is real, but its cause remains uncertain and current explanations are tentative.

evidence.quote: In the last 40 years - even as people have gotten even richer and continued to eat all sorts of junk, the rate of appendicitis has actually dropped almost 60%.
And we're not exactly sure why. Perhaps, after a few decades, their intestines have simply adjusted to the new diets and have stopped freaking out as much. Or maybe, it's more complicated than that.

private.explanationZh: 这道题需要综合推断。原文明确说美国发病率确实下降了，但紧接着又说研究者并不清楚原因，后面的解释都只是试探性的猜测，因此最稳妥的结论是下降现象存在而原因未定。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有说已经证明糖失去作用，这是过度推断。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文说人们实际上更富了，而且仍在吃垃圾食品，所以该项与原文相反。
optionRationalesZh.c: 说话者没有否定饮食与阑尾炎的关系，反而仍用其他国家的例子支持这种关系。
optionRationalesZh.d: 该项最符合原文：变化是真实的，但解释仍然不确定。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-162-q01: 讲座先解释粪石如何引发阑尾炎，但主体重点在于饮食变化如何影响粪石形成，并据此解释不同地区和不同时期的阑尾炎变化模式。其他选项不是范围过窄，就是与原文不符。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-162-q02: 原文明确说夏季阑尾炎更多，进一步支持饮食假说，因为天气暖时人们会吃更多糖，而这“似乎会造成更多粪石”。其余选项中的缺水、细菌直接增殖、暑假更易诊断都没有被提到。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-162-q03: 提到韩国、伊朗和智利，是为了说明一些正在变富的国家如今也出现了类似美国20世纪早期的阑尾炎上升模式，从而支持饮食与富裕变化相关的解释。其他选项涉及遗传、稳定纤维摄入或寒冷气候，均非原文意思。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-162-q04: 原文说美国过去40年阑尾炎下降近60%，但“并不完全确定原因”，后面只提出肠道适应等暂时解释，并强调仍是“大问号”。因此只能推断下降确实存在，但成因未明。

public.prompt: What is the speaker mainly discussing?

options.text: Why fishing companies often rename seafood products for legal reasons
How internet users create more accurate scientific names for species
How animal names can shape public attitudes and even influence conservation outcomes
Why some carnivores are genetically unrelated to modern dogs

evidence.quote: It turns out that names can really affect our perception of animals, in a negative way...OR a positive one.
And for an animal, the RIGHT name can make us want to save it.

private.explanationZh: 全文围绕动物名称如何影响人类态度展开。前半部分讲名字会改变公众的感觉与消费选择，后半部分进一步说明一个更好的名字甚至能带来保护兴趣和资金，所以主旨是命名对公众认知和保护结果的影响。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中举了 seafood 改名的例子，但重点不是法律原因，而是名字对公众反应的影响。
optionRationalesZh.b: 互联网起名只在结尾被轻松提及，不是全文主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 该项最能概括全文：名字会改变人们对动物的看法，并进一步影响保护行为。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文末确实提到 painted dog 严格来说不算真正的狗，但那只是补充信息。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention the renaming of slimehead to orange roughy?

options.text: To show that a more appealing name can increase public demand for an animal
To explain why deep-sea fish are easier to conserve than land animals
To argue that scientists should always replace traditional species names
To show that consumers usually prefer foods with descriptive scientific labels

evidence.quote: But once someone in the fishing industry cleverly renamed the fish the much more nom-able 'orange roughy,' consumers started gobbling it up - actually almost to extinction.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查例子的作用。slimehead 改成 orange roughy 的例子不是在讨论分类学，而是在说明一个更顺耳、更诱人的名字会明显改变公众反应，甚至把需求推高到伤害物种本身。

optionRationalesZh.a: 该项准确说明了这个例子的论证功能：更吸引人的名字会增强公众购买欲。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有比较海鱼和陆生动物谁更容易保护。
optionRationalesZh.c: 说话者没有提出科学家应当一律改名的规范性主张。
optionRationalesZh.d: orange roughy 并不是更科学的标签，而是更适合营销的名字。

public.prompt: According to the speaker, why was the subspecies renamed the "Hong Kong pink dolphin"?

options.text: To distinguish the subspecies from a nearly identical species in Australia
To encourage restaurants to market the dolphin as a regional symbol
To highlight that the animal migrates through several Asian countries
To connect the animal to local pride and emphasize its unusual, appealing color

evidence.quote: was renamed the "Hong Kong pink dolphin" to inspire locals' sense of pride...and to capitalize on the animal's weird - but cutesy - color.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。原文直接给出两个改名目的：激发香港本地人的自豪感，同时利用这种动物奇特而可爱的粉色外观来吸引注意。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有说改名是为了与澳大利亚的物种区分。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中谈的是公众保护兴趣，不是餐饮营销。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有把跨国迁徙作为改名理由。
optionRationalesZh.d: 该项完整对应原文的两层原因：地方认同感加上颜色带来的吸引力。

public.prompt: What is the speaker's overall view of renaming animals for conservation?

options.text: It can help attract support, but by itself it is not enough to save endangered species.
It is mostly symbolic and has no measurable effect on funding or public interest.
It works only for marine animals and fails for land mammals.
It is scientifically misleading and therefore should be avoided in conservation.

evidence.quote: The species quickly garnered public interest - it was even chosen as a Hong Kong mascot - and extra conservation funding rolled in.
it's obviously going to take more than just marketing to save them.

private.explanationZh: 这道题要求综合态度判断。说话者承认改名能引来关注和资金，但又明确强调仅靠营销并不足以拯救濒危动物，所以他的立场是“有帮助，但绝不充分”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 该项准确概括了说话者的平衡态度：改名有现实作用，但不能单独解决保护问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文明确说公众兴趣和资金都增加了，所以不能说毫无效果。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中既谈海豚也谈 painted dog，没有说只对海洋动物有效。
optionRationalesZh.d: 说话者没有因为名字可能不够科学就反对这种做法。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-163-q01: 整段讨论的是名字如何改变人们对动物的印象，并进一步影响消费、保护意愿和保育结果。其余选项都只是局部例子或偏离主题。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-163-q02: 把slimehead改名为orange roughy的例子，用来说明更吸引人的名字会提升公众需求，甚至让人“吃到几乎灭绝”。这正是名字影响认知和行为的例证。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-163-q03: 原文明确说把这一亚种改名为“Hong Kong pink dolphin”，是为了激发本地人的自豪感，并利用它奇特但可爱的粉色外观。其他选项都不是文中给出的原因。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-163-q04: 说话者的立场是：改名确实能带来关注和资金，也帮助某些种群回升，但两种动物的数量仍然“极低”，显然不可能只靠营销就获救。因此A最符合整体态度。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the talk?

options.text: Why nineteenth-century farmers preferred manure to all imported fertilizers
Why European traders searched Peru for alternative food supplies
How coastal climates determine where most grain crops can be grown
How bird guano revived exhausted farmland and helped lead to modern fertilizer practices

evidence.quote: They were among the first people in the United States to spread this dried bird poop - called guano - on their farm fields in the hopes of restoring their livelihoods.
That's why, today, when the soil loses its nutrients because of farming, we don't have to move our farms to new, fertile soil. Instead we can move the fertility to our farms.

private.explanationZh: 全文先讲 guano 如何帮助贫瘠农田恢复产量，后面再解释它为什么有效，并说明人类由此理解了氮和磷的重要性，最终发展出现代施肥方式。因此主旨既包括历史作用，也包括它带来的现代农业启发。

optionRationalesZh.a: manure 只是被拿来做对比，全文并不是在讲人们为什么更喜欢 manure。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有把贸易焦点放在食物供应，而是肥料。
optionRationalesZh.c: 秘鲁干燥气候只是解释 guano 富集的原因之一，不是全文主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 该项最完整，既概括了 guano 对农田的作用，也概括了它对现代肥料观念的推动。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what made Peruvian guano especially useful to farmers?

options.text: It contained enough water to irrigate dry fields immediately.
It could replace crop rotation without changing soil chemistry.
It was rich in nitrogen and phosphorus that crops need to grow.
It carried seeds from coastal plants into inland farms.

evidence.quote: But what was so special about Peruvian guano that made it different from the other things they'd tried? Well, they didn't know it at the time, but the guano was especially rich in two elements - nitrogen and phosphorus - that all plants need in order to grow.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道直接细节题。说话者明确说明，秘鲁鸟粪之所以特别有效，是因为它富含植物生长必需的两种元素：氮和磷。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有把作用归因于灌溉或含水量。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中强调的正是补回土壤中的营养元素，而不是绕开土壤化学。
optionRationalesZh.c: 该项与原文完全对应，是 guano 有效的核心原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文没有提到携带植物种子。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare the nutrient percentages in guano and manure?

options.text: To explain why farmers preferred large quantities of fertilizer over small ones
To show that guano could restore soil much more efficiently than ordinary manure
To prove that manure was unknown to nineteenth-century American farmers
To show that phosphorus matters more than nitrogen in every crop

evidence.quote: And guano? It's 20% nitrogen and 4% phosphorus - a sprinkling of it can replenish the soil as much as an entire shovel full of manure, which is just 0.5% nitrogen and 0.2% phosphorus.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查数字比较的作用。说话者列出 guano 和 manure 的成分比例，是为了让听众直观理解：少量 guano 就能产生相当于大量 manure 的补土效果，从而突出其高效。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这里不是讨论农民偏好大份还是小份肥料，而是说明不同肥料的效率差异。
optionRationalesZh.b: 该项准确说明了比较数字的功能：突出 guano 在补充土壤养分方面更有效。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有说当时农民不知道 manure，恰恰是他们试过很多办法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有比较氮和磷谁更重要，只说两者都重要。

public.prompt: How does the speaker organize the ending of the talk?

options.text: By arguing that guano stayed popular because it was cheaper than synthetic fertilizers
By contrasting the farming practices of Maryland and Peru without drawing a broader conclusion
By shifting from the historical use of guano to the scientific insight it provided and then to modern fertilizer methods
By returning to the family story to show that modern farms still depend mainly on imported bird waste

evidence.quote: But people in Sandy Spring stopped using guano within 30 years, not just because it ran out - which it did - but also because guano helped people realize that it was Nitrogen and Phosphorus that were important to plants!
So we started grinding up rocks rich in phosphorus from fossilized bacteria poop, and rocks rich in nitrogen that got absorbed from the air.
Instead we can move the fertility to our farms.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查篇章结构。结尾先说明人们后来不再依赖 guano，然后点出它带来的关键科学认识，即植物真正需要的是氮和磷，最后再过渡到现代从岩石和空气中获取肥料养分的做法。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有说 guano 因为更便宜而继续流行，实际上人们后来停止依赖它。
optionRationalesZh.b: 结尾不是单纯做地域对比，而是明确提出更广泛的现代农业结论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 该项准确概括了结尾的推进方式：历史实践到科学认识，再到现代方法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文末没有回到“现代仍主要靠鸟粪”这一点，反而强调现代已能用别的方法补充肥力。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-164-q01: 讲座从家族农田衰败讲起，说明秘鲁鸟粪如何恢复贫瘠土壤，并进一步引出人们对氮、磷和现代肥料方法的认识。D完整概括了历史作用与后续影响。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-164-q02: 原文直接说明秘鲁鸟粪特别富含氮和磷，而植物生长需要这两种元素，所以它对农民特别有用。其他选项如灌溉、替代轮作、带来种子都不在原文中。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-164-q03: 比较鸟粪和粪肥中营养成分的百分比，是为了说明鸟粪补充土壤养分的效率远高于普通粪肥，一点点就能起到很大作用。不是为了讨论数量偏好或证明粪肥未知。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-164-q04: 结尾先说人们停止使用鸟粪，不只是因为耗尽，还因为鸟粪让人们认识到氮和磷的重要性；随后转到现代从岩石和空气中获取肥料成分的方法。这正是从历史用途转向科学认识再转向现代实践的组织方式。

public.prompt: What does the speaker mainly explain?

options.text: How fingerprints form and why each person's prints are effectively unique
Why some fingerprint patterns are becoming less common in modern populations
Why fingerprint scanners are more reliable than typed passwords
How stem cells determine every detail of human skin color

evidence.quote: To understand why, we first need to know how they form. Early on in fetal development, lumps of stem cell tissues - called volar pads - grow under the skin on each finger.
So my left thumbprint - and all my fingerprints and all your fingerprints - are each pretty much mathematically guaranteed to be unique

private.explanationZh: 全文围绕“指纹为什么会独一无二”展开。说话者先解释指纹在胎儿期如何形成，再说明哪些部分受遗传影响、哪些细节来自混沌且不可预测的发育过程，最后得出几乎每枚指纹都独特的结论。

optionRationalesZh.a: 该项同时概括了形成过程和独特性的原因，是全文最完整的总结。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有讨论某些纹型在现代人群中是否变少。
optionRationalesZh.c: 密码和赞助内容只是结尾的类比，不是主要讲解内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文没有讨论肤色形成。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what mainly determines whether a fingerprint's overall pattern is a loop, whorl, or arch?

options.text: The amount of pressure placed on the finger during childhood
The number of capillaries under the skin after birth
The direction in which the hand is usually used for writing
The size and orientation of the volar pad during fetal development

evidence.quote: Whether the volar pad is small or big or off to the side or grows unevenly determines the main pattern of the fingerprint - an arch, whorl, loop, or even a very interesting and exciting double loop whorl.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。原文明确说 volar pad 的大小、偏向一侧与否以及是否不均匀生长，决定了指纹的总体图案类型，如 arch、whorl 或 loop。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有把童年受到的压力当作决定总体纹型的因素。
optionRationalesZh.b: 毛细血管生长涉及更细小的脊线细节，不是总体模式的主要来源。
optionRationalesZh.c: 书写习惯从未在原文中出现。
optionRationalesZh.d: 该项直接对应原文，是说话者给出的明确答案。

public.prompt: Why can identical twins have the same main fingerprint patterns but still have different fingerprints?

options.text: Because only one twin is exposed to gravity while in the womb
Because fingerprints continue to change randomly throughout adult life
Because the overall pattern is influenced by genetics, but the fine ridge details arise from chaotic developmental differences
Because each twin develops a completely different set of volar pads from unrelated genes

evidence.quote: Because your volar pad size and orientation is somewhat genetic, many relatives, and most identical twins, have the same main patterns on each finger, and so DNA alone isn't where the uniqueness of fingerprints comes from. Instead, it comes from the chaotic way in which the fingerprints grow.
And because there are chaotic and unpredictable differences in each of these, whether a given ridge becomes a dead-end or a fork is essentially random, even for babies that develop in the same womb.

private.explanationZh: 这道题需要整合两处信息。原文先说主纹型部分受遗传影响，所以双胞胎可能相同；随后又说真正决定每枚指纹独特性的，是脊线分叉和终止等细节，而这些来自胎儿发育中的混沌差异。

optionRationalesZh.a: 重力方向只是众多影响因素之一，原文没有说只有一个双胞胎会受重力影响。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文讨论的是胎儿发育阶段，不是成年后持续随机变化。
optionRationalesZh.c: 该项准确整合了“遗传决定总体模式”和“混沌发育决定细节独特性”两层意思。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文反而说双胞胎常有相同主纹型，因此不是“完全不同的 volar pad 结果”。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare the number of possible fingerprints to a black dot representing all fingerprints in the world?

options.text: To suggest that most fingerprints have not yet been discovered by scientists
To show that fingerprint evidence is useless unless every print is stored in one database
To argue that fingerprint patterns are distributed evenly across all human populations
To illustrate how enormously the number of possible fingerprints exceeds the number that actually exist

evidence.quote: The average person has around 50 forks or dead ends at different places on each fingerprint.

A quadrillion is 10,000 times larger. And, remember, this is a simplification, because the number of potential unique fingerprints gets way, way bigger when you take into account the relative positions of these points.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查类比的作用。说话者先给出大量可能组合，再用“世界上所有指纹只像一个黑点”这样的对比，帮助听众直观理解：理论上可能的指纹数量远远超过现实中已经存在的人类指纹数量。

optionRationalesZh.a: 说话者不是在谈科学家是否已经发现了大多数指纹。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有说没有完整数据库就无法使用指纹证据，这与黑点类比无关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这里比较的是数量级，不是不同人群中的分布均匀性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 该项最准确地说明了这个类比的功能：突出可能性与现实数量之间的巨大差距。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-165-q01: 整段主要解释指纹如何在胎儿时期形成，以及为什么每个人的指纹实际上都是独一无二的。其余选项只涉及附带内容或明显偏题。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-165-q02: 原文说volar pad的大小、位置和形状决定指纹的大体类型，如弓形、环形或涡形。因此总体图案主要由胎儿发育时volar pad的大小和朝向决定。
