reasonsZh.minute-earth-165-q03: 说话者明确区分了两层：总体图案受遗传影响，所以同卵双胞胎常相同；但细小脊线如何相遇、分叉或终止，受多种混沌且不可预测的发育因素影响，因此最终指纹不同。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-165-q04: 黑点的比喻是为了让听众直观感受：世界上实际存在的指纹数量，相比可能出现的指纹组合，要小得多，因此指纹几乎必然唯一。不是在讨论数据库、科学发现数量或人群分布。

public.prompt: What issue does the talk mainly address?

options.text: Why San Francisco has safer buildings than any other earthquake-prone city
Why predicting earthquakes precisely is difficult even though some warning and modeling methods help
How seismic sensors detect every earthquake before it starts
How ocean faults create stronger quakes than faults near major cities

evidence.quote: But we can't say exactly when the quake might hit.
And unfortunately, our existing methods probably aren't going to help us get there. What we need is an earth-shattering breakthrough.

private.explanationZh: 全文核心在于解释为什么地震仍然难以被精确预测。开头直接说人们无法准确知道地震何时发生，结尾又强调现有方法达不到理想目标，因此主旨是“预测为何困难”，而不是单一技术细节。

optionRationalesZh.a: 建筑安全只是开头提到的应对措施之一，不是全文的主要讨论对象。
optionRationalesZh.b: 该项最完整，既包含预测困难，也包含文中提到的预警和建模进展。
optionRationalesZh.c: 传感器只能在地震已发出地下波后提供极短预警，不是“在开始前探测每次地震”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中研究海洋断层是为了改进模型，不是为了比较谁更强。

public.prompt: According to the speaker, what is the main limitation of current seismic warning systems?

options.text: They provide enough time for protective actions, but not enough time for people to evacuate a city.
They can predict earthquakes about two days before they happen, but only in coastal areas.
They work only when gas pipelines and trains have already been shut down manually.
They depend on animal behavior, which is too inconsistent to be useful.

evidence.quote: which is, surprisingly, enough time to turn off gas pipelines and stop trains and find cover, but it doesn't help people get out of town.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。说话者明确说当前预警足以支持关闭管线、停火车和寻找掩护，但时间太短，无法让整座城市的人安全撤离。

optionRationalesZh.a: 该项准确概括了原文对现有预警系统的优点和核心局限。
optionRationalesZh.b: 两天预警是理想目标，不是现有系统已经做到的能力。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文说预警有助于做这些动作，不是说只有先手动完成它们系统才有效。
optionRationalesZh.d: 动物行为属于曾被研究的候选前兆，不是当前预警系统的基础。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker discuss productive ocean faults that generate relatively harmless earthquakes?

options.text: To argue that ocean earthquakes are easier to predict because they release less stress
To explain why San Francisco should install its next sensors underwater
To provide examples of faults that let researchers repeatedly test and calibrate their models
To show that most useful earthquake research must avoid faults near cities entirely

evidence.quote: Luckily for researchers, a few ocean faults are more productive, and frequently cause large but relatively harmless quakes, giving us a regular way to calibrate and fine tune our models.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查举例目的。说话者提到这些海洋断层，是因为它们能更频繁地产生较大的但相对无害的地震，从而为研究者提供反复校准和微调模型的真实机会。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有把原因归结为“释放更少应力”，也没有说这就是更易预测的原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有提出给旧金山在海底安装新传感器的政策建议。
optionRationalesZh.c: 该项准确说明了举例的功能：为模型提供可反复验证的真实事件。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文没有说研究必须避开城市断层，只是说海洋断层更方便研究。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the progress scientists have made toward ideal earthquake prediction?

options.text: They have already achieved the two-day notice disaster experts want for complex faults.
They now rely mainly on radon gas and animal behavior to predict the timing of major quakes.
They have learned nothing useful from ocean faults because those quakes are too small.
They have improved their understanding of where some quakes may occur, but they remain far from precise short-term prediction.

evidence.quote: when this particular segment slips, it increases the chances its neighbor will slip, letting us predict where the next quake will happen. In some faults, we can even say that it'll happen within a couple of years.

even if we could do that, we'd still be a long way away from the ideal two-day notice.

And unfortunately, our existing methods probably aren't going to help us get there.

private.explanationZh: 这道题需要综合末段信息。研究者已经能从某些较简单的断层中看出相邻断层的连动关系，甚至把时间窗口缩小到几年内；但距离灾害专家理想中的两天预报仍然非常远。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文明确说距离理想的两天通知还差得很远。
optionRationalesZh.b: 氡气和动物行为只是被研究过却不可靠的候选线索，并不是主要依赖的方法。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这些海洋断层恰恰帮助研究者校准模型并学习断层相互作用，不是“什么都没学到”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 该项最符合原文的阶段性结论：在“哪里”和较长时间窗上有进展，但离精确短期预测还很远。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-166-q01: 全文核心是在解释：虽然我们有短时预警和一些建模进展，但要精确预测地震为什么仍然非常困难。其他选项要么夸大现有能力，要么只抓住局部内容。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-166-q02: 原文说当前系统能在破坏性地表波到来前提供一点时间，足以关气管、停火车、寻找掩护，但“不足以让人们撤离城市”。这正是其主要限制。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-166-q03: 提到那些较常发生但危害较小的海洋断层，是因为它们给研究者提供了反复校准和微调模型的现实样本，有助于测试对巨大地震的模型。不是为了说明海底传感器或海洋地震更易预测。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-166-q04: 文中说研究者已经从海洋断层学到断层段相互作用的重要性，某些情况下甚至能把时间范围缩小到几年，但这离理想的两天预警还很远。因此可以推断，科学家在“哪里、较长时间尺度上何时发生”方面有所进展，但离精确短期预测仍差得远。

public.prompt: How does the speaker organize the explanation of concrete's environmental problem?

options.text: By tracing the history of city planning before comparing several bridge designs.
By explaining why cement emits so much CO2 and then arguing that reducing limestone is the most important solution.
By listing the ingredients of concrete and then focusing on how to transport them between cities.
By comparing concrete with textbooks and toilets before describing construction schedules.

evidence.quote: Cement's one-two punch comes from how it's made.
But the real game-changer would be to stop using limestone itself in cement - then it wouldn't release CO2 when it breaks down.

private.explanationZh: 这道组织题考查全文推进方式。前半段先解释水泥为什么会产生大量二氧化碳，后半段再比较各种应对办法，并明确指出真正关键的变化是减少甚至不用石灰石。因此，讲座不是简单罗列事实，而是先说明问题机制，再收束到核心解决方向。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲座没有按城市规划史到桥梁设计史的顺序展开。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了前面解释排放来源、后面突出石灰石替代方案这一结构。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有把运输原料当作组织主线，重点是排放机制与替代方案。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。教材和厕所只出现在结尾的赞助背景里，不是讲座结构的主体。

public.prompt: What two processes make cement a climatic "one-two punch," according to the talk?

options.text: Heating limestone with fossil fuels and releasing CO2 when the limestone breaks down.
Mixing sand with water and moving wet concrete through pipelines.
Crushing finished concrete and shipping it to dense cities.
Cooling lime too quickly and storing rocks in open air.

evidence.quote: First, we have to heat limestone - which typically requires us to use lots of fossil fuels, which emit CO2.
Second, once we do that, the limestone chemically breaks down into lime, which goes into the cement along with some other stuff, and carbon dioxide, which goes into the air.

private.explanationZh: 这道细节题对应讲者对“one-two punch”的直接解释。第一重来自加热石灰石时通常要消耗化石燃料，第二重来自石灰石分解时本身还会释放二氧化碳。也就是说，水泥的高排放既来自能源使用，也来自原料的化学变化。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两个关键来源都与原文完全对应。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。搅拌沙、水和运输湿混凝土并不是讲者强调的两大排放来源。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文讨论的是制造环节，不是成品混凝土粉碎和运输。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。快速冷却和露天储石都不是文中给出的核心原因。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker invoke "a new New York City every single month"?

options.text: To argue that existing cities should all be redesigned to match New York.
To show that urban residents already use too little concrete per person.
To make the future scale of construction, and therefore concrete demand, feel concrete to the listener.
To suggest that most new buildings will be built only in the United States.

evidence.quote: the equivalent of a new New York City every single month for the next 40 years - we're going to need a lot of concrete.

private.explanationZh: 这里的纽约类比是修辞手段，不是在讨论纽约本身。讲者把抽象的全球城市化速度换成一个听众容易想象的大型城市规模，是为了突出未来建设量会极其庞大，从而让“需要大量混凝土”这件事更直观。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲者并没有主张全球照搬纽约的城市设计。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文不是说城市人均用混凝土太少，而是说总建设量会非常大。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确说明了这个例子的功能：把规模感具体化。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这个类比是为了量化建设规模，不是限定建设地点。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about alternative cements from the end of the talk?

options.text: They have already replaced ordinary cement in nearly all major cities.
They would matter only if urbanization stopped expanding.
They reduce emissions only when mixed with extra sand and water.
They appear promising, but wider adoption is still limited by cost, development stage, or caution.

evidence.quote: So far, most of these alternatives are either too expensive to make or too early in development or are being adopted tentatively because concrete is such an important structural material.

private.explanationZh: 这道推断题要求根据结尾的评价判断替代水泥的现实状态。讲者并没有说这些方案没希望，相反他说有些已经成功用于大型建筑和桥梁；但紧接着又指出，大多数方案仍受成本高、开发尚早、行业采用谨慎等限制。所以最合理的推断是：前景存在，但普及还没有完成。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文明确说只是“some”已经成功使用，远远不是几乎全面替代。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。替代水泥的价值恰恰是在城市继续扩张时更重要。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有把减排效果归结为额外加入沙和水。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它完整反映了“有希望但仍受现实约束”的结论。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-167-q01: 讲解先说明水泥为何会排放大量二氧化碳，包括加热石灰石和石灰石分解两部分；后面再提出多种应对办法，并强调真正的关键转变是少用或不用石灰石。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-167-q02: 原文明确说第一是加热石灰石通常要用大量化石燃料，会排放二氧化碳；第二是石灰石化学分解时本身也会释放二氧化碳。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-167-q03: “每个月一个纽约”是为了把未来城市建设规模说得具体可感，让听者直观理解未来对混凝土和水泥需求会有多大。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-167-q04: 结尾说替代性水泥已经存在，有些已成功用于大型建筑和桥梁，但目前仍受制于成本高、开发尚早或因结构安全而采用谨慎，因此最合理的推断是前景可观但推广受限。

public.prompt: What overall point does the speaker make about humans and fish?

options.text: Under cladistic classification, humans fall within the same broader group as the animals we usually call fish.
Humans evolved directly from modern goldfish rather than from apes.
Scientists now define fish only by where an animal lives.
Sharks and goldfish belong together mainly because they look alike.

evidence.quote: So if we wanted to group all the animals we currently call "fish" together under a Cladistic naming scheme, humans - and in fact all mammals - would also need to be part of that group.

private.explanationZh: 全文的核心结论是：如果按支序分类来给“鱼”命名，那么人类也会被包含进去。前面关于clade的定义、切树枝的方法、金鱼和鲨鱼的例子，最终都是为了支撑这个总论点，而不是为了讨论某一种鱼本身。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了讲座最重要的结论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文从未说人类直接起源于现代金鱼，这混淆了共同祖先与直接祖先。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲者强调的是祖先关系，不是生境。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。外形相似恰恰是传统分类的局限，不是本讲的中心。

public.prompt: Which definition of a clade does the talk provide?

options.text: A set of species that occupy the same habitat and eat similar food.
A branch chosen because it contains the oldest fossils in a lineage.
A category built only from animals with similar bodies.
A group consisting of one most recent common ancestor and all of that ancestor's descendants.

evidence.quote: A clade is a group that includes a single most recent common ancestor and every one of that ancestor's descendants.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道直接定义题。讲者清楚说明，clade必须同时包含一个最近共同祖先以及那个祖先的全部后代。只看环境、外形或化石年代都不符合这个定义。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这是按生态位或生活方式分组，不是按共同祖先分组。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。clade的判定标准不是化石是否古老。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。外形相似不是clade的必要条件。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它与原文定义完全一致。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker first choose a goldfish and a shark before adding humans to the tree?

options.text: To prove that body shape is more useful than ancestry when naming groups.
To set up a test case for whether a single cladistic "fish" group can include the animals people usually call fish.
To show that sharks and goldfish have no common ancestor at all.
To argue that humans are genetically identical to both animals.

evidence.quote: To figure out how we relate to fish, let's pick two animals we currently call fish - say, a goldfish and a shark - and first look at how they're related to each other.
Now let's use our Cladistic strategy and see if we can cut out a "fish" clade.

private.explanationZh: 讲者先挑选两种大家都习惯叫“鱼”的动物，不是因为它们本身最重要，而是为了搭一个分类实验。只有先看这两者能否被同一次“切枝”包含进来，后面把人类加入树中时，听众才更容易理解为什么“鱼”这个传统类别在支序分类下会出现问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲者并不是要证明外形比祖先关系更重要。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确说明了这个例子在论证中的铺垫作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文当然默认它们有共同祖先，否则就不存在更大的clade。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲座讨论的是分类逻辑，不是人类与鱼类基因完全相同。

public.prompt: What does the speaker imply about the traditional category "fish" by the end of the talk?

options.text: It should be abandoned immediately because it is never useful.
It works only for sharks and cannot include any bony fish.
It can still be practical in some contexts even though it does not form a true clade.
It is actually more precise than cladistic classification.

evidence.quote: No, it just means that category isn't a clade and in some circumstances that may be more useful.
although goldfish are related to sharks, they're actually more closely related to us.

private.explanationZh: 结尾并没有把传统“鱼类”概念彻底否定掉。讲者明确说，这个类别不是clade，但在某些情境下仍然可能更有用；同时他提醒听众，这种传统说法会遮蔽一些真实的亲缘关系。因此最合理的推断是：它有实用性，但并不等于严格的进化分类。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲者没有说它“永远没用”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有把这个传统类别缩窄到只适用于鲨鱼。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这正是结尾对传统类别的平衡评价。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲者并未说传统分类比支序分类更精确。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-168-q01: 整段核心论点是：如果按支序分类法把通常所说的“鱼”放进同一大类，人类和所有哺乳动物也必须包含在其中。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-168-q02: 原文直接定义 clade 为“一个最近共同祖先以及那个祖先的所有后代”组成的群。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-168-q03: 先选金鱼和鲨鱼，是为了测试能否用一次切割划出一个包含大家通常称为鱼的动物的单一支序群，之后再把人类加进去显示问题。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-168-q04: 结尾明确说，把金鱼和鲨鱼都叫鱼而不把人类叫鱼并没有“错”，只是那个类别不是 clade，而在某些情境下这样可能更有用。

public.prompt: What central difficulty with wave power does the talk emphasize?

options.text: Waves contain too little energy to matter for human electricity use.
Ocean waves can be captured only in tropical climates with calm storms.
Engineers still lack a cheap, efficient, commercially successful way to turn wave motion into electricity.
Wave devices work well, but governments refuse to connect them to power grids.

evidence.quote: That's because we haven't yet figured out an efficient way to use waves to spin turbines, and spinning a turbine is the best way we know of to convert the mechanical energy of a moving substance into electrical energy.
As a result, there's no commercially successful design for capturing energy from waves.

private.explanationZh: 主旨不是说波浪没能量，而是说人类还没有找到既高效又便宜、还能真正商业化的转化方式。开头先指出我们还不会有效地用波浪驱动涡轮，后面又总结目前没有商业成功的设计，所以全文围绕的是“转化与商业可行性”的难题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲者一开始就说海洋覆盖着“literal waves of energy”，并不是能量太少。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有把问题归结为只有热带海域才可用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它把效率、成本和商业化三层核心困难都概括出来了。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲者讨论的是技术与成本限制，不是电网接入政策。

public.prompt: What is the function of the stadium-wave analogy in the middle of the talk?

options.text: It clarifies that a wave travels as local up-and-down motion rather than as the sideways flow of the whole substance.
It proves that crowds could generate more electricity than the ocean can.
It suggests that sports arenas are the best testing sites for marine turbines.
It shows that turbine blades should move vertically instead of rotating.

evidence.quote: A wave, on the other hand, is not the flow of a substance as a whole.
It's like how at a stadium when the crowd does The Wave, the wave moves through the crowd but the people don't move sideways - just up and down.

private.explanationZh: 这个类比是为了把“波前推进”和“物质整体流动”区分开。讲者要说明，海浪不同于一整股水流，它更像局部区域上下振荡，所以不能像普通流体那样轻松持续地推动单个涡轮。体育场例子让这一抽象物理差别更容易理解。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它抓住了这个类比最关键的解释作用。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲者并没有比较人群和海洋谁更能发电。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。类比只是说明波的性质，不是在建议实验地点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文没有得出“叶片应垂直运动”的设计结论。

public.prompt: Which non-turbine design does the speaker mention near the end?

options.text: Underwater cables that turn temperature changes directly into current.
Sea walls that store compressed sand in shore tanks.
Floating towers that convert moonlight into battery power.
Buoys whose bobbing motion pulls on a generator.

evidence.quote: like buoys whose bobbing movement pulls on a generator - but their electricity ends up being just as expensive as from the designs that rely on turbines.

private.explanationZh: 这道细节题对应结尾提出的“超越涡轮范式”例子。讲者举出的非涡轮方案是浮标上下起伏，进而拉动发电机；但他马上补充说，这类方案发出的电依然同样昂贵。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有提到用温度变化直接发电的海底电缆。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。压缩沙子的岸边储罐并不是文中方案。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。月光发电显然不在原文讨论范围内。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确复述了讲者给出的非涡轮设计。

public.prompt: What broader conclusion about wave-energy technology follows from the speaker's examples?

options.text: Any system based on moving water automatically becomes cheaper once it reaches the ocean.
The main challenge is not just making electricity, but making it reliably and affordably in a harsh marine environment.
Wave energy will replace solar power as soon as engineers abandon generators.
Only devices built directly on the shoreline can ever work.

evidence.quote: So far, though, we haven't been able to get any of these turbines to spin fast enough or consistently enough to make electricity - at least cheap electricity; building things in the ocean is expensive, and then whatever you build gets exposed to saltwater, sand, debris, barnacles, and storms, so you have to spend a lot of money repairing it.

private.explanationZh: 这道推断题需要整合讲者对多种方案的评价。问题并不是“完全发不出电”，而是输出不够快、不够稳定，而且海上建设和维护都很贵。由此可以推出，真正难点在于把发电能力、可靠性和经济性同时做到位。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文恰恰强调海洋环境会让成本上升。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它把效率、稳定性和高维护成本三个层面都概括出来了。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲者没有预测波浪能很快替代太阳能。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中既提到近岸破浪，也提到浮动装置，结论并不是“只能岸边”。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-169-q01: 开头和后文都反复强调，问题不是海浪没有能量，而是目前还没有便宜、高效、商业上成功的方法把波浪运动稳定转成电力。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-169-q02: 体育场人浪的比喻是为了说明“波”传播时，介质整体并不横向流动，而只是局部上下振荡，这正是波浪难以直接带动涡轮的关键。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-169-q03: 结尾明确提到一种不依赖涡轮的方案：浮标上下起伏时拉动发电机。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-169-q04: 多个例子共同说明，难点不仅是发出电，而是要在海洋这种有盐、水、沙、碎片、藤壶和风暴的恶劣环境中，持续且低成本地产生电力。

public.prompt: What explanation does the talk give for the modern rise of Lyme disease?

options.text: Doctors have only recently begun naming ordinary skin rashes as Lyme disease.
Human-driven ecological changes have favored mice and ticks, which increases the spread of the Lyme-causing bacteria.
The bacteria suddenly evolved in the 1970s to infect humans for the first time.
People now spend less time outdoors, so their immune systems have weakened.

evidence.quote: Since then, Lyme disease has had an astronomical rise.
Mice thrive in today's fragmented landscape of woods, farm fields, and suburbs.
Plus, both mice and ticks are spreading further north today because climate change has warmed the winters that previously kept them in check.

private.explanationZh: 主旨题要求把“病例上升”与“原因链条”连起来。讲者先说明莱姆病近几十年显著增加，随后解释说，人类改变了景观结构、减少了捕食者，又让冬季变暖，这些变化共同让老鼠和蜱虫更容易扩张，进而提高携菌传播的机会。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。命名和识别历史是背景信息，不是现代大幅上升的主要原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了生态环境变化如何推动传播链扩大。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有说细菌是最近才突然进化出来感染人类。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲者没有把病例上升归因于人们户外活动减少。

public.prompt: Why are mice especially effective at producing more infected ticks?

options.text: They remove nearly all ticks before those ticks can feed on blood.
They keep infected ticks away from farm fields and suburbs.
They can be bitten only by ticks that already carry the bacteria.
They groom poorly and many mice already carry the bacteria, so feeding ticks often acquire it from them.

evidence.quote: Mice, on the other hand, aren't as effective at grooming, so they remove only half of their ticks, and as many as 90% of mice end up carrying the bacteria in their bloodstream.

private.explanationZh: 细节关键有两步：第一，老鼠梳理能力差，所以很多蜱虫能继续吸血；第二，很多老鼠血液里本来就带有细菌，因此原本未携菌的蜱虫一旦叮咬老鼠，就很容易被感染。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。会快速去除大量蜱虫的是负鼠等宿主，不是老鼠。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有说老鼠把感染蜱隔离在郊区之外。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲者强调的是未携菌蜱也会从老鼠身上获得细菌。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它把梳理差和携菌率高两个条件都说全了。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker bring up opossums and squirrels during the discussion of hosts?

options.text: To contrast them with mice and show that not every host helps infected ticks spread.
To claim that they are replacing mice in northern forests.
To identify the first animals in which Lyme disease evolved.
To explain why predators refuse to eat ticks that carry bacteria.

evidence.quote: but some hosts, like opossums and squirrels, gobble up most of the blood-suckers - including those carrying the Borrelia bacteria - so quickly that they rarely get infected, even when the tick is a carrier.

private.explanationZh: 这里举负鼠和松鼠，是为了做对比而不是岔开话题。讲者想说明，并不是所有被蜱虫叮咬的宿主都会同样促进传播；有些宿主会迅速吃掉许多蜱虫，因此反而不容易成为传播放大器。这样一对比，老鼠为何特别关键就更清楚了。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确说明了这个举例的对比功能。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有讨论它们是否在取代老鼠。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲者没有追溯疾病最初起源于哪种动物。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里说的是宿主吃掉蜱虫，不是解释捕食者为何拒绝它们。

public.prompt: If the control efforts described at the end work as intended, what is the most likely outcome?

options.text: Ticks will continue expanding northward faster than before.
The bacteria will become harmless even inside infected ticks.
The incidence of tick-borne disease will decrease because the chain involving mice, ticks, and bacteria is being disrupted.
Forest predators will disappear from managed landscapes.

evidence.quote: It's unclear yet whether these efforts will be successful, but if they are, mice, ticks and Borellia bacteria will garner a lot less of the lymelight.
Putting this knowledge to work will help reduce the incidence of tick-borne diseases in the years to come.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道结果推断题。结尾先说如果干预成功，老鼠、蜱虫和细菌都会少出风头，随后又明确指出，把这些知识用于实践会降低蜱传播疾病的发生率。因此最合理的推断就是：传播链被削弱后，病例会下降。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这是此前变暖带来的趋势，不是成功控制后的结果。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。干预措施不是让细菌“自动无害化”，而是减少传播。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确对应结尾对成功干预效果的总结。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲者提到的是通过森林管理促进捕食者，而不是让它们消失。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-170-q01: 主旨是人类改变了生态环境，导致老鼠更多、蜱虫更多且向北扩张，从而让携带伯氏疏螺旋体的传播链更强，莱姆病因此上升。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-170-q02: 文中说老鼠梳理蜱虫能力差，只去掉约一半；而且多达九成老鼠体内带菌，所以未感染的蜱虫叮咬老鼠后很容易获得细菌。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-170-q03: 提到负鼠和松鼠是为了和老鼠形成对比，说明并非所有宿主都会促进传播；有些宿主会迅速吃掉大多数蜱虫，因此不容易被感染。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-170-q04: 结尾说控制蜱虫、管理森林以促进捕食者、并更快识别症状，目的都是打断老鼠-蜱虫-细菌这条传播链；如果成功，发病率就会下降。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly trying to explain about extreme breath holding?

options.text: How elite breath holders lengthen their time by training around three built-in survival responses.
Why nearly everyone can safely hold a breath for more than eleven minutes.
How the spleen manufactures new oxygen whenever a diver feels panic.
Why online identity protection is essential for underwater athletes.

evidence.quote: But the best breath holders in the world have figured out how to hack three of these survival systems, and as a result, they can hold their breath for an astonishing amount of time.

private.explanationZh: 全文围绕一个中心展开：顶尖闭气者如何通过训练，绕过或利用人体的三套生存反应，从而把闭气时间延长到惊人的程度。后面关于保持冷静、触发潜水反射、忍受横膈膜痉挛的内容，都是对这条主线的展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了讲座最核心的解释对象。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文恰恰说普通人往往只能坚持大约一分钟。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。脾脏只是释放储备红细胞，不是制造新的氧气。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。网络安全只是结尾赞助部分，不是讲座主旨。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker spend time explaining the effects of panic on heart rate and oxygen use?

options.text: To argue that fear is the only reason inexperienced swimmers drown.
To show that oxygen consumption does not matter until the lungs are empty.
To make clear why trained calmness helps skilled breath holders last longer.
To prove that training cannot change the body's response to stress.

evidence.quote: But this panic over running out of air causes our heart to beat faster, which ironically causes us to expend more oxygen than we would have if we hadn't panicked in the first place.
through intensive training, they're able to remain calm and keep their heartbeat steady even as oxygen runs low, allowing them to double their breath-holding time.

private.explanationZh: 讲者先说明恐慌会让心跳加快、耗氧增加，随后立刻给出高手的反向做法：通过训练保持冷静和心率稳定。由此可以推断，前面对恐慌机制的解释，是为了说明“冷静”为什么能够显著延长闭气时间。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里不是在总结所有溺水事故的唯一原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。整段恰恰强调耗氧速度非常关键。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确抓住了这一段的论证目的。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文明确说训练可以帮助他们稳定心率。

public.prompt: According to the discussion of the mammalian dive reflex, what happens when the response is triggered?

options.text: The lungs refill from hidden air sacs while the limbs warm up.
The heart slows, blood is redirected toward vital organs, and the spleen releases extra oxygen-rich blood cells.
The diaphragm stops moving permanently until oxygen returns to normal.
The body sends more blood to the skin so heat can escape faster.

evidence.quote: Our brain tells our heart to slow waaaay down, which slows down the rate at which we use oxygen.
Then the blood vessels on our skin and in our limbs constrict, directing more of the blood flow - which contains oxygen - towards our brain and other vital organs.
Finally, our spleen - which serves as a blood reservoir - contracts, sending out a bunch of backup oxygen-rich red blood cells into our bloodstream.

private.explanationZh: 这道细节题要求整合潜水反射的三个步骤：心率下降以降低耗氧；四肢和皮肤的血管收缩，把更多含氧血液优先送往大脑等重要器官；脾脏收缩，释放额外的富氧红细胞。只有把三步连起来，才能完整对应正确选项。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有提到肺部隐藏气囊重新充气。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它完整涵盖了讲者列出的三个反应。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。横膈膜痉挛是后面另一部分内容，也不是永久停止。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文说的是把血液从皮肤和四肢收回来，而不是更多送往皮肤。

public.prompt: What enables elite breath holders to keep going after diaphragm spasms begin?

options.text: They stop using oxygen entirely once their face is underwater.
They prevent spasms from occurring in the first place.
They switch to an alternative source of air stored in the spleen.
They understand that some oxygen remains and train themselves to endure the spasms.

evidence.quote: But elite breath holders have figured out that at that point there's still some oxygen left.
So they repeatedly ignore those spasms, which allows them to hold their breath for several more minutes.

private.explanationZh: 这道推断题要把两个信息连起来：高手知道痉挛出现时并不等于氧气已经耗尽；正因为如此，他们才会通过训练反复忽视这种强烈信号，继续坚持几分钟。也就是说，关键不是没有痉挛，而是对痉挛信号的重新理解与忍受。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。脸入水会触发潜水反射，但不会让身体完全停止耗氧。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文不是说他们阻止痉挛发生，而是说痉挛出现后仍继续坚持。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。脾脏释放的是红细胞，不是可直接替代呼吸的新空气。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确概括了“仍有氧气”与“忍受痉挛”之间的关系。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-171-q01: 整篇主要解释顶级憋气者如何通过训练“破解”三套生存反应系统，因此能把憋气时间延长到惊人的程度。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-171-q02: 说明恐慌会让心跳加快、耗氧更多，是为了铺垫为什么训练出的冷静能够避免恶性循环，从而让高水平憋气者坚持更久。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-171-q03: 关于哺乳动物潜水反射，原文依次说心跳减慢、皮肤和四肢血管收缩把更多含氧血液送往大脑等重要器官，以及脾脏收缩释放额外富氧红细胞。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-171-q04: 文中明确说精英憋气者知道膈肌痉挛开始时其实还有一些氧气，因此通过训练反复忍受这些痉挛，继续坚持数分钟。

public.prompt: What aspect of Morocco is the talk mainly concerned with?

options.text: How ocean currents spread phosphorus evenly across the world's seafloor
Why Morocco's phosphate reserves matter to the future of agriculture
How animal waste became the main global fertilizer source
Why other nutrients can easily replace phosphorus in farming

evidence.quote: But it is one of the most important places in the world, because of phosphorus.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题考查对全文核心话题的把握。开头先说摩洛哥并不是传统意义上的超级大国，随后立刻说明它之所以重要，是因为磷资源。后文围绕磷对农业和文明的重要性，以及摩洛哥掌握大量可开采磷矿这一事实展开，所以正确答案是 b。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在“均匀 spread”。原文强调磷只在少数特殊地点集中，不是洋流把它平均分散到海底各处。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。全文的中心就是摩洛哥因拥有大量可获得的磷矿，而对全球粮食生产和农业未来至关重要。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在把动物粪便说成“主要来源”。原文明确说动物粪便并不足以补充人类所需的全部磷。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在“easily replace”。原文恰恰强调磷对植物生长不可或缺，而且人类正面临磷资源短缺问题。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why must farmers keep adding phosphorus to fields?

options.text: Farm animals use up most of the phosphorus before crops can absorb it
Ocean microorganisms remove phosphorus from farmland during storms
Erosion and harvesting carry phosphorus away from fields
Plants can use phosphorus only when nearby rocks are mined

evidence.quote: Soil naturally contains some phosphorus that's available to plants, but every year, some of it leaves our fields through erosion and some of it leaves when we harvest the plants that absorbed it. So we need to add more phosphorus.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。原文直接给出两个原因：一部分磷会因侵蚀流失，另一部分会随着被收割的植物离开农田，因此农民需要不断补充磷。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。原文只说会使用一些来自动物粪便的磷，并没有说家畜先把土壤里的大部分磷消耗掉。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。海洋微生物出现在磷矿形成的解释中，不是把农田里的磷带走的原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。侵蚀和收获都会让田地中的磷减少，所以需要再添加。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。岩石开采是人类补充磷的一种来源，不是植物吸收磷的前提条件。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare Morocco's reserves with China's rare earth metals and other countries' oil?

options.text: To show how unusually concentrated Morocco's accessible phosphate supply is
To argue that Morocco is wealthier than other resource-rich countries
To explain that phosphate rock and oil form in exactly the same way
To suggest that other resource powers should help manage Morocco's mines

evidence.quote: That's an unheard of concentration of a single major resource in one country.

private.explanationZh: 这道篇章组织题考查比较这一写法的作用。说话者先指出摩洛哥的可开采磷矿异常集中，随后拿其他国家拥有的稀土和石油做参照，目的就是突出这种集中程度在全球资源分布中极为罕见。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。比较的功能就是强调摩洛哥在可获取磷矿上的占比高得不寻常。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。比较的是资源储量集中度，不是国家财富水平。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。原文此处不是解释资源形成机制，而是比较各类资源在不同国家的集中程度。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。说话者没有提出这些国家应参与管理摩洛哥矿山，这属于无根据推断。

public.prompt: What does the speaker imply is the most sustainable long-term response to phosphorus scarcity?

options.text: Waiting for geological processes to restore farmland phosphorus naturally
Mining the seafloor until phosphate shortages disappear permanently
Expanding the use of animal waste until agriculture becomes self-sufficient
Recovering and reusing phosphorus much more effectively

evidence.quote: So if we plan to be a food-eating civilization into the distant future, we need to use and recycle phosphorus more effectively.

private.explanationZh: 这道推断题要求从结尾方案概括长期方向。说话者否定了等待自然重新形成磷矿的做法，也指出即使发现新矿或开采海底资源也终会耗尽，因此真正可持续的方向是提高利用效率并回收再利用磷。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。原文明确说自然地质循环太慢，要几百万年，人类等不起。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。原文说即便开采海底资源，最终也会用完，不可能“permanently”解决。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。前文已经说明动物粪便远远不够补足全部农业需求。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。结尾直接提出要更有效地使用和回收磷，这正是长期解决思路。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-172-q01: 开头先指出摩洛哥因磷而重要，后文集中说明磷对农业必不可少、摩洛哥掌握大量可开采磷矿，以及这对人类长期粮食供应的意义，主旨对应 b。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-172-q02: 原文明确说土壤中的磷每年会因侵蚀流失，也会在收获被植物吸收后的作物时被带走，所以农田需要不断补充磷，对应 c。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-172-q03: 说话者把摩洛哥与中国稀土、沙特和委内瑞拉的石油作比较，是为了突出摩洛哥可开采磷矿储量在全球占比异常之高，属于强调其集中程度，对应 a。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-172-q04: 结尾明确说如果想长期维持以粮食为基础的文明，就必须更有效地使用并回收磷；等待地质过程、海底开采或靠动物粪便都被说成不足或不可持续，因此选 d。

public.prompt: What development is the talk mainly about?

options.text: Why cancer cells always defeat the human immune system
How an accidental laboratory finding led to more targeted cancer treatment
Why chemotherapy has been completely replaced by antibodies
How organ transplants inspired the discovery of cancer cells

evidence.quote: If only we could figure out how to specifically target just cancer cells for destruction...which is exactly what some researchers made possible back in 1975 - by accident.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题要求概括整段讲述的核心发展。材料先说明传统治疗难以区分癌细胞和健康细胞，随后引出 1975 年一个偶然发现如何让研究者能够更有针对性地攻击癌细胞，所以最能概括全文的是 b。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。全文重点不是解释癌细胞为何总能战胜免疫系统，而是介绍人类如何更精准地识别并攻击它们。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。材料围绕一次实验室中的偶然发现如何发展为定向抗癌技术展开。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在“completely replaced”。原文只说抗体可以定向递送治疗，并未说化疗被彻底取代。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。器官移植排斥只是后面提到的应用之一，不是这项发现的起点。

public.prompt: Why did the researchers fuse immune cells with cancer cells in the lab?

options.text: To create cells that could both make antibodies and keep reproducing
To test whether cancer cells could be turned back into healthy tissue
To make immune cells divide more slowly and safely
To observe how chemotherapy affects hybrid cells

evidence.quote: Eventually, they found that by fusing immune cells with cancer cells in the lab, they could create hybrid cells with the antibody-producing power of the immune cell, and the longevity of the cancer cell.

private.explanationZh: 这道细节题直接对应实验目的。融合后的杂交细胞同时具备免疫细胞“制造抗体”的能力和癌细胞“能够长期存活并持续繁殖”的特点，所以答案是 a。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文精确说明了两类细胞各自贡献的优势：产抗体和长寿命。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。研究者不是想把癌细胞恢复成正常组织。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。目标不是让免疫细胞分裂更慢，而是让抗体供应能够持续。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。化疗不是这一实验设计的直接目的。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention antibodies carrying chemotherapy or radioactive particles?

options.text: To show that traditional chemotherapy is no longer necessary
To explain why cancer cells naturally attract poisonous substances
To illustrate how antibodies can deliver treatment directly to target cells
To argue that radiation is safer than every other treatment

evidence.quote: And we can even engineer antibodies that carry tiny doses of chemotherapy or radioactive particles, delivering these weapons directly to the cancer cells they're designed to recognize.

private.explanationZh: 这道修辞目的题考查举例的作用。说话者提到抗体还能携带化疗药物或放射性粒子，不是为了比较疗法优劣，而是进一步说明抗体可以把治疗精确送到目标癌细胞。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。原文没有说传统化疗已经不再需要，只是说明可以更定向地使用治疗。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。这里讲的是人工工程设计，不是癌细胞天然会吸引这些物质。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个例子正是为了展示抗体的定向递送功能。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。材料没有断言放疗一定比所有其他治疗更安全。

public.prompt: What does the talk suggest about basic research?

options.text: It matters most when scientists can predict a profitable drug from the start
It is valuable mainly when it remains inside one narrow discipline
It succeeds only when researchers begin with a medical treatment in mind
It can lead to major practical uses even when the original goal is different

evidence.quote: These scientists weren't actually trying to treat cancer at all; they were investigating natural disease-fighting molecules called antibodies,
Today, these manufactured antibodies serve as the basis of dozens of cancer-fighting drugs.

private.explanationZh: 这道推断题要求整合前后信息。研究最初并不是为了治疗癌症，而只是研究抗体；但后来这一发现发展成几十种抗癌药物的基础。由此可以推断，基础研究即使起点不是应用，也可能产生重大的现实价值。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。材料强调的重要性恰恰在于研究最初没有瞄准具体盈利药物。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。后文明确说这项技术后来扩展到癌症、自身免疫病和移植排斥等多个领域。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。原文直接说研究者一开始并不是为了治疗癌症。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。全文通过这一发现的发展过程说明，基础研究常常会带来出人意料的广泛应用。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-173-q01: 全篇先讲传统疗法伤及正常细胞，再讲 1975 年一次偶然的实验发现如何带来更有针对性的抗癌治疗，主旨最符合 b。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-173-q02: 研究者需要稳定抗体来源，而产抗体细胞寿命短；把免疫细胞和癌细胞融合后，得到既能产抗体又能长久繁殖的杂交细胞，因此是 a。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-173-q03: 提到抗体可以携带化疗药物或放射性粒子，是为了举例说明抗体不仅能识别目标癌细胞，还能把治疗直接送到这些目标细胞上，对应 c。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-173-q04: 说话者强调最初研究者并不是为了治疗癌症，而后来的研究者发现其巨大应用价值；结尾赞助段也强调自由探索会带来创新应用，因此应选 d。

public.prompt: What argument is the talk mainly making about basic research?

options.text: Basic research should be limited to projects with immediate medical applications
Basic research often justifies public investment because it can produce large long-term benefits
Basic research is less reliable than private industry for creating useful technology
Basic research matters mostly because it helps scientists win prestigious awards

evidence.quote: you might question whether this was a good use of public money, but it was actually a great economic investment.
Yet even the most conservative estimate suggests that overall, basic research more than pays for itself, and can even outperform more traditional investments.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题需要把开头的“老鼠按摩”例子和后面关于回报率的总结合起来看。说话者的核心论点是：基础研究看似用途不明，但长期来看常常带来巨大社会和经济收益，因此公共投入是有道理的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。全文反复强调很多高回报成果一开始并没有明确应用目标。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。无论是婴儿按摩、基因组计划还是整体回报率估算，都在支持这一主张。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。作者并没有说基础研究比私营行业更不可靠，反而认为其总体回报可观。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。诺奖只是个别例子，全文重点不是学术声望，而是公共价值与投资回报。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what happened when hospitals tried massage on premature babies?

options.text: They needed more expensive equipment to monitor the babies
Their hair and nails grew faster but their immune systems weakened
They left the hospital about six days sooner on average
They gained weight at first but showed no lasting improvement

evidence.quote: They grew faster, their immune systems improved, and they were discharged an average of six days earlier.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。原文明确说明，接受按摩的早产儿长得更快、免疫系统更好，而且平均能提前六天出院，所以最准确的是 c。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。材料没有提到为了按摩而增加昂贵设备。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。原文说免疫系统 improved，而不是变弱。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。提前约六天出院是原文直接给出的具体结果。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。原文呈现的是多方面积极效果，不是“起初变好但没有持续改善”。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention the scientist who levitated frogs with electromagnets?

options.text: To show that Nobel Prize winners usually begin with profitable ideas
To argue that unusual experiments should be judged only by their entertainment value
To prove that economists can measure every social benefit exactly
To illustrate that research with no obvious payoff can later lead to valuable applications

evidence.quote: Take the scientist who used electromagnets to levitate frogs, which seemed like a non-profitable dead end. But this kind of creative thinking eventually resulted in him developing graphy, a Nobel Prize-winning material with tons of applications, and a $42 million market in 2017.

private.explanationZh: 这道修辞目的题考查举例的功能。青蛙悬浮这个例子看上去毫无商业前景，但后来却通向具有广泛用途和市场价值的新材料。作者借它说明，基础研究的收益路径往往曲折，不能只看眼前用途。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。这个例子恰恰说明高价值成果并不是从一开始就带着明确盈利目标出现的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。作者不是把这类实验当娱乐，而是在说明其潜在的长期价值。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。这里不是为了证明经济学家能精确量化一切收益，而是说明 payoff 常常来得很间接。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个例子正是为了展示看似无利可图的研究后来也可能产生重要应用。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about why taxpayers must support basic research?

options.text: Because many of its gains spread beyond the original lab or funder, private firms have weak incentives to pay for it
Because taxpayers are better than scientists at predicting which discoveries will pay off
Because government support guarantees that every project will earn a profit
Because private firms are not allowed to fund long-term scientific work

evidence.quote: But the thing about basic research is that these dividends rarely go back to the lab that did the work, or whoever funded it. So private profit-seeking firms are not likely to invest heavily in the basicist basic research.

private.explanationZh: 这道推断题要求根据结尾的因果链得出结论。作者指出基础研究带来的“红利”往往不会回到最初的实验室或资助者手里，因此逐利的私营企业缺乏足够动力去承担这类投入，于是公共资金就变得必要。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了原文结尾关于收益外溢和私人激励不足的逻辑。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。作者并没有说纳税人更擅长预测哪项研究会成功。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。全文强调的是高概率的整体回报，而不是每个项目都保证盈利。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。私营企业并非“被禁止”投资，而是通常缺乏足够的利润动机。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-174-q01: 整段核心论证是基础研究虽然起初没有明确回报，但常能产生巨大的长期社会和经济收益，因此值得公共资金投入，b 最贴切。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-174-q02: 原文直接说给早产儿做类似按摩后，他们长得更快、免疫系统改善，而且平均提前六天出院，所以答案是 c。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-174-q03: 电磁悬浮青蛙的例子前面先说有些研究看似没有利润回报，后面说明这种创造性思路最终导向有大量应用的新材料，因此这个例子用来说明无明显用途的研究后来也可能变得非常有价值，选 d。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-174-q04: 结尾说基础研究的红利很少回到原实验室或资助者手中，所以逐利企业不太愿意大力投资；因此若想继续获得这些收益，就需要纳税人支持，对应 a。

public.prompt: What central claim does the speaker make about air conditioning?

options.text: It saves lives and improves comfort, but it also worsens climate change
It has already become less efficient than heating in every part of the world
It was invented mainly to cool large internet servers
It can be replaced completely by urban trees in the near future

evidence.quote: But keeping our indoor climate cool has, ironically, made the outdoor climate much warmer, and fixing the planet-warming effect of air conditioners is going to be one of the major challenges of the 21st century.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题抓住全文的核心悖论。空调一方面在热浪中救人、提升舒适度，另一方面又通过多种机制加剧全球变暖，因此 a 最准确地概括了说话者的中心论点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时包含了空调的好处和它对气候造成的负面影响。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。原文只说目前供暖总体贡献仍更大，并没有说所有地区、所有情况下都如此。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。服务器只是开头顺带提到的用途之一，不是发明空调的主要原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。文末的绿化是缓解方案之一，并不意味着短期内能完全替代空调。

public.prompt: Why are refrigerant leaks especially damaging to the climate?

options.text: They make buildings use more drinking water for cooling
The leaked chemicals trap far more heat than carbon dioxide does
They react with city air and immediately form new fossil fuels
They stop air conditioners from moving indoor heat outside

evidence.quote: the refrigerants we use are thousands of times more powerful than CO2 at trapping the Earth's heat, so they warm the Earth nearly 60% extra on top of the warming from the CO2 released to run the air conditioners.

private.explanationZh: 这道细节题直接问制冷剂泄漏为何特别有害。原文明确说这些制冷剂在锁住地球热量方面比二氧化碳强得多，因此即使泄漏总量不大，也会造成显著额外增温。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。材料没有提到饮用水浪费。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。关键原因就是这些化学物质的温室效应远强于二氧化碳。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。原文没有说它们会立刻生成新的化石燃料。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。题干问的是气候危害机制，而不是机器散热功能是否失效。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker describe greener cities near the end of the talk?

options.text: To show that heating systems should replace every form of cooling
To explain why only wealthy countries can operate air conditioners
To give examples of how to reduce the need for air conditioning itself
To argue that refrigerant recycling alone will solve the problem

evidence.quote: And we'll need to go even further, by redesigning our entire cities to include more green spaces with trees that can provide shade, encourage hot air to mix with cool air, and cool the air themselves through evaporation.

private.explanationZh: 这道修辞目的题问结尾“更绿的城市”这一段的作用。它不是简单补充背景，而是在给出一种从源头降低制冷需求的思路：通过城市设计和绿化本身降温，从而少用空调。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。作者并没有主张用供暖系统替代所有制冷。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。这里讨论的是城市降温设计，不是国家贫富差异。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。绿地和树木的例子正是为了说明如何减少对空调的依赖。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。原文提出的是多种并行措施，不认为只靠回收制冷剂就够了。

public.prompt: What is likely to happen if current air-conditioning practices continue unchanged?

options.text: Cooling demand will fall as buildings become naturally colder
Heating will remain the larger energy use in every future scenario
Refrigerants will stop affecting climate once more people can afford cooling
Rising temperatures and wider access to cooling will reinforce each other

evidence.quote: Then, as the earth gets warmer, we use more air conditioning, which warms the earth even more in a vicious feedback cycle that's only going to get worse over time and as developing countries get richer and more people have access to air conditioners.

private.explanationZh: 这道推断题考查对因果链的理解。原文描绘了一个恶性反馈循环：气候越热，人们越依赖空调；空调越多，又会进一步加剧变暖；再加上越来越多国家变富后普及空调，这个循环会持续强化。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。原文没有说建筑会自然变冷，反而说全球气温会继续升高。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。作者明确提到未来某个时候空调用能会超过供暖。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。更多人用空调不会让制冷剂停止影响气候。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这正是说话者所描述的未来趋势和反馈机制。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-175-q01: 开头先说空调在热浪中救命、提高舒适度，随后马上指出它反过来加剧全球变暖，整篇围绕这种矛盾展开，所以选 a。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-175-q02: 原文明确说空调制冷剂即便泄漏总量不算大，但它们的温室效应比二氧化碳强几千倍，因此对气候特别有害，对应 b。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-175-q03: 结尾列举绿地、白屋顶、对流窗户等城市和建筑设计，是为了说明除了提升空调本身效率，还可以通过环境设计减少对空调的需求，因此是 c。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-175-q04: 原文说气候越暖，人们越用空调；而空调又会进一步让地球变暖，形成会越来越严重的恶性反馈循环，因此应选 d。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the talk?

options.text: Cyanobacteria disappeared after algae learned their metabolic tricks
Microbes that once helped make complex life possible are now harming ecosystems because humans favor their return
Early Earth had enough oxygen to support modern aquatic animals
Modern agriculture has removed the need to study aquatic microbes

evidence.quote: In fact, cyanobacteria actually helped make our existence possible in the first place.
But our success today is now making things awesome again for cyanobacteria.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题要求整合前后对比。前半部分说蓝细菌曾帮助创造适合复杂生命生存的条件，后半部分又说人类活动让它们重新兴盛并带来生态危害，所以 b 最能概括全文的反讽性核心。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。原文说蓝细菌被藻类挤到边缘，但并没有消失。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它同时抓住了蓝细菌过去的积极作用和现在在人的影响下造成的危害。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。开头明确说早期空气中没有氧气。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。材料恰恰说明现代农业和人类活动让研究这类微生物更重要。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what helped cyanobacteria continue dominating the oceans after oxygen first appeared?

options.text: They evolved to use oxygen through aerobic respiration
They moved from oceans onto land before algae spread
They stopped relying on nutrients such as nitrogen and phosphorus
They formed permanent partnerships with fish and plants

evidence.quote: But over time the surviving cyanobacteria evolved, to not just tolerate oxygen, but to use it, with a sort-of reversal of photosynthesis, which we now call aerobic respiration.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。原文清楚说明，幸存下来的蓝细菌后来不但能耐受氧气，还学会利用氧气进行有氧呼吸，这帮助它们继续在海洋中占据优势。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文直接给出了这一关键变化。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。材料没有说它们迁移到陆地。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。原文没有说它们不再依赖这些营养。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。文中没有提到它们与鱼类或植物形成永久共生关系。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention fertilizer washing from farm fields into rivers and oceans?

options.text: To argue that phosphorus is more dangerous than carbon dioxide
To explain why algae now benefit more than cyanobacteria do
To identify a human activity that feeds cyanobacterial blooms
To show that modern farms depend on cyanobacteria for crop growth

evidence.quote: because we over-fertilize our farm fields, the rain washes a lot of that fertilizer into rivers and oceans, providing a level of delicious nutrients that cyanobacteria haven't seen for perhaps billions of years.

private.explanationZh: 这道修辞目的题考查一个细节为何被提到。说话者提到化肥被雨水冲入河海，是为了指出人类活动怎样人为制造出高营养环境，从而促成蓝细菌大量繁殖。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。这里不是比较磷和二氧化碳谁更危险。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。重点是蓝细菌获得了营养优势，不是藻类受益更多。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这一信息的作用就是指出一种推动蓝细菌爆发的人类行为。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。原文没有说现代农业依赖蓝细菌来促进作物生长。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about why cyanobacterial blooms create dead zones?

options.text: Toxins from living mats immediately remove oxygen from the atmosphere
Fish and insects leave the water before decomposers arrive
The blooms block all sunlight, so every aquatic organism stops breathing
When many cyanobacteria die, decomposers use up the water's oxygen while feeding on them

evidence.quote: floating mats of their dead bodies serve as food for oxygen-breathing decomposers, who temporarily use up all the available oxygen in the water, killing fish, shrimp, insects, and plants in sometimes dangerously massive dead zones.

private.explanationZh: 这道推断题要求根据死亡后的过程理解“死区”形成机制。关键不是活着的蓝细菌直接抽走氧气，而是它们大量死亡后被分解者消耗；分解者在分解尸体时会把水中的氧大量用掉，于是形成死区。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。原文说毒素会让动物和人类生病，但死区的直接机制是分解者耗尽水中氧气。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。材料没有说鱼和昆虫会在分解者到来前主动离开。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。原文没有把原因归结为完全遮光，而是归结为分解过程耗氧。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这是根据结尾因果链得出的准确推断。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-176-q01: 全篇先回顾蓝细菌如何通过光合作用帮助创造适合复杂生命的环境，再说明今天人类活动又让它们大量繁殖并伤害生态系统，所以主旨对应 b。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-176-q02: 氧气出现后，大多数早期生命会被毒害，但幸存的蓝细菌后来演化为不仅耐氧，还能利用氧进行有氧呼吸，因此继续在海洋中占优势，选 a。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-176-q03: 提到农田中过量肥料被雨水冲入河流和海洋，是为了指出一种人为活动正在给蓝细菌提供它们喜欢的大量营养，从而助长水华，对应 c。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-176-q04: 原文说蓝细菌成群死亡后，其尸体成为分解者的食物，而这些耗氧分解者会暂时用尽水中的可用氧，进而造成大面积死亡区，所以选 d。

public.prompt: What central idea does the talk emphasize about studying microbes?

options.text: How microscopes reveal the chemical structure of individual microbes in the lab
Why satellites can reveal large-scale effects of microbes and complement close-up study
Why plant diseases are the only microbial problem that can be seen from space
How remote sensing can completely replace field and laboratory observations

evidence.quote: Except, it's sometimes more useful to observe microbes from space ... well, at least observe their effects.
But by using both microscopes and macroscopes, we can magnify our understanding of Earth - and other planets.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查主旨。开头先提出“从太空看微生物效应”有时更有用，结尾又总结说要把显微镜和“宏观镜”结合起来理解地球与其他行星，因此核心意思是卫星观测能在大尺度上补充近距离的微生物研究。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在把重点缩小成实验室里的微观结构观察。原文强调的不是显微镜如何看清结构，而是卫星如何补充这类近距离观察。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它概括了全文从森林、浮游植物、霍乱到火星的共同线索：卫星看到大范围效应，显微镜负责近距离确认。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在“only”。文中除了植物病害，还谈到浮游植物相关的霍乱预测以及火星岩石中的生命迹象。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在“completely replace”。结尾明确说卫星永远不会完全取代实验室工作和地面观察。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why is space-based data more efficient for monitoring infected vegetation?

options.text: It can identify the exact microbe species inside every leaf sample
It avoids the need to measure visible and infrared light from plants
It allows people to inspect large areas of land in a short time
It works only after damaged forests have already caught fire

evidence.quote: using space-based data is significantly more efficient because we can inspect large swaths of land in minutes.

private.explanationZh: 题目问效率高的直接原因。原文明确说卫星数据更高效，是因为几分钟内就能检查大片土地，所以重点在覆盖范围大、速度快。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在把卫星说成能直接识别每片叶子里的具体微生物。文中没有这样说，显微镜才用于观察感染植物组织中的微生物。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在与原文相反。前文正是说可以测量植物反射出的可见光和红外线变化。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它直接改写了“inspect large swaths of land in minutes”的信息。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在把用途说得太晚、太窄。卫星数据是为了优先监测高风险区域，不是只在已经起火后才有用。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the discussion of phytoplankton and cholera?

options.text: Satellite patterns can help warn people before contaminated water reaches inland users
Cholera bacteria can survive only inside floating phytoplankton blooms
Ships are no longer useful for studying ocean microbes of any kind
Inland flooding occurs only when phytoplankton concentrations are low

evidence.quote: satellite data showing a combination of warm waters, high ocean levels, and a concentration of phytoplankton can help us forecast cholera outbreaks before they reach these waterways

private.explanationZh: 这题考查根据例子推出作用。既然卫星数据能在霍乱进入人们使用的河湖之前进行预测，就可以推断这种信息具有预警价值，能帮助人们提前准备。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它把“before they reach these waterways”进一步表述为在内陆用水者受影响前发出预警。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在“only”。原文只是说浮游植物水华有时会成为霍乱细菌的栖身地，并没有说这是唯一环境。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在绝对化。文中只说与卫星相比，用船收集同等规模数据会非常慢，并没有说船完全没用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在无依据。原文提到的是高浮游植物浓度与风险预测有关，不是低浓度才导致内陆洪水。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention Mars and microscope-equipped rovers?

options.text: To argue that Martian rocks are easier to study than Earth forests
To show that satellites have made microscopes unnecessary in planetary science
To explain why salt-loving microbes can no longer live on Earth
To illustrate that satellite clues can guide later close-up searches for life

evidence.quote: Using that knowledge, we've started to direct our microscope-equipped rovers to the most likely locations to find traces of life.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到火星和漫游车，不是为了换话题，而是把前面的论点推广到行星探测：先用卫星在大范围内找线索，再把近距离设备派到最可能的位置。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在比较对象。原文没有说火星岩石比地球森林更容易研究。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在与结论矛盾。作者强调卫星不会完全取代显微镜，漫游车恰好说明近距离工具仍然必要。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在内容捏造。文中说的是盐生微生物可能曾在火星存在，并未说它们无法在地球生存。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个例子正是为了说明卫星发现的大尺度模式可以引导后续更精细的生命搜索。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-177-q01: 转录反复对比显微镜与卫星，核心结论是卫星能看到微生物造成的大尺度影响，并与近距离研究互补；并未说只谈植物病害，也未说能完全取代地面或实验室观察。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-177-q02: 原文明确说使用太空数据“significantly more efficient because we can inspect large swaths of land in minutes”，所以原因是能在短时间检查大片土地。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-177-q03: 文中说温暖海水、高海平面和浮游植物集中这类卫星信号可以帮助我们在霍乱进入人们使用的河湖前进行预测，因此可推知它能提前预警。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-177-q04: 提到火星与带显微镜的探测车，是为了说明先用卫星找到最可能有生命痕迹的位置，再进行近距离搜寻，突出两类工具的配合关系。

public.prompt: What broader claim does the speaker make about how humans browse online?

options.text: Human web browsing follows animal foraging principles about when to stay or move on
Internet use has created a kind of decision-making that is unrelated to biology
Humans, chickadees, and burglars all search for rewards in exactly the same way
Online behavior is too new for scientists to explain with existing models

evidence.quote: online, we forage for information just as, say, a chickadee forages for fruit;
both you and the chickadee will forage in one place until the rate of reward you're getting there drops below what you think you're likely to get elsewhere.

private.explanationZh: 主旨是把上网浏览解释成一种“觅食”行为。作者先把找网站类比为鸟找果树，再说明人和鸟都会在当前回报下降到不如别处时离开，因此全文核心就是这个跨物种的觅食模型。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了全文用动物觅食理论解释人类网络浏览的中心论点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在与原文相反。作者正是要说明网络行为仍然能用生物学和生态学模型解释。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在“exactly the same way”。原文说的是共享一些决策约束和机制，不是所有行为细节完全相同。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在与前文矛盾。文中明确说生态学家早已有模型，而且这些模型还能预测人类在网站之间移动。

public.prompt: According to the study mentioned in the talk, what tended to happen when burglars traveled farther to commit a crime?

options.text: They were more likely to be caught before taking anything
They usually stole a larger number of small objects
They tended to come away with more expensive loot
They often decided that the trip was not worth the effort

evidence.quote: a study of more than 400 robberies in the Netherlands found that the farther burglars travel to commit their crimes, the more expensive their loot tends to be.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。原文对荷兰抢劫案研究的结论说得非常直接：作案路程越远，抢到的战利品往往越贵。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在原文没有提到被抓的概率。研究关注的是距离和战利品价值的关系。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在把“更贵”改成“数量更多”。原文说的是价值，不是件数。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确复述了研究发现。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在无依据。文中没有说他们常常放弃作案。

public.prompt: What do the reward-tracking neurons suggest about human online behavior?

options.text: It depends mainly on language abilities that only humans possess
It relies partly on ancient decision systems shared with other animals
It is controlled by a brain system that evolved specifically for websites
It can be improved only by studying monkeys in laboratory settings

evidence.quote: We probably optimize like animals because we are animals, and in fact, we share critical decision-making circuitry.

private.explanationZh: 这题要求根据神经元的例子推断。既然人类和猴子共享关键的决策回路，那么人类在网站之间切换的行为就不是全新的，而是部分依赖古老而共享的机制。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在把重点放到人类独有的语言能力上。原文强调的是跨物种共享的决策回路。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它把“share critical decision-making circuitry”转化成对人类网络行为来源的合理推断。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在“specifically for websites”。网站非常新，不可能是这种神经机制进化时的专门对象。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在“only”。猴子研究提供证据，但原文没有说改善这类行为只能靠实验室猴子研究。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention TV shows, DMV lines, jobs, and relationships?

options.text: To show that modern people waste more time than animals do
To argue that online browsing is less important than other kinds of decisions
To prove that people consciously calculate rewards in every situation
To extend the stay-or-leave tradeoff beyond web surfing to everyday life

evidence.quote: But it's not just web surfing...at what point do you move on from a lame TV show, or ditch the long line at the DMV, or give up on a job - or even a relationship - that you're not that into?

private.explanationZh: 作者在结尾连举多个生活场景，是为了把前面关于“何时离开当前选择”的模型推广到更广泛的人类决策中，而不是只谈上网。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在比较“谁更浪费时间”并不是该段的功能。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在重点不对。作者不是贬低上网，而是说明同一种原则适用于更多情境。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在“consciously”。前文还特别说这种计算是下意识的。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个例子组的作用就是把觅食式权衡扩展到日常生活。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-178-q01: 开头用找网站类比鸟类觅食，随后明确说同一个觅食模型也能预测人类在网站之间如何移动，因此主旨就是网络浏览遵循类似动物觅食的停留或离开原则。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-178-q02: 原文直接说荷兰那项研究发现，窃贼走得越远，战利品往往越贵，因此应选“more expensive loot”。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-178-q03: 讲话人说猴子有追踪回报率的神经元，人类也有，而且这种机制可能跨很多代传下来，所以可推知人类上网行为部分依赖与其他动物共享的古老决策系统。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-178-q04: 列举电视剧、车管所排队、工作和关系，是把“什么时候该离开当前选择”这一权衡从上网扩展到日常生活中的其他情境。

public.prompt: What overall issue about farm drainage is the speaker mainly discussing?

options.text: Why most farms fail because they do not use enough fertilizer
How agricultural drainage improves farming while also creating environmental costs
Why wetlands are more productive than cropland in every situation
How underground pipes were invented to stop all river flooding

evidence.quote: too much water is also deadly for crops.
So, thanks to drainage we get new and more productive farmland, which helps us grow lots of extra crops. But we also lose wetlands and gain some new problems.

private.explanationZh: 主旨是农业排水的两面性。前面解释为什么农田会因为太湿而需要排水，后面总结排水确实带来更多、更高产的耕地，但也伴随湿地损失和新的环境问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在把中心转成化肥不足。化肥只是后文提到的污染来源之一，不是全文主题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它完整概括了排水对农业的好处和对环境的代价。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在绝对化。原文并没有说湿地在所有情境下都比农田更高产。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在过度简化。文中反而说排水会让水更快进入河流，从而加剧洪水问题。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why can crop roots drown when soil stays too wet?

options.text: Water fills the soil spaces that normally provide underground cells with air
Wet soil makes plants produce too much oxygen above ground
Roots can absorb only fertilizer, not water, from saturated soil
Soil bacteria immediately consume the roots once the field is wet

evidence.quote: the cells underground take it from tiny air pockets in the soil.
if the soil is too wet for too long, the roots can literally drown, killing the entire plant.

private.explanationZh: 题目问根部“淹死”的原因。原文先说明地下细胞依赖土壤中的小空气孔获取氧气，随后说明土壤长期过湿时根会被“淹死”，所以关键机制就是空气空间被水占据后缺氧。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它把两句信息整合起来：根需要土壤中的空气，太湿时这些空气空间不足。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在把原因放到地上部分的氧气生产。原文讨论的是地下根部如何获得氧气。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在无依据。文中没有说饱和土壤里根只能吸收化肥。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在原文没有提到细菌立刻吃掉根部。重点是缺氧和土壤结构问题。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention wood-chip trenches and buffers of native plants?

options.text: To explain how farmers can move more water into streams during storms
To show that pesticides are harmless once they enter drainage water
To give examples of ways to reduce chemical pollution from drained water
To argue that restoring wetlands is less effective than underground pipes

evidence.quote: We can also send the water through trenches filled with wood chips or buffers of native plants, both of which can filter out some of the chemicals before the water flows into a nearby stream.

private.explanationZh: 这里举木屑沟和本地植物缓冲带，是为了说明并不是只能接受排水带来的污染，也可以通过具体措施在水进入河流前先过滤掉部分化学物质。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在方向相反。作者提这些做法是为了减轻危害，不是为了把更多水更快送进河流。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在与原文相反。原文明确把化肥和农药视为会污染水体的化学物质。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确说明了这些例子的修辞功能。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在比较对象不对。这里没有讨论它们是否比湿地恢复更有效。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the speaker's conclusion about field drainage?

options.text: The best solution is to eliminate drainage from agriculture entirely
Drainage creates problems only when farmers use too much fertilizer
Wetlands can be replaced with underground pipes without any losses
It is unrealistic to expect all of drainage's benefits without any tradeoffs

evidence.quote: And unfortunately, gaining the benefits without any of the downsides may be a pipe dream.

private.explanationZh: 结尾用“pipe dream”表明作者的判断：排水确实有农业收益，但想把所有好处都保留、同时完全没有代价，这种期待并不现实。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在过度推断。作者没有主张彻底取消农业排水，而是强调权衡和减害。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在把问题缩小到化肥。文中还提到洪水、河道侵蚀、湿地消失等多种后果。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在与结论相反。作者明确说排水伴随湿地损失和其他代价。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它正是对“without any of the downsides may be a pipe dream”的合理转述。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-179-q01: 整篇先讲排水怎样保护作物、提高生产力，再讲洪水、污染、湿地消失等代价，因此主题是农业排水既有农业收益也有环境成本。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-179-q02: 原文说地下细胞靠土壤中的微小空气袋获取氧气；土壤过湿太久时，根会被淹死，所以原因就是水占据了原本提供空气的土壤空间。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-179-q03: 木屑沟和本地植物缓冲带是在讲排水带出的化学物质进入溪流前如何被过滤，因此它们是减少排水化学污染的例子。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-179-q04: 结尾明确说想获得排水的好处却没有任何坏处可能只是“pipe dream”，可推知不现实的是既保留全部收益又完全没有代价。

public.prompt: What main problem and solution direction does the talk focus on regarding exercise?

options.text: Why exercise guidelines differ so much from one country to another
How treadmill desks have solved the global problem of inactivity
Why regular exercise is difficult for many people and how modern life might encourage more movement
How mental health benefits are the only reason people should be active

evidence.quote: unfortunately, that's really hard, because it seems to require fighting against our genetics.
some people are calling for us to reincorporate more non-exercise physical activity back into modern life.

private.explanationZh: 全文先解释运动为什么难，坚持锻炼像是在对抗遗传倾向；随后又转向可能的解决方向，即把更多非锻炼式身体活动重新融入现代生活，所以主旨同时包含“难点”和“改进思路”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在把话题缩成各国指南差异。那只是开头背景，不是核心论证。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在与原文不符。跑步机办公桌只是结尾玩笑式提法，根本没有说它解决了全球问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它同时概括了演讲对原因和可能对策的讨论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在“only”。原文还谈到寿命、疾病风险和日常环境设计等内容。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why might ancient people who rested when they could have had an advantage?

options.text: They avoided all injuries by never moving unless they were forced to
They conserved energy for getting food and were more likely to survive and reproduce
They developed stronger memories than people who were physically active
They had more time to invent modern transportation systems

evidence.quote: ancient people who took it easy when they weren't searching for food replenished more energy than people who also did pointless physical activity. As a result, they had more energy for hunting and gathering, and were more likely to survive and reproduce.

private.explanationZh: 这是直接细节。作者解释说，在食物来之不易的环境里，能在非必要时休息的人会保留更多能量，用于觅食、生存和繁殖，因此这种倾向更可能被传下来。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在绝对化。原文说的是节省能量带来优势，不是完全不动就能避免所有伤害。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它完整概括了原文的因果链：节能，觅食更有力，生存繁殖机会更高。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在把优势说成记忆更强。记忆是开头谈现代缺乏运动的后果，不是祖先休息的优势。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错得离题。现代交通系统与该段的进化解释无关。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker list roads, stairways, airports, lawns, and gaming?

options.text: To provide a series of examples showing how daily life could be redesigned to increase incidental activity
To rank which kinds of exercise burn the most calories in modern cities
To show that people prefer outdoor exercise to indoor exercise in every culture
To explain why formal workouts are no longer necessary for healthy adults

evidence.quote: If we built fewer roads and more paths, it would be easier for people to use their own power to get to school or work. If we put stairways at the main entrance to buildings rather than hiding them in a back corridor, more people would use them rather than elevators. If our airports had fewer human conveyor belts, more people would walk. If we turned our lawns into gardens, we could be active by raising and gathering food.

private.explanationZh: 这一长串例子在篇章中的作用很清楚：作者从解释问题转到提出方案，用不同生活场景展示怎样通过环境设计，让人们在不知不觉中多活动。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确说明了这串例子在结构上的功能。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在原文没有比较哪种活动消耗热量最多。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在“every culture”且偏离重点。作者不是在比较文化偏好。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在与后文矛盾。作者随后明确说大多数人仍然可能需要有意识地锻炼。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about incidental physical activity in modern life?

options.text: It is already sufficient for most people who have desk jobs
It can replace intentional exercise as long as cities add more sidewalks
It matters only for people who dislike sports and games
By itself, it probably will not be enough for most people to stay healthy

evidence.quote: And most people wouldn't be able to easily incorporate enough incidental physical activity into their daily life. So to be healthy, most of us would still probably need to intentionally exercise in addition to being incidentally physically active.

private.explanationZh: 根据结尾两句可以推断，作者认可顺便活动身体有帮助，但也认为单靠这种零散活动通常不够，大多数人仍需要专门锻炼。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在与原文相反。作者说大多数人并不容易把足够多的顺便活动纳入日常。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在过度推断。增加步道会有帮助，但作者没有说这样就能完全替代有意锻炼。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在无依据。文中讨论的是多数人的健康需求，不是某一类性格或兴趣人群。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它是对原文结论的直接合理推断。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-180-q01: 文章先解释多数人为什么难以运动，原因与遗传倾向和现代环境有关，后半段再提出通过重新设计日常生活来增加活动量，因此C完整概括了问题与解决方向。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-180-q02: 原文说古人如果在不找食物时节省体力，就能把更多能量用于狩猎和采集，更可能生存和繁殖，所以答案是保存能量以获取食物并提高生存繁殖机会。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-180-q03: 道路、楼梯、机场、草坪和游戏是一串并列实例，用来说明怎样把现代日常环境改造得更容易产生附带性的身体活动。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-180-q04: 讲话人明确说大多数人无法仅靠附带性活动就获得足够运动量，仍可能需要刻意锻炼，因此可推知单靠附带性活动通常不够。

public.prompt: What central point does the speaker make about COX in modern life?

options.text: A body system that evolved to protect humans can also cause serious problems in modern conditions
Modern medicine has made infections and injuries more dangerous than heart disease
The best way to improve health is to eliminate COX activity in everyone
Aspirin was developed mainly to help people with stomach ulcers

evidence.quote: in an ironic twist, we may be more likely to be harmed by our COX's response to perceived threats than we are from the actual injuries and infections it's supposed to protect us from.

private.explanationZh: 主旨集中在“保护机制反过来伤人”。作者说明 COX 原本用于应对受伤和感染，但在现代环境下，它对并非真正威胁的情况也会启动反应，结果造成新的重大健康问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它抓住了全文最核心的反转：原本有益的防御系统在现代条件下可能带来伤害。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在颠倒意思。作者说现代人的主要威胁已不再主要是伤害和感染。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在绝对化。文中明确说我们并不想完全消除 COX，因为它仍有重要作用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错得离题。阿司匹林是在讨论如何降低某些 COX 相关风险时出现的，不是为溃疡而开发。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what does COX help do during an infection?

options.text: It produces cholesterol deposits that trap invading microbes
It shuts down blood flow so pathogens cannot spread
It blocks immune cells from entering damaged tissue
It helps dilate blood vessels so fluid and immune cells can reach the intruders

evidence.quote: when we get an infection, the nearby COX makes a different chemical that helps dilate our blood vessels, so that a rush of fluid brings our tough immune cells to fight the intruders.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道直接细节题。原文明确说感染时 COX 会促使血管扩张，让液体和免疫细胞赶到现场对抗入侵者。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在把 COX 的作用说成制造胆固醇沉积。胆固醇沉积是后文谈现代问题时提到的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在方向相反。这里是帮助更多液体和免疫细胞到达，而不是关闭血流。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在与原文相反。COX 的相关反应正是为了让免疫细胞进入现场。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它基本就是对原句的准确转述。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the example of broken cholesterol deposits?

options.text: COX can repair damaged blood vessels before a clot forms
COX can mistake internal buildup for a wound and create a dangerous clot
Cholesterol deposits protect the heart by storing extra platelets
Heart attacks occur only when people already have an infection

evidence.quote: If one of the globs happens to break open, COX gets confused and initiates the same sticky platelet response that it does when you actually rupture a blood vessel. Except that this time the resulting clot can stop blood flow altogether.

private.explanationZh: 这题要求从例子中推出机制。胆固醇斑块破裂并不等于真正的外伤，但 COX 会“搞混”，照样启动凝血反应，于是反而形成危险的血流阻塞。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在与原文不符。这里强调的不是成功修复血管，而是错误触发凝血导致风险。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了 COX 把异常沉积误判成伤口后的后果。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错在无依据。原文没有说胆固醇沉积有保护作用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在绝对化。文中说心脏附近发生这种堵塞会导致心脏病发作，并未要求先有感染。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker discuss both the benefits and the risks of regular Aspirin use?

options.text: To show that Aspirin is mainly useful because it protects the stomach lining
To argue that everyone over fifty should take the same dose every day
To show that weakening COX can help some high-risk people even though it also creates tradeoffs
To prove that internal bleeding is less serious than any COX-related disease

evidence.quote: Taking Aspirin regularly has some risks: by blocking COX, we're more prone to internal bleeding and stomach ulcers. But even so, studies show that when people over 50 with high risk of heart problems regularly take low-dose Aspirin, they on average have reduced health care costs and longer lifespans.

private.explanationZh: 作者在这里同时讲好处和风险，是为了说明这不是简单的“药越多越好”。对某些高风险人群，降低 COX 反应可能净收益更大，但这种做法也伴随明确代价，因此需要权衡。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错在内容相反。前文说 COX 本身帮助保护胃黏膜，而阿司匹林阻断 COX 反而会增加胃溃疡风险。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错在过度概括。原文说的是“over 50 with high risk of heart problems”，不是所有五十岁以上的人。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确解释了作者为什么要并列呈现益处与风险。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错在无依据。作者没有比较哪一种问题“更不严重”，而是在讨论净收益和副作用。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-181-q01: 中心论点是COX原本用于应对受伤和感染，但在现代条件下常被不必要地触发，反而导致心脏病、中风、自身免疫等严重问题。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-181-q02: 原文直接说感染时附近的COX会制造一种化学物质，帮助血管扩张，让液体和免疫细胞赶来对抗入侵者。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-181-q03: 关于胆固醇斑块破裂的例子，讲话人说COX会把它误当成血管破裂并启动血小板反应，结果形成危险血栓，因此应选B。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-181-q04: 同时讲阿司匹林的好处和风险，是为了说明抑制COX对某些高风险人群有帮助，但也会带来出血和胃溃疡等权衡，并不是无代价的万能办法。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the talk?

options.text: Why adults often struggle with new languages because of social and behavioral barriers
Why children should avoid learning second languages before age ten
Why native-like fluency depends mostly on inherited intelligence
Why second languages can be learned only in school

evidence.quote: But the real culprits may be two sociological barriers that come with adulting.

private.explanationZh: 主讲人先提到成年人学语言往往不如儿童顺利，随后明确指出，真正的关键并不是大脑老化本身，而是成年生活带来的两类社会性和行为性障碍。因此全文核心是在解释成年人学新语言困难的主要原因。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了全文中心：成年人的困难主要来自投入不足和过度怕犯错这两类障碍。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有主张儿童应避免早学第二语言；相反，儿童通常更容易达到高水平流利度。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲者专门淡化了“年轻大脑天生更适合”的解释，没有把原因归结为先天智力。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中提到学校是儿童必须使用新语言的场景之一，但绝不是唯一学习场所，更不是全文主旨。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why do children often gain fluency faster than adults?

options.text: They are required to memorize grammar rules every day.
They usually have to keep using the new language even when they want a break.
They already know more pop culture in the new language.
They receive more correction from employers and coworkers.

evidence.quote: But kids often have to keep trying even when they don't want to, which means they do it more, which helps them gain fluency quicker.

private.explanationZh: 这一题考查中段细节。讲者强调，儿童即使不想用新语言，也常常不得不继续使用；这种高频、持续的实际使用直接帮助他们更快获得流利度。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说儿童主要靠每天死记语法规则来获得流利度。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句明确说明儿童被迫持续使用新语言，因此练习更多、流利度提升更快。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文说儿童需要跟上流行文化，这解释的是他们更有投入动机，不是这道题对应的直接原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有提到雇主或同事给儿童更多纠正，这与原文不符。

public.prompt: What does the speaker suggest is the most effective strategy for an adult learning a new language?

options.text: Waiting until every sentence is correct before speaking
Studying verb rules alone before trying real conversations
Communicating often in many situations despite making mistakes
Focusing only on reading and listening until fluency appears naturally

evidence.quote: simply letting loose and trying to communicate as often as possible in as many situations as possible - mistakes and all - seems to be the very best way to learn a new language.

private.explanationZh: 讲者并不是鼓励成年人先把规则学完再开口，而是从“最有效的方法”反推出一种更大胆的学习策略：即使会出错，也要在尽可能多的场景里频繁沟通。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文批评成年人过度担心说错，等待完全正确再开口正是低效做法。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲者承认成年人在套用规则上可能更擅长，但并未把孤立学语法说成最佳策略。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据句直接把“放开去交流、允许犯错”描述为学习新语言的最佳方式。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文强调积极输出和多场景交流，而不是长期只输入不说。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention Kató Lomb near the end of the talk?

options.text: To show that adults can still become highly successful language learners
To argue that older learners should study only one extra language
To prove that language learning automatically gets easier with age
To explain who first discovered bilingualism

evidence.quote: some adults have learned to fluently speak several extra languages.
One of those hyperlinguists - Kató Lomb, started learning new languages after age 10 and kept learning more and more throughout her life - including a final language when she was well into her 80s.

private.explanationZh: 这里举 Kató Lomb 不是为了补充人物知识，而是作为结尾例证，支撑前一句“成年人也能流利掌握多种额外语言”的论点。这个例子把抽象结论变成了可感知的现实案例。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。她被当作反例来说明：成年人若跨过障碍，仍然可能成为非常成功的语言学习者。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文恰恰说她学了多种语言，不是只学一种。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲者并未说年龄越大越容易学语言，只是说高龄也并非绝对障碍。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有讨论谁“发现了”双语现象，Kató Lomb 也不是以此身份被提到。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-182-q01: 开头先指出成年人学习新语言往往不如儿童，随后明确说“真正的罪魁祸首”可能是成年生活带来的两种社会性障碍：投入不足和害怕犯错，因此主旨是成年人因社会和行为障碍而常难学好新语言。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-182-q02: 原文明确说成年人想暂停使用新语言时通常可以暂停，而孩子即使不想也常常必须继续使用，所以他们练得更多、流利得更快。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-182-q03: 讲话者明确总结说，最好的方法似乎就是“放开手脚”，尽可能频繁、在尽可能多的情境中去交流，哪怕会犯错，因此应选经常主动交流这一项。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-182-q04: 提到Kató Lomb是为了举出成年后仍持续学习多种语言、甚至八十多岁还学新语言的实例，用来证明成年人也能非常成功地学会语言。

public.prompt: What is the speaker's main point about trees and climate?

options.text: Planting trees can help remove carbon, but it must be combined with other changes.
Trees are the only realistic solution to climate change.
Most atmospheric carbon comes from building large stadiums.
TeamTrees has already solved the global carbon problem.

evidence.quote: trees can do this entire job for us.
and we don't have enough space for an additional trillion trees every decade, so new trees alone won't cut it.

private.explanationZh: 讲者先肯定树木吸碳能力很强，但紧接着指出，仅靠新增树木并不足以应对问题。因此全文主旨不是“树无用”，也不是“树万能”，而是“植树有帮助，但必须与其他措施一起进行”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时保留了讲者的两层意思：树木重要，但单靠植树不够。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文明确说 new trees alone will not cut it，否定了“唯一方案”的说法。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。stadiums-worth of air 只是帮助听众想象空气体量的比喻，不是说体育场造成了大多数碳排放。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲者把 TeamTrees 说成一个起点，而不是问题已经被解决。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker say new trees alone will not be enough?

options.text: They remove carbon too slowly to matter.
Young trees are always cut down right after planting.
Humanity keeps adding more carbon while lacking space for that many new trees.
Trees can absorb carbon only during their first year of growth.

evidence.quote: we're currently putting an additional trillion stadiums-worth of carbon into the air every decade,
and we don't have enough space for an additional trillion trees every decade, so new trees alone won't cut it.

private.explanationZh: 这道题对应的是中段的两个并列原因：一方面人类还在持续排放更多碳，另一方面可供继续种下海量新树的空间不足，所以只靠新种树木无法完成任务。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说树吸碳“太慢而没有意义”，而是说规模与空间问题限制了单一植树方案。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有提到新树会被立刻砍掉。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。答案完整概括了证据中的两个关键限制：持续新增排放，以及没有足够空间继续种同等规模的树。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文没有说树只在生长第一年吸碳，这属于无根据添加。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker say that 20 million trees are both a lot and only a fraction of what is needed?

options.text: To show that the campaign is meaningless compared with global emissions
To acknowledge the campaign's limits while still presenting it as a useful start
To explain why the Arbor Day Foundation refused to join the effort
To argue that small donations should be postponed until a larger plan exists

evidence.quote: 20 million is both a ton of trees, and at the same time just a fraction of the trillion trees we need to plant, but we've got to start somewhere.

private.explanationZh: 这句话的作用是平衡评价：讲者既没有夸大 TeamTrees 的作用，也没有否定它，而是承认其规模相对全球需求仍然有限，但仍值得作为行动起点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲者没有说活动毫无意义，反而认为必须从某处开始。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句同时表达了“有限”和“值得开始”两层目的。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有说 Arbor Day Foundation 拒绝参与。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲者是在鼓励现在就开始行动，而不是劝大家等更大计划出现后再捐。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the speaker's goal in mentioning a message to politicians?

options.text: The speaker wants scientific research on trees to stop.
The speaker believes public participation could pressure leaders to act on climate change.
The speaker thinks politicians are already doing enough.
The speaker wants donations to replace all environmental policy.

evidence.quote: we'd crush the goal and send a message to politicians that it's time to get serious about climate change.

private.explanationZh: 讲者把公众捐树和“向政客传递信息”联系起来，隐含意思是：大量公众参与不仅能直接种树，也能形成政治压力，促使领导者更认真对待气候问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文完全没有主张停止树木研究。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这里的“message to politicians”说明讲者希望公众行动转化为政策层面的压力。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。若讲者认为政客已经做得够多，就不会说“it's time to get serious”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲者没有把捐款当成政策替代品，而是把它视作更大行动的一部分。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-183-q01: 前半段说明树木可以从空气中移除碳，后半段马上补充“仅靠新树还不够”，还需要新型能源、更可持续的食物以及保护现有树木，因此主旨是种树有帮助但必须配合其他改变。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-183-q02: 原文直接说人类每十年还会继续向空气中增加额外的大量碳，而且没有足够空间每十年再多种那么多树，所以新树本身不够。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-183-q03: 讲话者说两千万棵树一方面很多，另一方面又只是所需一万亿棵中的一小部分，紧接着又说“总得从某处开始”，所以作用是承认局限，同时强调这仍是有价值的起点。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-183-q04: 讲话者说如果很多订阅者都参与，就能“向政客发出信息，是时候认真对待气候变化了”，可推知他认为公众参与能向领导者施压，推动他们采取行动。

public.prompt: How is the talk mainly organized?

options.text: By comparing dragon stories from different cultures in time order
By ranking dragons from least biologically plausible to most plausible
By explaining how one film studio designed every major dragon
By moving from small reptiles to large mammals

evidence.quote: To rank these dragons' believability, we'll start with the most egregiously unnatural characteristics of dragons and move up from there.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接说明了篇章组织方式：讲者要按照“可信度”从最不自然的特征一路往上排。因此本题考查的是整体结构，而不是某个具体科学事实。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲者没有按不同文化或历史先后比较龙的传说。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句明确说会从最离谱的特征开始，一路往更可信的方向推进。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中会提到电影版本，但全文并不是围绕某一家工作室的设计流程展开。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲者讨论的是虚构龙的生物可行性，不是从小型爬行动物讲到大型哺乳动物。

public.prompt: Why are six-limbed dragons placed in the bottom tier?

options.text: No living animals have ever used wings for flight.
Their fire breathing would make their skeletons unstable.
Earth's bony animals share a four-limbed evolutionary blueprint.
Their tails would be too short to balance their bodies.

evidence.quote: all bony creatures on Earth evolved from a four-limbed fish whose body is the blueprint for every other bony creature ... including a hypothetical dragon, clearly a bony creature. This would make it very unlikely for a six-limbed dragon to evolve on Earth.

private.explanationZh: 讲者把六肢龙放到最低档，理由不是它们不够酷，而是它们违背了地球上硬骨动物共同继承的四肢身体蓝图，所以从进化角度看非常不可信。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文并未否认翼可用于飞行；相反，鸟、蝙蝠和翼龙都被当作模型。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。火焰呼吸在前面被提到不现实，但六肢龙被归入底层的直接原因是肢体数量问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据句把四足鱼的身体蓝图作为所有硬骨动物的共同起点，这正是六肢龙不可信的原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有用尾巴长度来解释六肢龙为何垫底。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about a dragon that has a keel but relatively small wings?

options.text: It could glide easily without much effort.
It might have the right chest structure, but natural flight would still require impossible speed and forces.
It would evolve into a penguin before adulthood.
It would be more realistic than the Hungarian Horntail.

evidence.quote: The more a creature weights, the bigger its wings need to be or the faster it needs to fly to generate the necessary lift to keep it afloat.
they'd need to fly at ridiculously fast speeds in order to sustain flight and the forces involved would destroy any flesh and bone based wings.

private.explanationZh: 这题要求整合两处信息。单有 keel 只说明它具备附着飞行肌肉的结构条件，但如果翼面积相对体重太小，就必须以离谱的速度飞行，而那样的受力又会毁掉翅膀，因此仍然无法实现自然飞行。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说小翅膀会让飞行变轻松，恰恰相反，它会带来更苛刻的速度要求。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。答案同时整合了“有 keel 不等于能飞”和“翼太小会导致不可能的速度与受力”这两点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。企鹅只是举来说明某些参数组合会导致荒谬后果，不是说龙会进化成企鹅。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。Hungarian Horntail 之所以最现实，正因为它相对体重拥有更大的翅膀，不是小翅膀。

public.prompt: Why is the Hungarian Horntail presented as the most realistic dragon?

options.text: It is the only dragon that appears in a modern film franchise.
It has magical flight that frees it from physical limits.
It best matches the speaker's criteria for limbs, muscle support, and wing size.
It breathes fire in a way that closely resembles bombardier beetles.

evidence.quote: the most realistic has to be the one with the biggest wings in relation to their weight.
And the winner is: the Hungarian Horntail from Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire. It has four limbs, a keel to hold its muscles, and the biggest wings in relation to its weight.

private.explanationZh: 结尾并不是单纯公布“谁赢了”，而是在前面建立的三套标准基础上给出结论：四肢数量要合理、胸部结构要能支撑飞行肌肉、翼展和体重关系也要最接近可飞行状态。Hungarian Horntail 最符合这些条件。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。是否出现在现代电影里不是评判标准。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲者讨论的是遵守地球生物和物理规则的“现实性”，不是靠魔法摆脱限制。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据句明确列出它拥有四肢、keel，以及相对于体重最大的翅膀，这正是评判它最现实的原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲者开头基本放弃了“喷火现实化”的可能性，这不是 Horntail 胜出的关键。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-184-q01: 开头明确说他们要给这些著名幻想龙按“可信度”排序，并且会从最不自然的特征一路往上讲，因此整体结构就是从最不符合生物规律到最符合生物规律地排名。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-184-q02: 原文解释说地球上所有有骨骼的动物都来自四肢鱼类这一四肢蓝图，因此六肢龙在地球上进化出来“非常不可能”，所以被放在最低档。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-184-q03: 讲话者明确说只有龙骨还不够飞行，若体重大而翅膀太小，就必须以荒谬的高速飞行，相关作用力会毁掉肉和骨构成的翅膀，所以这类龙即使胸部结构对，天然飞行也不现实。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-184-q04: 结尾直接总结Hungarian Horntail有四肢、有用来附着飞行肌肉的龙骨，而且相对体重拥有最大的翅膀，最符合前面建立的评判标准。

public.prompt: What is the main argument of the talk?

options.text: Climate change can be solved mainly by making phones cheaper.
Smartphones matter because they show how design can focus on function instead of individual devices.
Alarm clocks and cameras used less energy than smartphones do.
People reduce energy use mainly by buying more electronics.

evidence.quote: The smartphone story could even be a model for fighting climate change; not because smartphones use a fraction of the energy of all the things they replace - although they do - but because they represent a different approach to design in general. And that approach is to focus on function rather than form.

private.explanationZh: 全文借智能手机的例子，不是为了夸手机本身，而是为了说明一种设计思路：不要只改进单个装置，而要先抓住所需功能，再寻找更系统、更节能的解决方案。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有把“让手机更便宜”当作气候方案。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句直接把智能手机当作模型，说明核心在于“以功能为中心”的设计思路。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文反而说智能手机替代的许多设备合起来会消耗更多能量。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲者强调的是重新设计系统，而不是通过购买更多电子产品来省能。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker describe a house that can stay warm without a furnace?

options.text: To show that focusing on the desired function can eliminate the need for an energy-using device
To argue that furnaces never work well in cold climates
To compare home heating costs in the early 2000s and today
To suggest that insulation matters more than transportation policy in every case
