evidence.quote: They designed and insulated their house so well that they could get rid of their furnace altogether, letting them heat their house with 99% less energy.

private.explanationZh: 这里的房屋例子是论证工具。讲者用它说明：如果目标功能是“保持温暖”，那么最优方案未必是造出更高效的炉子，而可能是通过整体设计让炉子本身变得不再必要。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个例子正是为了展示“围绕功能重新设计”能够直接取消一个耗能设备。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有否定炉子在寒冷地区的有效性。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲者没有比较两个年代的家庭取暖成本。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。例子只是在说明一种思路，并未断言保温在所有情境下都比交通政策更重要。

public.prompt: What transportation idea does the speaker support?

options.text: Replacing every personal car with a newer personal car
Building systems that let people get where they need to go with less dependence on personal cars
Limiting travel so people stay near home as much as possible
Using smartphones to control household furnaces remotely

evidence.quote: rather than just making cars more efficient, what if we focus on the desired function - getting where we want when we want - and create an efficient transportation system where we can drive less or get rid of our personal cars entirely?

private.explanationZh: 讲者把“交通工具更省油”与“人如何高效到达目的地”区分开来，隐含支持的是后者：通过更高效的交通系统减少对私人汽车的依赖，而不是仅仅换一辆更省油的车。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文明确说不应只停留在让汽车本身更高效。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。答案准确改写了“围绕出行功能设计系统、减少开车或摆脱私家车”的主张。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲者没有主张压缩正常出行需求，而是主张用更高效系统满足这种需求。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这与交通系统讨论无关，只是把手机话题机械挪用到错误场景。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker end the main discussion by saying, "it's not thinking efficient, it's thinking different"?

options.text: To summarize the shift from improving single devices to rethinking the function people want
To criticize smartphone companies for copying an advertising slogan
To claim that efficiency is impossible without legal reform
To suggest that unusual ideas are always cheaper than practical ones

evidence.quote: In other words, it's not thinking efficient, it's thinking different.

private.explanationZh: 这句结尾语起到总结和升华作用。讲者把前面关于手机、房屋和交通的多个例子压缩成一个核心对比：真正重要的不是孤立地提高单个装置效率，而是换一种思路，从功能需求出发重构系统。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了全文最重要的思维转向。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。即使句式让人联想到广告语，讲者在这里的功能是总结论点，而不是批评企业。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。法律改革只出现在赞助信息中，并非这句总结的核心。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有说“新奇想法总是更便宜”，而是说它们可能更节能、更有效。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-185-q01: 讲话者借智能手机整合许多设备的例子说明，应对气候变化的关键不只是改良单个设备，而是把注意力放在“功能”而不是“形式”或单一装置上，因此B最符合主旨。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-185-q02: 提到无需暖炉也能保暖的房子，是为了举例说明如果抓住“保持温暖”这一目标功能来重新设计，就可能彻底不需要某个耗能设备本身。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-185-q03: 关于交通，讲话者说与其只提高汽车效率，不如围绕“想去哪里就去哪里”这一功能去创造更高效的交通系统，从而少开车甚至不必拥有私家车。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-185-q04: 这句话紧接在“最节能的车或暖炉就是没有车、没有暖炉，但仍能移动和保暖”之后，是对全文核心转向的概括：不是优化单个设备，而是重想人真正需要实现的功能。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly about?

options.text: How artificial intelligence can help wildlife researchers handle huge amounts of animal data
Why underwater microphones are replacing all other wildlife research tools
How tourists can identify whale sharks without scientists
Why camera traps are less useful than handwritten field notes

evidence.quote: these methods can generate mountains of information really rapidly.
The AI turned out to be able to do this stuff about as accurately as humans - and the photos that took 14 years of human labor to classify took the program just a single day.

private.explanationZh: 整篇讲述的核心不是某一种设备本身，而是当野生动物研究产生海量信息后，人工智能如何帮助研究者更快地把这些信息转成可用数据，并由此改变研究流程。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了“AI 帮助研究者处理海量动物数据”的中心主题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。水下麦克风只是开头举的一个技术例子，不是全文要论证的唯一方向。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。游客视频只在后面作为补充数据来源出现，不是全文主题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文恰恰强调自动相机和 AI 的价值，没有说手写记录更有用。

public.prompt: How did the Snapshot Serengeti AI compare with human classifiers?

options.text: It was slower but better at spotting rare species.
It was about as accurate as humans and finished in a single day.
It needed humans to label every image before it could start.
It worked only on daytime photos of predators.

evidence.quote: The AI turned out to be able to do this stuff about as accurately as humans - and the photos that took 14 years of human labor to classify took the program just a single day.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查核心结果细节：AI 在准确率上与人类大致相当，但在处理速度上极快，把原本相当于十四年人类劳动的分类任务压缩到一天完成。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说它更慢；相反，它极大加快了处理速度。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。答案完整重述了准确率和速度两方面的比较结果。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中说算法通过试错学习识别，并未说必须先由人工把每张图全部标完。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。证据没有把能力限制在白天或捕食者照片上。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker bring up the AI program that searches YouTube for whale shark videos?

options.text: To show that AI can use massive online video collections as another source of wildlife data
To argue that vacation videos are more reliable than scientific observations
To explain that whale sharks are easier to study than land animals
To suggest that researchers should stop building camera networks

evidence.quote: One AI-based program spends each night combing through the 80 years of Youtube videos uploaded that day, looking for videos of whale sharks, like that awesome one someone saw on their vacation.

private.explanationZh: 讲者提到搜寻 YouTube 鲸鲨视频的程序，是为了把 AI 的用途从“处理科研项目内部数据”进一步扩展到“利用互联网海量公开视频”这一更广的场景。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个例子说明 AI 不只会处理相机陷阱照片，还能从巨大的网络视频库中提取研究信息。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲者没有说游客视频比科学观察更可靠，只是说它们也能成为有价值的数据来源。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。例子重点在数据来源与 AI 处理方式，不在比较哪类动物更容易研究。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲者并未建议停止相机网络建设，而是展示多种数据来源可以并存。

public.prompt: According to the end of the talk, what is an important task that humans still do better than AI?

options.text: Setting up every camera in the field by hand
Memorizing the spots on individual whale sharks
Interpreting patterns and drawing broader conclusions from the data
Uploading videos to the internet every night

evidence.quote: AI just might free up researchers' time and resources to connect the dots and draw new conclusions about their subjects - a task where artificial intelligence still can't beat the good old-fashioned human kind.

private.explanationZh: 结尾把 AI 与人类的分工说得很清楚：AI 擅长高效处理和分类大规模数据，但在把分散信息整合起来、理解意义并形成新的科学结论方面，人类仍然更强。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。结尾强调的不是体力性的布置工作，而是更高层次的分析任务。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。识别斑点正是 AI 程序已经能做的事之一，不是人类独占优势。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据句中的 connect the dots and draw new conclusions 正是人类仍优于 AI 的关键任务。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。上传视频是普通用户或平台行为，不是讲者强调的人类研究优势。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-186-q01: 整篇先讲野生动物研究会迅速产生海量信息，再讲AI如何识别物种、计数和分类行为，从而帮助研究者处理这些大量数据，因此主旨是AI帮助野生动物研究处理海量信息。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-186-q02: 原文明确说该AI“准确度大约和人类一样”，而且那些需要人类14年劳动来分类的照片，程序“一天”就完成了。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-186-q03: 举YouTube鲸鲨视频的例子，是为了说明AI不仅能处理科研相机照片，也能利用互联网上巨量视频这种另一类大规模数据来源来识别个体并记录观测信息。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-186-q04: 结尾说AI也许能释放研究者的时间和资源，让他们去“connect the dots and draw new conclusions”，并明确指出这是人工智能仍比不上人类的任务，因此应选解释模式并得出更广泛结论。

public.prompt: What broad change in human history is the talk mainly about?

options.text: Why ancient societies changed little over thousands of years
How rapid growth in human activity has transformed Earth and human influence
Why hockey stick graphs are misleading scientific tools
How universities are solving environmental problems

evidence.quote: That's because we're experiencing the Great Acceleration - a rapid and world-changing increase in the intensity and scale at which humans do nearly everything.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。核心句直接把“Great Acceleration”定义为人类几乎所有活动在强度和规模上的迅速扩大，说明整段都在讨论这种加速如何改变世界并放大人类影响。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这只是前半段用来对比过去变化缓慢的背景，不是整段主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了“Great Acceleration”以及人类影响变成行星尺度力量这一核心内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中提到曲棍球棒图只是引入现象，不是在评价这种图表本身是否误导。
optionRationalesZh.d: 大学内容只出现在结尾赞助和延伸部分，不是讲话主体。

public.prompt: According to the talk, if a person slept from 1020 to 1220, what would that person most likely notice upon waking?

options.text: People would be living much longer and using fossil fuels
The world would contain fewer domesticated animals
Daily life would be broadly similar, with only limited population growth
Mechanical industries would be replacing wood and animal power

evidence.quote: If someone went to bed in the year 1020 and slept for 200 years, they'd wake up in a world with slightly more people, living lives just as long (or as short), and with each person still using mostly wood for heat, and animals for power.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查细节。讲话者明确说，从1020年睡到1220年后，世界只是“人口略多一些”，寿命和能源使用方式都大体不变，所以整体生活模式仍然相似。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是1820到今天的对比内容，不是1020到1220的情况。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有说驯养动物减少，反而说人仍然依赖动物提供动力。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文直接说明只是人口略增，生活长短和能源来源基本一样。
optionRationalesZh.d: 机械工业和气候改变型燃料属于后来的现代世界，不适用于1220年。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention World War II and independence movements in Africa and Asia?

options.text: To explain when the Great Acceleration became a worldwide process
To claim fossil fuels quickly disappeared from Europe after the war
To show public health breakthroughs mattered only in Asia and Africa
To argue humans had already become a planetary force by the year 1020

evidence.quote: Initially, fossil fuels powered growth mostly in North America and Europe, but the big, planet-changing, worldwide Great Acceleration didn't happen until after World War II and independence movements in Africa and Asia, when people worldwide gained more access to these fuels, as well as to new farming technology and public health breakthroughs.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查举例和时间节点的作用。这里提到二战后和亚非独立运动，不是为了讲政治史本身，而是为了说明全球更多地区获得能源、农业技术和公共卫生进步之后，“大加速”才真正扩展成全球性的现象。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个历史节点用来解释加速何时从局部增长变成全球范围的巨大变化。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文说的是最初增长主要发生在北美和欧洲，并没有说欧洲战后停止使用化石燃料。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文把公共卫生突破与更广泛的全球获得联系起来，不是说它只对亚非重要。
optionRationalesZh.d: 讲话者恰恰说早期人类还“不算什么大事”，1020年时远未成为行星尺度力量。

public.prompt: Based on the talk, what is likely true about the world 200 years from now?

options.text: It will probably look much like the world of 1950
Human influence on the planet will certainly disappear
Its population size can be predicted from earlier centuries
Its conditions are difficult to predict because change is happening so quickly

evidence.quote: And because the world has gotten so different so quickly, if someone fell asleep today and woke up in 200 years, there's really no telling what they'd find.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查基于结论的推断。讲话者明确说，由于世界变化太快，现在睡去两百年后醒来会看到什么“真的很难说”，因此未来最突出的特点是不确定性高。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文强调变化越来越快，不支持未来会像1950年那样相似。
optionRationalesZh.b: 讲话者没有说人类影响会消失，反而一直在强调其规模巨大。
optionRationalesZh.c: 整段就是在说明旧时代的经验已经不足以稳定预测未来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它直接转述了“变化太快，因此难以预料”的结论。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-187-q01: 全文围绕“Great Acceleration”展开，说明人类活动规模和强度的迅速上升如何改变地球并让人类成为行星尺度的力量，所以主旨是b。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-187-q02: 原文说1020年的人若睡200年醒来，只会看到人口略增，人们寿命和生活方式基本相同，仍主要用木材和动物提供动力，因此c最准确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-187-q03: 提到二战和亚非独立运动，是为了说明真正全球性的“大加速”并不是一开始就发生，而是在更多地区获得燃料、农业和公共卫生进步之后才全面展开，所以选a。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-187-q04: 结尾明确说如果今天睡去200年后醒来“there’s really no telling what they’d find”，原因是世界变化太快，因此d直接对应原意。

public.prompt: What problem with the term "concussion" is the talk mainly discussing?

options.text: Why sports organizations collect injury statistics
Why concussion is a precise diagnosis that doctors fully agree on
How the vague label of concussion can interfere with diagnosis, data, and treatment
How brain scans have already solved most concussion-related problems

evidence.quote: But progress in treatment has been hampered by a small problem: no one can seem to agree on what exactly a concussion is.

private.explanationZh: 主旨题。讲话从一开始就指出治疗进展受阻，是因为人们对“concussion”究竟是什么意见不一，后文进一步展开其如何影响诊断、数据和治疗，所以C最能概括全文。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中确实提到体育组织，但那只是原因之一，不是全文主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 与原文相反，讲话者强调的正是人们无法就其定义达成一致。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它概括了术语过于笼统带来的多个后果。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文说高级设备多在研究实验室，现实中很多情况并没有被彻底解决。

public.prompt: Why can concussion-like symptoms sometimes mislead doctors?

options.text: Because they usually indicate a severe brain bleed
Because similar symptoms can come from non-brain problems such as neck or inner-ear injuries
Because memory and vision tests are too expensive to use routinely
Because patients generally refuse to describe what they feel

evidence.quote: A headache could actually be the result of a neck injury, dizziness a consequence of an inner ear problem, and unexpected fatigue could be due to emotional trauma, or even a symptom of pre-existing depression rather than recent injury.

private.explanationZh: 细节题。讲话者列举头痛、眩晕、疲劳等症状都可能来自颈部损伤、内耳问题或情绪创伤，而不一定来自脑损伤，所以这些症状会让医生把不同问题都笼统归为concussion。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文说有小概率更严重损伤会被漏掉，但并不是说这些症状通常都表示严重出血。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文逐一说明这些症状可能来自非脑部原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有说这些测试因价格太高而不能常规使用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有把问题归因于患者不配合，而是症状本身缺乏特异性。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare concussion to a diagnosis of "chest pain"?

options.text: To show that a broad symptom label can hide different underlying problems
To argue heart and lung injuries are easier to diagnose than brain injuries
To suggest chest pain should be treated with rest and avoidance of bright lights
To explain why hospitals require brain scans for all patients

evidence.quote: It's kind of like if a doctor were to diagnose you with "chest pain" and prescribe a generic treatment without determining if the pain is coming from your heart, your lungs, or your ribs.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道修辞目的题。胸痛类比的作用是说明：如果只给出一个症状性标签，而不追查真正原因，就可能把来源完全不同的问题混在一起，结果治疗也会过于笼统。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确抓住了类比所强调的“同一症状可能有不同病因”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 讲话者并没有比较哪类器官更容易诊断。
optionRationalesZh.c: 类比不是在推荐胸痛治疗方案，而是在批评笼统诊断。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文反而说常规检查常常不包含脑部扫描，不是解释医院为何一律做扫描。

public.prompt: If doctors made more specific diagnoses instead of relying on the broad term concussion, what would most likely improve?

options.text: Athletes would return to play more quickly than before
Researchers and patients would benefit from clearer information about the injury
Doctors could stop asking patients about their symptoms
All severe brain injuries would be caught immediately

evidence.quote: researchers would have better data to work with and patients could potentially get better treatment - all of which would help us deal with one of our literal biggest headaches.

private.explanationZh: 推断题。结尾明确说，更具体的诊断会让研究者得到更好的数据，也让患者更可能得到更合适的治疗，因此最可能改善的是信息质量和治疗针对性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文说简单诊断对某些体育组织有利，因为那样运动员能更早复出；更具体诊断未必会更快。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它综合了“更好数据”和“更好治疗”两个直接后果。
optionRationalesZh.c: 更具体诊断仍然需要症状信息，原文没有说可以不再问症状。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是绝对化表述。更具体诊断会降低问题，但原文没有承诺所有严重损伤都会立刻被发现。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-188-q01: 演讲主要批评“concussion”这个标签过于笼统，既妨碍准确诊断，也混淆数据，还可能影响治疗，因此c概括最完整。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-188-q02: 文中明确举例说明，头痛可能来自颈部损伤，眩晕可能来自内耳问题，疲劳也可能与情绪创伤或既有抑郁有关，所以b正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-188-q03: 把“concussion”比作把病人诊断为“胸痛”，是为了说明这种按症状下的宽泛标签会掩盖不同的真正病因，因此a最符合修辞目的。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-188-q04: 结尾说如果诊断更具体、甚至弃用“concussion”，研究者会得到更好的数据，病人也可能得到更好的治疗，所以b是最合理推论。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly explaining about new apple varieties?

options.text: Why old apple trees usually produce sweeter fruit than young trees
Why developing a successful new apple variety is slow, uncertain, and difficult
How the McIntosh became the only profitable apple in North America
Why grain breeders are now replacing fruit-tree breeders

evidence.quote: So, I suppose it would be good to have new varieties of apples to choose from - even if they're not as amazing as the McIntosh - but it turns out that's way harder than it sounds.

private.explanationZh: 主旨题。讲话者先提出“拥有新苹果品种会很好”，紧接着指出这件事远比听起来困难，后文便具体解释其慢、难、结果不确定，因此B最概括全文。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有讨论老树为何更甜，这不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它概括了新苹果品种开发过程中的时间成本、遗传不确定性和市场风险。
optionRationalesZh.c: McIntosh只是引入例子，不是全文结论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 讲话并未说谷物育种者取代果树育种者，而是借谷物做对比。

public.prompt: According to the talk, how were McIntosh apples reproduced after the original tree began to die?

options.text: By grafting its branches onto the roots of other apple trees
By storing its seeds and planting them in identical soils
By crossing it with Honeycrisp trees to keep its flavor
By growing cut branches in water until they made roots

evidence.quote: its branches were cut and fused onto the roots from other apple trees, in a process called grafting that was repeated time and time again to generate lots of clones

private.explanationZh: 细节题。原文明确说，在原始McIntosh树死亡前，人们把它的枝条切下并接到其他苹果树的根上，通过反复嫁接制造出许多克隆。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确复述了通过嫁接产生克隆的过程。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文强调的是克隆和嫁接，不是靠种子繁殖；种子后代也不会保持完全一致。
optionRationalesZh.c: Honeycrisp是后文另一个品种，与保留McIntosh风味的复制方式无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有描述把枝条泡水生根这种方法。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about crossbreeding apple trees from the talk?

options.text: It usually produces offspring identical to the parent tree
It lets breeders predict fruit quality within a single year
It removes the need for grafting and field testing
It often produces disappointing offspring even when the parents seem promising

evidence.quote: Because of their complicated genetics, even if we crossbreed parents that have good characteristics, nearly all of their offspring will grow poorly or have mediocre fruit.

private.explanationZh: 推断题。原文说即使亲本特征很好，由于果树遗传复杂，绝大多数后代仍会长势差或果实一般，因此可以推出交配组合往往不会轻易得到理想新品种。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这与原文相反。讲话者强调后代并不会和亲本保持一致。
optionRationalesZh.b: 一年见效是谷物的特点，不是苹果等果树的情况。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文恰恰说明即使有有潜力的个体，也还需要嫁接和长期测试。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它把“复杂遗传导致多数后代表现平庸”的含义概括出来了。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention the Honeycrisp apple near the end of the talk?

options.text: To prove new apple varieties always become best-sellers
To show that consumers care more about shelf-life than flavor
To give an example of a rare new variety that succeeded after decades of work
To argue universities should stop breeding older varieties

evidence.quote: The Honeycrisp apple, for example, debuted in 1991 after decades of breeding, growing, and testing, and - thanks to its, well, honey-like flavor and crisp texture, as well as its long shelf-life - it has been a best-seller ever since.

private.explanationZh: 修辞目的题。Honeycrisp被放在结尾，是为了证明尽管成功概率低、过程漫长，但育种者偶尔确实能培育出历经多年开发后又深受市场欢迎的新苹果品种。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文说的是“偶尔成功”，不是所有新品种都会畅销。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中提到风味、口感和耐储存期共同作用，不是只强调保质期。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确说明这个例子用来展示罕见但真实存在的成功案例。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文没有主张停止培育旧品种。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-189-q01: 整段都在解释培育新苹果品种为何“way harder than it sounds”，原因包括遗传不可预测、生长缓慢、测试周期长和市场不确定，因此b正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-189-q02: 原文直接说原始McIntosh树死前，其枝条被切下并嫁接到其他苹果树的根上，由此不断复制，所以选a。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-189-q03: 演讲说即使亲本特征很好，由于果树遗传复杂，几乎所有后代也会长得差或果实平庸，因此可推知交配繁育常产生令人失望的后代，选d。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-189-q04: 结尾提到Honeycrisp，是在说明尽管成功概率低，但偶尔经过几十年的培育、种植和测试，仍会出现一个成功的新苹果品种，因此c最贴切。

public.prompt: What does the talk mainly argue about the standard count of human bones?

options.text: Textbooks underestimate how important bones are to the heart
Most adults secretly have fewer than 100 bones
The standard count of 206 bones is oversimplified because bone counting depends on definition and variation
Babies and adults have identical skeletons despite appearances

evidence.quote: But those textbooks are way too sure of themselves.

private.explanationZh: 主旨题。讲话者从一开始就批评教材对“206块骨头”说得过于确定，随后用软骨到骨骼的连续谱、骨融合方式和个体差异说明这个标准数过于简化。

optionRationalesZh.a: 骨头给心脏供钙只是结尾附带信息，不是全文主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中并没有说大多数成人不到100块骨头，这明显曲解内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它概括了讲话者对“206”标准过度确定性的批评理由。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文相反，婴儿和成人在计数上会不同。

public.prompt: What example does the speaker use to show that textbooks count fused bones inconsistently?

options.text: The knee and wrist are both counted as single bones
The upper skull is counted as many bones while the similarly fused tailbone is counted as one
The middle ear and the little toe are both ignored in the standard count
The ribs are counted separately in babies but together in adults

evidence.quote: Despite that fusion, though, our textbooks still consider our adult upper skulls to be 22 different bones! Meanwhile, our somewhat useless tailbone fuses in a similar way, but our textbooks count it as only one bone.

private.explanationZh: 细节题。讲话者用头骨和尾骨作对比：两者都经历融合，但教材仍把成人上部头骨算作22块，却把以类似方式融合的尾骨算作1块，说明计数标准并不一致。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有用膝盖和手腕都算单骨来说明不一致。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它完整复述了讲话者举出的核心对比例子。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中说有些人会多出小脚趾骨或腕骨，并不是说它们都被忽略来说明融合问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 肋骨不是这里用来说明不一致计数的例子。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention the man who had 42 extra bones?

options.text: To illustrate that unusual bone counts can exist without a person realizing it
To suggest x-rays often create false images of extra bones
To show that extra bones always cause serious symptoms
To argue most textbooks are based on one patient's skeleton

evidence.quote: One guy was found to have made an extra 42 bones in his body - and he didn't even know about it until he got x-rayed!

private.explanationZh: 修辞目的题。这个夸张案例的作用是把前面的“骨骼计数存在个体差异”具体化：一个人甚至能多出很多骨头却毫不知情，从而强化标准206并不能准确代表所有人。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了这个例子用来强调“差异可能存在且不易察觉”的作用。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文不是在质疑X光的真实性，而是在说X光揭示了本人不知道的差异。
optionRationalesZh.c: 讲话者没有说额外骨头必然引发严重症状。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这个例子并不是关于教材样本来源，而是关于人体差异。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about a person whose body does not match the 206-bone standard?

options.text: The person must have a major disease
The difference would always be visible from the outside
The person's skeleton probably began as bone rather than cartilage
The person might never notice the difference without imaging

evidence.quote: you might not even know without getting a full body x-ray.

private.explanationZh: 推断题。结尾直接指出，如果你的身体比所谓206标准多骨或少骨，你甚至可能在没有做全身X光前都不知道，所以这种差异并不一定能被本人察觉。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文把这种情况当作人类正常变异的一部分，并未说一定是重大疾病。
optionRationalesZh.b: 讲话者恰恰强调许多人自己都不知道，说明未必能从外表看出来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文说所有骨骼最初都起始于软骨，不支持这个说法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确概括了结尾关于“可能不自知”的推断。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-190-q01: 讲话核心是在反驳“人体正好有206块骨头”这种过度确定的说法，理由是骨头的定义、融合方式和个体差异都会影响计数，所以c正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-190-q02: 说教材计数不一致时，举的就是上部头骨虽然融合了仍算22块，而尾骨以类似方式融合却只算1块，因此b是原文例子。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-190-q03: 提到那个多出42块骨头的人却直到拍X光才知道，是为了强调骨骼数量的异常差异可能存在而本人并不察觉，所以a正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-190-q04: 结尾明确说如果你的身体和206这一标准不同，你甚至可能在做全身X光前都不知道，因此最合理推论是d。

public.prompt: How does the talk explain turning waste into usable power?

options.text: Why sewage plants should stop separating water from waste
How poop can be processed into methane and used as a significant energy source
Why methane is less useful than fertilizer for most farms
How internet privacy tools protect treatment plants

evidence.quote: poop contains the raw materials necessary to produce methane gas, which you can burn to produce energy or heat.

private.explanationZh: 主旨题。讲话一开始就点明粪便中含有制造甲烷所需原料，而甲烷可以被燃烧来供能和供热，后文便围绕转化步骤和能源意义展开，因此B最符合全文。

optionRationalesZh.a: 分离污水只是流程中的一步，不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它概括了从粪便制甲烷并进一步发电供热的核心内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 讲话没有说甲烷比肥料更没用，反而强调其能源潜力。
optionRationalesZh.d: 网络隐私工具是结尾赞助内容，与主旨无关。

public.prompt: What happens inside the warm, oxygen-free tank?

options.text: The sewage water evaporates and leaves pure methane behind
Microorganisms remove nutrients to create a fertilizer-only solid
Microorganisms break down complex molecules until methane-rich gas is produced
The poop is dried so it can be burned directly like coal

evidence.quote: Then, we put the poop in a warm, oxygen-free tank where some poop-eating microorganisms break down the complex carbon-based molecules poop is made of into a series of simpler and simpler and simpler molecules until finally, other microorganisms happily gobble up these new molecules and breathe them out as a gas which contains some carbon-dioxide, but is mostly, and more importantly, made up of methane, the simplest carbon-based molecule.

private.explanationZh: 细节题。原文详细说明，在无氧温暖环境中，微生物一步步把复杂含碳分子分解成更简单分子，最终另一类微生物把它们转化并释放出以甲烷为主的气体。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有说靠蒸发得到纯甲烷，而且生成气体也不是纯甲烷。
optionRationalesZh.b: 剩余固体确实存在，但这不是氧缺罐里发生的核心过程。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确概括了微生物分解并产出甲烷气体的过程。
optionRationalesZh.d: 讲话明确说不能直接点燃马桶或粪堆，这个说法与原文相反。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker contrast plants that burn methane off with plants that use it for power or heat?

options.text: To show the difference between simply disposing of methane and using it productively
To prove carbon dioxide is harmless to the climate
To explain why fertilizer sales are always more profitable than electricity sales
To argue most treatment plants already power entire cities

evidence.quote: And since methane is a powerful greenhouse gas, most plants just burn it off to convert it into carbon dioxide, which is only 5% as potent a greenhouse gas, and they sell the leftover solids as fertilizer. But a few plants have started doing "double-doody" by burning the methane in generators to power their operations or in furnaces to heat their buildings, and some are producing so much gas and electricity they've started selling it.

private.explanationZh: 修辞目的题。这里的对比用来突出两种处理思路：一种只是为了减少温室效应而把甲烷烧掉，另一种则把甲烷进一步当作能源使用，说明粪便处理可以从单纯处置升级为资源利用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了“处置甲烷”与“利用甲烷发能”之间的对比作用。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文只说二氧化碳的温室效应强度较低，不等于完全无害。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有比较哪一种销售方式一定更赚钱。
optionRationalesZh.d: 讲话只说少数工厂开始这样做，并没有说大多数已经给整座城市供电。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the speaker's discussion of livestock waste?

options.text: Human waste is the only practical source of methane-based power
Farms can produce methane only if they stop selling fertilizer
Livestock-heavy places could generate especially large amounts of electricity from waste
States like Iowa already get most of their power from poop

evidence.quote: our excellent excrement could even become the largest source of power in states with tons of livestock, like Iowa - that is, our #2 would literally be #1.

private.explanationZh: 推断题。讲话者说，在牲畜很多的州，粪便甚至可能成为最大的电力来源，这意味着牲畜粪便丰富的地区尤其具备大规模废物发电潜力。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文明确比较了人类粪便和牲畜粪便，并强调许多国家牲畜粪便更多。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有说必须停止卖肥料才能产甲烷。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它把“牲畜多的州可能成为最大电源”合理推断为这些地方发电潜力特别大。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文说“could even become”，表示潜在可能，不是已经实现的事实。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-191-q01: 这段主要说明粪便如何经过处理变成甲烷，再被用于供能或供热，并指出其潜在规模，因此b最能概括主旨。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-191-q02: 原文说在温暖无氧的罐中，微生物把复杂含碳分子逐步分解，最终另一类微生物释放出以甲烷为主的气体，所以c正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-191-q03: 把“直接烧掉甲烷”与“用甲烷发电或供热”对比，是为了说明前者只是处理废气，后者则把它转化为有用能源，因此a符合目的。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-191-q04: 演讲指出多数国家的牲畜粪便远多于人类粪便，并说这种“poop power”足以为1300多万户供电，像爱荷华这样的牲畜大州甚至可能把它变成最大电力来源，所以c可由文意推出。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the talk?

options.text: Common names and first impressions often fail to match scientifically meaningful categories.
Yellow-and-black warning colors evolved only in bees.
Pine relatives are less diverse than most other conifers.
Astronomers have already agreed on a final definition of a moon.

evidence.quote: But the bottom line here is that names are both arbitrary and deceptive.

private.explanationZh: 结尾直接点明“names are both arbitrary and deceptive”。前面关于蜜蜂、松树、恐龙和月亮的四个片段，都是在说明人们凭外形、习惯叫法或直觉下定义时，往往会把事物分错类，所以主旨是常用名称和直观印象常常误导我们。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了四个案例的共同论点：名字和外表并不可靠，科学分类需要更严格的标准。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。蜜蜂只是第一个例子，整篇并不是专门讨论黄黑条纹如何进化。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。松树部分讨论的是如何区分松树与近亲，不是在比较整个类群的多样性高低。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。月亮部分恰恰说明定义月亮很困难，并没有形成最终一致的规则。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker explain that different stinging insects often converge on one appearance?

options.text: Because similar stripes help them attract the same flowers.
Because mimic species force all stinging insects to copy flies.
Because sharing a warning pattern helps predators learn to avoid both more quickly.
Because identical colors make it easier for insects to sting larger animals.

evidence.quote: Yet, if the two species look similar, far fewer of each kind will get chomped before the predator learns to avoid that pattern. So stinging species, like bees and wasps, often end up converging on a single appearance.

private.explanationZh: 这段话说明，当两种会蜇人的昆虫长得相似时，捕食者更快就能学会避开这种警戒图案，因此每一种被“试吃”的个体都会更少。也就是说，外形趋同带来的核心好处，是共同分担捕食者学习成本。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中讨论的是躲避捕食者，不是吸引花朵或提高采蜜效率。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。拟态者是后来“钻空子”的一方，不是它们逼迫会蜇昆虫去模仿苍蝇。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据明确说相似图案能让捕食者更快学会回避，从而保护两种会蜇人的昆虫。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。条纹的作用在于警戒捕食者，而不是提升蜇刺能力。

public.prompt: According to the dinosaur section, what separates modern classification from older ways of grouping animals?

options.text: It groups animals mainly by the habitats they occupy.
It relies on recent common ancestry rather than surface resemblance.
It separates animals by whether they are alive or extinct.
It focuses on whichever features are easiest for people to notice.

evidence.quote: we created animal families according to looks alone.
we can construct animal families by finding species' most recent common ancestors.

private.explanationZh: 恐龙那一段先批评过去“只按外表建家谱”，随后提出更合理的方法是找“最近共同祖先”。因此现代分类与旧方法的关键差别，不是看起来像不像，而是演化关系是否接近。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说现代分类主要按栖息地来分。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两段证据前后对照，明确把“外表相似”与“共同祖先”区分开来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。是否灭绝并不是这里强调的分类原则。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。作者正是在反对只凭容易观察到的表面特征分组。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker suggest that there may be no single perfect definition of a moon?

options.text: Because astronomers refuse to compare the sizes of orbiting bodies.
Because every moon has an irregular orbit around Earth.
Because only artificial satellites can be classified consistently.
Because orbiting objects form a continuum that does not fit one intuitive category cleanly.

evidence.quote: maybe there's simply no one definition for a moon that captures both the complexity of the different ways that stuff can orbit other stuff, and our nebulous intuitive "I know it when I see it" idea of what makes a moon.

private.explanationZh: 这里的核心意思是：天体的大小、圆度、轨道方式等情况很多，现实中的连续变化很难被一个简单标签完整涵盖，而人的直觉又模糊，所以“moon”未必能有一个兼顾所有情况的完美定义。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中不是说科学家拒绝比较尺寸，而是说单靠尺寸也未必足够。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话者并没有把问题归结为“都绕地球”或“轨道不规则”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讨论对象是天然卫星，不是说只有人造卫星才容易分类。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据明确强调轨道情形的复杂性与人类直觉之间存在张力，因此难有单一定义。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-202-q01: 开头先说现实中事物未必带着清楚标签，后面依次用蜜蜂、松树、恐龙和月亮说明常见名称或直觉分类常常不等于科学上更准确的分类，因此a最能概括全文。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-202-q02: 原文明确说如果两种有刺昆虫长得相似，捕食者更快学会回避那种警戒外观，于是每一种被吃掉的个体都会更少，所以答案是共享警戒图案能更快让捕食者学会躲避。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-202-q03: 恐龙部分先批评按外表分组会得到并不真实的“家族”，接着说现代分类依据化石和DNA去找最近共同祖先，因此现代分类与旧方法的区别就是看亲缘关系而不是表面相似。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-202-q04: 月亮部分反复说明大小下限、彼此质量比、圆度、可见性、轨道等标准都各有问题，说明绕转天体处在连续谱上，难以被单一定义和直觉类别完全整齐地涵盖，所以d最符合。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the talk?

options.text: Why bald eagles remain the best model for all conservation programs.
How modern habits are driving the loss of beneficial gut microbes and what might reverse it.
How antibiotics were originally developed for endangered birds.
Why hunter-gatherer diets prevented all infectious disease.

evidence.quote: Today, another extinction event is happening under eerily similar conditions, but it's happening on the microscopic level ... inside our guts.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接提出“另一场灭绝事件”正在肠道内发生，后文再解释原因包括加工饮食和抗生素滥用，并提出恢复措施。因此主旨不是讲鹰本身，而是讲现代生活方式如何让有益肠道微生物减少，以及如何补救。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。白头海雕只是类比和引入，不是全文中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它同时概括了问题成因与解决方向，最符合整篇结构。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有讨论抗生素的发明史。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话者只说旧饮食更适合某些微生物，并没有说能预防所有传染病。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker describe how bald eagles were brought back from the brink?

options.text: To provide a parallel suggesting that similar restoration strategies could help recover gut microbes.
To argue that bird conservation is more urgent than human health.
To show that pesticides are the only cause of microbial loss.
To prove that habitat destruction no longer matters in ecology.

evidence.quote: The good news is that we were able to bring bald eagles back from the brink.
So how can we apply all these strategies to saving the micro-eagles of our guts?

private.explanationZh: 作者先讲白头海雕如何被救回，再立刻追问这些策略怎样用于“我们肠道里的微型海雕”。这说明海雕案例的作用是搭建类比框架，把生态保护中的“恢复栖息地、补回物种、减少伤害”迁移到肠道微生物问题上。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。前后两句连在一起，明确把海雕复苏当作解决肠道微生物问题的参照。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。作者没有比较两者哪一个更重要。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。微生物减少的原因还包括饮食变化，不仅是类似农药的问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。海雕案例恰恰说明栖息地保护很重要，而不是不重要。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what do bacteria like Prevotella do?

options.text: They form a protective coating that blocks antibiotics.
They spread salmonella when gull populations rise.
They replace fiber and complex carbohydrates in processed food.
They help break down food into nutrients that can regulate the immune system.

evidence.quote: gut bacteria, like Prevotella, which break our food down into nutrients that can regulate our immune system, are disappearing from our guts

private.explanationZh: 证据明确说明 Prevotella 这类细菌能把食物分解成可调节免疫系统的营养物质，所以它们的功能是参与消化并间接影响免疫，而不是形成涂层或替代食物成分。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。形成保护层的是铅管视频里的矿物结垢逻辑，这里没有这个意思。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。沙门氏菌是海鸥爆发带来的类比，不是 Prevotella 的功能。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。加工食品缺少纤维和复杂碳水，细菌并不能“替代”这些食物成分。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据直接给出其作用：分解食物并产生可调节免疫系统的营养。

public.prompt: What does the speaker imply is one important step toward restoring missing gut microbes?

options.text: Avoiding all microbes that come from nonindustrial societies.
Eliminating every bacterium that can survive antibiotics.
Making the gut environment more suitable by protecting it and restoring supportive conditions.
Replacing complex carbohydrates with more processed foods.

evidence.quote: If we take the right measures to protect their habitat, we can ensure our gut bacteria - like bald eagles before them - don't leave an empty nest.

private.explanationZh: 结尾把解决思路总结为“保护它们的栖息地”。这说明作者强调的不只是补充某种细菌，而是先恢复有利于它们存活的肠道环境；这与前文增加纤维、减少不必要抗生素的建议是一致的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者提到哈扎人是为了说明某些菌常见于不同饮食环境，不是叫人回避它们。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话者主张减少不必要的抗生素使用，不是消灭一切能存活的细菌。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据把“保护栖息地”作为总括，最符合恢复缺失微生物的思路。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。加工食品正是问题来源之一，不是解决方案。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-203-q01: 全文核心是在把秃鹰濒危与肠道微生物流失作类比，说明现代加工饮食和滥用抗生素如何伤害有益菌，以及可以怎样恢复，因此b准确概括主旨。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-203-q02: 说秃鹰如何被救回来，不是为了继续讲鸟类保护本身，而是为了引出对肠道微生物可采取的对应措施，如恢复适宜环境、补回缺失种群、减少有害压力，所以其修辞目的就是提供平行类比。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-203-q03: 原文直接说像Prevotella这样的细菌“break our food down into nutrients that can regulate our immune system”，因此d与原文一致，其余选项都把作用说错了。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-203-q04: 结尾建议恢复纤维、补充有益菌、谨慎使用抗生素，本质上都是让肠道栖息环境重新适合这些缺失微生物生存，因此c最好地概括讲话者暗示的重要步骤。

public.prompt: Why does the talk identify carbonaceous chondrites as the most likely source of Earth's water?

options.text: They are the only objects that ever entered the inner solar system.
Their water has a heavy-hydrogen level similar to Earth's water.
They can manufacture new water after striking Earth.
Their ice survives best close to the young Sun.

evidence.quote: their water has heavy level of hydrogen similar to that of Earth water. Strongly suggesting that these Earth crashers are the source of our icecaps, clouds, rivers and oceans.

private.explanationZh: 讲话者用“重氢含量相似”这一化学特征把碳质球粒陨石与地球水联系起来。前面刚说典型彗星的重氢比例不匹配，因此这里最关键的证据就是二者水的化学指纹相近。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说只有它们进入过内太阳系。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。重氢水平与地球水相似，是作者支持这一来源假说的直接理由。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者明确说地球上的自然过程不可能制造出这么多水。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。靠近年轻太阳太热，冰根本难以保留，这与文意相反。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker explain that water vapor is lighter than the main gases in air?

options.text: To prove that clouds are made mostly of nitrogen and oxygen.
To show that condensation happens only in cold climates.
To clarify why moist rising air can stay buoyant and help build clouds.
To explain why hot-air balloons can never pass through clouds.

evidence.quote: As weird as that sounds, it's because water vapor is a gas. Just like the nitrogen and oxygen that make up most of the atmosphere. Basic physics dictates that a given volume of gas has the same number of molecules regardless of what those molecules are. And water is made of H plus H plus O. Which is lighter than both 2 Ns and 2 Os. So warm humid air is even more buoyant than warm dry air.

private.explanationZh: 这一大段解释的作用，是回答“云为什么能形成并向上长”。作者需要先说明潮湿空气为何更轻、更有浮力，后面上升、冷却、凝结和增强上升气流的过程才说得通，所以这里是在补足形成云的机制。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者不是在说明云的成分比例，而是在解释潮湿空气的浮力。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。凝结是否发生并不是被归结为“只在寒冷气候”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。说明水蒸气较轻，是为了论证暖湿空气更容易上升并促成云的形成。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。热气球只是类比，重点不是讨论它能否穿云。

public.prompt: How does the speaker explain the development of a meandering river?

options.text: By tracing how a small disturbance changes erosion and deposition until curves grow.
By listing famous rivers from the smallest to the largest.
By comparing river channels directly with glacier movement.
By arguing that most plains rivers were designed by human engineers.

evidence.quote: all it takes to turn a straight stretch of river into a bendy one is a little disturbance and a lot of time.

private.explanationZh: 这部分不是简单给定义，而是按照过程展开：先有小扰动，随后一侧侵蚀增强、另一侧沉积增加，最后弯道越来越明显。证据里的“a little disturbance and a lot of time”正好概括了这种因果递进式说明结构。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。作者确实用“微小扰动被时间放大”的过程来说明曲流如何形成。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里没有列举河流名单，也不是按大小排序。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者没有拿冰川运动作核心比较。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。整段强调自然侵蚀与沉积，不是人为设计。

public.prompt: Why does the talk compare Martian landforms with inverted relief on Earth?

options.text: Because Mars and Earth always receive sunlight from the same direction.
Because every ancient river channel on Mars is still filled with liquid water.
Because only deserts on Earth preserve stream channels.
Because Earth examples help scientists infer a plausible process for features on Mars.

evidence.quote: We can't know exactly how this river bed got turned inside out without getting down on the ground and digging around a little. But we can make some pretty good guesses by studying inverted relief on our home planet.

private.explanationZh: 作者承认我们无法直接在火星现场挖掘确认，但可以借助地球上已经理解得更清楚的“反转地形”案例来推断可能过程。因此比较的目的，是用地球类比来支持对火星地貌成因的合理推测。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。光照方向是前面视觉错觉的一部分，不是比较地球与火星的主要科学目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。作者说的是古河道和液态水曾经存在，不是现在仍然有水。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说只有沙漠才能保存这类遗迹。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据明确说通过研究地球上的反转地形，可以对火星作出“pretty good guesses”。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-204-q01: 讲者排除了典型彗星后，指出碳质球粒陨石最可能，因为其中的水具有与地球水相似的重氢含量，这是原文给出的关键证据，所以选b。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-204-q02: 这里解释水汽比氮氧更轻，是为了说明潮湿暖空气为什么更有浮力、能够继续上升并形成云，不是为了讨论云的成分或热气球限制，所以c正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-204-q03: 曲流一段是按过程展开的：先有小扰动，再导致一侧侵蚀加剧、另一侧沉积增强，随后弯曲不断放大，最终形成更明显的曲流，因此组织方式就是追踪一个小变化如何逐步放大。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-204-q04: 结尾明确说虽然不能完全知道火星河道怎样被翻转，但可以通过研究地球上的反转地形来做出相当好的推测，所以比较火星与地球是为了借助地球实例推断火星上可能发生的过程。

public.prompt: What is the main idea of the talk?

options.text: Beryllium-7 serves as an atmospheric clue that helps predict the Indian monsoon.
Radioactive atoms directly cause rainfall over India.
India receives most of its rain from storms moving east from Australia.
Nuclear-test sensors were built mainly to help farmers plant crops.

evidence.quote: By being able to use the knowledge of Hadley cell movement to precisely predict the onset of the rainy season, farmers in the Indian subcontinent - and there are a LOT of farmers there - can know precisely when they should plant their crops to grow as much food as possible.

private.explanationZh: 这句话把整篇的落点说得很清楚：通过理解哈德莱环流和铍-7信号，可以更准确预测印度雨季开始时间，并把这种知识用于农业。主旨因此是“把铍-7当作大气线索来预测季风”，而不是说原子本身制造降雨。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了从机制解释到实际预测用途的核心内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。作者明确说铍-7并不真正拥有“让它下雨”的力量。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中谈的是环流和季风，不是澳大利亚直接送来暴风雨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。CTBTO 的设备最初是为监测核试验而设，不是专门服务农业。

public.prompt: Why do equal Beryllium-7 concentrations in Dubna and Melbourne signal that monsoon rains will soon reach India?

options.text: They prove that both cities are receiving rain from the same cloud system.
They show the Hadley cells are aligned in a way that later pulls the rains across India.
They mean Beryllium-7 has chemically triggered condensation over the subcontinent.
They indicate that India has already entered the wet season.

evidence.quote: the Hadley cells are lined up such that they deposit equal concentrations of Beryllium at the surface in both Dubna and Melbourne. When that happens, we know that almost exactly seven and a half weeks later, they will drag the monsoon rains across India.

private.explanationZh: 这里的关键不是两个城市本身，而是它们的铍-7浓度相等反映了哈德莱环流的空间位置。作者紧接着说“when that happens”这些环流会在约七个半星期后把季风雨带到印度，所以这是一个环流排列状态的指示信号。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。证据完全没有说两个城市共享同一片降雨云系。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。相等浓度是哈德莱环流对齐状态的表现，而这种状态随后会把季风拖到印度。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者强调铍-7只是搭便车的线索，不是化学致雨剂。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。作者说的是未来七个半星期后将发生的事，不是印度已经进入雨季。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention full moons and hat making early in the talk?

options.text: To suggest that older explanations of weather were more accurate than modern science.
To argue that Beryllium-7 was first studied by historians rather than scientists.
To prove that all weather predictions depend on human superstition.
To show that strange correlations can make sense once an unseen mechanism is discovered.

evidence.quote: no one knew what connected a full moon to a super high tide, or what connected hat making to insanity - these bewildering connections only made sense once we figured out the invisible mechanisms responsible for them

private.explanationZh: 作者先举“满月与大潮”“制帽与疯狂”这两个看似离奇的相关性，是为了让听众接受一个论证框架：表面上荒诞的联系，背后可能有尚未看见的机制。这样就为后面解释铍-7与季风之间的关系做了铺垫。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者不是在贬低现代科学，而是在强调科学通过发现机制来解释相关性。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这两个例子与铍-7的发现史无关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者不是诉诸迷信，恰恰是在反对只停留在神秘相关性的看法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据直接说这些令人困惑的联系在找到“invisible mechanisms”后才说得通。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why does CTBTO collect Beryllium-7 data?

options.text: Its satellites measure crop yields across the Indian subcontinent.
Its researchers run annual experiments on the Asian monsoon.
Its sensors monitor radioactive atoms while checking for illicit nuclear tests.
Its stations were built to identify heavy hydrogen in comet water.

evidence.quote: they've set up a series of sensors in locations around the world that detect all sorts of radioactive atoms - including naturally occurring ones like Beryllium-7.

private.explanationZh: CTBTO 建立全球传感器网络的原始任务是监测非法核试验，而这些设备顺便也能探测到包括铍-7在内的放射性原子。因此获取铍-7数据是其监测体系的副产品和科研资源，不是它唯一的设立目的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说它通过卫星测量作物产量。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。作者没有把 CTBTO 描述成季风实验机构。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据清楚说明它的传感器用于探测各种放射性原子，这来自核试验监测任务。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。重氢和彗星来自另一段关于地球水源的话题，不属于本视频。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-205-q01: 全文重点是铍-7本身不制造降雨，但它能作为大气环流的线索，帮助预测印度季风到来，因此a最准确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-205-q02: 原文说当Dubna和Melbourne地表铍浓度相等时，说明Hadley cells处在某种排布状态，而几周后这些环流会把季风雨带拖到印度，所以b正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-205-q03: 满月与潮汐、制帽与疯狂这两个例子都是为了说明表面上荒诞的相关性，一旦找到背后的不可见机制就能说得通，从而为后面的铍-7与季风关系铺垫。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-205-q04: 原文明确说CTBTO设置传感器是为了监测非法核试验，而这些传感器也会探测包括铍-7在内的放射性原子，因此c直接对应文本。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly about?

options.text: How ancient Romans completely solved the problem of water contamination.
Why lead pipes remained in water systems and why relying on them still creates danger.
Why hard water is always unsafe to drink from any metal pipe.
How the EPA successfully removed every lead pipe in the United States.

evidence.quote: Yet today, hundreds of millions of us worldwide get our water from plumbing that contains lead. Seriously, humans? How did we get here?

private.explanationZh: 开头就提出核心问题：明知铅有毒，为什么全世界仍有这么多人通过含铅管道用水。后文分别解释历史原因、技术缓解办法和现实风险，所以主旨是“铅管为何延续至今以及这种依赖为何仍危险”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说古人已经彻底解决污染问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它同时概括了历史延续性与当下风险，最符合全篇。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话重点不是泛泛讨论硬水，而是铅管系统。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。作者对 EPA 的态度是批评而非表扬，更没有说已经全部拆除。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what helped lead pipes remain common even after their dangers were understood?

options.text: Their long history and practical advantages gave them strong momentum.
Scientists could not tell lead apart from safer metals.
Cities had already switched almost entirely to wooden pipes.
Lead pipes worked only in places with naturally soft water.

evidence.quote: By 1900, we had figured out these quirks and fully understood the dangers lead pipes posed, but two thousand years of plumbing had given lead some serious inertia.

private.explanationZh: 这句话明确说明，即使到 1900 年人们已经理解了危险，铅管仍因“两千年的管道历史”而具有巨大惯性。结合前文关于易提炼、易成形、耐用等优点，可以看出它之所以延续，是历史积累加上实用性共同作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据中的“serious inertia”正说明长期使用传统和实用优势让铅管难以退出。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。问题不在于分不清金属种类。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说城市大规模改用木管。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。软水会更容易让铅析出，这不是铅管得以保留的原因。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker discuss dissolved minerals forming a crust inside pipes?

options.text: To show that mineral-rich water completely removes lead from plumbing systems.
To argue that all cities should depend on droughts to clean their pipes.
To prove that hard water is more poisonous than lead itself.
To explain why lead pipes sometimes seemed less dangerous and how people tried to reduce the risk.

evidence.quote: we started adding dissolved minerals to water that didn't naturally contain them, artificially forming that protective crust that makes lead pipes safe...well, safe-ER.

private.explanationZh: 作者提到矿物结壳，不是为了说铅问题被彻底解决，而是解释两个现象：一是为什么有些地方铅管似乎没那么危险，二是后来人们如何利用这种机制去“降低”而非消除风险。末尾的“safe-ER”也特意强调这种办法并不完美。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者说的是把风险降一些，不是把铅完全移除。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。干旱导致屏障崩塌是灾难案例，不是建议。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有比较硬水与铅谁更毒。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。保护性结壳既解释了早先的困惑，也说明了后来采取的临时缓解策略。

public.prompt: What does the speaker imply about the EPA's current approach to lead in drinking water?

options.text: It has already solved the lead problem everywhere except Flint.
It is stricter than necessary because no amount of lead can reach consumers.
Monitoring alone is inadequate because the testing and standard both fail to fully protect health.
It works well as long as utilities stop using dissolved minerals.

evidence.quote: their flawed testing method systematically misses high lead levels in water. What's more, the lead limit the EPA has adopted isn't actually based on what's best for human health in the first place.

private.explanationZh: 作者批评 EPA 有两层：测试方法会系统性漏掉高铅值，标准本身也不是按最佳健康保护制定的。这意味着仅靠现有监测和限值并不足以真正保障安全，隐含立场是需要更彻底、更可靠的做法。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者说仍有数千万美国人面临风险，显然远不止弗林特。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话者认为标准不够保护健康，而不是过于严格。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。两条证据分别批评检测和标准，合起来说明现行监测式做法并不充分。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。作者没有说停止加矿物就能让制度有效，相反加矿物本身只是有限缓解。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-206-q01: 全文围绕两个层面展开：为什么铅管明知有害却长期留在供水系统中，以及为什么继续依赖它仍然危险，所以b最符合主旨。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-206-q02: 文中说铅丰富、易提取、柔软易制管、寿命长，再加上两千年使用历史形成惯性，后来工人偏好和行业推动也延续了它，因此a准确概括其持续普及的原因。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-206-q03: 讲者提到矿物形成硬壳，一方面解释为什么某些地方铅管似乎没有那么快让人生病，另一方面说明后来人们怎样通过人为加矿物来降低风险，所以d正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-206-q04: 结尾明确批评EPA没有推动更换铅管，而是监测限值；且其检测方法会系统性漏掉高铅值，标准也不以最佳健康保护为基础，因此暗示单靠现行监测办法远远不够。

public.prompt: How is the talk mainly organized?

options.text: By presenting four separate explanations about phenomena beyond Earth
By tracing one continuous history of lunar exploration
By comparing rival plans for deflecting meteorites
By arguing that one asteroid caused every major impact on Earth

evidence.quote: we've got four short videos about what lies beyond our Earth

private.explanationZh: 开头直接说明这是一组“关于地球之外事物的四个短视频”，所以整篇并不是围绕单一事件展开，而是用四个相对独立的小主题来组织内容。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文明确说有四个短视频，说明结构是分主题串联。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中并没有连续讲月球探索史，月球只是其中一个片段。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。防御陨石只在前面稍有提及，不是全文的组织主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有声称一个小行星造成了地球上所有重大撞击。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker suggest Hebe sends so many meteorites toward Earth?

options.text: It is the largest asteroid in the belt
Its minerals are especially easy to identify from Earth
Its location lets Jupiter repeatedly disturb debris from it
It is the only asteroid that has experienced violent collisions

evidence.quote: Hebe owes its unique status to its location at the edge of an empty band in the asteroid belt.
this so-called "orbital resonance" distorts the shape of the asteroid's orbit, and eventually it destabilizes into a potentially Earth-crossing path.

private.explanationZh: 说话者先指出 Hebe 的关键不在于单纯“够大”或“撞得多”，而在于它位于空带边缘；随后解释空带中的轨道共振会让岩石反复在同样位置受到木星影响，最终进入可能穿越地球轨道的路径。综合两句，核心原因就是位置触发了木星反复扰动。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文明确说 Hebe 虽然相对较大，但只占总质量的一小部分，大小不是决定性原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。望远镜识别矿物成分只是帮助科学家锁定 Hebe，不是它把更多碎片送向地球的原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。轨道位置加上与木星的共振，正是它更容易把碎片送入地球穿越轨道的关键机制。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。别的小行星也发生过许多碰撞，所以“经历过碰撞”本身不能解释 Hebe 的特殊性。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what brought the Moon into its one-side-facing-Earth pattern?

options.text: The Moon stopped rotating entirely
Earth's gravitational pull gradually adjusted the Moon's spin
Sunlight heated one side of the Moon more than the other
Asteroid impacts repeatedly pushed the same side toward Earth

evidence.quote: it took just 1000 years for the Earth's pull to adjust the moon's spin enough that one rotation of the moon corresponded to one trip around the earth, leaving one side forever locked facing Earth.

private.explanationZh: 这句话直接说明，是地球的引力在大约一千年里把月球自转调整到与公转同步，于是同一面长期朝向地球。题目问的是形成这种状态的原因，答案就是地球引力对月球自转的调节。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文不是说月球完全不转，而是说它每绕地球一圈，自转也正好一圈。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句直接说 Earth's pull 调整了月球自转。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说日照差异造成潮汐锁定。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。前文提到小行星撞击与月球起源有关，但这里解释锁定状态时依靠的是引力调节，不是反复撞击。

public.prompt: What does the talk suggest was most important in keeping early Earth warm under the faint young Sun?

options.text: Extra sunlight reflected from the poles
Heat released directly by early single-celled organisms
A permanently stronger magnetic shield around Earth
Heat-trapping gases in Earth's early atmosphere

evidence.quote: the most likely one is that Earth's early atmosphere included one or more ultra-insulating gases that kept its surface unseasonably toasty.

private.explanationZh: 在提出多个解释后，讲者明确说“最可能”的解释是早期大气含有一种或多种强保温气体，使地表异常温暖。因此可以推断，解决悖论的关键是温室保温，而不是别的因素。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。极地冰增加反照率会让地球更冷，不是更暖。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中说生命在温暖环境中发展起来，并没有说生命本身直接把地球加热。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。太阳风假说讨论的是形成某些气体的可能来源，不是说磁场本身就是主要保温机制。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据句直接把“超强保温气体”列为最可能答案。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-207-q01: 开头明确说这是“four short videos about what lies beyond our Earth”，后文也依次讲小行星陨石、月球背面、地球大气、年轻太阳悖论，整体就是四段独立解释。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-207-q02: 文中强调Hebe的关键不只是撞击多，而是位于小行星带空带边缘；进入该带的岩石会与木星形成轨道共振，被反复拉扯，轨道最终变成可能穿过地球的路径。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-207-q03: 关于月球总以同一面朝向地球，原文解释为地球引力不断拉扯月球隆起并调整其自转，约1000年后使月球一转正好对应绕地球一周。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-207-q04: 谈到“faint young sun paradox”时，讲者说最可能的解释是早期地球大气中存在一种或多种“ultra-insulating gases”，也就是能强烈保温的温室气体。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the talk?

options.text: Why some animals are much louder than others
How sound frequency affects the range and function of animal calls
Why water always carries sound better than air
How human hearing evolved from echolocation

evidence.quote: It's because the howl sound and the chirp sound behave very differently from each other - and that has big implications for why some animals chirp and others howl in the first place.

private.explanationZh: 开头就点明核心：狼嚎和蛙鸣之所以传播效果不同，是因为两类声音本身的行为不同，而这又影响动物为什么会发出不同类型的叫声。整段随后围绕“频率如何影响传播距离与用途”展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文一开始就说狼和树蛙的叫声音量其实差不多，所以重点不是谁更响。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。全文解释了频率怎样影响传播距离，并进一步影响动物叫声的功能。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。水中传播更远只是后面举的一个补充例子，不是全文主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中谈到回声定位，但并没有讨论人类听觉的进化。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why do high-frequency calls usually travel a shorter distance?

options.text: They lose energy faster and are more likely to bounce off obstacles
They are always produced at a lower volume
They can move only through water
They gradually change into lower-frequency waves

evidence.quote: The first reason is that, as a wave waves, it releases energy as heat, and the more frequently a wave waves, the more energy it releases as it travels along, and therefore the sooner it runs out of energy.
Low-frequency waves tend to bend around objects - a tree, a building, a Bigfoot - whereas high-frequency waves are much more likely to reflect off them.

private.explanationZh: 文中给了两个原因：高频波在传播时更快把能量以热的形式散掉，而且更容易被障碍物反射走。所以它们既更快“没劲”，也更难绕过障碍继续前进。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两个证据句分别对应“能量损失更快”和“更易被障碍物反射”这两个关键原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。开头明确说狼和树蛙叫声的音量差不多，问题不在音量。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。高频声音当然也能在空气中传播，文中没有说只能在水里。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有说高频波会在传播中自动变成低频波。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention bats, dolphins, and even a kernel of corn?

options.text: To show that bats and dolphins mainly use sound to attract mates from far away
To show that low-frequency calls are best at moving around obstacles
To explain why dolphins prefer medium-pitched sounds underwater
To show that high-frequency calls are useful when animals need echoes from small objects

evidence.quote: Bats and dolphins, for instance, use high-frequency vocalizations precisely because these calls reflect off the tiniest of obstacles like an insect, a fish, or - in a bizarre experiment designed to test the limits of dolphin echolocation - a kernel of corn.

private.explanationZh: 这里的例子不是在补充动物趣闻，而是在说明高频声的特殊用途：它们虽然不擅长远距离传播，却非常适合打到细小物体上并返回回声，因此能帮助动物探测微小目标。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里不是讲远距离求偶，而是讲回声定位。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。绕开障碍物是低频声的优势，而这句话专门说明高频声的另一种用途。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说海豚偏爱“中频”声音。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。玉米粒这个夸张例子正是为了强调高频回声可以来自非常小的物体。

public.prompt: Which animal would most likely rely on a low-frequency call?

options.text: An animal trying to make its call disappear quickly in a crowded habitat
An animal trying to locate tiny prey by listening for echoes
An animal trying to reach group members that are very far away
An animal calling to a nearby partner on the same branch

evidence.quote: That's why animals that communicate from afar - say, social animals that roam long distances, like wolves and elephants, or solitary animals in search of far-flung mates, like koalas and humpback whales - use really low-frequency calls that don't run out of energy quickly or bounce off of things along the way.

private.explanationZh: 根据原文，低频叫声最适合“从很远处”传递信息，因为它们不容易迅速耗散，也不容易被沿途障碍挡开。因此最可能使用低频叫声的，是需要跨很大距离联系同伴的动物。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。题干说的是低频叫声，快速消失并不是它的优势。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。通过回声寻找微小猎物对应的是高频叫声的用途。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据句直接把远距离社交或寻偶动物列为低频叫声的典型使用者。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。近距离交流没有必要特别依赖低频的远距离传播优势。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-208-q01: 整段都在解释声音频率如何影响动物叫声的传播距离与用途：低频适合远距离交流，高频适合反射探测小物体。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-208-q02: 文中给出两个原因：高频波在传播中更快损失能量，而且更容易被树木等障碍物反射，所以通常传不了太远。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-208-q03: 提到蝙蝠、海豚和玉米粒，是为了说明高频叫声适合打到很小的物体上再反射回来，也就是回声定位的用途。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-208-q04: 文章说需要远距离联系同伴或寻找远处配偶的动物，会使用低频叫声，因为这种声音不容易耗散，也不容易被障碍物挡开。

public.prompt: Why is building a clone army so difficult, according to the talk?

options.text: Clone soldiers are too expensive to feed and train
Cloning works well for animals but not for humans
Even making one clone is unreliable, and repeated cloning becomes even harder
Egg cells cannot receive DNA from other cells

evidence.quote: it's super-hard to make even one clone, and it's almost impossible to make a clone army.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接给出论点：连做出一个克隆体都非常困难，更不用说大量复制成“军队”。后文所有细节都在解释为什么这件事在技术上如此不可靠。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。经济和基础设施问题被提到过，但说话者强调在那之前先会撞上科学难题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有把问题简化成“只对人类不行”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据句准确概括了全文主旨：单个克隆已难，多代或大规模克隆更难。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中说的是可以把别的体细胞 DNA 放进卵细胞，问题在于后续发育常常失败。

public.prompt: Why are ordinary body cells poorly suited for cloning?

options.text: Their DNA carries tags and tangles linked to specialization, which makes division difficult
They contain only half a set of DNA
They lack the proteins needed to enter an egg cell
Their DNA is always too damaged to copy

evidence.quote: This specialization makes DNA from non-sex cells particularly unsuited for cloning purposes because those tags and tangles make it hard for the clone zygote to divide.

private.explanationZh: 体细胞虽然也有完整 DNA，但为了执行各自功能，它们的 DNA 已经带上各种“别用”“缠起来”的标记。正因为这些标记和缠结妨碍了受精卵样的分裂过程，所以不适合作为克隆材料。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据句直接点出体细胞 DNA 不适合克隆的原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。只有精子和卵细胞才是半套 DNA，普通体细胞不是。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说问题在于缺少进入卵细胞的蛋白。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文强调的是标签与缠结，不是“总是受损”。

public.prompt: Why would cloning a clone be even harder than making the first clone?

options.text: A clone gradually loses half of its DNA over time
A clone can survive only if its cells never specialize
The enzymes work better on clone DNA than on ordinary DNA
A clone already starts with extra DNA markings, and later specialization adds even more

evidence.quote: its baseline DNA already has some extra tags and tangles, and as its cells specialize, that tagged and tangled DNA will only get taggier and tanglier.

private.explanationZh: 这句话说明第二代克隆更难，不是因为 DNA 数量变了，而是因为第一代克隆本身就带着额外的标签和缠结，随后在发育与分化中还会进一步累积，所以再拿它去克隆只会更糟。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说克隆体会逐渐失去半套 DNA。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。问题不在于“不能分化”，而在于分化会进一步增加标签和缠结。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文恰恰暗示酶对这种更复杂的 DNA 更无能为力。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据句把“先天更多、后来更乱”这个原因说得非常明确。

public.prompt: If scientists could apply the new super-enzyme much more broadly, what would it most directly make possible?

options.text: It would eliminate the ethical concerns about cloning
It would make repeated generations of cloning much more feasible
It would allow extinct species to be cloned without any DNA
It would guarantee that every clone embryo survives normally

evidence.quote: some scientists - the non-mad kind - recently identified a super-enzyme that can handle multiple generations worth of tagged and tangled DNA
This technique only seems to work in certain rodents, though, so your clone army will just have to wait

private.explanationZh: 前一句说明这种超级酶的价值在于它能处理“多代”累积下来的标签和缠结；后一句又说明目前它只在某些啮齿类中有效，所以还无法真正支撑克隆大军。由此可推断，如果能更广泛适用，它最直接带来的就是多代连续克隆的可行性上升。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。酶最多解决技术难题，不会自动消除伦理争议。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据明确把这类酶与“多代克隆”联系在一起。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。没有 DNA 就无法克隆，文中也没提出这种能力。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文没有说它能保证每个胚胎都成功，只是说明它可能处理更复杂的 DNA。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-209-q01: 主旨就是：连制造一个克隆体都很不稳定，而克隆体的DNA还会带着更多标记和缠结，因此一代代复制会更困难，所以“clone army”几乎不可行。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-209-q02: 普通体细胞虽然也有完整DNA，但为了执行各自功能会在DNA上加“do not use”标签并缠结起来，这种专门化让克隆合子难以顺利分裂。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-209-q03: 讲者明确说克隆体本身的基础DNA已经多了一些tags和tangles，之后细胞再专门化只会让这些标记和缠结更多，因此再克隆更难。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-209-q04: 新发现的super-enzyme被说成能处理多代累积的tags和tangles；如果能广泛应用，最直接的结果就是让“clone of a clone”这类多代重复克隆更可行。

public.prompt: How is this talk primarily structured?

options.text: It follows one experiment from setup to conclusion
It links four science stories about waste and byproducts in biology and society
It argues that disgust is always the safest guide to action
It compares only human digestion with koala digestion

evidence.quote: we hope you'll enjoy these four science stories
I'm starting to realize this is more a compilation about ingesting poop and poop byproducts

private.explanationZh: 前面先说明这是一组“四个科学故事”，后面又自我总结说这更像一个关于“摄入粪便及其副产品”的合集。两处信息结合起来，可以确定整篇是以多个相关案例串联成的编排方式。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。全文不是单一实验的线性叙述，而是多个片段组成的合集。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据一处指出“四个故事”，另一处点明共同主题是粪便及其副产品。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者反而多次说明嫌恶感可能是不理性的，不是全文在论证“总该听 disgust 的”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。内容远不止人类和考拉消化比较，还涉及再生水、动物食粪、肠道移植等。

public.prompt: Why does the talk say a fetus does not produce ordinary feces in the womb?

options.text: Fetuses never swallow amniotic fluid, so nothing enters the gut
All fetal waste is converted directly into urine
Bile destroys every digestive byproduct before it reaches the intestines
Most poop-forming material stays with the mother, and the fetus lacks the bacteria-rich large intestine that makes ordinary feces

evidence.quote: So most of the potential poop products stay with the mother.
a fetus's large intestine is mostly sterile and devoid of the billions of bacteria that break our waste down and make up as much as 50% of the brown pulp known as feces.

private.explanationZh: 作者给出两个关键条件：第一，真正会形成粪便的大部分原料留在母体；第二，胎儿大肠几乎没有那些会把废物进一步分解成普通粪便的细菌。所以胎儿会形成胎便样物质，却不是通常意义上的粪便。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文明确说胎儿会吞咽羊水。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中说的是许多排尿废物由母体处理，不等于所有废物都变成尿。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。胆汁会参与分解部分废物，但这并不是“不会形成普通粪便”的完整原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。两条证据分别对应“原料不在胎儿这边”和“缺少造成立即成粪的细菌环境”。

public.prompt: What does the recycled-water example suggest about public disgust?

options.text: People may reject safe recycled water until it is psychologically distanced from sewage
People usually accept recycled water once they hear one technical explanation
Recycled water is unpopular mainly because it is less clean than reservoir water
Public opposition disappears in any region that has experienced drought

evidence.quote: Studies show that time and distance are some of the best antidotes to disgust:
Singapore quickly gained public support for their recycled water by calling it NEWater.

private.explanationZh: 这部分并不是在说再生水不安全，而是在说明人们会因为“它曾经是污水”而产生心理排斥。无论是让水先在水库里待一段时间，还是重新命名为 NEWater，本质上都是把它在心理上和污水拉开距离。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两条证据都指向同一结论：接受度取决于是否成功减弱“污染联想”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说技术解释本身就足够，反而强调时间、距离和命名这些心理因素。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中明确说管道水和水库水一样干净。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。干旱可能让人更愿意接受，但原文没有说经历过干旱就一定不再反对。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker introduce fecal microbiota transplantation near the end of the talk?

options.text: To show that probiotics usually overpower C. diff without any other treatment
To argue that insurance companies eagerly promote microbial transplants
To illustrate that restoring a diverse gut community can succeed where more antibiotics may fail
To prove that healthy human feces are completely free of bacteria

evidence.quote: Ironically, the standard treatment for C diff is yet more antibiotics - which sometimes works, but in many cases the antibiotic resistance of C diff means the playing field is simply cleared of its competitors and its population can grow even more vigorously.
And in 411 of 473 documented cases, the transplanted bacterial community quickly became dominant, causing the patient's digestive system to recover and stabilize

private.explanationZh: 前一句先说明“继续上抗生素”常常会因为清空竞争者而让 C. diff 更占优势；后一句再给出粪菌移植成功恢复稳定肠道群落的结果。放在这里的作用，就是用一个医学案例说明“补回多样的微生物群落”有时比继续杀菌更有效。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中专门说 probiotics 的剂量远远不够强，不足以造成这种效果。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。作者反而提到保险公司对这类操作也会犹豫。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。前后两句构成鲜明对照，说明重建群落多样性可以解决单靠抗生素难以解决的问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。健康粪便里当然有大量细菌，移植成功靠的正是这些微生物群落，而不是“没有细菌”。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-210-q01: 开头先说“we hope you’ll enjoy these four science stories”，后面也确实串联了四段与排泄物或其副产品相关的科学故事：胎儿、再生水、动物食粪、粪菌移植。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-210-q02: 文中解释普通粪便之所以不形成，是因为大部分可能形成粪便的物质都留在母体，由母体先消化处理；而胎儿大肠又基本无菌，缺少形成普通粪便所需的大量细菌。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-210-q03: 再生水案例说明，很多人并不是因为水不干净才反对，而是因为它和污水的心理联结太近；只要增加时间、距离或重新命名，接受度就会上升。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-210-q04: 末尾引入粪菌移植，是为了举例说明当抗生素可能因清空竞争者而失败时，重新建立一个健康多样的肠道菌群反而可能帮助患者恢复。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what quality of jet fuel makes it especially hard to replace on long-haul flights?

options.text: It contains a lot of energy for how heavy it is
It can be stored safely only in extremely cold tanks
It produces no pollution during flight
It is made by mixing several lighter fuels together

evidence.quote: Jet fuel has a high "specific energy", meaning it contains a lot of energy for how heavy it is, yet planes still need a lot of it, because they need to be able to fly literally around the world.

private.explanationZh: 作者强调喷气燃料“单位重量所含能量很高”，即比重大但能量密度也高，而飞机为了长途飞行仍然要带很多。正因为长途飞行极度依赖这种高比能，替代它才会特别困难。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据句直接定义了 jet fuel 的关键优势就是高 specific energy。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说喷气燃料必须依靠极冷储罐来安全储存。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。开头恰恰说它会造成大量污染。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有把喷气燃料描述成多种轻燃料混合而成。

public.prompt: Why are fuels like natural gas and hydrogen poor replacements for jet fuel in airplanes?

options.text: They freeze too easily at high altitude
They need bulky, heavy containers because they take up too much space as gases
They provide too little energy for their weight
They are too radioactive to use safely

evidence.quote: Other fuels like natural gas, propane, butane, and hydrogen hold enough energy for their weight, but since they're gases, they take up more space and require big, heavy storage containers that would wipe out any benefit from flying with a lighter fuel.

private.explanationZh: 这类燃料的问题不是“单位重量能量不够”，而是它们是气体，占空间太大，还需要又大又重的容器来装。这样一来，原本在重量上的好处就被储存成本抵消掉了。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文讨论的是体积和储存容器，不是高空冻结。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句完整说明了气体燃料在飞机上的核心障碍。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者明确说它们“hold enough energy for their weight”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。放射性太强说的是核燃料，不是天然气或氢。

public.prompt: What is the main argument of the talk?

options.text: Air travel can become cleaner only if long-haul flights end completely
Hydrogen is already the clear replacement for jet fuel
Existing airplanes could switch fuels easily if fuel were cheaper
Most alternatives have major drawbacks, so synthetic jet fuel may be the strongest candidate

evidence.quote: So, there's not really a great alternative to jet fuel.
That's why the best bet may be to create synthetic jet fuel that holds as much energy as conventional jet fuel, is just as safe, causes less pollution, and could work within the current system.

private.explanationZh: 作者先总结“并没有真正理想的替代品”，随后立即提出因此最有希望的方向是合成喷气燃料。也就是说，全文不是单纯列举替代品，而是在比较缺点后得出“合成喷气燃料最值得押注”的中心论点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有主张取消长途飞行，而是在讨论更可行的燃料替代路线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。氢虽然单位重量能量高，但因储存问题并没有被认定为明确赢家。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者一开始就说替代燃料还牵涉飞机重设计，所以绝不是“轻松切换”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。两条证据先否定理想替代品，再提出 synthetic jet fuel 是 best bet，正好概括全文主旨。

public.prompt: Why does the talk suggest synthetic jet fuel is still expensive?

options.text: Passengers are not yet willing to pay more for cleaner flights
Petroleum can no longer be extracted at large scale
Synthetic fuel still needs more efficient production and much larger-scale manufacturing
One successful small factory would be enough to make it cheap

evidence.quote: That's because conventional jet fuel is made from petroleum, which formed - for free - all around the world over millions of years, and which we've spent the last couple hundred years figuring out how to extract and use at massive scales.

To make synthetic jet fuel work, not only do we need to figure out how to more efficiently mimic the natural processes that occurred over millions of years, we also need to scale up the industry so it can produce fuel as cheaply as conventional jet fuel.

private.explanationZh: 作者把贵的原因拆成两层：传统喷气燃料依靠自然长期形成且人类已经建立起大规模产业；而合成喷气燃料既要提高制造效率，又要把产业规模做大，才可能把成本压下来。所以推断出的关键障碍就是“效率 + 规模”都还不够。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中分析的是生产端和产业端原因，不是消费者支付意愿。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。相反，作者说人类已经花了几百年学会大规模开采和使用石油。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。两条证据分别对应传统燃料的规模优势，以及合成燃料当前仍需提升效率并扩大规模。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。作者强调的是整个产业扩张，不是靠一个小工厂就能解决。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-211-q01: 讲者说喷气燃料的关键优势是specific energy高，也就是单位重量所含能量很多；长途飞行尤其依赖这一点，所以很难替代。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-211-q02: 天然气、丙烷、氢等虽然按重量看能量够高，但它们是气体，占空间大，还需要又大又重的储存容器，结果抵消优势。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-211-q03: 整篇先排除多种替代燃料的主要缺点，最后得出结论：与现有系统兼容、又有机会减少污染的合成喷气燃料，可能是最有希望的选择。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-211-q04: 原文说合成喷气燃料目前更贵，是因为人类还需要更高效地模拟天然形成过程，并把产业规模扩大到足以像传统石油燃料那样低成本生产。

public.prompt: How is the talk mainly organized?

options.text: It presents four separate examples related to how humans evolved.
It traces one uninterrupted timeline from fish to modern humans in strict chronological order.
It compares humans with other animals mainly to prove human superiority.
It argues that modern technology now drives evolution more than natural selection does.

evidence.quote: to bring you four fascinating stories about how we humans have evolved.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接说明这是关于“人类如何演化的四个有趣故事”，所以整篇是按四个案例并列展开，而不是按单一时间线连续讲述一个过程。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确，原文明确说要讲四个关于人类演化的故事，说明结构就是分案例展开。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对，全文虽然提到不同时间段，但并不是从鱼一路按年代顺序讲到现代人的单线叙事。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，讲话者并没有试图证明人类比别的动物更“高级”，而是在解释不同演化现象。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，谈话核心是演化与自然选择相关现象，不是强调现代技术主导演化。

public.prompt: What does the speaker imply about the claim that humans are 99 percent chimpanzee?

options.text: It is useless because genomes cannot be compared in any meaningful way.
It is an oversimplification because major mismatches are excluded and raw change counts do not capture overall similarity.
It is accurate for behavior but not for DNA.
It is wrong mainly because humans have fewer genes than chimpanzees.

evidence.quote: So, yes, we share 99% of our DNA with chimps - if we ignore 18 percent of their genome and 25 percent of ours.
So just counting up the number of genetic changes doesn't really tell us that much about how similar or different two creatures are.

private.explanationZh: 讲话者并不是简单否认“99%”这个说法，而是指出这个数字建立在排除大量不匹配片段的比较方法上，而且基因变化数量本身也不能充分代表两个物种在整体上有多相似，因此这个说法过于简化。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，原文说比较基因组仍然能学到很多东西，只是不能把“99%”当成完整结论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，两段证据共同表明：一方面比较时忽略了大块不匹配区域，另一方面变化数量不等于生物学相似度。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，原文讨论的是基因组比较的方法问题，不是把行为和DNA对立起来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，文中提到的是染色体融合和大片段差异，并没有把结论建立在“基因数更少”这一点上。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why do humans tend to spot snakes quickly?

options.text: Snakes are usually larger than other predators.
Humans are taught from childhood to watch for reptiles.
Ancient primates evolved specialized adaptations for detecting snake-like threats.
Snakes automatically trigger fear because of the sounds they make.

evidence.quote: But eventually, some of these primates evolved what amounted to snake detectors - adaptations like neurons devoted specifically to snake-spotting - that helped the protomonkeys quickly recognize a wriggling stick as dangerous and get out of Dodge.

private.explanationZh: 这一段明确说明，灵长类祖先后来进化出了类似“蛇探测器”的适应性，包括专门识别蛇的神经元，因此人类会更快把蛇样目标识别为危险。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，文中没有说蛇因为体型更大才更容易被发现。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对，解释来自演化形成的适应，而不是后天教育训练。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确，原文直接提到专门用于发现蛇的适应性与神经机制。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，文中强调的是视觉识别与威胁检测，不是声音触发。

public.prompt: What explanation does the speaker give for the way mortality changes at very old ages?

options.text: Medical care becomes equally effective for everyone after age 100.
Natural selection weakens with age until no protective effect is left to lose.
Cellular damage stops accumulating after people reach 100.
Large populations make death less likely for centenarians.

evidence.quote: Eventually, the force field wears off entirely, so while people's chances of dying at that age are really high, they don't go up any further because there's no protection left to lose.

private.explanationZh: 讲话者把自然选择比作保护人类免受死亡威胁的“力场”。随着年龄增加，这种保护越来越弱，到极老年时几乎完全消失，所以死亡概率虽然很高，但不会继续无限上升。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，原文没有把曲线变化归因于医疗效果趋同。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，证据明确用“力场消失”来说明自然选择的保护作用耗尽。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，前文反而说细胞损伤会随着时间不断累积。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，人口规模只用于说明纪录被打破的概率，不是死亡率平台化的原因。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-212-q01: 开头明确说要讲“四个关于人类如何进化的故事”，后面也依次展开四个彼此独立的案例，所以整体结构是并列呈现四个例子。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-212-q02: 讲者指出“99%”这个说法建立在排除大量大片段不匹配区域之后的比较之上，而且单纯统计DNA差异数量也不能充分反映整体相似性，所以这是过度简化。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-212-q03: 原文说早期灵长类后来进化出类似“蛇探测器”的适应，比如专门识别蛇的神经元，因此人类会更快发现蛇。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-212-q04: 讲者把自然选择比作随年龄减弱的保护力场：年龄越大，清除有害基因的作用越弱，最后没有更多保护可失去，因此极高龄后的死亡率可能趋于持平。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the talk about coal?

options.text: Coal has been extremely useful, but its harms now make replacing it increasingly necessary.
Coal is the cheapest fuel mainly because mining has become safer than ever.
Coal should remain dominant until forests can replace it as a fuel source.
Coal smoke was a problem only in medieval London.

evidence.quote: But for as long as coal has been awesome, coal has also been the worst.
It may take a while before we can fully replace the energy source that made and still powers the modern world, but it's high time we try to go coal turkey.

private.explanationZh: 整篇先承认煤炭长期以来确实非常好用、推动了现代世界的发展，随后重点强调它带来的健康与气候代价，并以“现在该戒煤了”作结，所以主旨是“煤炭有用，但应当尽快被替代”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确，两段证据分别概括了煤炭的双重性和作者最终主张。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对，讲话者强调的是煤炭的危险性，而不是采矿变得更安全。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，原文并没有建议重新依赖森林燃料，反而提出风能、太阳能等现代替代方案。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，伦敦禁煤只是历史例子，谈话重点是煤炭长期且持续的多重危害。

public.prompt: Why did the King of England's coal ban in London fail?

options.text: Coal miners refused to obey the king.
New coal reserves were discovered inside the city.
Londoners preferred coal's smell to wood smoke.
People still needed fuel because nearby trees for alternatives had already been cut down.

evidence.quote: But Londoners had already chopped down all the nearby trees that could've been used as an alternative fuel source, and not having any fuel stunk more than coal, so within a few years, coal was back.

private.explanationZh: 禁令失败不是因为人们喜欢煤，而是因为附近可当替代燃料的树已经被砍光了；和“没燃料可用”相比，煤烟再难闻也只能继续用煤。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，原文没有把失败原因归结为矿工抗命。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对，文中没有说城市内部发现了新煤矿。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，讲话者明确说人们并不喜欢煤的气味，只是没燃料更糟。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确，证据直接说明缺乏木材替代燃料导致煤又回来了。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker say that "today's different"?

options.text: Modern cities are farther from coal mines than London was.
People now understand coal's harms and also have safer alternatives available.
Coal has become too rare to mine efficiently.
Climate change has already removed every other fuel source.

evidence.quote: But today's different. We now know without a doubt that using coal causes serious harm to our air, our water, and our health - killing millions of people each year.
we now have alternatives like wind and solar and hydro and nuclear, which are way safer than coal, way better for the climate, and on the whole, way cheaper.

private.explanationZh: 讲话者说“今天不同了”，原因有两层：第一，现代人已经清楚知道煤炭会严重伤害空气、水和健康；第二，现在已经有更安全、更便宜、对气候更友好的替代能源。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，原文没有把差异归因于城市与矿区的距离。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，两段证据分别对应“知道危害”和“有替代方案”这两个关键变化。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，讲话者没有说煤太稀缺，反而说地下储量还能供人类使用很多年。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，文中没有说其他燃料来源都消失了。

public.prompt: Why does the talk end by mentioning wind, solar, hydro, and nuclear?

options.text: To show that coal's pollution can easily be buried underground.
To suggest that energy use should return to preindustrial levels.
To present realistic replacements that strengthen the argument for moving away from coal.
To argue that all of those alternatives have already fully replaced coal.

evidence.quote: we now have alternatives like wind and solar and hydro and nuclear, which are way safer than coal, way better for the climate, and on the whole, way cheaper.

private.explanationZh: 结尾列举这些能源不是为了单独介绍它们，而是为了支撑一个论点：既然替代方案已经存在，而且更安全、更便宜、对气候更好，那么摆脱煤炭就具有现实可行性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，这里谈的是替代能源，不是埋藏煤炭污染。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对，讲话者并没有主张回到工业革命之前的低能耗状态。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确，这些例子在论证层面上就是为了证明“戒煤”并非空谈。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，文中说的是可以替代，并未说已经完全取代。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-213-q01: 全文先说明煤为什么极其有用，再集中讲其对矿工、空气、水和气候造成的严重伤害，结尾主张转向替代能源，因此主旨是“煤曾非常有用，但现在应逐步替代”。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-213-q02: 原文直接说伦敦附近可替代的树木已经被砍光，人们仍然需要燃料，所以禁煤令很快失败。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-213-q03: 讲者说“今天不同了”，接着解释我们如今既清楚知道煤的危害，也已经拥有风能、太阳能、水电和核能等更安全的替代方案。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-213-q04: 结尾列举风能、太阳能、水电和核能，是为了说明现在确实存在现实可行的替代选项，从而加强“应摆脱煤炭”的论证。

public.prompt: What pattern does the speaker use to develop the talk?

options.text: It presents several animal case studies showing how some species become winners and others become losers.
It gives one continuous history of a single endangered species.
It argues that human actions always help predators more than prey.
It ranks animal species from strongest to weakest using one formula.

evidence.quote: Today, we're sharing four tales of winners and losers in the animal kingdom.

private.explanationZh: 开头已经交代这是一组“动物界赢家与输家的四个故事”，说明全文采用多个案例并列的结构，通过不同情境展示哪些物种受益、哪些物种受损。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确，证据直接说明整篇由多个故事构成。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对，全文不是围绕单一物种展开，而是不断切换案例。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，有的内容涉及捕食者，有的涉及入侵种和生态系统，结论并不是“总是帮助捕食者”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，讲话者没有使用统一公式给动物排位。

public.prompt: What explanation does the speaker favor for why many American monkeys evolved prehensile tails but African and Asian monkeys did not?

options.text: American monkeys inherited grasping tails from reptile ancestors.
African and Asian monkeys lost their tails because predators attacked them.
Differences in forest structure made extra gripping ability more useful in the Americas.
Scientists have proved that Asian monkeys almost never travel in trees.

evidence.quote: The forests in Central and South America are chock full of vines that monkeys use like tightropes to traverse between trees, so having an extra grip is helpful for balance - and helpful in case of emergency.
Prehensile tails take a lot of extra energy to operate, so it's possible that they just aren't useful enough to those monkeys to be worth having.

private.explanationZh: 讲话者倾向于“藤蔓理论”：美洲森林里藤蔓多，额外抓握能力对平衡和移动更有价值；而在亚洲等地，这种尾巴需要额外能量，却未必足够有用，因此较不容易被保留下来。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，文中没有把原因追溯为来自爬行动物祖先。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对，原文完全没有说尾巴差异是捕食者造成的身体损伤。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确，两段证据一起说明了环境收益与能量成本之间的差异。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，讲话者只是说那里猴子更常下地移动，不是“几乎不在树上活动”。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention Yellowstone after discussing mesopredators?

options.text: To provide an example of how restoring an apex predator can help restructure an ecosystem.
To show that coyotes are more dangerous to humans than wolves are.
To argue that national parks should remove all predators.
To prove that wolves are the only species affected by trophic downgrading.

evidence.quote: Reintroducing wolves to Yellowstone helped restore the whole ecosystem.

private.explanationZh: 黄石公园的例子用来把前面对“中型捕食者上位”和“营养级降级”的抽象讨论落到具体案例上，说明重新引入顶级捕食者会连锁影响整个生态系统。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确，这个例子正是为了展示恢复顶级捕食者后的生态连锁效果。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对，重点不是比较狼和郊狼对人的危险性。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，原文恰恰是在说明某些地方重新引入捕食者有生态价值。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，文中提到很多物种都会受影响，不只有狼。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about controlling invasive species from the final section of the talk?

options.text: A single pesticide usually solves the problem permanently.
Effective control depends on understanding the invader's biology and timing interventions carefully.
Introducing another predator is usually the safest long-term solution.
Removing most of an invasive population is enough to keep it from returning.

evidence.quote: the best approach is a year-long multistep assault that specifically targets ant-riddled areas at the times when the egg-laying queens are mature and susceptible to our treatments.
But with many invasives, even 99% isn't enough, because it doesn't remove whatever advantage gave the invaders an edge in the first place.

private.explanationZh: 最后一节强调，治理入侵物种不是简单“多喷药”就行，而是要了解其生活史、繁殖时机和生态优势，精确安排多步骤干预；否则即使消灭掉大多数个体，也可能卷土重来。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，原文强调的是长期、多步骤、针对性的方案，不是一次性药剂。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，两段证据共同说明了“理解生物学特征+把握时机”的必要性。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，文中把很多生物防治失败案例当成反面教材。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，证据明确说很多情况下连消灭99%都不够。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-214-q01: 开头就说要分享“四个动物界赢家和输家的故事”，后文也依次用猴子尾巴、顶级捕食者与中型捕食者、黑熊与雪貂、入侵物种等案例展开，因此结构是多个案例并列。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-214-q02: 讲者更倾向“藤蔓理论”：中南美森林藤蔓很多，额外抓握能力更有助于在树冠间移动与保持平衡；亚洲森林藤蔓较少，猴子也常下地，因此这种尾巴未必值得保留。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-214-q03: 提到黄石公园是为了举例说明，重新引入狼这样的顶级捕食者可以压制郊狼等中型捕食者，并进一步带动整个生态系统恢复。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-214-q04: 最后一段强调不能靠随便喷药或只消灭大部分个体来永久解决问题，而要先了解入侵者的习性和生物学特征，并在合适时机进行针对性干预。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the video?

options.text: To explain why educational channels deserve support in a platform environment that still spreads misinformation.
To compare several competing theories about ocean boundaries.
To argue that every inaccurate video is part of an organized disinformation campaign.
To teach viewers how YouTube's recommendation system is programmed.

evidence.quote: we're making this video to ask you to support educational channels like ours, and here's why....

private.explanationZh: 开头直接点明目的：这段视频是在请求观众支持像 MinuteEarth 这样的教育频道，后文则用错误信息泛滥和高质量内容制作成本高来说明原因。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确，证据已经明确交代了整段视频的核心目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对，海洋边界只是举过的一个例子，不是视频主旨。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，讲话者反而说很多错误信息并非都来自邪恶的宣传行动。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，视频批评平台效果，但没有讲解算法技术细节。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention climate-change and COVID-19 videos on YouTube?
