options.text: To show that misinformation on the platform can shape understanding of major real-world issues.
To argue that only science topics are harmed by false claims online.
To show that most viewers completely ignore educational channels.
To prove that false videos are always removed quickly.

evidence.quote: a recent report shows that over 20% of views on YouTube videos related to climate change were on videos containing false information.

private.explanationZh: 讲话者举气候变化和新冠疫情相关视频，是为了证明平台上的错误信息不是小问题，而是会影响公众理解重大现实议题，因此支持可靠教育内容很重要。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确，这个例子用具体数据强化“错误信息会误导公众”的论点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对，讲话者没有说只有科学主题受害，只是拿这些重要主题举例。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，证据谈的是错误信息的观看量，不是说观众完全不看教育频道。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，讲话者恰恰批评 YouTube 没有做够。

public.prompt: According to the speaker, what is one reason fact-based videos take so much time to produce?

options.text: Their creators must record everything in a professional studio.
Their writers spend hours checking academic sources so even small points are accurate.
Their animations require scientists to draw every frame by hand.
Their hosts constantly travel to interview viewers in person.

evidence.quote: we spend hours pouring over dozens of academic papers, sometimes just to get a single sentence right.

private.explanationZh: 这里直接说明，高质量科普视频耗时的一个关键原因是作者要反复查阅大量学术论文，甚至为了写对一句话都要花上很多时间。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，证据没有提到专业录音棚是主要耗时来源。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，原文明确说作者会花数小时查阅大量论文来保证准确性。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，虽然文中提到插画师也看文献，但没有说科学家手绘每一帧。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，讲话者没有说主持人要到处旅行采访观众。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about Patreon support from the end of the talk?

options.text: It mainly helps the channel pay for new filming locations.
It reduces dependence on unstable ad revenue and helps the channel keep producing careful educational content.
It is the only way viewers can see new MinuteEarth videos.
It matters more than sharing or engaging with videos.

evidence.quote: patreon support lessens our reliance on ad-based revenue sources - which have become way less reliable in these times of global pandemic - and enables us to keep making educational videos

private.explanationZh: 结尾对 Patreon 的说明表明，它的作用不是提供花哨福利，而是降低频道对不稳定广告收入的依赖，让团队能够继续投入时间制作准确、严谨的教育视频。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，原文没有把 Patreon 的价值说成主要用于外景拍摄。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，证据直接点明了“减少广告依赖”和“继续制作教育视频”这两个作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，Patreon 不是观看普通新视频的唯一途径。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，讲话者也明确提到分享和互动同样是支持方式。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-215-q01: 视频的核心目的是说明YouTube上存在大量误导性内容，而认真做科普需要大量工作，因此呼吁观众支持像MinuteEarth这样的教育频道。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-215-q02: 讲者举气候变化和新冠视频为例，是为了说明平台上的错误信息会影响人们对重大现实议题的理解，不只是无关紧要的小错误。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-215-q03: 原文明确说他们写脚本时会花很多小时查阅几十篇学术论文，有时只是为了把一句话写准确，这正是制作耗时的重要原因。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-215-q04: 结尾说Patreon支持能减少对不稳定广告收入的依赖，并帮助他们继续制作严谨、准确的教育内容，所以应选这一项。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare climate change to a leaky roof?

options.text: To argue that climate damage is mostly a problem for homeowners.
To show that acting early can cost less than waiting for much larger damage.
To explain why insurance companies should lead climate policy.
To suggest that climate solutions work only for wealthy countries.

evidence.quote: It's like if your house has a leaky roof: you can either wait until your entire house is moldy and rotten to have it fixed, or you can fix the leak now and save a lot of money on repairs.

private.explanationZh: 这个比喻的作用是把抽象的气候成本变成日常经验：如果现在就修补问题，花费较小；如果拖到灾害全面扩大后再处理，代价会高得多。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，比喻的重点不是房主，而是“及早处理更省钱”的逻辑。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，证据明确对比了提前修补与拖延造成的大额损失。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，原文没有讨论保险公司在政策中的角色。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，比喻说明的是成本时机问题，不是国家贫富差异。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what is the average cost of slowing climate change over the next 40 years?

options.text: About $600 per person.
About $6,000 per person.
About $50,000 per person.
About $500,000 per person.

evidence.quote: we'll need to switch away from fossil fuels and pull some carbon back out of the atmosphere, which will cost on average $6000 per person over the next 40 years.

private.explanationZh: 原文直接给出数字：在未来四十年里，为了减缓气候变化，人均平均成本大约是 6000 美元。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，少了一个数量级，文中不是600美元。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确，证据中明确写出是每人平均6000美元。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，5万美元是文中提到的避免损失规模之一，不是前40年的行动成本。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，50万美元左右对应更远期避免的损失，不是当前行动成本。

public.prompt: What does the speaker suggest is the economic benefit of limiting warming to about 2 degrees Celsius?

options.text: It would eliminate every climate-related loss completely.
It would delay damages until after 2100 without reducing them.
It would avoid most disasters and therefore most of the associated economic harm.
It would make fossil fuels cheaper while renewable energy stays expensive.

evidence.quote: Fortunately, if we limit warming to just 2 degrees Celsius, we can avoid most - but not all - of these disasters and as a result also avoid most of the economic damages.

private.explanationZh: 讲话者的逻辑是：把升温限制在约2摄氏度并不能完全消除风险，但能避免“大多数”灾害，因此也能避免大多数由灾害带来的经济损失。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对，原文明确说是避免大多数而不是全部损失。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对，证据说的是减少损害，不是简单把损害推迟。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确，证据直接把“避免多数灾害”和“避免多数经济损失”联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，文中没有把收益解释为化石燃料更便宜。

public.prompt: What overall conclusion does the speaker draw for people alive today?

options.text: Slowing climate change is financially sensible even apart from concern for future generations.
The economic effects of climate change are too uncertain to guide decisions.
Only people born after 2060 will gain from climate action.
Individuals should focus on personal savings rather than changes in energy systems.

evidence.quote: doing so will save each of us on average $100,000 in avoided damages during our lifetimes, leaving us with more money in our wallets.

private.explanationZh: 结论部分明确把论点落到“活着的我们”身上：即使先不考虑未来世代，仅从自己这一生的经济账来看，减缓气候变化也是划算的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确，证据明确说当前活着的人一生中平均也能避免约10万美元损失。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对，整篇恰恰在用成本与收益数字说明这件事足以指导决策。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对，讲话者专门强调即使不考虑未来世代，对今天活着的人也有好处。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对，原文讨论的是能源转型与减排投资，而不是回避系统性改变。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-216-q01: 漏屋顶的比喻用来说明越早处理问题，成本越低；如果等到损害扩大后再修复，代价会高得多，对应到气候行动也是一样。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-216-q02: 讲者直接说未来40年减缓气候变化的平均成本约为每人6000美元。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-216-q03: 原文说如果把升温限制在2摄氏度左右，就能避免大多数灾害，因此也能避免大多数相关经济损失。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-216-q04: 结论部分明确指出，即使不考虑未来世代，对今天活着的人来说，减缓气候变化在经济上也划算，因为一生中平均可避免约10万美元损失。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the talk?

options.text: Human attempts to solve problems often create unintended consequences, although some unexpected effects can also be useful.
Most environmental problems can be solved once governments collect better data from sensors.
Technologies become dangerous mainly because people forget why they invented them.
Climate change is the single cause behind wildfires, malaria, and nuclear testing.

evidence.quote: We are masters of unintended consequences.
Sometimes the unanticipated results of our actions can actually be the bomb!

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。讲话人在前半段连续讲空调、灭火政策和农业如何带来反效果，后半段又补充核试监测网络带来了意外的科学收益，因此核心观点不是“人类做的事都糟糕”，而是“人类行为常常会产生未预料到的后果，其中有害也有利”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了全文主线：人类的解决方案常引发意外后果，而且结尾还特意说明这种意外后果有时也会带来好处。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。传感器网络只出现在最后一个例子里，不能代表全文主旨。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中强调的是系统性的副作用和反馈，不是单纯因为人们忘记发明初衷。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。野火、疟疾和核试监测被并列为不同案例，文中并没有说它们都由气候变化单独造成。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why do old air conditioners create an extra climate problem when they are discarded?

options.text: Their metal parts keep releasing stored heat for years after disposal.
Their leaked refrigerants trap far more heat than carbon dioxide.
Their filters spread smoke that makes nearby cities warmer.
Their motors continue using electricity after they are removed.

evidence.quote: most of their chemical refrigerants leak out and evaporate.
the refrigerants we use are thousands of times more powerful than CO2 at trapping the Earth's heat.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。这里的关键不是空调报废后继续耗电，而是制冷剂泄漏。讲话人明确说这些化学制冷剂泄漏到空气中后，温室效应比二氧化碳强得多，因此报废环节本身会额外加剧变暖。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说金属外壳会多年持续放热。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两处证据连起来构成完整因果：旧空调报废时制冷剂泄漏，而这些制冷剂的增温能力远强于二氧化碳。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话人讨论的是制冷剂和城市热量外排，不是滤网传播烟雾。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。空调运行时会用电，但题目问的是“丢弃时”额外造成的问题。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about wildfire policy from the speaker's discussion of Congress?

options.text: Controlled burns have been rejected because they always destroy more trees than wildfires do.
Forest managers already have enough money for prevention, but local communities refuse to cooperate.
Funding is focused on fighting current fires, which leaves less support for prevention and can increase future risk.
Congress mainly funds preventive work, but the Forest Service prefers emergency spending.

evidence.quote: Congress allocates the vast majority of fire management funds to emergency measures rather than preventive ones.

So fire managers are often forced to steal money from already meager fuel reduction programs to fight this year's blazes, thus fueling the risk that next year's fires will be costlier, deadlier, and more disastrous.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。讲话人虽然没有直接说“政策设计失败”，但通过拨款结构和“挪用”预防资金去灭当年的火这一描述，可以推断出政策重心偏向应急而不是预防，这会形成恶性循环，让未来火灾更难控制。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中恰恰说在适当条件下控制燃烧能让火更慢、更冷、破坏更小。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话人指出问题首先在资金不足和拨款结构，而不是地方社区拒绝配合。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这正是两段证据合并后的含义：钱先花在眼前灭火上，预防被压缩，于是未来风险更高。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。证据明确说国会把“大多数”资金给了应急措施，不是给预防。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker end with the global network for detecting nuclear tests?

options.text: To argue that nuclear tests are now the greatest environmental threat on Earth.
To prove that volcanoes and whales are easier to study than climate change.
To suggest that governments should stop sharing scientific data across countries.
To show that unintended consequences can also produce valuable scientific knowledge.

evidence.quote: This background noise is a headache for the people listening for nuclear explosions, but it's a boon for lots of other listeners who can also access the data with permission.
The system may have been built to detect nuclear tests, but it's led to an explosion of knowledge about our planet

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。结尾这个例子和前面多个负面案例形成对照：同样是意外后果，这次不是制造麻烦，而是让科学家获得关于鲸鱼、流星、火山、地震等现象的新知识。作者借此把全文主旨补充完整。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里重点不是核试本身的危险性，而是监测系统的副产品。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中列举这些研究用途，是说明数据带来的额外价值，不是比较哪个课题更容易研究。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话人说许多研究者能在获准后使用数据，语气是肯定共享价值，不是反对共享。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。作者明确说该系统原本为核试而建，却意外促进了对地球的认识。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-217-q01: 全文先讲空调、野火和疟疾等负面连锁后果，最后又讲核试监测网络带来的科学收益，主旨就是人类解决问题常产生意外后果，但有些意外结果也可能有益。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-217-q02: 原文明确说旧空调报废时制冷剂会泄漏，而这些制冷剂“比二氧化碳强上千倍”地滞留热量，所以额外的气候问题来自泄漏制冷剂。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-217-q03: 关于国会的部分说大多数资金被拨去应急灭火而不是预防，火灾管理者还会挪用本就不多的减灾资金去扑救当年的大火，因此未来风险反而会上升。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-217-q04: 结尾转到核试监测网络，是为了补充说明“意外后果”不全是坏事；这个本来为核爆而建的系统意外带来了大量关于地球的科学知识。

public.prompt: What does the speaker suggest is the most likely long-term future of gas stations?

options.text: Gas stations may survive by adapting to new vehicle fuels rather than disappearing completely.
Gasoline cars will vanish as quickly as horse-drawn vehicles did.
Pharmacies are likely to become the main places where drivers refuel in the future.
Electric vehicles already dominate both new car sales and the total number of cars on the road.

evidence.quote: our gas stations may simply get converted into stations for selling new types of fuel.
the future of gas stations may be quite stable.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。全文不是简单预测“加油站消失”，而是论证它们可能像过去的马厩一样，随着交通能源变化而转型，继续存在于新的交通基础设施中。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两处证据直接点明加油站可能通过转型延续下来。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话人特别提醒不要太早给加油站“写悼词”，说明不会那么快消失。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。药房只是早期历史例子，不是未来结论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中给出的数字正好说明电动车占比仍然很低。

public.prompt: Where did Bertha Benz get fuel after her car ran out during the 1888 trip?

options.text: At a horse stable that stored petroleum.
At a pharmacy that sold ligroin.
At a repair shop built for automobiles.
At a factory owned by Karl Benz.

evidence.quote: pharmacies of the era sold a petroleum product called ligroin
she visited a pharmacy and was able to finish her journey.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。讲话人先说明当时药房出售一种叫 ligroin 的石油产品，然后说 Bertha Benz 去了药房，靠这个燃料完成旅程。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。马厩只用于和马车时代作对比，不是她补给燃料的地方。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两段证据一前一后，明确给出燃料种类和地点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。专门的加油站和汽车维修设施是在更晚的时候才出现的。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有提到她去工厂取油。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker say gas stations should not be eulogized yet?

options.text: Drivers strongly prefer compressed-air cars to electric ones.
Gasoline prices are expected to fall for the next twenty years.
Electric vehicles still make up a small share of cars, and replacing the whole fleet will take time.
Most charging stations have already been converted into gas stations.

evidence.quote: Only 1 of every 200 cars on the road today is electric and 1 of every 40 new cars purchased is electric, although that rate is increasing.
it's projected that it might take 20 years before even a quarter of the cars on the road are electric.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。作者先给出当前电动车渗透率仍低，再补充车辆更替速度慢，因此即使趋势在增长，加油站也不会立刻失去作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。压缩空气车只是被列为一种可能的替代动力，没有说消费者更偏好它。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有讨论未来汽油价格。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这是对两组数字的合理概括：现有占比低，而且整体替换需要很长时间。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中说的是未来某些加油站可能转型，不是现在充电站已经反向改造成加油站。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention that many stables were converted into dealerships and repair shops?

options.text: To show that horse owners resisted the arrival of automobiles.
To explain why early car companies invested heavily in farming.
To argue that repair shops are more profitable than fuel sales.
To suggest that gas stations could also be repurposed instead of disappearing.

evidence.quote: lots of stables ended up getting converted into dealerships and repair shops for the new type of carriage.
our gas stations may simply get converted into stations for selling new types of fuel.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。马厩的例子不是历史闲笔，而是一个类比：旧交通体系中的设施没有完全消失，而是转成了服务新交通方式的设施。作者借此推测加油站未来也可能如此。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里没有讨论马主的态度。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有把马厩与农业投资联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话人没有比较维修业务和燃料销售的利润。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。前后两句构成明显类比，直接服务于“加油站转型”的预测。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-218-q01: 结尾明确说即使未来全面转向非石油燃料，加油站也可能仍处于核心位置，只是被改造成出售新型燃料的站点，因此最可能的长期结果是适应而不是消失。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-218-q02: 开头讲贝莎·本茨的车没油后，去了一家出售ligroin的药房，靠它完成旅程，因此答案是药房。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-218-q03: 说“不必急着给加油站写悼词”后，讲话者马上给出原因：现在路上电动车只占很小比例，而且因为人们换车不频繁，整个车队的替换要花很久。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-218-q04: 提到马厩后来被改成经销店和维修店，是类比说明旧基础设施可以转作新用途，所以加油站未来也可能被重新利用，而非彻底消失。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the talk?

options.text: To present several stories showing different ways extreme weather is measured, managed, judged, and perceived.
To prove that one weather event can explain most climate disagreements.
To argue that sea-level rise is more important than all other weather threats.
To compare the quality of weather forecasts in different countries.

evidence.quote: today, we're bringing you four stories about extreme weather,
How we experience extreme weather in the first place.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。开头就说明这是关于 extreme weather 的四个故事，后面四段内容分别谈最热地点、城市防御与法律责任、以及人们如何感知极端天气，体现的是多个维度的综合讨论，而不是单一论点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了四个案例的共同功能：从不同角度理解极端天气。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。最后一部分谈的是认知分歧，但不是全文唯一重点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。海平面上升只属于其中一个故事。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话中涉及预测能力和媒体，但并不是国家间天气预报质量比较。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker rely on NASA satellite data rather than weather stations to identify the hottest place on Earth's surface?

options.text: Because satellites can predict future heat waves, but weather stations cannot measure temperature directly.
Because weather stations are sparse and measure shaded air, while satellites provide much broader surface coverage.
Because weather stations are located only in cold countries, while satellites observe deserts.
Because satellites measure underground heat, which weather stations always miss.

evidence.quote: there are only 11,119 official weather stations scattered across the globe
Their resolution isn't amazing, since the satellites measure temperature averaged over each square kilometer of the globe, but that's still 13,000 times better than weather stations.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。作者前面先指出地面站数量很少、覆盖很稀疏，后面又说卫星虽然分辨率也有限，但仍比气象站细得多，因此更适合寻找“地表最热”的区域。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里讨论的是定位最热地点，不是预测热浪。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它整合了地面站稀少和卫星覆盖更广这两个关键信息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说气象站只设在寒冷国家。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。卫星测的是地表红外辐射，不是地下热量。

public.prompt: What does the discussion of the "act of god" defense suggest about future disaster lawsuits?

options.text: The defense will become easier to use because climate change makes disasters more common.
Courts are likely to stop considering any weather-related evidence at all.
The defense may become harder to use because better forecasting and evidence of human influence weaken claims that disasters were purely natural and unforeseeable.
Lawyers will use the defense mainly in cases involving damaged food shipments.

evidence.quote: you just have to prove two things: one, that taking reasonable precautions couldn't have prevented the damage, and two, that the damage was caused by a natural force with no human influence.
the success of the act of god defense may soon be relegated fully to the margarines.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。讲话人先说明这种抗辩成立的两个条件，随后指出如今预测更强、人类影响更容易被识别，因此满足这两个条件越来越难，未来这类抗辩成功率可能下降。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。灾害更常见并不自动让抗辩更容易，作者恰恰说会更难。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说法院会拒绝气象证据，相反，科学证据变得更重要。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这是对法律条件和作者结论的综合推断。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。黄油车案只是引入例子，不是未来诉讼的主要类型。

public.prompt: Why does the final story focus on personal experience and media coverage?

options.text: To show that media reports are more accurate than measurements from weather stations.
To argue that political beliefs are the only factor shaping climate opinions.
To prove that recent storms are less severe than people think.
To explain why people can disagree about whether weather is becoming more extreme.

evidence.quote: these days we get less of our weather information first-hand and more from the media
As a result, much of what we hear about the frequency of extreme weather can end up reinforcing whatever we already believe, rather than giving us new information about what's really going on.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。作者最后不是在继续介绍天气现象本身，而是在解释认知差异从何而来：人们既受个人近期体验影响，也会从媒体中选择符合自身立场的信息，所以会对“天气是否更怪”得出不同判断。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者并没有说媒体比气象测量更准确，反而提醒媒体会强化偏见。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。政治倾向只是因素之一，个人经历和媒体接触也被明确提到。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者讨论的是知觉与信息过滤，不是在证明大家夸大了风暴严重性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。两段证据都指向同一个目的：说明分歧是如何形成的。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-219-q01: 这段内容分成四个极端天气故事，分别谈最热地点如何测量、城市如何应对风暴、法律上如何判断灾害责任、以及人们如何感知极端天气，整体目的就是展示极端天气在这些不同层面的故事。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-219-q02: 原文说地面气象站既稀少又测的是离地约1.5米阴影中的空气温度，而NASA卫星每天都能大范围测量地表红外热辐射，因此更适合找“地表最热处”。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-219-q03: 讲者指出“天灾免责”需要证明灾害无法通过合理预防避免，且没有人类影响；但如今预测技术更强，而且越来越多灾害带有人类活动痕迹，所以这种辩护未来会更难成立。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-219-q04: 最后一节强调个人近期经历、政治立场和媒体表述都会影响人们对天气是否更极端的判断，因此其作用是解释为什么人们会对同一问题得出不同看法。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly trying to explain about hyenas?

options.text: Humans often dislike hyenas because cultural stories and human-centered judgments distort how we see them.
Hyenas are more dangerous to people than other large predators.
Hyenas became hated mainly because Disney films misrepresented them.
People who live near hyenas generally admire them more than outsiders do.

evidence.quote: one negative depiction begets another
Hyenas' PR problem might also stem from the fact that, just as we view animals' appearance through a human lens, we also view their behavior through a human lens

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。讲话人把原因分成两层：一是长期的负面文化叙述会不断复制自己，二是人类会用“像不像人”“合不合人类规范”的眼光评判动物，因此对鬣狗产生系统性偏见。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时涵盖了文化再生产和人类中心视角这两个核心原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话人明确说“会伤人”本身不足以解释，因为很多危险动物仍然受喜爱。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者甚至说这种丑化“远在迪士尼之前”就已经存在。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中提到与鬣狗共处的人群也未必更喜欢它们。

public.prompt: According to the speaker, what kind of animal traits tend to repel humans?

options.text: Traits that make animals too intelligent to control.
Traits that would look unhealthy, old, or ugly on a person.
Traits that show animals are highly social hunters.
Traits that allow animals to survive in extreme climates.

evidence.quote: one thing that turns us humans off from animals is traits that - on a human - would look unhealthy, old, or just plain ugly

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。这一题几乎是直接转述：研究者发现，人类会把一些如果出现在人身上会显得病态、衰老或丑陋的特征，投射成对动物的反感。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有讨论“过于聪明而难以控制”的特征。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这是讲话人明确给出的研究结论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。高社会性或群体狩猎不是这里提到的反感来源。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。极端环境适应能力与本题证据无关。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker group hyenas with snakes, bats, and rats?

options.text: To argue that all four animals spread the same diseases.
To show that these animals are closely related evolutionarily.
To show that species seen as violating human norms are often cast as villains.
To prove that people fear only nocturnal animals.

evidence.quote: it might violate our standards for what's acceptable, instinctively triggering a deep dislike in us.
animals that we also reliably typecast as villains.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。作者把这些动物放在一起，不是为了做生物分类，而是说明一种共同的心理机制：当某些动物的行为或外形违背人类直觉中的“正常”，人们就更容易把它们叙述成反派。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里没有讨论共同疾病传播。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话人关心的是人类印象，不是亲缘关系。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。两处证据一处解释原因，一处说明结果，正好支持这个推断。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。蛇并不一定夜行，题干也不是在讲昼夜活动模式。

public.prompt: What point does the speaker make at the end by saying our reactions to hyenas may say more about us than about them?

options.text: Scientists still know almost nothing about hyena behavior.
Hyenas would be liked more if they looked like dogs.
Folklore is the only reason anyone dislikes hyenas.
Human bias can be a poor basis for judging an entire species.

evidence.quote: our tendency to write off entire species based on our own limited perspective might say more about us than it does about them

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。结尾句是全文的评价性总结：作者并不是要强迫听众喜欢鬣狗，而是要指出，人类常用自己狭窄的价值标准给整个物种贴标签，这种判断方式本身就值得反思。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者讨论的是人类判断方式，不是科学知识不足。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。外形因素是其中一个原因，但结尾上升到更广泛的人类偏见问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。民间故事只是来源之一，作者还谈到外貌和行为被人类化解读。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它把原句中的“limited perspective”准确转化为“偏见不应成为判断物种的依据”。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-220-q01: 全文围绕“为什么人们讨厌鬣狗”展开，核心解释是文化中的负面讲述再加上人类以自身标准评判外貌和行为，扭曲了我们对鬣狗的看法。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-220-q02: 讲者直接说研究发现，会让人反感的动物特征，是那些放在人身上会显得不健康、衰老或丑陋的特征。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-220-q03: 她把鬣狗和蛇、蝙蝠、老鼠并列，是为了说明当一种动物的外貌或行为看起来违背人类规范时，人们常把这类物种都塑造成“反派”。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-220-q04: 结尾说我们对鬣狗的反应也许更多反映了我们自己，意思就是人类自身的偏见并不是评判整个物种的可靠依据。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the talk?

options.text: Several different conditions can drive extinction, especially when species cannot adapt fast enough to change.
Evolution usually guarantees that any species with enough food will survive.
Blob simulations prove that predators are the only real cause of extinction.
Most extinctions happen because species fail to reproduce at all.

evidence.quote: mostly, blobs - and species in general - go extinct.
the main cause of a population surviving or dying off was often a result of how quickly the environment changed.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。全文围绕“物种为什么会灭绝”展开，模拟和真实案例都不断说明：当环境、竞争或捕食等变化来得太快，进化来不及应对，灭绝就会发生。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它既概括了全文主题，也抓住了多次重复出现的“变化速度”这一关键。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话人强调的正相反：即便物种曾经成功，灭绝依然十分常见。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中讨论了食物减少、竞争、捕食、人类扰动、运气等多种机制。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。繁殖失败不是全文给出的唯一或主要总解释。

public.prompt: In Jasper's simulation, what often determined whether a population survived or died off?

options.text: Whether predators were eliminated before the first generation.
How quickly the environment changed.
How brightly colored the blobs became over time.
How long individual blobs stayed near the center of the board.

evidence.quote: the main cause of a population surviving or dying off was often a result of how quickly the environment changed.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。这是 Jasper 直接总结出的模拟结论：环境变化如果缓慢，群体还有时间适应；如果骤变，群体就可能来不及演化而灭绝。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。捕食只是后面讨论的另一种机制，不是这句总结中的核心变量。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这是原文的直接信息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。颜色只是后面可视化速度变化的方式，不是生存决定因素。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有把停留在棋盘中心的时间作为主要决定因素。

public.prompt: Why does Kate keep connecting the blob simulations to ferrets, Tasmanian tigers, and birds in Guam?

options.text: To argue that blob experiments can replace field research on animals.
To prove that predators are always introduced intentionally by humans.
To show that the simulated patterns mirror real extinctions caused by rapid human-driven changes.
To show that island species are the only animals threatened by extinction.

evidence.quote: This is what we see so often when invasive species dominate native ones.
we humans tend to change things at a speed that evolution just can't keep up with.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。Kate 一再把 blob 世界和真实动物案例对照，是为了说明模拟不是抽象游戏，而是在提炼现实中的灭绝机制，尤其是人类引发的快速环境变化和物种入侵。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。作者用真实案例来解释模拟，不是说模拟能取代野外研究。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说所有捕食者都是人类故意引入的。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。两处证据分别把模拟现象连到现实案例，并点出背后的共同驱动力。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。鸟类关岛案例只是其中一个例子，灭绝风险远不限于岛屿物种。

public.prompt: How does the talk develop in its final section?

options.text: It abandons the simulation and focuses only on advertising the app.
It argues that random chance matters more than all environmental pressures.
It concludes that cows and wild oxen should still be classified as the same species.
It shifts from extinction as loss to the idea that a lineage can disappear by evolving into a new species.

evidence.quote: extinction isn't necessarily about failure
that extinction wasn't really a dead end.
before it did, a subgroup of the population evolved into a totally new species

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是A。最后一段在结构上明显转向“重新定义灭绝”：前面主要谈灭绝如何发生，结尾则说明某个旧物种的消失也可能意味着新物种的出现，因此灭绝未必只是彻底终结。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。宣传内容确实出现，但在那之前作者已经完成了重要的概念收束。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。随机性在前一段被提到，但最终收束并不是强调它高于一切。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。作者是用牛和野牛祖先说明“旧种消失而新种延续”的思路，不是主张二者仍属同一物种。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。三处证据共同支持这一结构变化：从“失败/终点”的理解转到“演化分支”的理解。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-221-q01: 整段通过模拟和现实案例说明，灭绝可能由环境骤变、竞争、捕食、入侵物种、随机性等多种条件导致，尤其是在物种来不及适应变化时。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-221-q02: Jasper明确总结说，种群能否存活“通常取决于环境变化得有多快”；慢变时还能适应，快变时就会灭绝。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-221-q03: Kate不断把模拟对应到雪貂、袋狼和关岛鸟类，是为了表明模拟中的模式确实映照了现实世界中由人类快速改变环境而引发的灭绝事件。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-221-q04: 最后一部分先谈随机灭绝，随后转向“新物种形成是否意味着旧物种灭绝”的哲学问题，并用野牛和奶牛说明一条谱系可以通过演化成新物种而让旧物种消失。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the talk?

options.text: Why combined sewer systems overflow more often now and how cities are trying to respond
How indoor plumbing quickly eliminated water pollution in large cities
Why Lake Michigan is the most vulnerable waterway in the world
How separate sewer systems became the standard in every modern city

evidence.quote: As urbanization continues and storms get worse, combined sewer systems are overflowing more and more.

private.explanationZh: 主旨是解释合流制下水道为什么越来越常溢流，以及城市正在尝试哪些补救办法。证据句直接概括了问题加剧的原因，后文又继续介绍了多种应对方案。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。全文围绕溢流变多的原因与治理思路展开。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中说早期系统把多种废物流一起送走，后来才发现污染问题，并没有说它消除了水污染。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。芝加哥和密歇根湖只是开场例子，不是全文重点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中明确说彻底更换为分流系统过于昂贵麻烦，并非已经普及。

public.prompt: Why did cities add processing plants to their sewer systems?

options.text: To store stormwater until it could evaporate safely
To clean wastewater before releasing it into nearby waterways
To separate toxic road chemicals from household sewage completely
To send untreated sewage farther away from city residents

evidence.quote: when we realized dumping massive amounts of sewage into nearby lakes & rivers wasn't a great idea, the cities soon added processing plants to clean the wastewater before it was released.

private.explanationZh: 这里考查明确细节。城市后来加建处理厂，是因为人们意识到把大量污水直接排入湖泊和河流并不好，所以要先净化再排放。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说处理厂的主要作用是等水自然蒸发。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句明确说处理厂是为了在排放前清理废水。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说处理厂能把所有污染物完全分离开。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。处理厂是为了净化，不是把未经处理的污水排得更远。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the effect of urban development on sewer overflows?

options.text: It reduces overflow risk by giving stormwater more channels to escape
It matters only in cities that have never built treatment plants
It worsens overflows because more paved land sends rainwater into sewers instead of soil
It has little effect compared with population growth alone

evidence.quote: because cities have continued to build an increasingly widespread web of concrete and pavement, less of that megastorm water can soak into the ground and more of it gets funneled into the sewers.

private.explanationZh: 这题要求根据因果链做推断。城市铺设更多混凝土和路面，会减少雨水下渗、增加进入下水道的径流，因此更容易造成溢流。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。证据恰好相反，硬化地表会让更多水进入下水道。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这种影响来自地表径流变化，不以是否建有处理厂为前提。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确概括了证据句中的机制。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中强调的是暴雨更频繁和城市硬化共同加剧问题，不是人口增长单独主导。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention parks, roof gardens, and Chicago's large overflow tank?

options.text: To argue that replacing old sewer pipes is inexpensive
To show that toxic storm runoff is harmless once it reaches rivers
To explain why Chicago caused the global overflow problem
To give examples of alternative solutions cities are using instead of fully rebuilding sewer systems

evidence.quote: Some are investing in parks and roof gardens in hopes of better soaking up those megastorm rains before they get to the sewers. And Chicago is digging a 10 billion gallon in-case-of-poopmergency overflow tank. Combined sewers have helped cause this problem, here's hoping these combined solutions can help head off the coming poopocalypse.

private.explanationZh: 说话者列举公园、屋顶花园和超大储水池，是为了展示旧城在不全面翻修管网的情况下，正在尝试的多种替代性治理方案。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。前文刚说全面更换管道会非常昂贵且扰民，这里不是在说便宜。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中只说某些城市宁可先放出相对没那么毒的雨水，也没有说它无害。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。举芝加哥是为了说明解决思路，不是追究谁制造了全球问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这些例子正是“组合式解决方案”的具体体现。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-222-q01: 讲座先用芝加哥污水外溢引入，随后解释合流制下水道为何如今更常溢流，并介绍城市正在采取的应对办法。A完整概括主旨，其他选项都与原文不符或范围过窄。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-222-q02: 原文明确说城市后来加入processing plants，是为了在排入附近水体前先清理废水。B与原文直接对应，其余选项都不是文中给出的原因。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-222-q03: 原文说城市铺了越来越多混凝土和路面，导致更少雨水渗入地下、更多雨水被导入下水道，因此更容易溢流。C准确概括这一推论。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-222-q04: 说到公园、屋顶花园和芝加哥的大型储水设施，是为了举例说明许多城市没有全面更换管道，而是在尝试其他解决方案。D最符合话语功能。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why can some freshwater shrimp sell for extremely high prices?

options.text: Because they live much longer than ordinary aquarium shrimp
Because rarity and human desire make difficult-to-breed color patterns valuable
Because wild shrimp naturally develop more colorful shells than bred shrimp
Because aquarium stores have very high maintenance costs for all shrimp

evidence.quote: aquarium shrimp prices are driven by the thing that always drives ridiculously high prices: human behavior and scarcity.

private.explanationZh: 主旨是解释高价虾并不是因为寿命或实用价值，而是因为稀缺性和人类对稀有物的追逐，再加上这种颜色和花纹难以稳定培育。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲者明确说贵的和便宜的寿命都差不多。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句直接点出“人类行为和稀缺性”是高价核心。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中说野外个体大多颜色普通，鲜艳花纹多靠突变和选育。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。全文没有把价格重点归因于店铺维护成本。

public.prompt: What do rare genetic mutations change in these shrimp?

options.text: They increase the shrimp's lifespan by about eighteen months
They make the shell thicker so it reflects more light
They alter how pigment cells are stacked and arranged, changing color and pattern
They allow breeders to raise shrimp without selecting among offspring

evidence.quote: slight genetic mutations can cause the cells to change the order in which they're stacked, which changes the shell's color and can also shift how the pigment cells are laid out across the shrimp's shell, which changes the pattern.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。文中把机制讲得很具体：突变会改变色素细胞的堆叠顺序和分布位置，从而改变颜色与花纹。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。18个月是寿命信息，不是突变造成的变化。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有提到壳变厚或反光增强。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它完整对应证据句中的两个变化：颜色和图案。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。恰恰相反，育种者需要长期挑选后代。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention several different theories from psychologists and economists?

options.text: To prove that only one field can explain luxury purchases
To show that rarity matters only when an item is useful
To suggest that shrimp breeders themselves disagree about shell colors
To emphasize that people clearly pay for rarity even if the precise reason is debated

evidence.quote: Why people value rarity depends on which psychologist or economist you ask.

private.explanationZh: 讲者列出多种理论，不是为了定论，而是为了说明：具体心理机制也许有争议，但“稀有性能推高价格”这一现象很明显。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲者不是在排他地支持某一个学科解释。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。后文还特别说这些东西未必有特别高的内在价值。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里讨论的是消费者为何偏爱稀有物，不是育种者对颜色的争论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确概括了“原因可争论，现象很确定”的作用。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker end by mentioning a white painted shrimp that sells for $10,000?

options.text: To provide a striking example of how rarity can outweigh an animal's ordinary appeal
To show that white shrimp are especially useful in home aquariums
To argue that painted shrimp are the first shrimp ever bred in captivity
To compare shrimp prices with the cost of maintaining large fish tanks

evidence.quote: You probably haven't, however, bought a white painted shrimp, which isn't a very compelling crustacean compared to some relatively cheap shrimp, but because of its rarity, it sells for a whopping $10,000

private.explanationZh: 结尾举这只“看起来也没多特别”的白虾，是为了把前面的抽象论点落到一个夸张、好记的实例上，突出“稀有本身就能拉高价格”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。例子强调的是稀有性压过了内在吸引力。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说这种虾在水族箱里更实用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。没有任何“第一种人工繁育虾”的信息。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里比较的是价格与吸引力，不是养殖成本。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-223-q01: 讲座核心解释的是：某些淡水虾价格极高，是因为稀有颜色和花纹难以稳定繁殖，而人类又会为稀有性支付高价。B准确概括主旨。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-223-q02: 原文说罕见突变会改变色素细胞堆叠顺序和在壳上的分布，从而改变颜色与图案。C与原文表述一致。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-223-q03: 讲者列出心理学家和经济学家的不同解释后，紧接着说“不管确切原因是什么，人们确实会为稀有性付高价”。因此D最能概括这一作用。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-223-q04: 结尾提到售价一万美元的白色虾，是用一个醒目的例子说明：即使这种虾本身并不特别吸引人，稀有性仍能把价格推得极高。A最符合目的。

public.prompt: What point does the talk mainly make about winter mortality?

options.text: Almost all seasonal deaths come from summer driving and swimming
Only infectious diseases make winter more dangerous than other seasons
Winter deaths rise sharply, especially because cold weather also increases heart attacks and strokes
Seasonal differences disappear once global numbers are considered

evidence.quote: What really keeps Death late at the office is that deaths from heart attacks and strokes also rise when it's chilly.

private.explanationZh: 主旨是说明冬天死亡人数更高，而且关键不只是流感和肺炎，寒冷还会显著增加心脏病和中风。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。夏季某些死因会上升，但远不及冬季额外死亡。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲者明确说真正让冬季死亡暴增的，还有心梗和中风。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它概括了全文最核心的解释。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。全球层面并没有消失，只是南北半球时间相反。

public.prompt: Why do flu and pneumonia deaths increase in winter, according to the speaker?

options.text: People exercise less, so their lungs become permanently weaker
Winter air creates new diseases that do not exist in summer
Hospitals close more often during cold weather
The microbes survive better in cold, dry conditions and spread more easily indoors

evidence.quote: the bugs that cause those infections survive better in colder, drier weather and jump from host to host more easily as people crowd together indoors.

private.explanationZh: 这道题直接对应原文细节：病原体在寒冷干燥环境中更容易存活，而人们冬天聚集室内，也让传播更顺。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有把原因归结为缺乏运动。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。不是冬天“创造了新疾病”，而是已有病原体更易传播。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有提到医院关闭。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这正是讲者给出的两部分原因。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker say that narrowed blood vessels are "great! Kind of."?

options.text: Because they help conserve heat but also raise the risk of dangerous clots
Because they prevent all winter illnesses except pneumonia
Because they matter only for people who already had strokes
Because they mainly explain why summer deaths increase

evidence.quote: our blood vessels constrict to keep us from losing precious heat to the outside world - which is great! Kind of.

narrower vessels also mean that our platelets - sticky blood cells that we sometimes want to clump together to form clots - are more likely to clump together when we don't want them to, blocking arteries and causing deadly heart attacks and strokes.

private.explanationZh: 这里的“Kind of”表示转折：血管收缩有保暖的好处，但也会让血小板更容易聚集，从而增加堵塞血管的风险。需要把前后两句合起来理解。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时保留了“有益”和“有害”两层意思。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说它能预防除肺炎外的所有疾病。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲者解释的是普遍生理机制，不限于既往患者。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这段是在解释冬季风险，不是夏季死亡。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention opposite seasons in the two hemispheres at the end of the talk?

options.text: To show that summer dangers are actually worse than winter ones
To explain why Death never really gets a worldwide off-season
To argue that climate differences make seasonal data unreliable
To introduce a comparison between northern and southern driving habits

evidence.quote: because the hemispheres of the world experience seasons at opposite times, when it's Summer in one side of the world, it's Winter in the other.

private.explanationZh: 结尾提到南北半球季节相反，是为了把前面“冬天更危险”的讨论扩展到全球尺度，说明死神并没有真正的淡季。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲者并没有把夏季危险说得比冬季更严重。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这是结尾句在结构上的作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里不是在质疑数据可靠性，而是在补充全球视角。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。结尾没有转向驾驶习惯比较。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-224-q01: 讲座主要说明冬季死亡人数显著上升，而且增加的大头不仅是流感和肺炎，更包括寒冷导致的心脏病和中风。C最完整。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-224-q02: 原文明确说这些病原体在更冷、更干燥的天气中存活更好，而且人们挤在室内时更容易在人与人之间传播。D直接对应。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-224-q03: “great! Kind of.”的意思是血管收缩一方面有助于保温，另一方面也让血小板更容易形成危险血栓，增加心梗和中风风险。A准确表达这种两面性。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-224-q04: 结尾提到南北半球季节相反，是为了说明即使某一半球到了夏天，另一半球仍是冬天，所以从全球看Death并没有真正的淡季。B最符合组织作用。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly about regarding long-distance underwater sound?

options.text: Why underwater microphones near Bermuda often fail to record whale sounds
How pressure alone controls all sound movement in the ocean
Why scientists in 1960 used explosives to map the entire seafloor
How the SOFAR channel allows sound to travel extremely far underwater and why that matters

evidence.quote: These sounds traveled halfway around the world thanks to an underwater sound superhighway.

private.explanationZh: 全文围绕“海底声音高速公路”展开：先解释声音为何能在 SOFAR 通道中传播得极远，再说明它对鲸、监测系统和海洋研究的意义。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲者不是在讨论设备故障。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中说温度是主要因素，压力只是次要因素之一。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。爆炸实验只是引入现象，不是全文重点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它同时概括了机制与用途两个核心部分。

public.prompt: What happens when sound waves enter the ocean layer where sound travels most slowly at the right angle?

options.text: They can become trapped in that layer and keep bending up and down within it
They immediately speed upward and escape into the air
They scatter off the surface and disappear within a few meters
They stop moving because cold water blocks all vibration

evidence.quote: if they enter a layer of water where sound travels particularly slowly, at just the right angle - or if they start out there in the first place - they can get stuck in that slow layer, bending up and down and up and down.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查关键机制。原文明确说，声波如果以合适角度进入最慢层，就可能被“困”在里面，上下反复弯折传播。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这几乎是对原句的准确转述。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文说的是被困在层内，不是立刻逃到空气中。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。恰恰相反，这种情况下它们不容易散失。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。冷水不会让振动停止，文中讨论的是传播路径改变。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker say, "If you've ever skied, you may get why"?

options.text: To compare the ocean directly with snowy mountain weather patterns
To introduce an analogy that makes the bending of sound toward slower layers easier to understand
To suggest that skiers are more likely than other people to hear whales
To show that skiing was historically used to discover the SOFAR channel

evidence.quote: If you've ever skied, you may get why;

private.explanationZh: 讲者在这里不是突然换话题，而是用滑雪进入慢雪区再被转回去的体验，帮助听者理解声波为何会朝传播更慢的区域弯折。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。类比对象是运动方向变化，不是天气系统。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确说明了这个滑雪例子的修辞功能。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有说滑雪者更容易听见鲸。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。滑雪只是解释工具，不是历史发现方法。

public.prompt: Why can scientists use changes in sound speed in the SOFAR channel to learn about the ocean?

options.text: Because only nuclear tests can alter the speed of sound underwater
Because whales change the ocean's pressure whenever they call
Because the sound speed there reflects temperature changes that indicate ocean conditions
Because microphones in the channel can directly measure the ocean floor's chemical composition

evidence.quote: changes in the speed of sound in the SOFAR channel can help us track changes in the temperature of the ocean - a critical measure of our planet's health.

private.explanationZh: 这里需要把用途和原理联系起来：既然声速会随海水条件变化，尤其与温度变化相关，那么监测速速变化就能反映海洋状态。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。核试验只是能被监测到的声音来源之一，不是改变声速的唯一因素。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有把鲸叫声与海洋压力变化直接联系。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据句明确指出可借此追踪海洋温度变化。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。水听器记录声音，不会直接测量海底化学成分。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-225-q01: 整段讲的是SOFAR channel如何让声音在海水中传播得极远，以及这对鲸类通信、核试验监测和海洋研究为什么重要。D最准确概括主旨。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-225-q02: 原文说如果声波以合适角度进入那个声速最慢的水层，或一开始就在其中，它们会被困在里面，上下弯折传播。A与原文一致。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-225-q03: 讲者提到滑雪，是为了用滑雪板进入慢雪区会被转向的类比，帮助听者理解声波为什么会朝声速更慢的水层弯折。B正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-225-q04: 原文最后说SOFAR channel中的声速变化能帮助追踪海洋温度变化，而海温是重要的海洋状况指标。因此C是唯一合理推论。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the collaboration described in the talk?

options.text: To show how a scientific message can become distorted as it passes from one retelling to another
To prove that social media users understand research better than journalists do
To compare drawing skills across a group of educational YouTube channels
To argue that games are a better way to teach science than formal papers are

evidence.quote: Popular media stories about science often don't match up with the actual research they're based on, because the information gets shaped along the way.

private.explanationZh: 整段合作的核心，是通过一场“传话/画图”实验展示科学信息在传播链条中如何逐步变形，最后与最初研究相差很远。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了整场活动的主题和隐喻。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。视频批评的是传播过程中的变形，不是简单夸某一群体更懂科学。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。画图只是媒介，不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有主张游戏整体优于论文教学。

public.prompt: How does the speaker organize the explanation of how a claim becomes misleading?

options.text: By contrasting two unrelated studies and then dismissing both of them
By tracing a step-by-step chain from research findings to broader and more exaggerated retellings
By listing several experiments before revealing that none of them actually happened
By focusing on one scientist's motives and then shifting to a debate about funding

evidence.quote: A scientific journal might only accept a paper about the one part of the research that shows a positive correlation. A not-so-science literate journalist might not realize the effect was only found in non-human subjects. And a social media manager might confound correlation and causation while he makes as clicky a post as possible.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查结构。讲者按传播链逐步推进：期刊、记者、社交媒体管理者，每一步都把信息再加工一点，最后导致误导性结论。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里不是在对比两项互不相干的研究。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确描述了“层层转述、层层失真”的组织方式。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲者没有先列多个实验再统一否定。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。重点不在某位科学家的动机或经费争论。

public.prompt: Why are the participants' reactions included near the end of the talk?

options.text: To identify which participant made the first factual mistake
To provide new scientific evidence about muscle growth
To emphasize how dramatically the original message changed by the final round
To show that the players were mainly competing to win the game

evidence.quote: Pretty much everything about the original script changed by the time Joe was done!

private.explanationZh: 结尾加入参与者回看和吐槽，不是单纯搞笑，而是强化实验结果：原始脚本经过多轮转述后，几乎面目全非。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。反应片段没有系统追责谁先出错。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里不是新增科学证据，而是在回顾传播失真。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它点明这些反应在结构上的总结作用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。活动重在展示误传，不是比赛输赢。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about why inaccurate science headlines spread so easily?

options.text: People deliberately invent false research because journals refuse to publish clear writing
Most readers ignore any claim that does not involve animals or alcohol
Scientists usually intend for social media posts to replace their original studies
Each retelling rewards simplification and exaggeration, so the message becomes more appealing but less accurate

evidence.quote: By the time science makes it to the masses, its takeaway could be way different from the research it's purporting to cover.

Now replace those graph cats with humans. Did I say humans? I meant giant biceps! And we see the same upward trend, a great example of fake news. And that always makes for a catchy title. [Tom]: While there was plenty of anecdotal evidence

private.explanationZh: 根据这句总结以及中段把图表硬改成“巨型二头肌”“吸睛标题”的例子，可以推断：信息在传播中不断被简化、夸张、做成更抓眼球的说法，因此更容易扩散，却也更容易失真。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中说的是传播链条中的重塑，不是有人故意凭空捏造全部研究。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。动物和啤酒只是示例素材，不是传播规律本身。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。没有证据表明科学家希望社交媒体替代原研究。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它把“更吸引人”和“更不准确”两面都概括出来了。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-226-q01: 这次合作通过类似传话游戏的过程展示：科学信息在一次次转述中会逐渐走样、失真。A最能概括整个活动的主旨。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-226-q02: 讲者按链条顺序说明信息如何变化：研究结果到面向大众的报道，再到社交媒体上的夸张说法，属于一步步追踪失真的过程。B正确。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-226-q03: 结尾放入参与者的反应，是为了让听者更明显看到原始信息到最后版本已经发生了多么大的变化。C最符合修辞目的。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-226-q04: 从讲座举的例子可推知，每一轮转述都更偏向简化、吸睛和夸张，因此信息虽然更容易传播，却也离原始研究更远。D最符合全文推论。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker introduce balsa as a "hardwood" at the beginning of the talk?

options.text: To show that the terms hardwood and softwood do not reliably describe how hard the wood feels.
To argue that balsa is genetically closer to pine than to oak.
To show that lightweight wood is mainly used for toys rather than furniture.
To suggest that model-airplane makers prefer woods from flowering trees.

evidence.quote: But guess what other tree is a "hardwood"? Balsa - that lightweight, some might even say soft wood that people use for making model airplanes. So, "hardwood" doesn't necessarily mean that the wood is hard.

private.explanationZh: 说话者一开始举出轻而软的轻木，却把它叫作 hardwood，是为了立刻打破听众对词面意思的直觉。这个例子服务于全文核心：hardwood 和 softwood 这些名称并不总是直接对应木材摸起来到底硬不硬。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。轻木这个反例正好说明 hardwood 这个标签并不稳定地表示“硬度”，而是引出后文对命名来源的解释。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说轻木在遗传上更接近松树，反而后文说明 hardwood 与 angiosperm、softwood 与 gymnosperm 通常对应。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。model airplanes 只是轻木用途的例子，不是说话者举这个例子的主要目的。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。是否偏好开花树木不是重点；这里要说明的是名称会误导人对物理硬度的判断。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what mainly causes hardness differences among softwoods?

options.text: Differences in how efficiently animals spread their seeds.
Differences in growth speed, which affect cell-wall thickness and density.
Differences in whether their seeds are encased in fruit.
Differences in how often they are cut for timber and fuel.

evidence.quote: The differences in hardness that do exist among softwoods come mainly from the speed at which different species grow and mature: fast growth leads to thinner cell walls and less-dense, softer wood.

private.explanationZh: 原文直接解释了 softwoods 之间仍然存在硬度差异，但主要来源不是分类名称，而是不同树种生长和成熟速度不同。生长快会形成更薄的细胞壁和更低密度的木材，因此更软。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。动物传播种子是后面谈 hardwoods 多样化时提到的，不是 softwoods 硬度差异的主要原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。说话者明确把硬度差异归因于生长速度，并进一步解释到细胞壁厚度和密度。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。种子是否包裹在果实中是分类特征，不是这里解释硬度差异的机制。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。中世纪伐木历史解释的是名称来源，不是不同 softwood 物种硬度为何不同。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about hardwoods that co-evolved with animal pollinators?

options.text: They all grow more slowly than any softwood species.
They became easier for medieval loggers to cut.
They were able to diversify across many environments and develop a wider range of wood structures.
They stopped relying on genetic traits for classification.

evidence.quote: Hardwoods, on the other hand, have co-evolved with the animals that pollinate their flowers and distribute their seeds. This has allowed hardwoods to spread rapidly in a variety of environments, leading to tree-mendous species diversity, including in the variety of their cellular structure.

private.explanationZh: 这里需要把两层意思连起来：动物传粉和传播种子，让 hardwoods 能更快进入多种环境；环境扩展又带来了物种与细胞结构的更大多样性。所以可推断它们的木材结构范围也更宽。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说所有 hardwood 都比所有 softwood 长得更慢，生长速度不是这里要推出的结论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。伐木工是否更容易砍伐是历史命名背景，和动物传粉导致的生态扩张无关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文明确说这种共同进化让 hardwoods 快速扩散到多样环境，并带来细胞结构多样性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。分类仍然依赖遗传相关特征，文中没有说 hardwoods 不再依赖这些特征。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the continued use of the terms "hardwood" and "softwood"?

options.text: Scientists still use them because they perfectly match the Janka hardness scale.
People keep them mainly because no tree species has both flowers and hard wood.
Loggers still need them because modern taxonomy cannot classify trees.
The labels remain common largely out of habit, even though they can be misleading.

evidence.quote: Knowing this, you'd think we wood have retired the softwood/hardwood terminology by now, but some habits die hard.

private.explanationZh: 结尾这句话不是在补充新科学事实，而是在总结一个社会语言层面的现象：尽管前面已经说明这些词并不总准确，人们还是沿用它们，主要因为习惯很难改变。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。前文正是在说明这些术语并不总和实际硬度完全对应，所以不是“完美匹配”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这个说法既不是文中论点，也没有解释为什么术语仍被保留。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。现代 taxonomy 明明可以分类；问题不是无法分类，而是旧名称继续流行。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。some habits die hard 明确暗示保留这些标签主要是出于惯性与习惯，而不是因为它们最精确。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-227-q01: 开头先举轻而软的轻木却属于hardwood，目的是说明hardwood和softwood这两个名称并不可靠地对应木头实际硬不硬。其他选项要么说成基因关系，要么转到用途或制作者偏好，原文都没有支持。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-227-q02: 原文明确说软木之间硬度差异主要来自生长和成熟速度，不同速度会导致细胞壁厚薄和木材密度不同，所以选b。其余选项分别说到动物传播、种子是否包裹、砍伐频率，都不是这里解释软木硬度差异的主要原因。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-227-q03: 文中说硬木与传粉和传播种子的动物共同进化，因此能迅速扩散到多种环境，进而形成巨大的物种多样性和细胞结构多样性，所以应选c。其余选项把结论说成所有硬木都更慢、生物分类停止依赖遗传，或回到中世纪伐木，都不符合原文。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-227-q04: 结尾说按理早该淘汰softwood/hardwood这种说法，但“some habits die hard”，可推知这些标签继续流行主要是习惯使然，尽管会误导，所以选d。其余选项把原因说成完美符合Janka尺度、没有树既开花又硬、或现代分类学无效，都没有依据。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention pregnancy tests at the start of the talk?

options.text: To introduce a familiar example of a cheap strip test whose basic design can be adapted to detect many other targets.
To argue that home pregnancy tests are more accurate than laboratory medical tests.
To claim that COVID-19 can already be diagnosed by using an ordinary store-bought pregnancy test.
To explain that the first disease tests were invented for monitoring pregnancy.

evidence.quote: Luckily, that test already exists at your local drug store: it's called an early pregnancy test.

private.explanationZh: 开头先提 pregnancy test，是一种引人注意的切入方式。说话者不是在真正讨论怀孕检测本身，而是借一个人人熟悉、便宜、易用的试纸格式，引出 lateral flow assay 可以迁移到很多别的检测任务上。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。后文马上说明同类试纸还能测病原体、过敏原和污染物，说明这里的作用是用熟悉例子引出通用检测原理。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有比较家用验孕棒和实验室测试谁更准确。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。说话者明确说不能直接拿普通验孕棒测 plague 或 COVID，只是“同类型”的测试可以被改造。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话没有回顾疾病检测的发明史，重点是当前这种测试形式的可迁移性。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why do these tests not require fancy pipettes or very steady hands?

options.text: The testing pad automatically neutralizes extra fluid before analysis begins.
The antibodies measure the sample before it enters the paper strip.
Capillary action pulls water-based liquid through the paper in a predictable way.
The color-change line works only when the sample volume is extremely small.

evidence.quote: if you have a small enough tube, you can draw any water-based liquid through it in a predictable way; you can pee a lot or a little and at pretty much any orientation - or bleed or spit or dissolve chocolate - onto a testing pad, and the force of surface tension will slowly and predictably drag the fluid along microscopic tubes within the testing paper.

private.explanationZh: 原文把关键原因说得很直接：capillary action 会让水基液体沿着试纸内部的微小通道稳定前进，所以不需要用精密移液器人工控制体积或方向，液体自己就能以可预测方式流动。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说 testing pad 会先“中和”多余液体。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。抗体负责识别蛋白，不负责让液体在进入试纸前被测量。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。predictable way 和 slowly and predictably drag the fluid 明确说明原因就是毛细作用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中恰恰说可以很多或很少、不同方向地加样，不是只能极少量。

public.prompt: What point does the speaker make about choosing antibodies for a COVID-19 test?

options.text: The best antibodies are the ones that bind to as many viruses as possible.
Test designers have to balance the risk of false positives against the risk of false negatives.
Dyed antibodies are less reliable than undyed antibodies in most medical settings.
Antibodies are useful only for blood tests, not for saliva tests.

evidence.quote:
You don't want to use one that is not specific enough, as it may lock on to other things besides the virus (causing false positives).

But you also don't want to use one that is too specific, as it might miss actual COVID infections (causing false negatives).

private.explanationZh: 这里不是在背一个定义，而是要理解设计取舍。抗体不够特异会误报，太特异又会漏报，因此合适的测试必须在这两种错误之间找到平衡。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。bind 得越广并不越好，太不特异反而会造成 false positives。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两句连续对比正是在说明设计者同时面对误报和漏报两类风险。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有把有色抗体和无色抗体做可靠性高低比较。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。前面明确说科学家在开发 saliva test for COVID-19 virus，所以抗体并不限于血液检测。

public.prompt: How does the talk mainly develop its explanation?

options.text: It compares several laboratory machines and then ranks them by cost.
It describes a failed COVID-19 test and then explains why people rejected it.
It traces the history of pregnancy testing from early medicine to modern microfluidics.
It moves from a familiar consumer test to the underlying mechanisms and then to current COVID-19 applications.

evidence.quote: But the same type of test - that is, a sample pad and a paper stick that changes colors to show a positive or negative result - can check people's spit for pathogens
And the way these tests analyze those liquids is by taking advantage of a second basic concept - antibodies - which immune systems produce to attach to particular proteins.

Professor Amy Shen and her team - including grad students Shivani Sathish, Ainash Garifullina, and postdoc Riccardo Funari - are currently developing a blood test for COVID-19 antibodies that uses microfluidics to combine the speed of the lateral flow assay with the quantitative nature of more complex optical assays

private.explanationZh: 整段结构很清楚：先从大家熟悉的试纸外形切入，再解释毛细作用和抗体识别这两个工作机制，最后落到 COVID-19 的现实应用与改进方向。因此最准确的是“熟悉例子→原理→应用”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲话没有比较多台实验室机器，更没有按成本排名。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里不是讲某个失败案例，而是讲这种检测形式如何工作并被继续开发。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有系统梳理 pregnancy testing 的历史演变。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。三段证据分别对应 familiar example、mechanism 和 COVID application，完整反映了篇章推进方式。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-228-q01: 开头提妊娠试纸，是为了用大家熟悉的廉价纸条检测作为切入，说明同类lateral flow test还能改造成检测病原体、污染物、过敏原等多种目标，所以选a。原文明确否认普通验孕棒已经能测COVID，也没有说它比实验室检测更准或疾病检测起源于怀孕检测。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-228-q02: 文中说第一条基本原理是毛细作用，水基液体会以可预测的方式沿测试纸中的微小通道移动，因此不需要精密移液器或特别稳的手，所以选c。其他选项把原因归于垫片中和多余液体、抗体先测量样本、或必须极小体积，都不是原文说法。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-228-q03: 原文说抗体若不够特异会导致假阳性，若过于特异又可能漏掉真实感染造成假阴性，所以设计者需要在这两类风险之间取得平衡，选b最准确。其余选项都把意思歪曲成越广越好、染色抗体较差、或抗体只适用于血液检测。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-228-q04: 整段结构先从熟悉的验孕试纸切入，再解释两大机制：毛细作用和抗体，最后回到COVID检测及相关应用，因此最佳概括是d。其他选项说比较实验机器、介绍失败案例、或追溯验孕史，都与实际组织方式不符。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the talk?

options.text: What thunder is and why it can sound different from one situation to another.
Why storms usually produce more lightning in open fields than over forests.
How ancient myths about storms led scientists to discover electricity.
Why cloud-to-ground lightning is always louder than lightning inside clouds.

evidence.quote: It wasn't until we got a handle on the science of both storms and sounds that we figured out what thunder is and why it sounds the way - well, the many ways - it does.

private.explanationZh: 这句几乎直接点明全文主旨：谈话要解释 thunder 本质上是什么，以及为什么它会呈现 many ways 的不同声音。其他选项都只抓住了局部内容或文中根本没有主讲的内容。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文明确说要弄清 what thunder is and why it sounds the many ways it does，这就是全篇主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话没有讨论开阔地和森林上空哪种地方更常有 lightning。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。神话只在开头作为历史背景一带而过，不是文章重点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中并没有绝对地说 cloud-to-ground lightning “总是”更响，这个选项范围过窄也过绝对。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why can distant thunder sound more like a boom or rumble than a sharp clap?

options.text: The air around distant lightning is heated more slowly than the air near the ground.
The shockwave loses energy, and higher frequencies fade faster than lower ones.
Nearby trees amplify only the lowest frequencies in every storm.
Distant lightning bolts produce less electrical current than nearby ones.

evidence.quote: As a shockwave travels through the air, it loses energy and that intense burst of pressure smooths out; what's more, the wave's higher frequencies dissipate more quickly than its low frequencies.

private.explanationZh: 题目问的是远处 thunder 为什么不再像 sharp clap。原文给出两个直接原因：冲击波传播时会损失能量、压力峰会被抹平，而且高频比低频更快消散，所以听起来更低、更拖长。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里没有说远处空气升温更慢，重点在传播过程中波形和频率成分的变化。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两层机制都在原句中出现：能量损失与高频更快衰减。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。树木只是在后面解释回声和重叠时被提到，不是每次都“只放大低频”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话没有把远近声音差异归因于 lightning 本身电流大小不同。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention a supersonic jet, a whip, a balloon, and static electricity?

options.text: To show that these sounds are often mistaken for thunder during storms.
To argue that thunder has fewer frequencies than other sudden noises.
To illustrate that thunder is one example of a shockwave, and shockwaves share similar sound qualities.
To explain why human ears respond more strongly to artificial sounds than natural ones.

evidence.quote: Those same qualities are actually common to any abrupt and intense burst of pressure, from the bang of a supersonic jet to the crack of a whip to the pop of a balloon to the snap of a tiny bolt of static electricity; that's why all shockwaves sound surprisingly similar.

private.explanationZh: 列举 jet、whip、balloon 和 static electricity 不是为了扩展话题，而是为了把 thunder 放进“shockwave”这个更一般的类别中。通过类比，听众更容易理解 thunderclap 为什么具有那种突然而宽频的声音特征。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说这些声音常被误听成 thunder。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文恰恰说 thunderclap contains an incredible range of frequencies，不是更少。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。例子共同服务于一句总结：all shockwaves sound surprisingly similar。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话没有讨论人耳对人工声和自然声的偏好差异。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the sounds a listener may hear during a single storm?

options.text: They usually come from one part of one lightning bolt.
They are limited to either claps or rolls, but not both.
They vary because pressure waves from different parts of different bolts reach the listener at different times.
They depend mainly on myths people associate with storms.

evidence.quote: Within a single storm, lots of waves of pressure from lots of different parts of lots of different lightning bolts intermingle and reach your ears at different times, so you'll often hear a symphony of claps, booms, rumbles, rolls, and more.
It's a perfect storm of sound that launched a thousand origin - and Thor-igin - stories.

private.explanationZh: 这里要把结尾的机制和结果连起来理解。单场风暴中，不同闪电、不同部位产生的压力波会以不同时间到达耳朵，因此听到的不是单一一种 thunder，而是 clap、boom、rumble、roll 等多种声音混在一起。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文明确说是来自 lots of different parts of lots of different lightning bolts，而不是单一来源。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。结尾列出的正是多种声音并存，不是二选一。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个选项准确概括了原因和结果：不同来源的压力波分时到达，于是形成丰富多变的声音组合。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。神话只在开头作背景，结尾这里解释的是物理机制，不是文化联想。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-229-q01: 开头就说要解释thunder是什么，以及为什么它会以多种方式发声，后文也一直围绕这两点展开，所以主旨是a。其余选项都只抓住局部或加入原文没有的绝对化说法。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-229-q02: 原文明确说冲击波在空气中传播时会损失能量，而且高频比低频衰减更快，因此离得越远，雷声越不像尖锐clap，而更像boom或rumble，所以选b。其他选项提到加热更慢、树木总是只放大低频、或远处闪电电流更小，文中都没这样说。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-229-q03: 提到超音速飞机、鞭子、气球和静电，是为了说明这些都是突然强烈压力爆发形成的冲击波，因此它们和雷鸣有相似的声音特征，故选c。并不是说这些声音常被误认成雷，也不是在比较频率多少或人耳对人工声更敏感。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-229-q04: 文中后面明确说在一次风暴中，来自许多不同闪电、不同部位的压力波会以不同时间到达耳朵，所以听众会听到各种claps、booms、rumbles和rolls，可推知答案是c。其余选项不是过度缩小来源范围，就是错误地说只能听到一种类型，或把原因归于神话。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker use the example of surveying hedgehogs?

options.text: To show how roadkill can provide data more easily and cheaply than trying to study elusive live animals directly.
To prove that hedgehogs are the most useful species for climate research.
To argue that road surveys are more humane than trapping animals.
To explain why scientists prefer night work to laboratory work.

evidence.quote:
Like, say you wanted to survey hedgehogs. Normally, you'd have to tromp through well, hedges, night after night, maybe dragging along expensive equipment, just to find the elusive critters.

And to gather information about individual hedgehogs, you'd have to use fancy traps, and get special permits. Or you could just find them flattened on the pavement, document where you found them, gather the specimens, and study them to your heart's content.

private.explanationZh: 刺猬例子把 roadkill 的研究价值讲得非常具体：活体调查既费钱又费力，还要设备和许可；而路杀个体反而成了现成样本。这个对比是为了说明 roadkill 为科学家提供了意外而高效的数据来源。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。整个例子都在比较直接追踪活体与利用 roadkill 样本的成本、难度和便利性。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有说刺猬是“最有用”的物种，它们只是一个便于理解的案例。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话没有把重点放在 humane 与否的伦理比较上。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。夜间工作只是说明寻找刺猬很辛苦，不是要讨论科学家工作偏好。

public.prompt: According to the talk, what did the lack of roadkilled small mammals in the Everglades suggest?

options.text: Traffic patterns had shifted away from the swamp.
Invasive pythons had already reduced many of those animals before they could reach the road.
Local raccoons and opossums had learned to avoid cars.
Climate change had delayed the mammals' breeding season.

evidence.quote: In the Everglades, a sudden lack of wrecked raccoons, squashed opossums, and battered bunnies indicated that something was amiss - and that something turned out to be invasive pythons nomming all the inhabitants of the swamp before they could even get to the road.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查对具体案例的把握。Everglades 里不是因为公路更安全，而是因为很多小型哺乳动物在上路之前就被 invasive pythons 吃掉了，所以 roadkill 数量突然下降。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说交通路线变化导致样本变少。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。句子直接指出真正原因是 invasive pythons 在动物到达公路前就把它们吃掉了。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。没有证据表明这些哺乳动物学会了避开汽车。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这个 Everglades 例子不是关于气候变化，而是关于入侵蟒蛇。

public.prompt: What does the talk suggest about differences in how often species are hit by cars?

options.text: Such differences make roadkill useless for any serious scientific research.
Scientists should study only species that freeze when cars approach.
The differences can be managed if researchers recognize the bias and adjust their analysis accordingly.
Fast-moving species are always overrepresented in roadkill data.

evidence.quote: But as long as scientists understand this variation, they can account for it in their analysis.

private.explanationZh: 这里的推断点在于“有偏差”不等于“不能研究”。说话者承认不同物种被撞的概率不同，但紧接着说明只要研究者理解这种 variation，就能在分析中校正它。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文并没有否定 roadkill 研究，反而明确说偏差是可以被 account for 的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。armadillos 只是一个容易被撞的例子，不代表科学家只该研究这类物种。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个选项准确概括了原句的逻辑：认识偏差，然后在分析中处理偏差。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有得出所有 fast-moving species 都一定过度代表的绝对结论。

public.prompt: Which description best matches the way the talk is organized?

options.text: It argues that cars should replace field equipment in most wildlife studies.
It lists the deadliest roads in different ecosystems before discussing archaeology.
It contrasts insects with mammals and then explains why birds are hardest to study.
It starts by presenting roadkill as an unpleasant problem, then shows several research uses and ends by placing that work in a broader scientific tradition.

evidence.quote: All this death, though, has an unexpected upside: the car-casses are actually a bone-anza for scientists.
OK, so this is all a little morbid and a lot depressing, but roadkill ecologists are simply following in the footsteps of other opportunistic researchers
They're simply part of the grand tradition of scientists who turn car-nage into knowledge.

private.explanationZh: 篇章推进很明显：先承认 roadkill 很惨，但马上提出“unexpected upside”；中间给出一串研究实例；最后又把 roadkill ecology 放进更广义的 opportunistic research 传统中。这正是选项 d 所概括的结构。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲话不是建议用汽车取代常规野外设备，而是说明副产品可以被利用。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有罗列“最危险公路”，考古学也只是结尾类比中的一部分。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文章并不是按 insect、mammal、bird 的分类结构展开。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个选项同时概括了开头的问题设定、中段的应用例证和结尾的学术定位。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-230-q01: 刺猬的例子用来对比两种研究方式：直接追踪活体既费力又贵，还要许可；而路杀样本更容易获得位置和个体信息，所以它说明roadkill能让研究更省力省钱，选a。原文没有说刺猬最适合气候研究、路面调查更人道，或科学家偏爱夜班。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-230-q02: 文中说大沼泽地里被撞死的浣熊、负鼠和兔子突然减少，说明出了问题，而问题就是入侵蟒蛇在这些动物到达道路前就把它们吃掉了，所以选b。其他选项关于交通转移、动物学会躲车、或繁殖季延后，都不是原文解释。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-230-q03: 讲者先承认不同动物被撞频率不同，随后马上说只要科学家理解这种差异，就能在分析中加以校正，因此正确推断是c。a把这种偏差说成让研究完全无用，b和d都把局部例子误当成普遍研究原则。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-230-q04: 整段先把roadkill呈现为令人不舒服的现象，再举多种科研用途，最后说这些生态学家只是延续其他机会型研究者的传统，因此组织方式最符合d。其余选项不是夸大成让汽车替代设备，就是加入并不存在的道路排行、昆虫对比等内容。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare equal masses of TNT and a marshmallow at the start?

options.text: To show that total stored chemical energy and the rate of energy release are different issues.
To argue that marshmallows are a practical substitute for industrial explosives.
To show that TNT becomes harmless when divided into very small pieces.
To prove that foods always contain more energy than manufactured chemicals.

evidence.quote: This itty-bitty-bit of TNT - seven grams' worth - contains enough chemical energy that, when detonated, it could lift a bowling ball about six hundred meters into the air. And this is seven grams of sugary deliciousness, better known as a marshmallow. Its chemical energy could lift that same ball nearly two thousand meters.

private.explanationZh: 开头这个夸张对比不是要说 marshmallow 比 TNT 更危险，而是先制造认知冲突：总能量更大，不等于破坏性更强。这样后文才能解释真正关键在于能量释放速度，而不只是总量。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个比较的修辞作用就是把“总能量”和“释放速率”区分开，为后文核心解释铺垫。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话完全没有建议用 marshmallow 替代炸药。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。题干比较的是相同质量的两种物质，不是 TNT 颗粒变小会不会无害。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文中没有得出所有食物都比人造化学品含能更高的普遍结论。

public.prompt: According to the talk, why doesn't a roasting marshmallow release its energy explosively?

options.text: Its sugar molecules cannot rearrange once heat is added.
The reaction depends on oxygen at the surface, so the energy is released gradually as it burns.
Its water content immediately absorbs all of the heat from the flame.
The sugar turns into TNT only after the marshmallow has fully blackened.

evidence.quote:
But the marshmallow is missing an ingredient; it needs to combine with oxygen to form carbon dioxide and water - in other words, to burn.

Once you add some energy to get things started, only atoms on the marshmallow's surface - where its sugar meets oxygen from the air - will be able to rearrange themselves.

private.explanationZh: 说话者解释得很清楚：marshmallow 不像 TNT 那样内部已经具备全部反应条件，它还需要外部氧气，而且只有表面接触氧气的部分先反应，所以能量是逐步释放出来的，不会一下子全部爆开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文不是说糖分子不能 rearrange，而是说它们必须先与氧气接触才能 rearrange。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。缺少氧气、只能表面燃烧，这两点正是能量缓慢释放的关键。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。文中没有把原因归结为含水量吸热。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话没有说糖会变成 TNT，这显然误解了类比关系。

public.prompt: Under what conditions, according to the talk, could sugar release energy much more like TNT?

options.text: If it is packed into a larger solid block before being heated.
If it is mixed with extra water so the reaction can spread evenly.
If it is finely ground, exposed to enough oxygen surface area, and given enough energy to start reacting.
If it is cooled first so the molecules can store more energy.

evidence.quote: if sugar is ground so finely that it has enough surface area where it can interact with oxygen and if there's enough energy around to initiate a reaction, it too, can release its energy all at once

private.explanationZh: 这道题要求把条件整合出来：细磨会增加表面积，使更多糖同时接触氧气；同时还需要足够能量点燃反应。满足这些条件时，糖也可能像 TNT 一样几乎同时释放大量能量。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。做成更大的实心块只会减少单位质量暴露表面积，不符合原文机制。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文强调的是与氧气接触的表面积，不是加水。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个选项完整复述了原文给出的三个关键条件：细粉、充足表面积接触氧气、足够启动能量。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。冷却并不是文中提到的决定因素。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare rapid energy release to a flash flood near the end of the talk?

options.text: To show that water can never be as destructive as heat.
To argue that explosive materials are mainly dangerous outdoors.
To explain why gentle rain usually contains less total water than a downpour.
To clarify that destruction occurs when energy is released faster than the surroundings can absorb it.

evidence.quote: When it's released faster than that limit, whatever is left over has to go somewhere; extra energy ends up as destructive waves of pressure and heat, just like extra water becomes a destructive flash flood.

private.explanationZh: flash flood 的类比是为了把抽象的“释放过快”变得直观：不是总量本身，而是周围环境来不及吸收时，剩余能量才会转化成破坏性的压力和热。这个比喻帮助听众理解为什么速率决定破坏性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。类比不是比较水和热谁更危险，而是比较“超过吸收极限”这一共同机制。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文中没有把危险性限定在户外环境。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。前面的雨量类比服务于总量与速率的区别，这里 flash flood 更进一步解释破坏性的成因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个选项准确抓住了比喻真正要说明的点：释放速度超过环境吸收能力时，就会造成破坏。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-231-q01: 开头比较等质量TNT和棉花糖，是为了制造反差并引出核心区分：总化学能大小与能量释放速度不是一回事。棉花糖总能量更高，但释放很慢，所以选a。其余选项把它说成棉花糖可代替炸药、TNT切小就无害、或食物总比人工化学品能量高，均不符合原文。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-231-q02: 原文明确说棉花糖燃烧需要和空气中的氧结合，而且只有表面接触氧的糖原子先反应，因此能量会随着燃烧逐渐释放，而不是瞬间爆炸，故选b。其他选项不是原文原因，甚至与原文相反。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-231-q03: 讲者说如果糖被磨得很细，从而获得足够大的与氧接触表面积，并且周围有足够能量启动反应，它也能像TNT那样一下子释放能量，所以选c。其他选项说做成更大固体块、加水、先冷却，都没有文本支持。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-231-q04: 结尾把快速释放能量比作山洪，是为了说明真正造成破坏的关键在于释放速度超过环境吸收上限，多余能量便转化为破坏性的压力和热，所以选d。a、b、c都偏离了这个类比的解释功能。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the talk?

options.text: To compare four starter Pokemon with real animals and decide which one seems most biologically plausible
To explain why electric animals are the most successful hunters in nature
To describe how fictional creatures have changed over the history of video games
To show that no Pokemon ability has any parallel in the real animal world

evidence.quote: We started wondering whether these abilities were actually plausible; after all, there are some weird adaptations that exist in the animal kingdom.
That's why for "most likely to actually exist," squirtle, we choose you!

private.explanationZh: 这题考查整段讲话的主旨。开头提出核心问题：这些宝可梦能力在真实生物学中是否可能存在；结尾则给出比较后的结论，认为Squirtle最有可能真实存在。所以整段并不是单独讲某一种动物，而是把四个“starter”逐一和现实生物作对照，再作出最终判断。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了讲话的整体结构：提出“是否 plausible”的问题，逐个比较四只角色，最后选出最可能真实存在的对象。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。电击动物只是在讨论Pikachu时出现的一个局部例子，既不是全文重点，也没有说它们是最成功的猎手。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话没有讨论电子游戏历史，也没有追溯这些角色如何演变。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。说话者明确指出现实中确实存在一些相似适应，甚至最后认为Squirtle最可能真实存在，因此不是“完全没有对应”。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker say Squirtle has the best chance of existing in real life?

options.text: Because turtles can produce electricity more efficiently than fish can
Because real turtles already show behaviors related to expelling or spraying water
Because turtles often grow defensive plants on their backs
Because turtles can breathe fire by mixing chemicals in their mouths

evidence.quote: In fact, sea turtles can expel excess saltwater out of their mouths and nostrils
the diamondback terrapin is disturbed while drinking, it will shoot the water from its throat to lighten the load and make a faster getaway.

private.explanationZh: 说话者判断Squirtle最可信，不是因为现实里已经有完全一样的“喷水攻击”，而是因为乌龟类动物已经具有接近这一能力的基础行为：海龟会排出盐水，菱斑龟受惊时会把喉中的水喷出去。这说明“从口部或喉部喷出水”在生物学上比较容易想象。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。电击能力是讨论Pikachu时提到的，而且电击动物都是鱼，不是乌龟。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两处证据都说明现实乌龟已有与“喷水”相近的机制，因此Squirtle最合理。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。背上长植物的类比对应的是Bulbasaur，不是Squirtle。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。化学喷射和可能的“喷火”讨论对应的是Charmander，不是乌龟。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about why Pikachu is less realistic than an electric fish?

options.text: Its body would be too small to store enough water
Its electric ability would depend on having a plant growing on its back
Its land-based lifestyle makes electric shocks less practical in air
Its predators would quickly evolve immunity to electricity

evidence.quote: There are real-world animals that can generate electric shocks - in fact, this ability has independently evolved six times!
so hypothetically aquatic pikachus would be realistic, but pikachus are exclusively terrestrial, and air is a muuuuch worse conductor of electricity than water

private.explanationZh: 这题要综合两处信息推断。讲话先说现实里确实有能放电的动物，因此“放电”本身不是问题；接着又说明这些动物全是鱼，而且空气比水差得多的导电性使陆地版Pikachu不现实。可见问题不在于电能力本身，而在于陆生环境不利于这种适应。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲话没有说Pikachu的问题在于储水不足，这与Squirtle相关而非Pikachu。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。背上长植物是Bulbasaur的设定，与电击是否可行无关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据明确把“都是鱼”和“空气导电差”联系起来，说明陆生身份才是关键障碍。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话没有讨论捕食者是否会对电击产生免疫，因此这是无依据的扩展。

public.prompt: How is the talk mainly organized?

options.text: By tracing the history of Pokemon from their invention to the present
By grouping animal adaptations according to the continents where they evolved
By debating two opposing scientific theories about fictional creatures
By examining each starter one at a time and then choosing the most realistic one

evidence.quote: First up are bulbasaurs
OK, so then there's charmanders
Squirtles the turtles, on the other hand
But what of our final contender: pikachus
That's why for "most likely to actually exist," squirtle, we choose you!

private.explanationZh: 这题考查篇章结构。讲话不是按历史、地区或理论争论来展开，而是按四个候选角色逐个分析：先Bulbasaur，再Charmander，再Squirtle，最后Pikachu，然后在结尾给出胜者。多个顺序提示词和最后的结论共同支持这一结构判断。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲话没有讲Pokemon的创作史或发展史。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。虽然用了多种现实动物作类比，但并未按地理区域组织材料。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话不是在对立理论之间辩论，而是在做案例比较和可行性评估。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。四个顺序标记和最终“we choose you”明确显示出逐一比较后下结论的组织方式。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-232-q01: 转录先说明要检验四个宝可梦能力是否有真实生物学基础，结尾再选出“最可能真实存在”的 Squirtle，因此主旨是逐一比较并作出判断。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-232-q02: 文中明确说海龟能排出盐水，菱背龟受惊时还会把水从喉咙喷出，因此 Squirtle 最合理，因为现实乌龟已有相关喷水行为。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-232-q03: 转录指出会放电的动物全是鱼类，且空气比水导电差得多，所以陆生的 Pikachu 比电鱼不现实，关键在于陆地环境不利于放电。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-232-q04: 讲述顺序是按 Bulbasaur、Charmander、Squirtle、Pikachu 依次讨论，再在最后宣布 Squirtle 最可能存在，因此结构题应选逐个考察后作结论。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of this program?

options.text: To let several MinuteEarth creators present favorite videos and explain why they value them
To compare children’s educational products sold by different sponsors
To summarize only one scientific topic in greater detail than any earlier video
To train listeners to identify errors in published scientific studies

evidence.quote: three of our writer-directors will each share one of their favorite MinuteEarth videos and explain why it's at the top of their list

private.explanationZh: 开头已经直接说明节目目的：三位编导各自分享自己最喜欢的一支MinuteEarth视频，并解释原因。后文的几个片段确实按这个框架展开，所以主旨不是某单一科学主题，而是“分享精选视频并说明理由”的合集节目。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了节目开场设定，也符合后面轮流介绍 favorite picks 的展开方式。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。赞助内容只在开头或结尾出现，不是整期节目的核心任务。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目并非只深入讲一个主题，而是串联了多个不同视频片段。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有把重点放在训练听众找研究错误，而是在介绍喜欢这些视频的理由。

public.prompt: According to the first featured video, why can poor soils support high plant diversity?

options.text: Because poor soils always contain more phosphorus than rich soils do
Because poor soils keep fast-growing species from dominating all the resources
Because only tropical climates can produce any plant diversity at all
Because harsh weather immediately eliminates the strongest competitors

evidence.quote: The fastest-spreading species soak up most of the extra nutrients
So poor soils inhibit the greedy and allow everyone else to scrabble by.

private.explanationZh: 这题需要整合因果链。第一段说明在富营养土壤中，扩张最快的植物会抢走更多营养、水分和阳光；第二段总结说贫瘠土壤抑制了这些“greedy”快速生长者，因此别的物种也能共存。换句话说，贫瘠不是直接“滋养更多物种”，而是阻止少数强者垄断资源。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲话恰恰说这些热点地区的氮和尤其是磷供应很低，不是更高。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它把“快生长者抢占资源”和“poor soils inhibit the greedy”两处核心意思连起来了。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。热带雨林只是一个例子；讲话还提到其他高多样性生态系统，论点也不是“只有热带才有多样性”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话提到 harsh weather 常常对应“poor soils AND few plant species”，所以这不是产生高多样性的解释。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker discuss ferns when talking about microbreweries?

options.text: To show that breweries depend directly on forest plants for their ingredients
To argue that large companies should stop advertising to customers
To illustrate how smaller competitors survive by specializing in niches that dominant rivals do not exploit well
To prove that economic competition is less intense than biological competition

evidence.quote: That's how understory ferns and microbreweries can succeed - by specializing in conditions the "big guys" are not so good at: the so-called empty niches.
Similarly, microbrews, which invest in being odd, trendy, and strongly flavored, can persevere by attracting aficionados not swayed by the marketing and lower prices of larger breweries.

private.explanationZh: 蕨类植物并不是跑题的自然例子，而是用来类比小型酿酒厂的生存策略。讲话强调，无论是森林下层的蕨类还是货架上的microbrew，都不是靠正面硬拼“大个子”，而是靠占据大公司或大树不擅长利用的“空位生态位”。这个例子服务于类比说明。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲话没有说啤酒厂直接依赖森林植物原料，这不是举例目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讲话是在解释竞争机制，不是在给大公司提出广告政策建议。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确抓住了 fern 例子的修辞功能：帮助听众理解 niche specialization。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话恰恰指出自然和经济都受到类似竞争逻辑驱动，而不是说经济竞争更弱。

public.prompt: What is the speaker’s main point about why Kelvin’s estimate of Earth’s age was wrong?

options.text: He used too few mine temperatures and should have measured ocean water instead
He overestimated the heating effect of radioactivity in Earth’s core
He assumed the crust was cooler than the deep mantle for only a few centuries
He treated Earth like a solid object that only slowly diffuses heat, overlooking mantle convection

evidence.quote: Kelvin's error is usually attributed to the fact that he didn't know about radioactivity
Kelvin's real oversight was in thinking of the Earth like a baked potato - a solid lump through which heat slowly diffuses.
the warm rock rises in convection currents - over millions of years - spreading heat more evenly throughout the planet.

private.explanationZh: 这题要抓住讲话纠正“常见误解”的重点。说话者明确说，Kelvin的错误通常被归咎于他不知道放射性，但那并不是关键；真正的问题是他把地球当作像烤土豆那样主要靠热扩散的固体，而忽略了地幔会发生对流，从而把热更有效地带到地壳附近。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。讲话没有说问题在于测量点太少或应该去量海水。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。说话者指出“放射性”并不是主要解释，更没有说Kelvin高估了它。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讲话讨论的是热传输机制，不是把时间尺度误设为几百年这样的问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它同时包含了两个关键点：错误模型是“baked potato”，真正缺失的是 mantle convection。

reasonsZh.minute-earth-233-q01: 开头直接说三位创作者要各自分享自己最喜欢的 MinuteEarth 视频并解释原因，后文也依次这样展开，所以主旨是让几位创作者介绍并说明偏爱原因。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-233-q02: 第一段相关视频解释了贫瘠土壤无法让快速生长物种建立压倒性优势，从而不给它们独占水、光等资源，所以更多较慢物种能共存。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-233-q03: 讲 fern 是为了类比 microbrewery：都在强大竞争者阴影下，通过利用大者不擅长的“空生态位”而生存，因此是说明小竞争者靠 niche 生存。
reasonsZh.minute-earth-233-q04: 末段明确说 Kelvin 真正忽略的是把地球当成像烤土豆那样只靠缓慢热扩散的固体，没有考虑地幔对流把热量更均匀地带上来。

public.prompt: What is the talk mainly about?

options.text: How certain plants accumulate large amounts of nickel and what advantage that may give them
Why most mining companies now prefer plant-based fertilizers to metal tools
How nickel became the main nutrient controlling all plant growth
Why drought-prone plants always contain more metal than salt

evidence.quote: This shrub is super metal.
So plants that can take up toxic metals - and survive - become toxic themselves, without having to expend energy to concoct their own defensive toxins.

private.explanationZh: 全文的中心不是采矿产业或单一营养元素，而是解释一种“金属囤积植物”现象：这些植物为什么能吸收异常高浓度的镍、如何把金属运输和隔离起来，以及这种能力可能给它们带来什么生态好处。结尾进一步提出这种金属囤积可能充当防御。
