private.explanationZh: 节目开头就把核心问题说得很清楚：人成年以后，什么时候应该停止在经济上依赖父母。后面的意大利案例只是围绕这一总问题展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目涉及职业培训，但那是David案件中的细节，不是总议题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 美国离婚案例只是结尾问答，不是整段讨论中心。
optionRationalesZh.c: expenses只是法律判决的一部分，不是主题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。financially depend on your parents准确概括了节目主线。

public.prompt: What did the Italian court decide in David's case?

options.text: David should keep receiving money from his father, including help with extra expenses.
David had to repay the money he had already received from his father.
David's mother was solely responsible for supporting him from then on.
David could receive support only if he returned to his former job.

evidence.quote:
The court ruled that David, who still lives with his mother, should carry on receiving three-
hundred euros a month from his separated father, as well as half of any extra expenses he
incurred.

private.explanationZh: 判决内容非常具体：David继续每月领取300欧元，而且父亲还要负担他额外开销的一半，所以答案是继续提供经济支持。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文同时提到固定月付和额外费用分担。
optionRationalesZh.b: 判决方向相反，不是要求David归还。
optionRationalesZh.c: 法庭要求的是父亲继续支付，不是把责任全给母亲。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有把“回原工作”设为条件。

public.prompt: Why does the report mention that the ruling "has set a precedent for all future cases"?

options.text: To show that David's father can appeal the decision to a lower court.
To explain that hairdressing courses are now legally protected in Italy.
To indicate that the decision may influence how similar disputes are judged later.
To suggest that David's case copied an earlier American lawsuit exactly.

evidence.quote: which has set a precedent for all future cases

private.explanationZh: 这里提到precedent，不是补充花边信息，而是强调这次判决的法律意义超出David个人：未来遇到类似的成年子女赡养争议时，法院可能会参考这个案例。

optionRationalesZh.a: precedent说的是未来参考价值，不是上诉路径。
optionRationalesZh.b: 课程内容不是这句的重点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。for all future cases直接说明会影响类似后案。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有说它复制了某个美国案件。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Callum's comment about parents and children?

options.text: Parents usually stop giving advice once their children start working.
Parents tend to keep seeing their children as their children regardless of age.
Parents are most supportive when courts require them to be.
Parents understand financial independence better than emotional independence.

evidence.quote:
However old you are, you are still
your parents' child. Yes, absolutely.

private.explanationZh: Callum的意思不是谈法律责任，而是在谈父母视角下的亲子关系：无论子女多大，在父母眼里仍然是“自己的孩子”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他没有说开始工作后父母就停止建议。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这正是“However old you are”这句话的核心含义。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这句话和法院强制无关，谈的是普遍情感认知。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有比较财务独立和情感独立。

reasonsZh.bbc-20081029-adult-children-q01: 节目一开始就提出主题：成年人到了什么阶段还应不应该继续在经济上依赖父母。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081029-adult-children-q02: 法院裁定David继续每月从父亲那里获得钱款，并且父亲还要承担一半额外开销。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081029-adult-children-q03: precedent被解释为今后类似案件可以援引这次判决，因此这里是在说明该裁决会影响未来同类案件。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081029-adult-children-q04: Callum说无论你多大，仍然是父母的孩子，因此可推知父母往往不论孩子年龄多大都还是把他们当作自己的孩子。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the programme?

options.text: The history of Yiddish expressions in modern English.
Competing ideas about whether success comes from communication, diplomacy, or silence.
Famous quotations that were incorrectly attributed to philosophers.
The role of diplomats in maintaining international relations.

evidence.quote:
words and sayings which can
help us understand how to be successful in life

Communication is the key to success but use diplomacy at home, at work - wherever you are.
There may well be people listening who think the very opposite is true: keep quiet – silence is
the key to success in romance and at work.

private.explanationZh: 前一处证据说明节目谈的是帮助人理解“如何在生活中取得成功”的wisdom，后一处则把“沟通与外交式表达”和“保持沉默”这两种路径并列起来。因此整期节目的重点是比较这些相互竞争的人生智慧。

optionRationalesZh.a: Yiddish表达只是后面解释schtum时顺带提到，不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。节目围绕communication、diplomacy和silence哪种更有用展开。
optionRationalesZh.c: 开头名言问答只是热身，不是主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: diplomats只是词义说明，和节目总主题不同。

public.prompt: Which three areas of life are mentioned in the clip about success?

options.text: family life, politics, and education
friendship, travel, and romance
health, work, and study
home, work, and romance

evidence.quote:
Communication is the key to success but use diplomacy at home, at work - wherever you are.
There may well be people listening who think the very opposite is true: keep quiet – silence is
the key to success in romance and at work.

private.explanationZh: Vanessa先说at home、at work，接着又说in romance and at work，合并起来就是home、work和romance三个生活领域。

optionRationalesZh.a: politics和education都没有出现在该片段里。
optionRationalesZh.b: romance对了，但friendship和travel并未提到。
optionRationalesZh.c: health和study没有出现。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。三个领域就是home、work和romance。

public.prompt: What does Callum imply when he says the best response in an argument depends on the situation, the relationship, and the person's temperament?

options.text: There is no single communication strategy that works in every conflict.
Silence is usually wiser than honesty in personal relationships.
Older people should be given more freedom to win arguments.
Diplomacy matters only when people get angry very quickly.

evidence.quote:
it depends on the
situation, your relationship, the person you're talking to... For example, how
old are they, how well do you know them and how do they get angry? Very
quickly or not? All these things you have to think about.

private.explanationZh: Callum列出一连串变量，说明他反对“一招通吃”的建议。要不要沉默、要不要直接沟通，取决于具体情境、人际关系和对方性格。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。depends on...已经明确表达“没有唯一固定策略”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 他没有断言沉默通常更聪明，只是说要看情况。
optionRationalesZh.c: 年龄只是需要考虑的因素之一，不是结论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 他没有把diplomacy限定在“对方很快生气”这一种情况。

public.prompt: Why does Vanessa string together phrases like "keep schtum," "don't get off the fence," and "won't have had to state your case"?

options.text: To show that English has too many informal expressions for silence.
To criticize people who use Yiddish words in ordinary conversations.
To illustrate a passive strategy of staying silent and avoiding a clear position.
To argue that wise people should never express disagreement openly.

evidence.quote:
If you just keep 'schtum' and ride out the bad situation, you don't say anything, you don't get
off the fence. In fact, you don't register at all. You're just, quiet, quiet, laid back and retiring, it
will all go away – or it will all improve on its own and you won't have had to state your case
either way.

private.explanationZh: Vanessa连续堆叠多个表达，是为了把“沉默应对冲突”这种策略描画得非常具体：不表态、不站队、等事情自己过去。这些短语共同服务于这个修辞目的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她不是在抱怨表达太多，而是在用多个表达强化同一策略。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她没有批评Yiddish借词，只是自然使用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。整串表达都围绕“保持沉默、避免明确立场”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 她描述的是一种观点或策略，不是绝对规范。

reasonsZh.bbc-20081029-the-wisdom-of-keeping-quiet: 节目围绕成功之道展开，对比了沟通、圆融表达和保持沉默这几种彼此竞争的看法。
Vanessa提到的是at home、at work以及in romance，Callum也复述为home、work和romance。
Callum强调要看情境、关系和对方脾气，说明没有一种固定沟通策略适用于所有争执。
Vanessa连续使用这些说法，是在描绘一种保持沉默、避免表态、消极等待问题自行过去的策略。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the programme's discussion of poetry?

options.text: How poetry uses metaphor, symbolism, and storytelling to communicate ideas
Which British poem won a popularity competition
Why lawyers in Britain often begin writing poems
How to write a formal request to the Queen

evidence.quote:
Dave Neita is a barrister – a lawyer who speaks on behalf of people or
organisations in the higher courts of law. But Dave is also a poet, and in his
interview, he uses the words 'metaphors' and 'symbolism' – both very useful for
talking in English about poetry

oetry can communicate feelings, thoughts and ideas by way of metaphors and symbolism.
But poetry can also tell stories and that is the flexibility of poetry; it lends itself to everyone to
do with it as they choose.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题要抓住 Dave 对诗歌功能的概括。他明确说诗歌可以通过隐喻和象征传达 feelings, thoughts and ideas，还可以讲故事，说明节目核心不是竞猜最受欢迎的诗，也不是介绍诗人职业背景，而是在说明诗歌如何作为一种灵活的表达方式发挥作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项完整概括了原文中“metaphors and symbolism”以及“tell stories”两层功能，是全段讨论的中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。最喜欢的诗歌竞猜只是节目开头和结尾的穿插环节，不是主体讨论内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目提到 Dave 既是 barrister 又是 poet，但并没有讨论英国律师为什么写诗。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。写给女王的诗只是后半段的一个例子，用来说明诗歌还能讲故事，不是整段节目的主题。

public.prompt: According to Callum, what does "Mind the Gap" symbolize in Dave Neita's poem?

options.text: The space between a train and a platform
The difference in wealth between rich and poor people
The emotional distance between poets and readers
The geographic separation between Jamaica and England

evidence.quote:
ave has used 'mind the gap' – to symbolise the gap or the
difference in wealth between rich people and poor people.

private.explanationZh: 这是直接细节题。Callum 明确解释说，这里的 “mind the gap” 不是地铁里的字面意思，而是象征富人和穷人之间的财富差距，所以答案必须选和贫富差距有关的选项。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这是 London Underground 里这个短语的原始字面含义，但在诗里它被重新赋予了社会意义。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文直接说它象征“the difference in wealth between rich people and poor people”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有把它解释成诗人与读者之间的情感距离，这属于无根据延伸。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。虽然节目 later 提到 Jamaica 和 England，但这里的象征对象并不是国家之间的距离。

public.prompt: Why does Yvonne introduce Dave Neita's "Letter to the Queen" after discussing "Mind the Gap"?

options.text: To show that poetry can tell stories as well as send messages
To prove that poems are usually more formal than interviews
To explain why Dave became a barrister in England
To reveal the answer to the favourite-poem quiz

evidence.quote:
e also heard how poetry is flexible so it 'lends itself to' other things
such as sending a message, as in 'Mind the Gap' - or even telling a story.

e decided to write a
letter to the Queen – except, his letter was really a poem which told his story!

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查上下文组织作用。Yvonne 先总结诗歌很灵活，可以“sending a message”也可以“telling a story”，随后马上引入《Letter to the Queen》作为“讲故事”的实例。因此她提到这首诗的目的，是用第二个例子补足对诗歌功能的说明。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。前一句先提出诗歌既能传达信息又能讲故事，后一句立即用这首诗作“told his story”的例子。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有比较诗歌和采访在正式程度上的差异。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Dave 的职业身份只是背景信息，引入这首诗的目的不是解释他的职业选择。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。最喜欢的诗歌答案是在节目结尾揭晓，与这里引入《Letter to the Queen》没有关系。

public.prompt: In the poem "Letter to the Queen," why does the speaker ask for a place in Buckingham Palace?

options.text: He wants the Queen to publish his poem in a royal collection
He has been invited to work at Buckingham Palace immediately
He says he has nowhere to stay because he is arriving on short notice
He is comparing palace life with the London Underground

evidence.quote:
don't have anywhere to stay
Because I'm coming up on short notice
S
please, I'm asking you to fix me up a place inna Buckingham Palace

private.explanationZh: 这道题是末段的细节定位。诗里先说“我没有地方住”，又说“我是临时决定来的”，接着才提出请女王安排 Buckingham Palace 的住处。也就是说，这个请求的直接原因是住宿问题和来得仓促。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。诗中没有提到出版或收藏诗作。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。诗中没有说他受到王宫工作的邀请，这是无中生有。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文先后给出两个原因：无处可住、来得仓促，然后提出住宿请求。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。London Underground 的 “Mind the Gap” 属于另一首诗的象征，不是这里请求住宿的原因。

reasonsZh.bbc-20081112-poetry-q01: 节目主体一直在解释诗歌如何通过metaphor、symbolism以及讲故事来传达feeling、thoughts和ideas；竞猜最喜欢的诗只是开头结尾的小环节。A最符合主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081112-poetry-q02: Callum明确解释“mind the gap”在这首诗里象征“the gap or the difference in wealth between rich people and poor people”。因此答案是B。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081112-poetry-q03: Yvonne先说诗歌不仅能传达信息，还能“tell a story”，随后引出《Letter to the Queen》并说明这其实是一首“told his story”的诗，所以这里的目的就是展示诗歌既能传递信息也能讲故事。A最准确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081112-poetry-q04: 诗中直接说“I don't have anywhere to stay / Because I'm coming up on short notice / So please...fix me up a place...”。可见他要求住在白金汉宫，是因为临时到来而且没地方住。C有直接文本支持。

public.prompt: According to Nadia Denton, why is the BFM International Film Festival important?

options.text: It gives Black film-makers a way to showcase stories overlooked by the mainstream
It is designed to reward the shortest films ever made
It mainly teaches viewers technical film vocabulary
It focuses only on school films about Caribbean education

evidence.quote:
to celebrate its tenth anniversary, the emphasis of the 2008
BFM has been on films made by Black people - who are based here in Britain.

Because it's important for us to showcase the stories of groups and communities which
otherwise would go unnoticed, which don't get the correct representation in the mainstream,
who perhaps are stereotyped, who perhaps don't have many other platforms for expression.

private.explanationZh: 主旨句直接来自 Nadia 对节日意义的回答。她强调这个电影节的重要性在于让平时在主流媒体里得不到正确呈现、容易被刻板化、也缺少表达平台的群体讲述自己的故事，因此核心是“提供展示被忽视故事的平台”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项概括了 showcase、go unnoticed、don't get the correct representation 等关键信息。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目开头关于最短电影的问题只是 quiz，不是电影节存在的目的。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。词汇解释只是主持人辅助听众理解，不是电影节的主要功能。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有说该节只放映教育题材或只关注学校相关电影。

public.prompt: What does Lawrence Coke mean when he says young people are "standing on the shoulders of giants"?

options.text: They should admire old films more than new ones
They benefit from the sacrifices and achievements of earlier generations
They need to become film-makers before discussing history
They are physically stronger than their grandparents

evidence.quote:
The whole thing was a very cathartic experience on one level because, I always say that the
young people of today don't understand that they're standing on the shoulders of giants.

it was the right thing to do – leaving children behind, wives, and… I just…I'm in awe of
that.

6 Minute English © bbclearningenglish.com 2008

private.explanationZh: “standing on the shoulders of giants” 不是字面意思，而是比喻后辈今天拥有的生活条件，建立在前一代人的巨大牺牲与努力之上。后文提到那些移民年纪很轻，却离开家人来到英国帮助“mother country”，这正是“giants”的具体内涵。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里谈的是历史贡献和代际承接，不是审美上更偏爱老电影。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个选项准确转述了前辈通过艰难付出为后代铺路的含义。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Lawrence 并没有说年轻人必须先当导演才能理解历史。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这是把比喻误解成字面意义。

public.prompt: Why does Yvonne mention that the winning film included people like her own father?

options.text: To show that the film connects with real migrant experiences in her own family
To argue that her father should have received an award
To explain why the festival was moved to the BFI
To suggest that only Caribbean viewers can understand the film

evidence.quote:
It
was a wonderful blend of story-telling and interviews with Caribbean people
who'd arrived in Britain from the 1950s, like my own father.

my father simply
wanted to work hard and earn money.

private.explanationZh: Yvonne 提到自己的父亲，不是为了把话题转成个人回忆，而是为了说明获奖影片所呈现的加勒比移民经历并非抽象历史，而是与真实家庭经历直接相连。这样能让听众更具体地感受到这部电影的历史与情感分量。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。她借自己父亲把影片中的移民故事落到真实个人经历上，增强了节目中的历史现实感。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有讨论她父亲是否参与或应获奖。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。电影节地点安排是前文背景信息，和这里提到父亲的目的无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她是在扩大听众对影片意义的理解，而不是排他性地限定观众群。

public.prompt: Why was education especially important to many Caribbean migrants in the programme?

options.text: Many wanted younger relatives to gain opportunities they themselves had missed
Schools in Britain refused to accept Caribbean children
Most migrants planned to become teachers after arriving
The film festival required educational achievements for entry

evidence.quote:
He wanted his parents to have what they
needed and he especially wanted his younger brothers and sisters to go to
school.

most of them left school and started work when they were
fourteen years old.

private.explanationZh: 这里需要整合两处信息。上一处说他们特别希望弟弟妹妹能上学，后一处解释原因：很多移民自己十四岁就离校工作，教育机会有限。所以教育之所以重要，是因为他们希望下一代拥有自己错失的机会。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项同时涵盖了“希望 younger brothers and sisters 去上学”和“自己很早离校”的因果关系。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说英国学校拒收加勒比孩子。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目只说他们重视教育，没有说多数人计划当老师。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。教育重要性来自移民家庭经验，不来自电影节报名条件。

reasonsZh.bbc-20081126-bfm-international-film-fest: Nadia说这个电影节重要，是因为它可以“showcase”那些否则会被忽视、在主流中得不到正确呈现、又常被刻板化的群体故事。A是对这层意思的准确改写。
Lawrence后面解释上一代人经历艰难、帮助英国、养家并为后代创造条件，William和Yvonne也说年轻人应理解自己和父母是英国历史的重要一部分。因此“standing on the shoulders of giants”指今天的年轻人受益于前辈的牺牲与成就。B正确。
Yvonne说获奖影片采访了1950年代来到英国的加勒比人，“like my own father”，这是把影片内容和她自己家庭的真实移民经历联系起来，说明影片具有真实而切身的意义。A最符合修辞目的。
Yvonne说父亲想让弟弟妹妹上学，随后又补充多数移民14岁就离校工作，所以教育对他们尤其重要，是因为他们希望年轻亲属获得自己当年缺失的机会。A有充分依据。

public.prompt: What central issue does the programme explore?

options.text: Whether massage therapy should be reserved for blind people or opened to others
Why South Korean cities have too many bath houses
How blind people can travel more safely across bridges
Whether global disability statistics are reliable

evidence.quote:
nce 1963, people in South Korea who are blind
have been the only ones allowed to work as massage therapists. W

is massage work a valuable protection for a vulnerable minority, or a patronising excuse
for a lack of equality?
Yv

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始提出韩国法律只允许盲人从事按摩工作，结尾则把争议概括成一句问题：这是对弱势群体的有价值保护，还是缺乏平等的托辞。两处合起来就表明，整期节目的核心是在讨论这项职业限制是否公平。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项准确概括了法律保护与平等竞争之间的冲突，是整期节目的中心议题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。bath houses 只是说明按摩服务广泛存在的场景，不是核心问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。桥梁相关内容只出现在抗议描述中，不是节目主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。开头的世界残障人口比例只是暖场 quiz，不是主要讨论目标。

public.prompt: According to John Sudworth, why are so many sighted practitioners giving massages illegally?

options.text: They are replacing blind therapists in hospitals only
They want to protest against tourism in South Korean cities
There is more demand for massages than licensed blind therapists can meet
The government temporarily invited them to help

evidence.quote:
ere may be seven thousand, but they can't cope with demand. South Korean cities are
awash with massage parlours, barbers' shops and bath houses – all offering massages by
unlicensed, sighted practitioners. An estimated half a million of them in total.

private.explanationZh: 这道题是中段细节。John 先说七千名盲人按摩师“can't cope with demand”，也就是无法满足需求，接着说城市里到处都是由无证视力正常者提供按摩的场所。因此非法从业人数多，直接原因就是需求太大而合法供给不足。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有把非法从业范围限定在医院。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里讨论的是市场需求与供给，而不是针对旅游业的抗议。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文先给出“can't cope with demand”，再说明大量无证 sighted practitioners 的存在，逻辑非常直接。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文明确说他们是“unlicensed”，不是被政府临时授权。

public.prompt: Why does the report mention that some blind massage therapists jumped off bridges into the Han River?

options.text: To show that massage businesses are located near dangerous rivers
To emphasize how desperate blind therapists are to defend their legal protection
To prove that the protests caused many serious injuries
To explain how sighted practitioners became criminals

evidence.quote:
e massage therapists have even jumped off bridges into the Han River.
Wi

private.explanationZh: 这里不是在提供地理细节，而是在用一个极端抗议例子凸显盲人按摩师的焦虑与绝望程度。紧接着 William 就总结说，他们非常想保住自己作为唯一合法从业群体的权利，所以这句的修辞功能是加强冲突强度。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。桥和河只是抗议发生的地点，不是节目想强调的重点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个极端行为的作用是让听众感受到他们 defending their monopoly 时的强烈 desperation。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文随后还特别说目前没有人严重受伤，因此这不是为了证明严重伤亡。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。视力正常者违法从业的原因在前文需求与法律限制里，这里不是在解释那个问题。

public.prompt: What concern do blind massage therapists have about changing the law?

options.text: They think massage work is too difficult for sighted people to learn
They fear that future competition could leave them with few other job options
They want the courts to ban all massage businesses permanently
They believe customers only trust blind therapists

evidence.quote:
ple are worried that this won't always be the case - and say it would be
really difficult for them to find other types of work. Bu

private.explanationZh: 末段说明，虽然眼下“工作似乎够大家做”，但盲人按摩师担心这种情况不会永远持续；一旦法律改变、竞争增加，他们会更难找到别的工作。因此他们真正担心的是未来生计保障，而不是技术能力或顾客偏好问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说他们认为 sighted people 学不会按摩。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文明确说他们担心将来情况变化，而且自己很难找到其他工作，这正是对未来竞争的担忧。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目讨论的是是否保留该职业保护，而不是永久禁止一切按摩行业。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有把顾客信任作为他们反对改法的核心理由。

reasonsZh.bbc-20081203-blind-masseurs-q01: 节目核心讨论的是韩国按摩师法律是否应只保留给盲人，还是应开放给非盲人从业者；双方都被呈现出来。A准确概括中心议题。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081203-blind-masseurs-q02: John Sudworth明确说七千名盲人按摩师“can't cope with demand”，城市里因此充满由无执照明眼人提供的按摩服务。也就是说需求超过持证盲人能满足的范围，所以很多明眼人非法从业。C正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081203-blind-masseurs-q03: 报道提到一些盲人按摩师跳桥抗议，紧接着William说这说明他们“really do want to keep their legal right”并且“desperate to hold onto their monopoly”。因此此细节是为了强调他们捍卫法律保护时的绝望程度。B最合适。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081203-blind-masseurs-q04: Yvonne说盲人担心虽然现在工作够多，但未来未必如此，而且他们认为自己很难找到别的工作。所以他们担心法律改变后竞争加剧，自己会缺少其他就业选择。B符合推断。

public.prompt: What is the programme mainly about?

options.text: A famous cricket competition about the colors of cricket balls
The life of Eddie Gilbert, an exceptional cricketer who was recognized too late
How boomerangs became official cricket equipment
Why white players refused to face fast bowlers in the 1930s

evidence.quote:
Today – a sporting programme as we hear the story of an extraordinary
Australian cricketer. Sadly, he's only just been recognised - thirty years after
his death.

private.explanationZh: 开头已经明确点题：节目要讲一位“extraordinary Australian cricketer”的故事，而且他是在去世三十年后才终于得到承认。这正是 Eddie Gilbert 整个故事的核心，因此主旨应聚焦于他的才能与迟来的认可。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。球的颜色只是节目开头的 quiz，不是主要内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个选项同时抓住了“extraordinary cricketer”和“recognized thirty years after his death”两点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。boomerang 只是在中段被提到，作为解释其球速的可能原因，不是节目主题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目并没有把主线放在 white players 是否拒绝面对快投手上。

public.prompt: Before Eddie Gilbert could travel to play against white cricketers, what did he usually need?

options.text: A written permit and a chaperone
A new cricket ball and a train ticket
A doctor and a lawyer
A place at Buckingham Palace and a letter to the Queen

evidence.quote: needed written permission to leave his Aboriginal settlement and was normally chaperoned.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道直接细节题。报道清楚说明 Eddie 要先得到 written permission 才能离开 settlement，而且通常还会被 chaperoned，也就是有人陪同监管。因此答案必须同时包含这两个条件。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。written permission 和 chaperoned 都是原文直接点出的两个要求。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有提到新球或车票是特别限制条件。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。医生和律师都不是 Eddie 出行比赛前必须配备的人。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这个选项把别的节目内容混入本题，和本篇无关。

public.prompt: Why does the programme mention Eddie Gilbert's years of throwing a boomerang?

options.text: To show that Aboriginal hunters usually played cricket as children
To suggest one reason Eddie developed such unusual bowling speed
To prove that the story about the dog must be true
To explain why he represented Australia internationally

evidence.quote:
s believed that Eddie's many years of throwing a boomerang – an
Australian type of hunting weapon – was the reason why he could bowl so fast.
An

private.explanationZh: 节目提到 boomerang，并不是把话题转向狩猎文化，而是在解释 Eddie 为何能成为当时最快的投球手之一。句中直接把长期投掷 boomerang 与他的快速 bowling 联系起来，所以这是对其能力来源的推测性说明。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有泛化到“Aboriginal hunters usually played cricket”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。句子明确说这“was the reason why he could bowl so fast”，正是在解释其速度来源。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。狗的故事属于 folklore，和 boomerang 这句并不是证据关系。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文恰恰说他“never represented his nation”，所以这项解释相反。

public.prompt: Why does Yvonne mention Eddie Gilbert's unmarked grave before talking about the statue?

options.text: To argue that statues matter more than sporting achievements
To criticize Queensland for using bronze instead of stone
To stress how neglected Eddie had been and why the statue matters
To show that Eddie preferred anonymity after retirement

evidence.quote:
ly, Eddie was buried in an unmarked grave so his statue is
very important - especially to aborigines. And now, more people will know the
name Eddie Gilbert.

R

private.explanationZh: 这里的“unmarked grave”与“statue”形成强烈对比。前者强调 Eddie 长期被忽视、没有得到应有纪念；后者说明现在的雕像具有补偿性和象征性意义，能够让更多人认识他的名字。因此这句话的作用是突出迟来的公共承认为何重要。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目并没有讨论雕像是否比运动成绩更重要。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。材料完全没有批评雕像材质。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文先说无名墓，再说雕像“very important”，正是在强调过往被忽视与现在纪念价值之间的关系。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有说 Eddie 自己希望匿名，相反是在说社会长期没有正确记住他。

reasonsZh.bbc-20081210-aboriginal-cricketer-q01: 节目开头就说这是关于一位非凡的澳大利亚板球手的故事，而且他在死后三十年才得到认可。整段内容都围绕Eddie Gilbert的经历与迟来的承认展开，所以B是主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081210-aboriginal-cricketer-q02: Nick Bryant明确说Eddie去和白人比赛前，需要“written permission”离开原住民定居地，而且通常还会被“chaperoned”。A与原文完全对应。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081210-aboriginal-cricketer-q03: Yvonne说人们认为Eddie多年投掷回旋镖“was the reason why he could bowl so fast”。提到回旋镖，是在提出他球速异常快的一个原因解释。B正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081210-aboriginal-cricketer-q04: Yvonne先说Eddie被埋在无名墓中，接着说因此雕像“is very important”。这种先后安排是在强调他过去多么被忽视，以及如今立像为何具有补偿和纪念意义。C最符合修辞目的。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the programme?

options.text: A secret nuclear-emergency script that was hidden for decades and later made public
A disagreement about whether the BBC should stop using radio
How the National Archives stores ancient poetry manuscripts
A scientific explanation of how nuclear weapons are built

evidence.quote:
Now today's topic is about an extraordinary document which has been kept
secret for more than thirty years but it's now been made public.

It was to be recorded and broadcast every two hours if there was a nuclear
attack.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开头就说明主题是一份被保密三十多年、如今公开的“extraordinary document”，后文进一步解释它其实是一份在核攻击发生时要反复播出的应急广播稿。因此整期节目的主旨，是介绍这份解密的核战广播脚本及其意义。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两个证据合起来完整定义了这份文件：长期保密、后来公开、用途是核袭击时广播。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有讨论是否停止广播，而是在谈一份历史应急脚本。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。National Archives 只是文件存放背景，不是节目主旨，更与诗歌手稿无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目不是科普核武器制造原理。

public.prompt: According to the script, what were listeners told to do?

options.text: Change stations, remain indoors, and wait for police
Stay tuned, stay calm, and stay in their own homes
Leave home quickly, stay silent, and avoid the radio
Stay with neighbors, stay informed, and travel only by car

evidence.quote:
ay tuned to this wavelength, stay calm and stay in
your own homes.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道原文直接列举的信息题。脚本用并列结构给出三项要求：继续收听当前频率、保持冷静、待在自己家里。因为三项都被清楚说出，所以答案必须完整对应这三条。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文说 stay tuned，恰恰不是换台，也没有提等待警察。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。三个动作与原文完全一致，是最直接的复述。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文不是要求离家，而是要求 stay in your own homes。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有提和邻居待在一起，也没有指定只能驾车。

public.prompt: Why does Callum suggest that staying at home would be safer than trying to get away?

options.text: Because being outside would help people find official information faster
Because officials wanted people to protect their radios from theft
Because leaving home could expose people to radiation, panic, and traffic chaos
Because the BBC expected most homes to lose electricity immediately

evidence.quote:
ember, there is nothing to be gained by trying to get away. By leaving your homes you
could be exposing yourselves to greater danger.

Y

re's a danger of radiation sickness in the air. You'd be a little safer staying at
home. But also, you know, there could be panic on the streets, the traffic would
be chaos, it would be very dangerous. So

private.explanationZh: 这里需要把脚本命令和 Callum 的解释结合起来理解。脚本说离家会更危险，Callum 进一步推断原因包括空气中的 radiation sickness 风险，以及街头恐慌和交通混乱。所以“留在家里更安全”并不是空泛命令，而是基于多重外部危险。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Callum 没有说外出更容易获得信息，反而强调外出危险更大。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有把理由归结为保护收音机等财产问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个选项准确整合了 Callum 给出的三类危险：辐射、恐慌、交通混乱。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有说官方预期大多数家庭立刻断电，也不是这里的论证重点。

public.prompt: Why did one government official want the announcement to be recorded by a familiar BBC voice?

options.text: To make the message sound more suitable for entertainment radio
To ensure listeners trusted the announcement and did not think the BBC had been destroyed
To train famous presenters for future television work
To prevent military officials from hearing the script first

evidence.quote:
government official felt it was really, really
important for someone from the BBC, with a voice that most people knew
and trusted, should be the one to record the announcement. And this was
because the government didn't want people to believe that the BBC had been
'obliterated' – totally destroyed – gone forever!

C

private.explanationZh: 这句话清楚说明了声音选择背后的传播目的：在极端危机下，公众更容易相信熟悉而可信的 BBC 声音；同时，政府也不希望人们误以为 BBC 已经被彻底摧毁。因此使用熟悉播音员的作用，是建立信任并维持信息权威。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里不是为了娱乐效果，而是危机传播中的公信力问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。known and trusted 的 BBC 声音与“不让人以为 BBC 被 obliterated”两点都直接支持这个选项。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有任何 presenter career training 的意思。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。官员是否先听到脚本并不是这里讨论的目的。

reasonsZh.bbc-20081219-nuclear-script-q01: 节目围绕一份冷战时期核攻击应急广播稿展开：它被保密三十多年，后来解密公开。其余内容都在解释这份脚本及其用途，所以A准确概括主题。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081219-nuclear-script-q02: 脚本原文直接说“Stay tuned to this wavelength, stay calm and stay in your own homes.” 这与B逐项对应，没有歧义。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081219-nuclear-script-q03: Callum解释说外出会面临空气中的辐射风险、街上的恐慌以及交通混乱，因此离家反而更危险。C完整概括了这些原因。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081219-nuclear-script-q04: 节目明确说官员希望由大家熟悉且信任的BBC声音来录制，因为政府不想让人们以为BBC已经被“obliterated”。因此目的是增强可信度并避免民众误以为BBC已被摧毁。B正确。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the program?

options.text: To describe how Churchill became Britain's most famous radio speaker.
To explain what jargon is and consider how it can both help insiders and confuse others.
To compare different recycling sites managed by local councils.
To argue that every technical expression should be removed from English.

evidence.quote:
They're only used by people in special or technical situations because they are
the only people who really understand.

but 'a civic amenity
site' is jargon so it's confusing because it could be a wonderful community
centre or a smelly rubbish tip.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查对整段节目的主旨把握。前半段先定义 jargon 是只被特定群体理解的词语，后半段再用 civic amenity site 说明这类表达会让公众困惑，所以节目既在解释 jargon 是什么，也在讨论它的双重作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目最后只把丘吉尔当作反对行话的例子，并不是在介绍他的演讲生涯。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个选项同时概括了定义 jargon 和讨论其利弊两个核心部分。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目提到 civic amenity site 只是为了说明术语会让人听不懂，不是比较回收场所。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有主张彻底消灭所有专业表达，而是讨论它何时有用、何时造成问题。

public.prompt: Why does Yvonne mention 'a civic amenity site'?

options.text: To prove that Paul Ross already understood the council list.
To introduce a place where local politicians usually meet residents.
To give a concrete example of jargon that sounds unclear to the public.
To recommend a better English name for community centers.

evidence.quote:
but 'a civic amenity
site' is jargon so it's confusing because it could be a wonderful community
centre or a smelly rubbish tip.

private.explanationZh: 这里的例子不是为了补充一个新话题，而是为了展示 jargon 的实际问题。这个短语听起来正式，却不能让普通人立刻明白到底指的是好设施还是垃圾场，因此起到“用实例说明术语会遮蔽意义”的作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: Paul 只说 sort of，表示他并不真正确定意思，因此这不是证明他完全懂。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有说这是政治人物和居民见面的地方，这是凭空添加。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。Yvonne 用这个例子把“行话让公众困惑”具体化了。
optionRationalesZh.d: 她并没有提出命名方案，只是在指出这个词容易误导人。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about Joanne Good's view of jargon?

options.text: She thinks jargon should be limited to official documents.
She dislikes jargon because it excludes radio listeners.
She only accepts jargon used by English teachers.
She enjoys jargon because it makes her feel part of an informed group.

evidence.quote:
She says that she loves jargon because it puts her 'in the know' –
she feels part of a special group of people who understand and use it.

private.explanationZh: Joanne 的关键态度词是 loves 和 in the know。她喜欢 jargon，不是因为它更准确，而是因为它让她觉得自己属于懂门道的人群，所以可以推断她把 jargon 当成一种群体认同。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她没有主张把行话限制在公文中，这超出了原文。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是 Yvonne 更接近的态度，不是 Joanne 的态度。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文说的是一般工作场合中的 shorthand，不只限于英语教师。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。evidence 明确说明她喜欢 jargon 带来的“圈内人”感觉。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers end with Churchill's example and the simpler version 'Thanks for your letter'?

options.text: To show that plain language can replace needlessly formal jargon.
To suggest Churchill preferred letter writing to public speaking.
To explain how officials thanked citizens in the past.
To show that jargon is useful when messages need to sound impressive.

evidence.quote:
He didn't like things like 'the receipt of your communication is hereby
acknowledged with thanks' - which means?

6 Minute English © bbclearningenglish.com 2008

William: Thanks for your letter!

private.explanationZh: 节目最后把一长串官样文章直接换成 Thanks for your letter，是一个非常明确的对照。这个结尾的功能是让听者看到：复杂、正式、难懂的表达其实可以用更直接的普通英语替代。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。复杂句和简单句的对照正是在强调 plain English 的优势。
optionRationalesZh.b: 例子关注的是语言风格，不是丘吉尔个人偏好写信还是演讲。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有讨论历史礼貌规范，只是在比较表达是否清楚。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目整体并不支持为了“显得厉害”而用行话，这与说话人立场相反。

reasonsZh.bbc-20081224-jargon-q01: 节目先定义 jargon，再讨论它为何让圈内人交流方便、却让公众困惑，主旨对应 b。其余选项都把个别例子或立场夸大成全文中心。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081224-jargon-q02: Yvonne 提到 civic amenity site，是为了说明 jargon 听起来含糊，公众甚至分不清它是社区中心还是垃圾场，因此 c 最贴切。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081224-jargon-q03: Joanne 说自己 love jargon，喜欢 being in the know，说明她享受这种让自己成为知情圈内人的感觉，所以选 d。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081224-jargon-q04: 结尾用 Churchill 反感冗赘公文语，并把复杂句改成 Thanks for your letter，是在强调清楚朴素的表达能替代没必要的官样 jargon，因此 a 正确。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: How physical warmth can influence judgments and generous behavior.
Why people with cold hands survive longer in ice.
How volunteer work changes personality tests.
Why warm drinks are the best way to study childhood memories.

evidence.quote:
we hear the results of some research into
whether there's any real link between warm hands and a warm heart.

those who had held warm cups of coffee were more likely to be generous in
their judgements than those who had held cups of ice. In a second study, the scientists found
that the volunteers were more likely to give a gift to another person if they had warmed their
hands first.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接提出节目要谈“温暖的手和温暖的心之间是否有真实联系”，后面的研究结果说明温度会影响评价他人以及是否愿意送礼。因此主线不是冰块纪录或志愿者工作，而是身体温度如何影响判断和行为。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项准确概括了研究问题和两项实验结果。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目开头的冰上纪录只是趣味问题，不是主要讨论内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 志愿者只是实验参与者，不是节目讨论的中心。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有说热饮是研究记忆的最佳方法，这属于误读。

public.prompt: In the first study, what did the volunteers hold before judging personalities?

options.text: Small gifts or cards.
Thermometers and notebooks.
Warm coffee cups or cups of ice.
Charity boxes for donations.

evidence.quote:
some were given warm cups of coffee to hold so their hands were warm,
while others were given cups of ice to hold.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题，直接考查第一项实验的设置。说话人明确对比了两组人：一组拿热咖啡杯，另一组拿装冰的杯子，然后再去判断别人的个性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中送礼是第二项实验里的结果，不是第一项实验里手上拿的东西。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有提到温度计或笔记本。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这正是第一项实验中操控手部温度的方法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 慈善捐赠箱没有出现，是无关干扰项。

public.prompt: According to the program, why might describing someone as 'warm' affect how people judge that person?

options.text: It proves warm people are morally better than cold people.
It activates early associations between warmth and kindness.
It reminds people of scientific terms they learned at school.
It makes listeners focus only on someone's physical health.

evidence.quote:
describing people as either warm or cold, taps into emotions and
experiences that date back to childhood.

private.explanationZh: 节目把原因追溯到 childhood experiences，也就是人们很早就把“温暖”与善意、关爱联系在一起。于是当我们听到某人是 warm 时，会自动激活这种早期情感联想，进而影响判断。

optionRationalesZh.a: 研究团队明确提醒这种判断并不总是可靠，所以不能说 warm 就等于道德更好。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据中的 taps into childhood emotions 正是在解释这种联想机制。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这里谈的是情感经验，不是学校里学到的科学术语。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文说的是人格判断和情绪联结，不是单纯关注身体健康。

public.prompt: What is William implying when he says he would refuse a warm cup of coffee from a salesman?

options.text: He thinks the coffee would be too expensive.
He suspects the salesman might spill the drink.
He wants to avoid talking to salespeople altogether.
He worries warmth might make him buy something he does not need.

evidence.quote:
Because if my hands are nice and warm, I just might buy something that I don't
really need or want.

private.explanationZh: William 并不是单纯不想喝咖啡，而是把前面实验结论应用到现实购物场景中。他担心手变暖以后，自己会变得更容易产生好感或更慷慨，从而买下本来并不需要的东西。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他没有提到价格问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有表达对意外洒出的担心。
optionRationalesZh.c: 他担心的是判断受影响，不是完全拒绝和销售人员交流。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这正是 he might buy something he doesn't need or want 的含义。

reasonsZh.bbc-20081231-warm-hands-warm-heart-q01: 整期节目围绕研究结论展开：身体上的温暖会影响人们对他人的判断，以及更愿意赠礼等行为，主旨是 a。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081231-warm-hands-warm-heart-q02: 报道明确说第一项研究中，一组人拿 warm cups of coffee，另一组人拿 cups of ice，然后再评价人格，所以答案是 c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081231-warm-hands-warm-heart-q03: 节目解释 warm/cold 对人的描述会唤起童年里把温暖和善良联系起来的经验，因此称某人 warm 会激活这种早期联想，选 b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20081231-warm-hands-warm-heart-q04: William 说如果 salesman 先给他热咖啡，他的手变暖后，可能会买下其实并不需要或不想要的东西，这正是 d 的意思。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion about Ralph Berold?

options.text: How MBA celebrations changed South African prisons.
Why Uganda became the only African country fighting HIV/AIDS.
How prison overcrowding prevented any legal action.
How Ralph Berold's studies led him into HIV/AIDS activism that improved prison care.

evidence.quote:
who used to think of himself as 'an academic' - but then his studies lead him to
become 'an activist' within the area of HIV/AIDs.
I felt that it was the most pressing sort of social public work to be done in the country.

private.explanationZh: 节目先介绍 Ralph 从 academic 转向 activist，再说明原因是他认为 HIV/AIDS 是当时最紧迫的社会工作。后面关于监狱案件的内容则展示了这种转变带来的实际结果，所以主旨是“学术背景如何推动他走向行动并改善医疗处境”。

optionRationalesZh.a: MBA 只是背景信息，节目重点不是庆祝毕业。
optionRationalesZh.b: Uganda 只出现在最后的知识问答里，不是 Ralph 的主要故事。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文恰恰说明他们采取了法律行动，而不是被阻止。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个选项把身份转变、行动领域和实际影响都概括出来了。

public.prompt: According to Yvonne, what did overcrowding in the prison lead to?

options.text: It reduced the number of sick inmates.
It contributed to more prisoners getting tuberculosis.
It encouraged doctors to visit more often.
It made anti-retroviral drugs easier to distribute.

evidence.quote: this lead to more prisoners getting TB - tuberculosis.

private.explanationZh: Yvonne 在总结监狱问题时直接说 overcrowding 导致更多囚犯感染 TB。题目只考一个明确后果，因此要抓住 lead to 之后的具体信息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有说病人减少，反而说明情况更糟。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据明确指出 overcrowding 带来更多结核病例。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有说医生因此更常来，恰好相反，医疗资源不足。
optionRationalesZh.d: 药物并没有因为拥挤而更容易分发，这是相反方向的干扰。

public.prompt: Why does Yvonne note that some prisoners had committed horrific crimes?

options.text: To stress that the prisoners still deserved humane medical treatment.
To explain why TAC decided not to work with the prison service.
To show that the court blamed the prisoners for the crisis.
To argue that only nonviolent prisoners should receive medicine.

evidence.quote:
although it was difficult spending time with people
who'd often committed horrific crimes, it was important to remember that they
were still people.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到 horrific crimes 不是为了谴责罪犯，而是为了突出一个道德转折：即使这些人犯过严重罪行，他们依然是人，依然有生命权和基本治疗权。这个补充增强了 TAC 行动的伦理力度。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个细节的作用就是强调“仍然是人”，因此仍应得到治疗。
optionRationalesZh.b: TAC 实际上是介入并帮助了他们，不是退出。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这里不是在说明法院责怪囚犯，而是在说明行动者如何看待他们。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文没有做暴力与非暴力囚犯的区分。

public.prompt: Besides obtaining treatment, what broader effect did Ralph say the case had?

options.text: It persuaded all prisoners to volunteer for TAC.
It immediately solved prison overcrowding across South Africa.
It pushed the government to take HIV/AIDS in prisons more seriously.
It led politicians to replace doctors with prisoner caregivers.

evidence.quote:
They managed to change the attitude of the South African government - and
politicians began to take the issue of HIV/AIDs in prisons a lot more seriously.

private.explanationZh: 题干强调 besides obtaining treatment，说明不能只看到个案层面的药物和医生，还要抓住态度层面的变化。Ralph 说他们改变了政府态度，让政治人物更认真地看待监狱中的 HIV/AIDS，这就是更广泛的影响。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说所有囚犯后来都成了志愿者。
optionRationalesZh.b: 拥挤问题并没有被描述为立刻在全国范围内解决。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。evidence 直接指出政府态度发生了变化。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文强调增加医生和药物，不是用囚犯护理者取代医生。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090218-academic-to-activist-q01: 节目重点讲 Ralph Berold 如何从学术研究者转向 HIV/AIDS 维权行动者，并推动监狱医疗改善，因此 d 最完整准确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090218-academic-to-activist-q02: Yvonne 在总结问题时明确说监狱 overcrowding led to more prisoners getting TB，所以答案是 b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090218-academic-to-activist-q03: Yvonne 提到这些囚犯可能犯下 horrific crimes，是为了强调即便如此他们仍然是人，仍应享有生命权和基本医疗，因此 a 正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090218-academic-to-activist-q04: Ralph 说这场案件不仅带来药物，也让 government was taking the issue a lot more seriously，说明更广泛的影响是推动政府更严肃对待监狱中的 HIV/AIDS 问题，所以选 c。

public.prompt: What is the program mainly about?

options.text: How Barbie sales fell after debates about political correctness.
Why London women prefer realistic dolls to global brands.
Barbie's worldwide popularity and the debate over whether she is a suitable role model.
How toy companies use street interviews to design new products.

evidence.quote: The topic for today’s programme is the cultural icon – Barbie.

today we’ve been
talking about Barbie and whether she’s a suitable female role model.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始点明主题是 cultural icon Barbie，结尾又总结说讨论的是她是否适合作为 female role model。把这两处连起来，就能看出节目主线是“Barbie 的全球影响力”与“她是否值得模仿”的争议。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目最后说 Barbie 仍然很受欢迎，没有说销量下滑。
optionRationalesZh.b: 街头采访只是讨论形象问题，不是比较伦敦女性的玩具偏好。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个选项同时覆盖了 Barbie 的知名度和 role model 争议。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有讨论公司如何用采访来开发产品。

public.prompt: According to Richard Dixon, in how many countries is Barbie sold?

options.text: 150 countries.
50 countries.
350 countries.
1,500 countries.

evidence.quote:
it’s a global brand in 150 different countries and our sites are
developed everywhere to make sure we unite girls around the world
through Barbie.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道直接信息题。Richard Dixon 明确说 Barbie 是一个 global brand，销售覆盖 150 different countries，因此只要抓住数字即可作答。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。150 是讲话人明确给出的数字。
optionRationalesZh.b: 50 低估了 Barbie 的全球覆盖范围。
optionRationalesZh.c: 350 混淆了前文提到的 350,000 销量数字。
optionRationalesZh.d: 1,500 明显夸大，不符合原文。

public.prompt: Why does Kate include comments from women in London?

options.text: To prove that nearly everyone in London dislikes Barbie.
To compare British and American toy prices.
To advertise Barbie's anniversary events.
To present contrasting public opinions about Barbie's image and influence.

evidence.quote:
We’re going to hear what some women on the streets of London had to say
on the subject

Well, she doesn’t think that Barbie is a role model at all….she says Barbie
is only a doll and should only be played with.

private.explanationZh: Kate 引入街头采访，是为了把抽象讨论变成真实公众声音。几位女性的评论并不完全相同，有人批评 Barbie 的身材和刻板形象，也有人认为她只是玩偶、不必过度上纲上线，因此这些片段的作用是呈现不同看法。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目并没有证明“几乎所有人都讨厌 Barbie”，而是呈现多种意见。
optionRationalesZh.b: 采访内容围绕形象和 role model，不是玩具价格。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这里不是宣传活动，而是收集公众观点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。街头采访的核心功能就是展示关于 Barbie 的不同公共评价。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the third woman's comment about people 'going politically correct mad'?

options.text: She wants Barbie to become a more serious female leader.
She thinks some criticism is overdone because Barbie is only a doll.
She believes political correctness makes toys more educational.
She thinks only children should be allowed to discuss Barbie.

evidence.quote:
I think she’s a doll you know and it’s
like everyone going politically correct mad. It’s a doll and it’s a dressing
up doll and that’s fine, yeah, no problem with it

private.explanationZh: 第三位女性的逻辑是：Barbie 首先只是 a doll，所以人们不必把她上升到过度敏感的政治正确争论。她并不是在称赞 Barbie 的价值观，而是在淡化批评的严重性，因此可以推断她觉得外界反应有些过头。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她没有要求 Barbie 扮演更严肃的社会角色。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。她把 Barbie 定位为玩偶，说明她认为很多批评被夸大了。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有说政治正确会让玩具更有教育意义。
optionRationalesZh.d: 她并没有限制谁可以讨论 Barbie，只是在弱化争议。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-barbie-s-50th-anniversary-q: 节目一方面介绍 Barbie 的全球流行与销量，另一方面讨论她是否适合作为女性 role model，主旨对应 c。
Richard Dixon 明确说 Barbie 是 a global brand in 150 different countries，所以答案是 a。
Kate 插入伦敦街头女性的评论，是为了呈现对 Barbie 身材形象和示范作用的不同看法，而不是证明单一立场，因此选 d。
第三位女士说大家 going politically correct mad，接着强调 Barbie is a doll，说明她认为外界对 Barbie 的批评有些过头，因为它终究只是个洋娃娃，所以选 b。

public.prompt: What is the discussion mainly about?

options.text: How Alexander Graham Bell searched the White House for a bullet.
Why one Londoner chose a certain inauguration event and what she expected from Obama.
How French promoters created the first Obama-themed cocktail party.
Why witnesses and politicians take the same kind of oath.

evidence.quote:
today, we find out how one
French-Londoner decided to mark this historic event.

I just wanted to watch the
inauguration with like-minded people.

private.explanationZh: 开头先交代节目要看一位 French-Londoner 如何纪念这一历史事件，后面 Alice 又说明自己为什么选了那个场合。再往后，节目继续追问她对 Obama 的期待，所以主线正是“她为什么这样看 inauguration，以及她对 Obama 有何看法”。

optionRationalesZh.a: Alexander Graham Bell 的故事只是开场问答，不是主体。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个选项概括了 Alice 的选择动机和她对 Obama 的期待。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有说这些活动是法国推广者首创，也不是在讲派对史。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目解释 sworn in 只是辅助理解，并非整段讨论的中心。

public.prompt: Who does Alice describe as 'like-minded people'?

options.text: Organizers who were paid to run the event.
Londoners interested in American history.
People who wanted to hear live DJ music.
People who supported Obama and welcomed a Black U.S. president.

evidence.quote:
People who are for Obama – who support Obama and who are happy to have a Black
president of the United States of America.

private.explanationZh: Alice 没有把 like-minded people 说成一般意义上“兴趣相同的人”，而是具体限定为支持 Obama、并且为美国出现一位黑人总统感到高兴的人。题目考查的就是这个明确定义。

optionRationalesZh.a: promoters 是另一类人，不是她所说的 like-minded people。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她强调的是政治立场和情感认同，不是对历史感兴趣。
optionRationalesZh.c: 她反而没有选择 DJ party，因此这项不对。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个选项几乎就是对原话的准确转述。

public.prompt: Why does Alice mention cocktail parties and DJ parties?

options.text: To contrast those party-style events with her wish to focus on the inauguration itself.
To show that Obama preferred informal celebrations to official ceremonies.
To complain that promoters had canceled the best London events.
To explain how DJs influenced voters during the election.

evidence.quote:
Because all the events, you have all these promoters doing something around it, you know,
and throwing some cocktail parties and some DJ parties and I just wanted to watch the
inauguration with like-minded people.

private.explanationZh: Alice 先提到周围有很多 promoters 在办 cocktail parties 和 DJ parties，然后马上说自己只是想看 inauguration。这样的对比说明她提到这些活动，是为了突出自己并不想参加热闹的派对，而是想把注意力放在总统就职本身。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。前面列举派对，后面说 just wanted to watch the inauguration，构成明显对照。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这些话是 Alice 在讲自己的选择，不是在说明 Obama 的偏好。
optionRationalesZh.c: 她没有抱怨活动被取消。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文完全没有谈 DJ 对选民的影响。

public.prompt: What concern does Alice express about expectations for Obama?

options.text: People doubt he can handle the basic duties of the presidency.
People expect him to end racial conflict immediately inside the United States only.
People expect him to represent Black people worldwide instead of mainly serving the United States.
People care more about his public image than any political decision he makes.

evidence.quote:
people are putting too many expectations on Obama. Personally, all I'm
asking is that he's a good president for the United States first because most people think that
he's going to be the president for all Black people around the world but that's not the case.

private.explanationZh: Alice 的担心不是 Obama 无法胜任总统，而是公众把他象征化得太厉害，仿佛他应该替全世界黑人承担代表责任。她强调 first 是 a good president for the United States first，因此可以推断她反对这种超出职位边界的期待。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她没有质疑 Obama 的基本执政能力。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她说的是“全世界黑人”，不是仅限美国国内。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这正是她所说 too many expectations 的具体内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 她谈的是代表性期待，不是公众只看形象不看政策。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-obama-inauguration-party-q0: 节目主要讲 Alice 为什么挑选某个观看奥巴马就职的活动，以及她对奥巴马的期待与保留，因此 b 最符合整体讨论。
Alice 直接解释 like-minded people 是支持 Obama、并且高兴美国迎来一位黑人总统的人，所以答案是 d。
Alice 提到 cocktail parties 和 DJ parties，是为了和她真正想要的观看方式形成对比：她不想把焦点放在派对，而是想专心看 inauguration 本身，所以选 a。
Alice 说很多人以为 Obama 会成为 all Black people around the world 的总统，但那并不是事实；她只要求他先做好美国总统，因此她担心外界把全球黑人群体的期待都投射到他身上，选 c。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion?

options.text: Explaining what private fostering is and why local authorities must be notified about it
Comparing adoption with permanent guardianship in the UK
Arguing that relatives should replace foster carers in most cases
Describing how courts usually remove children from their families

evidence.quote:
BAAF wanted to help
us all understand what 'private fostering' is.

if a child under sixteen - or
under eighteen years old if they're living with a disability, stays with someone
who isn't a close family member for 28 days or more, the local authority must
be told by law.

private.explanationZh: 整段节目先说明什么是 private fostering，随后进一步解释在什么情况下这种安排必须依法通知地方政府，所以核心主题不是一般收养或法院程序，而是“私人寄养的定义及其监管要求”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。前一处证据点明节目要帮助听众理解 private fostering，后一处证据补充说明这类安排达到法定条件时必须通知地方政府，两者合起来正好概括全文主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目开头确实借 adoption 和 fostering 做区分，但重点不是讨论永久监护制度本身，而是引出 private fostering 的概念。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目明确说近亲不能成为 private foster carers，这一项把内容反过来说了。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目提到 care order 只是为了说明普通 fostering 由地方政府安排，不是整段讨论的主要焦点。

public.prompt: According to Moira Keen, who arranges a private fostering arrangement?

options.text: The child's parents or guardians
The local authority after a care order
The child's teacher and social worker
A close family member such as an aunt

evidence.quote:
Private fostering is an arrangement that's made by the child's parents or person having
guardianship - or care of the child, to be looked after by somebody who's not a close family
member.

private.explanationZh: Moira Keen 直接给出定义：private fostering 是由孩子的父母或监护人作出的安排，而不是由地方政府启动。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文明确说这种安排是由 child's parents 或 person having guardianship/care of the child 作出的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这描述的是普通 fostering，而不是 private fostering。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说教师或社工负责作出这种安排。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文恰恰强调照看者不能是 close family member。

public.prompt: Why does the local authority need to assess a private fostering arrangement?

options.text: To make sure the child is safe and the carer can meet the child's needs
To decide whether the child should be adopted immediately
To provide money to every family that takes in a child
To choose which relative will become the legal parent

evidence.quote:
A local authority needs to be able to assess the child's need and to assess the carer's capacity
to meet those needs to ensure the children are safe and that they're well looked after.

private.explanationZh: 根据 Moira 的表述，评估的目的有两层：一是判断孩子需要什么，二是判断照看者有没有能力满足这些需要，最终目标是保证孩子安全并受到妥善照顾。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。选项完整概括了 evidence 中的两个评估维度和最终目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说评估是为了立刻转入收养程序。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文明确说这类安排并不由地方政府提供经济支持，因此不是为了统一发钱。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。private fostering 本来就不涉及让亲属成为 legal parent，这项把监护性质说错了。

public.prompt: What concern does Moira Keen raise about private fostering arrangements for parents living overseas?

options.text: They are illegal for children with disabilities
They are financially supported only for short stays
They should be used only as a last resort because local authorities do not support them financially
They usually require children to return home after 28 days

evidence.quote:
My advice to parents who are living overseas, looking to send their children to live in a
private fostering arrangement in the UK is that these arrangements are not supported
financially by the local authority and should only be a matter of last resort.

private.explanationZh: Moira 对海外父母的建议非常明确：这种安排没有地方政府的财政支持，因此不应轻易采用，只能作为最后手段。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文说的是残障儿童的年龄门槛不同，不是说这种安排对他们违法。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文不是“短期资助”，而是明确说 not supported financially。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个选项同时包含了“没有财政支持”和“只能作为最后手段”两个关键信息。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。28 天是通知地方政府的法定时长门槛，不是必须回家的期限。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-private-fostering-q01: 节目先区分fostering和private fostering，再说明何时必须通知local authority以及通知的原因，所以主旨是解释什么是private fostering，以及为什么必须向地方政府报备。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-private-fostering-q02: Moira Keen明确说private fostering是由孩子的父母、监护人或负责照看孩子的人安排的，不是由local authority安排。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-private-fostering-q03: 录音说明地方政府需要评估孩子的需要，也要评估照看者是否有能力满足这些需要，以确保孩子安全并得到妥善照顾。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-private-fostering-q04: Moira Keen说这类安排不会得到local authority的经济支持，因此对于海外父母来说，这种做法只应作为最后手段。

public.prompt: What is the discussion mainly about regarding the special school in Sudan?

options.text: How a special school helps former child soldiers return to civilian life
Why the war in Sudan made all schools close permanently
How international agencies recruit teachers for British schools
Why older students should avoid learning languages

evidence.quote:
Their weapons were taken away so that they could
become a part of society again. And this included going to school, something
they hadn't done during the country's twenty-one years of war.

Most of them get employed within the organisations as security guards, others work in houses
of the foreigners within the country as cleaners, others work as messengers in offices

private.explanationZh: 节目先介绍这些前童兵被解除武装后重新回归社会，其中包括去上学；后面又说明学校帮助他们获得工作。两段证据合起来表明，讨论重点是这所特殊学校如何帮助前童兵重新融入平民社会。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。前段谈重新回归社会并去学校，后段谈毕业后的就业出路，完整概括节目主线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目并不是讨论“所有学校永久关闭”，而是聚焦一所特殊学校的作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。没有谈英国学校招聘教师，这与 Sudan 的主题无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目恰恰说英语等语言学习有助于找工作，并不是劝他们回避语言学习。

public.prompt: How long does Santo Khamis say it usually takes before the students change for the better?

options.text: A few weeks
About six months
About a year
More than five years

evidence.quote: but after a year, they change for the better and I'm proud of that.

private.explanationZh: Santo 直接说 these students “after a year” 会变好，因此答案是大约一年。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。几周太短，原文没有这样说。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。半年是常见干扰项，但证据明确给出的是 one year。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。与原文时间表达完全对应。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文强调这段变化“isn't very long at all”，显然不是五年以上。

public.prompt: Why does Yvonne mention what the students study and the jobs many of them later get?

options.text: To show that the school gives them practical ways to rejoin society
To prove that military training is useful in every profession
To argue that universities are the only realistic goal for them
To suggest that language classes are less important than history

evidence.quote:
But learning languages, including English, really helps
them to get jobs!

Most of them get employed within the organisations as security guards, others work in houses
of the foreigners within the country as cleaners, others work as messengers in offices

private.explanationZh: 主持人提到课程和就业去向，不是为了罗列学科，而是为了说明学校教育能转化成实际就业能力，帮助这些前童兵重新进入社会。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两段证据形成清晰因果链：学习内容，尤其语言，帮助他们找到工作，从而完成社会再融入。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有把就业成效归功于军事训练，而是归功于学校教育。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文反而说目前还上不了普通中学，更谈不上大学是唯一现实目标。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有贬低历史，相反只是特别指出语言对就业尤其有帮助。

public.prompt: Why are Santo and his colleagues trying to set up a special secondary school?

options.text: Because the current school refuses to teach agriculture
Because the students are too old for ordinary secondary classes with younger children
Because employers require a second diploma before hiring guards
Because the peace agreement requires all adults to become farmers

evidence.quote:
they're too old to go to school with
young people. But Santo and his friends and colleagues are trying to set up a
special secondary school for them so that they can learn agriculture and
become farmers.

private.explanationZh: 从这段话可以推出，之所以要另设一所特殊中学，是因为这些学生年龄偏大，不能和普通年轻学生一起上学，所以需要适合他们年龄和目标的新安排。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说现有学校拒绝教农业，只是说新的中学将来希望提供这类学习。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据先给出年龄过大这一限制，再说明因此要另办特殊中学。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目提到工作类型，但没有说雇主要求第二张文凭。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。成为农民是设想中的培训方向，不是和平协议的硬性规定。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-school-for-ex-soldiers-q01: 节目主要介绍苏丹一所为前童兵设立的特殊学校，以及它如何帮助这些人重新受教育、找工作并回到正常社会生活。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-school-for-ex-soldiers-q02: Santo Khamis直接说这些学生“after a year, they change for the better”，所以答案是大约一年。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-school-for-ex-soldiers-q03: Yvonne提到他们学的科目和之后找到的工作，是为了说明这所学校不仅上课，还提供重新融入社会的实际路径。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-school-for-ex-soldiers-q04: 录音说他们目前不能进入普通中学，因为年龄太大，不适合和年轻学生一起上学，所以才想建立专门的中学。

public.prompt: What is the discussion mainly about in Karima Grant's writing and experiences?

options.text: Karima Grant's stories about resilient children facing difficult new surroundings
How to publish travel guides about New York City
Why children's games break world records
Why adults are naturally more creative than children

evidence.quote:
Karima Grant has been writing stories since she was a little girl and now her
stories are published! But Karima is most proud of her award-winning stories

I love to write about resilience and children because I think that children are very hardy
beings. So that's usually what I find myself writing about – about how children find resilience,
how they express it, how they cope and how they usually resort to creative means to problem
solve.

private.explanationZh: 节目介绍 Karima Grant 及其代表作，随后进一步点明她写作的核心主题是儿童的韧性、应对能力和创造性解决问题，因此主旨应概括为“写关于在困难环境中展现韧性的儿童的故事”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。第一段给出作者和作品，第二段给出作品反复关注的核心主题，两者合并后正是本项。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目不是教人写旅游指南，New York 只是故事背景的一部分。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。开头的接吻纪录只是节目惯例问答，不是主体内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目反而说成年人可以向孩子学习创造性应对问题。

public.prompt: According to Karima Grant, what do children often do when coping with difficult experiences?

options.text: Hide their feelings from adults
Depend mainly on strict rules
Resort to creative ways to solve problems
Wait for other people to rescue them

evidence.quote:
I love to write about resilience and children because I think that children are very hardy
beings. So that's usually what I find myself writing about – about how children find resilience,
how they express it, how they cope and how they usually resort to creative means to problem
solve.

private.explanationZh: Karima 明确说孩子在应对困难时通常会“resort to creative means to problem solve”，也就是转向创造性的办法来解决问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有强调隐藏情感，而是强调表达韧性与创造性应对。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。strict rules 并未出现在证据中，也不符合“creative means”的意思。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。几乎是对关键句的准确释义，但换成了更自然的表达。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。证据强调孩子主动寻找办法，而不是被动等待救援。

public.prompt: Why does Yvonne include Karima's mother's reaction to New York?

options.text: To show that the stories unexpectedly echoed a real immigrant experience
To prove that Karima wrote the stories only for her mother
To explain why Karima stopped living in the United States
To show that Sophie is based on a professional critic

evidence.quote:
My mother said that was her impression(s) when she first landed in the United States and in
New York in particular. She just thought – 'Oh, this is so ugly'. It didn't look anything like
what she knew, so it (a) strange resonance (it had) for me.

private.explanationZh: 主持人加入母亲的反应，是为了说明 Karima 写出的故事并不只是虚构情节，而是意外地呼应了母亲初到美国时真实的移民体验，因此作品对她而言更有分量。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。evidence 中的 strange resonance 正是在说故事与母亲真实经历发生了呼应。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目说故事后来对她母亲也有意义，但没有说写作对象仅仅是母亲。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有讨论 Karima 是否因此停止在美国生活。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。母亲是在回忆个人感受，不是在进行专业文学批评。

public.prompt: Based on the ending, what does Yvonne suggest is likely as more people move to different countries?

options.text: Fewer people will want to read children's stories
Writers will avoid personal subjects
There will be more stories and lessons to share
Cities will become less important in fiction

evidence.quote:
there are even more
stories to tell

private.explanationZh: 在结尾处，Yvonne 先说随着越来越多人迁移，会有“更多故事可讲”，William 紧接着补充“and lessons to be learned”，因此最合理的预测是未来会有更多故事和经验教训被讲述出来。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。结尾暗示内容会增加，而不是读者兴趣下降。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目整体恰恰强调个人经历可以转化成有价值的故事。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它同时呼应了 Yvonne 的 stories to tell 和 William 的 lessons to be learned。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有说城市在文学中会变得不重要，这个推断没有依据。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-story-at-the-ready-q01: 讨论核心是Karima Grant写作的主题：有韧性的孩子如何在陌生而困难的新环境中应对问题，Sophie from Senegal moving to New York正是这个例子。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-story-at-the-ready-q02: Karima明确说孩子在应对困难时通常会“resort to creative means to problem solve”，也就是用有创造性的方式解决问题。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-story-at-the-ready-q03: Yvonne提到Karima母亲初到纽约时的反应，是为了说明这些故事意外地呼应了真实移民经历，因此这些故事对Karima变得更特别。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090226-story-at-the-ready-q04: 结尾Yvonne说随着更多人迁往不同国家，会有“more stories to tell”，William补充“and lessons to be learned”，所以推断会有更多故事和经验可分享。

public.prompt: What is the main idea of the report?

options.text: Research suggests that chemicals may help explain love and could lead to new applications
Small animals are damaging farms across North America
Older couples should delay marriage until science improves
Neuroscience cannot study human emotions effectively

evidence.quote:
we'll hear a report about some research
which might help to explain some of the mysteries of love - such as why we
fall in love.

His studies on prairie voles which like humans, form lifelong relationships, has led him to
believe that this most powerful of human emotions is governed by surges of chemicals,
including a compound called oxytocin.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就说要介绍帮助解释爱情之谜的研究，随后具体说明 Young 教授认为爱情受到包括 oxytocin 在内的化学物质激增所支配，因此主旨是“化学研究如何解释爱情，并可能带来后续应用”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。前一段给出研究主题，后一段给出核心发现，两者构成节目主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。prairie voles 只是研究模型，不是农业害兽新闻。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有劝老年伴侣推迟婚姻，这只是开头 trivia 的干扰。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。整段恰恰展示 neuroscience 正在尝试解释爱情，而不是宣称它做不到。

public.prompt: Why did Professor Young study prairie voles?

options.text: Because they can survive snake poison
Because they form lifelong relationships like humans
Because they are the largest animals on the prairie
Because they are easy to train for matchmaking experiments

evidence.quote:
His studies on prairie voles which like humans, form lifelong relationships, has led him to
believe that this most powerful of human emotions is governed by surges of chemicals,
including a compound called oxytocin.

private.explanationZh: 证据中直接说明 prairie voles “like humans, form lifelong relationships”，这正是它们成为研究爱情机制模型的原因。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。蛇毒解药是后面解释 antidote 时举的例子，与 voles 无关。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文明确给出它们与人类相似的关键点：形成终身关系。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。体型大小并不是研究理由，原文也没有这么说。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有提到训练它们做配对实验，这属于无依据扩展。

public.prompt: According to the report, who might use a genetic love test?

options.text: Animal rescue centers
University students choosing courses
Local courts deciding marriage cases
Matchmaking agencies

evidence.quote:
there's even the prospect of a genetic love test where matchmaking
agencies would attempt to determine a person's pre-disposition to a happy married life.

private.explanationZh: 报道明确说，这种 genetic love test 的使用者可能是 matchmaking agencies，也就是帮人介绍对象、撮合婚姻的机构。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。动物救助机构不在报道的应用场景里。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有把此测试与选课或教育决策联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有提到法院使用这种测试。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。matchmaking agencies 是原文点名的潜在使用者。

public.prompt: Why does Yvonne say Minnie Munro might have found her man sooner if a love test had existed?

options.text: To introduce new scientific data about prairie animals
To criticize people who marry late in life
To humorously connect the research back to the opening question about marriage
To show that genetic testing was already common in 1991

evidence.quote:
Minnie may have found her man a little sooner if a genetic love test had
been available, don't you think?

private.explanationZh: 这句话出现在节目结尾，用轻松幽默的方式把前面关于 genetic love test 的研究重新扣回节目开头“最老新娘”的婚姻问题，是一种首尾呼应。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这不是在补充科学数据，而是在做节目式收束。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。语气是玩笑式的，并非批评晚婚。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它把 opening trivia 和中段研究内容巧妙连接起来，功能是修辞性的。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。句子是假设 if had been available，恰恰说明当时并没有这种常见技术。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090301-love-drug-q01: 报道主要讲研究者认为化学物质可能解释人为什么会相爱，并进一步谈到失恋解药和基因爱情测试等潜在应用。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090301-love-drug-q02: 录音明确说prairie voles和人类一样会形成终生伴侣关系，因此Professor Young选择它们来研究爱情。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090301-love-drug-q03: Pallab Ghosh说这种genetic love test可能由matchmaking agencies使用，用来判断一个人是否更容易拥有幸福婚姻。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090301-love-drug-q04: Yvonne在结尾把前面的婚姻趣味问题和爱情测试研究联系起来，是一种轻松幽默的收束，并不是在补充新的科学事实。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: How recession changes food choices and helps some low-cost food businesses
How restaurant owners can raise prices during hard times
Why recessions have no effect on obesity
What ancient Greek family life was really like

evidence.quote:
Today we’ll be looking at how changes in the economy are affecting
what people eat.

Not so many decades ago a slimmed down wallet meant a slimmer waistline. To be
poor was to be underfed, and more often than not - skinny. Not so these days
apparently. A study in California has concluded that when poverty rates increase by
10%, obesity rates also go up by 6%.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头就明确说主题是经济变化如何影响饮食；后段又把这一主题推进到贫困与肥胖的关系，说明讨论的核心是经济衰退如何改变人们吃什么，并带来相关商业和健康后果。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它既概括了开头提出的饮食变化，也容纳了后面对低价食品与肥胖现象的讨论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目不是商业建议，不是在教商家涨价。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。后半段明确说经济困难与肥胖率上升有关，不是“没有影响”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。古希腊词源只是开场问题，不是主体。

public.prompt: According to Mathew Price, what change in eating habits is happening as customers cut spending?

options.text: They are cooking more vegetables at home
They are shifting from healthier food to fast food
They are buying more expensive organic products
They are skipping meals instead of eating snacks

evidence.quote:
As customers cut down on spending there's evidence that they're also changing what
they buy. Shifting from the stuff that's good for them ... <crunch> ... And instead
munching away <crunch> on fast food.

private.explanationZh: Mathew Price 直接指出，人们在削减开支时，也把购买内容从“对他们有好处的食物”转向 fast food。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说他们更多在家做蔬菜。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。该项是对 shifting from healthy food to fast food 的准确转述。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。削减开支通常与买更贵的有机食品方向相反，且节目未提及。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文强调的是食物类型改变，不是靠跳过正餐来省钱。

public.prompt: Why does Mathew Price mention McDonald's one dollar menu?

options.text: To show one reason some budget food companies can grow during a recession
To argue that all restaurants should sell only one item
To prove that Americans eat more than British people
To explain where the word budget originally came from

evidence.quote:
Hence, the so-called recession beating companies. Like McDonald's. As with some
other budget food retailers they're planning to expand this year. Helped in part by the
attraction of their one dollar menu.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到 one dollar menu，不是单纯举菜单例子，而是作为证据说明某些低价食品企业为何能在衰退时期逆势扩张：因为它们的低价选项对节省开支的消费者更有吸引力。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。McDonald's 被当作 recession-beating company 的具体例子，one dollar menu 正是其吸引力来源之一。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有主张所有餐厅都应该只卖一种产品。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里不是跨国饮食比较，而是经济环境中的价格吸引力。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。词源问题出现在开头 trivia，与这里的例子无关。

public.prompt: What does the California study suggest about poverty today?

options.text: Poorer people now tend to become thinner than before
Having less money is now linked to higher obesity rates
Only people in California are affected by recessions
Obesity falls whenever wages stop rising

evidence.quote:
Not so many decades ago a slimmed down wallet meant a slimmer waistline. To be
poor was to be underfed, and more often than not - skinny. Not so these days
apparently. A study in California has concluded that when poverty rates increase by
10%, obesity rates also go up by 6%.

private.explanationZh: 这段话先对比过去“穷意味着更瘦”，随后说“现在并非如此”，并给出贫困率上升伴随肥胖率上升的数据，因此可以推断现代贫困与更高肥胖风险相关。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。证据明确说过去如此，但现在并非如此。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了贫困率与肥胖率同向上升这一研究结论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。研究来自 California，不代表结论只适用于那里。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文谈的是 poverty rates 与 obesity rates，不是工资停止上涨时肥胖必然下降。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090305-cutting-down-eating-up-q01: 节目重点是经济衰退如何改变人们的饮食选择，以及为什么一些低价食品公司在衰退中反而表现更好。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090305-cutting-down-eating-up-q02: Mathew Price说顾客在削减开支时，也在改变购买内容，从对自己更健康的食物转向快餐。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090305-cutting-down-eating-up-q03: 提到McDonald's的一美元菜单，是为了举例说明一些budget food companies为什么能在recession期间吸引顾客并继续扩张。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090305-cutting-down-eating-up-q04: 节目引用加州研究说贫困率每上升10%，肥胖率也会上升6%，说明如今钱更少反而和更高的肥胖率相关。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: The effects of not getting enough sleep on people, especially schoolchildren
The history and goals of National Bed Month events
How to train yourself to sleep longer on weekends
Why some people can stay awake for record-breaking lengths of time

evidence.quote: what happens when you don’t get enough sleep.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头先提到现代人睡眠不足，然后明确说本期要讨论“睡眠不够时会发生什么”。后面教师和专家的例子都在展开这个核心主题，重点是睡眠不足带来的后果，而不是活动宣传或世界纪录本身。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了节目主线：讨论睡眠不足造成的认知、行为和健康影响。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。National Bed Month 只是引入话题的背景，不是整期节目的主要内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。周末补觉只是在一个片段中被提到，用来说明孩子暂时补回部分睡眠，不是节目重点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。最长不睡觉多久只是开头的小测问题，结尾才回收，不构成主体内容。

public.prompt: Why does the teacher mention that children can “sleep in” on weekends?

options.text: To show that weekend sleep is usually more dangerous than weekday sleep
To explain that they sometimes recover part of their lost sleep
To argue that schools should begin classes later on Saturdays
To suggest that children who sleep in never feel tired on Fridays

evidence.quote:
they get the chance often to sleep in on Saturday or Sunday and so they
can catch up on some of the sleep

private.explanationZh: 这里提到周末“sleep in”不是单纯解释一个词，而是说明孩子们在周六或周日有机会多睡一点，从而“补回”部分睡眠。这个信息帮助听者理解为什么孩子在周中会越来越疲惫，也说明周末补觉只是有限缓解。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。教师没有说周末睡觉更危险，讨论的是补觉机会，不是风险比较。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文直接说周末可以多睡并“catch up on some of the sleep”，意思就是补回部分缺失的睡眠。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有提出学校周六开课或调整周末课程时间。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。恰恰相反，原文说到周五时他们会更 stressed、tetchy、sleepy，说明周末补觉并不能让他们整个星期都不累。

public.prompt: How does the sleep expert organize his explanation of the damage caused by sleep deprivation?

options.text: By contrasting children’s sleep problems with adults’ work schedules only
By listing historical cases before defining key scientific terms
By moving from intellectual effects to behavioral effects and then to a physical health association
By explaining treatments first and then describing possible symptoms

evidence.quote:
One intellectually - sleep is
important for concentration and vigilance. Another aspect is behavioural.
there's also this association with obesity

private.explanationZh: 专家先说第一类损害是智力层面，涉及 concentration 和 vigilance；接着说第二类是行为层面；最后又补充“更令人担心”的肥胖关联。这说明他的说明顺序是按影响类别逐步展开，而不是按历史、治疗或年龄群体来组织。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。专家并没有把重点放在儿童与成人作息对比上。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有先讲历史案例再下定义；这里是在说明睡眠不足的影响类别。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。两段证据连起来，正好体现“智力影响→行为影响→身体健康关联”的组织结构。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。专家没有先讲治疗方案，反而一直在列举后果。

public.prompt: According to the expert, what is a more worrying effect associated with sleep deprivation?

options.text: Improved vigilance
Longer weekend sleep
Better classroom behavior
Obesity

evidence.quote: there's also this association with obesity

private.explanationZh: 在最后一个片段里，专家明确说更令人担心的是睡眠不足与 obesity 的关联，所以答案必须是肥胖。其余选项要么与原文相反，要么只是背景信息，不是“更令人担心的影响”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。vigilance 是睡眠重要性的一个方面，不是睡眠不足带来的积极结果。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。周末多睡只是前面教师举的例子，不是专家最后强调的风险。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。睡眠不足会让行为变差，不会让课堂表现更好。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原文直接点出与 obesity 的关联。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-a-good-night-s-rest-q01: 节目开头明确说要讨论睡眠不足会带来什么后果，后文重点也集中在学生和一般人睡眠不足的影响，不是讲全国床月历史、周末补觉技巧或破纪录不睡觉的人。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-a-good-night-s-rest-q02: 老师提到周末可以sleep in，紧接着说这样他们能catch up on some of the sleep，作用是说明孩子会在周末补回部分缺失睡眠。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-a-good-night-s-rest-q03: 专家先说智力方面，提到concentration和vigilance；再说行为方面，提到tetchy、irritable、hostile；最后补充更令人担心的是与obesity有关，结构对应选项c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-a-good-night-s-rest-q04: 专家最后一段明确说more worryingly有与obesity的关联，因此更令人担心的相关后果是肥胖。

public.prompt: Which aspect of April Fool's Day does the program mainly discuss?

options.text: Why radio stations should stop broadcasting comedy programs
How April Fool’s jokes, hoaxes, and spoof stories trick people
How to grow spaghetti trees in different countries
Why newspapers are more reliable than television on April 1st

evidence.quote:
we are all allowed to have some fun and play a few jokes on our
friends and colleagues.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就说 April Fool’s Day 是大家可以开玩笑、捉弄别人的日子，后文又延伸到 media hoax、spoof story 和 gullible people 等内容，所以主旨是讨论愚人节恶作剧和媒体骗局如何让人上当。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目并没有主张电台停止喜剧节目，而是在举媒体恶作剧的例子。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它概括了节目从 practical jokes 到 media hoaxes 的整体讨论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。spaghetti trees 只是著名 hoax 的一个例子，不是主题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有得出“报纸比电视更可靠”的结论。

public.prompt: According to Carrie, what is the problem with media hoaxes today?

options.text: Journalists no longer know how to write spoof stories
Most newspapers refuse to publish April Fool’s jokes
People may assume a genuine story is a hoax
Audiences stop reading the news after seeing one hoax

evidence.quote:
often we'll see a story and we think it's a hoax when
actually it's true.

private.explanationZh: Carrie 指出的问题不是媒体做不出骗局，而是受众已经预期会有骗局，所以看到某条新闻时，反而可能把真实新闻误判成愚人节假新闻。答案要抓住“把真的当成假的”这一点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她没有说记者不会写 spoof story。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文正相反，说报纸上常会有这种故事。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文明确说人们会把其实真实的故事当作 hoax。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她没有说观众因此停止读新闻，只说判断真假的难度增加。

public.prompt: Why does Carrie mention the BBC “spaghetti trees” story?

options.text: To prove that Swiss farmers really changed British eating habits
To compare television jokes with radio smell experiments in detail
To explain why the BBC stopped making April Fool’s programs
To give a famous example of a media hoax that fooled many people

evidence.quote: the most famous media hoax is actually a BBC hoax

a large number of
people contacted the BBC wanting to know how to cultivate their own spaghetti
trees! Unsurprisingly, as Carrie said, even after 50 years it remains one of the
most successful TV hoaxes of all time.

private.explanationZh: 提到 spaghetti trees 不是为了讲农业，而是为了举一个极有代表性的 media hoax。后面还补充说很多人真的联系 BBC 询问如何种 spaghetti trees，这进一步说明它为什么是“著名而成功”的例子。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目并没有说瑞士农民真的改变了英国饮食，这本身就是骗局内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里重点不是系统比较两种 prank，而是举一个经典案例。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说 BBC 因此停止制作愚人节节目。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个例子用来具体说明什么叫成功的媒体骗局。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the people who said they could smell things through their radios?

options.text: They were easily taken in by the prank
They were testing a real new broadcasting technology
They already knew the BBC was joking and wanted to help
They were annoyed because the prank lasted beyond midday in England

evidence.quote:
'gullible' person will believe most things you tell them, no matter how unlikely
they are to be true.

Many people actually contacted the BBC saying that they could indeed smell
things coming through their radio.

private.explanationZh: 节目先定义 gullible 是很容易相信离谱事情的人，后面又说许多人真的联系 BBC，说自己闻到了广播里的气味。把这两处信息结合起来，可以推断这些人正是被恶作剧轻易骗到的人。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两段证据结合后，最合理的推断就是他们很 gullible，容易上当。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目明确把这件事当作 prank/April Fool 的例子，不是真实技术测试。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文说他们“actually contacted the BBC”，语气显示他们是真的相信了。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里和 noon 规则无关，那个信息出现在节目结尾的答题环节。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-april-fool-s-day-q01: 节目主要讨论愚人节中的practical jokes、hoaxes和spoof stories，以及它们如何让人上当，不是在批评广播喜剧、讲意大利面树种植，或比较报纸电视可靠性。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-april-fool-s-day-q02: Carrie说现在大家都会去找愚人节假新闻，结果常会把其实是真实的新闻当成spoof story，所以问题是人们可能把真新闻误认成恶作剧。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-april-fool-s-day-q03: 她提到BBC的spaghetti trees故事，是作为最著名的media hoax例子，说明这种假新闻怎样成功骗到很多人。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-april-fool-s-day-q04: 文中先定义gullible和taken in，随后说很多人打电话说真的闻到了气味，可推知这些人是轻信了恶作剧的人。

public.prompt: Why does Kate begin the program by speaking in “Old English”?

options.text: To prove that modern English speakers should return to historical grammar
To test whether Jackie has studied medieval literature recently
To demonstrate how some older words are no longer used or understood today
To show that pronunciation has stayed exactly the same for centuries

evidence.quote:
using words which were in use a few hundred years ago and
that we don’t use any longer.

private.explanationZh: Kate 开头故意说 Jackie 听不懂的话，随后马上解释这些词曾经在几百年前使用，但现在不再用于日常表达。这个开场的作用是把“词语会变化、会消失”这个主题具体化，而不是考 Jackie 的文学背景。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有主张回到旧语法，只是在讨论语言演变。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她并不是正式测试 Jackie 的学术知识，而是在引出话题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。她的示范正是为了让听者直接感受到旧词如今已不再常用、也可能不易理解。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目讨论的是变化，而不是“完全不变”的发音。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: Why number words are always more useful than pronouns in science
How computer errors create new forms of Old English
Why extinct animals are named after modern vocabulary
How English changes over time and why some words survive while others disappear

evidence.quote: language and how it evolves and develops.

private.explanationZh: 主持人明确说本期要讨论语言以及它如何演变发展。后面科学家谈最古老的词、可能灭绝的词，以及高频词更容易被保留，都服务于这个中心：英语如何变化，以及哪些词会留下、哪些词会消失。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目提到数字词和代词只是举例，不是要比较它们在科学中的用途。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目中的 computer 是研究工具，不是错误来源；也不是在创造 Old English。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。extinct 在节目里是拿来解释词义，不是讨论动物命名。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确概括了节目从旧词到灭绝词再到保留机制的整体内容。

public.prompt: Which word does the scientist predict may be lost in the future?

options.text: dirty
who
five
retained

evidence.quote:
dirty’ seems to have the highest rate of change and so we might predict that

sometime in the next 750 years, that word will be lost’.

private.explanationZh: 科学家在预测可能灭绝的词时，明确点名 dirty，并说明它变化速度最高，因此未来大约 750 年后可能消失。其他几个词不是这里被预测要消失的词。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文直接说 dirty 可能在未来消失。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。who 被前面列为最古老的词之一，不是这里说的“将消失”的例子。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。five 也是最古老词汇例子，不是预测要消失的词。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目把 retained 当作解释性词汇，没有说它会消失。

public.prompt: According to the scientist’s explanation, which words are most likely to remain in English?

options.text: Words that sound the most unusual to modern speakers
Words that are used frequently in everyday speech
Words that describe extinct animals and ancient tribes
Words that appear only in scientific interviews

evidence.quote:
the frequency with which words are used in our common

everyday speech, is a strong predictor of whether or not they’ll be retained’.

private.explanationZh: 科学家说，词在日常语言中的使用频率，是判断它是否会被保留的强预测指标。由此可推断，越常用的词越可能继续存在。这个结论不是靠单个词例，而是来自对规律的概括。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说“听起来奇特”会帮助词被保留。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。高频日常词最可能被 retained，这是原文的核心结论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。extinct animals 只是主持人用来解释 extinct 一词的例子。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目强调的是 common everyday speech，而不是只出现在访谈里的词。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-disappearing-words-q01: Kate先用所谓“Old English”开场，随后马上解释这些是几百年前使用、现在日常不再使用的词，目的就是示范旧词如今已不常用、也不易被理解。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-disappearing-words-q02: 节目核心是英语如何不断演变，哪些词很古老，哪些词可能消失，以及什么样的词更可能被保留，所以主旨应是英语随时间变化以及词汇存亡。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-disappearing-words-q03: 科学家明确说dirty seems to have the highest rate of change，因此预测这个词未来可能会消失。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-disappearing-words-q04: 最后一段明确说词在日常语言中的使用频率，是预测它们是否会被保留的强指标，所以最可能留下来的是高频常用词。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of this quiz text?

options.text: Scientific reasons why electronic messages travel faster than letters
A debate about whether radio should replace the postal service
Instructions for starting an international shipping business
Vocabulary related to traditional letter writing and postal communication

evidence.quote: postal service as opposed to electronic means of communication
send letters to each other
to take time and effort to write something down

private.explanationZh: 整份文本围绕传统书信交流展开：先区分 postal service 与 electronic means of communication，再说互相写信的人是 pen pals，最后解释 put pen to paper。多个题干合起来说明主题是“邮寄书信相关词汇”，不是科技比较或商业说明。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文本没有解释电子通信为何更快，只是拿它来做对比背景。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里没有关于广播取代邮政的辩论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。内容是词汇测验，不是商业操作指南。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。三处证据共同指向邮政通信和写信表达。
