public.prompt: Which expression is used jokingly for the postal service?

options.text: snail mail
slug mail
woodlouse mail
worm mail

evidence.quote: d) snail mail

private.explanationZh: 题目第一问直接给出四个备选表达，其中 snail mail 是表示传统邮政的常见戏称。因为这一条本身就在题干选项中明示，所以属于直接细节题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。文本明确列出并认可 snail mail。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。slug mail 在文本中是干扰项，不是正确表达。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。woodlouse mail 只是捏造式干扰项。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。worm mail 同样不是文中认可的表达。

public.prompt: In this quiz, what does “put pen to paper” mean?

options.text: To deliver a letter successfully
To write something down with time and effort
To choose between postal and electronic communication
To correct mistakes in a printed newspaper article

evidence.quote: to take time and effort to write something down
d) put pen to paper

private.explanationZh: 第 6 题先用释义“to take time and effort to write something down”定义这个短语，再在选项中给出 put pen to paper 作为对应答案。因此它表示认真动笔写下来，而不是投递、选择媒介或改报纸。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。到达、投递对应的是 come through 一类表达，不是 put pen to paper。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。题干对该短语的释义就是“花时间和精力写下来”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。邮政与电子通信的对比属于第 1 题背景，不是这个短语的含义。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。文本没有把它解释为修改报纸文章。

public.prompt: What kind of communication is emphasized across the quiz as a whole?

options.text: Live radio conversations between presenters and guests
Instant messaging used mainly in the workplace
Personal written correspondence sent through the post
Advertising language designed for online shopping

evidence.quote: postal service as opposed to electronic means of communication
send letters to each other

c) This is the correct answer.

3. Which of these phrasal verbs means 'to arrive' (about a letter or a parcel)?

private.explanationZh: 把几道题合起来看，文本一直围绕邮寄信件展开：有与电子通信相对的 postal service，有互相写信的 pen pals，也有真正动笔写信的 put pen to paper。这说明整套测验强调的是传统书信往来。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文本不是关于广播对话，而是词汇测验。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。instant messaging 不符合文中对邮政书信的聚焦。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。多处题干共同指向“通过邮政发送的个人书面通信”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。没有任何关于购物广告语言的内容。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-keeping-in-touch-q01: 整篇Quiznet都围绕邮寄通信和写信相关词汇设计题目，如snail mail、pen pals、put pen to paper，因此主题是传统书信和邮政交流词汇。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-keeping-in-touch-q02: 题干第1题及后面的答案区都明确写出，邮政服务的戏称是snail mail。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-keeping-in-touch-q03: 第6题答案区明确解释put pen to paper就是take time and effort to write something down，因此对应选项b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090427-keeping-in-touch-q04: 整套题都在谈通过邮政寄送的个人书面往来，如笔友、信件到达、写信下笔等，因此强调的是通过邮寄进行的个人书信通信。

public.prompt: What central question about happiness does the program explore?

options.text: What contributes to happiness, especially beyond money and possessions
Why Denmark became wealthy during the economic recession
How to calculate international happiness rankings accurately
Why people should avoid spending time with friends and family

evidence.quote:
we're going to talk about happiness and
where we are likely to find it, especially when times are hard.
money doesn't actually buy happiness.

private.explanationZh: 开头先说节目要谈“幸福以及在艰难时期去哪里寻找幸福”，后面又明确总结“money doesn’t actually buy happiness”。两处结合起来，主旨就是探讨幸福来自什么，尤其说明它不只是金钱和物质。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它既涵盖“幸福来源”，也抓住节目对金钱局限的强调。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目提到 Denmark 是答题结果，不是在讨论其财富史。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有教授如何统计幸福指数。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。相反，节目把朋友和家庭关系视为幸福来源。

public.prompt: Why does the second speaker mention his wife, child, and satisfying work?

options.text: To show that family life always prevents contentment and joy
To give concrete examples of the relationships and work he believes lead to happiness
To argue that happiness depends mainly on expensive material possessions
To prove that only parents can understand what happiness means

evidence.quote:
it's about having good
relationships and making time for them, so I'm never happier than when I'm with my wife and
child and also having fulfilling and satisfying work.

private.explanationZh: 这位说话者不是在罗列私生活细节，而是在把抽象观点具体化。他先提出幸福与良好关系、为其留时间有关，然后用 wife and child 以及 fulfilling work 作为具体例子来支撑这个观点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文恰好把家庭和工作视为幸福来源。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。例子服务于他前面的论点：关系和有意义的工作带来幸福。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里完全没有强调昂贵物质，相反节目后面否定了“钱买来幸福”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。说话者没有把幸福限定为“只有父母才能理解”。

public.prompt: According to the third speaker, when does money stop contributing to happiness?

options.text: Only after retirement
As soon as someone buys a house
Once a person is above a certain basic level
Whenever a person has children

evidence.quote:
above a certain point wealth and possessions
don't contribute to happiness.

private.explanationZh: 第三位说话者明确指出，超过某个程度之后，财富和物质 possessions 就不再增加幸福感。他同时补充说最低生活保障仍然重要，所以关键不是“完全不需要钱”，而是“超过基本需求后，钱的作用明显下降”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有把退休作为界线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说买房这个具体事件就是转折点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文直接说 above a certain point 之后，财富不再贡献幸福。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。是否有孩子不是第三位说话者提出的标准。

public.prompt: How do the speakers’ views on happiness mainly relate to one another?

options.text: They all argue that happiness depends on owning more possessions every year
They mainly disagree about whether work and family reduce happiness
They conclude that happiness can be measured only by national surveys
They emphasize non-material sources such as slowing down, relationships, and contentment

evidence.quote: What makes me happy is slowing down.

it's about having good
relationships and making time for them, so I'm never happier than when I'm with my wife and
child and also having fulfilling and satisfying work.
money doesn't actually buy happiness.

private.explanationZh: 把三位说话者的观点合起来看，第一位强调 slowing down，第二位强调 relationships 和 fulfilling work，第三位强调钱到了一定程度后不再带来幸福。三者共同指向：幸福更多来自非物质层面的生活状态、人际关系和内在满足。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。第三位说话者明确否定“更多 possessions 就更幸福”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目并没有把工作和家庭描述为降低幸福，反而把它们作为积极来源。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。national surveys 只在结尾答题时提到，不是各位发言者的核心结论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它整合了三段证据的共同方向，而不是只抓住某一句。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090429-happiness-q01: 节目开头就说要讨论幸福从哪里来，尤其在困难时期；后文多位说话者都在谈减速、关系、工作、金钱与物质，因此中心问题是幸福的来源，尤其是不只是金钱和财物。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090429-happiness-q02: 第二位说话者先提出幸福来自good relationships和fulfilling work，随后用妻子、孩子和满意的工作举具体例子来支撑这两个来源。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090429-happiness-q03: 第三位说话者明确说above a certain point，wealth and possessions don't contribute to happiness，也就是说当基本生活水平之上时，钱就不再继续增加幸福感。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090429-happiness-q04: 几位说话者虽然角度不同，但共同点是强调非物质来源：放慢节奏、满足感、人际关系、家庭、成就感，以及金钱超过基本线后作用有限，因此选d。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion?

options.text: How aging weakens the sense of smell compared with other senses
Why certain smells can bring back vivid memories and emotions
How people can classify pleasant and unpleasant smells in English
Why holidays are easier to remember than childhood experiences

evidence.quote:
certain smells can take us back to
another time and place.

we experience that memory as more
emotionally intense

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就点明主题：某些气味会把人带回过去的时间和地点。随后心理学家的解释进一步说明，气味带回来的记忆往往比其他线索触发的记忆更有情绪强度，所以整段讨论的核心是气味为何会唤起格外鲜明的记忆与情感。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目末尾确实提到年龄增长会让嗅觉变差，但那只是开头小测验的答案，不是整段讨论的主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。前半段提出“气味把人带回过去”，中段又解释这种记忆为何更强烈，完整概括了讨论中心。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目中有一段词汇讲解 pleasant 和 unpleasant smells，但那只是辅助内容，不是主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目举了假期和童年的例子，但重点不是比较哪类经历更容易记住，而是说明气味如何触发记忆。

public.prompt: According to the psychologist mentioned in the program, which other cues can trigger a memory?

options.text: Tasting something, touching something, and reading a name
Seeing something, smelling something else, and hearing music
Seeing something, hearing something, feeling something, or hearing the word for something
Hearing a story, looking at a map, or visiting a familiar place

evidence.quote:
memories can be triggered by seeing, hearing, feeling or
hearing the word for something.

private.explanationZh: 教授明确列举了几种除嗅觉之外也能触发记忆的线索：看到某物、听到某物、感觉到某物，或者听到某个词。题目考查的是对这一细节信息的准确辨认。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有提到“读到一个名字”，也没有把 tasting 单独列为教授给出的线索。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文提到的是 seeing, hearing, feeling, 以及 hearing the word for something，不是“闻到另一种气味”和“听音乐”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这一选项基本完整复述了教授列出的几类触发记忆的线索。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这些内容听起来合理，但都不是教授在该处明确列出的项目。

public.prompt: Why does James Watts buy a special deodorant or aftershave when he travels?

options.text: To create a scent that will help him relive the holiday later
To avoid the unpleasant smells of hotels and airports
To compare how fragrances differ across countries
To make it easier to recognize the people he met abroad

evidence.quote:
He deliberately imprints memories of holidays
in his mind each time he goes away somewhere new.

I deliberately buy a deodorant or an aftershave so in five/ten
years' time I can smell this and it can take me back

private.explanationZh: 这里需要把主持人的概括和 James 自己的话结合起来理解。主持人先说他是在有意识地把假期记忆和气味绑定起来，随后 James 解释自己会买一款特别的 deodorant 或 aftershave，这样几年后再闻到就能立刻被带回那次旅行。因此他的目的不是当下使用效果，而是为未来保存一个可再次唤起记忆的气味线索。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两段证据合起来表明他是故意用一种新气味给假期“做记号”，以便将来重新唤起那段经历。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有说他买这些产品是为了掩盖酒店或机场的难闻气味。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有说他在比较不同国家的香味差异；重点是记忆绑定。
optionRationalesZh.d: 他说闻到味道后会想起同伴和食物等经历，但不是为了靠气味辨认人。

public.prompt: Why does Kate mention smelling coffee every day?

options.text: To show that food smells are usually stronger than household smells
To argue that coffee is the smell most people associate with travel
To introduce a disagreement about whether smell or music is more powerful
To illustrate that frequent everyday smells are less likely to trigger special memories

evidence.quote:
smells only evoke special memories for us when we don't
smell them very often.

private.explanationZh: Kate 提到每天闻咖啡，并不是想讨论咖啡本身，而是拿一个日常、反复出现的气味做例子，说明如果某种味道过于常见，它就不容易和某个独特时刻形成强烈、专属的记忆联系。这个例子是在解释前面观点，而不是新增一个话题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有比较食物气味和家居气味谁更强，只是在说明“频率”对记忆唤起的影响。
optionRationalesZh.b: Kate 提咖啡不是为了说它最能让人想到旅行，恰好相反，她说天天闻到反而不容易触发特殊回忆。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这里没有引出 smell 和 music 的争论；那部分早已讨论过其他线索与气味的差异。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。咖啡例子用来具体说明“常闻到的味道不容易唤起特殊记忆”这一原则。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090430-smells-and-memories-q01: 节目开头直接说明主题是某些气味如何把人带回过去，并引发记忆与情绪，主旨最符合 b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090430-smells-and-memories-q02: 心理学家明确说，除气味外，记忆也可由看见、听见、感觉到某物，或听到相关词语来触发，因此选 c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090430-smells-and-memories-q03: James Watts 说他旅行时会特意买除臭剂或须后水，几年后再闻就能立刻回到那次假期，所以选 a。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090430-smells-and-memories-q04: Kate 用每天闻咖啡举例，是为了说明经常接触的日常气味通常不会唤起特别的旧记忆，因此选 d。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: How newspapers decide where to print horoscope columns
Why universities now prefer to study astronomy instead of astrology
Why horoscopes and astrology remain popular even though many people doubt them
How each star sign developed from ancient Babylonian religion

evidence.quote:
This is the idea that
you can find out what's going to happen to you based on what's going on in the
sky and your star sign.

horoscopes endure because they're actually about the people who
read the newspapers

private.explanationZh: 节目开头先介绍 horoscopes 和 astrology 的基本概念，后面又专门讨论它们为什么在很多人持怀疑态度的情况下仍然“endure”。因此整段节目的核心不是星座知识本身，而是这种做法为何持续流行。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目提到报纸刊登 horoscope，但并没有重点讨论编辑如何排版。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目提到有大学开设 astrology 课程，但不是在比较 astronomy 与 astrology 的学科地位。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。开头定义主题，后面解释其持续受欢迎的原因，完整概括了节目主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目提到 Babylon 只是为了说明历史渊源，不是在追溯每个星座的具体形成过程。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers include Jan Muir's experience of being told to write horoscopes?

options.text: To show that horoscope writers usually begin as professional astrologers
To suggest that horoscope columns can be produced by a simple formula rather than deep belief
To prove that Scottish newspapers are more serious about astrology than others
To explain why journalists prefer writing horoscopes to reporting the news

evidence.quote:
I was
told to sit down and write the horoscopes.

we were given this formula
where you had to write a positive, a negative and a maybe.

private.explanationZh: Jan Muir 的经历具有举例说明的作用。她并不是因为深信 astrology 才写 horoscope，而是作为新闻工作中的一项任务去完成，而且还揭示了一个固定公式：写一个正面的、一个负面的、再加一个不确定的内容。这个例子帮助听者理解某些 horoscope 栏目是怎样被机械化生产出来的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她明确说自己当时并不懂 astrology，也不是以 astrologer 身份入行。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两段证据共同说明，她被安排写 horoscope，而且写法遵循固定模板。
optionRationalesZh.c: 她的经历发生在苏格兰的报业集团，但重点不是说明苏格兰报纸更重视 astrology。
optionRationalesZh.d: 她原本以为会做更刺激的新闻工作，恰好说明她并不偏爱写 horoscope。

public.prompt: What does Jan Muir suggest is one reason horoscopes continue to be popular?

options.text: They give readers a rare section of the paper that feels personally directed at them
They are usually more accurate than political reporting and celebrity news
They help readers remember all twelve Zodiac signs more easily
They offer scientific evidence that personality depends on birth date

evidence.quote:
the one little bit in the newspaper refers to them, it's
all about them.

private.explanationZh: Jan 的意思是，在一份充满政治、饥荒、明星等宏大外部信息的报纸里，horoscope 是少数直接“写给你”的内容，所以读者容易被吸引。这是一种从原话中推断出的受欢迎原因，不是她在夸 horoscopes 的真实性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。她强调 horoscope 栏目“all about them”，说明它满足了读者想看与自己相关内容的心理。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她没有说 horoscope 比其他新闻更准确，节目整体也没有为其真实性背书。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有提到读者依靠 horoscope 来记忆十二星座。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目反而多次提到 people are sceptical，并未提供科学证据支持 astrology。

public.prompt: Why does Dr Nicholas Campion mention priests advising Babylonian emperors?

options.text: To argue that modern horoscope writers should return to religious authority
To show that astrology became popular only after newspapers started printing it
To explain why tabloids place horoscopes beside cartoons
To emphasize the long historical continuity behind modern horoscope columns

evidence.quote:
there is a direct lineage between the people writing the
horoscope columns and the priests 4000 years ago advising Babylonian Emperors.

private.explanationZh: Campion 提到四千年前为巴比伦皇帝提供建议的祭司，是为了把今天报纸里的 horoscope 专栏和非常古老的占星传统连起来。他关注的是这种文化传承与延续，而不是要求现代社会重新接受古代宗教权威。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他没有主张现代人应重新服从宗教权威，只是在说明历史连续性。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这与原意相反；他强调 astrology 早在报纸出现之前就已经存在。
optionRationalesZh.c: 他提到 tabloid 和 cartoons 是语境背景，不是要解释版面编排原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。Babylonian priests 的例子用来突出现代 horoscope 栏目背后有非常悠久的历史脉络。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090507-horoscopes-and-astrology-q0: 节目围绕星座和运势为何在很多人半信半疑的情况下仍然流行展开，主旨对应 c。
Jan Muir 讲述自己被要求按“正面、负面、也许”公式写运势，说明这类专栏可能是按固定套路生产，而非基于深信，因此选 b。
Jan Muir 认为运势专栏经久不衰的原因之一，是报纸里那一小块内容是在讲读者自己，带有强烈个人指向性，所以选 a。
Nicholas Campion 提到古巴比伦祭司为皇帝提供建议，是为了强调现代星座专栏背后有很长的历史延续性，因此选 d。

public.prompt: Which broader issue about housework does the discussion explore?

options.text: How attitudes toward spring cleaning and housework have changed, and why some people still value a clear out
Why most people now spend more time cleaning than people did in the past
How to choose the best cleaning products for every room in a house
Why professional cleaners believe spring is the only sensible time to clean

evidence.quote: today people have very varied attitudes to housework.
it kind of lifts your spirits to be able to have less clutter around.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头就说现代人对 housework 的态度差异很大，后面既谈到过去和现在的变化，也谈到为什么有人依然认为 spring cleaning 和 clear out 有心理上的好处。因此主线是“态度变化 + 仍然存在的价值”，而不是清洁技巧本身。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。前段铺陈的是态度差异，后段补充的是整理杂物为什么仍值得做，合起来就是整段讨论的中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有说现代人大多数比过去花更多时间清洁，反而强调条件变了、做法更灵活。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目不是操作指南，没有系统讲解各房间该用什么清洁用品。
optionRationalesZh.d: 专业清洁者认为 clear out 有好处，但并没有说只有春天才适合打扫。

public.prompt: How does Vivien Emery describe her house?

options.text: Perfectly tidy in every room because she dislikes clutter
Dirty in the kitchen and bathroom but neat everywhere else
Generally clean but often untidy, with clutter in some places
Messy mainly because she owns too many carpets and books

evidence.quote: The state of my house is reasonably tidy, especially the rooms you can see.

the kitchen's clean but it's very untidy - everything's on the
worktops.

private.explanationZh: Vivien 的描述有一个明显区分：clean 和 tidy 不是一回事。她说家里表面上还算整齐，但厨房虽然干净却非常凌乱，说明她强调的是“并不脏”，而不是“样样井井有条”。所以最准确的概括是：总体比较干净，但有些地方明显杂乱。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她明确说自己并不 obsessive，而且并没有说每个房间都完全整洁。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她说厨房和浴室是 clean 的，不是 dirty。问题在于 untidy。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。两段证据都指向这个区分：clean, but untidy, with clutter in places.
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目提到 clutter 和一些物品，但没有把问题归因于“地毯和书太多”。

public.prompt: What does the professional cleaner imply is the main benefit of having a clear out?

options.text: It reduces the amount of washing that has to be done each week
It improves a person's mood and mental clarity by reducing clutter
It makes it easier to impress visitors with a spotless house
It allows people to avoid doing a full spring clean in the future

evidence.quote: it just kind of clears the air, it clears your head.
it kind of lifts your spirits to be able to have less clutter around.

private.explanationZh: 这位 professional cleaner 不是把 clear out 的价值说成“更好看”或“更省事”，而是强调它带来的心理效果：头脑更清爽、空气更通透、心情也会更好。把两句证据结合起来，就能推断她认为减少 clutter 的主要好处在于心理和情绪层面。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有提到 clear out 会减少每周洗衣量。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。她把 clear out 和 clears your head、lifts your spirits 联系起来，明显在强调心理收益。
optionRationalesZh.c: 她没有把重点放在向访客展示“ spotless house ”上。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有说做一次 clear out 之后将来就可以不再 spring clean。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers play Edna McLaren's description of her mother's weekly routine?

options.text: To recommend bringing back a fixed cleaning schedule for modern households
To prove that spring cleaning was originally designed for large families
To show that people in the past relied more on hired cleaners than on machines
To contrast older, labor-intensive housework with modern conditions

evidence.quote:
How do you
think housework has changed over the years?

now we have vacuum cleaners and
washing machines to make our life easier.

private.explanationZh: Kate 在播放旧录音前先明确提出“housework 这些年怎么变了”这个问题，随后 Jackie 用 vacuum cleaners 和 washing machines 来对比过去。由此可见，这段旧式家务安排的录音并不是为了提供建议，而是为了突出过去家务更耗时、更依赖体力，从而和现代条件形成对照。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目并没有建议现代家庭恢复那种按星期几分配任务的老式安排。
optionRationalesZh.b: 录音没有讨论 spring cleaning 的起源，更没有把它和大家庭联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 相反，Jackie 强调现代人有机器帮助，过去更费力，并没有说过去更依赖 hired cleaners。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。引入旧录音的作用就是展示过去家务的劳动强度，并与现代条件比较。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090515-spring-cleaning-q01: 节目讨论现代人对大扫除和家务的不同态度，并解释为什么有人仍然觉得清理杂物有价值，最符合 a。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090515-spring-cleaning-q02: Vivien Emery 说家里“算整洁”，但厨房、浴室和楼下厕所都不太整齐，还有杂物，因此应选 c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090515-spring-cleaning-q03: 专业清洁人员强调 clear out 在心理上有帮助，能“清空气、清头脑、提振精神”，核心益处是减少杂乱并改善情绪与思路，所以选 b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090515-spring-cleaning-q04: 播放 Edna McLaren 对母亲每周清洁安排的描述，是为了与现代条件形成对比，展示过去家务更耗时费力，因此选 d。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: Whether British tourists should choose Turkey instead of Spain
The competing arguments for staying at home on holiday versus traveling away
How to save money by shortening a holiday to just a weekend
Why foreign travel is becoming impossible for most people

evidence.quote:
justify the cost of a holiday abroad and the idea of
going absolutely nowhere is actually becoming quite appealing.

I like going on holiday to see
other cultures, to listen to new languages and eat different food.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就把 staycation 与 holiday abroad 对立起来：Kate 提出“待在家里”越来越有吸引力，Jackie 立刻给出相反理由，强调旅行能接触新文化、新语言和不同食物。整段节目随后不断呈现两边论点，所以主线是“在家度假还是外出旅行”的对比讨论。

optionRationalesZh.a: Turkey 和 Spain 只是节目中的小测验内容，不是整段讨论的核心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两段开场证据已经把 staycation 与 travel away 的对立框架清楚建立起来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有把重点放在“缩短假期”这种省钱策略上。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目讨论的是 appeal 和 preference，而不是说出国旅行已经变得不可能。

public.prompt: According to the first woman, why is staying at home more liberating?

options.text: It lets her keep her own things around and avoids pressure to explore
It guarantees better weather and more privacy than going abroad
It allows her to meet new people without leaving her neighborhood
It makes it easier to plan sightseeing in a familiar place

evidence.quote:
it's so much more
liberating than going away somewhere new and not having your own things around and
having the pressure to explore all this new stuff.

private.explanationZh: 第一位女士直接说明了 staycation 为什么让她觉得 liberating：待在家里不会失去熟悉的物品，也不会感到必须去探索陌生环境的压力。题目考查的是对她原话中两个原因的准确把握。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项完整对应了她提到的两点：有自己的东西在身边，以及没有探索新事物的压力。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她没有提到天气或隐私。
optionRationalesZh.c: 她没有把 staycation 的好处说成“更容易认识新朋友”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 她赞成 staycation 恰恰不是为了 sightseeing，而是为了不必承担探索的压力。

public.prompt: In the second woman's argument, what does "the same old, same old" refer to?

options.text: The problem of returning from holiday to unfinished work
The habit of visiting the same foreign destination every year
The repetitive daily routine of life at home
The tendency of travel companies to repeat the same advertisements

evidence.quote:
When you stay at
home you know it's the same old, same old

I suppose she's referring to the everyday jobs of housework and
shopping and things like that.

private.explanationZh: “same old, same old” 在这里不是字面上的“同一个地方”，而是指待在家里时不断重复的日常生活模式。主持人随后用 housework 和 shopping 来解释这个表达，所以可以推断第二位女士是在说家庭日常的单调循环。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她谈的是 stay at home 时的状态，不是度假回来后遗留的工作。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这里不是在批评每年去同一个国外目的地，而是在说留在家里的重复感。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。后面的解释明确把它和 housework、shopping 等日常循环联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 旅行公司广告并没有出现在这段论证中。

public.prompt: Why does the second woman mention anticipation and Groundhog Day?

options.text: To argue that travel planning is as tiring as the holiday itself
To show that people exaggerate how repetitive home life really is
To explain why staycations require more imagination than foreign trips
To argue that holidays add excitement and break the monotony of everyday life

evidence.quote:
part of the
beauty of a holiday is the anticipation.
life becomes like Groundhog Day

private.explanationZh: 第二位女士承认准备旅行会有压力，但她马上补充说 anticipation 本身就很美好；随后又用 Groundhog Day 形容日复一日的重复生活。把这两点连起来看，她是在论证：假期之所以重要，不只是旅行过程本身，还因为它为生活带来期待感，并打破日常单调。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她提到准备旅行 stressful，但整体目的不是证明筹备和度假一样累，而是强调其值得。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她没有说人们夸大了重复感，恰恰相反，她承认这种重复存在。
optionRationalesZh.c: 她是在为外出度假辩护，不是在解释 staycation 为什么更需要 imagination。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。anticipation 对应“期待带来的兴奋”，Groundhog Day 对应“需要打破的单调”。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090518-staycations-q01: 节目核心是在比较宅度假和外出度假的两种立场及其理由，主旨对应 b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090518-staycations-q02: 第一位女士说待在家里更自由，因为身边有自己的东西，也没有必须探索新事物的压力，所以选 a。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090518-staycations-q03: 第二位女士把“the same old, same old”用于形容待在家时一成不变的日常生活与家务安排，因此选 c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090518-staycations-q04: 她提到 anticipation 和 Groundhog Day，是为了说明假期的期待感和外出能打破日常单调，为生活带来新鲜感，所以选 d。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the program?

options.text: Poor sanitation remains deadly because it is neglected, and historical investment shows that improvement can save lives
Modern medicine has already solved most sanitation problems in poor countries
HIV/Aids, TB, and malaria are less serious than diarrhea in every possible context
The main reason child mortality fell in Britain was the creation of the National Health Service

evidence.quote: poor sanitation still remains a mass child killer?

how we can save lives by learning a few lessons from the past - for
example, from the 19th century Britain - according to a new report.

private.explanationZh: Dima 在节目开头就直接提出两个核心点：第一，poor sanitation 仍然大量夺走儿童生命；第二，可以通过回看 19 世纪英国等历史经验来学习如何挽救生命。也就是说，这不是单纯的现状描述，而是“问题仍严重 + 历史经验提供解决方向”的论证。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两段开场证据刚好对应这个双重主旨：问题严重且被讨论的重点是如何从历史经验中寻找应对办法。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目完全不是在说问题已被现代医学解决，反而强调它仍是 mass child killer。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目只是拿这些疾病与 sanitation 相关腹泻做资源投入比较，不是在所有语境下给出绝对轻重排序。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目后面明确说 sanitation investment 的效果甚至大于 NHS 的出现，因此这个说法与原文不符。

public.prompt: Why do Dima and Jackie define terms like "sanitation" and "diarrhoea" at the start?

options.text: To entertain listeners before introducing a difficult political topic
To show that the program is mainly about vocabulary rather than public health
To prepare listeners for the report's later argument about health priorities and child deaths
To suggest that these words are new technical terms invented by WaterAid

evidence.quote:
We use it to mean systems for taking
dirty water and other waste products away from buildings in order to protect
people's health.
Health care priorities - that's deciding which diseases or illnesses to fight first.

private.explanationZh: 开头解释 sanitation 和 diarrhoea 并不是为了做单纯词汇教学，而是为了让听者后面更容易理解 WaterAid 报告关于 health care priorities 的论点。第二段证据直接把 discussion 引向“先对付哪些疾病”的政策选择，因此这些定义起的是铺垫和框架搭建作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目风格虽然轻松，但这里解释术语的目的不是娱乐，而是为后面的公共卫生讨论做准备。
optionRationalesZh.b: 词汇解释只是手段，不是节目主旨；核心仍然是 sanitation 与 child mortality 的问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。定义基础术语后，节目立刻进入 health care priorities 和儿童死亡的比较，因此这是明显的预备性说明。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有说这些词是 WaterAid 新发明的技术术语，它们只是需要先澄清含义。

public.prompt: What does the phrase "lack of political will is driving this neglect" imply?

options.text: Politicians are unsure whether sanitation affects child mortality
Governments are failing to act even though sanitation is a serious problem
Aid agencies want to invest more, but local communities refuse help
The report lacks enough evidence to persuade public-health experts

evidence.quote:
Lack of political will 'is driving this neglect' - in other
words, is the reason for not doing anything about the situation.

private.explanationZh: 这里的关键不是“不了解”，而是“不愿行动”。Jackie 紧接着把这句话解释成“the reason for not doing anything about the situation”，因此可以推断问题的严重性并不缺乏，而是政府层面缺少采取行动的意愿，才导致 sanitation 被忽视。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有强调 politicians “不确定”，而是强调他们缺乏 act 的意愿。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据中的 paraphrase 已明确说明 neglect 来自不作为。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文段没有把责任归到 local communities refusing help。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这里批评的是 political will，而不是 report evidence 不足。

public.prompt: Why does Oliver Cumming use nineteenth-century Britain as an example?

options.text: To argue that sanitation problems now exist only in rich countries
To show that charities were more effective than governments in the past
To explain why the National Health Service replaced sanitation policy
To provide historical evidence that government-led sanitation investment can sharply reduce child mortality

evidence.quote:
it was government
led investments in sanitation which in fact brought about the most significant reductions in
child mortality at the end of the 19th century.

private.explanationZh: Oliver 引入 19 世纪英国，不是为了讲英国史本身，而是为了给前面的政策批评提供一个有力的历史例证：当政府主导投资 sanitation 时，child mortality 会显著下降。这个例子在节目结构中的作用是“用历史证明现实政策选择的重要性”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他没有说 sanitation 问题如今只存在于富国，相反是在谈全球许多儿童面临的风险。
optionRationalesZh.b: 证据明确强调的是 government-led investments，不是 charity 的效果更大。
optionRationalesZh.c: 他不是在解释 NHS 为什么取代 sanitation policy，而是在比较两者对 child mortality 的影响。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。英国案例就是为了证明政府投资 sanitation 能带来显著的儿童死亡率下降。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090521-fighting-poor-sanitation-q0: 节目主旨是贫困卫生条件仍致命却被忽视，而历史经验表明卫生投资能够显著挽救生命，因此选 a。
开头先解释 sanitation 和 diarrhoea，是为了帮助听众理解后面关于卫生投入、健康优先级和儿童死亡的论述，所以选 c。
“lack of political will is driving this neglect” 被主持人解释为政府没有行动意愿，因此忽视了这一严重问题，选 b。
Oliver Cumming 用19世纪英国作例子，是为了提供历史证据，说明政府主导的卫生投资能大幅降低儿童死亡率，因此选 d。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion?

options.text: How rail stations plan to replace all snack machines with investment kiosks
Why a German company plans vending machines that sell gold as a valuable alternative purchase
How a BBC reporter tests whether airport security can detect gold bars
Why customers in Berlin prefer peanuts to chocolate bars

evidence.quote:
Today we'll hear a fascinating report about a new vending machine that's
unlike any other.
As well as chocolate bars you'll be able to buy gold bars.

private.explanationZh: 题目问讨论的核心内容。节目一开始先说这是“与众不同”的自动售货机，随后明确揭示这种机器将出售金条，因此主旨不是普通零食售卖，而是介绍一种出售黄金的特殊自动售货机及其话题价值。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有说所有零食自动售货机都会被投资机器取代，这把个别新机器夸大成全面替换。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。前文铺垫“不同寻常”，后文点明“可以买金条”，准确概括了讨论焦点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有谈记者测试机场安检是否能发现黄金，这是无中生有。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文提到花生和巧克力只是为了对比普通售货机商品，并非讨论顾客偏好。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers mention stocks and shares in the discussion?

options.text: To show that vending machines already sell company ownership in Germany
To compare snack sales with banking regulations
To explain why chocolate companies depend on the stock market
To explain why some people may see gold as a safer investment during the financial crisis

evidence.quote:
Well apparently, with the global financial crisis more and more people decide
that they can no longer rely upon stocks and shares the way they used to.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到 stocks and shares 的作用不是转话题讲股市知识，而是解释购买黄金的背景原因。说话人指出金融危机下，人们不像过去那样信任股票，于是会转向他们认为更安全的资产，比如黄金。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有说自动售货机出售公司股权，这是对词语的误解。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有比较零食销售与银行监管，和上下文无关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这里不是在分析巧克力公司融资来源，而是在说明投资者心态变化。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。该句直接说明金融危机让一些人不再依赖股票，从而为购买黄金提供理由。

public.prompt: What warning does Steve Rosenberg give about the new machines?

options.text: Customers should compare prices at several stations before buying
Customers may need special cards instead of cash
Customers should not hit the machine if it fails because cameras monitor them
Customers must buy snacks together with any gold purchase

evidence.quote:
But one word of warning. If you put in your money and nothing comes
out, don't start banging your fist on this treasure chest - built-in video cameras will be keeping
an especially close eye on all the customers.

private.explanationZh: Steve 的警告非常具体：如果投了钱却没有东西出来，也不要砸机器，因为机器自带摄像头，会严密监控顾客行为。答案必须同时包含“不要砸机器”和“摄像监控”这两个要点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有建议顾客去不同车站比价，警告内容不是价格问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有说必须使用特殊卡，机器仍然是投钱购买的场景。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。引文明确说如果机器没出货，不要用拳头砸，因为摄像头会密切盯着顾客。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文从未说买金条必须搭配零食，这是把普通售货机商品混进来制造干扰。

public.prompt: Why does Dima ask Kate to go through the vocabulary again before the program ends?

options.text: To review key expressions from the report so listeners leave with the language clarified
To replace Steve Rosenberg's report with a longer grammar lesson
To persuade listeners to avoid vending machines altogether
To reveal a second hidden price for the gold bars

evidence.quote:
Now, before we talk about the answer to this week's question, do you mind
going through some of today's vocabulary again Kate?

private.explanationZh: 这句话出现在节目收尾阶段，主持人请搭档“再过一遍今天的词汇”，可推知其目的在于巩固刚才报道中出现的关键词，让听众在结束前完成语言复习，而不是扩展新的新闻内容。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。节目是语言学习类内容，结束前回顾词汇明显是在帮助听众复习和巩固表达。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文只是请 Kate 回顾词汇，不是要把报道改成长篇语法课。
optionRationalesZh.c: 没有任何证据表明主持人想借词汇复习劝大家远离自动售货机。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这句和金价信息无关，更没有“第二个隐藏价格”的说法。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090702-gold-vending-machine-q01: 节目先引出一种“与众不同”的自动售货机，随后明确说明德国公司计划在车站、机场和购物中心安装售卖金条的机器，主旨就是介绍售金自动售货机及其背景。其余选项不是文中重点，或与内容不符。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090702-gold-vending-machine-q02: 提到“stocks and shares”后，主持人马上解释金融危机下越来越多人不再像以前那样依赖股票，转而购买他们认为更安全的贵金属，所以该处的作用是说明为什么有人会把黄金看作更安全的投资。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090702-gold-vending-machine-q03: Steve明确警告：如果投钱后机器没出货，不要捶打这台“treasure chest”，因为内置摄像头会密切监视顾客。该信息与C完全对应，其他选项均未被提到。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090702-gold-vending-machine-q04: 在节目结尾前，Dima请Kate再过一遍当天词汇，随后Kate逐项回顾自动售货机、on the go goodies、precious、stocks and shares、keep a close eye on等表达，明显是为了做语言复盘和巩固。

public.prompt: What issue is the program mainly examining?

options.text: Why counterfeit notes are more common in coastal regions
How the Australian government plans to replace the national dollar
How one Queensland town hopes a local currency can support businesses during the downturn
Why Australian tourists prefer cash to cards when they travel

evidence.quote:
the idea is to counter the global
economic downturn by supporting local businesses.

private.explanationZh: 整段讨论围绕一个小镇推出社区货币的做法展开，而其核心目的被明确说成是通过支持本地商家来应对全球经济低迷，所以主旨是“地方货币如何帮助地方经济”，不是全国货币改革。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目提到伪钞只是安全设计的一部分，不是整期节目的主要议题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文讲的是地方社区货币，不是澳大利亚政府更换全国法定货币。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。引文直接说明该计划是为了应对经济下滑并支持本地企业。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有把重点放在游客支付偏好上，这偏离了讨论中心。

public.prompt: What does the fact that more than 100 companies had already signed up suggest about the Baroon Dollar scheme?

options.text: Most residents wanted to stop tourists from shopping in Maleny
The scheme had already replaced the Australian dollar in August
Local shops were required by law to join the program
Many local businesses were willing to back it even before it officially began

evidence.quote:
despite the fact that the
scheme won't come into force until August, over 100 companies have already
signed up for it.

private.explanationZh: 计划尚未正式实施，就已有一百多家公司报名，这最合理地说明当地商家对该方案兴趣很高、支持度较强。句子没有提到法律强制或已经正式替代官方货币，因此应作出“提前获得支持”的推断。

optionRationalesZh.a: 签约数量只能说明参与意愿，不能推出居民想阻止游客购物。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文明说方案到八月才生效，所以不可能已经取代澳元。
optionRationalesZh.c: 没有任何法律强制加入的说法，这是无依据推断。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。尚未生效却已有大量公司加入，最能说明商家提前表达支持。

public.prompt: How does Darren Mitchell develop his explanation of the community currency?

options.text: He first compares Australian and European exchange rates and then predicts a national currency reform
He first describes secure design features in the notes and then explains how the project could help local businesses
He first criticizes tourists and then argues that only banks should issue money
He first explains how to counterfeit notes and then warns that police will close the scheme

evidence.quote:
We have built into the currency, the community currency, a number of security features that
stop counterfeiting. So, we think we have done a fairly good job there.

It really gives businesses
an extra boost.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查信息组织。Darren 先谈的是货币本身的设计与安全性，说明其“像真正货币一样”具备防伪特征；之后再转向更宏观的经济目标，说明这种做法会给企业“额外推动”。因此正确答案必须体现“先讲设计，再讲经济作用”的顺序。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有比较澳洲和欧洲汇率，也没有预测全国性货币改革。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两段证据分别对应安全设计与扶持商家，体现了清晰的发展顺序。
optionRationalesZh.c: Darren 没有批评游客，也没有主张只有银行能发货币。
optionRationalesZh.d: 他不是在教人如何造假，而是在强调内建设计能够阻止造假。

public.prompt: Why does Rebecca summarize terms such as 'currency' and 'stimulus package' near the end of the program?

options.text: To announce that the Baroon Dollar has already failed
To change the topic from money to tourism on the Sunshine Coast
To reinforce the economic vocabulary used in the discussion before the close
To prove that only economists can understand the report

evidence.quote:
We discovered what an economic
stimulus package is - a number of measures to help revive the economy.

private.explanationZh: 在节目尾声，Rebecca 逐项回顾前面出现的关键词并解释含义，明显是在做语言学习总结。这里的作用是帮助听众复习并巩固与本期话题相关的经济词汇，而不是继续推进新闻事件本身。

optionRationalesZh.a: 词汇回顾不是在宣布方案失败，原文也没有这样的结论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她仍然在解释与金钱和商业相关的词，不是在改谈旅游。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。尾声复盘关键词正是为了强化听众对本期经济表达的理解。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目面向学习者，回顾词汇是降低理解门槛，不是强调只有专家才懂。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090703-aussie-town-gets-own-money-: 节目核心是在讲澳大利亚昆士兰小镇Maleny推出社区货币Baroon Dollar，希望通过支持本地商家来应对全球经济低迷。其他选项要么缩小成假币问题，要么说成国家层面的货币替代，都不符合全文。
文中说该计划到8月才正式实施，但已经有100多家公司签约，这最能说明很多本地企业在正式开始前就愿意支持该计划。并没有说法律强制参加，也没有说已经取代澳元。
Darren Mitchell先在第一段录音中说明货币内置了多种防伪安全特征，第二段再说该项目是在应对金融危机，并会给企业“an extra boost”。结构上就是先讲安全设计，再讲对商业的帮助。
节目结束前Rebecca依次回顾currency、stimulus package、counterfeit、build into、hinterland、address、give an extra boost等术语，作用显然是收束并强化本期讨论中出现的经济和商业词汇。

public.prompt: What trend is the program mainly describing?

options.text: Safety problems caused by tourists who refuse to unpack
The rise of home swapping as a low-cost way to travel and experience other lives
Legal disputes between hotels and apartment owners
The history of holiday homes in the UK

evidence.quote:
our topic today is about the growing trend of exchanging your
house or apartment with someone in a different country for a limited period.

private.explanationZh: 开头已经明确点出主题：节目讨论的是 home swapping 这一“日益增长的趋势”，也就是去别国旅行时互换住房。后文又不断强调其省钱和体验他人生活的好处，所以主旨应概括为这种旅行方式及其吸引力。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有把重点放在“拒绝 unpack 的游客安全问题”上，这是偏题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。引文直接定义了 home swapping，后文又补充其低成本和体验感。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有讨论酒店业与公寓业主的法律冲突。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中提到 holiday home 只是词汇讲解，不是整期节目的核心。

public.prompt: According to the speaker, why can home swapping make a holiday cheap?

options.text: Because travel agencies waive passport fees for home swappers
Because every exchange includes restaurant vouchers
Because companies pay members to advertise empty homes
Because people often get both accommodation and a car, so they mainly pay airfare

evidence.quote:
Usually they include cars as well, so when you think
about it you're not paying for your accommodation or your vehicle. It really makes it a cheap
holiday. Basically all you've got to pay for is the airfares.

private.explanationZh: 题目问“为什么便宜”，原文给出非常直接的因果链：很多互换还包括汽车，因此住宿和车辆都不用额外付费，主要支出只剩机票。所以正确答案必须同时抓住“住宿+车辆免费”和“主要付机票”两个层面。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中完全没有护照费用减免的内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 没有提到餐券，这属于凭空添加。
optionRationalesZh.c: 也没有说公司会付钱让会员宣传空房。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原文逐项说明不用付住宿费和车费，基本只需承担机票。

public.prompt: What does the father like most about staying in another person's home?

options.text: It lets him avoid talking to local people
It guarantees luxury accommodation in city centers
It makes him feel almost like a local rather than an obvious tourist
It gives him a chance to compare hotel chains around the world

evidence.quote:
because you're living in someone's house you
can kind of pretend you're not a tourist and you're actually nearly a local so you can feel a bit
smug about that.

private.explanationZh: 父亲最看重的不是物质条件，而是身份体验上的变化。住进别人家会让他“假装自己不是游客，而几乎是本地人”，说明他喜欢这种更接近日常生活、没那么游客化的感受。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文并未说他想回避本地人，恰好相反，重点是更像当地人。
optionRationalesZh.b: 没有任何“保证豪华市中心住宿”的说法。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。引文直接说明他喜欢那种“几乎是当地人”的感觉。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目在这里不是比较酒店，而是在强调住家交换区别于住酒店。

public.prompt: Why does Kate end by asking, 'What better way of finding out about how people live than living in someone's house?'?

options.text: To suggest that hotels should offer private kitchens
To argue that home swapping can provide cultural immersion, not just save money
To warn that most swaps fail because families miss their own homes
To explain why houseboats are the most common exchange option

evidence.quote:
Often if you
stay in a hotel, you get to see very little of the real culture of the country you're
visiting. What better way of finding out about how people live than living in
someone's house?

private.explanationZh: Kate 在结尾把 home swapping 的价值从“省钱”提升到“深入体验文化”。她先说住酒店往往看不到真实文化，再反问“还有什么方式比住进别人家更能了解他们怎么生活”，因此这句话的作用是突出文化沉浸感。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她没有讨论酒店设施改进，重点不是厨房。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。前后句明确把住家交换和真实文化体验联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有警告大多数交换会失败。
optionRationalesZh.d: houseboat 只是前面举的一个例子，并非结尾论证重点。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090715-house-swap-q01: 开头直接说明节目谈的是“growing trend of exchanging your house or apartment”，并强调它让人可以用较低成本度假、体验别人生活，因此主旨是换房旅游作为一种低成本且有沉浸感的旅行方式。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090715-house-swap-q02: 第二段录音明确说通常还包括汽车，因此住宿和车辆都不用付钱，“Basically all you've got to pay for is the airfares.” 所以便宜的原因就是往往同时省下住宿和交通，只主要支付机票。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090715-house-swap-q03: 父亲说，因为住在别人的房子里，你几乎可以假装自己不是游客，而是“nearly a local”，这正对应C。其他选项与原话相反或无依据。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090715-house-swap-q04: Kate最后反问“还有什么比住进别人家更能了解别人怎么生活的方式呢”，这是在总结换房旅游不仅省钱，还能更深入体验当地真实生活和文化，所以B最符合她这句话的修辞目的。

public.prompt: What topic are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.text: Changing attitudes toward tanning and the reasons it remains popular
Why British weather prevents all tanning
How celebrities design sunbeds for winter use
Why parasols were banned in the early twentieth century

evidence.quote:
Having a tan is
regarded differently all over the world. In some countries it's seen as attractive
and in other parts of the world, people want to be as pale as possible.

private.explanationZh: 这段话概括了节目主轴：不同社会对晒黑的看法不同，而“晒黑为何受欢迎”也是主持人随后不断展开的话题。因此主旨不是单一产品或天气，而是围绕 tanning 的社会态度和流行原因。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。引文直接点出各地对晒黑的不同看法，后文继续解释其流行原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 英国天气只是背景，不是节目总主题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 名人只是后面用来说明流行趋势的例子，不是主要讨论对象。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中提到 parasols 是历史背景说明，不是节目的核心问题。

public.prompt: What does Rebecca mean when she says she and Kate are 'living in the wrong era in history'?

options.text: They wish they had worked outdoors before the twentieth century
They think modern cosmetics are less effective than older ones
They believe sunbeds should have been invented much earlier
They would have suited a time when pale skin was fashionable and tans were not admired

evidence.quote:
I think we're both living in the wrong era in history as before the 20th
century, the fashion was for pale skin.

private.explanationZh: Rebecca 和 Kate 前面都表示自己并不积极追求晒黑。接着 Rebecca 说她们“生错了时代”，并说明20世纪前流行的是白皮肤。可推知她的意思是：如果放在那个以苍白肤色为美的年代，她们的审美取向会更合适。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她不是在羡慕户外劳动者，而是在评论审美时代差异。
optionRationalesZh.b: 句子没有比较新旧化妆品效果。
optionRationalesZh.c: 她也没有谈到 sunbed 发明时间。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。后半句直接解释了“生错时代”的含义，即当时流行白皙而非晒黑。

public.prompt: Why does Ilda De Vico mention celebrities on TV?

options.text: To argue that only famous people can achieve a convincing tan
To show that media figures help make the tanned look seem desirable and common
To prove that celebrities prefer natural sun over creams
To explain why television lighting makes everyone look pale

evidence.quote:
You see the celebs on TV, they're all sprayed up. Being
tanned is popular.

private.explanationZh: 提到 celebs 的作用是举证“晒黑为什么会显得流行”。她的逻辑是：电视上的名人都喷成古铜色，这会强化大众对晒黑外表的接受和模仿，因此名人被用作流行趋势的说明，而不是单独讨论他们本人。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她并没有说只有名人才晒得好，只是说名人也都在这样做。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。名人形象被当成社会流行风向的证据，说明晒黑外观被广泛推崇。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有比较自然晒黑和涂抹产品孰优，反而提到很多是“sprayed up”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 句子重点不是电视灯光，而是名人的造型对流行的影响。

public.prompt: What is the purpose of Kate's vocabulary review near the end of the program?

options.text: To compare British and foreign fashion history in more detail
To repeat the weekly quiz so listeners can change their answers
To gather the key terms from the discussion into one final learning recap
To discourage listeners from using fake tan products

evidence.quote: Let's have look at the vocabulary we've come across today:

private.explanationZh: 这句话明显是进入收尾复盘环节的提示语。Kate 不是在增加新信息，而是在把前面涉及的 sunbed、parasol、fake tan 等词集中回顾，服务于语言学习目标，所以其目的应理解为总结关键词汇。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她在此处不是展开更细的历史比较，而是进入词汇回顾。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这句话没有重新出题的意思，quiz 答案在后面单独揭晓。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。句子直接表明接下来要回顾今天出现过的词汇。
optionRationalesZh.d: 词汇回顾并不等于劝阻使用 fake tan，这把教学功能误读成态度表态。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090716-tanning-q01: 节目从英国人晒黑、假晒黑、历史上对白皙与古铜肤色的不同看法、以及名人影响等角度展开，主线就是社会对晒黑的态度变化及其持续流行的原因。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090716-tanning-q02: Rebecca说她和Kate“living in the wrong era in history”后立刻解释，在20世纪以前流行的是白皮肤，晒黑是穷人户外劳动的标志。她们前面又都表示自己并不追求晒黑，因此意思就是她们更适合那个崇尚白皙、不崇拜古铜色的时代。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090716-tanning-q03: Ilda说大家觉得棕色皮肤更好看，又提到电视上的名人都喷得很黑，接着总结“Being tanned is popular.” 她提到名人，是为了说明媒体人物强化了“晒黑很好看、很流行”的印象。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090716-tanning-q04: Kate在结尾集中回顾sunbed、wide brimmed hats、parasols、in vogue、sun lounger、sun worshipper、bastes、fake tan、celeb等词，明显是在做整期关键词汇的最终复习总结。

public.prompt: What central conflict does the discussion focus on?

options.text: How aluminium is extracted in different parts of India
A conflict between an indigenous community and a mining project on its land
Why London companies are moving their headquarters
The history of the Indian Supreme Court

evidence.quote:
we're talking about a protest by an Indian tribe whose historical
land - the Niyamgiri Mountain range - is about to be used for industrial mining.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就把冲突双方摆出来：一边是当地部落，一边是即将在其历史土地上开展的工业采矿。后文无论是活动人士还是公司高管的发言，都是围绕这场土地与开发之间的冲突展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目不是技术性介绍铝矿开采流程。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。引文直接说明是部落抗议其历史土地被用于采矿。
optionRationalesZh.c: 伦敦抗议只是事件的一部分，不是核心冲突本身。
optionRationalesZh.d: 最高法院只是后文提到的批准机构，不是整期节目的主题。

public.prompt: Why does Dr Jenna describe nature as the indigenous people's 'lifeline'?

options.text: Because it supplies the essentials of life and supports their way of living across generations
Because it gives them legal advice during protests
Because it allows them to travel from the mountains to London
Because it turns bauxite into a valuable export

evidence.quote:
They worship something which has
contributed to their existence for generations. That's their lifeline. It has river, it has food, it
has everything.

private.explanationZh: Dr Jenna 用 lifeline 一词，强调自然不仅具有宗教意义，而且直接提供河流、食物等生存基础，并且这种依赖已经延续了很多代。因此这里的重点是“自然维系其生计与生活方式”，而不是抽象的精神口号。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据明确列出河流、食物和代际延续，说明自然支撑着他们的生存。
optionRationalesZh.b: 自然不可能提供法律建议，这明显不符合语境。
optionRationalesZh.c: 引文没有谈交通出行，London 抗议是另一条线索。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这里不是在讲矿产出口价值，而是在讲部落与自然的生命联系。

public.prompt: How are the two viewpoints in the program mainly contrasted?

options.text: The activist focuses on tourism income, while the executive focuses on weather patterns
The activist argues for more mining jobs, while the executive argues against development
The activist discusses factory safety, while the executive discusses tribal religion
The activist emphasizes dependence on the land and the harm of displacement, while the executive emphasizes legal approval and balanced development

evidence.quote:
Indigenous people are the owner of the land - and first of all we displace them
and we give them some job which has no meaning for them.

India has a very good legal system, regulatory system both
balancing development and the social development. I think we've got to start respecting the
legal system.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查节目如何安排对立立场。活动人士一方强调土地归属、被迫迁移以及“给工作也没有意义”的文化断裂；公司高管一方则转向制度与发展逻辑，强调法律审批和发展与社会之间的平衡。正确答案必须同时抓住这两种论证重心。

optionRationalesZh.a: 两人都没有把重点放在旅游收入或天气上。
optionRationalesZh.b: 活动人士并没有主张增加采矿岗位，这和原文立场相反。
optionRationalesZh.c: 工厂安全和部落宗教并不是这组对比的主轴。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。两段证据分别体现了“土地与迁移之害”和“法律与发展平衡”的对照。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the campaigners after the mining project receives legal approval?

options.text: They accept the ruling and end their protests immediately
They plan to move away and seek jobs with the company
They remain opposed and intend to continue fighting the project
They are waiting for tourist groups to organize the next protest

evidence.quote:
Still campaigners are not convinced. They
say they want Vedanta to give up the mining project and to leave the area for
good. Until then, they say, they won't stop their fight.

private.explanationZh: 虽然项目已经获得法律层面的最终批准，但引文清楚表明 campaigners “仍然不信服”，并且在公司放弃项目前“不会停止斗争”。因此可以推断，他们的反对并未因审批结束而消失，反而会继续。

optionRationalesZh.a: 与原文直接相反，文中明确说他们不会停止斗争。
optionRationalesZh.b: 他们要求公司离开，而不是自己去应聘公司工作。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据明确说明他们仍然反对，并将继续抗争。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文没有提到旅游团体组织下一次抗议，这属于无依据添加。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090811-tribal-protest-over-indian-: 节目一开始就说主题是印度部落因其历史土地将被工业采矿而发起抗议，随后围绕Kondh族与Vedanta采矿项目之间的矛盾展开，因此中心冲突就是土著社区与采矿项目的土地冲突。
Dr Jenna说自然是他们的“lifeline”，并解释那里有河流、食物、他们赖以生存的一切，而且这种关系延续了许多代，所以这里的意思是自然提供了生存必需品并支撑其代代相传的生活方式。
节目先让活动人士强调土著对土地和自然的依赖，以及迁离会让很多事失去意义；再让公司高管强调项目已经获得最高法院批准，而且印度制度能平衡发展与社会发展。D准确概括了这种对照关系。
在说明最高法院已给出最终批准后，节目立刻补充说活动人士仍不信服，希望公司放弃采矿计划并离开该地区，“Until then ... they won't stop their fight.” 因此可以推断他们仍会继续反对。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: How couch surfing works as a free hospitality network and why people find it useful
Why hotels are replacing private homes for budget travelers
How travelers can get visas by using social websites
Why guidebooks are more reliable than local hosts

evidence.quote:
Couch surfing is a relatively new term used to describe an
internet-based hospitality service - the practice of either offering your sofa to a
stranger to sleep on or staying with someone you've never met before.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头先定义了 couch surfing，说明它是一种基于互联网的免费接待方式；后面又继续谈接待者的动机和旅行者得到的好处，所以主旨是介绍这种住宿网络如何运作以及它为什么有吸引力。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这项准确概括了节目内容：先解释 couch surfing 是什么，再说明人们为什么愿意参与。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有说酒店正在取代私人接待，重点恰好是免费的私人接待模式。
optionRationalesZh.c: 签证只是中间举出的动机之一，不是节目主要讨论的网站功能。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文强调 guidebook 之外的本地体验价值，并没有说 guidebooks 更可靠。

public.prompt: Why does Kate explain what a host is?

options.text: To compare hosts with professional tour guides
To clarify the term for people who let travelers stay in their homes
To show that couch surfing usually includes paid meals
To suggest that Rebecca should host foreign visitors

evidence.quote:
Hosts are what we call people who have guests in their house, for eg. if you
came to stay in my house for a night or two, I would be your host.

private.explanationZh: Kate 紧接着要讨论为什么有人愿意让陌生人住进家里，所以她先解释 host 这个词，目的是帮助听者理解后面关于接待者动机的内容，而不是提出新的事实信息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有把 host 和职业导游作比较，这里只是解释词义。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这正是她解释这句话的目的：界定 host 指让客人住在自己家里的人。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目强调住宿是免费的，没有说通常包含付费餐食。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这里不是给 Rebecca 提建议，而是在做术语铺垫。

public.prompt: According to Fleur Britain, what did couch surfing allow travelers to do?

options.text: Stay only in places listed in guidebooks
Avoid meeting local residents
Experience places and social events not found in guidebooks
Choose the most luxurious lodging in each city

evidence.quote:
There are so many compelling things to the idea of couch surfing in that you get amazing
access to the inside story. So we would start off by looking at our guidebooks, you know,
instead we got the inside track, and were taken to things that weren't in the guidebook,

private.explanationZh: Fleur Britain 说 couch surfing 的吸引力在于可以得到 inside story 和 inside track，也就是接触攻略之外的本地信息与活动，因此正确答案是能体验 guidebook 上没有写到的地方和社交场景。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文恰恰说她们没有停留在 guidebook 的信息上，而是接触到书上没有的内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目强调的是认识当地人、进入本地生活，而不是回避他们。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这项准确转述了 inside track 和 things that weren't in the guidebook 的意思。
optionRationalesZh.d: 前面 Rebecca 还说这并不豪华，所以这项与原文相反。

public.prompt: According to the program, which city was the most popular destination for couch surfing at that time?

options.text: London
New York
Paris
Moscow

evidence.quote: The answer is in fact Paris with 22,000 surfers visiting the city.

private.explanationZh: 节目结尾直接公布答案：当时最受 couch surfing 欢迎的城市是 Paris，所以这是一道直接细节题。

optionRationalesZh.a: London 只是前面给出的选项之一，结尾没有把它公布为答案。
optionRationalesZh.b: New York 也是干扰项，节目最后没有选择它。
optionRationalesZh.c: Kate 明确说答案是 Paris，并补充了 22,000 surfers 的信息。
optionRationalesZh.d: Moscow 从未出现在这一题的选项或答案里。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090812-couch-surfing-q01: 节目先定义couch surfing，再说明其运作方式，并讨论房主与旅行者各自的好处，主旨是这种免费接待网络如何运作及其价值。其余选项都缩小或歪曲了范围。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090812-couch-surfing-q02: Kate紧接着说“Hosts are what we call people who have guests in their house”，明显是在解释host这个词在本话题中的含义，即让旅行者住在家里的人。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090812-couch-surfing-q03: Fleur Britain说她们被带去导游书里没有的地方、朋友家和聚会，说明couch surfing让旅行者接触到指南书之外的地点和社交活动。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090812-couch-surfing-q04: 节目结尾直接公布答案：最受欢迎的城市是Paris，因此答案唯一明确。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: People's memories of the moon landing and why it was a major achievement
How astronauts are trained for future missions
Why moon travel ended immediately after 1969
How science fiction changed after the first moon walk

evidence.quote:
Now, you can't failed to have noticed that this month (July)
marks the 40th anniversary of the first ever moon landing back in 1969.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就说明话题是首次登月四十周年，后面既回顾人们对那一天的记忆，也不断强调这件事的历史意义，所以主旨是回顾登月及其重要性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这项同时包含“回忆”和“重大成就”两层意思，最完整地概括了节目。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有介绍宇航员训练过程。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目并没有讨论为什么 1969 后月球旅行立刻结束，只在结尾补充最后一次登月时间。
optionRationalesZh.d: science fiction 只是某位说话人的背景，不是节目的主题。

public.prompt: What does the first speaker remember about hearing the news of the moon landing?

options.text: A teacher announced it during class
He watched it alone on television
He was walking with his mother when she pointed to the moon
His father woke him in the middle of the night

evidence.quote:
I was out walking after dark with my mother. And she pointed up and she said 'There
are men on the moon'.

private.explanationZh: 第一位说话人回忆的是自己晚上和母亲在外面走路时，母亲指着月亮说“有人在月球上”，所以正确答案是和母亲散步时听到这个消息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有提到老师或课堂。
optionRationalesZh.b: 他说的是在外面和母亲走路，不是独自看电视。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这与原句完全一致，是第一位说话人最核心的记忆。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是第二位说话人的经历，不是第一位说话人的回忆。

public.prompt: Why does Kate mention that extra electricity was needed in the UK?

options.text: To criticize the quality of black-and-white television
To show how many people were watching the moon landing
To explain why some viewers missed the event
To compare British and American power systems

evidence.quote:
the electricity had
to provide extra power in order to cope with the extra demand.

private.explanationZh: Kate 提到电力部门不得不额外供电，不是在讲技术细节，而是在用一个非常具体的现象说明当时观看登月直播的人数之多，社会关注度非常高。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她没有借此批评黑白电视质量。
optionRationalesZh.b: 额外供电说明有大量观众同时收看，这正是这句话的作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这句话不是解释有人错过直播，而是强调观看热潮。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有比较英国和美国的电力系统。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Kate's final comment about the moon landing?

options.text: People now see the event as less important than before
The landing seems ordinary once its history is reviewed
The achievement remains remarkable because it was accomplished with limited technology
Another moon visit should wait until technology changes completely

evidence.quote:
when you stop to think about what a huge achievement it
was, especially for its time, it really takes your breath away.

private.explanationZh: Kate 在结尾强调，真正回头想这件事时，会意识到它在当时技术条件下有多么惊人，因此可以推断她认为登月至今仍然令人震撼，关键原因是“在当时的条件下完成了这件事”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她的语气是赞叹，不是说现在人们觉得它不重要。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文说 it really takes your breath away，明显不是普通平常的事。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这项准确转述了 especially for its time 的含义。
optionRationalesZh.d: Kate 没有主张暂停再次登月，这超出了原文。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090814-moon-landing-q01: 节目核心是回顾人们对登月的记忆，并反复强调其历史意义和技术条件下的巨大成就，不是在讲训练、停滞原因或科幻史。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090814-moon-landing-q02: 第一位说话者明确回忆自己在天黑后和母亲散步，母亲指着月亮说“有人在月球上”，与选项c完全对应。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090814-moon-landing-q03: Kate提到英国需要额外供电，是为了说明半夜仍有大量人起床看登月直播，突出收视规模之大。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090814-moon-landing-q04: Kate最后说，平时习以为常，但一想到在当年的技术条件下完成这件事，仍然令人震撼，所以可推知这一成就至今仍然非凡。

public.prompt: What overall point does the program make about clouds?

options.text: Why listeners should notice clouds more carefully and how clouds are understood
How satellites are the only reliable way to predict storms
Why Latin names have replaced modern weather study
How to become a professional meteorologist

evidence.quote:
wherever you are in the world, clouds are part of our
surrounding and they are constantly changing which in itself makes them
interesting to watch. But how much attention do we give them and do clouds
get the appreciation they deserve?

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就把重点放在“云是否得到应有欣赏”上，后面又介绍分类、命名和文化态度，因此主旨不是单纯的天气知识，而是引导听者重新认识云、并理解它们。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这项既包含“更关注云”的核心问题，也涵盖后文对云的分类和文化意义的说明。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有说卫星是唯一可靠方式，这完全不是核心内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 虽然提到拉丁名称，但那只是分类历史的一部分，不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目不是职业指导，也没有教人如何成为气象学家。

public.prompt: What does the program say about Luke Howard?

options.text: He was a professional meteorologist working for the government
He was an amateur meteorologist and a pharmacist by profession
He founded the Cloud Appreciation Society
He invented modern satellite cloud photography

evidence.quote:
He was an
amateur meteorologist and a pharmacist by profession.

private.explanationZh: 节目明确说明 Luke Howard 不是职业气象学家，而是把天气研究当作爱好、职业上是 pharmacist，所以 B 与原文完全一致。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文专门强调他不是 professional meteorologist，所以这项相反。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这项准确复述了原句。
optionRationalesZh.c: Cloud Appreciation Society 的创始人是 Gavin Pretor-Pinney，不是 Luke Howard。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有提到卫星摄影，这属于无根据添加。

public.prompt: Which type of cloud is identified as a sign of fair weather?

options.text: Stratus
Nimbus
Cirrus
Cumulus

evidence.quote: A cumulus cloud is the fair-weather cloud, the puffy cotton wool ones.

private.explanationZh: 第三段听力直接说明 cumulus cloud 就是 fair-weather cloud，因此答案是 cumulus。

optionRationalesZh.a: stratus 在后文被解释为均匀的灰云，不表示好天气。
optionRationalesZh.b: nimbus 被说成雨云或风暴云，更接近坏天气。
optionRationalesZh.c: cirrus 是又高又薄的云，节目没有说它象征 fair weather。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文直接说 A cumulus cloud is the fair-weather cloud，所以这是正确答案。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention the saying used in Iran and Iraq?

options.text: To prove that clouds signal rain in every culture
To show that attitudes toward clouds are not negative everywhere
To explain where the cloud naming system first developed
To argue that Arabic weather science is more accurate than British weather science

evidence.quote:
There's a Arabic phrase that they use in Iran and Iraq which is when someone is lucky or
blessed they say his sky is always filled with clouds.

private.explanationZh: 前面节目一直在谈英国文化里 cloud 的负面联想，随后举出伊朗和伊拉克的说法，是为了形成对比，说明不同文化对云的情感色彩并不一样。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这里不是在讨论降雨规律，而是在谈文化表达中的态度差异。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这正好概括了举例目的：别的文化里，云也可能象征幸运和祝福。
optionRationalesZh.c: 云的命名系统前面已经讲过，这里不是回到命名来源。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有比较哪种天气科学更准确，只是在比较文化联想。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090826-clouds-q01: 节目一方面鼓励听众更留意云，另一方面介绍云的分类、命名与文化态度，整体重点正是更认真地观察并理解云。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090826-clouds-q02: 录音明确说Luke Howard是amateur meteorologist，职业则是pharmacist，因此b与原文完全一致。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090826-clouds-q03: 第三段插音直接说“A cumulus cloud is the fair-weather cloud”，所以表示好天气的是cumulus。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090826-clouds-q04: 前面先说英国常把云和负面情绪联系起来，随后举Iran和Iraq的说法，是为了对比说明并非所有文化都把云看成负面象征。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the report?

options.text: How pet snakes are cared for in Bangladesh
Why hospitals in Dhaka are overcrowded
How snakebite treatment in Bangladesh could improve by combining local practice with modern methods
Why monsoon floods mainly damage transportation

evidence.quote:
Almost 700,000 people are bitten by venomous snakes there every year, and
6,000 people lose their lives. However, according to a new survey, only 3% of
cases are treated in hospital. So who's dealing with the remaining 97% and
how?

The doctors who conducted this survey said that many more Bangladeshis would survive
snake bites, if the country's traditional healers and snake charmers received training in modern
techniques. The vast majority are treated by mystic healers known as an Ojhas. By tradition,
these men are snake experts.

private.explanationZh: 节目先交代孟加拉国蛇咬伤问题非常严重，而且绝大多数病例不在医院处理；随后报告提出，如果传统治疗者接受现代技术训练，存活人数会更多。因此主题是如何改进治疗，而不是单纯介绍洪水或医院情况。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目讨论的是 venomous snake bites 和治疗体系，不是宠物蛇护理。
optionRationalesZh.b: 虽然提到只有 3% 在医院治疗，但重点不是医院拥挤。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这项把“问题规模”和“改进方向”两部分都概括出来，最符合主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: monsoon 只是后来解释危险为何上升的背景，不是整篇报告的中心。

public.prompt: Why do the survey doctors want traditional healers to receive modern training?

options.text: Because healers already treat most victims and are regarded as snake experts
Because hospitals refuse to admit most snakebite patients
Because healers have discovered an antidote unknown to doctors
Because modern medicine is unavailable during the dry season

evidence.quote:
The doctors who conducted this survey said that many more Bangladeshis would survive
snake bites, if the country's traditional healers and snake charmers received training in modern
techniques. The vast majority are treated by mystic healers known as an Ojhas. By tradition,
these men are snake experts.

private.explanationZh: 这里不是说传统治疗者天然比现代医生更有效，而是因为现实中“绝大多数”患者已经由这些人接触和处理，而且他们在当地传统里被视为 snake experts，所以给他们现代训练最有可能带来实际改善。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这项抓住了原文中的两个核心依据：他们处理的人最多，而且传统上就是蛇类专家。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有说医院拒收患者，只是说大多数病例没有在医院治疗。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有说传统治疗者掌握未知解毒剂。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有把原因归结为 dry season，反而强调 monsoon 更危险。

public.prompt: When are snake bites especially dangerous in Bangladesh?

options.text: During the hot, humid summer from March to June
During the monsoon season from June to October
During the mild winter from October to March
During the harvest season in late autumn

evidence.quote:
The most dangerous time of year is now, when during the annual monsoon, about half of
Bangladesh disappears under water, and both snakes and people are forced to move to dry
land.

private.explanationZh: 记者在第二段录音中直接说 most dangerous time of year 是 annual monsoon，因此正确答案是 monsoon season。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是主持人给出的一个选项，但录音没有选择它。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文直接说 during the annual monsoon 是最危险的时候。
optionRationalesZh.c: mild winter 只是干扰项，与原文不符。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有提到 harvest season。

public.prompt: According to the report, why do snake bites become more likely during the monsoon?

options.text: Snakes become more aggressive in cold weather
People spend more time farming at night
Floodwater pushes both snakes and people onto the same dry areas
Hospitals close during periods of heavy rain

evidence.quote:
Snakes, in the same way as people, are forced by rising
water to move to dry land, making their encounters more likely.

private.explanationZh: Kate 在总结时把因果链条说得很清楚：上涨的洪水把蛇和人都赶到干地上，因此相遇概率上升，蛇咬事件也更容易发生。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说冷天气让蛇更具攻击性，这也不是 monsoon 的重点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有讨论夜间务农行为。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这正是讲话者明确给出的原因，也是最准确的推断。
optionRationalesZh.d: 医院是否关闭并不是蛇咬事件增多的原因。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090903-bangladesh-snake-bites-q01: 节目焦点是孟加拉蛇咬伤问题，以及调查提出的改进方向：让传统治疗者结合现代医疗技术来提高生存率。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090903-bangladesh-snake-bites-q02: 报道说绝大多数患者由传统治疗者处理，而且这些人按传统被视为蛇类专家，因此医生希望他们接受现代训练以挽救更多生命。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090903-bangladesh-snake-bites-q03: 第二段录音直接说明最危险的时间是annual monsoon，因此答案是季风季节。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090903-bangladesh-snake-bites-q04: 记者解释说季风期大量地区被淹，蛇和人都被迫转移到干地，因此更容易相遇并发生咬伤。

public.prompt: What issue does the program explore through its discussion of hair colour?

options.text: How movies have changed stereotypes about hair colour
Whether red hair is linked to pain sensitivity and related behavior
Why most dental patients need less anaesthesia than before
How to change hair colour without damaging it

evidence.quote:
today we'll be talking about how your natural looks - and in particular your
hair colour - can tell others what kind of person you are - or at least reveal
some of your qualities.

private.explanationZh: 节目以“头发颜色能否透露某些特征”为切入点，随后把讨论集中到 redheads 对疼痛更敏感、也更可能回避看牙医这一研究上，所以核心问题是红发是否与疼痛相关特征有关。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有讨论电影中的刻板印象变化。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这项准确概括了从 hair colour 引出的核心研究主题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目说的是红发者可能需要更多麻醉，不是一般牙科患者的整体趋势。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目完全不是染发指南。

public.prompt: What did the study find that the red-haired group was more likely to avoid?

options.text: Undergoing surgery
Reading scientific reports
Visiting the dentist
Walking alone at night

evidence.quote:
They found that the red hair group were more
sensitive to pain and as a result were twice as likely to avoid visiting the dentist.

private.explanationZh: 第一段录音直接说 red hair group 因为更敏感于疼痛，所以 twice as likely to avoid visiting the dentist，因此答案是看牙医。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说他们更可能回避所有手术。
optionRationalesZh.b: scientific reports 从未被提到。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这与录音原句完全一致，是直接细节信息。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有把夜间行走和研究结果联系起来。

public.prompt: Why does Professor Sessler compare red hair with age and sex?

options.text: To show that those other visible traits do not predict anaesthetic requirement
To argue that older people should avoid dental treatment
To claim that women feel less pain than men
To prove that hair colour naturally changes with age

evidence.quote:
that tells you nothing
about their anaesthetic requirement. You see a man and a woman - that tells you nothing
about their anaesthetic requirement. But you see a redhead - aha!

private.explanationZh: 教授把年龄和性别放在一起比较，是为了突出反差：这些常见外部特征并不能告诉你一个人的 anaesthetic requirement，但 red hair 却可以提供线索。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这正是比较的作用：先排除年龄和性别，再突出 red hair 的预测价值。
optionRationalesZh.b: 教授没有建议老年人避免牙科治疗。
optionRationalesZh.c: 他说男女比较 tells you nothing，并没有断言女性更不怕痛。
optionRationalesZh.d: 头发颜色是否会随年龄变化不是这段论证的重点。

public.prompt: What conclusion does Rebecca draw after hearing the explanation?

options.text: Redheads are less sensitive to pain than other people
The age and sex comparisons are useful, but hair colour is not
Hair colour has no connection to anaesthetic needs
Redheads are more sensitive to pain than people with other hair colours

evidence.quote:
However, if you're a ginger head, you're
more sensitive to pain

private.explanationZh: Rebecca 在总结中明确指出，如果你是 ginger head，你会对疼痛更敏感。她是在把前面教授的解释转述成更直接的结论，因此答案是红发者更敏感。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这与 Rebecca 的总结正好相反。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她说前两种比较不能说明问题，而 hair colour 反而有关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目一直在说明 red hair 与 pain sensitivity/anaesthetic requirement 有联系。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这项准确转述了 Rebecca 的总结性结论。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090909-ginger-whingers-q01: 节目借发色讨论的核心问题，是红发是否与更强的疼痛敏感性以及由此产生的回避看牙等行为有关。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090909-ginger-whingers-q02: 第一段录音明确说红发组更怕痛，因此更可能avoid visiting the dentist，答案唯一明确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090909-ginger-whingers-q03: Professor Sessler拿年龄和性别作对比，是为了说明这些外在特征都不能告诉你麻醉需求，只有红发这一外显特征与之有关。
reasonsZh.bbc-20090909-ginger-whingers-q04: Rebecca总结说前两种比较都不能说明问题，但ginger head更敏感于疼痛，因此她得出的结论是红发者比其他发色的人更怕痛。

public.prompt: What is the main issue discussed in the program?

options.text: Whether a 13-year-old should be allowed to sail around the world alone
How young sailors can qualify for international races
Why Dutch schools encourage students to take ocean voyages
How social workers organize record-breaking competitions

evidence.quote:
Laura Dekker was planning to sail around the world
alone

They said such an adventure would not be
in her best interest at an age that's very important for her development.

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。节目一开始就围绕劳拉的单人环球航行计划以及政府是否应允许她这样做展开。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有讨论任何正式赛事资格或青年水手的参赛标准。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有提到荷兰学校鼓励学生参加远洋航行。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。社会工作者和当局介入是为了儿童保护，不是在组织破纪录活动。

public.prompt: Why does the reporter use the phrase "the new rat race to become a superchild"?

options.text: To praise parents who train children to become more independent
To suggest that some adults push children into intense record-seeking competition
To argue that sailing is the easiest way for teenagers to become famous
To show that psychologists oppose all kinds of childhood ambition

evidence.quote:
Psychologists say the new rat race to become a 'superchild', or the

private.explanationZh: 这里的表达并不是字面意义上的“赛跑”，而是在批评一种社会竞争心态：一些有野心的成年人可能推动孩子去争当“最年轻完成壮举的人”。这个短语的作用是强化家长驱动和破纪录冲动，而不是单纯赞美孩子有梦想。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里不是称赞家长培养独立，而是在提示过度推动的风险。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。紧接着的解释就说这种竞争可能被有野心的父母所助长。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说航海是最容易出名的方式，重点是争当最年轻纪录保持者。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。心理学家批评的是某种竞争趋势，不是反对所有儿童志向。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about Julie Thomas's view of the authorities' decision?

options.text: She thinks parents should always make the final decision without interference
She mainly objects because Laura lacks sailing experience
She believes society may need to overrule parents in order to protect a child
She is neutral and refuses to take any position on the case

evidence.quote: The difficulty is that as parents you can't always be the final arbiters in these things.

You
know, society does have a role in protecting children and making sure that they do really

private.explanationZh: Julie Thomas 先说父母并不总是这些事情的最终裁决者，又说社会有责任保护儿童并确保他们真正理解自己在做什么。这表明她总体上认可公共机构在儿童高风险事务中进行干预，因此更接近支持当局的做法。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她说的恰恰是父母“不总是”最终裁决者。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她讨论的是儿童保护与理解能力，不是劳拉的技术水平。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。她明确强调社会保护儿童的角色，这意味着必要时可以限制父母决定权。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。虽然她没有直接表态“我支持”，但她的原则性表述明显带有倾向。

public.prompt: According to the program, why did the Dutch authorities stop Laura's voyage for now?

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目提到船长八米多，但没有说船只长度违法。

reasonsZh.bbc-20090929-dutch-girl-s-sailing-dream-: 节目开头和后续讨论都围绕13岁的Laura是否应被允许独自驾船环游世界展开，其他选项都不是核心议题。
记者紧接着解释说，心理学家认为一些有野心的父母会助长孩子去争当最年轻完成艰巨壮举的人，这个短语是在批评这种竞逐。
Julie Thomas说父母并不总能做最终裁决者，社会也有保护儿童、确保他们真正明白自己在做什么的责任，可推知她支持在某些情况下由社会或当局凌驾于父母决定之上。
主持人明确说当局认为这次冒险在她这一重要成长阶段不符合她的最佳利益，因此暂时阻止航行。

To prepare listeners to follow the legal and social argument in the report

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: How the Internet was invented during the digital revolution

beginning to cause problems as more and more public services rely on their

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。数字革命只是背景介绍，不是节目主旨。

public.prompt: According to Ellen Helsper, how did non-users' expectations change from 2003 to the present?

options.text: The share expecting to go online soon rose from about 20% to about 40%
The share stayed roughly the same at about 40%
The share dropped from about 40% to almost zero
The share expecting to go online soon fell from about 40% to about 20%

evidence.quote:
in 2003 about 40% of people who weren't using

private.explanationZh: 这是一道明确数字信息题。Ellen Helsper 说，2003年大约40%的非用户表示可能会在下一年开始使用互联网，而现在这一比例降到了大约20%。这说明非用户转向上网的意愿明显减弱。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文趋势正好相反，是下降不是上升。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文明确说从40%降到20%，并非保持不变。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有说接近零，而是“大约20%”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。两个数字与原文完全一致。

public.prompt: What do the reasons in Extract 2 suggest about many current non-users?

options.text: Lack of interest and perceived relevance is becoming a bigger barrier than access alone
Most of them are eager to go online as soon as prices fall slightly
They are mainly prevented by government restrictions on web access
They understand the internet well but reject it only for political reasons

it's just not relevant to my life , I

private.explanationZh: 受访者强调，越来越重要的理由不是“接不上”或“太贵”，而是“我不感兴趣”“这和我的生活无关”。这说明很多当前非用户面对的主要障碍，正在从技术或经济问题转向主观兴趣与相关性感知。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两处证据都指向态度和相关性问题在上升。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文恰恰表明他们并不是急着上网。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有提到政府对网络接入的限制。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文说的是兴趣和生活相关性，不是政治原因。

Because future citizens need equal access to information and choice, not just services
Because internet use is the only way to communicate with friends
Because anyone offline will immediately lose all public benefits

private.explanationZh: 她的论点超越了“办事方便”这一层。她认为未来社会中的公民，如果不在线，就无法和他人一样获得信息与选择机会。因此上网关系到社会参与和机会公平，而不仅仅是使用公共服务。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说政府打算用网站取代所有书籍。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文明确说重点不只是公共服务，而是平等的信息和选择机会。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。与朋友联系只是前面主持人举的例子，不是这里的核心论证。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她没有说不上网就会立刻失去所有公共福利，这种说法过于绝对。

reasonsZh.bbc-20091001-the-digital-divide-q01: 节目先定义digital divide，再讨论越来越多服务转到网上后这类差距为何成问题，因此核心是网络接入和使用差距的重要性。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091001-the-digital-divide-q02: Ellen Helsper明确说，2003年约40%的非网民说自己可能会在下一年上网，而现在降到约20%。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091001-the-digital-divide-q03: 第二段说现在更重要的原因已不是“没法接入”或“太贵”，而是“不感兴趣”“与我的生活无关”，说明障碍正更多转向兴趣和相关性感知。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091001-the-digital-divide-q04: Martha Lane Fox说，未来若不在线就无法成为社会中的“proper citizen”，而且她强调的不只是公共服务，而是与别人一样获得信息和选择。

public.prompt: Why does the correspondent describe the event with sports terms such as "lane one," "lane two," and "sprint"?

To criticize sports reporting for exaggerating simple news stories

To turn the technology comparison into a vivid race that is easier to imagine

public.prompt: What was Winston carrying during the race?

public.prompt: What broader point does the race result make about broadband in South Africa?

So the aim of the competition was simple: to discover which would be the
fastest to deliver a large amount of data from one office to another one, 60
miles or nearly 100 kilometres away: a supposedly fast broadband system - or a
carrier pigeon.

private.explanationZh: Kate 的总结非常清楚：这场比赛就是比较，在相距约60英里的两间办公室之间，究竟是宽带系统还是信鸽能更快传送大量数据。其他选项都把目的改成训练、海上测试或广告宣传，偏离原文。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说要训练鸽子取代办公室员工。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。比赛是在两间办公室之间，不是海上长途测试。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。与主持人的概括完全一致。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有把这场比赛说成商业广告。

reasonsZh.bbc-20091014-pigeon-vs-web-q01: 前面主持人就提醒听众注意记者如何使用体育隐喻让报道更有色彩，所以这些词的作用是把技术比较描写成一场生动易懂的比赛。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091014-pigeon-vs-web-q02: 记者明确说Winston腿上绑着一个四GB的memory stick。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091014-pigeon-vs-web-q03: 报道直接说这一结果反映了南非乃至整个非洲宽带的状况，因为鸽子轻松取胜，而那时互联网传送的数据只到了4%。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091014-pigeon-vs-web-q04: Kate总结比赛目的就是比较在两间办公室之间传送大量数据时，宽带和信鸽哪一个更快。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the program?

options.text: To explain why people overspend and introduce a first step for controlling that behavior
To compare luxury brands that appeal most strongly to shoppers
To argue that all borrowing from banks should be banned
To teach listeners how to become professional financial advisers

evidence.quote:
In today's programme, we're going to try to understand why
we spend money, our emotional response to it and the first step in how to
control it.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头直接说明目标：理解人为什么花钱、花钱时的情绪反应，以及控制这种行为的第一步。因此主旨是解释过度消费的心理动因，并给出初步应对方法，而不是品牌比较或专业理财训练。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。主持人明确交代了这就是本期节目的任务。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目不是时尚品牌比较节目。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。虽然提到债务文化，但并没有主张全面禁止借贷。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目提供的是面向普通听众的行为建议，不是职业培训。

public.prompt: Why did Helen keep buying expensive things she could not afford?

options.text: Because she was investing in objects that would certainly rise in value
Because shopping temporarily helped her manage low mood and low self-esteem
Because her job required her to own luxury items for work
Because she wanted to prove that advertisements never influenced her

evidence.quote:
There seemed to be a correlation between how down I was feeling and how much money I
would spend.
My shopping was a way of controlling my mood.

I thought that if I bought the same stuff
as they were buying, then I would have that same happy, successful, beautiful feeling.

private.explanationZh: Helen 直接把自己的消费与情绪低落联系起来，说购物是在“控制情绪”，同时又相信买到那些成功、漂亮的人拥有的东西后，自己也会获得同样感觉。这说明她反复购买昂贵物品，主要是为了短暂缓解低落情绪和低自尊，而不是理性消费。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她没有把这些物品当作升值投资，后来反而对自己的消费感到恶心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。多处证据都指向购物在短时间内起到情绪补偿作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目提到她失业后问题更严重，并不是工作要求她购买奢侈品。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她恰恰承认自己受到了外界理想化形象的影响。

public.prompt: Why does Dr Graham Lawlor use the word "leakage" when talking about personal spending?

options.text: To suggest that banks are secretly removing cash from shoppers' accounts
To argue that shopping addiction is mainly caused by broken payment systems
To emphasize that money can disappear little by little without people noticing unless they track it
To show that people should stop buying anything except essentials

evidence.quote:
He uses the word leakage which means something which we lose or
which disappears without us really noticing.

you literally go out with a piece of paper and a pencil and
you write down everything and that way you can track the leakage.

private.explanationZh: “Leakage”这个词带有“慢慢漏掉”的形象感。节目先解释它是指在我们不太注意时失去的东西，随后医生建议把每一笔支出都写下来，才能追踪这种流失。这个词的修辞作用，是强调金钱会在不知不觉中一点点消失，必须通过记录才能看见。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有暗示银行在偷偷扣钱。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里讨论的是消费习惯和记录方法，不是支付系统故障。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。定义和建议共同说明，这个词是为了突出隐形、持续的小额流失。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。医生建议的是记录与分类支出，而不是停止所有非必要购买。

public.prompt: According to the program, what percentage of the population are shopaholics?

evidence.quote: Thankfully the answer is actually 10%

private.explanationZh: 节目结尾核对答案时明确说，正确数字其实是10%。这是一道结尾处的直接信息题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。1%不是节目给出的答案。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。25%从未在节目中出现。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。50%是嘉宾先前的猜测，不是正确答案。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。主持人明确说答案是10%。

reasonsZh.bbc-20091015-shopaholics-q01: 节目开头就说要理解人们为何花钱、花钱的情绪反应，以及控制这种行为的第一步，因此主旨是解释过度消费并给出初步控制办法。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091015-shopaholics-q02: Helen自己说花钱多少与她情绪低落有关，购物是控制情绪的方式，而且她的自尊心很低，所以正确原因是购物暂时缓解低落和低自尊。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091015-shopaholics-q03: 医生先解释leakage是我们在没注意时流失的东西，再建议把每笔开销记下来去追踪，可见这个词强调钱会一点点不知不觉地流走。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091015-shopaholics-q04: 节目末尾主持人核对答案时明确说shopaholics约占人口的10%。

public.prompt: What central issue are the speakers discussing in this programme?

options.text: How the prefix e- changed English vocabulary over time
How cyber-attacks could seriously damage societies that rely on online networks
Why hospitals should stop using digital systems
How governments can replace public services with online versions

evidence.quote:
the next
world war could take place online.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。节目开头把“电子战争”定义为一种可能在线上发生、而且破坏力极大的冲突，因此讨论重点不是词汇本身，而是社会对网络依赖所带来的安全风险。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。前缀 e- 的解释只是引入话题，不是整段节目的核心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了节目围绕网络攻击风险及其社会后果展开的讨论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目强调的是网络中断的危险，不是主张医院停止数字化。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有讨论政府把服务迁移上网的政策利弊。

public.prompt: According to Dr Toure, what is one possible consequence of being cut off from the network?

options.text: Schools would close permanently
Online criminals would become impossible to trace
Natural disasters would become less damaging
Some patients in hospitals would die

evidence.quote: there are some patients that will die in the hospital.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。Dr Toure 用医院病人的例子说明，一旦网络中断，后果并不是抽象的不方便，而可能直接危及生命。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。原文没有说学校会永久关闭。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文提到的是网络犯罪的追踪措施，不是说断网后无法追踪。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。海啸只是用来类比破坏力，不是说自然灾害会减轻。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原文明确说医院里有些病人会死亡。

public.prompt: Why does Rebecca mention that individual users can be tracked through internet addresses?

options.text: To suggest that privacy is no longer important online
To compare cybercrime with transportation problems
To give an example of a safeguard already used against online crime
To show that cybercrime is easy to eliminate completely

evidence.quote:
it's already possible to track individual users through
internet addresses, including those suspected of committing online crimes.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查修辞目的。Rebecca 先说已经存在一些 safeguards，然后马上举出通过网络地址追踪嫌疑人的做法，作用是举例说明现有防护措施。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。后文恰恰强调不能侵犯隐私。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这里与交通或 e-transportation 毫无关系。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。该信息是作为“已有 safeguard”的具体例子出现的。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。原文没有说网络犯罪因此就容易被彻底消除。

public.prompt: What principle does Dr Toure suggest should guide efforts to fight cybercrime?

options.text: Security should protect people without violating privacy or blocking access to information
Governments should reduce ordinary internet use as much as possible
Schools and hospitals should disconnect from public networks entirely
Online communication should be limited during emergencies

evidence.quote:
protecting people in cyberspace does not mean invading their privacy,
does not mean denying them that basic human right of access to information.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。Dr Toure 不是提出某个技术细节，而是在说明治理原则：网络安全不能以侵犯隐私和剥夺信息获取权为代价。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这是对原句原则性要求的准确概括。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文没有主张普遍减少互联网使用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有提出学校和医院要完全断开公共网络。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。原文谈的是权利边界，不是紧急状态下的临时限网。

reasonsZh.bbc-20091119-e-wars-q01: 节目开头明确说明主题是“e-wars”，并解释网络攻击可能像自然灾害一样严重，重点是高度依赖网络的社会会因此受重创。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091119-e-wars-q02: Dr Toure 直接说如果今天网络被切断，“some patients ... will die in the hospital”，对应医院里的部分病人会死亡。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091119-e-wars-q03: Rebecca 在回答“如何保护自己免受网络攻击”时举例说可以通过网络地址追踪用户，这是在说明现有防护措施中的一个具体例子。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091119-e-wars-q04: Dr Toure 强调网络安全措施不能侵犯隐私，也不能剥夺获取信息的基本权利，所以原则是兼顾安全、隐私和信息获取。

public.prompt: What does the programme mainly explain about synesthesia?

options.text: Why children gradually develop the five senses at different speeds
How unusual tastes are normally caused by illness
How a condition that mixes the senses affects one person's experience
Why musicians often connect color with emotion

evidence.quote:
This is the name
we give a condition that some people have where their senses are mixed up
or confused.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。节目先定义联觉，再通过 James 的个人经历说明这种感官混合如何具体表现和影响生活，因此主旨是介绍这种现象及其体验。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目不是在讲儿童感官发展。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这里不是关于疾病导致的异常味觉。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它同时概括了“感官混合”这一概念和 James 的亲身体验。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。音乐与颜色只是联觉的一个例子，不是整段节目的中心。

public.prompt: In James Wannerton's example, what does the word "coffin" taste like to him?

options.text: Fresh lemon
A hard-boiled sweet
Jelly and chocolate
A bitter medicine

evidence.quote: coffin for example tastes like a sweet, a hard-boiled sweet.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。James 直接举例说，coffin 这个词在他那里会对应一种硬糖的味道，用来说明联觉体验的任意性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。lemon 是前面说明联觉时用的别的例子。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文明确说是 a hard-boiled sweet。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。jelly and chocolate 出现在后面谈听别人慢慢说话时。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。原文没有说是苦药味。

public.prompt: Why is it hard for James to describe some of his taste experiences to other people?

options.text: Because many of the sensations do not fit clear, familiar food categories
Because he quickly forgets which sounds produced them
