Because other people refuse to believe that synesthesia exists
Because the same word gives him a different taste every day

evidence.quote: I get a lot of tastes that I can't articulate as foodstuffs.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。James 说很多味道无法被他表达成某种 foodstuff，说明这些感受并不能轻易归入常见食物类别，所以很难让别人理解。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这是对 cannot articulate as foodstuffs 的合理推断。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文没有说他会忘记对应的声音。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。难点在于感受本身难以归类，不在于别人是否相信。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。前文反而说每个声音都会保持同样的味道。

public.prompt: Why does James mention people who speak slowly?

options.text: To show that he prefers short conversations
To argue that slow speech creates more accurate taste sensations
To suggest that most people with synesthesia dislike conversation
To illustrate how synesthesia can interfere with understanding speech

evidence.quote:
I have a problem with people who speak slowly. It's just the more words that
go in, the more taste sensations I get

private.explanationZh: 本题考查修辞目的。提到“说得慢的人”不是为了表达社交偏好，而是为了举例说明：进入耳中的词越多，他感到的味觉越多，这会妨碍他专心听内容。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。原文不是在说他偏好简短交谈。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文强调的是 distracting，而不是更准确。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有把这一点推广到“大多数联觉者”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个例子正是为了说明联觉如何干扰听觉理解。

reasonsZh.bbc-20091130-synesthesia-q01: 节目主要借 James Wannerton 的例子解释联觉这种感官混合的状况，以及这种状况如何影响他的日常体验。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091130-synesthesia-q02: 原文明确说“coffin ... tastes like a sweet, a hard-boiled sweet”，答案唯一对应硬糖。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091130-synesthesia-q03: James 说很多味觉体验无法“articulate as foodstuffs”，也就是难以归入别人熟悉的食物类别，因此不容易描述给别人听。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091130-synesthesia-q04: 他提到别人说话慢时，进入的词更多，会不断带来味觉感受，导致他难以专心理解内容，这个例子是为了说明联觉会妨碍听懂讲话。

public.prompt: At the start of the programme, how does Kate contrast diary-keeping today?

options.text: Most people now keep diaries only for school assignments
Published diaries have replaced private ones almost completely
People no longer keep handwritten diaries
Some people still write privately by hand, while others share diary-like writing online

evidence.quote:
still handwrite in a private notebook while others blog on the Internet for all
to see.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。Kate 开头明确对比了当代写日记的两种方式：有人仍然私下手写，也有人把类似日记的内容公开发到网上。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目没有说如今主要为了学校作业写日记。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。Kate 说的是两种形式并存，不是前者被后者取代。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。原文明确说 some still handwrite。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这正是节目开头给出的对比。

public.prompt: Why does Gyles say he records things in a diary to "pin them down"?

options.text: Because writing helps make passing experiences feel more real and fixed
Because he wants to prepare his diaries for publication immediately
Because he often forgets major historical events
Because a diary is the best way to reassure other people

evidence.quote:
It's like sort of looking in the mirror to reassure yourself that you're there. And it does make
things seem more real.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。Gyles 说写日记会让事情“更真实”，又说不写的话一切都会飞快流逝，因此 pin them down 的意思是把稍纵即逝的经历固定下来。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它把 make things seem more real 和 pin them down 的含义结合起来了。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文没有说他写日记是为了立刻出版。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。这里谈的是日常经历流逝，不是忘记重大历史事件。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。原文说的是 reassure yourself，不是安慰别人。

public.prompt: Why does Gyles describe re-reading his old diaries as traumatic?

options.text: Because he realized his diaries contained too little domestic detail
Because he discovered that he had written inaccurate accounts
Because looking back forced him to confront missed opportunities
Because the diaries reminded him of how difficult publishing can be

evidence.quote:
I've been keeping a daily diary since I was 11 years of age but before now, I've not looked
back on it and it's actually been quite a difficult experience, you know, seeing the …you
know, the missed opportunities - so it's been quite traumatic.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。Gyles 把痛苦感和 missed opportunities 直接联系起来，说明重读旧日记之所以令人难受，是因为它让他重新看到过去本可以做却没有做的事情。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。原文没有把痛苦归因于 domestic detail 太少。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有说他发现内容失真。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。missed opportunities 是他觉得 traumatic 的核心原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。这里与出版过程无关。

public.prompt: Why does Gyles call a diary "a kind of time capsule"?

options.text: To suggest that diaries should focus only on famous historical events
To emphasize that ordinary domestic details can become valuable records later
To show that diaries are mainly useful to professional historians
To argue that diaries should be hidden until long after the writer dies

evidence.quote:
It is a kind of time capsule and in a sense the best diaries I think are ones that give
you that domestic detail and not necessarily also about the great events of history.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查修辞目的。Gyles 用 time capsule 这个比喻，是为了强调日记能保存一个时代的生活痕迹，尤其是那些看似普通的家庭和日常细节，过后反而更有价值。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。他反而说最好的日记不一定主要写重大历史事件。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个比喻正是为了突出日常细节在后来阅读中的价值。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有把日记限定为给历史学家使用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。time capsule 是比喻保存价值，不是要求必须等作者去世后再看。

reasonsZh.bbc-20091203-dear-diary-q01: Kate 开头对比如今写日记的两种方式：有人仍然手写私人日记本，也有人把类似日记的内容发到网上公开分享。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091203-dear-diary-q02: Gyles 说不写的话一切都会匆匆流逝，写下来能让事情更真实，并把它们固定下来，因此“pin them down”指把流逝的经历记录得更具体可感。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091203-dear-diary-q03: 他明确说回看旧日记时会看到“missed opportunities”，这种对错失机会的回顾让他感到痛苦和创伤。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091203-dear-diary-q04: 他把日记比作时间胶囊，是为了说明日记里那些家庭日常细节虽然当下普通，但过后会成为有价值的记录。

public.prompt: What overall topic do Jackie and Neil introduce at the beginning of the programme?

options.text: How formality at work is shown and how workplace norms have changed
Why radio presenters deserve more respect than television presenters
How employees can negotiate higher salaries
Why restaurants often ban jeans in Britain

evidence.quote: topic of today’s programme is formality, or lack of formality at work.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。节目一开始就点明主题是职场中的正式与非正式，以及相关行为规范，因此后面的称呼、着装和办公室规则都服务于这个主题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了整段节目讨论的核心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。新闻播报员的着装只是后文的一个例子。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有讨论薪资谈判。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。餐馆 dress code 只是解释词义时的举例。

public.prompt: When Carrie first joined the BBC, how did she have to address her boss?

options.text: By his first name, Eric
As sir in every exchange
As Mr Bowman rather than by his first name
By job title only, without using a name

evidence.quote: you had to call him ‘Mr’, so he was ‘Mr Bowman’.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。Carrie 明确说当时必须叫老板 Mr Bowman，而不是直接叫 Eric，这体现出当时工作场合更强的等级和正式感。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。她明确说自己不会直接叫 Eric。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。sir 是节目开头的玩笑式称呼，不是 Carrie 当年的规定。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文直接给出了这种称呼方式。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有说只能用职位称呼。

public.prompt: Why does Carrie describe a department where staff had to ask permission to go to the toilet and were sometimes timed?

options.text: To explain why she immediately decided to leave the BBC
To show that bathroom rules are usually the biggest issue in offices
To argue that strict control made offices more efficient
To illustrate how rigid workplace rules could be in the past

evidence.quote:
ask permission if they wanted to go to the toilet. They weren’t
allowed to just leave the office. And in fact, she timed them sometimes too

private.explanationZh: 本题考查修辞目的。这个极端例子不是为了讨论上厕所本身，而是为了让听者具体感受到过去某些办公室规则有多严格、多缺乏灵活性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。原文没有说她因此立刻离职。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。厕所规定只是用来说明管理风格，不是整段节目的重点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有称赞这种严格控制提高了效率。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个例子正是为了突出过去职场的刻板和严苛。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the survey result that 85 per cent would prefer a precise dress code?

options.text: Most workers want to dress formally every day
Many workers prefer clear guidance about what is acceptable to wear
Only a small minority care about workplace clothing rules
Employees generally want to wear dinner jackets to work

evidence.quote: 85 per cent would prefer a precise dress code

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。很多人希望有 precise dress code，说明他们更想得到明确标准，而不是在模糊规则里自己猜什么算合适。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。偏好明确规则不等于偏好每天正式着装。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这是对调查结果最合理的概括。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。85% 不是少数。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。dinner jacket 只是节目里提到的旧例，不代表普遍偏好。

reasonsZh.bbc-20091210-mr-smith-or-john-q01: 节目一开始就说主题是工作中的正式与非正式，以及办公室互动和着装规范如何体现这种变化。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091210-mr-smith-or-john-q02: Carrie 明确说刚进 BBC 时必须称呼老板为“Mr Bowman”，而不能叫他的名字 Eric。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091210-mr-smith-or-john-q03: Carrie 讲述上厕所都要请示、还会被计时的经历，是为了举例说明过去工作场所的规章曾经多么严格僵硬。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091210-mr-smith-or-john-q04: 85%的人希望有明确着装规范，说明很多员工更想得到清楚的穿着指导，知道什么才算合适。

public.prompt: What broader point does the programme make about TV singing talent shows?

options.text: They mainly train singers for long careers in experimental music
They attract people with the promise of fame, even though many careers fade quickly afterward
They are losing popularity because viewers dislike competitive formats
They succeed only in countries with weak local music industries

evidence.quote:
aspiring pop stars or performers try to impress judges with their musical talent
in the hope of getting a record deal, fame and fortune.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。节目一方面解释人们为何被这类节目吸引，另一方面指出许多参赛者赛后只会短暂走红，因此 B 最能概括全段的核心观点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目没有说这类节目培养长期的实验音乐生涯。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它同时覆盖了“追求成名”和“成名短暂”这两层重点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目并没有说观众正在失去兴趣。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有把成功归因于某些国家的音乐产业薄弱。

public.prompt: According to Pete Waterman, what quality helped Simon Cowell build the show into a major format?

options.text: His willingness to promote only unusual music
His patience with weak performers
His lack of interest in commercial success
His strong drive to seize opportunities and build success

evidence.quote:
He's driven. He sees an
opportunity and he's built it into the world's biggest format.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。Pete Waterman 说 Simon Cowell is driven，并进一步解释他会看到机会并把它发展成全球性 format，因此关键品质是强烈的进取心。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。前文反而说节目里的音乐很 mainstream。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。Simon Cowell 以刻薄著称，不是以耐心著称。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。原文没有说他对商业成功不感兴趣。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。is driven 和 sees an opportunity 都支持这个概括。

public.prompt: Why does Pete Waterman say, "Artistic ambition! Give me a break - it's about being famous!"?

options.text: To reject the idea that the show is mainly motivated by creativity
To praise Simon Cowell for encouraging original musicians
To explain why albums usually outsell singles after the programme ends
To show that fame always damages musical quality

evidence.quote: Artistic ambition! Give me a break - it's about being famous!

private.explanationZh: 本题考查修辞目的。这句话是对“也许这是出于艺术追求”的直接反驳，强调推动这类节目的核心动力并不是创造性，而是成名。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了这句强烈反驳的话所起的作用。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这里不是在赞扬原创性支持。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。这句话与单曲或专辑销量无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。原文并没有提出 fame 一定会伤害音乐质量这一普遍结论。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about many artists who come through these shows?

options.text: They usually leave the music industry before releasing any songs
They tend to become more successful with each later album
They often enjoy only a brief period of success before interest declines
They normally build long and fulfilling careers after the show

evidence.quote:
they have a bright moment following the show perhaps 2 or 3 successful singles or a
sucessful initial album and the story kind of starts to wane

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。George Ergatoudis 说这些歌手往往只有一个 bright moment，之后热度开始 wane，因此可以推断他们的成功通常比较短暂。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。原文说他们可能会有几首成功单曲或一张成功专辑。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有说后续专辑会越来越成功。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这是对 bright moment 后 interest starts to wane 的准确概括。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。原文明确否定了 long or fulfilling careers。

reasonsZh.bbc-20091217-almost-famous-q01: 节目整体意思是这类选秀节目用成名机会吸引大量人参加，但很多参赛者后续事业并不持久，很快就会降温。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091217-almost-famous-q02: Pete Waterman 说 Simon Cowell“driven”，看到机会就把节目做成世界级模式，说明关键品质是强烈的进取心和抓住机会的能力。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091217-almost-famous-q03: 他说“Artistic ambition! Give me a break - it's about being famous!”是在否定节目主要出于艺术创造这一说法，强调核心驱动力是成名。
reasonsZh.bbc-20091217-almost-famous-q04: 节目后段明确说很多从节目中出来的艺人只有短暂高光，可能有几首成功单曲或一张专辑，之后公众兴趣就开始减弱。

public.prompt: What contrast about credit cards do the speakers emphasize early in the program?

options.text: They are convenient to use, but they can also lead people into debt.
They are difficult to use, but they protect people from overspending.
They are mainly valuable because they eliminate interest charges.
They are less practical than cash, but they are safer for daily spending.

evidence.quote:
it's often more
convenient than carrying cash.

credit cards can
encourage you to spend more than you can really afford, and you could fall
into debt.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始先说明信用卡比携带现金更方便，随后马上补充说它也可能让人花超过自己负担能力的钱，最后陷入债务。因此说话者强调的是“便利”和“负债风险”并存的对比，而不是单方面称赞或否定信用卡。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两段证据分别对应“更方便”和“可能陷入债务”，正好构成节目早期强调的核心对比。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有说信用卡难用，反而明确说它更方便。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目说银行会通过利息获利，所以并不是消除利息费用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有说信用卡比现金更不实用；相反，先强调的是它的便利性。

public.prompt: According to the program, how do banks make money from credit cards?

options.text: By charging customers each time they carry cash.
By charging interest on debts that have not been paid off.
By selling cash machines to other banks.
By requiring travelers to buy special insurance.

evidence.quote:
Banks make profit from credit cards by charging interest each month on the
unpaid debts.

private.explanationZh: 原文直接说明银行通过对未偿还债务按月收取利息来从信用卡业务中获利，因此答案是收取利息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。文中没有说银行靠“每次取现收费”作为这里的主要答案。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句直接给出银行盈利方式，就是对未还清的债务收取利息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目提到ATM的发展，但没有说银行通过出售ATM赚钱。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有提到旅行保险这一点。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention the introduction of cash machines after discussing plastic money?

options.text: To argue that cash machines made credit cards unnecessary.
To show that banks preferred machines to customers.
To show that people getting used to cards helped bring about another banking change.
To prove that plastic money was first invented in London.

evidence.quote:
People were slowly getting used to plastic money, which would eventually be crucial to
another banking revolution: cash machines.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到现金机，不是为了转移话题，而是为了说明人们逐渐接受“塑料货币”之后，才为后续银行业的另一场变革铺平道路。也就是说，信用卡和ATM的发展在逻辑上是连着的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。原文没有说ATM让信用卡变得没必要，反而强调两者发展有关联。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这里不是在比较银行更喜欢机器还是顾客。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据句明确说人们适应塑料货币最终对另一场银行革命，也就是现金机，至关重要。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。原文说的是ATM首先安装在伦敦，但并没有说塑料货币是先在伦敦发明的。

public.prompt: Why does Dan tell the story about Frank McNamara forgetting his wallet?

options.text: To show that restaurant meals were unusually expensive in the 1940s.
To suggest that his wife created the first credit card.
To explain why banks began installing ATMs.
To illustrate one account of how the idea for a credit card emerged.

evidence.quote:
So he was so embarrassed by this that he decided, with his fellow diners, to set
up the Diners' Club credit card, so that you could pay for meals without
needing any money.

private.explanationZh: Dan 讲这个故事，是为了说明信用卡概念是如何被想出来的：一次忘带钱包、付不了饭钱的尴尬经历，促成了后来为吃饭付款而设立信用卡的想法。这是“起源说明”，不是一般性的趣闻。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。故事重点不是餐费高，而是他忘了钱包。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。妻子只是送钱来，并不是她发明了信用卡。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。ATM的发展在前面另一部分讨论，这个故事不是解释ATM。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据句明确说明这次尴尬经历如何促成了 Diners' Club 信用卡的设立。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100203-credit-cards-q01: 节目开头明确对比了信用卡的两面性：一方面“more convenient than carrying cash”，另一方面会让人“spend more than you can really afford”并“fall into debt”，所以A最准确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100203-credit-cards-q02: Dan直接说明银行如何从信用卡获利：对“unpaid debts”每月收取“interest”，因此答案是B。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100203-credit-cards-q03: 原文说人们逐渐习惯“plastic money”，这最终对另一场银行业变革即自动取款机“would eventually be crucial”，所以提到ATM是为了说明对卡片的适应推动了另一项变革，选C。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100203-credit-cards-q04: Dan讲McNamara忘带钱包的故事，是在解释信用卡想法如何“first emerged”。他还补充说这只是一个流传的故事，但用途明显是说明信用卡观念的起源，因此D正确。

public.prompt: What problem is emphasized when the speakers discuss plastic pollution in the seas?

options.text: It causes large numbers of marine animals to die each year.
It mainly affects shipping schedules in the Pacific.
It has already been solved in countries with recycling programs.
It is limited to waste dumped directly from fishing boats.

evidence.quote:
Now plastic pollution in the seas kills over a million sea birds and 100,000
mammals and turtles each year.

private.explanationZh: 这里突出强调的是塑料污染对海洋生物造成的大规模伤害。原文用具体数字说明，每年有大量海鸟、哺乳动物和海龟因此死亡，所以重点是生态危害，而不是运输或管理问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据句直接给出每年死亡的海鸟、哺乳动物和海龟数量。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有讨论航运时刻表。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目最后只是鼓励改进，并没有说问题已经解决。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目明确说很多塑料来自陆地，不只是渔船直接丢弃的垃圾。

public.prompt: According to the ecologist, what share of plastic produced each year is used for packaging?

evidence.quote:
40% of
those are packaging materials that are mainly used once and then discarded.

private.explanationZh: 材料中明确说每年生产的塑料里有40%是包装材料，而且这些包装大多只用一次就被丢弃，所以答案是40%。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。10%明显低于原文数字。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。25%不是节目给出的比例。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据句直接给出40%。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。60%高于原文所说的比例。

public.prompt: Why does Kate react strongly to the comparison with France?

options.text: To show that she doubts the garbage patch exists at all.
To underline how enormous the garbage patch may be.
To argue that France should help clean the Pacific.
To change the topic from ocean waste to European geography.

evidence.quote: What? Twice the size of France? That’s absolutely huge!

private.explanationZh: Kate 的强烈反应是为了突出这个垃圾带可能大得惊人。把它和法国面积作比较后，她立刻说“太大了”，说明这个比较的作用是强调规模，而不是怀疑事实或转移话题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。她表达的是震惊于规模，不是否认其存在。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。她的反应直接强化了“面积非常大”这一信息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。这里没有讨论法国应承担什么责任。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。比较法国只是帮助听众理解规模，不是转向地理话题。

public.prompt: What overall solution do the speakers encourage at the end of the program?

options.text: Ban all sea travel until ocean pollution decreases.
Move plastic waste from the oceans to the land.
Focus only on cleaning up fishing equipment.
Reduce waste, use less packaging, and recycle more.

evidence.quote:
if we all try and reduce waste, and use less packaging, and
recycle more, then maybe things will get better.

private.explanationZh: 节目结尾给出的总体应对思路很明确：减少废弃物、减少包装、增加回收。这不是单一措施，而是一组面向源头和使用习惯的综合做法。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目没有提出停止海上运输。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。把垃圾从海里搬到陆地并不是节目提出的总体方案。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目不只关注渔具，还讨论包装和整体塑料使用方式。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据句完整概括了结尾倡导的解决方向。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100211-plastic-pollution-q01: 节目明确强调海洋塑料污染每年会杀死“一百多万只海鸟”和“十万只哺乳动物和海龟”，核心问题就是大量海洋动物死亡，因此选A。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100211-plastic-pollution-q02: 生态学家Richard Thompson明确说每年生产的塑料中“40% of those are packaging materials”，所以答案是C。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100211-plastic-pollution-q03: Kate对“twice the size of France”的反应是“absolutely huge”并说“very frightening”，作用是强调垃圾带可能极其巨大，因此B最符合。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100211-plastic-pollution-q04: 结尾Dan总结说如果大家都“reduce waste, use less packaging, and recycle more”，情况也许会改善，所以节目鼓励的整体解决办法就是D。

public.prompt: What main issue does the program examine?

options.text: How pension payments should be calculated each month.
Whether compulsory retirement should continue as people live longer.
Why younger workers prefer part-time jobs.
How to replace private companies with state employers.

evidence.quote:
there's increasing discussion over whether it's
right to have a compulsory retirement age.

private.explanationZh: 节目核心不是养老金计算，也不是青年就业，而是围绕“强制退休年龄是否还合理”展开讨论。由于人们寿命延长，这一制度是否应继续存在成为讨论重点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目虽然解释了 pension，但重点不是月度计算方式。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句直接点出正在讨论的核心问题就是是否应保留强制退休年龄。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有把重点放在年轻人的兼职偏好上。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目也没有讨论用国家雇主取代私营公司。

public.prompt: Why does Andrea Murray mention an extra 18 months of work and £15 billion?

options.text: To support the case for later retirement with an economic argument.
To show that retirement mainly benefits private banks.
To argue that pensions should be completely abolished.
To prove that most people already work much longer than expected.

evidence.quote:
if we extended people's working lives by 18
months they calculate would add £15 billion in to the economy.

private.explanationZh: 这里给出“多工作18个月”和“给经济增加150亿英镑”这组数字，是为了证明延后退休不仅是观念问题，也有明确的经济收益，因此是在为支持更晚退休提供论据。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。数字被用来说明延长工作年限会给经济带来明显好处。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这里谈的是整体经济收益，不是私人银行受益。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有主张彻底取消养老金。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。她说的是如果延长工作年限会怎样，不是说大多数人已经这么做了。

public.prompt: According to the program, why might continuing to work improve people’s health?

options.text: Because employers usually provide free medical treatment after age 65.
Because working longer guarantees a shorter commute and less stress.
Because continued engagement and job satisfaction can support well-being.
Because retirement automatically causes people to lose their pensions.

evidence.quote:
they
have greater job satisfaction and better health if they continue working and continue
being engaged.

private.explanationZh: 节目把“继续工作”和“持续投入”联系到更高的工作满足感与更好的健康状况，因此可以推断，保持参与感和目标感被视为有助于身心状态。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目没有提到免费医疗。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有说继续工作会保证更短通勤或更低压力。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据句把 job satisfaction、better health 和 continue being engaged 直接联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。养老金问题不是这一健康论点的依据。

public.prompt: Why does Kate mention people who look forward to retirement?

options.text: To suggest that most people dislike their jobs.
To argue that the economy cannot support older workers.
To show that pension rules are identical worldwide.
To acknowledge that working longer is not attractive to everyone.

evidence.quote:
But of course there are people
who look forward to retirement their entire working lives.

private.explanationZh: 在前面讨论延后退休的好处之后，Kate 补充说有些人一生都在期待退休，这一作用是平衡论述，承认“继续工作更久”并不符合所有人的愿望。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。她没有说“大多数人”都讨厌工作。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这里不是经济承受能力问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有在这里比较全球统一的养老金制度。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这句话的功能就是提出另一面，说明并非人人都想延后退休。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100215-retirement-q01: 整期节目围绕“是否应该取消强制退休年龄”展开，因为人们寿命更长，反复讨论“compulsory retirement should be scrapped”，所以主问题是B。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100215-retirement-q02: Andrea Murray提到多工作18个月可为英国经济增加£15 billion，这是在用经济收益支持延后退休的观点，因此选A。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100215-retirement-q03: 她说继续工作的人会有“greater job satisfaction and better health”并持续“being engaged”，说明健康改善与持续投入和工作满足感有关，所以C正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100215-retirement-q04: Kate后面补充说有些人一生都期待退休，想放松、运动、看书，这一段是在承认并非所有人都觉得延后工作有吸引力，因此D最合适。

public.prompt: What is the main goal of the Clink restaurant project as described in the program?

options.text: To entertain prison staff with luxury meals.
To raise money to build more prisons.
To train prisoners for work and reduce the chance that they will commit crimes again.
To persuade the public that prison food should be more expensive.

evidence.quote:
The idea is that they can use these skills to help them get jobs after they leave
prison.

This is
about reducing the risk of crime when people are released and allowing people who have
made a personal commitment to do it, to change their lives.

private.explanationZh: 项目目标不是单纯经营一家特别的餐厅，而是通过培训囚犯烹饪和服务技能，帮助他们出狱后找到工作，并进一步降低再次犯罪的风险。两段证据合在一起说明了“就业训练 + 减少再犯”这一核心目的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。虽然餐厅也服务工作人员和受邀者，但这不是项目的主要目标。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有说该项目是为了筹建更多监狱。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。前一段讲就业技能，后一段讲减少犯罪风险，两者共同构成项目主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有把重点放在提高餐费或改变公众对价格的看法上。

public.prompt: Why does Callum say the name “Clink” is a good fit for the restaurant?

options.text: Because it sounds like the name of a famous chef.
Because it refers to prison slang while also sounding like a stylish restaurant name.
Because it is the official legal term for a prison dining hall.
Because it reminds listeners of a chain of seaside restaurants.

evidence.quote:
‘clink’ is a slang
name in English for a prison. But it also has that kind of a posh name that you
might get for a restaurant in London – ‘Clink’.

private.explanationZh: Callum 提到这个名字，一方面因为 clink 是“监狱”的俚语，和地点相符；另一方面它听起来又像伦敦某种时髦餐厅的名字，因此这个名字同时传达了“监狱”和“餐厅”两层意味。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目没有说这是某位名厨的名字。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句明确给出双重原因：既是监狱俚语，又有高档餐厅的感觉。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。它不是正式法律术语，而是 slang。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有提到海边连锁餐厅。

public.prompt: What kind of preparation do prisoners receive in the project?

options.text: Training in cooking and hospitality, using local ingredients and prison-grown vegetables.
Training in law and court reporting for public trials.
Training in banking so they can manage prison budgets.
Training in architecture so they can redesign jail buildings.

evidence.quote:
The prisoners receive training in cooking and hospitality, and cook meals
using local ingredients and vegetables grown inside the jail.

private.explanationZh: 原文直接说明囚犯接受的是烹饪和 hospitality 方面的训练，并且使用本地食材和监狱内种植的蔬菜做饭，因此答案是与餐饮服务相关的职业准备。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据句完整说明了训练内容和食材来源。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有提到法律或庭审报道训练。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有提到银行或预算管理。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有提到建筑设计训练。

public.prompt: According to Peter Dawson, how can the project reduce crime?

options.text: By keeping prisoners inside for longer periods of time.
By making prison life more comfortable than ordinary life.
By preventing the public from visiting prisons.
By helping committed prisoners change their lives after release.

evidence.quote:
This is
about reducing the risk of crime when people are released and allowing people who have
made a personal commitment to do it, to change their lives.

private.explanationZh: Peter Dawson 的意思是，这个项目并不是单纯“对囚犯好”，而是要帮助那些愿意改变的人在出狱后走上不同的人生道路，从而降低重新犯罪的风险。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。这里不是靠延长刑期来减少犯罪。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文明确说这不是为了单纯对人好或让监狱更舒服。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。是否让公众参观不是他的论点核心。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据句把“释放后减少犯罪风险”和“改变人生”直接联系起来。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100225-prison-restaurants-q01: 节目说明该项目让囚犯接受烹饪和服务培训，目的是帮助他们出狱后找工作，并“reduce the risk of crime”，所以主目标是C。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100225-prison-restaurants-q02: Callum明确说“clink”既是英语里监狱的俚语，又像一个时髦高档餐厅的名字，所以这个名字合适的原因是B。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100225-prison-restaurants-q03: 原文直接说囚犯接受“training in cooking and hospitality”，并用本地食材和监狱里种的蔬菜做饭，因此A是准确复述。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100225-prison-restaurants-q04: Peter Dawson说该项目通过 resettlement 帮助那些真正下决心的人“change their lives”，从而降低再犯罪风险，所以D正确。

public.prompt: Why does Dan ask Kate to review the trunk, branches, and leaves at the start of the program?

options.text: To reconnect listeners with last week’s tree-climbing vocabulary before moving on.
To prove that Kate is a professional botanist.
To compare British trees with Swedish trees.
To introduce a quiz about forest pollution.

evidence.quote: The trunk is the main body of the tree, the branches are the tree's arms and the leaves are the flat green parts at the end of the branches.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始先回顾 trunk、branches 和 leaves 的含义，是因为这是“tree climbing programme”的第二部分。说话者先帮听众接上上周内容，再进入这一期新的树木案例和记录。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。回顾基础词汇的作用是承接上周内容，帮助听众进入本期讨论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这里不是为了证明 Kate 的专业资格。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。此处还没有开始比较不同国家的树。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目主题不是森林污染测验。

public.prompt: How tall does Tony Russell say the General Sherman tree is?

options.text: About 100 feet tall.
About 275 feet tall.
About 955 feet tall.
About 36 feet tall.

evidence.quote: In height, it's about 275 feet tall.

private.explanationZh: Tony Russell 在介绍 General Sherman tree 时直接给出高度，大约是275英尺，因此答案非常明确。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。100英尺接近树干周长相关数字，不是高度。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句直接给出 275 feet tall。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。955 与后面树龄 9,550 年有关，不是高度。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。36 英尺是直径相关数据，不是高度。

public.prompt: Why does James choose to rig his hammock where he does?

options.text: So that he can stay hidden from traffic below.
So that he can measure the diameter of the tree more easily.
So that he can catch the last rays of sunlight while camping there.
So that he can move closer to the oldest tree in the forest.

evidence.quote: So if I rig my hammock here, I should catch the last rays of sun.

private.explanationZh: James 选择在那个位置挂吊床，是因为他就在树冠上方，能够接住最后一缕阳光。也就是说，这个位置的好处与露营时的光照有关，而不是测量树木或躲避交通。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。虽然他后来听得到车声，但挂吊床的直接原因不是隐藏自己。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这里不是为了测量树的直径。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据句直接说明这样做是为了 catch the last rays of sun。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有说他在接近最古老的树。

public.prompt: Why does Dan ask Kate about the largest tree in the world near the end of the program?

options.text: To correct the earlier claim about how tall the General Sherman tree is.
To give another example showing that a tree can be remarkable for size in a different way.
To argue that Sweden has fewer impressive trees than Mexico.
To explain why professional climbers prefer shorter trees.

evidence.quote: But Kate, could you tell us about the largest tree in the world? Well, the largest tree by volume, including all its trunk, branches and leaves, is called El Tule and can be found in Mexico.

private.explanationZh: 在节目结尾，Dan 让 Kate 介绍世界上最大的树，并不是为了推翻前面关于最高树或最老树的信息，而是为了再补充一个“树木之最”的例子。这样节目就把“最高”“最老”“最大”这几种不同的突出特征并列起来，帮助听众从不同角度理解树木规模可以如何被描述。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。这里没有纠正前面关于 General Sherman 高度的数据，前面的高度信息已经讲清楚了。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。结尾提到 El Tule，是为了补充另一个关于树木规模的例子，说明树不仅可以按高度或年龄出名，也可以按体积显得惊人。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有把瑞典和墨西哥放在竞争关系里比较，更没有说瑞典的树不如墨西哥令人印象深刻。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。这里讨论的是树木记录和尺寸特点，不是职业攀树者为什么偏好矮树。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100303-tree-climbing-part-2-q01: Dan一开始说上周讨论过爬树并学过树的各部分词汇，然后请Kate快速回顾，这是在帮助听众衔接上一期内容再继续新内容，所以选A。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100303-tree-climbing-part-2-q02: Tony Russell明确说General Sherman tree“is about 275 feet tall”，因此答案是B。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100303-tree-climbing-part-2-q03: James说如果把吊床架在这里，“I should catch the last rays of sun”，所以他选择那个位置是为了在露营时接住最后的阳光，选C。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100303-tree-climbing-part-2-q04: 节目先谈最高的树，临近结尾又谈“largest tree by volume”，这是为了再举一个例子，说明树的“惊人”也可以体现在不同的尺寸维度上，不只是高度，因此B最符合。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: A Copenhagen hotel offers meals for pedal-generated electricity mainly to promote energy awareness.
Denmark is replacing hotel solar panels with bicycles to meet national energy targets.
The speakers debate whether tourists in Copenhagen should cycle instead of eating out.
Most of Denmark's electricity is now being produced by guests at urban hotels.

evidence.quote:
Well a hotel in
Copenhagen has started offering guests a free meal if they make electricity for
the hotel by cycling.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就点出核心内容：哥本哈根一家酒店让客人骑车发电来换取免费餐食。后文又说明这样做的重点不是发很多电，而是让人思考能源使用，所以主旨应概括为“以餐食奖励骑车发电，并传达节能意识”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时概括了酒店的做法和后文说明的环保意识目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目只说酒店外面也有太阳能板，没有说全国酒店要用自行车取代太阳能。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。两位主持人并没有讨论“是否应当少吃饭多骑车”，重点是介绍这个酒店方案。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。报道明确说每位骑车者只能产生很少电力，不可能构成丹麦大部分电力来源。

public.prompt: According to the report, about how long must an average guest cycle to qualify for a main course?

options.text: About 10 minutes
About 15 minutes
About 30 minutes
About 60 minutes

evidence.quote:
roughly fifteen minutes of
cycling for someone of average fitness, to qualify for a main course.

private.explanationZh: 记者直接给出数据：普通体能的人大约骑十五分钟，就能达到获得主菜所需的发电量。这是一道定位细节题，答案来自明确信息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。十分钟比原文所说更短，原文没有给出这个数字。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文明确说大约十五分钟。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。三十分钟把时间翻倍了，不符合报道内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。一小时明显过长，和原文信息不符。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers mention that each cyclist produces only enough electricity to power a couple of light bulbs?

options.text: To suggest that the hotel's bicycles are poorly designed
To compare bicycle power with the hotel's solar panels
To emphasize that the scheme is mainly about energy awareness, not large-scale electricity production
To show that most guests refuse to take part in the project

evidence.quote:
the point is to encourage guests to think about their consumption of
energy.

private.explanationZh: 这里先说“只能点亮几个灯泡”，随后马上解释真正的重点是让客人思考自己的能源消耗。因此这个细节的作用不是抱怨发电太少，而是揭示方案背后的教育性、提醒性目的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有批评设备设计差，而是在解释项目目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。太阳能板是后面补充的绿色措施，这里不是在做技术对比。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。该句直接说明目的在于促使客人反思能源消耗。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有说大多数客人拒绝参与。

public.prompt: What broader context does the final report segment provide for the hotel's cycling idea?

options.text: It suggests Denmark relies on tourists to finance wind farms.
It shows the hotel copied an unpopular government policy.
It argues bicycle use matters more than every other environmental measure.
It connects the hotel project to Denmark's wider cycle-friendly and renewable-energy culture.

evidence.quote:
heavy investment in renewable energy, including wind farms which generate a fifth of the
country's electricity.

private.explanationZh: 结尾把酒店活动放回丹麦的大背景中：丹麦本身就重视绿色议题、骑行文化和可再生能源投资。由此可推断，这家酒店的做法不是孤立噱头，而是和更广泛的社会环境相呼应。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文说的是丹麦投资风电，不是依赖游客出资。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。没有提到该政策“不受欢迎”，也没有说酒店是在照搬政府规定。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。结尾是在提供背景，并未宣称骑车比所有环保措施都更重要。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。风电投资和骑行友好城市的信息共同构成了这一推断。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100420-cycling-for-food-q01: 节目核心围绕哥本哈根一家酒店让住客骑车发电换取免费餐食，并明确说明重点是促使客人思考能源消耗，因此a最符合主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100420-cycling-for-food-q02: 报道原话说平均体能的人大约骑十五分钟就能获得主菜，所以答案是b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100420-cycling-for-food-q03: 提到“只能点亮几只灯泡”是为了说明这项活动并不是为了大量发电，而是为了提高住客的能源意识，所以选c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100420-cycling-for-food-q04: 最后一段把酒店做法放到丹麦更广泛的环保背景中，强调其自行车友好文化和风能投资，因此d最准确。

public.prompt: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.text: Methods scientists use to count insects in rainforests
The biological richness of Borneo's rainforest and some unusual species found there
Reasons tourists prefer listening to rainforests at night
A debate about whether orangutans should live in protected areas

evidence.quote:
Today we're going to be talking about the rainforest and in particular the
rainforest in Borneo.

private.explanationZh: 开头先限定话题是婆罗洲雨林，后文展开的重点是这里极高的生物多样性以及新发现的奇特物种，因此主旨不是旅游体验，也不是单一动物保护，而是整体的生态丰富性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有聚焦“如何统计昆虫”的方法。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了地点、主题和后文的发现内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。夜间录音只是帮助说明雨林体验，不是节目主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目提到猩猩等动物，但不是围绕是否应居住在保护区展开辩论。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers include Kaz's rainforest recording?

options.text: To prove that clouded leopards are active at night
To show that the rainforest usually becomes silent after dark
To illustrate that people in the rainforest often hear animals before seeing them
To compare natural sounds with human-made sounds in Borneo

evidence.quote: In the rainforest you tend to hear more than you see.

private.explanationZh: Kaz在播放录音前先说，在雨林里往往是“先听到，后看到”。录音的作用就是把这个抽象描述变成可感知的例子，让听者体会雨林环境的“声音先于视觉”的特点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。录音并不是为了证明云豹夜行，更没有点出具体就是云豹。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。录音恰恰说明雨林并不安静，而是充满声音。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。录音服务于前面那句概括，说明听觉在线索获取中的重要性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里没有做人类噪音和自然声音的对比。

public.prompt: What is unusual about the frog David Norman mentions?

options.text: It has no lungs and breathes through its skin.
It can fly between trees without wings.
It changes color to resemble leaves.
It lives only in rivers outside the rainforest.

evidence.quote:
There's a frog, for example, as well, that has no lungs, this is extraordinary. This is a creature
that has evolved to breathe entirely through its skin

private.explanationZh: 这是报道里最突出的奇特生物之一。原文明确说这种青蛙没有肺，而是完全通过皮肤呼吸，所以答案必须同时抓住“没有肺”和“通过皮肤呼吸”两个点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两个关键特征都和原文一致。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说这只青蛙会飞。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。变色伪装没有被提到。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文并未说它生活在雨林外的河流中。

public.prompt: What does the final statistic about 10 million species and 1.7 million discovered suggest?

options.text: Scientists have almost finished cataloguing life on Earth.
Most undiscovered species probably live only in the oceans.
The Borneo discoveries are not very important compared with global research.
A large share of Earth's species remains undocumented, so places like Borneo can still reveal major finds.

evidence.quote:
There are about 10 million species perhaps on the planet and we've only discovered 1.7
million of those.

private.explanationZh: 结尾给出的全球统计不是单纯重复数字，而是让听者推出一个更大的结论：人类已知物种只占一部分，因此像婆罗洲这样的高多样性地区仍然可能带来重要新发现。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。已发现170万、总数约1000万，显然离“快完成”很远。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有把未发现物种限定在海洋。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。恰恰相反，这个统计强化了婆罗洲发现的重要性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它是对结尾数字最合理的概括性推断。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100504-secrets-of-the-rainforest-q: 节目主要讨论婆罗洲雨林的生物多样性，以及那里发现的一些罕见和奇特物种，所以b最贴切。
Kaz在播放录音前后都说明在雨林里往往先听到动物再看到动物，录音是用来具体展示这一点，因此选c。
原文明确说这种青蛙没有肺，完全通过皮肤呼吸，所以答案是a。
结尾说地球上可能有一千万种物种，但只发现了一百七十万种，说明仍有大量物种未被记录，因此像婆罗洲这样的地方仍可能有重大发现，选d。

public.prompt: Why is the Hollywood sign the central topic of the program?

options.text: Because the program explains how Hollywood movies are funded by property companies
Because the program argues it was originally designed as a tourist map
Because the program discusses why it is seen as an important symbol worth preserving
Because the program compares its design with famous Paris landmarks

evidence.quote:
Even though the Hollywood sign is so famous, it was in danger of being
demolished as there was a plan for a property company to build luxury homes
on the site.

private.explanationZh: 节目不是泛泛谈好莱坞电影，而是围绕“好莱坞标志为何值得被拯救”展开。开头先交代它差点被拆除，后面再说明它为何具有象征意义，因此主线是标志的文化价值与保护。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。地产公司只是造成拆除风险的背景，不是节目主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说这个标志最初是用来给游客指路。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它概括了节目围绕的核心问题：这一标志为何重要且值得保存。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。巴黎只通过埃菲尔铁塔类比出现，不涉及设计来源。

public.prompt: Which landmark does Hugh Hefner compare the Hollywood sign to?

options.text: The Statue of Liberty
The Eiffel Tower
Tower Bridge
Big Ben

evidence.quote: I made the comment early on back in '78 that it was our Eiffel Tower.

private.explanationZh: Hefner用“我们的埃菲尔铁塔”来说明好莱坞标志具有国家级、国际化的象征意义。这是一个直接细节题，关键是听出被类比的地标名称。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。自由女神像没有在这段比较中出现。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文明确提到 Eiffel Tower。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Tower Bridge 是Kaz后来举的英国例子，不是Hefner的比较对象。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。Big Ben 也是Kaz举的英国地标例子。

public.prompt: Why does Kaz mention Tower Bridge, the Houses of Parliament, and Big Ben?

options.text: To show that Britain plans to copy Hollywood's preservation campaign
To offer examples of landmarks whose significance goes beyond tourism and helps explain the idea of an icon
To argue that British landmarks are more famous than the Hollywood sign
To correct Hugh Hefner's comparison with the Eiffel Tower

evidence.quote: In London we have Tower Bridge.

private.explanationZh: Kaz提到英国地标，不是为了转换话题，而是为了帮助听者理解“iconic”这个概念：某些地标不只是景点，还承载文化认同和象征意义。这个例子起的是说明作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她没有说英国要模仿拯救好莱坞标志的行动。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。举英国例子是为了具体化“象征性地标”这一概念。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。她不是在做谁更有名的竞争比较。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她是在补充类似例子，不是在反驳Hefner。

public.prompt: According to Hugh Hefner, why does Hollywood represent the best of America?

options.text: Because government agencies use films to explain national policy
Because Hollywood attracts more tourists than any other American landmark
Because movies express an immigrant dream and have shaped people's hopes across generations
Because film studios are America's oldest public institutions

evidence.quote:
the best
of what we are comes essentially not from government and essentially comes from the movies.
It comes from Hollywood. It's an immigrant dream.

private.explanationZh: Hefner把“美国最好的部分”归于电影和好莱坞，而不是政府；他进一步说这是“移民的梦想”。据此可推断，他看重的是电影长期塑造希望、可能性和美国梦的能力。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Hefner明确说最好的美国形象并非主要来自政府。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里谈的是象征意义和梦想，不是游客数量。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它综合了“来自电影”“移民梦想”“影响世代”这几层含义。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文没有谈到电影公司是不是最古老的公共机构。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100506-sign-of-the-times-q01: 节目围绕好莱坞标志为何被视为值得保护的重要象征展开，包括拆除危机、筹款和其象征意义，因此主旨是c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100506-sign-of-the-times-q02: Hugh Hefner明确说过这个标志是“our Eiffel Tower”，所以答案是b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100506-sign-of-the-times-q03: Kaz举出伦敦地标，是为了帮助解释“icon”不仅是旅游景点，还承载更深的象征意义，因此b最符合说话目的。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100506-sign-of-the-times-q04: Hefner说美国最好的一面主要来自电影和好莱坞，它体现了移民之梦，并影响了一代又一代人的希望，所以应选c。

public.prompt: What aspect of Loch Ness are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.text: Scientific proof that a monster lives in Loch Ness
The history and continuing mystery of the Loch Ness Monster legend
Weather vocabulary used by people in northern Scotland
A travel guide to the deepest lakes in Europe

evidence.quote:
today's programme is all
about what is perhaps the most famous mystery associated with Scotland.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接把节目定位为苏格兰最著名的“谜团”之一，后面再追溯传说历史、报道传播过程和警方反应，所以主题应概括为尼斯湖水怪传说的历史与持续神秘性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有提供科学证据来证明水怪真实存在。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它最完整地概括了“传说历史 + 未解之谜”的双重重点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。dreich 只是开场小问题，不是节目主体。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目并不是旅游指南。

public.prompt: According to Kate's summary, since when has the modern Loch Ness legend developed?

options.text: Since more than a thousand years ago
Since the 1930s
Since the police letter of 1938
Since tourists began visiting Scotland after World War II

evidence.quote:
But it's really been
since the 1930s that the modern legend has developed.

private.explanationZh: Kate把“传说起源很久以前”和“现代传说的形成”区分开了。题目问的是 modern legend，因此要抓住她总结中的时间点：从20世纪30年代开始形成。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。那对应的是更早的传说起源，不是“现代传说”的形成。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。Kate明确说现代版本自1930年代发展起来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。1938年的信件是后续事件，不是现代传说开始的时间。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有提到二战后旅游兴起导致传说形成。

public.prompt: Why does Callum point out that even the police thought the monster was real in the 1930s?

options.text: To show that earlier belief in the monster was stronger than his own present-day skepticism
To prove that the police later captured the monster
To explain why Scottish newspapers stopped reporting sightings
To suggest that legends are usually created by government officials

evidence.quote: But back in the 1930s even the police thought it was real.

private.explanationZh: Callum前一句才说自己觉得这更像神话、是给游客看的传说，接着补充“连警方当时都当真”，其作用是形成对比：过去社会对水怪的相信程度比他现在的怀疑态度强得多。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个信息主要用于突出历史上信念之强，与当下怀疑形成反差。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说警方抓到过水怪。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。也没有讨论报纸为何停止报道。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里不是在概括“传说通常由政府制造”。

public.prompt: What does the report about the hunting party and the 1938 letter suggest?

options.text: Authorities mainly wanted to promote tourism by inventing a threat.
Some officials treated the creature as real enough to deserve protection.
The police believed the hunting party had already killed the creature.
The monster was considered dangerous because it attacked boats.

evidence.quote:
a hunting
party - led by Peter Kent from London - who planned to tackle the monster with a harpoon
gun.
creature should be preserved

which means it should be
protected.

private.explanationZh: 一方面，有人带着鱼叉枪来猎捕水怪；另一方面，警方信中又说这个“生物应该被保存/保护”。把这两点合起来，最合理的推断就是：至少有些官员把它当成可能真实存在、因而值得保护的生物，而不只是笑谈。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说当局编造威胁来发展旅游。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。猎捕威胁与“应当保护”共同支持这一推断。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。信件表达的是担忧和保护，不是说猎人已经成功杀死它。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。报道没有说它袭击船只。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100512-the-loch-ness-monster-q01: 节目主要讨论尼斯湖水怪传说的起源、传播以及其至今仍带有神秘色彩，所以b概括最完整。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100512-the-loch-ness-monster-q02: Kate总结说现代版本的传说是从20世纪30年代发展起来的，因此答案是b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100512-the-loch-ness-monster-q03: Callum先表达自己如今更倾向把它看作神话和旅游传说，再说30年代连警察都当真，是为了突出过去的相信程度比他现在的怀疑更强，所以选a。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100512-the-loch-ness-monster-q04: 报道说警长担心猎人用鱼叉枪猎杀怪物，并在1938年的信中说这种生物应被保护，说明一些官员当时认真到认为它值得保护，因此选b。

public.prompt: What issue about Robin Hood does the program mainly emphasize?

options.text: How many Robin Hood films have been made since 1908
Why Russell Crowe thinks traditional green-tights portrayals are unrealistic and wants a more dignified Robin Hood
Whether Sherwood Forest is an accurate filming location
Why medieval soldiers preferred lightweight clothing in battle

evidence.quote:
The latest film version has just been released. It stars Russell Crowe as Robin
Hood and he wasn't happy with this image of Robin Hood dressed in green
tights.

private.explanationZh: 节目虽然以Robin Hood为题，但核心讨论集中在新版电影如何改变传统形象。开头就把焦点放在Russell Crowe不认同“绿色紧身裤”这一旧印象，因此主旨是新版对角色形象的重新塑造。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目提到许多影视版本和首部电影年份，但这不是主线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了节目围绕的冲突：旧形象不够真实、有尊严，新版要重塑。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目并没有重点讨论拍摄地点是否准确。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。主持人并未展开中世纪服装史讨论。

public.prompt: What does Russell Crowe say he wanted to give Robin Hood?

options.text: A stronger accent
A larger band of followers
A more famous costume
Some dignity

evidence.quote:
to give the guy some
dignity

private.explanationZh: Kate在复述时直接引用了Crowe的说法：他想“给这个人一些尊严”。这道题考查的是对核心措辞的准确捕捉。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说要给他更强的口音。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。也没有提到扩大 Merry Men 的人数。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Crowe恰恰不满意传统服装形象，而不是想让服装更出名。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原文明确说 wanted to give the guy some dignity。

public.prompt: Why does Crowe mention gorse bushes and brambles?

options.text: To explain why Sherwood Forest is difficult for camera crews to enter
To humorously show that tights would be impractical in a rough forest battle
To argue that Robin Hood should avoid fighting outdoors
To suggest old Robin Hood stories were based on botany research

evidence.quote:
The proximity and number of gorse bushes and brambles and everything.
Tights just don't seem to be a practical thing to wear.

private.explanationZh: Crowe提到这些带刺植物，不是为了讲植物知识，而是用一种半开玩笑的方式证明：穿紧身裤在树林里打斗很不实际。这个例子强化了他对旧版Robin Hood造型的不满。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。摄影团队进入森林的难度不是这里的讨论点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。植物例子服务于“紧身裤不实用”的幽默论证。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Crowe不是反对户外作战，而是反对不合适的服装。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里完全不是植物学研究。

public.prompt: What contrast do the speakers draw between older movie versions of Robin Hood and Crowe's version?

options.text: Older versions ignore Robin Hood's connection to Nottingham, but Crowe restores it.
Older versions focus on archery, whereas Crowe focuses only on sword fighting.
Older versions make Robin Hood seem comic or unserious, whereas Crowe wants him to seem gritty and respectable.
Older versions are historically accurate, whereas Crowe openly rejects history.

evidence.quote:
The image
from other movies doesn't seem to take the character seriously, it doesn't give
him enough respect.
got a ladder got a ladder again

private.explanationZh: Kate明确说旧电影里的形象“不够认真对待这个角色”，缺少尊重；而Crowe则想把他演得更 gritty、更 realistic。结尾重放“袜子又破了”的玩笑，也进一步强化了旧形象偏滑稽、缺乏战士气质的对比。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目并没有把差异放在是否恢复诺丁汉背景上。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。也没有形成“只谈弓箭 vs 只谈剑术”的对比。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它综合了“旧版偏滑稽/不够严肃”和“新版更硬朗、更有尊严”两层意思。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有说旧版更符合历史，也没有说Crowe拒绝历史。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100519-robin-hood-q01: 节目重点是Russell Crowe为何认为传统绿色紧身裤形象不现实，并希望把Robin Hood塑造成更有尊严、更写实的人物，所以选b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100519-robin-hood-q02: Russell Crowe原话就是他想“give the guy some dignity”，因此答案是d。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100519-robin-hood-q03: 他提到金雀花丛和荆棘，是在开玩笑说明穿紧身裤穿越森林打仗很不 practical，很容易被刮破，所以选b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100519-robin-hood-q04: Kate解释旧电影里的形象不够严肃，甚至有点滑稽，而Crowe想把他演得更 gritty、更真实、更受尊重，因此应选c。

public.prompt: Why does Callum describe a movie scene in which a cigarette is thrown into a pool of petrol?

options.text: To show that films often present physics differently from real life.
To argue that smoking is the main cause of petrol-station explosions.
To introduce a new film written by Professor Perkowitz.
To explain how gasoline can be handled safely in emergencies.

evidence.quote:
Well it's just one example of how the laws of physics are slightly different in
the movies.

private.explanationZh: Callum先讲电影里香烟扔进汽油池就会爆炸的桥段，是为了用一个听众容易想象的例子引出本期核心：电影中的科学规律常常和现实不一样。这个例子承担的是引题和说明作用，不是在讲吸烟危害或安全操作。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据直接说这是“电影里物理规律与现实不同”的一个例子。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目重点不是吸烟导致事故，而是电影与现实的科学差异。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Professor Perkowitz后面才出现，这里不是在介绍他写的电影。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。主持人还提醒不要自己尝试，因此这里不是安全指南。

public.prompt: What is Professor Perkowitz's view of science in films?

options.text: Every scientific detail in a film must be completely accurate.
Films should balance scientific accuracy with imagination.
Fantasy should replace science whenever a film wants excitement.
Most scientists agree that any inaccuracy ruins a film.

evidence.quote: Ideally I think should have some of each mixed in, in good proportions.

private.explanationZh: Perkowitz主张事实与幻想都应该有，而且要“按合适比例混合”。这说明他并不要求电影每一点都绝对准确，也不赞成完全抛开科学，而是支持准确性与想象力之间的平衡。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。他后面明确说并不是每一点科学内容都必须完全准确。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。mixed in good proportions就是两者平衡搭配的意思。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。他没有说幻想应取代科学，而是说两者都要保留。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。他还指出并不是所有科学家都同意他，因此不存在“多数人一致认为”。

public.prompt: According to Professor Perkowitz, what was scientifically wrong with The Day After Tomorrow?

options.text: It focused on space travel instead of climate change.
It denied that global warming could affect the real world.
It made the effects of global warming happen too dramatically and too quickly.
It avoided raising any public discussion about environmental issues.

evidence.quote:
It hyped-up the effects,
it made things happen more dramatically and more quickly than they really would

private.explanationZh: 题目问的是这部电影在科学上错在哪里。证据明确说它把全球变暖的影响夸大了，让事情发生得更戏剧化、也更快。所以错误不在主题本身，而在对后果速度和程度的夸张呈现。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。The Day After Tomorrow谈的是气候变化，不是太空旅行。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说它否认全球变暖，反而说影片就是关于全球变暖。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据逐字说明了问题：效果被夸大，而且发展速度过快。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。Professor Perkowitz还说这部电影引发了讨论，所以并非“没有引起讨论”。

public.prompt: What point does Callum make when he says he still enjoys The Day After Tomorrow even though some of it is "absolute nonsense"?

options.text: He only enjoys films that other scientists have approved.
Scientific mistakes always make movies more educational.
He thinks the film is completely realistic despite what others say.
A film can still be entertaining even if some of its science is inaccurate.

evidence.quote:
I have to say I really like that film and I always watch it when it's on. I know
some it is absolute nonsense but still, you know, I do enjoy it.

private.explanationZh: Callum一方面承认这部片有些内容“完全是胡说”，另一方面又说自己仍然很喜欢看。这说明节目此处强调的是：科学并不完全准确，并不妨碍一部电影具有观赏性，这也呼应了前面“允许一定灵活性”的讨论。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说他是根据科学家是否认可来决定喜不喜欢。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Callum谈的是娱乐性，不是教育价值。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。他明说some of it is absolute nonsense，不认为它完全真实。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。前半句承认不科学，后半句仍说喜欢，推出的就是“仍然好看”。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100527-movie-science-q01: 节目先举“香烟扔进汽油里电影里会爆炸、现实里却不会”的例子，紧接着说明电影中的物理规律常与现实不同，因此a最符合。其余选项都不是该例子的作用。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100527-movie-science-q02: Perkowitz明确说理想情况是事实与幻想“some of each mixed in, in good proportions”，并且认为可以有一定灵活性，所以应选“在科学准确性与想象之间保持平衡”的b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100527-movie-science-q03: 他谈到《The Day After Tomorrow》时说影片把全球变暖的影响表现得“more dramatically and more quickly than they really would”，即过于戏剧化且发生过快，所以c正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100527-movie-science-q04: Callum说虽然影片有些内容“absolute nonsense”，但他仍然很喜欢看，说明即使科学并不准确，电影仍然可以好看、有娱乐性，所以选d。

public.prompt: Why does the programme begin with the phrase "mirror, signal, manoeuvre"?

options.text: To introduce driving lessons by referring to a routine all drivers are taught.
To compare British driving rules with Japanese traffic laws.
To show that Kate dislikes memorising any kind of instructions.
To explain the cause of the vehicle-travel phobia mentioned later.

evidence.quote: my driving instructor made me learn off by heart.

They are
something that every driver is trained in.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始提到“mirror, signal, manoeuvre”，不是为了考交通法规细节，而是借一个典型驾驶口令把听众带入“学开车”这个主题。Kate说这是教练要求背熟的内容，Callum又补充说每个司机都会接受这方面训练，所以这个开头的作用是引入话题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两段证据一起表明这是一套驾驶训练中的常规步骤，适合作为节目开场。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里没有进行英国和日本交通规则的比较。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Kate只是回忆学车经历，并没有说自己讨厌记忆所有指令。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。关于phobia的内容只是节目小测验，不是这个短语被提起的原因。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the programme?

options.text: Why British driving tests should be made more difficult.
Why driving schools in Japan are struggling and changing to attract learners.
How tourists can drive safely in the Scottish countryside.
Why fear of travelling in vehicles is becoming more common.

evidence.quote:
In Japan driving schools are having a big problem recruiting
new learners and many are having to close.

private.explanationZh: 虽然节目表面上谈“学开车”，但主体内容集中讨论的是日本驾校招不到学员、竞争激烈，以及它们如何调整服务来吸引顾客。因此这个选项最能概括整期节目的中心内容。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有主张英国驾照考试应当更难。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据直接点出日本驾校招生困难，后文也一直围绕其应对方式展开。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。苏格兰只是Kate谈个人驾驶爱好时顺带提到，不是主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。车辆旅行恐惧症只是开头问答，不是节目主体。

public.prompt: Why does Callum mention efficient public transport and the difficulty of owning and parking a car in Japan?

options.text: To show that Japanese teenagers are forbidden to drive.
To argue that Japanese roads are more dangerous than British roads.
To explain why learning to drive may seem less necessary in Japan.
To suggest that Japanese people prefer holidays in Scotland.

evidence.quote:
perhaps it's not surprising that, you know, learning to drive is not as big a thing
in Japan as it was for me when I was a teenager.

private.explanationZh: Callum先提到日本公共交通效率高、拥有和停放汽车都很麻烦，然后得出“学开车在日本没那么重要”的结论。可见前面的现实条件是用来解释日本人为什么不像别处那样重视学车。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说日本青少年被禁止驾驶。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里谈的是必要性和便利性，不是道路危险程度。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据直接把这些生活条件和“学车没那么重要”联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。苏格兰来自Kate前面的个人经历，和这里的推理无关。

public.prompt: What service does the Musashi Sakai school offer to help learners wind down?

options.text: A holiday trip through the Highlands.
Free petrol and parking vouchers.
Extra lessons on English driving vocabulary.
A beauty-salon session and a massage.

evidence.quote:
Offering a session at a beauty salon and massage to help learners wind down after trying to
remember "mirror, signal, manoeuvre" is how the Musashi Sakai school is hoping to get more
customers.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道直接细节题。报道明确说明这所驾校为了吸引顾客，提供美容和按摩服务，帮助学员在紧张的驾驶课后放松下来。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Highlands是Kate谈度假时提到的地方，不是驾校服务内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有提到免费汽油或停车券。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。英语词汇解释来自主持人，不是学校的附加服务。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据逐字给出了beauty salon和massage两项服务。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100603-learning-to-drive-q01: 节目一开始提“mirror, signal, manoeuvre”，随后解释这是学车时人人都被训练牢记的三步，因此它的作用是引出驾驶学习这一主题，a正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100603-learning-to-drive-q02: 主体内容集中在日本驾校招生成困难、竞争激烈，以及它们如何转向更“service-orientated”来吸引学员，所以主旨是b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100603-learning-to-drive-q03: Callum提到日本公共交通高效、拥有和停车都困难，是在解释为什么在日本学开车不像别处那样必要或有吸引力，因此c最贴切。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100603-learning-to-drive-q04: 报道明确说该学校提供“a session at a beauty salon and massage”帮助学员放松，所以答案是d，没有歧义。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the 500-day Mars experiment described in the programme?

options.text: To test whether people can cope with the conditions of a long Mars journey.
To train astronauts to repair spacecraft during real space missions.
To compare business trips in Moscow with holidays on Earth.
To measure the exact size of Mars before sending a crew there.

evidence.quote:
And the idea is to see if
people can cope with the challenge of such a journey.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头已经说明，这个500天的密闭实验并不是真的飞去火星，而是模拟那种超长旅程的生活和心理条件，看人类能不能承受。因此正确选项准确概括了实验目的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据直接说实验的idea是看人们是否能应对这种旅程的挑战。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。维护和实验只是模拟过程中的任务，不是实验总目的。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。business trip只是开头的类比式引入。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。Mars大小是节目小测验，不是这个实验的研究目标。

public.prompt: What will the pretend astronauts do during the first 250 days?

options.text: Explore the Martian landscape in groups of three.
Carry out routine maintenance and scientific experiments.
Travel back and forth between Moscow and Australia.
Practice landing a spacecraft on a real desert surface.

evidence.quote:
The pretend astronauts will spend the first 250 days carrying out routine maintenance and
scientific experiments as if they were actually on the ultra-long-haul flight to Mars.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查前250天的具体安排。报道明确说他们会像真实火星飞行一样，做日常维护和科学实验，而不是提前“登陆火星”或进行真实飞行。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。三人进入“火星地貌”是第251天之后的事。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据完整列出了前250天的两项主要任务。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。整个实验在莫斯科的模拟环境中进行，不是真实往返旅行。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有提到真实沙漠着陆训练。

public.prompt: What happens on day 251 of the experiment?

options.text: The experiment ends early because of boredom and claustrophobia.
All six crew members are allowed to leave the spacecraft permanently.
Three crew members move into a sand-filled area meant to represent Mars.
Scientists begin a new series of business-trip interviews.

evidence.quote:
On day 251, three lucky members of the crew will be able to move out into what looks like a
large enclosed sand-pit. It's meant to be the Martian landscape.

private.explanationZh: 第251天是实验流程中的关键节点：三名成员可以离开模拟飞船，进入一个模仿火星表面的封闭沙坑区域。其余选项都夸大或改写了这个细节。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。boredom和claustrophobia是潜在挑战，不是第251天就导致实验结束。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。不是全部六人，也不是永久离开。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据准确描述了人数、时间和目的地。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。business trips只是开场引入，不是实验环节。

public.prompt: If the crew completes the experiment successfully, what does Richard Galpin suggest will happen?

options.text: Public concern about boredom in space travel will disappear completely.
Scientists will cancel future Mars research for several decades.
The crew will immediately be sent on a real mission to Mars.
Confidence in the possibility of a real human mission to Mars will increase.

evidence.quote:
it will
certainly boost hopes that a real human flight to Mars will take place in the coming decades.

private.explanationZh: 这里的关键词是boost hopes，意思是“增强希望”。如果模拟任务成功，它不会立刻变成真实火星飞行，但会提高人们对未来几十年实现载人火星任务的信心。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说所有担忧会完全消失，只说希望会上升。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。方向相反，成功会增强而不是取消相关期待。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目说的是coming decades，不是马上执行真实任务。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据明确把实验成功与“对真实载人火星飞行的希望增加”联系起来。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100610-journey-to-mars-q01: 节目开头直接说明，这个500天实验是模拟往返火星所需时间，目的是看人类能否应对如此漫长旅程的挑战，所以a正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100610-journey-to-mars-q02: Richard Galpin明确说前250天假想宇航员将进行“routine maintenance and scientific experiments”，与b完全对应。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100610-journey-to-mars-q03: 报道说在第251天，三名成员会进入一个大型封闭沙坑，那里被当作火星地貌，因此c正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100610-journey-to-mars-q04: Galpin说如果他们成功挺过无聊和幽闭感，将会“boost hopes”未来几十年实现真实载人火星飞行，因此d最准确。

public.prompt: Why are scientists using elephant seals in this research?

options.text: The seals can gather underwater information that may help predict future changes affecting the reef.
The seals can transport coral directly from Antarctica to Australia.
The seals can replace satellites that no longer work in Tasmania.
The seals can teach tourists how to dive near the Great Barrier Reef.

evidence.quote:
The Great Barrier Reef is perhaps one of the natural wonders of the world but it is
in danger from various environmental factors.

The elephant seals of the Southern Ocean have been recruited by Australian scientists because of
their diving skills.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题需要整合两层信息：第一，研究目标和大堡礁未来变化有关；第二，之所以选择海象海豹，是因为它们擅长潜水，能进入科学家平时难以观测的海域。正确选项同时概括了研究目的和方法。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它兼顾了“获取水下信息”和“帮助预测礁体变化”两点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说海豹运输珊瑚。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。卫星在节目中负责传输数据，不是被海豹替代。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。研究目标是环境监测，不是旅游或潜水教学。

public.prompt: How is the information collected by the seals sent to scientists?

options.text: Divers remove memory cards from the seals under the ice.
Sensors on the seals record the data, and it is transmitted by satellite when the seals surface.
The seals swim back to Hobart and deliver written reports.
Radar stations on the reef measure the water directly.

evidence.quote:
Small sensors glued
to the animals' heads, that measure salinity and temperature, are providing a rare glimpse into the
world beneath the waves. When the seals return to the surface the information is transmitted back
to Tasmania by satellite.

private.explanationZh: 这是过程细节题。节目先说传感器贴在海豹头上，测量盐度和温度；再说海豹浮出水面时，信息通过卫星传回塔斯马尼亚。正确选项完整复述了这个流程。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有提到潜水员取出存储卡。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。传感器采集数据、浮出水面后通过卫星传输，这两个环节都在证据中。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这是拟人化误读，节目说的是电子传输。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。测量工作由海豹身上的传感器完成，不是礁区雷达站。

public.prompt: Why does Kate explain the phrase "a rare glimpse into the world beneath the waves"?

options.text: To argue that salinity is more important than temperature.
To show that the seals spend very little time below the surface.
To emphasize that the seals give scientists unusual access to underwater conditions.
To suggest that scientists already know exactly what happens under the ice.

evidence.quote:
So 'a rare glimpse into the world beneath the waves'
is a way to describe the special view the scientists are getting of what is happening
under the water.

private.explanationZh: Kate专门解释这个短语，是为了帮助听者理解这不是字面意义上的“看一眼”，而是在强调科学家平时很难获得的水下观察机会。这个表达突出的是观测的稀缺性和研究价值。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里没有比较盐度和温度谁更重要。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这个短语不是在说明海豹待在水下时间短。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。special view正是在强调一种难得的观察窗口。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。恰恰因为这种观察难得，所以不是“已经完全知道”。

public.prompt: What do researchers in Hobart believe about changes off Antarctica?

options.text: They are unrelated to conditions in Australian waters.
They prove that the Great Barrier Reef has already disappeared.
They only affect shipping routes near Tasmania.
They may serve as an early sign of what will later happen in the Great Barrier Reef.

evidence.quote:
Government researchers in the state capital Hobart believe that changes off Antarctica are a
precursor to future events in the Great Barrier Reef.

private.explanationZh: 关键词是precursor to future events，意思是“未来事件的前兆”。研究人员并不是说南极附近的变化已经等同于大堡礁的结果，而是认为它可能提前预示之后会发生什么。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。证据明确说两者存在前后关联。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说大堡礁已经消失。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。研究焦点是环境变化与礁体预测，不是航运路线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确转述了precursor的含义，即前兆、先行信号。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100616-science-seals-q01: 节目说明科学家利用海象海豹的潜水能力获取南大洋水下信息，并借此理解未来大堡礁可能发生的变化，所以a正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100616-science-seals-q02: 原文说传感器粘在海豹头上测量盐度和温度，等它们浮出水面时数据通过卫星传回Tasmania，因此b与文本完全一致。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100616-science-seals-q03: Kate解释“a rare glimpse...”是在说明这些海豹让科学家得以罕见地看到海浪之下、水下环境中发生的情况，所以c最符合其修辞作用。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100616-science-seals-q04: Hobart研究人员认为南极外海的变化是大堡礁未来事件的“precursor”，即前兆或早期信号，因此d正确。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the programme?

options.text: How biting midges spoil the Highlands and why researchers are trying to control them.
Why traditional Highland clothing is changing in modern Scotland.
How mountain guides protect tourists from severe storms.
Why camping has replaced hill-walking in the Highlands.

evidence.quote: For thousands of years the grandeur of this landscape has been marred by the misery of the midge.

But now they're being trapped by researchers from Edinburgh. Here they are fighting a big battle
with the tiniest of monsters.

private.explanationZh: 主旨题要抓住两部分：一是midge长期给高地带来困扰，二是研究人员正在设法控制它们。两段证据合起来构成整期节目的中心，不是服饰、天气或旅游方式。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时概括了“问题”与“应对研究”两条主线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Highland dress只出现在开头小测验里。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有讨论导游如何应对暴风雨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。hill-walking和camping只是背景介绍，不是主旨。

public.prompt: Why does James Cook contrast the "grandeur" of the Highlands with "the misery of the midge"?

options.text: To argue that the Highlands are no longer worth visiting.
To show that a beautiful landscape is spoiled by a very small but troublesome insect.
To compare two different kinds of wildlife found in Scotland.
To explain why mountains are difficult to climb in winter.

evidence.quote: For thousands of years the grandeur of this landscape has been marred by the misery of the midge.

private.explanationZh: 这句话把“壮丽景观”和“蠓虫带来的痛苦”并列，是一个鲜明对比。它的作用是突出：破坏高地体验的不是巨大的灾难，而是一种很小却极其烦人的昆虫。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。后面主持人仍然推荐去高地旅游，所以不是劝人别去。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。对比的核心就是“景色很美，但体验被小虫破坏”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里不是比较两种野生动物，而是在对比景观与困扰。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。句子没有讨论冬季登山困难。

public.prompt: According to Dr Alison Blackwell, how do midges bite people?

options.text: They tear holes in clothing before biting exposed skin.
They inject poison through a tail and then fly away immediately.
They cut the skin, create a pool of blood, and then suck from it.
They feed only when campers are asleep inside tents.

evidence.quote:
they cut a hole in your skin and
create a pool of blood and then they put their mouth parts in and suck from that. And that itself
can be very painful.

private.explanationZh: 这是典型细节题。Dr Blackwell详细说明了midges叮咬的过程：先切开皮肤，形成血池，再把口器伸进去吸血。其他选项都加入了节目没有提到的机制。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。证据说的是切开皮肤，不是先撕破衣服。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目把口器比作剪刀，没有提到尾部注毒。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据完整描述了叮咬与吸血过程。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有把它们的活动限定为夜间或帐篷内。

public.prompt: What does Finn mean when he says midges "make up for" their lack of size "in number"?

options.text: They are harmless individually and dangerous only to larger animals.
They become much larger during the Scottish summer.
They compensate by flying much faster than other insects.
They often gather in such large swarms that they can look like a black cloud.

evidence.quote:
what they lack in size they make
up for in number.

There's so many that they look like a black
cloud.

private.explanationZh: 这句话不是说蠓虫会变大，而是说虽然个体很小，但数量极多。Finn后面立刻补充说它们多到看起来像一团黑云，因此正确选项准确表达了这层含义。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目强调它们会咬人并造成痛苦，不是“单个无害”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说它们会在夏天长得更大。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里谈的是数量补偿，不是速度补偿。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。两个证据合起来就是“个头小，但成群时数量惊人”。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100624-tiny-monsters-of-the-highla: 节目主要讨论高地里让人痛苦的蠓虫、它们如何破坏旅游体验，以及研究人员如何设法控制它们，因此主旨是a。
James Cook把高地的“grandeur”和蠓虫带来的“misery”对照，是为了突出美丽景观却被这种很小但烦人的昆虫破坏，故选b。
Dr Blackwell明确说蠓虫像剪刀一样割开皮肤，形成一小滩血，再把口器伸进去吸血，所以c与原文直接对应。
Finn说它们虽然个头小，但数量上弥补了这一点，后面解释说成群时看起来像一团黑云，因此d最准确。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: Why smiley-face symbols have become more useful than real smiles
How companies should stop applicants from smiling in interviews
Why people in different countries interpret smiles in opposite ways
How different kinds of smiles affect impressions and what makes a smile seem sincere

evidence.quote:
simply having a pretty or a big smile
isn't good enough any more.

private.explanationZh: 节目主旨是讨论“笑”不只是好看或大，还关系到给人的印象，以及什么样的笑容才显得真诚。后文关于坏笑容和最佳笑容的分析都围绕这个中心展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目虽然提到smiley face，但那只是开场小测验，不是核心话题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有主张禁止求职者微笑，而是在讨论什么样的笑更合适。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目提到“smile is the universal welcome”，强调的是跨语言可理解，不是不同国家完全相反的解读。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它概括了全文：先说光有漂亮或大大的笑不够，再分析几种不理想的笑，最后说明好的笑应当如何呈现。

public.prompt: Why does Kate say that everybody understands a smile, no matter what language they speak?

options.text: To argue that words are unnecessary in job interviews
To support the idea that a smile is a universal signal
To show that smiling habits are identical in every culture
To explain why people prefer e-mails to face-to-face talk

evidence.quote:
Everybody
understands a smile, no matter what language you speak.

private.explanationZh: Kate这句话是在解释“a smile is the universal welcome”这句格言。她借“无论说什么语言都懂”来说明笑容是一种跨语言、跨情境都容易被识别的信号。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她并不是说语言不重要，只是在说明笑容本身也能传递意义。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它直接支撑“微笑是普遍欢迎方式”这一观点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。她说的是“都能理解”，不是“每种文化的微笑习惯完全相同”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里与电子邮件无关，那只是节目开头提到smiley face时的背景。

public.prompt: According to the discussion, why might "The Enthusiast" be a bad smile in a job interview?

options.text: It suggests the speaker has rehearsed every facial expression
It makes the speaker seem cold and unwilling to engage
It may make the speaker seem a little too eager for some people
It hides the speaker's feelings behind a thin expression

evidence.quote:
it
might be proof, that you're a little too enthusiastic for some people

private.explanationZh: “The Enthusiast”的问题不在于笑得开心，而在于它可能让别人觉得你热情过头。节目明确说这种笑可能被当成“证据”，证明你对某些人来说有点过分热切。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这说的是“Big Freeze”那种看起来练习过、假的笑。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。冷漠、不欢迎别人更接近“Big Freeze”或“The Robot”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文明确说这种笑可能证明你“a little too enthusiastic”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。薄而小、缺少温度的是“The Robot”，不是“The Enthusiast”。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about a smile that spreads slowly across the whole face?

options.text: It is mainly useful for answering quiz questions
It is likely to look more genuine to other people
It works best when only the mouth moves
It is a sign that someone is trying to hide nervousness

evidence.quote:
The best
smile, they say, is slower and floods naturally across the whole face.

private.explanationZh: 这里需要根据“slower”和“floods naturally across the whole face”推断效果。节目随后解释这种笑看起来更sincere，也就是更真实、更自然。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这个特征和节目里的小测验没有关系。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。慢慢展开并覆盖整张脸的笑，被解释为更真诚自然。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目明确说不要“just smile with your mouth”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文强调的是真诚，而不是掩饰紧张。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100812-is-there-a-good-and-a-bad-s: 节目先谈微笑的重要性，随后重点区分三种“不好的微笑”，最后说明最好的是缓慢、自然铺满全脸的微笑，所以主旨是不同微笑如何影响他人印象，以及什么样的微笑显得真诚。
Kate紧接着“a smile is the universal welcome”这句话解释说，微笑是“universal sign”“signal”，人人都懂，所以她这样说是为了支持“微笑是普遍信号”这个观点。
谈到“The Enthusiast”时，Kate明确说研究显示这种笑容“might be proof that you're a little too enthusiastic for some people”，因此问题中的原因就是显得有点过于热切。
节目说笑得太快会显得“不真诚”，而最好的微笑是慢慢展开并自然铺满整张脸；Kate又把 sincere 解释为 genuine or real，因此可推知这种微笑更可能让别人觉得真诚。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: How expensive medical treatment for back pain has become
Why office workers should avoid all forms of exercise
The benefits of good posture and ways of walking that support it
Why nurses are better teachers than fitness trainers

evidence.quote:
some of the benefits of your good posture is you
will feel less tired, get fewer headaches, you will sleep better and look slimmer!

private.explanationZh: 节目围绕良好姿势的好处展开，并进一步讨论怎样通过正确走路来避免姿势问题。开头列出好姿势的多种益处，后面再进入具体建议。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目提到背痛造成工作日损失，但重点不是医疗费用。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有反对锻炼，反而强调正确行走能锻炼肌肉。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它概括了“好姿势的好处”以及“如何通过走路改善姿势”这两部分。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。Jo Kushar被介绍为护士和仪态老师，但节目主旨不是比较职业。

public.prompt: Which problem is included in Jo Kushar's list of "postural issues"?

options.text: Loss of hearing
Stomach infection
Poor eyesight
Repetitive strain injury

evidence.quote:
tension, neck, shoulder problems,
RSI, back pain.

private.explanationZh: Jo Kushar列举的“postural issues”包括tension、颈肩问题、RSI和背痛。RSI被主持人随后专门解释，因此是明确出现的项目。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。听力问题没有被提到。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。胃部感染不属于文中列出的postural issues。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。视力问题也没有出现在这份列表中。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。RSI就在原文列举的姿势相关问题之中。

public.prompt: Why does Kate play the first extract again?

options.text: To give Rosie more time to guess the weekly quiz answer
To prove that British people have the best posture in Europe
To help listeners notice the general term covering several posture-related problems
To compare computer work with carrying a heavy bag

evidence.quote:
Let's hear that extract again and see if you can hear the expression the speaker uses
for all these problems put together.

private.explanationZh: Kate重放第一段，不是为了重复内容本身，而是为了让听众抓住一个总括性表达。随后Rosie明确指出这个表达就是“postural issues”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。周测答案并不是重放这段音频的目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目里恰恰说英国人的姿势可能是欧洲最差。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。重放的目的就是让听众识别统称这些问题的表达。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这些例子出现在后面对RSI的解释中，不是重放该片段的主要目的。

public.prompt: Why does Jo Kushar advise people to lift their knees and shorten their steps?

options.text: To make walking look more formal in public
To help the body move in a balanced way and find natural alignment
To prevent people from moving too quickly at work
To strengthen only the leg muscles while resting the back

evidence.quote:
Shorten your step,
to find your natural alignment.

private.explanationZh: 这里要把后面关于“natural alignment”的解释和前面“exercise all your muscles in the right way, in a balanced way”结合起来理解。建议这样走路，是为了让身体更自然、更平衡，而不是单纯为了外表。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她讨论的是身体对齐和压力，不是公共场合的仪态表演。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。缩短步幅、抬起膝盖的目的，是找到自然对齐并让肌肉以平衡方式工作。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说是为了减慢工作中的移动速度。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文强调全身肌肉以正确方式运作，不是只锻炼腿部。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100818-stand-up-straight-q01: 节目开头介绍好姿势的好处，随后解释坏姿势带来的问题，并重点讲如何通过正确走路来改善姿势，因此主旨是好姿势的益处以及支持它的走路方式。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100818-stand-up-straight-q02: Jo Kushar列举“postural issues”时明确说到“tension, neck, shoulder problems, RSI, back pain”，其中RSI即重复性劳损，和选项d完全对应。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100818-stand-up-straight-q03: Kate说“Let's hear that extract again and see if you can hear the expression... for all these problems put together”，目的就是让听者注意概括这些问题的总称“postural issues”。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100818-stand-up-straight-q04: Jo Kushar说抬膝、缩短步幅可以“exercise all your muscles in the right way, in a balanced way”，并帮助找到“natural alignment”，所以建议的目的就是让身体更平衡并找到自然对位。

public.prompt: What does the term "boomerang generation" refer to in the program?

options.text: Parents who move house after their children graduate
Young people who return to live with their parents after university
Teenagers who leave school early to find work
Graduates who travel abroad before starting adult life

evidence.quote:
boomerang generation are young people who leave home to go to

University but then return to live with their parents when they’ve

finished their studies.

private.explanationZh: 节目先解释boomerang这个比喻，再明确给出定义：年轻人离家上大学，毕业后又回到父母家生活。因此答案是毕业后回家住的年轻人。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目说的不是父母搬家。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文直接定义了这个词，指上大学后又回家住的年轻人。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有谈辍学找工作的青少年。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。出国旅行并不是这个词在节目中的含义。

public.prompt: What is one main reason the speakers give for the growth of the boomerang generation?

options.text: Graduates prefer strict family rules to campus freedom
Universities are requiring students to live with their parents
Job shortages and financial pressure make independent living difficult
Most parents refuse to let their children rent shared housing

evidence.quote:
with a lack of jobs for the
class of 2010 the numbers are expected to rise.

private.explanationZh: 这里需要把统计信息和主持人的解释结合起来理解。毕业生回家人数上升，主要是因为工作难找、债务压力大，独立租房或合租都更困难。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目中Kate抱怨的恰恰是回家后规则更多、自由更少。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说大学强制学生和父母同住。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。缺少工作机会，再加上经济压力，是回家住现象增长的核心原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有说大多数父母拒绝合租，重点在于经济条件不足。

public.prompt: Why does the program include comments from both Kate and her mother Janice?

options.text: To prove that university study always harms family relationships
To compare television habits in different regions of the UK
To suggest that parents should charge rent immediately
To show that returning home can create disadvantages for both graduates and parents

evidence.quote:
I lost my independence because at University you get to do what you want to do
– and not be regimented by what you watch on the television.
we lost our privacy, because suddenly there is somebody else in the house.

private.explanationZh: 节目安排女儿和母亲各说一面：Kate失去独立，Janice失去隐私。这样不是单看某一方抱怨，而是展示“回旋镖一代”对双方生活都会带来实际代价。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说大学“总是”伤害家庭关系。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。看电视只是Kate举的生活细节，不是设置这两段话的主要目的。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有提出应立刻收租这样的政策建议。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。两段评论形成对照，说明孩子和父母都要做出让步。

public.prompt: According to Professor Gill Jones, what broader effect can returning home have on young people?

options.text: It can delay their feeling of full adulthood
It can make them less interested in higher education
It can force them to move abroad for work
It can end their legal right to live independently

evidence.quote:
wait til they leave home again before they can

really feel like adults.

private.explanationZh: 教授的重点不是法律身份，而是社会和心理意义上的“成为成年人”。年轻人虽然技术上已经成年，但回家住会让他们把真正独立的成年感推迟。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文明确说他们要等再次离家后，才真正感觉像成年人。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说他们会因此失去读大学兴趣。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。出国工作不是教授提出的后果。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目强调他们“technically adult”，所以法律上的成年权利并没有被取消。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100819-the-boomerang-generation-q0: Alice直接定义了这个词：年轻人离家去上大学，完成学业后又回父母家住，因此答案就是大学后回家和父母同住的年轻人。
节目说明主要原因是经济因素：毕业后难找工作、付不起自己住的费用，还有大学债务压力，所以独立生活更困难。
Kate说自己失去了独立性，母亲Janice说家里失去了隐私；节目并列这两人的评论，是为了展示回家同住对毕业生和父母双方都可能带来不便。
Professor Gill Jones说如果年轻人作为法律意义上的成年人却不能独立，就会带来“what does it mean to be adult”这类问题；随后主持人解释这是“defer adulthood”，即推迟真正感觉自己成年的时间。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the report from Jakarta?

options.text: To compare ticket prices on different train lines
To argue that crowded trains should be banned for everyone
To describe how Japanese factories design train seats
To explain why women-only train carriages were introduced there

evidence.quote:
By introducing women’s only carriages,
the state-run rail operator wants to put a stop to sexual harassment.

private.explanationZh: 这段报道的核心是在解释雅加达为什么推出女性专用车厢。原因不是票价或设计趣味，而是为了减少通勤中的性骚扰问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。票价不是讨论重点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有主张取消拥挤列车本身。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。粉色座位只是补充细节，不是报道的主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。报告重点就是介绍女性专用车厢作为应对骚扰的措施。

public.prompt: In the program, what does "taking advantage" mean?

options.text: Offering women extra seats during rush hour
Using crowded conditions deliberately to harass women
Finding a quicker way to board the train
Asking the rail company for better service

evidence.quote:
take advantage of the cramped conditions

on the train to bump or push into women on purpose.

private.explanationZh: 节目把taking advantage解释得很具体：一些男性利用车厢拥挤这个条件，故意碰撞或推挤女性。因此它不是“占便宜”的一般意义，而是借环境实施骚扰。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。让座并不是这里的意思。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文明确说是利用拥挤条件故意碰撞、推挤女性。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。快点上车与sexual harassment无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。向铁路公司提要求也不是该短语在文中的含义。

public.prompt: Why does Yanti say there must be other ways to stop sexual harassment as well?

options.text: To argue that the new carriages should be removed immediately
To claim that harassment happens only outside trains
To show that women-only carriages are only part of the solution
To suggest that crowded trains are not a serious problem

evidence.quote:
But there must be other ways to stop sexual harassment. We have to try and
raise awareness about the issue as well.

private.explanationZh: Yanti并没有否定女性车厢，而是在补充其局限。她认为这项服务也许能减少目标人群受害，但还需要提升公众对问题的认识，所以它只是部分解决方案。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她没有主张取消这项服务。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她讨论的恰恰是列车上的骚扰，同时强调更广泛的应对方式。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。她的意思是“有帮助，但不够”，还要提高意识。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她把问题说得很严肃，并未淡化拥挤列车中的风险。

public.prompt: Why does Alice mention that men can also be victims of sexual harassment?

options.text: To broaden the discussion beyond the assumption that only women are affected
To suggest that women-only carriages should be replaced by mixed seating
To explain the official answer to the quiz question
To show that harassment is mainly a workplace issue for men

evidence.quote:
not just women who can be sexually

harassed.

There are also many legal cases where men say they have

been sexually harassed at work or in other places too.

private.explanationZh: Alice在这里是扩展听众对概念的理解。前面话题集中在女性车厢，但她提醒大家，性骚扰的受害者不只限于女性，这能避免把问题理解得过窄。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。她是在打破“只有女性会遭遇性骚扰”的单一想象。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她没有据此提出取消女性车厢的建议。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里与节目开头的国家竞猜无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她说的是“work or in other places too”，不是把问题主要限定在男性职场。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100826-women-only-train-carriages-: 雅加达报道先说明女乘客抱怨遭到骚扰，再说铁路公司引入女性专用车厢是为了制止性骚扰，所以这段报道的主要目的就是解释当地为何推出女性专用车厢。
Alice把“taking advantage”解释为一些男性在拥挤列车上故意碰撞、触碰女性；Yanti也说他们利用拥挤环境故意这样做，所以意思就是故意利用拥挤条件骚扰女性。
Yanti先说这项服务希望减少骚扰目标人数，但紧接着说“there must be other ways”并且要“raise awareness”，说明她认为女性专用车厢只是解决方案的一部分，不是全部。
前文讨论焦点主要是女性在车厢中遭骚扰，Alice后来补充“it's not just women who can be sexually harassed”，明显是在扩大讨论范围，纠正只有女性会受影响的单一假设。

public.prompt: According to Catherine Sommes, what helps a new word enter the dictionary?

options.text: Approval from school teachers before people use it
A connection to major sports events only
A long history of use in formal literature only
Frequent use across different kinds of places and sources

evidence.quote:
not just a question of numbers, it’s a question of
different places it’s being used so everywhere from magazines, to newspapers to
chatrooms.

private.explanationZh: Catherine Sommes强调两个标准：不只是出现次数，还要看这个词是否出现在不同类型的语境里。也就是说，既要常见，也要分布广。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有提到教师审批。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。体育只是后来vuvuzela的例子，不是普遍标准。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有要求词语必须长期存在于正式文学中。
