optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原文明确说既看数量，也看使用场景是否多样。

public.prompt: Why does Catherine Sommes mention "de-friend" and "un-friend"?

options.text: To illustrate how social networking is creating new vocabulary
To warn listeners not to remove people from online contact lists
To show that dictionary editors prefer informal slang to all other words
To explain why magazines are more important than chatrooms

evidence.quote:
getting words in the dictionary now like
de-friend, or un-friend

private.explanationZh: 她举de-friend和un-friend，不是为了讨论社交礼仪，而是为了展示一个来源：社交网络正在不断产生新词，并且这些词已进入词典编纂者的视野。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两个例子用来说明社交网络怎样推动新词进入语言。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有在劝阻听众删除联系人。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。她没有说词典编辑偏爱非正式俚语。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。杂志和聊天室出现在前一段，是关于词语分布范围，不是这里举例的重点。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about a term that is used only within one specialized field?

options.text: It may be too limited to qualify for dictionary entry
It is guaranteed to enter the dictionary quickly
It is more valuable than words from social networking
It should replace older words in everyday speech

evidence.quote:
In order to make it into the dictionary it can’t just be

jargon – words which are specific to particular fields of interest.

private.explanationZh: 这里要根据“it can’t just be jargon”进行推断。若一个词只在某个专业圈子里使用、没有扩展到更广泛语境，它就可能还不够资格进入这本词典。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。只限于单一领域的术语，覆盖面太窄，可能无法入选。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文恰恰暗示仅是jargon并不足够。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有比较哪类词“更有价值”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。进入词典与否并不意味着它必须取代日常旧词。

public.prompt: According to Rob, what sound does he connect with the year 2010?

options.text: The hum of the vuvuzela at the Football World Cup in South Africa
The chatter of online chatrooms adding new words
The applause for the latest Oxford Dictionary edition
The noise of computers testing secret passwords

evidence.quote: vuvuzela at the Football World Cup in South Africa.

private.explanationZh: Rob把2010与南非世界杯上的vuvuzela嗡嗡声联系起来。这里不是在讨论词典标准，而是在用一个鲜明例子说明当年的代表性新词。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。Rob明确把2010和世界杯上的vuvuzela声音联系在一起。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。聊天室是前面谈词语使用范围时的例子，不是Rob这里提到的“2010的声音”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有把掌声或词典出版声与2010联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。dictionary attack是另一个词汇例子，不是Rob这里说的声音。

reasonsZh.bbc-20100902-new-words-in-english-q01: Catherine Sommes说不仅看使用次数，还看词语出现于多少不同场合，如杂志、报纸、聊天室，因此进入词典的重要条件是跨不同来源和场合被广泛使用。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100902-new-words-in-english-q02: 她在谈社交网络“so huge”之后举出de-friend和un-friend两个例子，并说现在很多这样的词进入语言中，所以举例目的就是说明社交网络正在产生新词汇。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100902-new-words-in-english-q03: Alice明确说要进入词典，“it can't just be jargon”，也就是不能只限于某个专门领域内部使用；因此可推知若一个词只在单一专业圈子里使用，它可能过于局限而不易入词典。
reasonsZh.bbc-20100902-new-words-in-english-q04: 在提到“Vuvuzela”后，Rob说2010年大概会因“the hum of the vuvuzela at the Football World Cup in South Africa”而被记住，因此对应选项a。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the program?

options.text: To explain the quiz answer about when Black History Month began in the UK
To show why Black History Month matters in Britain and how people can experience it through real examples
To give a detailed history of Nelson Mandela's imprisonment
To argue that schools should stop discussing slavery

evidence.quote:
It's a time when
people from all cultures and backgrounds get a chance to learn about, share in
and celebrate the contributions that black people have made to UK and world
history.

Aunty Rene proves that Black History Month can be an amazing
experience simply based on the people you meet.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头先定义英国黑历史月的意义，结尾又用Aunty Rene总结它为何令人难忘，因此主旨不是单纯报事实，而是说明这个活动为什么有价值，并通过具体人物让价值变得可感。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目里确实有一个关于年份的竞猜，但那只是引入话题的小环节，不是整段节目的主要目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。前半段解释黑历史月让不同背景的人了解并庆祝贡献，后半段用Aunty Rene说明这种体验如何通过真实人物落地。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Robben Island和Mandela只是用来解释“living history”的一个例子，节目重心并不是曼德拉生平。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目提到有些活动过度聚焦负面历史，但并没有主张学校停止谈论奴隶制。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about Mia Morris's view of Black History Month?

options.text: She thinks family history is more important than British history
She believes Black History Month should be cancelled because it separates people
She thinks one month is not ideal, but it still gives people a useful chance to explore history
She wants October events to focus mainly on painful events from the past

evidence.quote:
Ideally, we wouldn't need it; ideally, it would be all year round. But then say to people:
use the opportunity to explore more of your family and spend more time with your
family, finding out more about our very rich history.

private.explanationZh: Mia先说理想状态下黑历史应当全年都能接触到，随后又说既然现实不是这样，就要利用这个月份去探索家庭与共同历史。这说明她并非反对这个月份，而是把它视为不完美但仍然有用的契机。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她提到家庭历史，是作为进入“our very rich history”的路径，不是在把家庭史和英国史对立起来。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她没有主张取消，相反她明确说要“use the opportunity”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。她同时表达了“全年更理想”和“当下这个月仍值得利用”这两个层面的看法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。后文恰恰批评一些活动只聚焦负面事件，这不是Mia主张的重点。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker introduce the idea of "living history"?

options.text: To show that learning history can be interactive and connected to real people's experiences
To argue that museums are no longer useful for teaching history
To prove that Nelson Mandela created a new way of studying history
To explain why many events still focus on slavery

evidence.quote:
It's a more interactive way of learning about a specific event or
period of time in history than say, for example, simply looking at items in a
museum.

private.explanationZh: 这里解释“living history”的作用，是为了把黑历史月从抽象纪念活动转成一种可亲身感受、可与真实人物连接的学习方式。说话人强调的是互动性和体验感。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据明确把living history定义为比单纯看博物馆展品更互动的学习方式。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文只是对比互动学习与静态观看，没有否定博物馆本身的价值。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说Mandela发明了这种方式；他只是相关地点示例中的人物。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。关于奴隶制的讨论出现在别处，这里解释的是学习方式，不是活动主题偏向。

public.prompt: How is the program mainly organized?

options.text: It compares school history courses in several countries
It lists major black historical figures in chronological order
It contrasts teacher salaries in Guyana and Britain
It begins with a debate about the month and then supports its value with a concrete personal example

evidence.quote:
every year, there's a rather heated debate about whether we should or
shouldn't spend an entire month on black history

Aunty Rene proves that Black History Month can be an amazing
experience simply based on the people you meet.

private.explanationZh: 节目结构先提出“是否需要专门一个月”的争议，再用Aunty Rene这样的真实人物经历回应争议、证明活动的现实价值。这是典型的“提出问题，再给出具体支持”的组织方式。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有展开多国学校课程比较。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。它并不是按年代罗列人物，而是围绕一个当代活动展开。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。薪资只是采访Irene时出现的一个细节，不构成整体组织框架。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。前面是公共争议，后面是具体人物案例来证明这一活动为何值得存在。

reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-black-history-month-q01: 节目先介绍英国黑人历史月的意义，再讨论是否需要单独设一个月，并用Mia Morris与Irene Sinclair这些真实例子说明其价值；重点不是竞猜答案、曼德拉生平或反对学校谈奴隶制。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-black-history-month-q02: Mia Morris明确说“理想情况下”黑人历史应当全年都被了解，但既然现实不是这样，十月仍然是一个探索家庭与共同历史的机会，所以她并非主张取消，而是认为一个月不够理想但仍有用。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-black-history-month-q03: 说话者先解释living history比只看博物馆展品更互动，随后用Robben Island和Irene Sinclair说明历史可以通过真实人物与经历来体验，因此其作用是说明历史学习可以互动并连接真实人生。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-black-history-month-q04: 节目结构先从“是否应该专门用一个月谈黑人历史”的争论切入，再通过living history和Irene Sinclair这个具体人物案例来支持黑人历史月的价值，因此d最符合整体组织方式。

public.prompt: What is the discussion mainly about?

options.text: How dress codes are used to shape workplace appearance, especially in the Ukrainian government
Why BBC employees should return to wearing formal evening clothes
How African independence movements changed European fashion
Why office workers prefer bright colors and strong perfume

evidence.quote: in the Ukraine, government workers now have an official dress code.

Gone
are the flashy, provocative styles of the country's previous administration. In, are more
subdued fashions and colours.

private.explanationZh: 节目核心不是泛泛谈时尚，而是讨论“dress code”如何规定工作场合应穿什么，以及乌克兰政府如何用新规则改变公务员形象。后面的具体规则都服务于这个中心。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。前文明确点出乌克兰政府有正式着装规范，后文又说明其目标是从夸张风格转向更克制的样式。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。BBC员工穿不穿正式服装只是开场闲聊，不是主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。非洲国家的例子只是一个引入性问题，用来说明服装规则可能承载政治意义。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目实际上说的是限制强烈香水和夸张穿着，不是在提倡它们。

public.prompt: Why does the presenter mention an African country that banned Western-style suits after independence?

options.text: To show that formal clothing is always more expensive than local clothing
To illustrate that dress rules can express political identity and a break from colonial history
To prove that most governments prefer casual clothes at work
To explain why the BBC changed its own office wardrobe

evidence.quote:
This was done to
show that the country had broken links with its colonial past

private.explanationZh: 这个例子并不是为了考时装知识，而是为了说明着装规定有时不仅关乎美观，还会承载政治象征意义，例如与殖民过去切割。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有谈服装成本，也不是经济问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据直接说明禁穿西装是为了表明与殖民过去断开联系。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有得出“大多数政府喜欢休闲装”的结论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。BBC着装只是开场背景，与这个非洲国家的政治举措不是同一论点。

public.prompt: According to the report, what were women government workers in Ukraine advised to do?

options.text: Wear bright colors in the morning and high heels in the afternoon
Choose short skirts if they also wore flat shoes
Avoid strong morning perfume and revealing clothes, and prefer flats to high heels
Wear the same outfit for at least two days if it looked professional

evidence.quote:
Women were told what perfume to wear - scents with sharp aromas should only be worn
in the afternoon, it suggested. They should also avoid short, tight skirts and outfits that
revealed too much cleavage. Flats were preferred to high heels.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题，正确选项必须同时抓住三个要求：早上不要用刺激性强的香水、避免过于暴露的服装、以及平底鞋优于高跟鞋。只有C完整对应原文。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有要求早上穿亮色，反而限制早上使用强烈香水。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文明确说应避免短而紧的裙子，而不是在某种条件下允许。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确整合了香水、服装暴露程度和鞋子三项规定。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。连续两天穿同一套衣服是对男性的建议之一，而且还是“不应该”。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about Alice's reaction to the dress code for men?

options.text: She thinks the rules are too relaxed and should be much stricter
She believes men should wear identical suits every day
She thinks expensive suits are the best way to look professional
She accepts that fit matters, but she thinks some expectations may be impractical

evidence.quote:
I think that's difficult for me because suits can be very expensive. It is true men
don't look so good in suits that are too small - but I think I'd just say wear a
clean shirt and a different tie and you'll look different.

private.explanationZh: Alice并没有完全否定“合身”的想法，但她马上指出西装很贵，并提出更实际的替代办法。这说明她认为规范有一定道理，可是执行层面未必现实。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她不是嫌规则太松，而是在质疑其现实可行性。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她的建议恰恰是通过干净衬衫和不同领带来显得不同，而不是天天同一套。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。她提到西装贵，是在说明问题，而不是主张必须买昂贵西装。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。她承认太小的西装不好看，但又强调成本和更务实的做法。

reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-dress-codes-what-can-you-we: 讨论核心是职场着装规范，重点放在乌克兰政府公布的新dress code及其具体要求上；其余选项要么只是开场玩笑，要么偏离主题。
主持人提到独立后的非洲国家禁穿西式西装，是为了说明服装规定不仅关乎美观，也能表达政治身份与摆脱殖民过去的立场。
报道明确说女性上午不要用强烈香水、避免短而紧的裙子和过度暴露的服装，并且平底鞋优于高跟鞋，所以c完整对应原文。
Alice认为合身确实重要，但也说西装很贵，并建议只要干净衬衫和不同领带就能显得不同，说明她接受部分原则，但觉得某些要求未必实际。

public.prompt: What aspect of estate agents' language is the discussion mainly about?

options.text: How estate agents describe homes persuasively without sounding unfair
Why London's smallest house should become a museum
How to write poetic property descriptions
Why buyers prefer old houses to new flats

evidence.quote:
estate agents do seem to
have a language all of their own.

In 1968 it became illegal - against
the law – to advertise a house or a flat in a way that might discriminate against
different groups of people or encourage discrimination.

private.explanationZh: 节目围绕房产中介的“语言”展开，一方面讨论他们如何把房子说得更吸引人，另一方面强调用词又必须避免歧视和法律风险。因此主线是“宣传效果”和“表达边界”的结合。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。前一段指出中介有自己的一套语言，后一段说明这种语言还受到法律约束。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。最小房子的宽度只是节目里的竞猜，不是核心讨论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。诗意描写只是后半段的一个策略案例，主题不是教人写诗。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目并未比较买家对新旧房屋的偏好。

public.prompt: Why does the program use the advertisement line "Beautiful 3 bedroom house, close to temples"?

options.text: To recommend a neighborhood with many historic buildings
To show how a seemingly simple description can imply discrimination
To prove that religious buyers pay more for houses
To explain why estate agents prefer short advertisements

evidence.quote:
In 1968 it became illegal - against
the law – to advertise a house or a flat in a way that might discriminate against
different groups of people or encourage discrimination.
Beautiful 3 bedroom house, close to temples.

private.explanationZh: 这个例子不是单纯描述地段，而是拿来说明某些看似无害的措辞，可能暗示“欢迎某类人、不欢迎另一类人”，从而触碰歧视问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目并没有把它当作地理推荐，而是讨论其潜在排斥性。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。前文明确铺垫了“歧视性广告”问题，这句广告语就是用来说明风险的例子。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有讨论宗教买家出价更高。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。例子的重点在措辞含义，不在广告长短。

public.prompt: In the discussion, what does the word "panoramic" suggest about a flat?

options.text: It is small but elegantly decorated
It has several bedrooms and a balcony
It offers a wide view of the surrounding area
It needs a lot of repair work

evidence.quote:
Well, that means there's a good view of the area. So you can see all around you
from the flat.

private.explanationZh: 这里直接解释了“panoramic”的含义，即从公寓里可以很好地看到周围区域，是“视野开阔”的意思。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这更接近bijou的含义，不是panoramic。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这是具体房屋配置，不是panoramic这个词本身的意思。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文明确说可以看见周围区域，正是开阔景观之意。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。需要修理更接近characterful背后的暗示，不是panoramic。

public.prompt: Why did Paul Bonnett and Paul Lyle move toward more poetic property descriptions?

options.text: Because legal descriptions were no longer allowed in advertisements
Because buyers care only about sea views and terraces
Because short factual listings were too expensive to print
Because poetic language could help the advert stand out and appeal to buyers' emotions

evidence.quote:
while factual descriptions appeal to or satisfy our heads – our
intellect – poetry appeals to our hearts – how we feel.

The room fills your eyes with air and space. The first thing you see is the sea meeting the
sky. The windows lead out onto the terrace, taking you above and beyond with room to
breathe.

private.explanationZh: 节目先明确对比“事实描述打动理智”和“诗意描述打动感受”，再给出一段明显更具画面感的改写。这说明他们转向诗意语言，是为了让广告更突出，并激发买家的情感想象。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说事实描述违法，只说广告不能歧视。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。海景与露台只是示例房源的内容，不足以解释整体策略转变。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有提到刊登成本。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。前者提供原理，后者提供实例，两者共同支持这一推断。

reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-estate-agents-q01: 整段讨论围绕房产中介如何用语言把房子说得更吸引人，同时又要避免违法或歧视性表达；诗意描述只是后半部分的一个具体做法，不是全部主题。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-estate-agents-q02: “close to temples”这个例子被用来说明看似普通的房屋描述也可能暗示偏向某宗教群体、排斥其他人，因此它的作用是展示语言如何隐含歧视。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-estate-agents-q03: Alice直接解释panoramic表示能看到周围区域、视野很好，因此意思是拥有宽阔的周边景观。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-estate-agents-q04: 原文说Paul Bonnett希望广告“stand out”以吸引更多顾客，而诗歌式表达比纯事实描述更能打动“hearts”，所以他们转向更诗意的语言是为了更醒目并触动买家的情感。

public.prompt: What broader point about Cockney does the discussion mainly emphasize?

options.text: Why standard English should replace regional dialects in London
How Cockney fits into changing London English and gives speakers a sense of identity
How museums preserve church bells in East London
Why restaurants are the best place to hear rhyming slang

evidence.quote:
the exhibition at the British Library tells us
about the 1500 year history of the English language, as used by people around
the world. And of course, there's information about the Cockney dialect in the
section on London English too.

Cockney rhyming slang gives East Enders a
strong sense of identity, just like any other language.

private.explanationZh: 节目不是孤立地介绍几个俚语词，而是在“London English”这个更大框架中讨论Cockney，一方面谈它作为伦敦英语的一部分如何存在，另一方面谈它怎样承载东区人的身份认同。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有主张用标准英语取代地方变体，反而强调语言演变和多样性。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。前一段给出“London English”的背景，后一段点出身份认同功能，合起来就是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Bow bells和展览只是引入，不是主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目中没有把餐馆描写为听Cockney的最佳场所。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about why Cockney rhyming slang first became useful in East London?

options.text: It helped some speakers keep outsiders from understanding them
It made it easier for immigrants to learn formal English
It was designed by museum curators for exhibitions
It allowed financial businesses to give better customer service

evidence.quote:
working-class people in the East End of
London started speaking it because they didn’t want the ruling-class to
understand their conversations.

private.explanationZh: 根据原文，Cockney最初的一项功能就是“让某些人听不懂”，也就是作为一种带有遮蔽作用的语言代码。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据直接说明他们不想让统治阶层听懂谈话。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有说它是帮助移民学习正式英语的工具。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。博物馆展览是在介绍它，不是发明它。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。金融机构用Cockney是后来的趣味应用，不是它最初形成的原因。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers play a listener's message in Cockney rhyming slang?

options.text: To show that Cockney is too confusing to be useful today
To prove that only London-born people can understand Cockney
To let listeners hear authentic slang in use and try decoding it
To compare Cockney pronunciation with Jamaican English

evidence.quote:
reading out a message from a listener. But, it’s all in Cockney
rhyming slang. How much will we understand?

private.explanationZh: 这一段的功能是“展示真实使用场景”。主持人不是在抽象定义术语，而是让听众直接听一段Cockney，再一起拆解意思。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目语气是把它当成有趣的理解挑战，而不是证明它无用。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她们随后就为听众解释内容，说明并非只有伦敦出生者才能理解。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。问题句“How much will we understand?”明确表明这是一个听辨和解码示范。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。该段没有做与牙买加英语的语音比较。

public.prompt: Why did the financial institution decide to use Cockney in its cash machines?

options.text: Because customers had demanded a full translation of banking terms
Because it thought using Cockney would be entertaining and engaging
Because Cockney had become the official language of London business
Because the British Library required businesses to support dialects

evidence.quote:
they thought it would be fun if it
asked you: "please enter your Huckleberry Finn".

private.explanationZh: 这里的因果关系非常明确：那家机构之所以这么做，是觉得这样会“fun”，也就是增加趣味性和吸引力，而不是出于制度性要求。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有提到客户提出这种要求。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据里直接给出原因是“they thought it would be fun”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说Cockney成了官方商务语言。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。英国图书馆的展览只是文化背景，不是企业必须遵守的规定。

reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-london-english-cockney-q01: 节目强调Cockney既是不断变化的伦敦英语的一部分，也能体现东伦敦人的身份认同；并非主张标准英语取代方言，也不是在谈钟楼或餐馆。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-london-english-cockney-q02: 主持人与Alice都提到这种押韵俚语像一种“code”，最初是工人阶级不想让统治阶级或孩子听懂谈话，因此可推断它帮助说话者防止外人理解。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-london-english-cockney-q03: 播放听众留言是为了让大家直接听到真实的Cockney俚语如何使用，并现场一起解码其含义，而不是证明它没用或只有本地出生者才懂。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-london-english-cockney-q04: 节目最后明确说那家金融机构认为如果取款机用Cockney提示输入密码会“fun”，说明其目的就是让服务更有趣、更吸引人。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: A university media blackout experiment and what it suggests about students' dependence on media
How Facebook became more popular than universities
Why students should avoid television but keep using the internet
A long-term study proving that media always harms mental health

evidence.quote:
the ban is just affecting hundreds of
university students, as part of an experiment to see how they react without
media.

As a nation we now spend half our waking hours using the media in some form
and these young people can’t imagine being permanently unplugged.

private.explanationZh: 节目先介绍伯恩茅斯大学的“media blackout”实验，最后又把学生反应上升到更广泛的媒体依赖现象，因此主旨是“实验及其揭示出的依赖性”，而不是单一平台或单一建议。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。前一段给出实验背景，后一段给出更广泛的含义，完整概括了节目中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Facebook数据只是问答环节，不是主要话题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。实验是全面断开媒体接触，不是选择性保留某一媒介。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目明确说长期结果还不知道，因此没有“证明”。

public.prompt: Why does Alice explain the term "guinea pig"?

options.text: To argue that animal testing is similar to media research
To criticize the experiment for treating students badly
To clarify that the term refers to a person taking part in a test
To suggest that the volunteers were selected because they kept pets

evidence.quote:
a guinea pig here is a strange term for describing someone who
takes part in a new experiment or test.

private.explanationZh: 说话人专门解释guinea pig，是为了避免听众按字面理解成动物，从而明确它在这里是“实验参与者”的比喻用法。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她只是解释词义，并没有把本实验与动物实验做价值比较。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里的语气是说明用词，不是批评实验伦理。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据直接把该词定义为参加新实验或测试的人。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。宠物信息只是顺带形容豚鼠，不是志愿者被选中的原因。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the students' reported symptoms?

options.text: Most of them had already planned to leave the university
They were mainly worried about missing one television program
The experiment proved that media causes permanent emotional damage
Many of them were more dependent on media in daily life than they had realized

evidence.quote:
They are reporting withdrawal symptoms, overeating, feeling nervous, feeling isolated,
disconnected, they don’t know what to do with themselves all the time, just going round
their room or their house in silence and they really hate that!

private.explanationZh: 这些反应像“戒断”一样强烈，说明媒体不仅是消遣，而是深度嵌入了他们的日常节奏。由此可以推断，许多学生原先并未充分意识到自己对媒体的依赖程度。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。症状与退学计划没有关系。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文描述的是广泛的不适与失去方向感，不是只想看某个节目。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有得出“永久伤害”的结论，而且长期结果未知。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。强烈的不适和无所适从最合理地指向日常依赖被突然切断。

public.prompt: Based on Caroline Scott's comment, what would she most likely want after about two hours without media?

options.text: To continue the blackout for another full day
To get access to her phone or the internet again
To replace media with overeating
To join a new experiment without any rules

evidence.quote:
Caroline: Yeah it is quite nice to be totally separated for about two hours I would say,
maximum. Other than that I would like to have my phone on me, or the internet, or
something.

private.explanationZh: Caroline明确说，完全分离两小时左右还不错，但再往后她就希望身边有手机、互联网之类的媒介。因此最可能的后续行为就是重新接触媒体。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她说两小时已经差不多是上限，不支持继续延长一天。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。她直接表示之后想要手机或互联网。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。overeating是另一组学生症状，不是Caroline在这段里的偏好表达。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她的态度并不是想继续接受更严格的实验，而是想恢复接触媒体。

reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-media-blackout-q01: 节目主要讨论伯恩茅斯大学的media blackout实验，以及学生在24小时断媒介后表现出的困难，从而反映他们对媒体的依赖；并不是在讨论Facebook流行度或长期定论。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-media-blackout-q02: Alice专门解释guinea pig在这里不是动物，而是参加新实验的人，因此其修辞作用是澄清术语含义。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-media-blackout-q03: 学生出现戒断、暴食、紧张、孤立、不知所措等反应，说明他们平时对媒体的依赖程度比自己意识到的更深；原文没有支持退学、只想电视节目或永久伤害这些说法。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-media-blackout-q04: Caroline明确说完全脱离媒体大约两个小时还不错，但之后她就想要手机、互联网之类的东西，因此最可能想重新获得手机或网络。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the program?

options.text: Men face more health problems mainly because they study medicine less often.
Men are often less willing than women to seek medical help, which can worsen health outcomes.
Men are biologically weaker than women in every area of physical strength.
Men recover quickly from heart problems if they ignore early warning signs.

evidence.quote:
They find it
difficult to ask for medical help when they need it, which can be dangerous.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查节目主旨。节目并不是说男性天生在所有方面都更弱，而是强调很多男性在需要时不愿求医，这种延误会让健康后果更严重，因此在健康意义上他们被称为“较弱的一方”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有把问题归因于男性较少进入医学院；医学院人数只是开头和结尾的竞猜内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据直接指出男性在需要时难以寻求医疗帮助，而且这种做法可能很危险。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目明确区分了传统体力印象和健康层面的“weaker sex”，并没有说男性在所有体力方面都更弱。
optionRationalesZh.d: Doug 的例子恰好说明忽视症状会更危险，而不是会更快恢复。

public.prompt: Why did Doug Devaney say he did not return to the hospital right away?

options.text: He believed the tablets had completely cured his angina.
He could not find transportation after collapsing in the street.
He did not want to make a fuss or become a burden to other people.
He was waiting for a doctor to call him back with test results.

evidence.quote: 'Well - I've already been there; I don't want to be a fuss, I don't want to be a burden.'

private.explanationZh: Doug 直接给出两个理由：他不想“小题大做”，也不想给别人添麻烦。这一细节具体展示了节目所讨论的男性在求医时常见的犹豫心理。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他没有说药物已经完全治好了自己；事实上后来他仍然胸痛并倒在街上。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有提到交通问题，这是无根据添加的信息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据原话就是他不想“be a fuss”也不想“be a burden”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有提到医生回电或检查结果。

public.prompt: What does Peter Baker imply about many men's attitude toward illness?

options.text: They associate asking for help with admitting weakness.
They trust doctors but dislike prescribed medicine.
They misunderstand the meaning of lifestyle choices.
They believe vulnerability is only emotional, not physical.

evidence.quote:
They mustn't show weakness, admit a vulnerability. And I think this makes
it much harder for them to ask for help from a doctor or another health professional.

private.explanationZh: Peter Baker 的话说明，许多男性把求助和承认自己软弱、脆弱联系在一起，所以更难主动去找医生。这需要把“show weakness”和“ask for help”之间的关系推断出来。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据把“不表现软弱/脆弱”和“更难寻求帮助”直接联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.b: 他谈的是男性观念和求助障碍，不是对药物的偏好。
optionRationalesZh.c: “lifestyle choices”是节目里的另一个词汇点，不是这一段的核心意思。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目后文解释 vulnerability 既可能是身体上的，也可能是情绪上的，所以该说法过窄。

public.prompt: Why does Yvonne return to the medical-school numbers near the end of the program?

options.text: To introduce a new reason that hospitals are difficult to use.
To argue that women should replace men in health care professions.
To prove that angina has become more common since the 1960s.
To reveal the answer to the opening quiz before the program closes.

evidence.quote:
Now earlier Rob, I asked: since the early 1960s, how many
women enter medical school each year?

private.explanationZh: 这里回到节目开头提出的竞猜问题，是 BBC 6 Minute English 常见的结尾结构：在收尾前回收前文问题并公布答案。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这句话没有展开新的医疗论点，只是在回收前面的提问。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目并没有提出让女性取代男性的主张。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这里谈的是医学院入学人数，不是心绞痛发病率。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。主持人明确回到 earlier question，作用就是在结束前公布开头题目的答案。

reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-men-are-the-weaker-sex-q01: 节目核心一直在说明：男性常因不愿求医、拖延就诊而让健康后果更严重，Doug和Peter Baker的例子都在支持这一点。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-men-are-the-weaker-sex-q02: Doug明确说他不想“make a fuss”，也不想“be a burden”，所以没有马上回医院。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-men-are-the-weaker-sex-q03: Peter Baker说男性不愿承认自己有问题，不愿表现出弱点或脆弱，这说明他们把求助看成承认软弱。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-men-are-the-weaker-sex-q04: 节目开头提出医学院人数的竞猜，结尾Yvonne回到这个数字只是公布开头问题的答案并收尾。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: How Chinese farmers identify rare flowers in Buddhist writings.
Why seed banks preserve plant species that may disappear and help restore them later.
Why urban centres are better than farms for protecting plants.
How to grow flowers that bloom only every three thousand years.

evidence.quote:
Today, we’re talking about seed banks – a place where the seeds from all kinds
of plants are carefully stored. Unfortunately, researchers are warning that about
one fifth of the world's plants are at risk of becoming extinct.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头就点明主题：介绍 seed banks，同时说明大量植物面临灭绝风险。整期内容围绕为什么要保存种子，以及这些种子如何帮助未来重新种植和保护植物多样性展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 稀有花朵只是后面的趣味问答内容，不是整期节目的主线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。开头同时给出“seed banks”和“plants at risk of becoming extinct”两层核心信息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有说城市中心更适合保护植物，反而提到城市开发会导致植物减少。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目并不是花卉栽培教程，三千年开花的例子只是结尾问答。

public.prompt: According to Paul Smith, which seeds are most important for the bank to collect?

options.text: Seeds from species that are both highly threatened and especially useful.
Seeds only from plants already extinct in the wild.
Seeds mainly from countries with the coldest climates.
Seeds from flowers that bloom very rarely.

evidence.quote:
absolutely essential that we get the right species – those which are most threatened,
those which are most useful, because we know that there's a good chance that many
plant species will become extinct over the next few decades.

private.explanationZh: Paul Smith 明确说要优先保存“最受威胁的”和“最有用的”植物种子。正确答案必须同时覆盖这两个筛选标准，而不是只抓住其中一个片面信息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据原句同时列出 most threatened 和 most useful。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目讨论的是防止灭绝，而不是只收集已经灭绝植物的种子。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有把寒冷国家作为筛选标准。
optionRationalesZh.d: 罕见开花的花只是节目中的小测验素材，不是种子库的主要收藏原则。

public.prompt: Why does David Shukman describe the cold stores and special clothing?

options.text: To criticize the cost of running the bank.
To compare the bank with ordinary supermarkets.
To show the scale and storage conditions involved in preserving seeds.
To explain why researchers prefer urban plants.

evidence.quote:
And this is where all those seeds end up - 1.8 billion of them in one of these cold stores
kept at minus 20. That's why you have to wear the cold weather equipment.

private.explanationZh: 这段描写的作用不是单纯渲染环境，而是让听众具体感受到种子库规模之大，以及保存种子所需的低温条件和专业措施。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有批评成本问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有把种子库与超市进行比较。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。1.8 billion、minus 20 和 cold weather equipment 都是在说明保存环境与规模。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这段与城市植物偏好无关。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the statement about reaching 25% by 2020?

options.text: The bank has nearly completed its work and needs few more samples.
The bank is reducing its focus to only food crops.
The bank expects most endangered plants to disappear before 2020.
Protecting global plant biodiversity is a large, ongoing project.

evidence.quote:
And by 2020, the Millennium Seed Bank hopes to have seeds from about 25%
of the world's plants. So there's lots of work to do.

private.explanationZh: 从“到 2020 只希望达到 25%”以及“there's lots of work to do”可以推断，全球植物多样性的保存工作规模很大，而且仍然远未完成。

optionRationalesZh.a: 如果目标只到 25%，显然不是“几乎完成”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 证据没有说范围缩小到食用植物。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有做出这种时间性的灭绝预测。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这是对数字和总结语句的合理整合推断。

reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-seeds-q01: 节目主要讨论种子库为何保存濒危植物种子，以及未来如何用这些种子重新种植植物。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-seeds-q02: Paul Smith明确说必须收集“most threatened, most useful”的物种种子，也就是既最受威胁又最有用的种子。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-seeds-q03: David Shukman描述冷库、零下20度和专门防寒装备，是为了展示种子保存所需的条件和规模，而不是批评成本或谈城市植物。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-seeds-q04: 节目说去年只达到全球植物多样性的10%，到2020年希望达到25%，可推知这是一项规模很大、仍在持续推进的长期工程。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: The London Tube inspires both complaints and appreciation from different users.
Visitors to London usually prefer buses because the Tube map is confusing.
Safety technology has made the Tube completely reliable.
The history of the Tube is more important than its present-day use.

evidence.quote:
many people have a love-hate
relationship with the underground – we either love it or hate it.

private.explanationZh: 节目总线索就是主持人说的 love-hate relationship：一方面谈到延误、拥挤、不可靠等烦恼，另一方面又谈到速度、覆盖面以及有人对 Tube 声音的欣赏。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。开头直接提出 love-hate relationship，统领全篇内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目反而说游客觉得地图很好用，不容易迷路。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目开场就播报延误，显然并非“完全可靠”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 虽然提到历史，但这不是本期讨论重心。

public.prompt: What surprised Natalie when she first used the Tube?

options.text: She could not understand the station announcements.
People stayed silent and avoided eye contact even when standing very close together.
Most passengers preferred reading to listening to music.
Visitors found the map too difficult to follow.

evidence.quote:
Nobody speaks to each other on the Tube; nobody looks at each other either most of the
time. And at first, it was strange being that close to strangers

private.explanationZh: Natalie 最惊讶的是，在拥挤到彼此很近的情况下，人们仍然几乎不说话，也尽量避免眼神接触。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她提到的不是广播难懂，而是人与人之间的互动方式。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据明确说 nobody speaks，nobody looks，而且大家站得很近。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有把 Natalie 的感受归结为阅读或听音乐偏好。
optionRationalesZh.d: 相反，前文说地图很好，不容易迷路。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers include Wang Fei's poetic description of Tube sounds?

options.text: To prove that the Underground is quieter than other transport systems.
To explain how station announcements are recorded.
To show that foreign passengers are usually confused by the Tube.
To encourage listeners to notice familiar experiences from a fresh perspective.

evidence.quote: it often takes fresh eyes to look at something we take for granted.

private.explanationZh: 主持人借 Wang Fei 的描写把日常通勤体验“重新陌生化”，让听众重新注意那些平时习以为常、被忽视的声音与感受。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目并没有比较不同交通系统谁更安静。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这不是技术说明，也没有讲录音过程。
optionRationalesZh.c: Wang Fei 的片段体现的是审美观察，不是困惑或迷失。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。fresh eyes 和 take for granted 正好说明其修辞作用。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the beeping sound on Tube carriages?

options.text: It is mainly intended to make the journey feel more musical.
It warns passengers about delays on different lines.
It provides practical safety information, especially for visually impaired passengers.
It replaces the need for passengers to listen to announcements.

evidence.quote:
And this beeping's really important for blind or visually
impaired people to know when the doors are open or shut.

private.explanationZh: 从这里可以推断，beeping 的主要功能是提供车门开关等安全信息，尤其帮助视障乘客判断何时可以安全上下车。

optionRationalesZh.a: 虽然节目有声音审美描写，但这里强调的是功能性，不是音乐感。
optionRationalesZh.b: 延误信息来自广播，不是车门提示音。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据直接把 beeping 与 visually impaired passengers 的安全判断联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 提示音是辅助信息，不能替代所有广播。

reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-the-london-tube-q01: 节目一开始就说人们对地铁有“love-hate relationship”，随后既谈抱怨也谈优点，主旨就是这种又爱又恨的体验。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-the-london-tube-q02: Natalie说最让她惊讶的是大家在地铁里彼此不说话、不看对方，尽管人与人站得很近。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-the-london-tube-q03: Wang Fei的诗意描述之后，Alice说这让人用新鲜的眼光看待习以为常的事物，所以作用是让听众重新注意熟悉体验。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101210-the-london-tube-q04: 后文明确解释beeping声对盲人或视障人士很重要，能帮助他们判断车门开关、何时安全上下车。

public.prompt: According to the program, what is essential if Africa is to become food-secure?

options.text: Reducing the number of people working on farms.
Importing more staple crops from Asia.
Making agricultural modernization a top political priority.
Replacing all staple crops with GM crops.

evidence.quote:
He believes that leaders in Africa need to make agriculture a top priority. And
he says that the modernisation of agriculture is the responsibility of African
Presidents.

private.explanationZh: 节目强调，要让非洲实现粮食自给，关键不是局部小修小补，而是把农业现代化提升到国家领导层面的优先事项。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有主张减少农业劳动力。
optionRationalesZh.b: Juma 的方向是提高自给和出口能力，不是增加进口。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据把 top priority 和 modernisation of agriculture 直接并列。
optionRationalesZh.d: GM crops 只是愿望清单中的一部分，不是全部替代。

public.prompt: What does the phrase "agriculture and economy are one and the same" suggest in this context?

options.text: Africa's broader economic progress depends heavily on improving agriculture.
Only agricultural workers should make economic policy.
Farming and trade should be managed by separate ministries.
Agriculture matters less than infrastructure investment.

evidence.quote:
They are the
same – here that the economy in Africa is completely dependent on
agriculture.

private.explanationZh: 这句话并不是字面上把两个词当作同义词，而是在强调农业在非洲经济中的基础性地位：农业发展会直接影响更广泛的经济进步。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。Yvonne 明确解释为经济“completely dependent on agriculture”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文谈的是依赖关系，不是决策权只属于农业工人。
optionRationalesZh.c: Juma 反而说总统不应把这件事简单交给部门处理。
optionRationalesZh.d: 基础设施很重要，但该句强调的不是“农业不重要”，而是农业极其关键。

public.prompt: Which action is included in Juma's wish list?

options.text: Limiting road construction to protect desert land.
Increasing investment in roads, irrigation, and processing facilities.
Replacing labour with imported food until harvests improve.
Ending scientific research on crop development.

evidence.quote:
The wish list - and some will call it that - is a long one. Boost investment in roads,
irrigation and energy schemes. Mechanise all farms, and build storage and processing
facilities.

private.explanationZh: 愿望清单列出了多项非常具体的措施，包括道路、灌溉、能源、机械化以及储存和加工设施。正确选项必须与这些细节相符。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文说要 boost investment in roads，不是限制修路。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。选项准确概括了证据中的多项具体措施。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有提出靠进口食品来替代农业改造。
optionRationalesZh.d: Juma 主张拥抱科学，而不是停止科研。

public.prompt: Why does Alice call the quiz question a trick question?

options.text: Because Kenya imports all three crops rather than growing them.
Because staple crops cannot be compared across countries.
Because rainfall can alter which crop matters most.
Because all three options can be considered staple crops in Kenya.

evidence.quote:
Well, it was a trick question because all three are grown in staple crops
in Kenya. Apparently rice not as much as wheat and maize but it’s catching up.

private.explanationZh: 所谓“trick question”是因为题目表面上像只有一个答案，但主持人最后说明三个选项都属于肯尼亚的 staple crops，因此它故意误导听者按单选思路去想。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她没有说三种作物主要依赖进口。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有否认不同国家之间可以比较 staple crops。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有把这个问题与降雨变化联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。Alice 直接解释 trick question 的原因就是 all three。

reasonsZh.bbc-20101216-farming-in-africa-q01: 节目明确说非洲领导人需要把农业作为最高优先事项，农业现代化是总统的责任，这是实现粮食自给的关键。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101216-farming-in-africa-q02: Yvonne解释“one and the same”时说，非洲的经济完全依赖农业，所以这句话是在说农业改进关系到更广泛的经济发展。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101216-farming-in-africa-q03: Juma的愿望清单包括增加对道路、灌溉、能源、仓储和加工设施的投资，选项b与原文一致。
reasonsZh.bbc-20101216-farming-in-africa-q04: Alice说这是个陷阱题，因为稻米、玉米和小麦这三个选项在肯尼亚都算主食作物。

public.prompt: What do the survey results mainly suggest about families in the UK?

options.text: Most people think hobbies are more satisfying than family life.
Despite negative public discussion, families remain a major source of happiness for many people.
Single-parent families are viewed as socially unacceptable.
Most people want parenting classes to become compulsory.

evidence.quote:
But despite the negative view of families we
might read about in the newspapers, most people said that their families made
them happy.

private.explanationZh: 节目先提到媒体中关于家庭问题的负面叙事，随后用调查结果反转这一印象：对多数人来说，家庭仍然是重要的幸福来源。

optionRationalesZh.a: 调查里 family 的幸福感比例高于 hobbies，不是相反。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据中的 despite... most people... made them happy 就是全文主旨。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目说单亲家庭在现代英国并不罕见，也被社会接受。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中只是提到 parenting classes 的社会讨论，并没有说大多数人要求强制实施。

public.prompt: According to the survey result cited by Chris Warburton, what did many people think about unhappy couples with children?

options.text: They should not stay together only for the children's sake.
They should remain together until the children finish school.
They should ask relatives before separating.
They should attend parenting classes first.

evidence.quote: Nearly two thirds of you think it’s better not to stay together for the sake of the kids.

private.explanationZh: 这是一道细节题。调查结果说接近三分之二的人认为，不应仅仅为了孩子而维持一段不幸福的关系。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据直接给出 it’s better not to stay together for the sake of the kids。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有提出“等孩子毕业再说”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 调查并未提到要先征求亲属意见。
optionRationalesZh.d: parenting classes 是前文背景话题，不是这项调查结论。

public.prompt: What does the result about reporting a relative to the police suggest?

options.text: Most people believe family members are often wrongly accused.
People care more about money than about family loyalty.
Many people think a serious crime should be reported even when it harms family relationships.
Most crimes inside families are kept secret because the consequences are too mild.

evidence.quote:
A big majority of people said that they would report to the police if they thought they had
committed a serious crime. 84 per cent of people are prepared to do that even if the
consequences were pretty tough.

private.explanationZh: 从“即使后果很艰难也愿意报警”可以推断，很多人认为严重犯罪面前，法律责任会压过单纯的亲属包庇。

optionRationalesZh.a: 证据没有讨论误判问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这里谈的是 serious crime 与 family consequences，不是金钱。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这是对 84% 愿意报警且后果艰难这一结果的合理推断。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文恰恰说明多数人愿意报告，而不是普遍隐瞒。

public.prompt: What is Rob suggesting when he says that voting Tory would not be a secret for all families?

options.text: Every family in Britain supports the Conservative Party.
Political opinions are never considered personal matters.
Family secrets are always more serious than political disagreements.
Whether something counts as a family secret depends on the family's own norms and sensitivities.

evidence.quote:
That person’s grandmother voted for the Conservative Party! Of course,
that wouldn't be a secret for all families.

private.explanationZh: Rob 的意思是，什么算“家庭秘密”并没有统一标准；同一件事在一个家庭里可能很敏感，在另一个家庭里却完全普通。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他没有说所有英国家庭都支持保守党。
optionRationalesZh.b: 他说的是“并非对所有家庭都是秘密”，不是“政治观点永远不私密”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有把秘密按严重程度做绝对比较。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这句话强调 family secret 取决于各自家庭的价值观和敏感点。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-families-q01: 节目先提到媒体常负面讨论家庭，但调查结果显示大多数人认为家庭让自己幸福，因此b最符合主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-families-q02: Chris Warburton说将近三分之二的人认为，不应仅仅为了孩子而维持不幸福的婚姻关系。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-families-q03: 84%的人表示即使后果很严重，也会举报犯下严重罪行的亲属，这说明很多人认为严重犯罪应被举报，即便会伤害家庭关系。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-families-q04: Rob说祖母投保守党“并非对所有家庭都是秘密”，意思是某件事算不算家庭秘密，要看不同家庭自身的价值观和敏感点。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers focus on the Victorian tea set?

options.text: To show how tea helped Britain build factories
To explain how tea became Britain's favorite national drink
To compare British tea with herbal tea
To describe how the British Museum buys objects

evidence.quote:
through this tea set, we begin to understand why tea became so
popular.

private.explanationZh: 节目明确说“通过这套茶具，我们开始理解茶为何变得如此流行”。因此谈维多利亚茶具的目的，不是介绍博物馆流程，而是借它说明茶如何在英国变成全民性的饮品。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中提到工业化，是后面讨论工人和戒酒的背景，不是谈茶具的核心目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。茶具被当作切入口，用来解释茶为什么会在英国流行并成为国家性饮品。
optionRationalesZh.c: 开头只顺带提到 herbal tea，没有把两类茶作系统比较。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目只说茶具被列入“100件物品”，没有讨论博物馆如何购买藏品。

public.prompt: Why does Neil MacGregor say a cup of tea could be seen as less British than people assume?

options.text: Its main ingredients came from Britain's empire rather than Britain itself
Most British people preferred coffee during the Victorian period
Tea was originally produced in British factories
Sugar was added only in modern times

evidence.quote:
what could be less British than a cup
of tea, given that tea is made from plants grown in India, China or Africa and is
usually sweetened by sugar from the Caribbean?

private.explanationZh: 这里的推论关键在于“英国性”的反转：说茶“没那么英国”，是因为茶叶和糖都来自英国之外的殖民地或海外地区。说话人不是否认英国人爱喝茶，而是强调其来源并不本土。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据直接指出茶叶来自印度、中国或非洲，糖来自加勒比地区。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目末尾只说咖啡如今也是一种选择，没有说维多利亚时期多数英国人更爱咖啡。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目说的是茶叶种植园，不是英国工厂最初生产茶叶。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有把加糖说成现代才出现的做法，反而强调糖早就来自加勒比。

public.prompt: According to the program, why did the ruling classes promote sobriety among workers?

options.text: They wanted workers to spend less money on tea
They hoped children would stop drinking water
They needed a labor force that could work productively
They planned to replace factory jobs with plantation work

evidence.quote:
Britain was becoming an industrialised nation and workers
were required to be as productive as possible.

private.explanationZh: 节目把“提倡戒酒”放在工业化背景下解释：国家需要工人尽量高效生产，所以统治阶层希望劳动力保持清醒。答案要抓住“productive as possible”这一因果线索。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有说推广戒酒是为了让工人把钱省下来买茶。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目说的是当时水不安全、很多穷人甚至儿童会喝酒，不是“停止喝水”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。工业化需要高生产率，因此清醒、勤勉的工人受到鼓励。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有提到要把工厂工人改派到种植园工作。

public.prompt: What does the final statistic mainly suggest about tea in Britain today?

options.text: Tea drinking is decreasing because coffee is more popular
Tea is mainly consumed for historical reasons
Tea remains a major part of everyday British life
Most tea drinkers in Britain work for the Tea Council

evidence.quote: 120,000,000 cups of tea every day!
Now you can see why Britain is a nation of tea drinkers!

private.explanationZh: 结尾先给出每天1.2亿杯的惊人数值，再总结英国是“喝茶民族”。这两个信息合起来表明，茶并不是过时习惯，而是当代英国日常生活里仍然非常重要的饮品。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目只说咖啡近年也成了大生意，但没有说茶因此在下降。
optionRationalesZh.b: 统计数字强调的是现实中的大量饮用，不只是出于历史纪念。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。巨大日饮用量直接支持“茶仍是英国日常生活的重要组成部分”。
optionRationalesZh.d: Tea Council 只是数据来源，节目没有说多数饮茶者与该机构有关。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-tea-drinking-in-the-uk-q01: 节目明确说讨论茶并不只是因为主持人喜欢喝茶，而是因为这个维多利亚时期茶具能帮助人们理解茶为什么会在英国变成最受欢迎的国民饮料，因此b最符合主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-tea-drinking-in-the-uk-q02: Neil MacGregor说茶叶来自印度、中国或非洲，糖来自加勒比地区，重点是茶的主要原料并非产自英国本土，所以a正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-tea-drinking-in-the-uk-q03: 节目说工业化时期统治阶层希望工人保持清醒、勤勉并尽可能高效工作，因此推广戒酒和饮茶，c与原文一致。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-tea-drinking-in-the-uk-q04: 结尾给出英国每天喝1.2亿杯茶，并说这说明英国是一个喝茶的国家，这主要表明茶如今仍深度存在于英国日常生活中，所以选c。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the discussion?

options.text: To explain why internet use should be limited
To celebrate the Web's anniversary and discuss its impact
To compare the Web with older forms of television
To describe how to create a personal website

evidence.quote: the 20th anniversary of the World Wide Web.

changed our lives -
and in such a short space of time.

private.explanationZh: 开头先说明节目在庆祝万维网二十周年，接着说它在很短时间内改变了我们的生活。两处信息共同限定了主旨：既有纪念意味，也有对其影响的讨论。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有主张限制互联网使用，而是在回顾其发展与意义。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据同时体现“周年纪念”和“改变生活”的双重目的。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有把万维网与电视作为主要比较对象。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目介绍历史和影响，不是教学如何建网站。

public.prompt: What did the first web page mainly contain?

options.text: Instructions for buying a computer
News reports from CERN
Information about Tim Berners-Lee's project
A directory of all websites

evidence.quote: It was just information about the project that he was working on.

private.explanationZh: 这是直接细节题。节目明确回答第一页“只是关于他正在做的项目的信息”，因此要选择与项目介绍对应的选项。

optionRationalesZh.a: 没有提到购买电脑的说明。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目只说网页位于 CERN，不是新闻报道。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文直接说明网页内容就是项目相关信息。
optionRationalesZh.d: 当时网页并不是“所有网站目录”，而且那时也不存在这种规模。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers ask several people how they would cope without the World Wide Web?

options.text: To prove most people spend exactly 57 minutes online each day
To show different ways people depend on the Web in daily life
To explain how HTML replaced telephones
To show that older people dislike new technology

evidence.quote:
Listen to what they said when we
asked: how would you cope without the World Wide Web?

keeping in contact with people far away would also be
impossible.

private.explanationZh: 插入街访不是为了给出统计答案，而是通过不同人的回答展示互联网在现实生活中的功能，尤其是远距离联系、人际关系和替代方式等层面。第二段证据直接显示了一种具体依赖。

optionRationalesZh.a: 57分钟是结尾问答的答案，不是这段街访问答的目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。街访让听者看到人们在社交联系等方面如何依赖万维网。
optionRationalesZh.c: HTML 的解释出现在前面，不是街访段落的功能。
optionRationalesZh.d: 街访没有把年龄作为重点，也没有统一表达“反感新技术”。

public.prompt: What does Tim Berners-Lee's later comment suggest about the future of the Web?

options.text: The Web has already reached its limits
Research on the Web should focus only on design
The Web still has much unrealized potential
Developing countries have little need for the Web

evidence.quote:
only scratched the surface of
what could be realised with deeper scientific investigation into the Web

only just begun to understand how useful the World Wide Web
can really be

private.explanationZh: “只触及表面”和“我们才刚开始理解它有多有用”都指向同一推论：万维网未来还有很大的发展空间与尚未实现的用途，而不是已经发展完毕。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这与“only scratched the surface”正好相反。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原话提到 design、operation 和 impact on society，不是只研究设计。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。两段证据都强调潜力尚未被完全开发。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目反而特别提到它对发展中国家也很有用。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-the-world-wide-web-turns-20: 开头直接说在庆祝万维网二十周年，随后整段都在谈它如何改变生活以及未来潜力，所以主旨是庆祝周年并讨论其影响，b正确。
节目明确说第一张网页“just information about the project that he was working on”，因此是关于Tim Berners-Lee所做项目的信息，选c。
节目采访几个人讨论没有万维网会怎样，是为了展示人们在取暖系统、社交关系、远距离联系、文件处理等方面对网络的不同依赖，因此b最准确。
Tim Berners-Lee说人类“only scratched the surface”，说明万维网还有很多尚未实现的潜力，故c正确。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the discussion?

options.text: How politicians learn to use MRI machines
Whether certain brain structures are related to political beliefs
Why British universities avoid brain research
How to identify brain parts from Latin names

evidence.quote:
the structure of the brain, and how it could be related to our political
beliefs.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接点明节目主题：讨论大脑结构以及它是否与政治信念有关。因此主旨不是扫描设备本身，也不是脑部术语测验。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目提到 MRI 是研究方法，不是教政治人物如何使用设备。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据句就是对整期讨论主题的概括。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有说英国大学回避这类研究，反而介绍了相关研究。
optionRationalesZh.d: 开头的小测验只是引入，不是整段讨论的核心。

public.prompt: What did the MRI scans measure in the study?

options.text: The speed of blood flow through the brain
The number of political opinions a person held
The thickness of grey matter
The amount of scanner noise during the test

evidence.quote: the thickness of the grey matter in the brain

private.explanationZh: 题目问研究具体测量什么。原文明确说 MRI 扫描可以测量大脑灰质的厚度，因此这是直接信息提取。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说他们测血流速度。
optionRationalesZh.b: 政治观点是被比较的变量，不是 MRI 直接测量的对象。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据短语直接给出 grey matter 的厚度。
optionRationalesZh.d: 扫描噪音只是背景声，不是研究指标。

public.prompt: According to the findings, people who described themselves as liberal tended to have what kind of brain feature?

options.text: Thicker grey matter in the anterior cingulate
Thinner grey matter in every brain region
A larger pons and corpus callosum
No measurable difference from conservatives

evidence.quote:
where the nerve cells or neurons are - was thicker the more people described
themselves as liberal or left-wing

private.explanationZh: 节目报告的核心发现之一是：越把自己描述为自由派或左翼的人，在前扣带皮层相关位置的灰质越厚。选项必须同时抓住 liberal/left-wing 和 thicker grey matter 的对应关系。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据明确把 liberal or left-wing 与 thicker 联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文并没有说所有脑区都更薄，且这与自由派的方向相反。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目后面只说这些是真实脑部名称，没有报告它们与政治立场的结果。
optionRationalesZh.d: 研究恰恰报告了可测量差异。

public.prompt: Why does the program mention studies showing that computer games can change grey matter?

options.text: To argue that politics is less interesting than gaming
To suggest brain structure may be changeable rather than fixed
To prove conservatives play fewer games
To explain how MRI machines are programmed

evidence.quote:
simply teaching someone computer games for a few minutes each
week, can cause their grey matter in certain areas of the brain to change thickness.

So perhaps even people who seem hard-wired to believe certain things may be
able to change their minds and the shape of their brains too.

private.explanationZh: 这里举电脑游戏的例子，不是换话题，而是为前面的研究加上限定：既然灰质会快速变化，那么脑结构与政治倾向的关系未必是天生固定不变的。第二句把这个推论说得更清楚。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有比较政治和游戏哪个更有趣。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两段证据共同说明灰质结构可能会变化，因此不能简单看成固定标签。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有把游戏使用和保守派数量联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这里讨论的是灰质可塑性，不是 MRI 程序设置。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110114-brains-and-politics-q01: 节目一开始就说主题是大脑结构以及它如何可能与政治信念相关，所以b准确概括主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110114-brains-and-politics-q02: 原文明确说MRI scans can measure the thickness of the grey matter in the brain，因此测量的是灰质厚度，选c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110114-brains-and-politics-q03: 节目说前扣带皮层（anterior cingulate）灰质越厚，人们越倾向把自己描述为liberal或left-wing，所以a正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110114-brains-and-politics-q04: 提到电脑游戏研究，是为了说明灰质可以相当快地发生变化，因此大脑结构未必是固定不变的，这正支持b。

public.prompt: What is the main idea of the discussion?

options.text: How doctors compare animal heartbeats
Why plastic is replacing all human organs
How an artificial heart gave one patient a new chance to live
Why transplant waiting lists are now unnecessary

evidence.quote:
This total artificial heart has given a new lease of life to a patient in the United
States

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就概括核心内容：一个全人工心脏让美国的一位病人获得“新生”。这既说明技术对象，也说明其对患者生命的意义，所以最能代表主旨。

optionRationalesZh.a: 动物心跳问题只是节目里的小测验，不是主题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目聚焦一个特定人工心脏案例，没有说所有器官都会被塑料替代。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据句直接概括了技术与患者结果。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有说等待名单因此不再需要。

public.prompt: Why did doctors decide to replace Troy Golden's whole heart?

options.text: He wanted a quieter machine
He could not find a donor and a normal pump would not work
His disease had already been cured
His doctors wanted to test the device on a healthy patient

evidence.quote: put on a list for a heart transplant in January 2010

heart was in such a bad condition a normal heart pump wouldn’t
work for him

private.explanationZh: 节目给出两个直接原因：他在移植名单上却找不到捐献者，而且心脏状况差到普通心泵也不能使用。只有把这两点合并起来，才能完整解释为什么医生采取“整颗心替换”的方案。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说更换整颗心是为了降低噪音。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。缺少供体加上普通心泵无效，构成医生改用全人工心脏的关键背景。
optionRationalesZh.c: 相反，病情非常严重，并没有被治愈。
optionRationalesZh.d: 患者并不健康，这也不是对健康人做实验。

public.prompt: Why does the program mention that Troy Golden's wife can hear the heart beating?

options.text: To show that the device is difficult to repair
To explain why the heart needs more batteries
To show that the device's noise reassures her that he is alive
To prove patients prefer artificial hearts to natural ones

evidence.quote:
she can hear it beating so she knows I'm alive, so
it's very comforting to her.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到妻子的感受，是为了把技术效果转化成具体的人类体验：机器虽然会发出声音，但那个声音意味着“他还活着”，所以反而让家人安心。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这段没有谈维修难度。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目只说装置有电池，并未把妻子的感受与电池需求联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文明确说听到心跳声让她知道丈夫活着，因此感到安慰。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目只反映这个家庭的具体感受，不能推广为所有病人的偏好。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Troy Golden's comment about the artificial heart?

options.text: He regrets agreeing to the operation
He is more concerned with feeling alive than with the heart being natural
He expects a donor heart within days
He no longer notices the device at all

evidence.quote:
I
guess it's not mine, but it is wonderful to feel alive again.

private.explanationZh: 他说“它不是我的”，随即又强调“再次感到活着真是太好了”。这表明他最看重的是重新获得生命感，而不是执着于器官是否天然属于自己。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这句话表达的是感激和欣慰，不是后悔。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。转折词 but 清楚显示重点落在“活着”的价值上。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有说他马上会得到供体心脏。
optionRationalesZh.d: 前面他说自己除了声音之外不太注意它，但这里更强调的是“活着”的感受，不是完全注意不到。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110303-a-new-lease-of-life-q01: 整段讨论围绕Troy Golden接受全人工心脏并重新获得生机展开，因此主旨是人工心脏如何让一位病人重新获得活下去的机会，选c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110303-a-new-lease-of-life-q02: 节目说明他需要移植，但普通心脏泵对他无效，所以医生把他的整颗心脏移除并换成人工心脏；题目中的b准确概括了原因。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110303-a-new-lease-of-life-q03: Troy说装置声音很大，他妻子能听到心跳声，并因此感到安心，因为这让她知道他还活着，所以c正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110303-a-new-lease-of-life-q04: Troy说“我想它不算是我的心，但再次感到活着真是太好了”，可推知他更看重重新活着的感觉，而不是心脏是否天然属于自己，因此选b。

public.prompt: What central question does the discussion explore about citizen journalism?

options.text: To compare what citizen journalists and traditional journalists each contribute
To teach listeners how to create a blog
To show that internet use is highest in South Korea
To argue that newspapers should stop verifying stories

evidence.quote:
Could citizen journalists
ever replace traditional journalists?

private.explanationZh: 节目围绕“公民记者能否取代传统记者”展开，因此核心不是教操作，也不是做网络普及率排名，而是比较两类新闻生产者各自的作用与局限。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个问题句直接设定了整段讨论的主线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有提供建博客的步骤。
optionRationalesZh.c: 网络普及率只是开头问答，不是主要讨论目标。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目多次强调 verify 的重要性，不是要取消核实。

public.prompt: Why does Peter Barron say that high-quality material rises to the top?

options.text: To argue that strong citizen-produced content can become trusted
To show that social media posts spread fastest when they are short
To criticize established media brands for ignoring blogs
To explain why Google ranks stories by political position

evidence.quote:
the very high quality material rises to the
top and becomes a trusted brand, alongside the trusted brands that already exist.

private.explanationZh: Peter Barron举这句话，是为了支持一个观点：在信息发布更民主化的环境中，优质内容即使来自非传统媒体，也可能逐渐获得公众信任，成为“trusted brand”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据直接把“高质量材料”与“trusted brand”连在一起。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有讨论帖子长度与传播速度。
optionRationalesZh.c: 他不是在批评传统媒体，而是在说新旧可信来源可以并存。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有谈 Google 按政治立场排序新闻。

public.prompt: Why does Alan Rusbridger use the example of Wikileaks?

options.text: To show that citizen journalists can safely publish any raw data
To show that huge amounts of information still need journalists to analyze and verify them
To argue that newspapers should never work together
To suggest online leaks are usually meaningless

evidence.quote:
300 million words would have been
completely meaningless if it had been dumped on the internet, as well as being
completely unsafe.

the journalist still has a valuable role as mediator, analyser and finder and
verifier of stories.

private.explanationZh: Wikileaks在这里是论证例子：光有大量原始材料并不够，如果没有记者筛选、核实、整理和解释，信息既可能毫无意义，也可能不安全。因此这个例子是为“传统记者仍有价值”服务的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文恰好说直接把材料丢到网上会不安全，不支持这个说法。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两段证据分别说明“原始材料的问题”和“记者仍有的功能”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目提到多家媒体合作处理材料，并没有反对合作。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这里不是说所有泄露都没意义，而是说未经处理的大量数据难以理解。

public.prompt: According to Anne McElvoy, when is citizen journalism likely to struggle most?

options.text: When people already agree about who is right
When a story is visible in the streets
When events are unclear and audiences need analysis to understand them
When traditional journalists have access to social media

evidence.quote: much tougher time when we have situations

you do rely a bit on the old trade to have analysis skills and
to help you out there.

private.explanationZh: Anne McElvoy认为，公民新闻在“情况不清楚、受众不知道该站哪边”时会更吃力，因为这类局面需要传统新闻的分析与解释能力。第二段证据直接点出这种依赖。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她明确说在大家大致知道立场时，公民新闻反而很有价值。
optionRationalesZh.b: “story is on the street”正是她认为公民新闻有用的情形之一。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。不清楚发生了什么时，更需要分析技能来帮助理解。
optionRationalesZh.d: 她讨论的是分析能力，不是谁能使用社交媒体。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-citizen-journalism-q01: 节目提出核心问题是公民记者能否取代传统记者，随后分别呈现Google、Guardian和Economist的看法，本质上是在比较两者各自的作用与局限，所以a最符合。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-citizen-journalism-q02: Peter Barron说高质量内容会“rise to the top and become a trusted brand”，意思是优质的公民生产内容也可能成长为受信任的信息来源，因此a正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-citizen-journalism-q03: Alan Rusbridger用维基解密举例，是为了说明海量原始信息如果没有记者筛选、分析、删改和核实，就会毫无意义甚至不安全，因此b正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-citizen-journalism-q04: Anne McElvoy说当人们并不清楚哪一方占理、甚至不清楚事情究竟怎么回事时，公民新闻会面临更大挑战，因为此时需要传统记者的分析技能帮助理解，所以选c。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of this discussion?

options.text: Why attitudes toward shark’s fin soup are changing in Taiwan
How Taiwanese fishing boats are designed for long trips
Which shark species is the longest in the world
Why Chinese New Year customs are identical across Asia

evidence.quote:
We're talking about a traditional dish that many people expect to find on the
menu to celebrate the New Year!

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就点出主题：这是很多人过年会吃的传统菜，而后面整段讨论都围绕台湾是否继续食用鱼翅汤、媒体宣传和反对运动展开，所以主旨是人们对鱼翅汤的态度正在变化。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。该选项概括了节目后续关于禁食鱼翅、媒体影响、年轻人与名人反对等内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目只顺带提到渔船节省空间，并不是在介绍船只设计。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这只是开场小测验，不是整段讨论的核心。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目谈的是一道菜及其争议，并没有比较整个亚洲的新年习俗是否相同。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers include the Taipei restaurant owner’s comments?

options.text: To show that some business owners think the campaign has hurt them and reject a ban
To prove that most customers now prefer vegetarian dishes
To explain how restaurants preserve shark fins for export
To show that the media invented the controversy from nothing

evidence.quote:
The media coverage has affected us,
but many of the fish are caught whole, not just for the fins. In Taiwan, we eat all the
shark, not just the fin.

private.explanationZh: 餐馆老板的话提供了与禁令支持者相对的立场：他认为媒体报道影响了生意，而且辩称台湾人并非只吃鱼翅。这段话的作用是展示争议中的反对观点及其经济关切。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。他明确说媒体报道影响生意，并强调整条鲨鱼都会吃，说明他并不认同全面禁止。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有提到顾客改吃素食。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里没有讨论出口或保存流程。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。老板说媒体影响了生意，但并没有说媒体凭空制造了整场争议。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about Finn’s view of eating shark’s fin soup?

options.text: He thinks the soup is acceptable only when served at weddings
He enjoyed it but feels uneasy because of the sharks’ suffering
He believes the campaign is mostly based on false media reports
He opposes eating any kind of fish for cultural reasons

evidence.quote:
But at the same time, you feel a bit bad
because of the cruelty to the shark.

private.explanationZh: Finn 先承认自己喝过鱼翅汤，也说味道不错，但紧接着表示会因为对鲨鱼的残忍而感到内疚，所以他的态度是“吃过且觉得好吃，但在伦理上不舒服”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。他提到是在婚礼上喝过，但没有说只有婚礼场合才可以接受。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这正好概括了他既认可味道、又对残忍做法感到不安的复杂态度。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。他没有说反对运动是假的，反而承认残忍问题让人难受。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。他并没有反对所有鱼类，只是对鱼翅涉及的残忍做法有顾虑。

public.prompt: What do the speakers suggest is at the core of the issue?

options.text: Whether shark’s fin soup is expensive enough to remain prestigious
Whether Taiwan should stop serving traditional wedding food
Whether sharks are being killed wastefully just for their fins
Whether the media should stop interviewing restaurant owners

evidence.quote:
And I think that's really at the core of the issue
here, isn't it?

private.explanationZh: 在结尾处，Finn 说如果整条鱼都被食用而不是浪费掉，就会“有很大不同”，随后又说这正是问题核心。由此可推知，核心并不是传统本身，而是只取鱼翅、造成浪费与残忍的做法。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目确实提到“prestigious”，但结尾强调的核心不是价格或地位。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。婚宴只是一个例子，核心争议并非是否取消所有传统菜肴。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。结尾明确把“是否整条鱼被利用”与问题核心联系起来，指向只割鱼翅的浪费与残忍。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。媒体采访只是讨论的一部分，不是他们总结出的核心问题。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-shark-s-fin-soup-q01: 节目先介绍鱼翅汤及禁用争议，随后讨论媒体报道、台湾年轻人和名人的反对态度，主线是台湾对鱼翅汤的看法正在变化。其余选项只是局部细节或无关扩大。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-shark-s-fin-soup-q02: 餐馆老板说“there's hardly any business”，并表示鱼是整条捕捞、台湾“eat all the shark”，明显用来呈现商家认为运动影响生意且不赞成禁令的立场。其他选项都不是这段话的功能。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-shark-s-fin-soup-q03: Finn明确说自己喝过而且“it did taste very nice”，但同时“you feel a bit bad because of the cruelty to the shark”，所以是喜欢味道却对残忍做法感到不安。其余选项都超出或违背原文。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-shark-s-fin-soup-q04: 结尾处Yvonne问“if the whole fish is being eaten”，Finn回答如果整条鱼都吃掉、不浪费，“that’s really at the core of the issue”。核心就是是否只是为了鱼翅而浪费性地杀鲨鱼。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of this program?

options.text: Why dreams during REM sleep are difficult to remember
New evidence that sleepwalking can run in families and why that matters
How hospitals treat all sleep disorders with medication
Why adults sleepwalk more often than children

evidence.quote:
This is a new development by scientists in the United States
that shows that sleepwalking is genetic

private.explanationZh: 开头直接说明本期要谈的新发现：梦游具有遗传性。后文又用案例、专家评论和 DNA 研究结果说明其风险与潜在应用，因此主旨就是“梦游可能会家族遗传及其意义”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。REM 只是开头小测验的一部分，不是整期节目的中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。该选项同时概括了遗传发现和后面关于风险、检测、治疗意义的讨论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目并没有说所有睡眠障碍都靠药物治疗。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目说儿童和成人都可能梦游，但重点不是比较谁更多。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers include Margaret Brand’s experience?

options.text: To show that sleepwalking can happen without awareness and can lead to risky actions
To argue that morphine is a common treatment for sleepwalking
To prove that most sleepwalkers spend hours outside their homes
To show that only people who live alone can sleepwalk

evidence.quote:
wake
up in the morning and find that things had been moved or eaten or forgotten – and it
had to be me because I was the only person in the flat.

private.explanationZh: Margaret Brand 的经历用来把“梦游”具体化：她有时根本不知道自己夜里做过什么，第二天才从物品被移动、被吃掉等痕迹判断出来。这说明梦游可能在无意识状态下发生，而且会带来实际风险。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。她的例子突出两个点：当事人可能毫无察觉，而且行为可能危险。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她提到吗啡是为了说明梦游时可能误服药物，不是推荐治疗。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。她说自己常在厨房醒来，没有说大多数梦游者会在户外待很久。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。独居只是让她更容易判断行为是自己做的，不代表只有独居者会梦游。

public.prompt: According to the program, what did scientists find in the family they studied?

options.text: A hormone imbalance that usually disappears with age
A defective gene on chromosome 20 linked to sleepwalking
A sleep pattern caused mainly by stress at work
A genetic change that guarantees all children will sleepwalk

evidence.quote:
Scientists examined the DNA – the genetic code of a family of four generations
who suffer from sleepwalking, and found that they carried a defective gene,
chromosome 20.

private.explanationZh: 节目明确说，研究者检查了四代家族成员的 DNA，发现他们携带第 20 号染色体上的缺陷基因。这是节目中最直接的研究发现。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有提到激素失衡。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这与原文对 chromosome 20 上 defective gene 的描述完全一致。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目讨论的是遗传线索，不是工作压力造成的睡眠模式。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文说是 50-50 的概率，不是所有孩子都会梦游。

public.prompt: What is the speakers’ overall view of the new genetic discovery?

options.text: It already explains every case of sleepwalking completely
It is promising but still needs more research before broad conclusions are made
It is too weak to be useful for any tests or treatments
It proves REM sleep directly causes sleepwalking

evidence.quote:
More work is needed to see if the
discovery will explain all cases of sleepwalking, but in the meantime, the researchers say
it should help them to develop tests and treatments.

private.explanationZh: 结尾既强调“还需要更多研究”，又指出这项发现有望帮助开发检测和治疗方法，所以整体态度是谨慎乐观，而不是夸大或否定。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文明确说还不知道能否解释所有案例。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它同时抓住了“仍需研究”和“有潜在帮助”这两个关键信息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目恰恰说这项发现可能有助于测试和治疗。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。REM 是节目开头的知识题，研究发现并没有证明 REM 直接导致梦游。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-sleepwalking-q01: 节目围绕新发现展开：梦游具有遗传性，会在家族中出现，并讨论这对检测和治疗的意义。REM只是开头小测，其他选项不是全篇主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-sleepwalking-q02: Margaret Brand说自己常常不知道梦游过，醒来才发现东西被移动或吃掉，甚至睡着时服用吗啡。这段材料的作用是说明梦游可能在无意识中发生，并伴随危险行为。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-sleepwalking-q03: 节目明确说科学家研究四代家族DNA，发现他们携带“a defective gene, chromosome 20”。这与选项b完全对应；其余选项不是文中发现。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110310-sleepwalking-q04: 节目说这一发现可能帮助开发测试和治疗，但Michelle Roberts也强调“More work is needed”来看是否能解释所有病例，因此总体态度是有前景但仍需更多研究。

public.prompt: What broader problem is this discussion mainly about?

options.text: The history of football development in Cairo
Training methods used by professional Scottish clubs
How football rivalries can lead to wider violence and what authorities might do about it
Why local derbies are always harmless entertainment

evidence.quote:
A bit of friendly rivalry probably doesn’t hurt anybody, but sometimes football
rivalries spill over into violence.

private.explanationZh: 节目从“球队 rivalry 有时会演变成 violence”切入，后面又讨论了比赛混乱、家庭暴力上升以及如何通过赛程、酒类管理和转播来减少问题，因此主旨是球迷对立如何扩散成更广泛的暴力及其应对。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。开头小问答提到 Cairo，但节目主体不是讲开罗足球史。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有讲训练方法，而是讲社会后果。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它概括了节目从问题到应对措施的完整讨论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目明确说 rivalry 有时会升级成暴力，不是总是 harmless。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers mention the latest Celtic-Rangers match?

options.text: To give a concrete example of how a rivalry escalated into disorder
To compare Scottish tactics with Egyptian tactics
To show that managers should replace sent-off players immediately
To explain why referees use a final whistle

evidence.quote:
the mayhem during the latest clash has been described as an
embarrassment to Scottish football.

private.explanationZh: Celtic 和 Rangers 的比赛被作为具体案例，说明激烈 rivalry 如何从“heated exchanges”进一步发展为混乱、冲突和社会尴尬。这是用实例支撑前面提出的问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个案例正是为了把抽象的 rivalry 与实际混乱联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有把这场比赛拿来做苏格兰与埃及战术比较。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。重点不是换人规则，而是冲突升级。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。提到 final whistle 是描述赛后冲突，不是在解释裁判器具。

public.prompt: What does Les Grey imply about reducing violence around these matches?

options.text: Fans would calm down mainly if ticket prices increased
Changing when games are played and how alcohol and TV coverage are handled could help
Police should ignore incidents that happen after the match ends
Rivalry disappears naturally when clubs use more foreign players

evidence.quote:
If that game hadn’t been on a Wednesday night, we’d have seen a lot more trouble had
that been a Sunday lunchtime game or even a Saturday afternoon game. So we’ll be
looking at when to play these games. We need to look at the licensing laws. We need to
look at the coverage of the games, and by that I’m going to upset a lot people and say
perhaps we need to stop televising one or two of them.

private.explanationZh: Les Grey 不只是描述问题，还提出了几个可以降低暴力风险的方向：调整比赛时间、关注酒类许可法规、重新考虑转播方式。由此可推知，他认为外部管理措施会影响暴力程度。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有提票价。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这正是他提出的几项干预措施的综合概括。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。相反，他强调赛后家庭暴力等问题需要认真处理。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文完全没有谈到外援人数。

public.prompt: How is the discussion mainly organized?

options.text: From a definition of rivalry to a specific case and then to possible solutions
From one team’s season record to a quiz about world cities
From fan interviews to a summary of transfer news
From refereeing rules to a history of Glasgow

evidence.quote:
A bit of friendly rivalry probably doesn’t hurt anybody, but sometimes football
rivalries spill over into violence.

If that game hadn’t been on a Wednesday night, we’d have seen a lot more trouble had
that been a Sunday lunchtime game or even a Saturday afternoon game. So we’ll be
looking at when to play these games. We need to look at the licensing laws. We need to
look at the coverage of the games, and by that I’m going to upset a lot people and say
perhaps we need to stop televising one or two of them.

incidents of
domestic violence jumped by 70% after the last Celtic versus Rangers mat

private.explanationZh: 节目结构很清楚：先定义和概述 rivalry 可能升级成暴力，再用 Celtic-Rangers 的案例展开，最后转向官员和警方提出的对策。因此最准确的是“定义问题—举例—讨论解决办法”的组织方式。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。该选项准确概括了节目推进顺序。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有围绕赛季战绩展开，也没有以城市问答作为主体结构。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。没有转会新闻这部分内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。虽然提到苏格兰和比赛规则相关词汇，但主体不是裁判规则或格拉斯哥历史。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110317-football-rivalries-q01: 节目先定义足球宿敌关系，随后重点讨论这种对立如何“spill over into violence”，并介绍政府、警方可能采取的措施，所以主旨是球迷对立引发更广泛暴力及应对。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110317-football-rivalries-q02: 提到凯尔特人与流浪者的最新比赛，是为了给出一个具体案例：比赛中有人被罚下、教练冲突、场面混乱，展示宿敌关系如何升级为失序和暴力。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110317-football-rivalries-q03: Les Grey提到比赛时间安排、酒类销售法规以及电视转播都需要调整，认为这些改变可能减少赛前赛后的暴力问题，因此b最符合他的隐含建议。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110317-football-rivalries-q04: 节目结构很清楚：先解释rivalry和derby，再举凯尔特人对流浪者的案例，最后讨论政府和警方的解决办法，所以是从定义到案例再到对策。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: How satellite navigation is popular but can be risky when people rely on it too much
How to install car entertainment systems correctly
Why GPS abbreviations are hard to remember
Why ships usually travel faster than aircraft

evidence.quote: Engineers have been asking if we’re becoming too reliant on them.

private.explanationZh: 节目虽然承认 sat nav 很流行，但真正要讨论的是“过度依赖”带来的问题，后文举了司机走错路和 GPS 信号受干扰等例子来展开这一主旨。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了节目先说流行、再说风险的核心思路。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目不是安装教程。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。GPS 缩写只是开头提问，不是主体。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目提到船只是为了说明信号干扰风险，不是比较交通工具速度。

public.prompt: Why does Antony Chmarny say sat navs should be used only as an aid to driving?

options.text: To stress that drivers must still use their own judgment
To suggest that paper maps should be banned from cars
To argue that all one-way streets should be removed
To prove that voice navigation is more dangerous than screen navigation

evidence.quote:
If it doesn’t look suitable, don’t drive down it, don’t drive down a
one-way street the wrong way if a sat nav tells you to do that.

private.explanationZh: 这句话的作用不是单纯给驾驶建议，而是强调设备只能辅助，真正做决定的还是司机自己；如果路况明显不对，人应当优先相信常识。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。后面 Alice 也直接总结为“use your common sense”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有主张禁止纸质地图。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里不是讨论城市道路规划。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文没有比较语音和屏幕导航哪一种更危险。

public.prompt: What does Professor Martyn Thomas’s ship example suggest about GPS interference?

options.text: It causes problems only when crews ignore radio messages
It can create believable but inaccurate position data that may mislead other systems
It mainly affects paper maps rather than onboard equipment
It can happen only when a transmitter is placed on the ship itself

evidence.quote:
quite often the onboard systems, without saying
anything was wrong, was reporting their position as being just subtly wrong.

private.explanationZh: 关键不只是“系统出错”，而是系统在没有报错的情况下给出“看起来合理但其实偏差”的位置信息。这种错误更危险，因为它还会被传播给其他系统和船只。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。例子并没有把问题归咎于船员忽视无线电。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确抓住了“没有报警却位置 subtly wrong”的危险。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文说的是 onboard equipment 和广播出的位置信息，不是纸质地图。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。前文说干扰可以从高处发射，并不要求发射器在船上。

public.prompt: How do the speakers develop the discussion?

options.text: By moving from everyday driving mistakes to a broader warning about technical vulnerability
By comparing different brands of sat nav voices
By describing the history of road signs before GPS was invented
By arguing that all employers should track company cars

evidence.quote: Engineers have been asking if we’re becoming too reliant on them.

we should be careful about becoming too reliant on GPS and sat navs, because
they can be interfered with.

private.explanationZh: 节目先从司机迷路、卡在窄路等日常案例入手，再上升到工程师关于信号脆弱性的担忧，组织方式是由生活化问题逐步推进到更广泛的技术风险。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了从日常驾驶失误到技术层面警告的推进。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。个性化语音只是轻松插曲，不是结构主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有回顾路牌历史。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。结尾只顺带提到公司车屏蔽装置，不是节目主论点。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110324-satellite-navigation-system: 节目先说sat nav很流行，接着举司机受误导的例子，再讨论人们过度依赖以及GPS信号易受干扰的风险，因此主旨是它虽普及但过度依赖有危险。
Antony Chmarny说如果路看起来不合适就别开进去，即使sat nav这样指示也不该逆行。随后Alice总结为“use your common sense”，所以他是在强调司机仍要自己判断。
Martyn Thomas举船只例子说明，系统有时会报告极端错误，更可怕的是“without saying anything was wrong”时位置会“just subtly wrong”，而且还会把错误位置广播给其他船，因此是看似可信但不准确的数据会误导其他系统。
节目先讲日常驾驶中sat nav导致的桥下卡车、山路事故，再扩展到工程师对GPS脆弱性和干扰风险的警告，所以组织方式是从日常错误推进到更广泛的技术脆弱性。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of this discussion?

options.text: To explain why James Watt’s reconstructed workshop reveals the breadth of his work
To prove James Watt invented every important machine of the Industrial Revolution
To compare Scottish museums with London museums
To describe how modern engineers build steam engines

evidence.quote:
This week we’re talking about an inventor’s
workshop which has been reassembled after almost 200 years.

private.explanationZh: 开头就指出节目在谈一间被重新组装的发明家工作坊，后面几位讲者都借这个工作坊说明 James Watt 不只是“蒸汽机”人物，而是兴趣和项目都非常广泛，因此主旨是通过重建的工作坊展示他工作的广度。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它既点出“reassembled workshop”，也点出节目真正想说明的“breadth”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目反而澄清他并非凭空发明了蒸汽机，而是改进它。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Science Museum 只是展示地点，不是馆际比较主题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目不是现代工程教学。

public.prompt: Why does Tom Fielden describe the workshop as a cornucopia of gadgets, tools, and contraptions?

options.text: To suggest the museum arranged the objects carelessly
To show the workshop was used mainly for storing food
To argue that Watt preferred simple equipment
To emphasize how full and varied the workshop contents are

evidence.quote:
the contents, a
regular cornucopia of gadgets, tools, contraptions, you name it, have all been
painstakingly reassembled here in the main hall of the Science Museum.

private.explanationZh: “cornucopia” 在这里不是字面意思，而是强调工作坊里东西极多、类型丰富、令人惊叹。这一描述帮助听者理解工作坊为何值得重建与展出。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文说 objects were painstakingly reassembled，恰好说明整理得很仔细。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。cornucopia 是比喻，不是说里面存放食物。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这段话强调的是丰富多样，不是设备简单。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。该比喻突出的是数量多、种类杂、内容丰富。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Ben Russell’s comments about Watt’s other projects?

options.text: Watt focused almost entirely on a single steam-engine design
The workshop provides evidence of a wide range of practical and scientific interests
The museum has identified every object with complete certainty
Watt’s non-engine work mattered little because it was unfinished

evidence.quote:
it shows a
lot of his other projects as well, from chemistry to pottery, instrument making, even
musical instrument making.

private.explanationZh: Ben Russell 列举了化学、陶器、仪器制作、乐器制作等多个领域，说明这个工作坊提供了 James Watt 多方面兴趣与实践活动的实物证据，而不是只围绕蒸汽机。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。列举这么多领域正说明他并非只做一种蒸汽机设计。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。该选项准确概括了“other projects as well”的含义。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文说对象数量巨大，但没有说每件都被完全确定识别。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讲话者是在强调这些项目的重要性和多样性，而不是贬低它们。

public.prompt: Which organizational pattern best describes the discussion?

options.text: It introduces Watt’s reputation, then uses the reconstructed workshop to show how diverse his work was
It begins with museum ticket prices and ends with a quiz about inventions
It presents a debate between two curators about whether Watt mattered
It traces the history of vacuum cleaners before discussing steam

evidence.quote:
This week we’re talking about an inventor’s
workshop which has been reassembled after almost 200 years.
He was, if you like, a one man innovation centre.

private.explanationZh: 节目先介绍 Watt 的身份与这间重建工作坊，再通过记者和馆员的描述不断扩展到“他做过许多别的事”，最后用“one man innovation centre”总结他的多面性，所以结构是“背景介绍—工作坊证据—多样性结论”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这一选项最符合节目从背景到证据再到总结的推进方式。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。票价并未出现，发明顺序小测验只是结尾小环节。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。几位讲者都在补充说明，而不是进行是否重要的辩论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。吸尘器只出现在小测验里，不是主体结构。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110331-watt-s-workshop-q01: 节目核心不是单讲蒸汽机，而是借重建的工作坊说明James Watt兴趣广泛、项目多样，因此a最准确概括主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110331-watt-s-workshop-q02: Tom Fielden把工作坊称为“a regular cornucopia of gadgets, tools, contraptions”，随后主持人解释为大量而丰富的收藏，所以这是为了强调工作坊内容非常充实且多样。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110331-watt-s-workshop-q03: Ben Russell说工作坊记录了Watt整个工作生涯与兴趣，不仅有engine，还有chemistry、pottery、instrument making等，说明这个工作坊为其广泛的实践和科学兴趣提供了证据。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110331-watt-s-workshop-q04: 节目先介绍Watt的名声与蒸汽机背景，再通过重建工作坊和馆员评论展示他并非只做蒸汽，而是涉猎极广，所以a最符合整体组织结构。

public.prompt: According to the discussion, what is the origin story behind the term P.O.S.H.?

options.text: It referred to the shaded cabin locations preferred by wealthy passengers on sea voyages.
It described a formal pronunciation guide used by the royal family.
It was the nickname for a political movement defending the upper class.
It came from a fashion label associated with expensive clothing.

evidence.quote:
It's thought that P.O.S.H. stands for Port Out, Starboard Home.
This relates to the location of the best cabins for the richest people on long sea
crossings, who preferred shadier cabins in strong sunshine - port side on the
way out, starboard side on the way home!

private.explanationZh: 节目明确解释，P.O.S.H. 被认为来自远洋航行中富裕乘客偏好的舱位位置：去程选左舷阴凉舱，回程选右舷阴凉舱。因此这个词的来历与富人乘船时选择较舒适、较阴凉的舱房有关。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文直接说明该缩写与富人长途海运时偏好的最佳舱位位置有关。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目用女王的讲话示范了 posh 口音，但并没有说这个词源自王室的发音指南。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。关于保护上层阶级的讨论是后面的社会争议，不是这个词的来源。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目提到 posh 可以形容时髦、昂贵，但没有说它来自某个服装品牌。

public.prompt: Why does Alice include the recording of Queen Elizabeth II?

options.text: To prove that upper-class accents are now the most popular in Britain.
To illustrate the kind of accent people often describe as posh.
To show that the Queen publicly supported Julian Fellowes's argument.
To contrast a modern pop-culture accent with an older London accent.

evidence.quote: That was Queen Elizabeth II speaking in an accent we would usually call posh.

private.explanationZh: Alice 播放女王的讲话，不是为了讨论王室观点，而是给听者一个具体例子，让大家直接感受到什么样的发音通常会被称为 posh。这个片段承担的是示范和说明作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说这种口音如今最受欢迎，反而提到它在某些职业或社交圈未必讨喜。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文紧接着就说那是通常会被称为 posh 的口音。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。女王录音只是口音例子，不是在表达对某个作者观点的支持。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里不是拿流行文化口音与伦敦口音做历史对比，而是单纯示范 posh accent。

public.prompt: What is James Delingpole mainly arguing about toffs?

options.text: They should stop using posh accents in public life.
They should avoid television programs that mock regional speech.
They should receive the same protection from hostile remarks as other minorities.
They should be excluded from political debates about class.

evidence.quote:
He wants posh people - or what he calls toffs - to be treated as equally as other
minorities.

private.explanationZh: Delingpole 的核心观点不是要求同情，而是要求一种“公平对待”。他把 toffs 也看成应受保护的少数群体之一，认为公开侮辱他们不应被轻易接受。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说他主张上层人士停止使用 posh 口音。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。虽然他提到电视节目中的言论，但重点不是回避电视节目，而是批评双重标准。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文直接说他希望 posh people 像其他少数群体一样被平等对待。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。他是在要求更平等的讨论环境，不是把他们排除出政治讨论。

public.prompt: What does Owen Jones think the debate about laughing at posh people is really doing?

options.text: It proves that Britain no longer has a class system.
It shows that upper-class people dominate television comedy.
It encourages more people from all classes to enter journalism.
It distracts attention from the lack of class representation in politics and the media.

evidence.quote:
he thinks the debate is trying to disguise the fact that people
from all classes are not represented in politics and the media?

private.explanationZh: Owen Jones 的意思更深一层：他认为这场关于“能不能嘲笑 posh people”的争论，本质上是在转移视线，让人们忽略英国政治和媒体中阶层代表性失衡的问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。相反，节目一直在讨论阶级差异依然存在。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说上层人士主导电视喜剧，这不是 Owen Jones 的论点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里谈的是掩盖结构性代表问题，不是鼓励更多人进入新闻界。
