optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。Alice 的转述明确指出，这场争论是在掩盖各阶层在政治与媒体中代表不足的事实。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110616-posh-people-q01: 节目说明 P.O.S.H. 被认为代表 Port Out, Starboard Home，指富裕乘客在海上航行时偏好的阴凉舱位，因此答案是 a。其余选项都没有得到 transcript 支持。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110616-posh-people-q02: Alice 播放女王录音后立刻说明这就是人们通常会称为 posh 的口音，所以录音的作用是举例说明这种口音，而不是证明它最受欢迎或表达女王立场。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110616-posh-people-q03: James Delingpole 认为 toffs 是仍可被公开攻击的少数群体，主张他们也应像其他 minorities 一样免受敌意言论，因此答案是 c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110616-posh-people-q04: Owen Jones 认为这场关于是否能嘲笑 posh people 的争论，实际上是在转移人们对英国政治和媒体阶层代表性不足问题的注意力，所以答案是 d。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion?

options.text: How cyber crime is becoming a more commercial, business-like activity.
Why home users should stop using all security software.
How social media companies advertise their services online.
Why traditional robbery has become easier than online crime.

evidence.quote:
This online crime is becoming more commercialised and this is what experts
were recently discussing at a meeting in London.

private.explanationZh: 节目主线不是单纯定义 cyber crime，而是在说明这种犯罪活动越来越商业化、产业化。后面关于 botnet 租赁、恶意代码投放以及政府应对，都是围绕这一主线展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。开头直接点出网络犯罪正在变得更商业化，这是整段讨论的中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有主张用户停止使用安全软件，反而提到使用安全软件是好主意。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。讨论对象是网络犯罪集团，不是社交媒体公司的广告业务。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目对比了传统犯罪与网络犯罪，但重点不是说传统抢劫更容易。

public.prompt: Why does Charlie McMurdy compare cyber criminals with an old armed robbery team?

options.text: To argue that internet crime is less dangerous than robbery.
To show that online criminals can cooperate internationally without gathering in one place.
To suggest that most hackers used to work in pubs.
To claim that police methods for cyber crime should ignore international links.

evidence.quote:
Criminals can meet virtually anywhere and work as a criminal enterprise - like
a business.

private.explanationZh: 这个类比的作用是突出网络犯罪的组织方式已经改变。过去的犯罪团伙需要线下碰头，而网络犯罪分子可以分散在世界各地，通过网络虚拟协作，仍然像企业一样运作。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目并没有说网络犯罪危险更小，反而强调其规模和威胁都很大。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。该比较正是为了说明他们不必线下聚集，也能跨国协作。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。提到酒吧只是反衬旧式犯罪会面方式，不是说黑客曾在酒吧工作。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。McMurdy 明确强调每项调查都有国际层面，不是要忽视国际联系。

public.prompt: According to Tony Osborn, what service can part of a cyber-crime organization sell?

options.text: Legal insurance for people whose computers have been hacked.
Government training courses for future cyber experts.
Temporary access to a botnet that customers can use to spread malware or spam.
A secure messaging platform for ordinary office workers.

evidence.quote:
you can
rent our botnet for $100 or whatever, and that will give you access to these number of
devices.

private.explanationZh: Tony Osborn 用非常直接的商业语言描述这种模式：组织的一部分甚至可以把 botnet 当服务出租，付费者就能获得对一批受控设备的访问权限，并进一步发送恶意代码或垃圾信息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有提到任何合法保险服务。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。培训网络专家是 John Bassett 提到的公共投入方向，不是犯罪组织出售的服务。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文明确说可以花钱租用 botnet，并获得这些设备的访问权。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里讨论的是恶意 botnet 业务，不是正常办公通讯平台。

public.prompt: What does John Bassett imply is the foundation of effective cyber security?

options.text: Replacing internet users with automated security systems.
Spending all available funds only on military defence.
Eliminating email so that spam cannot spread.
Education that supports public awareness and the development of expertise.

evidence.quote:
the cornerstone of good cyber security has to be
based on education.

private.explanationZh: Bassett 虽然列出多个花钱方向，但最后把 education 说成 cornerstone，说明他认为教育是基础。这里的教育不仅指课堂学习，也包括公众意识提升和培养更多网络安全专家。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有主张用自动系统取代用户。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。国防只是支出方向之一，Bassett 没说应把所有资金只投向军方。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。垃圾邮件是问题之一，但节目没有提出取消电子邮件这种极端做法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原文明确把 education 说成良好网络安全的基石。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110623-botnets-q01: 整段讨论围绕 cyber crime 如何越来越商业化、像生意一样运作展开，包括出租 botnet、按服务收费等内容，因此主旨是 a。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110623-botnets-q02: Charlie McMurdy 把网络罪犯和传统持械抢劫团伙对比，是为了说明网络罪犯即使分散在世界各地，也能在线协作，不必在同一地点碰面，因此答案是 b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110623-botnets-q03: Tony Osborn 明确说犯罪组织的一部分可以出租 botnet，让客户在一段时间内利用这些设备传播 malware 或 spam，所以答案是 c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110623-botnets-q04: John Bassett 列出多项优先事项后，明确说良好 cyber security 的基石必须建立在 education 上，并且这会支持 public awareness 和专家培养，因此答案是 d。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: NASA's final shuttle flight and what the end of the shuttle era means.
How the Moon landings led to cheaper private tourism in space.
Why reusable rockets replaced every other kind of spacecraft.
How American and Russian astronauts trained together in Texas.

evidence.quote:
Today we're talking about NASA's final
shuttle flight.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头就明确指出主题是 NASA 的最后一次航天飞机飞行。后面关于历史意义、成本、失业影响以及未来谁来送宇航员上天，都是围绕这一“航天飞机时代结束”展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。开场白直接点明主题就是 NASA 最后一趟航天飞机任务及其意义。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目提到登月人数只是问答，不是主线，也没有说因此带来廉价私人太空旅游。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说可重复使用火箭已经取代一切其他航天器。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目提到 Texas 的就业损失，但不是关于美俄联合训练。

public.prompt: According to the BBC report, what had many people expected space travel to become when Columbia launched?

options.text: Slow but extremely safe.
Cheap and commonplace.
Limited to scientific research only.
Dependent on Russian rockets.

evidence.quote:
people believed space
travel would be cheap and commonplace , but in fact it was expensive and
dangerous.

private.explanationZh: 记者明确对比了“人们原先的期待”和“现实结果”。期待是太空旅行会变得便宜而普遍，但现实却是昂贵且危险。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说它会变慢，且现实被描述为危险，不是极其安全。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文直接说 people believed space travel would be cheap and commonplace。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说大众期待太空旅行只限科研。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。依赖俄罗斯是航天飞机退役后的过渡安排，不是 Columbia 发射时的期待。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about Jerry Mulberry's reaction to the final shuttle mission?

options.text: He is mainly angry that private companies will take over NASA's role.
He is relieved because the shuttle program never achieved anything important.
He has mixed emotions and still has not fully absorbed that the program is ending.
He is unconcerned because he no longer follows space travel.

evidence.quote: Yeah, it's kind of a sad and a happy time.

private.explanationZh: Jerry 直接说这是“sad and happy time”，说明他的情绪是复杂的、矛盾的。一方面不舍项目结束，另一方面也为其成就感到自豪，因此最合理的推断是他既伤感又欣慰，而且仍在慢慢接受这个事实。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说他主要因私营公司接手而愤怒。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。恰恰相反，后文提到 NASA 希望人们记住航天飞机项目的成就。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原话直接体现了悲喜交加的复杂感受。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。既然他说这是 sad and happy time，就说明他非常在意，而不是无所谓。

public.prompt: Based on Dan's explanation, what is most likely to happen before private companies can regularly carry astronauts into space?

options.text: NASA will restart the shuttle program immediately after the final mission.
The Moon missions will resume within the next three years.
Most shuttle workers will be rehired to design tourist spacecraft.
NASA will rely on Russia to transport its astronauts for a period of time.

evidence.quote:
NASA is hoping that private companies will be able to take astronauts
into space, though that probably won't happen for several years. In the
meantime, NASA will pay Russia to fly its astronauts up for them.

private.explanationZh: Dan 明确给出时间顺序：私营公司短期内还做不到，因此“与此同时”NASA 会付钱让俄罗斯把宇航员送上去。这正是对近期最可能情况的直接预测。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目说航天飞机项目因成本过高而结束，没有说会立刻重启。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有预测三年内重启登月任务。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。虽然提到失业，但并没有说大多数员工会被重新雇用去做旅游飞船。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原文直接说明在私营公司成熟之前，NASA 会付费请俄罗斯执行运输任务。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110707-nasa-s-last-shuttle-flight-: 节目从开头到结尾都在讨论 NASA 最后一趟航天飞机任务，以及航天飞机时代结束意味着什么，包括历史意义、成本、失业和未来安排，因此答案是 a。
BBC 报道明确说 Columbia 当年被认为会开启一个 space travel becomes cheap and commonplace 的新时代，所以答案是 b。
Jerry Mulberry 说这是 sad and happy time，又说这种感受还没有 really set in，说明他情绪复杂，而且还没有完全消化项目结束这件事，因此答案是 c。
Dan 说明 private companies 还要过几年才能把宇航员送入太空，在此期间 NASA will pay Russia to fly its astronauts，因此最可能发生的是暂时依赖俄罗斯，答案是 d。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: Why British people have often avoided complaining and why that may be changing.
How airlines should redesign their luggage systems.
Why coffee quality has improved in British cafes.
How legal complaints in the United States are processed.

evidence.quote:
But apparently we're getting better
at complaining about problems.

private.explanationZh: 节目从一开始就把主题定为“complaining”，重点讨论英国人过去为什么不爱投诉，以及现在为什么似乎越来越愿意投诉。后面谈到邮件、推特、电视节目和民主权利，都是围绕这一变化展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。开头明确指出英国人正在变得更会投诉，这正是整段节目的核心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。航空投诉只是最后的知识问答，不是节目主旨。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。开场提到难喝咖啡只是引入话题，不是关于咖啡质量改进的节目。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目确实提到美国航空投诉数据，但重点不是美国法律程序。

public.prompt: Why does Dan mention the television program That's Life!?

options.text: To argue that television is the main reason airline complaints increased.
To show that media programs helped encourage consumers to challenge unfair treatment.
To prove that British people complain more loudly than Americans.
To introduce a legal definition of a formal complaint.

evidence.quote:
It made sure that people weren't ripped off, and encouraged people to complain
if they were.

private.explanationZh: 提到 That's Life! 的作用，是举一个文化层面的例子，说明电视节目曾主动鼓励公众在遭遇不公时站出来投诉，所以英国的投诉文化可能部分因此发生变化。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有把航空投诉增长归因于电视。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文直接说这个节目确保人们不被坑，并鼓励他们在被坑时投诉。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里没有做英美人在“投诉声音大小”上的比较。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。正式投诉的定义前面已经解释，这里提到节目是为了说明社会文化影响。

public.prompt: What does Esther Rantzen mean when she says people should 'grasp the nettle and complain'?

options.text: They should complain only after collecting legal evidence.
They should use angry language so companies will listen.
They should deal directly with an unpleasant problem instead of avoiding it.
They should complain quickly before speaking to anyone else.

evidence.quote:
if you grasp the nettle, it means you do an unpleasant task
quickly, and then it won't hurt as much.

private.explanationZh: 这个习语的重点不是“发脾气”，而是鼓起勇气正面处理一个令人不舒服的问题。放在投诉语境里，就是不要一味忍耐，而要果断、直接地把不满表达出来。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说一定要先准备法律证据。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Alice 还补充说投诉时应当保持礼貌，不是用愤怒语言。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。Alice 的解释就是大胆处理不愉快的问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里强调的是勇敢面对问题，不是机械地要求先于所有交流步骤。

public.prompt: According to the program, what caused most airline complaints in the US in 2010?

options.text: Rude airline staff.
Lost luggage.
Overpriced food on planes.
Delayed or cancelled flights.

evidence.quote:
the answer was delayed or cancelled flights. Often, lost luggage
is the main cause of complaints throughout the year, but you'll remember that
in 2010, with the ash cloud and some very extreme weather, there were lots
and lots of cancelled flights

private.explanationZh: 节目最后公布答案时说，2010 年美国航空投诉最多的原因是航班延误或取消，并进一步解释这与火山灰云和极端天气导致大量航班取消有关。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。粗鲁员工是题目中的干扰项，不是公布的正确答案。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目特地说明平时全年常见原因往往是行李丢失，但 2010 年不是。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有提到机上食物价格是主要投诉原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。主持人直接公布答案就是 delayed or cancelled flights。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110721-complaining-q01: 节目的核心是在解释英国人过去为什么不爱抱怨，以及这种情况为什么可能正在变化，所以答案是 a。其他选项只是局部细节或无关内容。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110721-complaining-q02: Dan 提到 That's Life! 是为了说明媒体节目曾鼓励消费者在遭遇不公平对待时采取行动并提出投诉，因此答案是 b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110721-complaining-q03: Esther Rantzen 说要 grasp the nettle and complain，随后 Alice 解释这个短语表示果断处理一件不愉快的事情，所以意思是不要回避问题，而要直接面对并投诉，答案是 c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110721-complaining-q04: 节目最后给出本周问题答案：2010 年美国航空投诉最多的原因是 delayed or cancelled flights，因此答案是 d。

public.prompt: According to Dan, why is pulling a sickie treated as a serious issue?

options.text: Because people missing work creates a large economic cost.
Because it usually leads to criminal charges against workers.
Because most employers cancel paid holidays after one absence.
Because surveys show only managers ever do it.

evidence.quote:
Now, according the consulting firm PricewaterhouseCoopers, people
not showing up to work costs the British economy around £32 billion a
year. So there's a serious impact from people skipping work

private.explanationZh: Dan 引用咨询公司的数据说明，不去上班会给英国经济造成巨额损失，因此“pulling a sickie”不是小玩笑，而是会带来真实成本的职场问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文明确说不上班让英国经济每年损失约 320 亿英镑。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说这种行为通常会导致刑事指控。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有提到雇主会在一次缺勤后取消带薪假。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目一开始就说三分之一的人承认做过这件事，并不是只有经理。

public.prompt: What view of skiving is shared by the two people in the street interviews?

options.text: It is always dishonest, no matter what the reason is.
Doing it occasionally may be understandable, but repeating it frequently is a problem.
It should be encouraged whenever workers dislike their jobs.
It is acceptable only if a person has a sports event to attend.

evidence.quote:
I think it's acceptable, but not too often. What can I say? It happens to everybody, you
know, big night. I just think it's part of everyday life.

But I think
taking one is OK, but if they do it again and again and again, then I think they need to
readdress that.

private.explanationZh: 两位受访者都没有把 skiving 说成绝对不可接受。共同点在于：偶尔一次还可以理解，但如果反复这样做，就说明问题更严重，需要重新审视。这个答案需要综合两人的话来判断。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。两位受访者都没有采取绝对否定立场。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。一个人说“可以，但别太频繁”，另一个人说“一次还行，反复就得重新看待”，两者都支持这一概括。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。虽然一位受访者提到如果你对工作不满意可以去面试，但共同观点并不是应当普遍鼓励旷工。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目提到世界杯或宿醉等例子，但受访者并没有把体育赛事当唯一可接受理由。

public.prompt: Why does Dan introduce the idea of duvet days?

options.text: To argue that workers should stay in bed whenever they feel bored.
To explain why Denmark gives employees the longest holidays in Europe.
To show one way companies try to reduce dishonest absences by allowing approved time off.
To show that most companies have stopped caring whether staff come to work.

evidence.quote:
A duvet day is a day when you can tell your office that you're not
coming into work, but you don't need to pretend that you're ill.

private.explanationZh: Duvet day 被引入，不是为了赞美偷懒，而是为了说明企业可以通过更灵活、公开的请假制度，减少员工撒谎装病的情况。这是节目讨论“如何减少 skiving”的一个管理策略例子。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有鼓励员工因为无聊就随时躺床上不上班。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。丹麦在节目中只是答题结果，与 duvet day 的目的无关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文说明这种制度允许员工诚实请假，因此能替代装病旷工。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。恰恰相反，这是一种管理缺勤的方式，不是雇主完全不在乎。

public.prompt: Which European country, according to the program, pulls the fewest sickies?

options.text: Latvia.
Austria.
Britain.
Denmark.

evidence.quote:
Denmark takes the fewest sickies or
pulls the fewest sickies each year.

private.explanationZh: 节目最后公布答案时明确说，丹麦是欧洲“pulls the fewest sickies”的国家，也就是最少装病请假的国家。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Latvia 是题目选项之一，但不是公布的正确答案。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Austria 也是干扰项，不是正确答案。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。英国在节目开头被说成是欧洲 false sick days 最多的国家之一，不可能是最少。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。主持人直接说 Denmark pulls the fewest sickies each year。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110804-pulling-a-sickie-q01: Dan 说不上班给英国经济造成大约 320 亿英镑的年度损失，因此 pulling a sickie 被视为严重问题的原因是它带来巨大的经济成本，答案是 a。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110804-pulling-a-sickie-q02: 两位街访者都表达了相近看法：偶尔 skive 可以理解，但如果反复这样做就有问题，所以答案是 b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110804-pulling-a-sickie-q03: Dan 引入 duvet days，是为了说明公司可以通过允许员工正式请假而不必假装生病，来减少不诚实缺勤，因此答案是 c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110804-pulling-a-sickie-q04: 节目最后明确核对说 Denmark pulls the fewest sickies each year，所以答案是 d。

public.prompt: What is the discussion mainly about?

options.text: How ebooks are becoming more popular and changing publishing
Why printed books are replacing digital reading devices
How online bookstores should set prices for novels
Why British readers are abandoning fiction

evidence.quote: Today we're talking about the rise of electronic books, or ebooks.

private.explanationZh: 节目先介绍电子书的兴起，随后讨论销量变化、出版业困境、自助出版以及作者如何适应数字环境，因此主旨是电子书日益流行并正在改变出版生态。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了整段关于电子书走红及其行业影响的讨论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。录音说的是电子书增长，不是纸质书反过来取代电子阅读。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有讨论网店如何给小说定价。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目重点不是英国读者是否放弃小说。

public.prompt: According to Sarah Lloyd, which authors are most likely to do particularly well in the digital era?

options.text: Authors who avoid online activity and focus only on print
Authors who build an online audience through public writing
Authors who publish only through large traditional companies
Authors who mainly shorten books for phone screens

evidence.quote:
build a digital audience; those that
blog or write their books a little bit in public.

private.explanationZh: Sarah Lloyd 明确表示，表现特别好的作者会经营数字受众，会写博客，或者以较公开的方式写作，因此关键在于建立线上读者群。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她强调的是线上存在感的重要性，不是回避网络。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这一项准确改写了建立数字受众和公开写作的说法。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。她没有说依赖大型传统出版社的作者更容易成功。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她谈的是受众经营，不是专门为手机缩短内容。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Sarah Waters's comments about fiction?

options.text: Electronic formats will remove the need for strong plots
Readers will care more about devices than about story content
The delivery format may change, but core storytelling elements will still matter
Authors should now write only for phones and laptops

evidence.quote:
characters and plots and settings; the real sort of basic nuts and
bolts of fiction. I think people will still want that regardless of the format in which they're
getting it.

private.explanationZh: Sarah Waters 认为，不管读者通过什么载体阅读，小说仍然需要人物、情节和背景这些“基本部件”。因此可以推断，形式会变化，但叙事核心仍然重要。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她恰恰强调人物、情节和背景始终重要。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她没有说读者会更看重设备，反而强调内容本身。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这一项准确概括了“形式可变、核心不变”的推论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她没有主张作者只为某种设备写作。

public.prompt: What point does Dan make after the quiz result is revealed?

options.text: Ebook sales in the United States fell after 2010
The British Amazon site stopped expanding its catalogue
Ebook prices will soon be lower than paperback prices
The number of available ebooks is still rising and may reach the millions

evidence.quote: number is increasing all the time, so it might be in the millions soon.

private.explanationZh: 在公布数字之后，Dan 补充说电子书数量还在持续增长，甚至可能很快达到数百万，这就是他后续强调的信息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里谈的是可获得的电子书数量继续增加，不是销量下跌。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Dan 没有说英国亚马逊停止扩张，反而说数量一直在增加。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这一段没有讨论电子书和纸质书的价格比较。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。该项直接对应 Dan 在题目结果揭晓后的补充说明。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110809-ebooks-q01: 节目开头就说主题是电子书的兴起，后面又讨论其对出版业和作者的影响，a完整概括主旨；其余选项都与原文不符或范围过窄。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110809-ebooks-q02: Sarah Lloyd明确说做得特别好的作者是会建立数字受众、写博客或公开写作、在线生活的作者，因此b最准确；其余选项都与原话相反或无依据。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110809-ebooks-q03: Sarah Waters说无论在手机、笔记本还是其他载体上读书，人们仍然需要内容，人物、情节和背景这些小说的基本要素不会改变，所以应选c。其他选项都违背她的意思。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110809-ebooks-q04: 公布答案后，Dan紧接着说电子书数量一直在增加，很快可能达到数百万本，这正对应d；其余三项原文都没有说。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: Scientific reasons birds migrate to Scotland
British superstitions involving birds and animals
Methods for protecting endangered seabirds
Differences between British and Swiss folklore

evidence.quote:
today we're talking about superstitions involving birds and
animals

private.explanationZh: 节目开头就说明主题是与鸟类和动物有关的迷信，后面用海鸥、布谷鸟等例子展开，因此主旨就是英国相关民间迷信。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目讨论的是迷信和民俗，不是鸟类迁徙科学。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了整段节目的中心话题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有讨论保护濒危海鸟的方法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。提到瑞士等地只是顺带举例，不是核心比较。

public.prompt: Why does Alice explain what "embodiment" means after the seagull example?

options.text: To clarify why killing a gull is considered unlucky in that tradition
To argue that sailors created most Scottish folklore
To show that seagulls are biologically unusual birds
To compare seagulls with cuckoos and owls

evidence.quote: the seagulls have given physical bodies to the spirits of dead sailors

private.explanationZh: Alice 解释 embodiment，是为了让听者明白这则迷信的逻辑：海鸥被看作已故水手的化身，所以杀海鸥会被认为带来厄运。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。解释这个词正是为了帮助理解“为什么杀海鸥不吉利”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她没有讨论苏格兰民俗主要由谁创造。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里不是生物学说明，而是文化含义说明。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她此处没有把海鸥与其他鸟类作比较。

public.prompt: According to the superstition Dr Walton mentions, what should a person do after hearing a cuckoo call?

options.text: Run away from the bird as quickly as possible
Stay still and count backward quietly
Touch wood before the sound fades
Look for a sailor near the shore

evidence.quote:
If you hear a cuckoo calling and
then you start to run away from it as quickly as you can, the number of times you hear the
cuckoo calling before it fades into silence is the number of years you've got left to live.

private.explanationZh: Dr Walton 描述的迷信说法是，听到布谷鸟叫后要赶快跑开，然后在叫声消失前听到几次叫声，就代表还剩多少年寿命。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原话明确说要尽快跑离布谷鸟。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有提到站着不动或倒数。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。touch wood 属于前面介绍的总体迷信，不是这个布谷鸟传说中的做法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。水手内容属于海鸥迷信，不属于布谷鸟迷信。

public.prompt: What does Alice imply when she calls the cuckoo belief an "old wives' tale"?

options.text: It is a respected scientific explanation
It probably began with sailors rather than farmers
It is a traditional belief she considers untrue and unreasonable
It is becoming more popular in modern Scotland

evidence.quote:
An old wives' tale is what we call superstitions that are totally
untrue and ridiculous sometimes.

private.explanationZh: Alice 直接把这种说法定义为“完全不真实、甚至有些荒唐”的 old wives' tale，因此她明显是在表达怀疑和否定态度。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她明确说这种说法不真实、不合理，不是科学解释。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有推断这种迷信最初来自哪一类人。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。该项准确概括了她对这一传统迷信的评价。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她没有说这种迷信在现代苏格兰更流行。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110810-bird-superstitions-q01: 节目明确说今天讨论与鸟和动物有关的迷信，并围绕海鸥、布谷鸟和猫头鹰蛋展开，因此b最符合主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110810-bird-superstitions-q02: Alice解释embodiment，是为了说明为什么在那种传统里杀海鸥会被认为不吉利，因为海鸥被看作死去水手的化身，所以a正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110810-bird-superstitions-q03: Dr Walton说如果你听到布谷鸟叫，就要尽快跑离它；在声音消失前听到的叫声次数代表剩余寿命年数，因此a是直接细节信息。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110810-bird-superstitions-q04: Alice把这个说法称为old wives' tale，并进一步解释这种迷信是完全不真实、很荒唐的，因此她是在暗示这是传统但不可信、不合理的信念，c最准确。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the discussion about shopping spaces?

options.text: Why Asian malls are larger than European ones
How store design influences what shoppers buy
Why consumers prefer department stores to supermarkets
How online shopping is replacing physical shops

evidence.quote:
The layout of a shop has a large impact on what we buy and
how much money we spend.

private.explanationZh: 节目的核心论点是商店布局会影响顾客买什么、花多少钱，后面再用必需品位置和水果区陈列继续说明，因此主旨是店铺设计如何影响消费行为。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。亚洲大型商场只是结尾补充，不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了整段围绕“布局影响购物行为”的讨论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有比较消费者整体更偏好哪类商店。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目讨论的是实体零售空间设计，不是网购取代实体店。

public.prompt: Why are items like milk and bread usually placed far from the supermarket entrance, according to Professor Alan Penn?

options.text: So they stay cooler at the back of the store
So staff can restock them more easily
So shoppers pass more products and may add extra items to their trolleys
So customers learn the store layout more quickly

evidence.quote:
that's in order to get people to travel the full distance through the store. On the way
through, they'll see other things that they may have forgotten they needed and put them
into the trolley.

private.explanationZh: Professor Alan Penn 解释说，牛奶和面包放在远处不是偶然，而是为了让顾客走完整个商店；在途中他们会看到别的商品，并可能顺手放进购物车。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。录音没有提到冷藏或温度。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。重点是影响顾客行为，不是方便员工补货。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这正是教授给出的直接原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。目的不是帮助顾客更快熟悉布局，而是延长他们经过货架的路线。

public.prompt: Why does Alan Penn mention fresh fruit near the entrance?

options.text: To show how stores create a positive first impression that shapes shopping behavior
To argue that fruit is the most profitable supermarket product
To explain why shoppers should bring larger bags
To compare supermarkets with outdoor markets

evidence.quote:
It provides you with a very positive, healthy feeling, atmosphere as you step in
through the door.

private.explanationZh: 提到入口附近的新鲜水果，不是单纯描述摆放位置，而是用来说明商店如何通过营造“健康、积极”的第一印象，塑造顾客进店时的情绪和体验。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这准确说明了这个例子的功能：解释环境设计如何塑造消费体验。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。录音没有说水果利润最高。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。带更大袋子是节目开头的个人经历，不是这里举水果的目的。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里不是拿超市和露天市场作比较。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the example of the South China Mall?

options.text: Large shopping centres always attract enough retailers
A mall can be enormous without being commercially successful
Shoppers prefer empty malls because they are less confusing
Asian developers are building fewer malls than before

evidence.quote:
most of these shops are empty! In 2008 over 99 per cent of the shops in
the South China Mall were unoccupied

private.explanationZh: 虽然 South China Mall 规模极大，但绝大多数店铺空置，说明“面积大、店铺多”并不等于商业运营成功。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。事实恰恰相反，很多店铺是空着的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。该项是从“最大但大量空置”的事实得出的合理推论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说顾客偏好空商场。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。结尾说还有更多 mega-malls 在建设中，不是更少。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110825-shopping-q01: 整段讨论都在说明商店布局、灯光、颜色和设计如何影响顾客停留时间与购买行为，所以b概括最准确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110825-shopping-q02: Alan Penn明确说牛奶和面包放在最里面，是为了让顾客走完整个商店，并在途中看到其他商品、想起还需要别的东西，然后放进购物车，因此c正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110825-shopping-q03: 他提到入口附近放新鲜水果，是因为这会在顾客一进门时营造积极、健康、欢迎的氛围，用来影响购物体验和行为，所以a最贴切。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110825-shopping-q04: South China Mall虽然店铺数量最多，但Dan说其中99%以上是空的，这说明商场可以规模巨大却并不商业成功，因此b是合理且唯一可支持的推论。

public.prompt: What is the program mainly about?

options.text: How the World Wide Web made online information easier to access and spread widely
How military computers were used in 1969
Why the first website at CERN failed to attract users
Why website numbers will soon match the world population

evidence.quote:
the World Wide Web was new technology that allowed people to
access information more easily.

private.explanationZh: 节目围绕万维网二十周年展开，核心不是互联网军用历史，而是万维网如何让信息更容易被访问，以及它如何推动网站普及。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了“更容易获取信息”和“广泛传播”两条主线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。1969 年互联网的背景只是对比说明，不是节目重点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说 CERN 的第一个网站失败。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。网站数量只是结尾数据，不是整段节目的中心问题。

public.prompt: Why does the reporter describe the web as turning "gobbledygook computer language" into easy-to-read pages?

options.text: To show that the web made technical information easier for ordinary users to access
To criticize people who build websites without training
To argue that the Internet should replace the Web
To suggest that printed books are clearer than websites

evidence.quote:
turns all the gobbledygook computer language into easy to read pages, all
linked together, that you can access on any computer.

private.explanationZh: 这句话的作用是解释万维网的价值：它把一般人看不懂的计算机语言转化成易读页面，因此普通用户更容易获取信息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。该项准确说明这个比喻是在突出“可读性”和“可访问性”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。记者并没有批评建站者。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目是在区分 Internet 和 Web，不是在主张谁取代谁。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里不是纸媒和网站的清晰度比较。

public.prompt: According to Professor Wendy Hall, who were among the first people to start setting up websites?

options.text: Journalists and advertisers
Schoolchildren and teachers
Scientists, early adopters, and geeks
Soldiers and government officials

evidence.quote:
others, very early adopters, generally, scientists,
geeks, started setting up websites.

private.explanationZh: Wendy Hall 直接列出最早开始建网站的人群，包括 early adopters、scientists 和 geeks，所以答案是这些技术先行者。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。记者和广告商不是这一段列出的人群。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。学童和教师没有被提到。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。该项是对原文的准确改写。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有说最早建站的是军人或官员。

public.prompt: What conclusion do the speakers draw from the huge number of websites?

options.text: Most websites are run by universities
The Web remained a small tool for specialists
The Web led to broad participation and massive growth online
The Internet is now easier to understand than books

evidence.quote:
let
people set up their own websites and really get the most out of the
internet.

private.explanationZh: 节目在提到网站数量巨大之后总结说，万维网让人们可以自己建站并更充分利用互联网，因此合理结论是它推动了广泛参与和大规模增长。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说大多数网站属于大学。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。结尾强调的是大规模扩张，不是只服务少数专家。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。该项概括了结尾对万维网影响的总体判断。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里没有把互联网理解难度与书籍作直接比较。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110906-world-wide-web-celebrates-2: 节目主线是庆祝万维网20周年，并说明它如何把信息变成易读页面、让更多人访问和建立网站，因此a最能概括全文。
记者把网页描述为把难懂的计算机语言变成易读页面，目的就是说明万维网让普通用户更容易获取和使用信息，因此a正确。
Wendy Hall明确说最早建立网站的人包括early adopters、scientists和geeks，所以c是直接对应原文的细节答案。
节目最后说网站数量极其庞大，万维网让人们能够建立自己的网站并更充分地使用互联网，因此可推断它带来了广泛参与和大规模增长，c最符合。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: Why professional swimmers break world records
Why swimming matters and how children and adults can be encouraged to learn
Why public pools should ban games and toys
Why British adults prefer other sports to swimming

evidence.quote: swimming is very important for safety and good for fitness.

private.explanationZh: 开头先说明游泳对安全和健康的重要性，后面又分别谈婴幼儿学习和成人课程，因此主题是“为什么游泳重要，以及如何鼓励不同年龄人群学习”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。世界纪录只是开场小测，不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它概括了节目关于重要性、儿童学习和成人鼓励策略的整体内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目提到游戏道具是为了帮助学习，不是主张禁止。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有讨论英国成年人更偏好其他运动。

public.prompt: How many children does the BBC report say went through swimming classes in the last year?

options.text: Just under 30,000
About 100,000
More than one million

evidence.quote: last year alone, over 300,000 little ones have gone through classes.

private.explanationZh: 记者明确给出数字：过去一年里有超过 300,000 名小朋友参加了游泳课程，所以答案是 over 300,000。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这个数字远低于原文。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文说的是超过三十万，不是一十万左右。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。与原文数字完全一致。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文没有说超过一百万。

public.prompt: What does Jenni May suggest by saying swimming becomes "second nature" for babies?

options.text: Early experience makes being in water feel normal as they grow older
Babies can teach themselves without adult help
Older learners cannot become confident swimmers
Children only enjoy swimming before six months of age

evidence.quote:
And then it just becomes second nature to them so as they're older, they don't know any
different then being under the water and being wet.

private.explanationZh: Jenni May 的意思不是婴儿天生会游得更快，而是如果很早接触水环境，长大后会觉得在水里很自然、不陌生，这正是 second nature 的含义。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了“早接触导致后来感觉自然”的推论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目一直强调需要适当监督，不是无需成人帮助。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说成年人就不可能建立信心。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。她没有说六个月后孩子就不喜欢游泳。

public.prompt: Why does Rob mention diving for bricks and going through hoops near the end of the program?

options.text: To show that adult lessons can use playful activities to make swimming less intimidating
To explain how competitions for toddlers are organized
To prove that diving is more important than floating
To recommend replacing supervision with games

evidence.quote:
She suggests diving down for bricks and going through hoops as two methods
to make swimming more fun.

private.explanationZh: 这里举出捡砖块、穿呼啦圈等做法，是为了说明成人教学也会借助有趣活动降低紧张感，让学游泳更容易坚持。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。该项准确指出这些例子的功能是让成人课程更有趣、更不吓人。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里谈的是成人教学技巧，不是幼儿比赛安排。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有比较哪项技能更重要。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目反而多次强调 supervision 的必要性。

reasonsZh.bbc-20110908-swimming-q01: 节目先讲游泳对安全和健康的重要性，再讲婴幼儿和成年人如何被鼓励学习游泳，所以b准确概括主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110908-swimming-q02: BBC记者原话说过去一年里有over 300,000 little ones上了游泳课，因此c是明确的细节信息。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110908-swimming-q03: Jenni May说婴儿越早接触游泳，长大后在水里和弄湿这件事就会变得像天性一样自然，因此a准确表达其含义。
reasonsZh.bbc-20110908-swimming-q04: Rob提到潜下去捡砖和穿过圆环，是在说明成人课程也会用有趣、游戏化的方法鼓励人们游泳，让这件事没那么吓人，因此a最合适。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the discussion?

options.text: How tourists travel to Antarctica
How non-native plants are reaching Antarctica and threatening its ecology
Why Antarctica was named by the Ancient Greeks
How forests are being planted across Antarctica

evidence.quote:
We're
talking about alien plants in Antarctica.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就点明主题是南极的外来植物，后文再解释这些植物如何被带入、为什么会扩散，以及它们为何会威胁当地生态，所以主旨是外来植物入侵及其影响，而不是旅游、词源或造林。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中确实提到游客前往南极，但那只是种子传播途径的一部分，不是整段讨论的中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。开头直接说讨论的是“南极的外来植物”，后面所有信息都围绕它们的来源与生态威胁展开。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目开头和结尾都出现了词源小测验，但那只是穿插的 quiz，不是主体内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有说人们在南极主动种植森林，反而强调这些植物是不知不觉被带去并逐渐扎根。

public.prompt: According to the report, how are many seeds reaching Antarctica?

options.text: They are blown there by strong winds from South America.
They are packed deliberately by research teams.
They are carried unknowingly on visitors' clothing and boots.
They are released from melting ice.

evidence.quote:
scientists and tourists visiting the white continent and found that on average each is
carrying nearly ten seeds tucked away in boots and folds of clothing.

private.explanationZh: 证据明确说明，研究人员搜查到科学家和游客的靴子与衣物褶皱里平均藏着近十粒种子。也就是说，很多种子并不是被人故意带去，而是访客在不知情的情况下附着携带过去的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目还特别说种子不是靠风带去的，所以这个说法与原文相反。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文强调 visitors 并非有意种植或打包携带，关键点正是“without realising it”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据直接指出种子藏在靴子和衣物褶皱中，被访客无意带入南极。
optionRationalesZh.d: 冰层融化解释了植物更容易生长，不是种子进入南极的来源。

public.prompt: Why does the reporter mention islands offshore?

options.text: To show that invasive species have already damaged nearby ecosystems in a similar way
To explain where most Antarctic tourists begin their journeys
To identify the original habitat of the Antarctic plants
To suggest that Antarctica should be divided into smaller islands

evidence.quote:
The researchers say that in time these species threaten to overwhelm Antarctica's ecology
just as they have on islands offshore. They're advocating tighter checks on visitors.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到 offshore islands 的作用不是换一个地点继续讲故事，而是提供类比证据：这些外来物种在别的地方已经造成过生态压制，因此研究人员才主张加强检查。这是在强化风险判断。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。离岸岛屿被当作先例，说明类似入侵已经在别处造成生态压力。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有把这些岛屿说成游客出发地，重点是生态后果，不是旅行路线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这里不是追溯植物原产地，而是在比较入侵物种可能造成的影响。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有讨论把南极分成岛屿，这个选项完全偏离语境。

public.prompt: What is likely to happen if visitor traffic increases and climate change continues?

options.text: Antarctica will return to being completely ice-covered.
Only tourist areas will become warmer, while the rest remains unchanged.
Visitors will stop carrying seeds onto the continent.
Parts of Antarctica will become less white and less untouched.

evidence.quote:
Even
then, they say, increasing traffic to Antarctica, combined with climate change, means not all
of the continent is going to stay white and pristine.

private.explanationZh: 最后这句是在做趋势判断：即使加强检查，更多人流再加上气候变化，仍会让南极不可能始终保持“white and pristine”。因此最合理的预测是部分地区会更绿、也不再那么原始。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这与原文趋势相反。节目说南极不会全部继续保持白色与原始状态。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有把变化限定在旅游区，范围表述是“not all of the continent”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有说访客会停止携带种子，反而说即便 tighter checks 也无法完全避免。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这是对“不会一直保持白色和原始状态”的合理改写。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120309-antarctic-aliens-q01: 节目核心一直在讲外来植物如何被带入南极，以及这些植物可能威胁南极独特生态。其余选项只涉及开头小话题、词源测验或夸大成“种森林”，不符合全文主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120309-antarctic-aliens-q02: 文中明确说种子不是被风带去，而是人们无意间带在衣服和靴子上，研究人员搜查后发现平均每人带近十粒种子，因此c有直接文本支持。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120309-antarctic-aliens-q03: 记者提到近海岛屿，是为了说明这些物种在别处已经像这样威胁并压倒当地生态，因此这是用相似案例强调风险，不是讲游客路线、植物原产地或岛屿划分。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120309-antarctic-aliens-q04: 结尾明确说游客增加加上气候变化，意味着南极“不可能全部继续保持白色和原始状态”，所以最合理推断是部分地区会变得不那么白、也不那么原始。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the discussion about the Y chromosome?

options.text: Recent research suggests the Y chromosome is likely to remain stable for a very long time.
Scientists have proved that the Y chromosome is disappearing rapidly.
Researchers found that rhesus monkeys no longer have a Y chromosome.
Men and women now have equally complex sex chromosomes.

evidence.quote: Well it now seems that men can breathe a sigh of relief.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头先回顾一种旧担忧：Y 染色体可能在退化；随后立刻给出新的总体结论，说男性现在可以“松一口气”。这说明新的研究方向不是证明男性快要消失，而是表明 Y 染色体在很长时间内仍可能保持稳定。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。整段讨论的核心就是新研究让人放心，Y 染色体没有马上消失的迹象。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是旧研究带来的担忧，不是本节目最终强调的新结论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目比较了人类与恒河猴，但没有说猴子已经没有 Y 染色体。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中只说男性染色体不如女性“复杂”，并没有说两者现在同样复杂。

public.prompt: According to Rosie, what helps preserve the genes that remain on the Y chromosome?

options.text: Frequent changes in male hormones
New genes borrowed from rhesus monkeys
Natural selection protecting important genes
A growing number of female chromosomes

evidence.quote:
She also added that natural selection is doing a very good job of preserving
the genes.

private.explanationZh: Rosie 明确复述了研究者的观点：自然选择正在很好地保留下来这些剩余基因。题目问的是“什么在帮助保留这些基因”，答案必须直接对应这句解释，而不是扩展到别的生物学概念。

optionRationalesZh.a: 荷尔蒙在节目里被提到过，但不是保留基因的机制。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有说人类从恒河猴“借来”新基因，这属于无根据的推断。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据直接点出 natural selection 在 preserving the genes。
optionRationalesZh.d: 女性染色体数量或变化不是这里解释 Y 染色体保存的原因。

public.prompt: Why do Neil and Rosie imagine a world without men near the end of the program?

options.text: To summarize the laboratory methods used in the research
To shift from the scientific report to a light, speculative discussion
To argue that women should replace men in society
To correct an earlier mistake about the quiz question

evidence.quote:
Now there's only one way to end this programme, Rosie, and that is to
imagine a world without men.

private.explanationZh: 这句话不是继续报告研究数据，而是主持人主动把节目转入一个轻松设想的收尾环节。也就是说，这一段的作用是从科学新闻过渡到带有玩笑性质的讨论，让节目更有互动感。

optionRationalesZh.a: 此时节目已经不在讲实验设计或研究方法，功能不是总结实验。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。主持人明确说要用“想象没有男人的世界”来结束节目，说明是在转入轻松推想。
optionRationalesZh.c: 两人只是开玩笑式讨论刻板印象，并没有认真主张用女性取代男性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 纠正 quiz 的答案发生在更后面，但这句话本身不是为了更正前面的错误。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Neil's phrase "this entertaining debate"?

options.text: He believes the debate has produced a scientific conclusion.
He thinks Rosie has misunderstood the entire program.
He wants to return to discussing chromosome structure in detail.
He views the exchange as playful rather than serious research.

evidence.quote:
OK Rosie, this entertaining debate must
come to an end.

private.explanationZh: Neil 把这段对话称为 entertaining debate，说明他把后面的男女差异讨论视为一种有趣、带娱乐性的互动，而不是严肃的科研论证。因此最合理的推断是，这一段的语气是轻松而非学术性的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他说 entertaining debate，不表示他们得出了科学结论，反而暗示只是轻松讨论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 他没有批评 Rosie 误解节目，语气更像收尾时的玩笑。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这句话是在结束话题，不是在要求回到技术性分析。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。entertaining 强调的是娱乐和调侃色彩，而不是严谨研究。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120315-the-extinction-of-men-q01: 关于Y染色体的核心结论是：虽然有所减少，但过去2500万年一直稳定，未来很多百万年也可能继续稳定。其他选项与文本相反或无依据。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120315-the-extinction-of-men-q02: Rosie明确说自然选择正在很好地保存这些基因，因此剩余的重要基因得到保留。其余选项都没有出现在节目论述中。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120315-the-extinction-of-men-q03: 节目后段让两人想象“没有男人的世界”，明显是从前面的科学报道转入轻松、带玩笑性质的讨论，不是在总结研究方法或提出严肃社会主张。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120315-the-extinction-of-men-q04: Neil把这段交流称为“有趣的辩论”，说明他把后面的男女刻板印象讨论视为轻松打趣，而不是科学结论或严肃研究分析。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: Why the world's largest airports are closing
Why Hong Kong wants to expand its airport and what that would involve
How passengers can avoid noise at major airports
Why sea transport is replacing air travel in Asia

evidence.quote:
airport expansion has been in the news and particularly plans to expand
Hong Kong International airport which could turn it into the World's biggest.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就指出新闻焦点是香港国际机场扩建计划，而且后文不断讨论扩建原因、经济背景、造地工程以及环境争议，所以主旨是香港为何要扩建机场以及扩建意味着什么。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目讨论的是扩建，不是关闭大型机场。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。开头已经点题，后面所有内容都围绕扩建动机与实施代价展开。
optionRationalesZh.c: 噪音只是后段提到的副作用之一，不是节目主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有说海运正在取代航空，反而强调航空需求仍然存在。

public.prompt: According to Juliana Liu, what major construction challenge does the expansion require?

options.text: Creating new land from the sea for the runway
Moving the entire airport closer to the city center
Rebuilding the existing terminals on higher ground
Replacing passenger flights with cargo traffic

evidence.quote:
The project is likely to cost more than 17 billions dollars, much more than the existing
airport. It will be an enormous undertaking. Much of the space needed for the runway does
not yet exist, so it must be reclaimed from the sea.

private.explanationZh: 证据明确指出，跑道所需空间目前并不存在，因此必须从海里“reclaim”土地。这说明扩建中的关键工程难点不是搬迁机场，而是先造出可供建设的新陆地。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文直接说 needed space does not yet exist, so it must be reclaimed from the sea。
optionRationalesZh.b: 主持人反而解释了为什么不能简单建在更靠市中心的地方。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有提到把现有航站楼移到更高地势。
optionRationalesZh.d: cargo 增长是扩建原因之一，不是这道题问的工程任务。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers mention airport expansion plans in other places such as London?

options.text: To argue that Hong Kong should cancel its project immediately
To show that Hong Kong copied its plan from Heathrow
To place Hong Kong's proposal in a broader international context
To prove that London's airports are more efficient than Hong Kong's

evidence.quote:
Other countries around the world are also talking about expanding their
airports.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到伦敦等其他地方，并不是把焦点转到英国本身，而是说明机场扩建并非香港独有的问题，而是多个国家都在面对的全球性发展议题。它帮助听者把香港案例放进更大的背景中理解。

optionRationalesZh.a: 后文虽然提到争议，但这里不是主张立即取消，而是扩展背景。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有说香港照搬 Heathrow 方案，两个案例只是并列比较。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。提到其他国家的计划，是为了把香港方案放在国际讨论框架里。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有比较哪座机场更有效率，这不是该句的目的。

public.prompt: What concern about the expansion is emphasized near the end of the program?

options.text: It may reduce the number of visitors to Hong Kong.
It may make sea transport impossible in the area.
It may prevent any future need for air travel.
It may worsen pollution and affect people's health.

evidence.quote:
There is also
concern about pollution which apparently is already what most people
complain about in Hong Kong. And the air and noise pollution can certainly
affect our health.

private.explanationZh: 结尾部分把关注点从经济利益转到环境与健康代价，尤其指出污染本来就是香港居民常抱怨的问题，而新增的空气和噪音污染会影响健康。所以最突出的担忧是扩建可能加剧污染。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说扩建会减少游客数量，讨论重点是污染与健康。
optionRationalesZh.b: 没有提到海上运输会因此无法进行，这个选项超出了文本。
optionRationalesZh.c: 主持人明确说在可预见时期内仍然需要航空旅行，所以这项相反。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据直接把 pollution 和 health effect 联系起来。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120322-hong-kong-s-airport-expansi: 节目主线是香港为何要扩建机场，以及扩建涉及什么工程、成本和争议。其他选项不是全文重点，或与内容相反。
Juliana Liu明确说新跑道所需空间目前并不存在，必须“从海里填出来”，也就是海上造地，因此a与原文直接对应。
提到伦敦等地也在讨论扩建机场，是为了说明这不是香港独有问题，而是更广泛的国际性议题，从而给香港方案提供背景。
结尾强调环保人士担忧污染，而香港居民本来就抱怨污染，空气和噪音污染会影响健康，所以被突出的问题是污染和健康影响。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: Why London's theatre district has been attracting unusually strong audiences
How musicals first developed in London's oldest theatre
Why Olympic events will replace theatre performances
How ticket prices in New York compare with those in London

evidence.quote: More people than ever are going to see, or take in, a show.

private.explanationZh: 节目围绕 Theatreland 的“好消息”展开，也就是观众人数创新高。后文继续解释为何在经济不景气时仍有这么多人看戏，以及奥运会会不会影响这种势头，所以核心主题是伦敦剧院区为何能吸引强劲观众。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。开头就指出更多人 than ever 去看演出，后文都在解释这一现象。
optionRationalesZh.b: 最古老剧院只是 quiz 内容，不是节目主体。
optionRationalesZh.c: 奥运会只是后段的一个潜在影响因素，不是整个节目主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目提到 Broadway 是为了说明演出成功，不是在比较纽约和伦敦票价。

public.prompt: Why were theatre owners surprised by audience numbers?

options.text: Most tickets were sold to foreign tourists only.
Opera and ballet had become much cheaper than musicals.
New theatres had opened across the city at the same time.
Large audiences were still paying for shows during a severe economic downturn.

evidence.quote:
there's been surprise here in the UK that when the country is
going through one of the worst economic downturns for decades, people can still afford to
go.

private.explanationZh: 惊讶之处不在于剧院突然变便宜，而在于英国正经历几十年来最严重的经济低迷之一，观众却仍然愿意花高价进剧场。这种“逆经济周期”的消费表现正是 theatre owners 感到意外的原因。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说观众主要是外国游客，惊讶点在于经济环境下仍有消费能力。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文反而强调 West End 不便宜，票价可以很高。
optionRationalesZh.c: 并未讨论同时新开很多剧院，和 surprise 的逻辑无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据直接把 economic downturn 与 people can still afford to go 并列。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention that some shows transfer to Broadway?

options.text: To show that London audiences prefer American productions
To support the idea that London's productions are strong enough to succeed elsewhere
To explain why London theatres are lowering ticket prices
To suggest that theatre owners want to leave London

evidence.quote:
Some shows are sell-outs and others transfer to New York's Broadway, where
they're also drawing, or pulling in, big audiences.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到 Broadway，不是因为节目要转谈纽约，而是拿 Broadway 的成功来进一步证明伦敦剧场作品质量高、吸引力强，甚至换到另一个重要戏剧市场仍能吸引大量观众。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文并没有说伦敦观众偏爱美国作品，重点是作品走出去也成功。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。转到 Broadway 仍 draw big audiences，正好支持“world class drama”的说法。
optionRationalesZh.c: 提到 Broadway 与降价无关，节目并未说剧院因此下调票价。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这不是剧院老板想离开伦敦，而是作品跨城市延续成功。

public.prompt: What does Adam Spiegel suggest about Olympic visitors to London?

options.text: They will avoid central London because theatres are too crowded.
They will attend sports events instead of any other activity.
They will still see theatre as an important part of visiting the city.
They will mainly choose cheaper theatres outside the West End.

evidence.quote:
But he's confident
that people visiting London will still want to take in a show.

private.explanationZh: 虽然前面有人担心奥运会会把观众从剧院吸走，但 Adam Spiegel 的判断相反：来伦敦的人通常会把看演出视为城市体验的一部分，因此奥运访客仍可能继续去剧院。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他没有说游客会因为拥挤而避开市中心剧院。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这正是悲观者的担心，不是 Adam Spiegel 给出的观点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据直接说 visiting London still want to take in a show。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有提到游客会转向西区之外的廉价剧院。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120405-london-s-theatreland-q01: 节目围绕伦敦剧院区为何在经济不景气中仍吸引创纪录观众展开，重点是强劲票房与背后原因，不是剧院史、奥运取代演出或伦敦纽约票价比较。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120405-london-s-theatreland-q02: 报道明确说在国家经历严重经济低迷时，人们居然仍负担得起昂贵戏票，这正是剧院业主感到惊讶的原因。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120405-london-s-theatreland-q03: 提到一些剧目转到百老汇后也能吸引大批观众，是为了支持前面“伦敦作品质量高、世界级”的观点，说明这些制作在别处也能成功。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120405-london-s-theatreland-q04: Adam Spiegel认为世界普遍觉得来伦敦就包含看戏，因此即使因奥运来访，游客仍会把看剧视为伦敦之行的重要组成部分。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion?

options.text: Why heroin addiction is easy to treat in Australia

public.prompt: What personal experience influenced Bob Carr's position?

options.text: His younger brother died from a heroin overdose.
He was arrested for carrying soft drugs as a student.

private.explanationZh: 报道明确说 Bob Carr 的弟弟因海洛因过量死亡，这段个人经历解释了他为什么会公开支持软性毒品非刑事化。题目问的是推动其立场的 personal experience，所以必须抓住这条直接信息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文直接指出他的 younger brother died from a heroin overdose。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有说他年轻时曾因携带毒品被捕。

optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有提到家人经营戒毒中心，这属于无中生有。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers mention Switzerland and the Netherlands?

private.explanationZh: 这里提到瑞士和荷兰，是为了举出现实案例，说明支持者并不是空谈，而是参考了其他国家实施过的“carefully controlled programs”。这些例子在论证功能上属于支持替代政策的先例。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说这些国家已经消除了全部毒品犯罪，只说他们有受控项目。

optionRationalesZh.c: 没有说 Bob Carr 的政策直接来源于这些国家，这里是主持人举例说明。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。提到两国是为了提供受控项目的实例，说明 alternative approach 确实存在。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Rosie's comment that the issue is "a difficult subject"?

options.text: She has decided that all drugs should remain illegal.
She sees both potential benefits and serious risks in the debate.
She thinks the evidence discussed is irrelevant to Australia.
She believes the government has already solved the problem.

But, you know, I see the point of people

private.explanationZh: Rosie 先承认受控供给可能有减少犯罪和健康风险的理由，接着又说她理解担心毒品危害的人，因此她并没有简单站队，而是在承认双方都有可理解之处。这正是“difficult subject”背后的含义。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她没有明确表态所有毒品都应继续非法，只是说问题复杂。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。她同时看到支持方的逻辑和反对方对危害的担忧。
optionRationalesZh.c: 她并没有说证据与澳大利亚无关，整段讨论恰恰是在联系澳大利亚政策。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目整体都在说明争议仍未解决，所以不可能是“已经解决”。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120417-australia-s-war-on-drugs-q0: 节目中心问题是澳大利亚是否应将软性毒品持有非刑罪化，也就是合法化/除罪化。其他选项都只是局部细节或明显不符。
报道直接说Bob Carr的弟弟死于海洛因过量，这段个人经历影响了他的立场，因此a有明确文本依据。
提到瑞士和荷兰，是为了举例说明支持者引用的“受控提供毒品”方案在别国已有实践，用来说明一种替代政策路径。
Rosie先说这种受控策略可能减少犯罪和健康风险，但又承认人们担心毒品危害“也有道理”，因此可推断她看到双方都有理由，既有潜在好处也有严重风险。

public.prompt: What central development are the speakers focusing on in this program?

options.text: Why the Titanic remains the world's most famous shipwreck.
How a newly discovered ancient cargo ship could reveal information about early sea trade.
How police divers were trained near Varazze for rescue missions.

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。Titanic只是开头用来举例的著名沉船，不是整段讨论的中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了“新发现的古代货船”以及“帮助了解早期海上贸易”这两个核心信息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有讨论潜水员训练，警方潜水员只是参与搜索。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。联合国估算船只数量只是开场小测验背景，不是本节目主要内容。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers bring up the pottery shards found in the fishermen's nets?

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。碎片反复出现，正是搜寻行动开始前最直接的线索。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。发现碎片并不能证明船上“只有”陶器。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有借此比较现代捕鱼和古罗马贸易。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。原文没有说渔民因此停止工作。

public.prompt: What does the discussion suggest about the mud around the vessel?

options.text: It made the ship easy to pull to the surface quickly.
It prevented researchers from learning anything about the cargo.
It helped protect the ship and what it carried from damage.
It showed that the ship must have sunk only recently.

She sank into thick mud, which engulfed and

optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。preserved明确表明泥帮助保护了船和相关信息。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目明确说这艘船有大约两千年历史。

public.prompt: In the last part of the program, what issue becomes most important about raising the ship?

it might even be possible to bring the entire

Italian authorities to decide whether such a complex and hugely expensive operation

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目没有讨论渔民的所有权问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目并未把不同货物的价值比较当作结尾重点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。航线判断不是最后一段最重要的问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。两段证据一起表明，真正的焦点是“可行但代价很高，是否要做”。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120809-ancient-italian-shipwreck-q: 节目核心一直在讲意大利附近新发现的一艘古代货船，以及它可能帮助人们了解古代海上贸易。其他选项只是开头顺带提到的背景或无关内容。
渔民网里不断出现陶器碎片，被明确说成说明海底有异常物体的线索，并最终促成潜水员搜索。原文没有说船上只有陶器，也没有说渔民因此停工。
报道说厚泥‘吞没并保存了’这艘船，主持人也据此推断船货可能仍在，因此最合理的推论是泥帮助保护了船体和货物。
结尾部分先说把整艘船打捞上来在技术上可行，但随后强调这会是复杂而且极其昂贵的行动，关键问题就是是否值得实施。

attracts many tourists to the village.

the snail racing championships begin with

private.explanationZh: 细节题。镇长直接说大家会把蜗牛吃掉，只有冠军会被“赦免”，也就是不用被吃。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。原文没有说会卖给游客。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这里说的是赛后的命运，不是进入另一轮比赛。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。winner is pardoned 的意思就是冠军被放过，不进入食用环节。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有提到送去繁殖。

public.prompt: What does the final report suggest the championship is like for the community?

options.text: It serves as both a race and a shared local feast.
It mainly exists to improve commercial snail farming.
It has started to lose popularity because attendance is falling.
It is used chiefly to test whether heat changes snail behavior.

evidence.quote: After the competition, we sit down for a snail feast and eat 170kg of snails.

They'll be eaten and washed down with local red
wine.

private.explanationZh: 从赛后一起吃蜗牛大餐、再配当地红酒可以推断，这项活动不仅是比赛，也是社区性的聚会和庆祝。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两处证据共同表明它兼具竞赛和地方聚餐的性质。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有把它描述成农业训练活动。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。前文提到它会吸引很多游客，不能推出人气下降。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。高温只是背景信息，不是赛事的主要目的。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120816-snail-racing-q01: 节目主要介绍法国一个长期举办、还能吸引游客的蜗牛赛。其余选项把话题改成科学研究、运动宣传或禁赛争论，都不符合全文重点。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120816-snail-racing-q02: 主持人把常见口令‘Ready, steady, go’改成‘Ready, steady, slow’，明显是为了幽默地突出蜗牛比赛非常慢。并非安全提醒或特殊暗号。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120816-snail-racing-q03: 镇长明确说所有蜗牛都会被吃掉，只有冠军会被赦免，因此获胜蜗牛的特殊结果就是免于被做成大餐。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120816-snail-racing-q04: 结尾报道既写比赛，也写大家赛后一起吃蜗牛大餐并配本地红酒，所以节目呈现的是社区性的赛事加集体宴会，而不是科研或产业活动。

public.prompt: What broader topic ties the different Mars missions together in this program?

options.text: How space agencies can shorten the length of a Mars year.
How Mars missions are used to investigate the planet and the possibility of past life there.
Why NASA should stop relying on remotely controlled vehicles.
Why London traffic is harder to manage than interplanetary travel.

evidence.quote:
our story today is about driving on Mars, though the vehicle which
has been sent up there is remotely controlled.

they do hope the
explorations will give them some clues about whether there ever was life
on the red planet.

private.explanationZh: 节目从“在火星上驾驶”切入，随后讨论探测任务希望了解火星内部结构、环境以及过去是否存在生命，因此核心主题是火星探测及其科学目标。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。火星年的长度只是开头小测验。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它概括了探测任务和“是否曾有生命”这两个贯穿节目的核心目标。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有主张停止使用遥控车辆。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。伦敦交通只是开玩笑式比较，不是主旨。

public.prompt: According to the report, how would InSight investigate Mars's interior?

options.text: By collecting surface ice and sending it back to Earth.
By orbiting Mars and photographing crater walls.
By placing seismometers and using marsquake signals to infer internal rock boundaries.
By drilling directly into the core with a robotic arm.

evidence.quote: put seismometers on the surface of the Red Planet and listen for
work out where the different rock boundaries lie inside the planet

private.explanationZh: 报告明确说 InSight 会在火星表面放置地震仪，并通过接收到的震动信号判断行星内部不同岩层边界的位置。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目没有说会把冰样送回地球。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这里谈的是在表面放置仪器，不是轨道拍照。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。两段证据连起来就是它研究火星内部的方法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。原文没有说会直接钻到核心。

public.prompt: Why does the report focus on whether Mars has a molten core?

options.text: Because it could help explain magnetic protection, atmospheric loss, and why life seems absent today.
Because the rover needs liquid metal from the core as fuel.
Because a molten core would make driving on Mars easier for robots.
Because finding molten rock would prove intelligent life once lived there.

evidence.quote: It should also be able to establish whether Mars has a molten core.

It is Earth's swirling
iron core that gives it the magnetic field which protects our atmosphere and oceans from
being eroded by the Sun.

private.explanationZh: 这里的推理链是：地球的熔融铁核产生磁场，磁场保护大气和海洋；火星可能没有这样的条件，所以如今看起来没有生命。节目关注熔融核心，是为了理解火星环境为何变成现在这样。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它把“核心”“磁场”“大气”“生命可能缺失”这条因果链完整串联起来。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有说探测车把核心当燃料。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。讨论重点是行星环境，不是机器人驾驶难度。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。即使有熔融核心，也不能直接证明曾有智能生命。

public.prompt: Why does the program end by mentioning critics who want other exploration targets?

options.text: To show that many scientists oppose all unmanned missions.
To explain why Curiosity will be shut down ahead of schedule.
To argue that seismometers are less useful than cameras.
To contrast NASA's strong focus on Mars with calls for a broader agenda.

evidence.quote:
the agency is becoming Mars-
obsessed
they would like to see a more diverse list of exploration targets.

private.explanationZh: 结尾引入批评者，不是为了否定所有火星任务，而是为了形成对比：NASA 很关注火星，但也有人认为探索目标应该更广泛。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。批评者并没有反对所有无人探测。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文没有说 Curiosity 会提前结束。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。结尾不是在比较具体仪器优劣。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。两处证据共同体现了“聚焦火星”与“主张多样化目标”之间的对照。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120823-driving-on-mars-q01: 节目从好奇号讲到未来的InSight任务，核心都是借助火星探测任务了解这颗行星，并寻找是否曾有生命的线索。其他选项都把重点缩得过窄或偏离主题。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120823-driving-on-mars-q02: 报道明确说InSight会把地震仪放到火星表面，听‘火星震’，再根据接收到的信号模式判断行星内部不同岩层边界的位置。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120823-driving-on-mars-q03: 节目解释说如果火星没有像地球那样的熔融铁核，就缺少保护大气和海洋的磁场，这有助于说明火星为何失去大气、如今看起来没有生命，因此a最完整。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120823-driving-on-mars-q04: 最后提到批评者，是为了和NASA持续聚焦火星形成对比；批评者希望探索目标更多样，而不是只盯着火星。

public.prompt: What concern are the speakers mainly developing through the discussion?

options.text: How supermarkets can advertise unattractive produce more effectively.
Why global food waste is serious and how it is linked to wasted water.
Why one sandwich should never be thrown away under any condition.
How a conference in Sweden permanently solved world hunger.

evidence.quote:
Global
food waste.
food waste puts pressure on the supply of water too.

private.explanationZh: 节目从“全球食物浪费”展开，后面进一步强调它不仅浪费食物本身，还会给淡水供应造成压力，因此主线是食物浪费及其资源后果。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。超市和外观问题只是其中一个例子。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它概括了节目开头主题和结尾强调的水资源联系。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目不是在讨论任何情况下都不能丢掉一块三明治。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。会议只是讨论问题，并没有彻底解决世界饥饿。

public.prompt: In the report, what causes much food waste in wealthier countries?

options.text: Consumers and supermarkets reject produce that does not look perfect.
Most farms there cannot irrigate crops reliably.
People regularly export edible food before it reaches stores.
Pests destroy nearly all fruit before harvest.

evidence.quote:
In richer
countries, consumers expect their food to look so perfect that slightly blemished, though
perfectly nutritious, fruit and vegetables never reach the supermarkets.

private.explanationZh: 报告明确指出，较富裕国家里消费者希望食物外表完美，因此稍有瑕疵但依然有营养的果蔬进不了超市，结果被浪费。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它直接对应“look so perfect”和“never reach the supermarkets”这层意思。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。灌溉不足不是这里谈到的富裕国家主要原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。原文没有把出口行为作为主要原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。虫害是发展中国家农场浪费部分提到的问题。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about cutting farm-level waste in developing countries?

options.text: Consumer education by itself would solve most of the problem.
The need for change would disappear if fewer crops were exported.
Better farming methods, storage, and packaging would be required.
Manufacturing would need to replace agriculture as the main water user.

evidence.quote:
Inefficient farming
techniques are part of the problem and hot and humid conditions make storing crops
difficult. Pests and diseases take their toll too.

there needs to be investment in farming to improve things like food
storage and packaging

private.explanationZh: 原文先说明发展中国家的浪费多发生在农场，原因包括低效耕作、储存困难和病虫害；随后又提出要投资改进储存和包装。由此可推断，减少浪费需要改善农业方法和保存条件。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。消费者教育主要对应富裕国家外观偏好问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文没有把出口减少当作关键解决方案。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它综合了问题成因和后文提出的改进方向。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有主张用制造业取代农业。

public.prompt: Near the end of the program, what connection do the speakers stress most?

options.text: Freshwater use in agriculture is already shrinking around the world.
Water shortages matter only in places with hot, humid climates.
Population growth will reduce the need for irrigation by 2050.
Throwing away food also wastes the water used to produce it, and that pressure will grow.

evidence.quote:
If we're wasting food, then farmers are wasting water to grow the food
that is thrown away.

By 2050, it's likely we'll be
using 50% more fresh water to feed the extra 3 billion people on the
planet.

private.explanationZh: 结尾把讨论从“浪费食物”推进到“也在浪费种植这些食物所消耗的水”，随后又指出未来人口增长会让淡水需求更大，所以重点是这种压力现在存在、以后还会加剧。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。原文没有说农业淡水使用正在下降。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。水资源压力不是只在潮湿地区才重要。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。人口增长会提高而不是降低灌溉需求。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。两段证据一起构成节目结尾最重要的逻辑连接。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120830-global-waste-food-for-thoug: 整段讨论围绕全球食物浪费为什么严重，以及这种浪费如何连带浪费农业用水。三明治只是引子，瑞典会议也不是解决问题的结论。
报道明确说富裕国家的消费者期待食物外观完美，超市也因此要求农民丢弃有瑕疵但仍有营养的果蔬，所以a与原文直接对应。
节目说发展中国家的浪费多发生在农场，原因包括低效耕作、储存困难、病虫害，并进一步指出需要投资改善储存和包装，所以可推断需要更好的方法、储存和包装。
结尾强调农业消耗大量淡水，如果食物被扔掉，那么种这些食物用掉的水也被浪费；再加上到2050年人口增长，用水压力还会更大。

public.prompt: Which issue does the program mainly explore after the opening quiz?

options.text: A failed cocaine-smuggling attempt in Nigeria and efforts to detect traffickers.
Traditional ways of cooking roast chicken in Brazil and the UK.
The scientific relationship between chickens and extinct species.
How mechanics in Brazil can prepare financially for retirement.

evidence.quote:
all about a failed attempt at drug
smuggling in Nigeria.

Nigeria is a major transit point for drugs, but the authorities have made efforts to
improve security

private.explanationZh: 主旨是一宗失败的毒品走私事件，以及尼日利亚当局如何加强安保、识别运毒者。节目虽然从烤鸡切入，但重点并不是饮食。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时概括了案件本身和后面谈到的执法应对。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。烤鸡只是藏毒载体，不是主要话题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。鸡和灭绝物种的关系只是开场测验。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目中的“退休计划”指嫌疑人的非法打算，不是理财建议。

public.prompt: What did the suspect expect the drug money to help him do?

options.text: Pay for another flight back to Brazil.
Start a business as part of his retirement plan.
Buy better equipment for his mechanic work.
Persuade airport officials to ignore his luggage.

evidence.quote:
this was the suspect's retirement plan,
and he had been hoping to start a business with the drug sales.

private.explanationZh: 报道直接说这是嫌疑人的“退休计划”，他想把卖毒品得到的钱拿来开一家生意，因此答案必须同时包含“退休计划”和“创业”两层意思。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。原文没有提到用这笔钱买机票。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它完整对应 retirement plan 和 start a business。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。虽然他原来是机械工，但节目没有说他想继续做这份工作。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。原文没有说他打算贿赂机场人员。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers list drugs found in suitcases, wigs, and a stethoscope?

options.text: To show that airport doctors sometimes assist smugglers.
To prove the roast-chicken case was the largest seizure on record.
To illustrate how inventive smugglers can be in hiding drugs.
To explain ordinary packing rules for international flights.

evidence.quote: Smugglers are using increasingly imaginative ways to conceal their drugs.

drugs hidden in the lining of
suitcases, in wigs and even in a doctor

private.explanationZh: 这里列举多个藏毒地点，不是为了补充猎奇细节，而是为了支持前一句观点：走私者越来越会想办法隐藏毒品。也就是说，这些例子起到举例证明的作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。提到听诊器并不意味着医生帮助走私。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有说这起案件是历史上最大的一次。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。例子正是为了说明 conceal drugs 的方式越来越有“创意”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。这里不是在介绍正常的航空打包规则。

public.prompt: What do the final comments imply about airport body scanners?

options.text: They are presented as one useful tool for identifying more drug carriers.
They have replaced every other form of airport security.
They are mainly used to estimate the value of seized cocaine.
They were introduced only because of the roast-chicken smuggling case.

evidence.quote:
Nigeria is a major transit point for drugs, but the authorities have made efforts to
improve security

airport body scanners have helped identify more than one hundred drug carriers last
year.

private.explanationZh: 结尾先说当局一直在努力加强安保，接着说人体扫描仪帮助识别出一百多名运毒者。由此可推断，扫描仪被呈现为提升查缉效果的工具之一。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它符合“加强安保”与“帮助识别运毒者”之间的关系。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文只说它有帮助，没有说取代了所有其他措施。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。扫描仪的作用是识别携带者，不是估算毒品价值。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有说扫描仪只是因这起鸡肉藏毒案才引入。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120906-cocaine-chicken-q01: 开头测验后，节目主要讲的是尼日利亚一起失败的可卡因走私案，以及当局如何加强安检发现运毒者。其他选项只是引入话题的边缘内容。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120906-cocaine-chicken-q02: 报道直接说这次走私是他的‘退休计划’，他希望靠卖毒品赚来的钱去开一家生意，因此b最准确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120906-cocaine-chicken-q03: 主持人先说走私者越来越会想办法藏毒，接着举出行李箱内衬、假发和听诊器这些例子，目的就是说明他们藏毒手法非常有创意。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120906-cocaine-chicken-q04: 结尾说人体扫描仪帮助识别出一百多名运毒者，随后主持人又说这只是持续斗争中的一部分，说明扫描仪被呈现为一种有用工具，而不是唯一手段。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion and report?

options.text: How Rome's bridges were rebuilt after damage from the river
A romantic lock tradition in Rome and the dispute over removing the locks
Why tourists prefer the Tiber to other European rivers
How a novelist promoted bicycle travel in Rome

evidence.quote:
some people say that Rome is one of the most romantic
cities in the world.
The council says that rust from the padlocks threatens the fabric of the old bridge.

private.explanationZh: 节目先把罗马介绍为浪漫之都，随后核心内容集中在情侣挂锁表达爱情，以及市政部门因桥梁受损而拆除这些锁的争议，因此主旨是爱情锁传统及其引发的冲突。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有讨论桥梁重建，市政担心的是铁锈损害桥体，不是修复工程。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。前半段讲爱情锁如何流行，后半段讲议会为何要拆除，完整概括了全文。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有比较台伯河与其他欧洲河流的旅游吸引力，这只是开头测验背景。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中提到一本书启发了挂锁传统，但并未讨论推广自行车旅行。

public.prompt: According to the report, what do couples do on Ponte Milvio bridge?

options.text: They leave handwritten notes and take photos together
They attach ribbons to the bridge and keep the key
They fasten a lock and throw the key into the river
They place flowers on the bridge and light candles

evidence.quote:
They put a bicycle lock around a lamp post, and then tossed the key down into
the Tiber. They were meant to have locked themselves together in everlasting love.

private.explanationZh: 报道明确说情侣把锁扣在桥上的灯柱上，然后把钥匙扔进台伯河，用这个动作象征永恒的爱情。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说他们在桥上留下纸条或拍照，这与Rosie自己的恋爱习惯混淆了。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中说的是锁，不是丝带，而且钥匙被扔进河里，不是保留下来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。锁住后把钥匙扔掉，正是这一浪漫仪式的具体做法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 没有提到鲜花和蜡烛，这属于常见浪漫行为，但不是本报道内容。

public.prompt: Why does the reporter mention men in overalls armed with bolt cutters?

options.text: To show that the city is already acting to remove the locks
To explain how the locks were first put onto the bridge
To suggest that thieves are stealing bicycles nearby
To prove that the bridge is being prepared for repairs

evidence.quote:
It says the locks have to go. And as
I watch, they're being taken away by men in overalls armed with bolt cutters.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到穿工作服、拿着大剪钳的人，不是为了介绍工具本身，而是为了让听者直观看到市政部门已经开始拆锁，强化浪漫象征与官方管理之间的冲突。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。该细节突出的是拆除行动正在发生。
optionRationalesZh.b: 挂锁传统的起源在前面那本书的情节里，这里不是回顾起源。
optionRationalesZh.c: Neil后来拿bolt cutters开玩笑提到自行车小偷，但记者这里并不是在报道盗窃。
optionRationalesZh.d: 报道没有说这些人是在为桥梁维修做准备，而是在拆掉锁。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the author of the book that inspired the tradition?

options.text: He thinks the bridge should be closed to tourists
He believes the tradition has become too commercial
He wants the city to replace locks with notes
He believes the locks should remain as meaningful symbols

evidence.quote:
He says these symbols of love ought to be valued and respected,
and left in place.

private.explanationZh: 作者认为这些锁象征爱情，应该被珍视和尊重，并且留在原处，因此可以推断他支持保留这些锁。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有提到关闭桥梁或限制游客。
optionRationalesZh.b: 他批评的是官方拆除，不是商业化问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 没有任何证据表明他主张改用纸条。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。他明确说这些爱情象征应被重视并保留下来。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120913-lost-love-on-the-tiber-q01: 节目核心在讲罗马情侣挂锁并把钥匙扔进台伯河的传统，以及市政当局因桥梁受损而拆除挂锁的争议。其他选项都偏离了主线。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120913-lost-love-on-the-tiber-q02: 报道明确说情侣把自行车锁锁在桥上或灯柱上，然后把钥匙扔进台伯河，象征永恒爱情。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120913-lost-love-on-the-tiber-q03: 提到穿工装、拿断 bolt cutters 的人，是为了说明市政人员已经在现场拆除挂锁，不是在解释上锁过程，也不是说有小偷。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120913-lost-love-on-the-tiber-q04: 最后一段说引发这一传统的作者认为当局犯了大错，这些爱情象征应该被珍视、尊重并保留下来，所以可推断他支持保留挂锁。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: A plan to classify poor families in Nepal so aid can be directed to them
A debate about whether Nepal should stop collecting economic data
Why international lenders refuse to support Nepal
How food prices in Nepal compare with those in other countries

evidence.quote:
In today's programme, we have news of a pioneering scheme to help
families in one of the World's poorest countries.

They'll later allocate new
'poverty cards' which will make these families eligible in the future for government
subsidies

private.explanationZh: 节目开头说明主题是帮助尼泊尔贫困家庭的新方案，随后具体解释这种方案会发放“贫困卡”，让最贫困家庭获得补贴，所以主旨是识别贫困人口并定向提供援助。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了从调查识别到后续补贴的完整流程。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目讲的是启动大规模数据收集，而不是停止收集。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有讨论国际贷款机构拒绝支持尼泊尔。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目关注贫困识别和福利分配，不是物价国际比较。

public.prompt: According to the report, how are people in extreme poverty identified?

options.text: They own no land at all
They must travel far for medical care
They go hungry for several months of the year
They live only in mountain villages

evidence.quote:
people who go
hungry for either three, six or nine months of each year.

private.explanationZh: 记者给出的定义非常具体：极端贫困者是那些每年会有三个月、六个月或九个月挨饿的人。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有把“完全没有土地”作为判定标准。
optionRationalesZh.b: 医疗可及性是补贴可能覆盖的领域之一，但不是这里的界定方式。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。原文明确以每年挨饿数月作为极端贫困的识别标准。
optionRationalesZh.d: 山区地形会增加执行难度，但不等于极端贫困的定义。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention bias, fraud, and corruption?

options.text: To show why the new program may be difficult to carry out fairly
To argue that the government should cancel the program immediately
To explain why poverty cards are already being misused nationwide
To show that foreign groups are controlling the project

evidence.quote:
preventing bias against different ethnic
groups and protecting the programme from fraud and corruption.

private.explanationZh: 这些例子都是执行层面的风险。节目借它们说明，这个计划虽然有前景，但在信息真实性、公平性和廉洁性方面都面临现实挑战。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。三项风险共同说明项目实施并不容易。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有主张立刻取消，反而说许多人欢迎这一方案。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文说的是需要防止这些问题，不是说全国已经普遍发生。
optionRationalesZh.d: 没有提到外国组织控制该项目。

public.prompt: Why are Nepal's poorest people described as the best judges of the scheme?

options.text: They will decide how much international aid Nepal receives
Their lives should improve if the program truly works
They are responsible for collecting the survey data
They are less likely than officials to make errors

evidence.quote:
The best judges, of
course, will be Nepal's most poor - whose burden this new national programme is
supposed to ease.

Nepal's poor, whose lives may
be made a little easier.

private.explanationZh: “最佳评判者”这一说法意味着项目是否成功，最终要看最贫困人群的负担有没有真正减轻，也就是受益对象的实际生活是否得到改善。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说最贫困者决定国际援助额度。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。因为项目本来就是为了减轻他们的负担，他们的实际感受最能检验成效。
optionRationalesZh.c: 上门收集数据的是官员，不是最贫困者本人。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文强调的是受益结果，不是说他们更少出错。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120920-nepal-s-poverty-battle-q01: 整期节目围绕尼泊尔政府识别极端贫困家庭、发放 poverty cards，并据此提供补贴的计划展开。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120920-nepal-s-poverty-battle-q02: 报道明确把极端贫困者定义为一年中有三到九个月挨饿的人，因此正确项是按饥饿月数来识别。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120920-nepal-s-poverty-battle-q03: 节目提到偏见、欺诈和腐败，是在说明这项大规模计划执行时会遇到的公平性和管理难题，不是在说项目应立刻取消。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120920-nepal-s-poverty-battle-q04: 原文说最好的评判者是尼泊尔最贫困的人，因为这个国家项目本来就是为了减轻他们的负担；若项目有效，他们的生活会改善。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the photo project discussed in the program?

options.text: To advertise household products to urban Chinese consumers
To document rural families and show social change through their possessions
To compare Chinese photography with European photography
To persuade families to move from villages into cities

evidence.quote:
a photographer called Huang Qingjun has been travelling around
China for over a decade, taking photos of families standing outside their
houses.

The photographer wanted to capture the lives and material goods of rural
Chinese families. The photos reveal some of the social changes happening
across China.

private.explanationZh: 节目介绍摄影师长期拍摄中国家庭，并强调这些照片不仅记录家庭与物品，还揭示中国社会正在发生的变化，所以项目目的在于通过“家庭 possessions”呈现社会变迁。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目不是商业广告，也没有面向城市消费者推销产品。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。既有记录家庭生活，也有通过物品反映社会变化这层意义。
optionRationalesZh.c: 没有进行中欧摄影风格比较。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目提到城市化影响，但摄影项目本身不是劝人搬家。

public.prompt: What unusual request does the photographer make of the families?

options.text: He asks them to move as many belongings as possible outside the house
He asks them to wear traditional clothes from the 1950s
He asks them to sell old possessions before the photo is taken
He asks them to group themselves by age inside the house

evidence.quote:
Well, he asks the families to place all their belongings in front of the
house too.

private.explanationZh: 摄影师的特别做法是让一家人把能搬出来的物品尽量摆到屋外，再与这些物品一起拍照。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这正是该系列照片最独特的拍摄安排。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有说要穿五十年代服装。
optionRationalesZh.c: 没有提到先出售旧物，相反是把物品展示出来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 没有要求按年龄在屋内站位。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker compare the 'four big things' of the 1950s with those of the 1980s?

options.text: To show that photography equipment became cheaper over time
To illustrate how Chinese consumer desires changed across generations
To argue that older possessions were better made than newer ones
To explain why rural families stopped using bicycles completely

evidence.quote:
In the 1950s, a sewing machine was one of the 'four big things' that
Chinese families would crave.

By the 1980s, the four big things that people wanted were: a television, a
washing machine, a rice cooker and a fridge.

private.explanationZh: 这里并不是在背历史清单，而是通过两个时代“最想拥有的东西”不同，组织出一条消费观念和生活条件变化的线索，帮助听者理解照片背后的社会转型。

optionRationalesZh.a: 比较对象不是摄影器材价格。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两份清单形成鲜明对照，说明不同时代人们重视的消费品发生了变化。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有评价旧物品质更好。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有说农村家庭完全停止使用自行车。

public.prompt: Why does the photographer see some of these changes as a cause for optimism?

options.text: Because modern apartments are always larger than rural homes
Because government museums will preserve every demolished house
Because roads and electricity can expand mobility and access to information
Because urban families usually own fewer unnecessary possessions

evidence.quote:
Building roads to these remote areas means people can move
around more freely.

Neil: And by providing electricity, they can get news and ideas through the TV
or internet.

private.explanationZh: 尽管一些旧生活方式在消失，摄影师仍然乐观，因为基础设施改善意味着偏远地区的人能更自由流动，也能通过电视和网络接触新闻与观念。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说现代公寓一定更大。
optionRationalesZh.b: 没有提到博物馆保存被拆掉的房屋。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。道路和电力带来的行动与信息机会正是他乐观的依据。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目最后反而暗示城市富人可能拥有更多东西。

reasonsZh.bbc-20120927-family-belongings-q01: 摄影项目拍摄中国家庭与其家当，目的是捕捉农村家庭生活和物质拥有状况，并借此呈现社会变化。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120927-family-belongings-q02: 节目直接说摄影师让这些家庭把所有 belongings 尽可能搬到屋外，再和家人一起拍照。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120927-family-belongings-q03: 先说 1950 年代的“四大件”，再说 1980 年代的“四大件”，作用是展示不同年代中国家庭渴望的消费品如何变化。
reasonsZh.bbc-20120927-family-belongings-q04: 摄影师认为变化值得乐观，因为修路让偏远地区的人更自由流动，通电又让他们能通过电视或互联网获得新闻和观念。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion about genetically modified cows?

options.text: How genetically modified cows might reduce milk allergies, while also raising ethical concerns
Why all allergies are caused by environmental pollution
How dairy farmers in New Zealand market breast milk substitutes
Why campaign groups oppose every kind of medical research

evidence.quote:
going to be taking a look at how genetically modified food might be able
to help people with allergies.

campaign groups have questioned the ethics of genetically modifying farm
animals.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就说要讨论转基因食品如何帮助过敏患者，后面又补充活动团体对改造农场动物的伦理质疑，因此全文主旨是“潜在医疗益处”与“伦理争议”并存。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时概括了科学目标和争议点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有把所有过敏归因于环境污染。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中讨论的是牛奶蛋白与转基因奶牛，不是母乳替代品营销。
optionRationalesZh.d: 活动团体质疑的是这类做法的伦理，不是反对一切医学研究。

public.prompt: According to the report, what is unusual about babies' reactions to cow's milk?

options.text: Some babies' immune systems attack proteins in cow's milk but not in breast milk
Babies usually react more strongly to breast milk than to cow's milk
Most babies become allergic only after drinking milk for several years
Babies react only when cow's milk is mixed with other foods

evidence.quote:
Allergic reactions to cow’s milk are common in babies. Up to three percent treat it as
dangerous with their immune systems launching an attack on proteins found in cow's
milk, but never in their mother's breast milk.

private.explanationZh: 报道指出，有些婴儿会把牛奶中的蛋白质当成危险物质，于是免疫系统发起攻击；但对母乳不会这样反应，这正是问题的关键。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文明确对比了牛奶蛋白与母乳的不同反应。
optionRationalesZh.b: 与原文相反，原文说母乳不会引发这种攻击。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有说要喝几年后才出现过敏。
optionRationalesZh.d: 没有提到必须与其他食物混合才会发生反应。

public.prompt: What did the researchers do to the cows?

options.text: They trained the cows to produce more breast-milk-like proteins
They changed the cows genetically to stop a key protein from being produced
They fed the cows a new diet to wash the protein out of the milk
They removed all proteins from the milk after milking

evidence.quote:
researchers in New Zealand have cut out one of the main culprits - the protein
beta-lactoglobulin. They added new genetic material to disrupt its manufacturing
process in a technique called RNA interference.

private.explanationZh: 科学家锁定了主要致敏蛋白beta-lactoglobulin，并通过加入新的遗传物质干扰其生成过程，本质上就是对奶牛进行基因改造，使这种蛋白不再产生。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说训练奶牛产生类似母乳的蛋白。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。核心做法就是基因层面的干预，阻止关键蛋白生成。
optionRationalesZh.c: 没有提到通过饲料改变来清除蛋白。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中不是在挤奶后去除全部蛋白，而是在奶牛体内阻断某种蛋白的形成。

public.prompt: Why does the discussion end by mentioning super crops and world hunger?

options.text: To show that genetic modification is discussed as having possible benefits beyond this milk study
To prove that the cow experiment has already ended world hunger
To explain why campaign groups changed their minds completely
To argue that milk allergies are more serious than food shortages

evidence.quote:
campaign groups have questioned the ethics of genetically modifying farm
animals.

some people argue that genetic modification could be
used to produce super crops, and maybe, one day, help to stop world
hunger.

private.explanationZh: 节目先提出伦理质疑，随后又提到超级作物和世界饥饿，是为了把讨论从单一奶牛实验扩展到更广泛的转基因争论，说明支持者认为它可能带来更大的社会收益。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。结尾是在平衡争议，展示转基因技术被支持者寄予的更广泛希望。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目只是说“也许有一天”有帮助，远没有说已经解决饥饿。
optionRationalesZh.c: 没有说活动团体改变了立场。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有把两类问题做这种严重程度排序。

reasonsZh.bbc-20121004-genetically-modified-cows-q: 节目重点是转基因奶牛如何可能减少婴儿对牛奶的过敏，同时也引发伦理和健康层面的担忧。
报道说一些婴儿的免疫系统会攻击牛奶中的蛋白质，但不会攻击母乳中的蛋白质，这正是其“不寻常”之处。
研究人员加入新的遗传物质，干扰 beta-lactoglobulin 的生成过程，也就是通过基因改造让奶牛不再产生关键过敏蛋白。
结尾提到超级作物和世界饥饿，是把讨论从这项牛奶研究扩展到基因改造可能带来的更广泛潜在益处。

public.prompt: What is the central issue discussed in the program?

options.text: Whether writing on a famous painting should be seen as art or vandalism
Why modern galleries refuse to display abstract paintings
How Pablo Picasso influenced Mark Rothko's style
Whether museums should allow visitors to use marker pens

evidence.quote:
A man walked up to a painting by Mark Rothko, took out a marker pen
and wrote on it.

which they
consider not to be the work of an artist, but an act of vandalism.

private.explanationZh: 节目围绕一个人在Rothko画作上书写这一事件展开，并最终对比两种判断：这是艺术表达，还是破坏公物。因此中心问题正是“艺术还是故意毁坏”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了整段讨论的核心争议。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有说画廊拒绝展示抽象画。
optionRationalesZh.c: 开头提到毕加索只是引入话题，没有讨论其对Rothko的影响。
optionRationalesZh.d: 虽然事件涉及黑色记号笔，但节目关注的是行为性质，不是参观规则本身。

public.prompt: Why does Neil ask Jen to imagine someone writing on Guernica?

options.text: To test whether she knows who painted Guernica
To help listeners feel the shock of damaging a famous painting
To suggest that Guernica will be discussed later in detail
To compare Spanish art prices with British art prices

evidence.quote:
How would you feel if
someone went up to it with a black marker pen and wrote their name on
it?

Well, that's exactly what happened to another painting at the Tate Modern
gallery in London.

private.explanationZh: Neil先让Jen设想自己喜爱的名画被人乱写，再马上说明类似事情真的发生了。这个设问的作用是让听者先产生情感上的震惊，从而更好进入后面的新闻事件。

optionRationalesZh.a: Jen前面已经说出作品和作者，这里不是知识测试。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个假设场景主要是为了制造代入感和震撼感。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有继续详细分析《格尔尼卡》。
optionRationalesZh.d: 没有涉及西班牙与英国艺术品价格比较。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about Vladimir Umanets's attitude toward his action?

options.text: He regrets it completely and wants to apologize
He is pleased with it but frustrated that others do not understand it
He thinks it was a joke that went too far
He believes the gallery asked him to do it

evidence.quote:
I'm glad that I did it, obviously. I'm really… from one side I'm really happy, you know. I
really can have a good laugh, you know, from it and.... But from another side I'm sad
because people still can't see what it's all about, you know, and how beautiful it is,

private.explanationZh: 他一方面说自己很高兴、甚至能因此发笑，另一方面又说自己感到难过，因为人们看不懂这件事的意义。这说明他的态度是自我肯定但也因不被理解而受挫。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他说“我很高兴我这么做了”，明显不是完全后悔。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。他同时表达了满意和被误解后的失落。
optionRationalesZh.c: 虽然他提到可以笑一笑，但并没有说这只是玩笑失控。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有任何证据表明画廊请他这么做。

public.prompt: According to Umanets, what does Yellowism allow artists to do?

options.text: Replace abstract paintings with more realistic ones
Turn museum objects into yellow-colored copies
Take an existing work and place a new message on it
Create art only from everyday bathroom objects

evidence.quote:
Art allows us to take what somebody's done and
put a new message on it." The message he wrote on the Rothko painting
was "A potential Piece of Yellowism."

private.explanationZh: 节目直接引用了Umanets对Yellowism的定义：艺术允许人们把别人已经完成的作品拿来，再在上面放入新的信息。因此正确答案是“在既有作品上赋予新信息”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他没有说要用写实作品替换抽象画。
optionRationalesZh.b: Yellowism不是把作品简单涂成黄色。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这正是他亲自给出的定义。
optionRationalesZh.d: 他提到小便池是为了说明灵感来源，不是Yellowism只能使用这类物件。

reasonsZh.bbc-20121011-art-or-vandalism-q01: 整期节目讨论的核心问题，就是在名画上书写究竟应被看作艺术表达还是蓄意破坏。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121011-art-or-vandalism-q02: Neil 让 Jen 想象有人在《格尔尼卡》上乱写，是为了让听众代入并感受到破坏名画的震惊感和冒犯性。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121011-art-or-vandalism-q03: Umanets 说自己“glad”“really happy”，但又“sad because people still can't see what it's all about”，可推断他对行为本身满意，但对别人不理解感到沮丧。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121011-art-or-vandalism-q04: 节目直接引用 Umanets 的话：艺术让人“take what somebody's done and put a new message on it”，所以 Yellowism 的意思就是在现有作品上加上新信息。

public.prompt: According to Fiona McPherson, what is the most important requirement for a new word to make the list?
