options.text: It must be supported by real, independent usage.
It must be created by a well-known author.
It must be connected to politics rather than sport or culture.
It must already have entered major dictionaries.

evidence.quote:
First and foremost it’s about the evidence; so as long as people are using it and we can find
independent examples.

private.explanationZh: Fiona McPherson 明确说，首要标准是“evidence”，也就是这个词已经被人实际使用，而且还能找到独立的使用实例。所以入选并不取决于名人、题材或是否已经收录进词典，而是取决于可验证的真实使用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文直接把“evidence”和“independent examples”列为最重要的条件。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说词必须由知名作家创造，只强调是否有人真实使用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目提到新词可能来自流行文化、体育、政治等多个领域，并不限于政治。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。前面还特别说明这些词未必会很快进入词典，所以这不是首要条件。

public.prompt: What does Neil say the word "omnishambles" usually refers to?

options.text: A brief misunderstanding in the media
A series of political mistakes and blunders
A new dictionary of current expressions
A successful plan that spreads widely

evidence.quote:
Something that is an omnishambles is a complete and total disaster. It usually
refers to a series of mistakes and blunders by politicians.

private.explanationZh: Neil 先解释了 shambles 是一团糟，再说明 omnishambles 强调的是“彻底搞砸”。他随后把这个词的常见用法限定为政治人物犯下一连串错误和失误，因此正确答案必须同时包含“连续性”和“政治语境”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文强调的是严重而连续的失误，不是一次小误会。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。Neil 直接说它通常指 politicians 的一系列 mistakes and blunders。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。omnishambles 是被讨论的词，不是词典。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这个词表达的是“完全的灾难”，不是成功计划。

public.prompt: Why does the program include Ed Miliband's example from Parliament?

options.text: To show that Ed Miliband invented the word himself
To compare several different tax policies in detail
To illustrate how the word is used to criticize multiple government mistakes together
To explain why dictionaries avoid political vocabulary

evidence.quote:
Over the last month, we’ve seen the charity tax shambles, the churches tax shambles, the

6 Minute English © bbclearningenglish.com 2012

caravan tax shambles and the pasty tax shambles. So, Mr Speaker, we’re all keen to hear the
prime minister’s view as to why he thinks, four weeks on from the budget, even people within
Downing Street are calling it an omnishambles budget.

private.explanationZh: 这段议会发言把多个“tax shambles”并列起来，最后用“omnishambles budget”统一概括。节目引用它，不是为了讨论税收细节，而是为了具体展示这个词怎样把许多相关失误打包成一个更强烈的批评标签。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目 earlier 已说明该词最早出现在 BBC 政治喜剧中，不是 Ed Miliband 发明的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里列举不同税务问题只是素材，重点是这些问题如何被总称为 omnishambles。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个例子最核心的作用就是展示该词在真实政治语境中的批评功能。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目并没有借这个例子说明词典排斥政治词汇。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Fiona McPherson's comments about forms like "Romneyshambles"?

options.text: The original word has already replaced "shambles" in everyday English.
The judges only favor words used in American politics.
The word is likely to disappear because it is too tied to one event.
The word may last because people can adapt it to new situations.

evidence.quote:
Things like that are good indicators that
maybe this word will have staying power, but really time is the only thing that will, will tell.

private.explanationZh: Fiona McPherson 认为，出现像 Romneyshambles 这样的变体，说明这个词具有可扩展性。能被不断改造、套用到新的语境里，往往意味着它更可能保留下来，这正是她所说的“staying power”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说它取代了 shambles，只说它产生了衍生形式。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Romneyshambles 只是一个例子，不代表评委只偏好美国政治语境。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。她的判断方向正相反，变体的出现恰恰被视为可能持续存在的迹象。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。可变性和可迁移性正是她推测该词可能更长久流行的依据。

reasonsZh.bbc-20121122-omnishambles-q01: Fiona McPherson 明确说“first and foremost it’s about the evidence”，并解释为要能找到独立例证、不是只有一个人在用，所以答案是有真实且独立的使用证据。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121122-omnishambles-q02: Neil 解释说 omnishambles 指“a complete and total disaster”，而且“usually refers to a series of mistakes and blunders by politicians”，直接对应一连串政治失误。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121122-omnishambles-q03: 节目先解释词义，再播放 Ed Miliband 把多个 tax shambles 统称为 omnishambles budget 的例子，作用是示范这个词如何把多项政府失误合并起来进行批评。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121122-omnishambles-q04: Fiona McPherson 说像 Romneyshambles 这样的派生形式是“good indicators” ，说明这个词有适应新语境的能力，因此可能具有 staying power。

public.prompt: What does the report about the funeral suggest about King George Tupou V?

options.text: He was mainly respected by foreign governments rather than local people.
He was widely respected and drew a very large public response.
He preferred a quiet ceremony attended by only close officials.
He ruled only a small section of the islands.

evidence.quote:
1,000 pall-bearers took part in the ceremony to carry the coffin of the late king during the
funeral ceremony. Hundreds of foreign dignitaries and thousands of other mourners were
present at the Christian burial service

private.explanationZh: 报道突出葬礼规模极大：有一千名抬棺者、数百位外国政要以及成千上万的哀悼者。这样的场面共同指向一个结论，即这位国王具有广泛影响力，并且得到了公众层面的高度重视和纪念。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文既提到外国政要，也提到大量普通 mourners，不能缩小为“主要受外国政府尊重”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。大规模参与本身就是其受尊敬、引发广泛社会回应的证据。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。葬礼显然不是小范围、私密性的仪式。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目说 Tonga 分布在将近 200 个岛屿上，并非只统治其中一小部分。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers mention the protests in 2006 and then the reforms in 2010?

options.text: To explain why the funeral attracted foreign dignitaries
To argue that Tonga should end its monarchy immediately
To contrast his difficult beginning with the democratic changes he later introduced
To show that democratic elections caused the earlier riots

evidence.quote:
Many Tongan
people protested for democratic reform which led to riots in which eight
people died.

Rosie: Following these protests the king then chose to reduce his powers and let his
country have democratic elections for the first time in 2010.

private.explanationZh: 节目先交代他刚即位时面临抗议和骚乱，再说明他后来主动削减权力、推动选举。这种先负面背景、后改革结果的组织方式，目的是形成对照，突出他在短时间内完成的政治转变。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里不是在解释宾客构成，而是在交代其政治形象为何发生变化。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有主张立即废除君主制。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。前后两段信息构成鲜明对比，核心就在于“从争议到改革”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文顺序是先有抗议，再有改革与选举，不是选举导致骚乱。

public.prompt: Why does the report mention that the king drove around in a London taxi?

options.text: To show that he spent most of his time in Britain
To explain how people traveled during the funeral
To prove that he rejected traditional Tongan customs
To give a concrete example of an eccentric trait that people found endearing

evidence.quote:
like driving around in a London taxi
endeared him to many of his people.

private.explanationZh: “开着伦敦出租车到处转”被放进报道里，并不是交通信息，而是一个性格细节。它具体化了“eccentric”这个评价，同时又紧接着说明这种古怪特征反而让很多民众觉得他可亲、讨喜。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有据此说明他大部分时间住在英国。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这个细节与葬礼流程无关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目并没有把这一行为解释为反对传统习俗。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它正是“古怪但讨人喜欢”这一形象的例证。

public.prompt: Based on the end of the program, what will most likely happen next in Tonga?

options.text: The monarchy will continue under the late king's brother.
The islands will be divided among foreign dignitaries.
The country will stop using the name George for future kings.
The funeral reforms will be reversed by popular demand.

evidence.quote:
His brother will become the new king.
He’s also called George, so he will become George VI.

private.explanationZh: 节目结尾直接说明由已故国王的兄弟继位，而且新的君主名号也已给出。因此最合理的后续判断就是：Tonga 的君主制不会中断，而是按现有继承顺序继续下去。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文已经明确说明继位人选和新的王号。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。没有任何内容表明外国政要会决定或瓜分统治权。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。恰好相反，节目说新国王也将使用 George 这个名字。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有提到民众要求撤销改革，更没有说会因此改变继承安排。

reasonsZh.bbc-20121126-funeral-fit-for-a-king-q01: 报道提到 1,000 名抬棺者、数以千计的哀悼者和许多外国政要参加葬礼，结合 Rosie 说这说明他“really was quite popular”，可推断他受到广泛尊敬并引发巨大公众反应。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121126-funeral-fit-for-a-king-q02: 节目先说他 2006 年刚即位时并不受欢迎，还发生要求民主改革的抗议；随后又说他后来主动减少权力、推动 2010 年首次民主选举。这样的组织方式是在对比他艰难的开端与后来带来的民主改革。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121126-funeral-fit-for-a-king-q03: 在介绍他“eccentric”之后，报道举出他开伦敦出租车的例子，并马上说明这些特质“endeared him to many of his people”，所以这是一个具体例子，说明其古怪之处为何反而讨人喜欢。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121126-funeral-fit-for-a-king-q04: 节目结尾明确说 Tonga 仍将保留君主制，而且“His brother will become the new king”，因此最可能发生的是由其兄弟继位，王室继续存在。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: How ancient legends inspired modern ocean expeditions
Why navigators should stop using online maps completely
How a supposed island stayed on maps even though it did not really exist
Why researchers wanted to prove that Atlantis was real

evidence.quote: we'll be hearing about an island that has appeared on maps for several years, but which people now realise has never existed at all.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头就点明主题：地图上长期存在的一座岛，其实根本不存在。后面的采访和解释都是围绕这个核心问题展开的，所以主旨不是古代传说，也不是一般性的导航建议，而是“错误岛屿如何出现在地图上并长期流传”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目提到传奇岛屿只是开头问答，不是主体。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目讨论的是地图错误的成因与修正，不是主张彻底停止使用网络地图。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个选项最完整概括了整期节目的中心内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。Atlantis 只是节目末尾的答案，不是主要调查对象。

public.prompt: Why did Maria Seaton and her team become suspicious when they approached the area?

options.text: They were told by local people that the island had recently moved.
Their ship had lost its navigation equipment.
They saw another island nearby that was larger than expected.
Their charts showed deep water where the island was supposed to be.

evidence.quote: scientific map showed there was an island there. And yet the navigation charts on board the vessel showed that we had a water depth of 1,400 metres. So that's where we started getting suspicious.

private.explanationZh: 让研究团队起疑的关键，是“地图说这里有岛”与“船上海图显示这里有 1,400 米深的海水”之间的明显矛盾。深海条件与岛屿存在无法同时成立，所以他们开始怀疑地图信息本身有误。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有提到当地居民提供此类信息。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。问题不在设备丢失，而在不同地图信息互相冲突。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说他们看到另一座更大的岛。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。深水读数正是他们产生怀疑的直接原因。

public.prompt: What explanation does the program suggest for how Sandy Island remained on many maps?

options.text: One original mistake was repeated as later mapmakers copied earlier sources.
The island appeared and disappeared with changing sea levels every few years.
Researchers kept the island on maps because tourists wanted to visit it.
Space photographs were too detailed to remove the island quickly.

evidence.quote: one person made a mistake and put the island on the map and then other people just copied their map over and over again.

private.explanationZh: 节目给出的推断是：最早可能只是一次人为错误，但后来制图者不断沿用、复制前人的资料，导致错误被层层继承。也就是说，问题不是岛屿反复变化，而是信息复制机制让错误固化了。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文明确把错误的长期存在归因于反复复制。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目明确说“it was never there”，不是周期性沉没或浮现。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。没有任何内容提到旅游需求影响地图标注。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目并没有说卫星照片太详细导致无法删除，重点是资料来源会彼此引用。

public.prompt: Why does the program mention that Google asked users to report similar mistakes?

options.text: To show that digital maps are less useful than paper maps
To emphasize that maps rely on multiple sources and can be corrected when errors are noticed
To suggest that Sandy Island might still exist somewhere else
To prove that only Google, not other mapmakers, made the mistake

evidence.quote: Google Earth Maps Australia said they use a variety of sources to compile their maps, but that things change. And they say... They're advising people to tell them if they spot similar mistakes, could they let them know?

private.explanationZh: 这里提到 Google 欢迎用户反馈，不是为了推卸责任，而是为了说明制图本身依赖多种来源，错误一旦被发现，也需要通过不断修正来提高准确度。这个细节把节目从“错误如何产生”推进到“错误如何被纠正”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有比较纸质地图和电子地图孰优孰劣。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。多来源编图加上用户反馈，正体现了地图会动态修订这一点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里并不是在暗示岛仍然存在，而是在谈纠错机制。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。前文说错误出现在多种地图上，不只 Google 一家。

reasonsZh.bbc-20121129-invisible-island-q01: 整期节目围绕 Sandy Island 展开，核心内容就是这个岛多年出现在地图上，但考察后发现根本不存在，因此主旨是一个并不存在的岛为何长期留在地图上。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121129-invisible-island-q02: Maria Seaton 说科学地图显示那里有岛，但船上的导航海图显示水深 1,400 米，“that’s where we started getting suspicious”，所以他们起疑的原因是海图显示那里应是深水。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121129-invisible-island-q03: 澳大利亚海军制图部门说这“could simply be human error just repeated down the years”，Neil 进一步解释为一个人先犯错，后来的人不断照抄，因此答案是原始错误被后续制图者重复复制。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121129-invisible-island-q04: 在说到 Google 要用户报告类似错误之前，节目先说明地图是用“a variety of sources”编成的；接着提到发现错误后可以反馈修正，所以这个细节是为了强调地图依赖多种来源，也能在发现错误时被纠正。

public.prompt: What is the main issue discussed in the program?

options.text: Whether professional cycling events should return to London
Why cyclists should be required to wear more protective clothing
Which world cities deserve to be ranked highest for cycling
Who is responsible for danger and conflict between cyclists and motorists on the roads

evidence.quote:
The War on Britain’s Roads’ has been
investigating the issue.

private.explanationZh: 开头把纪录片名称和“the issue”并列提出，随后节目就围绕 cyclists 与 motorists 的冲突展开两方观点。也就是说，中心不是赛事或装备，而是道路危险究竟该由谁负责、冲突为何会发生。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有把重点放在职业赛事是否回归。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。装备只被顺带提到，不是主问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。最佳骑行城市只是开头问答的一部分。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它最准确概括了整段讨论的核心争议。

public.prompt: Why does Jan Etherington describe some cyclists as people who "go out to war"?

options.text: To portray a noticeable minority of cyclists as deliberately aggressive and confrontational
To praise cyclists for their courage in difficult traffic
To explain that cyclists are trained like soldiers
To show that helmets and lycra remove most road danger

evidence.quote:
noticeable aggressive minority that I
think the cycling community should recognise and address.

private.explanationZh: Jan Etherington 用“go out to war”这样的战斗隐喻，不是中性描写，而是在批评一小部分骑车人带着对抗姿态上路。她后面明确补充这是“noticeable aggressive minority”，说明矛头指向的是显眼但并非全部的少数群体。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个比喻的目的就是把某些骑车人的行为刻画成主动、好斗。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她并不是在赞扬，而是在批评。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说骑车人接受军事式训练。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里讨论的是态度和行为，不是装备防护效果。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Michael Hutchinson's comments?

options.text: Cyclists usually feel stronger than large vehicles in traffic
Reasonable cyclists avoid confrontation because they know they are vulnerable
Most accidents happen because cyclists want to race with trucks
Cyclists become safe as long as they wear casual clothes instead of lycra

evidence.quote:
You do feel quite vulnerable as a cyclist. Somebody drives past inches away , the first thing it is,
is frightening. I certainly don’t go to war, because frankly I’m not going to win.

private.explanationZh: Michael Hutchinson 的逻辑很清楚：骑车人面对大型车辆时处于弱势，因此首先感受到的是害怕，而不是进攻欲望。他说自己不会“go to war”，正说明理性的骑车人更可能避免冲突，因为他们知道自己并不占优势。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文强调的是 vulnerable，不是更强。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。脆弱感和“不可能赢”的判断共同支撑这个推论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Michael 的意思恰恰是否认骑车人主动想和卡车“开战”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。服装不是论点核心，核心是骑车人与大型车辆力量悬殊。

public.prompt: What does Rob imply when he says he can see the problem "from all sides"?

options.text: Pedestrians understand the issue better than cyclists or motorists do
The problem would disappear if everyone used bicycles
Different road users each have understandable concerns in this debate
He has stopped caring about cycling safety as a public issue

evidence.quote:
Well I’m a cyclist and a motorist – and a pedestrian – so I can see the problem
from all sides.

private.explanationZh: Rob 提到自己同时拥有 cyclist、motorist 和 pedestrian 三种身份，意思不是他更有权威，而是他能理解不同道路使用者各自的处境和担忧。因此这句话表达的是多方立场都有其合理性，而不是单方面归罪。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Rob 没有说行人比其他人更懂问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有把“人人骑车”当成解决方案。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。多重身份意味着他能理解这场争论中的多方顾虑。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。相反，他正是在认真看待这个安全议题。

reasonsZh.bbc-20121213-war-on-the-roads-q01: 节目一开始就提出骑车人与开车人之间的“battle”，后面分别呈现记者与骑行者的观点，讨论焦点始终是谁造成道路上的危险与冲突。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121213-war-on-the-roads-q02: Jan Etherington 说有些骑车人“go out to war”，紧接着说明这是“a minority”但却是“noticeable aggressive minority”，所以这句话的作用是把这部分人描绘成具有攻击性、好对抗的一群。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121213-war-on-the-roads-q03: Michael Hutchinson 说骑车人会感到“vulnerable”，卡车贴身经过会让人害怕，而且“only an idiot’s going to go to war with a bicycle”，可推断理性的骑车人因为知道自己脆弱，所以会避免正面冲突。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121213-war-on-the-roads-q04: Rob 说自己既是 cyclist、motorist 又是 pedestrian，所以“can see the problem from all sides”，意思是不同道路使用者各自都有可以理解的担忧与立场。

public.prompt: According to the program, what did December 21, 2012 represent in the Mayan calendar?

options.text: The end of a 5,000-year cycle
The beginning of the Mayan new year
The exact date when the world was certain to end
The anniversary of a major archaeological discovery

evidence.quote:
the ancient Mayan calendar said December
21 2012 marked the end of a 5,000 year cycle.

private.explanationZh: 节目对这一日期的定义非常明确：它在玛雅历法中标志着一个五千年周期的结束。节目也提到有人把它解读为世界末日，但那属于传播中的说法，不是这里所要求的历法含义本身。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文直接把这一天解释为 5,000 年周期的终点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有把它说成新年的开始。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目讨论的是“有人这样相信”，不是把它当作确定事实。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有把这一天与考古发现纪念日联系起来。

public.prompt: Why does the program include Professor Pedro Yac's comments?

options.text: To explain how Reuters conducted its public opinion poll
To prove that the Mayan prediction was scientifically correct
To present an alternative message that rejects fear and encourages reflection on human origins
To criticize people who study ancient calendars instead of modern science

evidence.quote:
Enough of the culture of fear. We should stop planting fear. Not in the present generations, not
in the future generations. We have to return to our roots.

private.explanationZh: Pedro Yac 的发言把注意力从“末日恐慌”转向“回归根源、反思人类与大地的关系”。节目引用这段话，是为了给玛雅历法提供一种非灾难化的理解框架：这一天可以成为思考价值与来源的契机，而不是制造恐惧的借口。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Reuters 民调是后面另一位记者提到的内容，与 Pedro Yac 这段发言无关。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Pedro Yac 并不是在证明末日预言准确，反而在反对恐惧式传播。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。反恐惧、重反思正是这段引言的功能。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里没有批评研究古代历法的人，而是在重新界定这一天的意义。

public.prompt: What does Tom Santorelli's report suggest about doomsday predictions?

options.text: They are believed almost exclusively in Central America.
They have discouraged filmmakers and publishers from covering the topic.
They are accepted by nearly everyone worldwide.
They attract substantial public and media interest.

evidence.quote:
The Doomsday prediction has spawned a
glut of films and books on the subject.

private.explanationZh: “spawned a glut of films and books”说明这一话题不仅被人谈论，而且已经刺激出大量影视和出版内容。由此可以推断，末日预言具有很强的传播性和吸引力，能引发广泛公众和媒体兴趣。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。记者说的是 worldwide，不是局限在中美洲。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文说它催生了大量作品，方向恰好相反。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目给出的比例是 nearly 10%，远不是“几乎所有人”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。大量相关书籍和电影正是高关注度的体现。

public.prompt: According to Alice, what is the correct English term for the event that usually occurs around December 21 in the northern hemisphere?

options.text: The winter equinox
The winter solstice
Midwinter
The harvest solstice

evidence.quote:
So the answer I was looking for is ‘the
winter solstice’. It’s the day when the sun at noon is at its lowest altitude above
the horizon.

private.explanationZh: 节目结尾 Alice 明确纠正了前面的选项判断：她要的准确术语是“the winter solstice”。她还补充定义，说这是正午太阳高度最低的一天，因此答案必须是这个专门术语，而不是较宽泛或错误的说法。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Alice 明说“winter equinox”这种说法并不存在。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。她在结尾直接给出了这个答案并作了解释。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。midwinter 可以表示这一时段附近，但不是她要求的精确术语。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目里没有出现“harvest solstice”这一说法。

reasonsZh.bbc-20121220-end-of-the-world-q01: Alice 直接说古玛雅历法认为 2012 年 12 月 21 日“marked the end of a 5,000 year cycle”，因此答案是五千年周期的结束。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121220-end-of-the-world-q02: Professor Pedro Yac 说要停止散播恐惧，回归根源，记住人类是“大地之母的孩子”。节目引用这段话，是为了提出一种不同于末日恐慌的信息：拒绝恐惧并反思人类起源与重要价值。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121220-end-of-the-world-q03: Tom Santorelli 说近 10% 的人认同这种解读，而且末日预言“has spawned a glut of films and books”，说明这种说法吸引了相当大的公众关注和媒体文化产出。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121220-end-of-the-world-q04: 节目末尾 Alice 明确纠正说答案不是 equinox，也不只是较宽泛的 midwinter，而是“the winter solstice”，因此正确术语是 winter solstice。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the talk?

options.text: why scientists are revising estimates of the number of human genes
how researchers found evidence of ancient viruses shared across mammals
why dolphins resist viral disease better than humans
how the placenta prevents viruses from entering DNA

evidence.quote:
Now, a new
scientific study has looked at the genes of lots of animals and found traces of
the same viruses that existed a long time ago.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查主旨。开头直接说明一项新研究在许多动物的基因中发现了同样的古老病毒痕迹，所以整段谈话的核心是跨多种哺乳动物发现古老病毒证据，而不是单纯讨论基因数量或某一种动物。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目一开始提到基因是什么，并有一个关于基因数量的测验，但那只是热身，不是整段讨论的中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。研究对象是许多动物，重点发现是它们共享古老病毒留下的痕迹，准确概括了全文主题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。海豚只是被顺带列为研究样本之一，节目没有讨论海豚比人类更能抵抗病毒。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。胎盘是在后面作为病毒也可能带来积极演化结果的例子，不是全文主线。

public.prompt: Which animals are mentioned as examples in the research?

options.text: humans, birds, horses, and sharks
mice, whales, lizards, and chimpanzees
elephants, frogs, dogs, and bats
humans, mice, elephants, and dolphins

evidence.quote: including humans, mice, elephants and dolphins

private.explanationZh: 这道题是细节定位题。记者原话明确列举了 humans, mice, elephants and dolphins，考生只要听准列举内容即可。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。原文没有提到 birds、horses 或 sharks，这是用常见动物制造的干扰。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。虽然 mice 出现在原文里，但 whales、lizards 和 chimpanzees 都没有被提到。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。只有 elephants 与原文一致，其余动物都不是原文列举对象。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。四种动物与记者列举完全一致。

public.prompt: Why does Helen mention the protein syncytin?

options.text: to argue that most viruses are harmless today
to explain how scientists counted human genes
to show that some ancient viruses contributed to useful evolutionary changes
to describe a disease that affects only female mammals

evidence.quote:
some viruses have
developed positive outcomes. For example the protein syncytin evolved from
a virus and has helped develop the female placenta.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查举例目的。Helen 先说有些病毒带来了积极结果，随后举出 syncytin 和胎盘的例子，作用是证明病毒并不只是留下疾病，也可能在进化过程中产生有益影响。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。她没有说“大多数病毒如今无害”，而是更谨慎地说某些病毒带来了积极结果。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这个例子与统计基因数量无关，而是用于说明病毒的演化影响。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。syncytin 的例子正是为了支持“某些病毒帮助了我们的演化”这一观点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。重点不是某种女性疾病，而是胎盘形成这一积极演化结果。

public.prompt: According to the talk, how might this research be useful in the future?

options.text: It may help scientists detect some cancers or infections earlier.
It may let scientists recreate the earliest mammals in laboratories.
It may prove that all viruses eventually become helpful.
It may show exactly how many genes every species has.

evidence.quote:
understanding these survivors from the distant past may provide clues to the early detection
of cancers or infections.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查结尾信息。研究负责人认为，理解这些来自远古的“幸存者”也许能为癌症或感染的早期发现提供线索，因此用途是帮助更早检测疾病。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。结尾明确说 may provide clues to the early detection of cancers or infections。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有提到在实验室重建最早的哺乳动物。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。原文只说有些病毒产生了积极结果，并没有得出“所有病毒最终都有益”的绝对结论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目的未来用途不是统计所有物种的基因数量。

reasonsZh.bbc-20121227-ancient-viruses-q01: 节目核心一直在讲研究人员比较多种哺乳动物基因后，发现共享的古老病毒痕迹；其余选项要么只提到开场小测验，要么是文中未说的内容。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121227-ancient-viruses-q02: 对应片段明确列出 humans, mice, elephants and dolphins，只有d与原文一致，其他选项都加入了未提到的动物。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121227-ancient-viruses-q03: Helen提到syncytin是为了举例说明某些病毒产生了积极结果，帮助了进化，例如帮助形成胎盘，因此是说明古老病毒也可能带来有益的进化变化。
reasonsZh.bbc-20121227-ancient-viruses-q04: 研究者Robert Belshaw明确说，理解这些远古幸存下来的病毒，可能为癌症或感染的早期发现提供线索，所以a最准确。

public.prompt: What is the discussion mainly about?

options.text: the history of radio aerials used by British soldiers
a wartime pigeon message discovered in a chimney and the effort to decode it
how pigeons were trained to fly faster than military aircraft
the invention of Julius Caesar's military code book

evidence.quote:
In the rubbish from the chimney he found a pigeon’s leg. Attached to
the leg was a small container and inside the container was a message written in
code.

the truth is that it was designed not to be cracked
easily.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题需要整合前后信息。前面介绍有人在烟囱里发现了带密码纸条的鸽子腿，后面又说明密码本来就不容易被破解，因此整段讨论主要围绕这条二战鸽子密信及其破译难题展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。无线电天线只是解释为什么当时会用鸽子传信的一个局部原因，不是全文中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它同时概括了“发现密信”和“难以破译”这两条主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有讨论如何把鸽子训练得比飞机更快，这明显偏离内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。凯撒密码出现在结尾答题环节，只是附带信息，不是本期主要讨论。

public.prompt: Why were pigeons especially useful for some army units during the war?

options.text: The units were moving too fast to set up radio equipment.
The units wanted to test a new kind of code in the field.
The pigeons could carry larger messages than radios could send.
The soldiers had permanently lost contact with their commanders.

evidence.quote:
it was on the move and basically it was
moving so fast it couldn’t put up an aerial for a wireless transmission to be sent so they would
quickly release a pigeon with a quick message.

private.explanationZh: 这是细节题。Gordon Corera 解释说，某些部队移动太快，来不及架设无线电天线，所以最快的办法是立刻放飞一只带有简短信息的鸽子。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文明确把“移动太快，来不及架天线”作为使用鸽子的原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有说他们是为了测试新密码才用鸽子。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。原文强调的是速度和便利，不是信息容量更大。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。没有提到他们永久失联，只是当时不便进行无线电传输。

public.prompt: Why does Gordon Corera mention a one-time pad?

options.text: to show that the message was probably sent by Julius Caesar
to compare military codes with ordinary household locks only for humor
to suggest that researchers have already found the missing key
to explain why the code may be impossible to break without the key

evidence.quote:
One is using something called a one-time pad which is a technique where you apply a random
key to a code and if you keep that secure and it’s truly random, it is basically unbreakable.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查解释性举例的目的。Gordon 提到 one-time pad，不是为了介绍历史背景，而是为了说明如果密钥是随机且保密的，那么没有钥匙就几乎无法破解该密码。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。凯撒只出现在节目最后的测验答案中，与 one-time pad 不是同一件事。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。锁和钥匙的类比主要由 Neil 用来帮助理解机制，不只是幽默比较。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。恰恰相反，节目强调没有钥匙就难以破解，并未说钥匙已经找到。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。提到 one-time pad 的核心作用就是说明这种编码方式为何会“基本无法破解”。

public.prompt: If researchers cannot find more contextual information, what is most likely to happen?

options.text: The pigeon will be identified as a fake.
The military history of the war will need to be rewritten.
The message will remain undecoded.
New computers will make the code easy to solve.

evidence.quote:
without more contextual information, basically who the sender was
who the recipient was, when it was sent and exactly the identity of the pigeon it may be
impossible to crack it.

private.explanationZh: 这道预测题基于结尾条件句。原文说如果没有更多背景信息，比如发件人、收件人、发送时间和鸽子身份，那么密码可能无法破解，所以最可能的结果就是这条信息继续保持未解状态。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目没有说这只鸽子可能是伪造品。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。是否改写二战史不是这里讨论的直接后果，范围过大。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。根据 may be impossible to crack it，可以推断信息会继续无法解读。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目强调缺的是背景和钥匙，不是单纯计算能力不足。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130110-6min-messenger-bird-audio-q: 整段讨论围绕在烟囱里发现的二战信鸽密信，以及人们如何尝试破解它展开，b概括最完整；其余选项都只涉及枝节或错误内容。
Gordon Corera解释说，某些部队移动太快，来不及架设无线电天线，所以会快速放飞信鸽传讯，a与原文直接对应。
提到one-time pad是为了说明如果密钥安全且随机，这种密码基本无法破解；没有密钥就很难甚至不可能破译，因此d正确。
原文说如果没有更多背景信息，比如发信人、收信人、时间和鸽子身份，密码可能无法破解，所以最可能的结果就是信息继续无法解码。

public.prompt: Why is the Mars mission described as a one-way trip?

options.text: The company wants volunteers to prove they can live without Earth.
Scientists can send people to Mars, but they cannot yet bring them back.
Return flights would be too expensive to insure.
The travelers will destroy their spacecraft after landing.

evidence.quote:
technology to get people from Mars back to Earth. The people
who will be going to Mars in our missions will be settling on Mars permanently.

private.explanationZh: 这道题是直接细节题。Bas 明确说现在有把人送到火星并维持生存的技术，但没有把人从火星送回地球的技术，所以这会是一次单程、永久定居的任务。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目讨论的是技术限制，而不是项目故意拿志愿者做证明。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文把“去得了、回不来”说得非常清楚。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。没有提到保险费用问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目也没有说着陆后会故意毁掉飞船。

public.prompt: Why does Bas Lansdorp say character matters more than specific jobs like doctor or engineer?

options.text: Technical training is unnecessary once the mission begins.
Only outgoing people can attract a television audience.
The crew must be able to live cooperatively under extreme isolation.
Experts on Earth will handle the difficult work later.

evidence.quote:
The thing that's important, the most important quality for our candidates is
character. You need to be the kind of person who other people want to be stranded with on an
uninhabited island.

private.explanationZh: 这道推断题需要理解“stranded with on an uninhabited island”的比喻。Bas 把任务成员比作被困在荒岛上的人，说明长期封闭相处时，能否与他人稳定合作比单一职业头衔更关键。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。Bas 不是说技术训练没用，而是说还有比职业标签更重要的因素。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这一段谈的是共同生活与性格适配，不是吸引观众。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。荒岛类比强调的是隔离环境下与人相处、共同承受压力的能力。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。原文没有说关键工作以后会交给地球上的专家处理。

public.prompt: What additional qualities does Bas say candidates need?

options.text: They should stay calm and be inventive in difficult situations.
They should be famous and comfortable speaking to reporters.
They should be wealthy enough to help finance the mission.
They should have previous experience living on islands.

evidence.quote:
You need to be calm and inventive. You need to be someone who is at their best when the
situation is at its worst.

private.explanationZh: 这是细节题。Bas 后面补充说候选人要 calm and inventive，而且要在情况最糟时也能发挥最好水平，所以关键是危机中的冷静和应变。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两项品质都被原文直接点出，并且和危机表现联系在一起。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目没有把名气或面对媒体的能力列为候选标准。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。融资方式在后面另谈，与候选人个人财富无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。荒岛只是比喻，不代表必须有真实的荒岛生活经历。

public.prompt: Why does Bas compare the mission to the London Olympics and mention people watching relationships develop on Mars?

options.text: to argue that space missions should be organized like sporting events
to show that life on Mars will soon be comfortable for families
to warn that public attention could harm the mission
to explain how the project expects to raise money through a large television audience

evidence.quote:
When people land on Mars we expect that even more
people will watch this than the London Olympics. And also in the years after that they will
watch as the base grows, as people start relations, end relations.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查说话者为何举后面的例子。Bas 提到奥运会的收视和未来观众会持续关注火星基地与人际关系，是为了说明项目将靠全球观众和媒体关注来融资。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。提到奥运会不是说任务组织形式像体育赛事，而是借它说明观众规模和收入潜力。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。他不是在证明火星生活舒适，只是在描述观众可能持续观看的内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。这里不是警告媒体风险，而是强调媒体关注会带来资金。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。整个例子都服务于前面的 finance 话题，核心就是通过电视观众筹资。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130117-6min-one-way-mars-audio-q01: Bas明确说现在有技术把人送到火星并维持生存，但没有把人从火星带回地球的技术，所以这是单程旅行。
reasonsZh.bbc-20130117-6min-one-way-mars-audio-q02: Bas说最重要的不是医生或工程师身份，而是character，还把它比作要成为别人愿意一起困在荒岛上的人，可推知重点是长期隔绝环境下的合作与相处能力。
reasonsZh.bbc-20130117-6min-one-way-mars-audio-q03: 他后面又明确补充需要calm and inventive，并且要在情况最糟时发挥最好，因此a准确概括了这些额外品质。
reasonsZh.bbc-20130117-6min-one-way-mars-audio-q04: Bas把任务和伦敦奥运会收视及后续看火星基地发展、关系变化联系起来，是在说明项目会通过吸引全球电视观众来筹资，因此d正确。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the discussion?

options.text: a school course teaching teenage boys etiquette and confidence
the history of finishing schools for wealthy girls
the rules for serving a formal meal in restaurants
the academic subjects most useful for job interviews

evidence.quote:
Today’s programme is
all about a course being run now for teenage boys in an English school.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题看开头即可。主持人明确说本期节目讲的是一所英国学校为 teenage boys 开设的一门课程，内容与礼仪和行为有关，后面又补充其目标包括自信与社交表现。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了课程对象、内容和讨论方向。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。finishing school 只是导入旧概念，不是节目主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。餐桌刀叉规则只是开场小测验，不是整段的主要话题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。求职相关作用在后半段出现，但不是唯一主题，更不是“学科”。

public.prompt: Why does John Parry mention that the girls asked for a course first?

options.text: to show that the girls behaved better than the boys
to explain how the etiquette lessons for boys got started
to argue that formal dances should be canceled
to prove that only girls cared about the Leavers' Ball

evidence.quote:
a group of girls actually wanted a course to prepare them for
the Year 11 L eavers’ Ball which is, which is something which most schools do these days.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查背景信息的作用。John Parry 提到最初是女生想为 Leavers’ Ball 做准备，随后男生说也想参加，目的是交代这门男生礼仪课“是怎么来的”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。这里不是比较男女谁更有礼貌。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它解释了课程产生的过程和直接动因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。原文没有主张取消舞会。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。恰恰相反，后来男生也表示想参与。

public.prompt: Who helps teach the boys, and what are they learning?

options.text: their parents; how to choose university courses
older students; how to organize formal dances
a local hotel; how to dress and act with confidence
restaurant critics; how to judge expensive meals

evidence.quote:
a local hotel who are teaching the boys, you know, all about
etiquette, all about how to dress, how to act with confidence and so on.

private.explanationZh: 这是细节题。校长直接说学校与一家 local hotel 密切合作，对方教男生 etiquette、穿着以及如何自信地表现自己。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。家长并不是文中提到的授课者，课程内容也不是选大学课程。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。没有说由高年级学生授课，也不是教他们组织舞会。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。地点和教学内容都与原文吻合。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。课程不是餐厅点评训练。

public.prompt: According to the headmaster, why can etiquette lessons be useful beyond social events?

options.text: They can replace most other career preparation at school.
They can guarantee that employers will never criticize students.
They can improve students' exam results in academic subjects.
They can strengthen social confidence for university and job interviews.

evidence.quote:
sometimes their social skills let them down. And we wanted our
students to, you know, walk with confidence, you know, hold their head up high in any
given social situation; whether that’ s, you know a ball, as we’re doing, or whe ther that’s g oing
for a university interview, a job interview.

private.explanationZh: 这道推断题要把“social skills let them down”和“university interview, a job interview”联系起来理解。校长认为学生即使知识和考试不错，也可能因为社交与自信不足吃亏，所以礼仪课的实际价值在于帮助他们更好地面对升学和求职场景。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。原文说的是补足社交能力，不是取代其他职业准备。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。课程也不可能保证别人永远不会批评学生，这说得过头了。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。校长明确区分了考试成绩和社交技能，课程重点不是提分。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。面试场景被直接点出，说明课程能增强相关社交自信。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130124-6min-boys-etiquette-web-130: 节目主要讨论英国一所学校给十几岁男生开设礼仪课程，内容包括穿着、举止和自信心培养，a最符合主旨。
John Parry提到先是女生想上为毕业舞会做准备的课程，随后男生也说想参加，这是在交代男生礼仪课是怎么开始的。
原文明说学校与当地一家酒店密切合作，由他们教男生礼仪、如何穿着以及如何自信地表现自己，所以c完全对应。
校长认为学生虽考试不错，但社交能力有时拖后腿；这些课程能帮助他们在大学面试和求职面试中更自信，因此d是合理推断。

public.prompt: What is Wayne Hemingway mainly arguing?

options.text: Science stories should be reported only after they change policy.
Bad news is unavoidable because audiences refuse all other topics.
News organizations should give more attention to stories that improve people's lives.
Designers should replace journalists in most newsrooms.

evidence.quote:
stories that could move mankind forward and positively impact on our lives don't get
the exposure they deserve.

private.explanationZh: 这道主旨题抓住 Wayne 的核心观点即可。他抱怨那些能推动人类进步、积极影响生活的故事得不到应有曝光，因此他的主要主张是新闻应更多关注这类积极、有建设性的内容。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。他并没有给科学报道设置这种条件。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。他批评的是新闻选择机制，而不是断言观众只接受坏消息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这正是他反复强调的中心论点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。他提到设计记者更多，是作为改进方向之一，不是要让设计师取代记者。

public.prompt: Which development does Wayne mention as a possible source of cheap, clean transport?

options.text: a solar airplane built in Japan
a bicycle made from recycled cardboard
an electric car designed for crowded cities
a train powered by stem-cell technology

evidence.quote:
And in Israel, Izhar Gafni
has developed a bicycle from recycled cardboard, which could bring cheap, clean transport to
some of the world's poorest and most polluted and congested cities.

private.explanationZh: 这是细节题。Wayne 举的第二个好消息例子就是以色列的 Izhar Gafni 发明了 recycled cardboard bicycle，并说它可能为贫穷、拥挤、污染严重的城市提供廉价清洁交通。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目没有提到太阳能飞机。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。纸板自行车正是原文点出的交通创新。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。电动车听起来合理，但不是节目中的例子。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。stem-cell 研究是第一个例子，而且与不孕治疗相关，不是交通工具。

public.prompt: What does Wayne imply when he says, "If that's fed to the people all of the time that's what they want"?

options.text: Constant exposure to bad news can shape what audiences come to prefer.
Viewers naturally understand science stories better than political ones.
People choose tabloids mainly because they are cheaper than broadsheets.
News editors should avoid all emotional reactions in reporting.

evidence.quote:
If that's fed to the people all of the
time that's what they want

private.explanationZh: 这道推断题考查代词和因果关系。Wayne 的意思不是说观众天生只爱坏消息，而是说如果媒体长期不断喂给他们这种内容，他们的口味就会被塑造，进而更加想看同类内容。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这里强调的是媒体供给对受众偏好的塑造作用。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文没有比较观众理解科学和政治新闻的能力。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。tabloids 与 broadsheets 只是类比，不是价格分析。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。他并不是在谈编辑要避免情绪，而是在批评坏消息消费循环。

public.prompt: According to Finn's summary, what change in newsroom staffing does Hemingway want?

options.text: more weather reporters and fewer science reporters
more war reporters and fewer design correspondents
more crime correspondents and fewer broadsheet editors
more design correspondents and fewer crime correspondents

evidence.quote:
He says that perhaps we need fewer crime correspondents and more design
correspondents.

private.explanationZh: 这道题考查结尾总结信息。Finn 复述 Wayne 的建议时明确说，需要 fewer crime correspondents and more design correspondents，这正是他希望新闻资源配置发生的变化。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。天气报道不是 Wayne 在这里要求增加的重点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。他主张的是减少 war reporters，而不是增加；同时他想增加 design correspondents。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。broadsheet editors 根本不是这部分谈到的岗位。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。Finn 的总结原话就是减少 crime correspondents、增加 design correspondents。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130131-6min-audio-q01: Wayne Hemingway的核心观点是新闻媒体应更多报道能推动人类进步、积极影响生活的好消息，而不是总聚焦坏消息，所以c最准确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20130131-6min-audio-q02: 他举的第二个例子明确是Izhar Gafni开发的recycled cardboard bicycle，可为贫穷且拥挤污染严重的城市提供廉价清洁交通，因此选b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20130131-6min-audio-q03: 这句话的意思是如果大众长期被灌输坏消息，他们的偏好也会被塑造成想看坏消息，因此a准确表达了这种含义。
reasonsZh.bbc-20130131-6min-audio-q04: Finn总结说，也许需要更少crime correspondents、更多design correspondents；虽然原文还提到science和war reporters，但题干问的是Finn总结中的一个人员变化，d与之直接一致。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: A café trend in which customers can pay for coffee for someone else
The reasons civet coffee is becoming more popular across Europe
How commuters can save money by making coffee at home
Why European cafés are replacing traditional community centers

evidence.quote:
we are going to talk about coffee and an interesting
new project which is going on in many European cafes.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接说明本期要谈咖啡，以及“许多欧洲咖啡馆正在进行的一个有趣新项目”。后文一直围绕 suspended coffee 的做法、对象和意义展开，所以主旨就是介绍这种替别人预付咖啡的咖啡馆趋势。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了整段节目：主题不是普通喝咖啡，而是介绍一种由顾客替他人预付咖啡的钱的做法。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目确实提到昂贵的 civet coffee，但那只是开头和结尾的竞猜，不是主体内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有讨论在家做咖啡来省钱，也没有把重点放在通勤者的消费建议上。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目谈到这种做法可能有社区作用，但并没有说咖啡馆正在取代社区中心，这个范围被夸大了。

public.prompt: According to the program, what is a suspended coffee?

options.text: A coffee delayed until the café becomes less busy
A free drink automatically given to every customer
Money given for a coffee that another person can drink later
A special kind of coffee sold only in European cafés

evidence.quote:
A suspended coffee is where you donate, or give, money for a coffee that
someone can drink later.

private.explanationZh: 节目中 Jennifer 直接下定义：suspended coffee 指的是你捐出一杯咖啡的钱，让别人在之后来喝。这不是暂缓供应，也不是所有人自动免费得到。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里的 suspended 不是“暂停供应”的意思，节目专门解释了它在这里指预付给别人喝的咖啡。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目说的是有人额外付款为别人买一杯，不是商家自动送给每位顾客。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它与原文定义完全对应：先付钱，之后由另一位有需要的人来喝。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。它不是某种咖啡品类，而是一种慈善式的付款方式。

public.prompt: Why does Jennifer introduce Hettie Clark's comments?

options.text: To argue that commuters spend too much money on coffee
To explain which kinds of people might receive a suspended coffee
To announce the answer to the quiz question
To show how cafés prepare different coffee drinks

evidence.quote:
to find out who
might ask for a suspended coffee.

private.explanationZh: Jennifer 在播放 Hettie Clark 的片段之前，明确说明目的就是“看看谁可能会来要一杯 suspended coffee”。因此这段引语的作用是引出受益人群，而不是谈价格、做法或竞猜答案。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里不是讨论通勤者花钱太多，而是转向说明这种咖啡会帮助哪些人。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。主持人点明了播放这段采访的目的，就是解释哪些处境中的人可能会来领取这种咖啡。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。竞猜答案在节目结尾才公布，这里与竞猜无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。Hettie 的片段也不是教大家制作咖啡，而是说明受助对象。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Jennifer's final opinion about suspended coffee?

options.text: The idea will work only in wealthy neighborhoods
The scheme depends mainly on expensive coffee sales
Cafés will probably stop charging regular customers
The idea could help bring people in a community closer together

evidence.quote:
I think it will be really good for
communities too, because it will bring people together.

private.explanationZh: Jennifer 最后的评价不是只说“这是个好主意”，而是进一步说明它对社区也有好处，因为它能把人们联系起来。由此可推断，她认为 suspended coffee 有促进社区凝聚力的作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有把这种做法限制在富裕地区，反而强调它可以帮助有需要的人。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说该项目依赖高价咖啡销售，这与 Jennifer 的结论无关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目从未暗示普通顾客会不再付钱，反而强调是一位顾客多付一杯的钱。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。她直接说这会“bring people together”，所以可以合理推断她看重其增强社区联系的效果。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130704-6min-suspended-coffee-audio: 节目开头就说要谈论欧洲很多咖啡馆里进行的一个新项目，后文持续解释“suspended coffee”是什么以及它如何帮助别人，主旨明显是顾客替他人预付咖啡的咖啡馆趋势。
Jennifer明确解释：suspended coffee就是你捐出一杯咖啡的钱，让别人之后来喝。这与选项c完全对应，其余选项都把它说成延后制作、自动赠送或特殊咖啡种类，均不符。
Jennifer在播放Hettie Clark片段前直接说，是为了弄清楚“谁可能来要一杯suspended coffee”。因此她引入这段话的目的就是说明哪些人会受益。
Jennifer最后评价这个想法“will be really good for communities too, because it will bring people together”，可推知她认为这会拉近社区成员关系。其余选项都没有文本支持。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: Why birdwatchers need official species lists
How birdsong is being featured and why it may benefit listeners
Why seagulls are replacing other British birds
How social media changed nature broadcasting

evidence.quote:
a BBC radio station in the UK is playing a different
birdsong every day.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头引出 BBC 的 “Tweet of the Day”，后面又继续谈不同鸟叫带来的联想，以及研究者认为鸟鸣可能帮助集中注意力。也就是说，节目主旨是介绍鸟鸣专题以及鸟鸣对听者可能产生的作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。官方鸟类名单只是竞猜内容，不是整期讨论的中心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它同时概括了节目两个核心部分：BBC 对鸟鸣的呈现，以及鸟鸣可能带来的心理和认知益处。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目举海鸥等例子只是说明不同声音带来的联想，不是在讨论哪类鸟取代别的鸟。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目开头借 tweet 开玩笑，但重点不是社交媒体如何改变自然广播。

public.prompt: According to the program, how many different birdsongs does Tweet of the Day feature?

evidence.quote:
Tweet of the Day features 265 different
birdsongs.

private.explanationZh: 节目中直接给出数字：BBC Radio 4 的 Tweet of the Day 收录了 265 种不同的鸟鸣。这是一个明确细节题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文明确说是 265 different birdsongs。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。396 是前面竞猜选项之一，不是节目中这个专题实际收录的鸟鸣数量。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。496 也是竞猜干扰项，并未被用来描述 Tweet of the Day。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。596 是英国官方鸟类名单中的物种数量，不是该节目专题收录的鸟鸣数。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about why researchers and broadcasters value birdsong?

options.text: They think birdsong matters only for identifying rare species
They want schools to stop students from sleeping after lunch
They believe recorded sounds are more useful than real birds
They see birdsong as both pleasant to hear and potentially helpful for concentration

evidence.quote:
some birdsongs can help you concentrate when
you're studying.

private.explanationZh: 节目先把鸟鸣描述为令人愉悦、能让人联想到季节和地点的声音，随后又引用专家和研究，说鸟鸣有助于集中注意力、让人更清醒。因此可以推断，人们重视鸟鸣，不只是因为它好听，也因为它可能有实际的认知帮助。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目提到 rare species 只是在竞猜答案部分，不是说明人们重视鸟鸣的主要原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目说午饭后播放鸟鸣让学生更警觉，但这只是实验结果，不代表目的只是防止睡觉。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目并没有说录音比真实鸟鸣更有用，Rob 甚至说开窗听外面的鸟就够了。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它综合了节目中的两个关键信息：鸟鸣令人愉悦，同时也可能帮助专注与思考。

public.prompt: What does Rob imply when he says he can just open the window instead of using an app?

options.text: The app probably contains inaccurate recordings
Studying works only if birds sing at sunrise
Natural birds outside his home already provide what the app promises
People should avoid using technology to learn about birds

evidence.quote:
all I need to do is open the
window and listen to the birds outside…

private.explanationZh: Rob 的意思不是批评应用程序本身，而是说他身边就有真实的鸟鸣，所以不需要额外借助手机应用。他把自然环境中的真实鸟鸣看成已经能够提供类似效果。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Rob 没有质疑应用里的录音是否准确。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里没有说只有日出时的鸟鸣才有作用，这把条件说得过窄了。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。根据他的表述，窗外真实存在的鸟鸣已经足以达到应用号称能达到的效果。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。Rob 只是说自己不需要这个 app，不等于主张所有人都该避免技术。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130711-6min-birdsong-for-web-13071: 节目先介绍BBC的Tweet of the Day每天播放不同鸟鸣，随后又讨论鸟鸣可能帮助专注、让人更警觉，所以主旨是鸟鸣如何被节目呈现，以及它为何可能对听众有益。
Jennifer明确说“Tweet of the Day features 265 different birdsongs”。因此正确答案是265。
节目说这些鸟鸣“may be enjoyable”且“could also be useful”，接着举出帮助学习专注、让人更清醒的研究，因此可推知研究者和广播人都看重它既好听又可能有认知益处。
Rob说甚至有手机应用播放鸟鸣帮助工作，但他“不需要app”，只要开窗听外面的鸟就行，意思是自然环境中的鸟鸣已经能提供这个app声称的效果。

public.prompt: What is the program mainly about in its discussion of restart parties?

options.text: Why mobile phones are designed to be fashionable
How repair manuals became popular again in Britain
Why restart parties encourage people to repair rather than discard gadgets
How European recycling laws force people to attend repair events

evidence.quote:
today we are
going to talk about 'restart parties' and looking at some of the words to do with
fixing and repairing.

private.explanationZh: 开头已经明确交代主题：节目要讨论 restart parties，以及和修理、维修有关的内容。后文一直围绕为什么人们不修东西、这些聚会做什么、以及它们希望减少浪费展开，所以主旨是鼓励修理而不是丢弃电子产品。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有把重点放在手机外观设计或时尚上。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。repair manuals 只是原因分析中的一部分，不是整期节目的主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确概括了节目对 restart parties 的介绍及其核心理念：修理、再用、减少浪费。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有提到欧洲法律强迫人们参加此类活动，这是无中生有。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers bring in Ugo Vallouri's comments?

options.text: To prove that restart parties are cheaper than repair shops
To show that Jen already knows how to fix her phone
To introduce a quiz about how many gadgets can be upgraded
To explain why many people no longer try to repair their gadgets

evidence.quote:
Let's hear from Ugo Vallouri who organised one of
these parties in London. Why does he think people don't try to repair their gadgets
anymore?

private.explanationZh: 在播放 Ugo 的话之前，主持人直接提出问题：为什么人们现在不再尝试修理自己的电子产品。这说明引入这段采访的目的，就是让 Ugo 给出原因解释。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。虽然修理可能省钱，但这不是此处播放采访的直接目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Jen 在前文恰恰说自己不太会修，这里不是为了证明她会修。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里与竞猜无关，也没有借 Ugo 引出新的竞猜。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。主持人的过渡语已经清楚表明，这段采访用于解释人们不修理电子产品的原因。

public.prompt: According to the program, what is one reason people often do not repair gadgets?

options.text: Spare parts are often unavailable or unreasonably expensive
Repair manuals are now easier to use than before
Most consumers dislike learning practical skills
Old gadgets cannot be repaired even by experts

evidence.quote:
He also says the small bits, or the spare parts, needed to fix
something are not available now – or cost a lot of money.

private.explanationZh: 节目转述 Ugo 的观点时指出，修理所需的小零件往往买不到，即使买得到，价格也很高。这是人们不愿修理而选择换新的一个直接原因。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它与节目中的转述完全一致：零件要么缺乏，要么价格离谱。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目说 repair manuals 变少了，不是更容易用了。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有把主要原因归结为消费者普遍讨厌学习技能。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目强调问题在于资料和零件，不是说专家也完全修不了旧设备。

public.prompt: Why does Rob emphasize how many phones are thrown away in Europe each year?

options.text: To show that restarting a gadget usually costs more than replacing it
To highlight why repair and recycling matter
To suggest Jen wants to become a repair technician
To argue that restart parties mainly help people buy better devices

evidence.quote:
that's a
lot of phones that could be repaired or just recycled

private.explanationZh: 节目结尾提到每年被丢弃的手机数量“非常多”，紧接着说这些手机本来可以被修理或回收。由此可推断，Rob 强调这个数字，是为了突出修理与回收在减少浪费方面的重要性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里没有比较修理和更换哪一个更贵。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。Rob 借这个巨大的数量说明，如果不修理和回收，就会造成大量浪费。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Jen 是否想当技术员与这句话没有关系。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。restart parties 的目标是减少浪费、帮助修理，不是帮助人们购买更好的新设备。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130718-6-min-restart-parties-for-w: 节目围绕“restart parties”展开，强调修理东西能省钱、减少浪费，并反对坏了就扔的disposable society，所以主旨是这些活动鼓励人们修理而不是丢弃电子产品。
Rob在引入Ugo Vallouri前直接问：他为什么认为人们现在不再尝试修理电子设备。可见引用这段评论的目的就是解释人们不修理的原因。
Ugo明确说维修手册越来越少，spare parts对消费者不可得，或者价格荒谬，迫使人们不去修而是升级买新。因此“零件缺货或太贵”是明确原因。
Rob先用欧洲每年丢掉多少手机做提问，最后又强调100 million phones could be repaired or recycled，明显是在突出为什么修理和回收很重要。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the program about the royal baby?

options.text: How British hospitals changed over time
Why text messages are better than Facebook for new parents
How royal quiz questions are written for radio audiences
How the program compares ordinary parenting with royal parenting

evidence.quote:
We’ll be discussing that soon and looking at some of the
language of having a baby.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头先从 royal baby 引入，但很快把重点放在“我们自己的育儿经验”和“王室父母需要的不同之处”之间的比较上。因此主旨不是单讲王室新闻，而是借此比较普通父母和王室父母的育儿情境。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。虽然提到 maternity unit，但节目并不是在讲英国医院历史。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。短信和 Facebook 只是举例说明现代父母如何报喜，不是节目重点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。竞猜只是节目固定结构之一，不是主题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。节目不断对比普通家庭与王室家庭在宣布出生、养育孩子等方面的相同与不同。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers include the old BBC broadcast about Prince Charles's birth?

options.text: To explain the medical risks of childbirth in the past
To show that radio announcements are less formal now than television news
To illustrate the traditional formality surrounding royal birth announcements
To prove that Prince Charles received more public attention than the new baby

evidence.quote:
when Prince Charles was born in 1948, the announcement was very formal.
Listen to the words that were used in this BBC broadcast

private.explanationZh: 播放 1948 年 BBC 播报的目的，是让听者直接感受到过去王室出生公告的正式、传统和讲究礼仪的风格，从而说明 royal birth announcements 的 protocol。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。广播内容并不是为了讨论过去分娩的医疗风险。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里比较的是王室宣布方式的正式程度，而不是“广播比电视不正式”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。主持人在引入时明确说 announcement was very formal，这就是这段材料的作用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有借此证明查尔斯受到的关注一定更多，重点在形式和礼仪。

public.prompt: What does the report about William and Kate suggest?

options.text: They plan to rely entirely on palace staff
They want to be more directly involved in raising their baby
They are worried that social media will damage their privacy
They believe modern parents no longer need experienced nannies

evidence.quote:
They don’t want the nanny to be a
replacement mother.

private.explanationZh: 如果他们“不想让保姆成为替代母亲”，就说明他们希望自己更多参与照顾和养育孩子，而不是把主要育儿责任都交给别人。这是从原句推断出的核心态度。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文方向相反：他们不想完全依赖保姆。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。拒绝让 nanny 成为 replacement mother，正说明他们想亲自更多参与育儿。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里没有谈到社交媒体隐私问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文并没有否定保姆的价值，只是否定“取代母亲”这种角色。

public.prompt: What point is Rob making when he says babies from all backgrounds need feeding and changing?

options.text: Some parts of caring for a baby are similar no matter what family the child is born into
Royal children usually receive less daily care than other children
Only future kings need constant feeding and changing
Ordinary parents should follow royal protocol more closely

evidence.quote:
But babies from all backgrounds need
feeding and changing.

private.explanationZh: Rob 这句话是在前面对比王室与普通家庭差异之后，强调一个共同点：不论出身如何，婴儿都需要最基本的照料。这是在指出育儿中有一些普遍而相同的方面。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确表达了 Rob 的结论：虽然身份不同，但照顾婴儿的某些基本任务是共通的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有说王室孩子得到的日常照料更少。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。恰恰相反，Rob 说的是“all backgrounds”，不是只有未来国王才这样。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里强调的是育儿的共性，不是要求普通父母遵守王室礼仪。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130725-6min-royal-baby-q01: Rob一开始就说要讨论自己的育儿技能和William、Kate需要的育儿方式有何不同，后面持续比较普通父母与王室父母的公告方式、保姆、养育参与度等，所以主旨是比较普通育儿与王室育儿。
reasonsZh.bbc-20130725-6min-royal-baby-q02: 在播放旧BBC播报前，Neil说当王室出生被宣布时，有很多protocol，即传统正式做法；随后播放1948年的正式播报，就是为了展示这种王室出生公告的传统正式性。
reasonsZh.bbc-20130725-6min-royal-baby-q03: Rob说据报道William和Kate想比过去的王室父母“become more involved in caring for their baby”，不想让保姆成为replacement mother，所以可推知他们想更直接参与养育。
reasonsZh.bbc-20130725-6min-royal-baby-q04: Rob先说新王室宝宝的人生会和他们的孩子很不同，但紧接着说任何背景的宝宝都需要喂养和换尿布，意思是无论出身如何，照顾婴儿的某些日常部分其实是相似的。

public.prompt: What issue is the program mainly exploring about anger?

options.text: Why research suggests modern life is making people angrier
How call centres train workers to calm customers
Why public transport causes more stress than any other problem
How anger management became popular in Britain

evidence.quote:
we are talking about anger – that's the strong
feeling you get when you feel someone has treated you badly or unfairly.

private.explanationZh: 开头先定义 anger，随后主持人明确说将讨论“为什么一些研究认为现代生活让我们更容易生气”。整期节目都围绕这一研究结论、原因和应对方式展开，所以主旨就是现代生活与愤怒之间的关系。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了整期节目的核心：现代生活如何与更强的愤怒感相关。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。call centre 只是结尾调查结果中的一个例子，不是主体内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。公共交通只是 Jennifer 个人举的烦恼例子，不是研究结论的唯一重点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目提到 anger management 只是最后的一个建议，不是主线。

public.prompt: According to Dr Sandi Mann, why does modern life make people angrier?

options.text: Because major life crises happen more often than before
Because fast-paced lives create ongoing stress and frustrations
Because people are physically stronger and react more aggressively
Because public transport has replaced other daily routines

evidence.quote:
Stress levels are that much higher these days. We've got so much more fast-paced life,
more going on – more stress, more ongoing frustrations rather than the big stresses.

private.explanationZh: Dr Mann 的解释很具体：如今生活节奏更快、事情更多，于是带来更多压力和持续性的挫折感。她强调不是只有“大压力”，而是大量 ongoing frustrations 在累积。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她说的重点不是重大危机更多，而是日常持续性的压力和挫折更多。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了她的原话：快节奏生活、更多事情、更多压力和持续性挫折。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。她没有把原因归结为身体更强或攻击性更强。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。公共交通只是个人烦恼例子，不是她概括现代生活的理论原因。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the discussion about raised expectations?

options.text: Technology has removed most reasons for anger in daily life
Adults usually react calmly when small plans go wrong
Higher expectations make people more upset by minor imperfections
Anger comes mainly from not understanding how Wi-Fi works

evidence.quote:
our
expectations have been raised. Now we expect things to be perfect.

private.explanationZh: 节目接着举 Wi‑Fi 不顺、火车晚两分钟等小事为例，说明当人们期待一切都完美时，即使只是小小的不如意，也更容易引发强烈恼怒。所以这里可推断“高期待”会放大小问题带来的情绪反应。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目恰恰在说现代技术和现代生活并没有消除愤怒，反而可能增加新的不满。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。主持人用“stamp our feet like children”说明很多成年人并不会总是冷静。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它概括了 raised expectations 的后果：对细小缺陷和延误更难容忍。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。Wi‑Fi 只是举例，不是说愤怒主要来自不懂技术。

public.prompt: According to the BBC survey mentioned in the program, what made British people most annoyed?

options.text: Someone jumping the queue
Delays on public transport
Rude service in shops
Being kept on hold by a call centre

evidence.quote:
being kept on hold by a call
centre, particularly for a long time, was the thing most likely to make
people angry.

private.explanationZh: 节目结尾公布调查答案时明确说明，最容易让英国人生气的是被客服电话长时间搁置等待。这是一个直接信息点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。插队是竞猜选项之一，但不是最终公布的答案。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。公共交通延误也在选项中出现过，但不是最让人生气的那一项。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Rob 个人讨厌店员无礼，但这不是调查中排名第一的来源。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。主持人公布答案时明确说，被 call centre 长时间 hold 住最可能让人发火。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130801-6-min-angry-for-web-130801-: 节目开头就说要讨论为什么一些研究认为现代生活让我们更愤怒，随后整段围绕快节奏、压力、挫折和过高期待展开，因此主旨就是研究为何认为现代生活使人更易发怒。
Dr Sandi Mann直接说现在压力更高，生活更快节奏，有更多事情、更多压力和持续性的frustrations，而不是重大压力事件。因此正确原因就是快节奏生活带来持续压力和挫折。
Rob解释说人们现在期待一切都完美，所以连Wi‑Fi不正常、火车晚两分钟这种小问题也会让人像孩子一样跺脚。可推知更高期待会让人对小瑕疵更生气。
节目最后核对答案时明确说，BBC调查发现最可能让人愤怒的是“being kept on hold by a call centre”，而且尤其是等待很久的时候。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the program?

options.text: To compare complaint statistics across industries in the UK.
To explain useful language and good practices for making and handling complaints.
To show why customers should write formal letters instead of calling.
To argue that transport companies receive the worst service complaints.

evidence.quote:
complain about something in English, it always helps to
know the right thing to say.

private.explanationZh: 节目核心是在讲英语中如何有效投诉，以及面对投诉时应采用什么样的表达和做法，不是在深入分析统计数据或主张某一种投诉渠道。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。节目开头提到零售和交通的投诉比例，只是引入话题，不是整期重点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。证据直接说节目会看投诉时的有用语言，且前文点出既包括提出投诉也包括处理不满顾客。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。原文没有主张书面投诉优于电话投诉，后面还大量用口头角色扮演示范。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。交通公司的例子只是闲聊导入，节目并没有论证它们收到最多或最严重的投诉。

public.prompt: Why does Business Betty introduce the example of the wrong T-shirts?

options.text: To prove that clothes suppliers usually make more mistakes than train companies.
To warn listeners not to order large numbers of products at one time.
To give a concrete scenario for showing how to state a complaint and request a solution.
To show that the customer should avoid being direct with a supplier.

evidence.quote:
Imagine you own a clothes shop and you’re unhappy about
some t-shirts you’ve just bought. You’re talking to the supplier about it.

private.explanationZh: Betty先设定一个具体商业场景，是为了把后面的投诉表达放进真实语境里，示范怎样清楚说明问题并提出解决要求。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。节目没有比较服装供应商和火车公司谁更容易出错。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。数量是场景细节，不是这段话要传达的主旨。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。这个衣服订单出错的情境正是后面练习投诉句型和解决方案请求的载体。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。Betty强调的是礼貌、冷静、清楚，不是否定直接说明问题。

public.prompt: What does Betty imply when she tells Neil to "take it seriously"?

options.text: He failed to treat the caller's problem as a real concern.
He focused too much on replacing the order quickly.
He should have asked for the customer's payment details first.
He was too formal for a customer service conversation.

evidence.quote:
When a customer has a
complaint, take it seriously! Find out what the problem is:

private.explanationZh: Betty这句话是在批评Neil第一次角色扮演时的态度。他随意敷衍顾客，所以Betty强调首先要认真对待投诉，再去弄清具体问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。证据明确要求先认真对待投诉，说明Neil之前没有把顾客的问题当回事。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。Betty批评的不是处理太快，而是不够认真、缺少基本服务步骤。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。文中没有提到付款信息，这不是Betty指出的缺失环节。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。Neil的问题恰恰不是太正式，而是太轻慢、太不礼貌。

public.prompt: Why does Feifei sound more convinced after the second role-play?

options.text: Neil promises to send more shirts than were originally ordered.
Neil explains that the train delays caused the mistake.
Neil refuses to speak by phone and asks for a written complaint.
Neil gives a specific solution and deadline for sending the correct shirts.

evidence.quote: Yes of course, I’ll get the correct order sent to you by the end of today.

private.explanationZh: 第二次角色扮演里，Neil不再敷衍，而是明确说明会把正确的货在当天结束前寄出。具体解决方案加上时间承诺，让Feifei觉得这次更可信。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。原文没有说会多送数量，只说会寄出正确的订单。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。火车延误是节目开头闲聊内容，与这次订单失误无关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。第二次角色扮演仍是电话沟通，也没有要求书面投诉。
optionRationalesZh.d: 对。关键在于他给出了清楚、可执行的补救方案，还附带明确时限。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130808-6min-customer-complaints-au: 节目一开始就说明要讨论complaining时的实用语言，以及提出投诉和处理投诉时该做与不该做的事，后面的示范和角色扮演也都围绕这个目的展开，所以主旨是讲如何提出和处理投诉。
Betty用错发T恤的情境来一步步示范怎样明确表示自己有投诉、怎样说明问题、以及怎样提出解决要求，所以这个例子是为了具体演示投诉和请求补救的说法。
Neil第一次角色扮演时对顾客说“it happens all the time”并很快结束通话，没有认真对待对方的问题。Betty随后说“take it seriously”，明显是在指出这一点。
第二次角色扮演里Neil不仅道歉和检查订单，还明确说会在“by the end of today”把正确的货送到，因此Feifei才觉得这次更可信。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the discussion?

options.text: The economic reasons some countries rank higher than others.
The health problems caused by trying to stay cheerful all the time.
A study about the factors and habits linked to happiness.
The differences between Finnish and Canadian lifestyles.

evidence.quote:
It' s
about a new study that tells us how we can make ourselves happier.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就说明主题是一项关于“怎样让自己更快乐”的新研究，后面整段讨论都围绕这项研究发现展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。国家排名只是节目中的提问环节，不是主体讨论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。节目没有把重点放在保持快乐带来的健康问题上。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。开头直接点明是关于让人更快乐的研究，后文也不断解释相关发现。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。芬兰和加拿大只是选择题选项，不是讨论重点。

public.prompt: According to the study, what matters more for happiness than material wealth?

options.text: Setting more ambitious long-term goals.
Leading an active and rewarding life.
Avoiding all negative emotions.
Spending more time on holidays and luxury goods.

evidence.quote:
the study found having an active life is most important for a
happy and rewarding life – that means a feeling of satisfaction and pleasure.

private.explanationZh: 研究在否定单纯物质追求之后，转而强调积极而有收获的生活方式更能带来幸福感，因此答案不是金钱、假期或彻底避免负面情绪。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。前文提到不快乐的人更在意人生目标，这并不表示更有野心就更快乐。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。证据直接说积极活跃的生活对幸福而有回报的人生最重要。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。节目末尾还说适度悲伤反而有益，因此不是要消灭所有负面情绪。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。节目明确说不是物质主义价值，不支持把假期和奢侈消费当核心答案。

public.prompt: According to Professor Veenhoven, why do elderly people tend to be happier?

options.text: They usually have more children at home.
They travel more than younger adults.
They care less about relationships.
They are wiser and deal with life better.

evidence.quote: Elderly people tend to be wise and for that reason, happier.

private.explanationZh: 教授的核心意思是：老年人因为更有智慧，所以通常更快乐。后面主持人的解释进一步补充说，他们更懂事，也更会处理生活中的问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。文中没有说老年人因为孩子在家更多而更幸福，反而后文提到孩子长大离家后幸福感会上升。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。节目没有把旅行频率作为老年人更快乐的原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。文中没有说他们更不在意关系，关系反而被视为提升幸福感的因素之一。
optionRationalesZh.d: 对。证据直接把“更有智慧”与“更快乐”联系起来。

public.prompt: Why does Rob mention that being sad for part of the time can be good for you?

options.text: To suggest that realistic emotional ups and downs are part of a healthy life.
To show that unhappy people should stop trying to improve their lives.
To argue that sadness is more useful than happiness.
To explain why children reduce happiness more than adults do.

evidence.quote:
being sad for ten per
cent of the time is actually good for you. Let's face it – we can't be happy all the
time!

private.explanationZh: Rob这里的作用是给Finn安慰，也总结研究的一点：幸福并不等于一直高兴，适度的低落是正常且有益的，情绪有起伏才更真实。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。后一句“我们不可能一直快乐”清楚说明他在强调正常的情绪波动。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。节目并没有让人放弃改善生活，而是在讨论哪些因素有助于幸福。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。这里不是说悲伤比快乐更有价值，只是说少量悲伤并非坏事。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。孩子与幸福感的关系在别处提到，但这句话的目的不是解释那个问题。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130815-6min-happiness-audio-q01: 整段讨论围绕一项关于幸福的研究展开，重点是哪些因素和生活方式与幸福有关，因此主话题是关于幸福因素和习惯的研究。
reasonsZh.bbc-20130815-6min-happiness-audio-q02: Rob先否定了物质因素的重要性，随后明确说研究发现“having an active life is most important for a happy and rewarding life”，所以比物质财富更重要的是积极而有满足感的生活。
reasonsZh.bbc-20130815-6min-happiness-audio-q03: Professor Veenhoven说老年人往往更快乐，因为他们更有智慧，也更会应对生活。Finn随后也复述了这一点，所以正确原因是他们更有智慧并且更会处理人生问题。
reasonsZh.bbc-20130815-6min-happiness-audio-q04: Rob提到“being sad for ten per cent of the time is actually good for you”，紧接着又说“we can't be happy all the time”，这里是在说明情绪有起伏是正常而健康的，不可能一直快乐。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: How the United Nations calculates national populations.
Why people move abroad for work and what effects that movement has.
Why the United States attracts more migrants than any other country.
How to apply for permanent citizenship in wealthy countries.

evidence.quote:
discussing global migration – that's the movement of people
around the world and particularly those who are moving abroad to look for new work.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接定义了本期讨论对象：全球迁移，尤其是为了找工作而去国外的人。后面内容也一直围绕动因、后果和相关政策展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。联合国数据只是节目提问的一部分，不是主要讨论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。节目重点就是劳动力跨国流动及其影响。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。节目结尾还指出卡塔尔而不是美国在移民占比上更高，这也不是主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。节目提到工作签证，但并不是一篇申请指南。

public.prompt: According to the speakers, why do many economic migrants move abroad?

options.text: Their families require them to study in another country.
Their home countries prevent them from taking any jobs.
Their skills may be more needed and better paid elsewhere.
They want to avoid sending money back home.

evidence.quote:
Their profession – or area of work they specialise in – may not be needed
where they live. Or the pay may not be very good but their skills might be in demand
in another part of the world, so they are forced to move there to get a job and earn
more money.

private.explanationZh: 这段话直接给出经济移民外出的原因：本地不需要他们的技能，或者待遇太低，而别处更需要他们、工资也更好。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。这里讲的是找工作，不是出国学习。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。原文并没有说母国完全禁止他们工作，而是说技能不被需要或收入不高。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。证据完整说明了“需求更大、收入更高”这一组合原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。后文恰恰说很多人会把钱寄回家，说明这不是他们外出的原因。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers mention jobs such as chefs and psychologists?

options.text: To argue that only highly educated migrants should travel abroad.
To show that healthcare jobs are becoming less important worldwide.
To suggest that migrants usually change careers after moving.
To show that different countries need different kinds of workers.

evidence.quote:
there is a need for chefs. And in countries like Norway, there is a
demand for psychologists.

private.explanationZh: 列举厨师、心理学家等职业，是为了把“各国劳动力需求不同”具体化，让听众理解迁移流向与岗位需求之间的关系。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。节目提到的职业层次很多，并不是在主张只有高学历者才该迁移。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。这里没有说医疗工作不重要，只是补充了其他行业的需求例子。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。例子强调的是哪里需要什么技能，不是说人们到了国外通常会改行。
optionRationalesZh.d: 对。不同国家需要不同职业，这正是这些例子的说明作用。

public.prompt: What do the speakers suggest at the end about working abroad?

options.text: It can also bring useful experience back to a migrant's home country.
It usually ends with migrants becoming citizens of richer countries.
It matters only when a country uses a points-based visa system.
It benefits migrants only if they become accountants or engineers.

evidence.quote:
some work experience that will benefit them when they get home: a sort of brain
gain!

private.explanationZh: 结尾把前面的“人才流失”稍微反转了一下：即使没有一夜致富，海外工作经历也可能在回国后发挥价值，因此迁移不一定只有负面结果。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。brain gain 明确表示经验和技能可能回流，惠及本人和母国。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。文中没有说多数人最终会取得更富国家的国籍。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。签证制度只是一个政策工具，不是结尾推论的核心。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。会计师和工程师只是前面的职业例子，不是收益成立的唯一条件。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130822-6min-global-migration-audio: 节目明确说要讨论global migration，尤其是人们为了找工作而移居国外，并继续谈到这种流动的原因及其对输出国和输入国的影响，所以主旨是人们为何为工作迁移以及这种迁移带来的影响。
Jennifer解释economic migrants时说，他们的职业在本地可能不被需要，或者报酬不高，但他们的技能在别的地方可能有需求、工资更好，所以许多人因此出国工作。
提到chefs、psychologists以及后面的accountants、engineers时，语境是在说明不同国家需要不同类型的劳动力，因此这些例子是为了展示各国对工种的需求并不相同。
结尾说即使国外并不一定容易致富，移民至少还能获得工作经验，回国后会从中受益，并把这称作一种“brain gain”，所以暗示出国工作也可能把有用经验带回本国。

public.prompt: Why does the speaker mention the manager's role in Indonesia?

options.text: To illustrate how arranging a meeting can become difficult when many schedules must be checked.
To show that managers in Indonesia prefer phone meetings to face-to-face meetings.
To argue that only managers should be allowed to call meetings.
To explain why meetings in Indonesia are usually informal.

evidence.quote:
In Indonesia the manager would normally arrange a meeting, he or
she will have to check if everyone can attend. It’s quite tricky to set
up a time that suits everyone due to different schedules, so
sometimes not everyone can attend.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到印尼经理安排会议的方式，不是为了介绍一个国家常识，而是为了说明：一旦要协调很多人的时间，安排会议本身就会变得棘手。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。证据把“要检查大家能否参加”和“时间难协调”直接联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。原文没有说印尼更偏好电话会议。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。说经理通常负责安排，不等于论证只有经理才能安排。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。这里强调的是排时间的难度，不是会议是否正式。

public.prompt: What is the main goal of the program?

options.text: To compare business news stories from different countries.
To show why meetings should usually be canceled.
To teach language for setting a meeting time and agreeing how to meet.
To persuade workers to avoid phone calls at work.

evidence.quote:
arranging meetings effectively is a really
important business skill.

private.explanationZh: 节目把“有效安排会议”明确为重要商务技能，后面通过角色扮演和点评具体示范如何约时间、改时间以及决定会议形式。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。国际例子只是引入，不是新闻比较节目。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。节目教的是如何安排，不是如何取消。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。整期节目的核心就是安排会议时会用到的表达与决策。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。节目里甚至把电话会议当作一种可行方案，并非反对打电话。

public.prompt: According to Business Betty, what did Neil and Feifei do well besides agreeing on a time?

options.text: They invited a manager to lead the conversation.
They decided what kind of meeting to have.
They apologized for calling at a busy time.
They moved the meeting to a different day.

evidence.quote:
you both used simple, direct questions to agree
the time. That was very good. And, as well as agreeing the time, I
liked that you discussed and agreed the type of meeting.

private.explanationZh: Betty的表扬有两层：一是他们用直接问题把时间谈妥，二是他们还讨论并确定了会议形式，所以“决定会议类型”是额外做好的一点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。角色扮演里没有邀请经理来主持。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。证据明确说除了时间，他们还就 meeting type 达成了一致。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。这里被表扬的不是道歉，而是时间安排和会议形式。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。时间从下午改到第二天下午两点，但Betty此处强调的附加优点不是改日期。

public.prompt: Why do Neil and Feifei decide to have the meeting on the phone?

options.text: Neither of them is available until the following week.
They want to include more colleagues from other countries.
They think phone meetings are always better than in-person meetings.
A face-to-face meeting is not necessary for this discussion.

evidence.quote:
Great! Do we need to be face to face for this one?
Neil: No, I don’t think so. Let’s do this one on the phone.

private.explanationZh: 两人之所以改成电话会议，不是因为永远偏好电话，而是判断这次事情不需要面对面处理，因此电话就足够。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。文中只是改到第二天下午两点，不是拖到下周。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。没有提到要加入更多国际同事。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。结论只针对 this one，并不是概括所有会议都更适合电话。
optionRationalesZh.d: 对。Neil直接回答不需要 face to face，因此选择电话。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130829-6min-arranging-meetings-aud: 印尼的发言者说经理通常要先检查大家能否参加，而由于每个人日程不同，很难找到适合所有人的时间，有时也不能全员到齐，所以这里提到经理是为了说明安排多人会议会很麻烦。
节目主题就是arranging meetings，后面的角色扮演和Betty点评都在教人如何提出时间、确认时间，以及确定会议形式，因此主目标是教授安排会议时间和方式的语言。
Betty明确说除了agreeing the time之外，她还喜欢他们“discussed and agreed the type of meeting”，也就是他们决定了这次会议是电话进行还是面对面进行。
Feifei问“Do we need to be face to face for this one?”，Neil回答“I don't think so. Let's do this one on the phone.”，说明他们改成电话会议是因为这次没有必要面对面。

public.prompt: What problem does Neil point out before Business Betty appears?

options.text: The office has too many private rooms for staff to use.
Feifei changes the air conditioning without politely asking first.
Open-plan offices make it impossible to communicate ideas.
Workers should never adjust the temperature at work.

evidence.quote:
you drive me crazy
because you never ask me if you can turn it down. You just do
it without saying anything. It really annoys me. It's so rude!!

private.explanationZh: Neil抱怨的重点不是温度本身，而是Feifei没有先礼貌地询问就直接去调空调，这正好引出后面关于礼貌请求的教学。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。对话没有抱怨办公室私密空间太多。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。证据明确指出问题在于“从不先问能不能调低”，即缺少礼貌请求。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。前面街采中有人说开放式办公室便于沟通，所以这不是Neil此处的意思。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。Neil并没有说绝不能调温度，而是说应该先开口礼貌地问。

public.prompt: According to Business Betty, which structure is a good way to make a polite request?

options.text: Would you mind + verb-ing
Is it ok if I + base verb
I'd rather you didn't + verb-ing
Please + infinitive only

evidence.quote:
If you want to
ask someone to do something, it's always a good idea to use a
very polite request. Start with the phrase 'Would you mind…'
and follow it with an -ing verb.

private.explanationZh: Betty给出的明确公式是：Would you mind 后面接 -ing 形式，用来礼貌地请别人做某事。其他结构在节目里有别的用途，但不是这一问所求。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。证据逐步给出了这一完整句型。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。Is it ok if I... 是请求许可，不是请别人做事的礼貌请求结构。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。I'd rather you didn't... 用于委婉拒绝对方，不是提出请求的基本结构。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。单独加 please 并不是Betty这里教授的核心句型公式。

public.prompt: What does the program mainly teach about workplace politeness?

options.text: Why open-plan offices should be replaced by private offices.
How to control office temperature more efficiently.
Why co-workers should avoid sharing equipment and meeting rooms.
Polite ways to make requests and ask permission at work.

evidence.quote:
If you're working in
an open-plan office, it's really important to consider the needs
and feelings of the people working around you.
If someone asks your permission, you can say yes.

private.explanationZh: 整期节目围绕共享办公环境中的礼貌表达展开：既包括请别人做事，也包括征求许可和回应许可，所以主旨是“礼貌请求与许可表达”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。节目没有主张用私人办公室取代开放式办公室。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。空调只是引子，不是整期节目的核心。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错。节目教的是如何礼貌协调共享资源，而不是避免共享。
optionRationalesZh.d: 对。两处证据分别对应共享办公室语境和 permission 语言，合起来概括全节目。

public.prompt: Why does Feifei reject Neil's plan to tidy up in the evening?

options.text: She wants Neil to ask Business Betty first.
Visitors are arriving from another office in the evening.
The room will be needed again before then.
Neil has already promised to clean a different room.

evidence.quote:
We need to use the
room again at 4pm.

private.explanationZh: Feifei不同意等到晚上再整理，是因为会议室下午四点还要再用。也就是说，问题在于时间安排不允许拖到晚上。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错。这里与向Betty请示无关。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错。文中没有说晚间会有别的办公室访客到来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。证据直接说明房间四点还要使用，所以必须更早收拾。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错。Neil没有提到自己已经答应清理另一间房。

reasonsZh.bbc-20130905-6min-asking-permission-audi: 在Betty出现前，Neil抱怨Feifei总是直接调空调，“you never ask me if you can turn it down”，所以他指出的问题是Feifei没有先礼貌地征求同意。
Betty直接说如果想请别人做事，要用“Would you mind...”并且后接-ing形式，还让两人给出例句，因此正确结构就是Would you mind + verb-ing。
整期节目都在教开放式办公室中的礼貌表达，重点区分礼貌请求和征求许可两类说法，所以主旨是如何在工作场景中礼貌地提出请求和请求许可。
Neil说他要到晚上再整理，Feifei立刻反对并解释“We need to use the room again at 4pm.”，所以她拒绝的原因是房间在那之前还要再用。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion?

options.text: How American lotteries set record cash prizes
Why retired lecturers often become media celebrities
Why some people devote themselves to entering competitions and how comping has changed
How advertising slogans are judged in professional contests

evidence.quote: talk more about 'compers' – people who regularly take part in competitions.

private.explanationZh: 节目先点明要谈的是“compers”，也就是经常参加比赛的人，后面又继续讨论他们为何着迷，以及网络时代如何改变这种活动，所以主旨是介绍这类人及其参赛方式的变化。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目中的彩票金额只是开场提问，不是整段讨论的核心内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: Martin Dove确实出名，但节目不是在解释退休讲师如何成为媒体名人。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这个选项同时涵盖了“compers”的定义和后文关于参赛方式变化的讨论，最符合全文。
optionRationalesZh.d: 写标语只是参赛形式之一，范围太窄，不能概括整段节目。

public.prompt: According to the discussion, which prize has Martin Dove won?

options.text: A scholarship to study advertising
A television studio tour
A year's supply of breakfast cereal
A yacht

evidence.quote: He's won a yacht, a racehorse and lots of smaller prizes too.

private.explanationZh: 原文直接列举Martin Dove赢过“a yacht, a racehorse and lots of smaller prizes too”，因此游艇是明确提到的奖品。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有提到奖学金，这属于无中生有。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有说他赢过参观电视台之类的体验奖。
optionRationalesZh.c: 虽然提到谷物盒上的比赛，但没有说他赢过全年麦片。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文明确点出他赢过游艇，所以这是正确答案。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about online competitions compared with competitions in the past?

options.text: They demand more creative work but attract fewer entrants
They are faster to enter, so each person may face lower odds of winning
They are mainly limited to expert compers who publish advice books
They usually offer smaller prizes, so fewer people try them

evidence.quote: The thing is now competitions are far easier to enter than they ever were.

And you can get
a million entries now, so it's a million to one.

private.explanationZh: Martin Dove说现在比赛“far easier to enter”，几乎只要不停点击；但也因为太容易，参赛人数会暴增到“a million entries”，所以可以推断单个参赛者中奖几率反而更低。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文强调的是进入更容易、更快，不是更需要创意，而且参赛者更多而不是更少。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这正是两处证据合起来表达的意思：进入更快，但竞争更激烈，个人胜算下降。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有说网络比赛只对专家开放，反而暗示任何人都能快速参加。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中并未说网络奖品更小，降低胜率的原因是报名人数暴增。

public.prompt: What was the biggest cash lottery prize mentioned in the program?

options.text: $890 million
$1 billion
$590 million
$10 million

evidence.quote:
It's actually only $590 million; that was won by an 84-
year-old woman in Florida last year.

private.explanationZh: 节目最后纠正Neil时明确说美国史上最高彩票奖金“actually only $590 million”，所以答案是5.9亿美元。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是主持人给出的干扰选项之一，最后被否定。
optionRationalesZh.b: Neil选择了这个答案，但主持人随后说明他答错了。
optionRationalesZh.c: 结尾明确公布正确数字是$590 million。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目只提到Neil曾中过10英镑，这不是美国最高彩票奖金。

reasonsZh.bbc-20140313-6min-compers-audio-q01: 节目核心一直在解释什么是comper、他们为什么沉迷参赛，以及网络时代让comping发生了什么变化。其他选项都只是局部细节。
reasonsZh.bbc-20140313-6min-compers-audio-q02: 原文明确说Martin Dove“has won a yacht, a racehorse and lots of smaller prizes too”，因此答案是游艇。
reasonsZh.bbc-20140313-6min-compers-audio-q03: Martin Dove说现在网上比赛只要不断点击，进入更快，但也因此会有更多人参加，中奖概率更低，所以应选“更快进入、个人胜算更低”。
reasonsZh.bbc-20140313-6min-compers-audio-q04: 节目最后核对问题时明确说Neil答错了，真正的最高奖金是“$590 million”。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: Why air pollution in Chinese cities is serious and what authorities are doing about it
Why fog is more dangerous than smoke in European capitals
How the WHO measures only road traffic in Iran
Why car ownership in Shanghai is falling quickly

evidence.quote:
In this programme we're talking about a serious problem that has been
affecting some of the big cities in China: that's air pollution.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接说明本期讨论的是中国一些大城市面临的严重空气污染问题，后文又进一步谈到成因和治理措施，所以A最能概括全文。

optionRationalesZh.a: 它既概括了污染问题本身，也包含后文谈到的治理方案，符合整段内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目提到伦敦只是比较案例，不是在专门比较雾和烟哪一个更危险。
optionRationalesZh.c: 伊朗只是竞猜答案的一部分，并非节目主体。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文说的是更多人开车，加剧污染，不是汽车数量迅速下降。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers mention London and Los Angeles?

options.text: To argue that those cities still have the worst air in the world
To show that weather is more important than human activity in every case
To compare train fares and fuel prices across countries
To show that other cities also had severe smog problems and later improved through laws and technology

evidence.quote:
smog used to be a problem in other world cities like London, where
a combination of fog and smoke from factories and trains sometimes made
the air quite dirty and dangerous.

But now, new laws and a change in technology mean the air in these
cities is cleaner and safer.

private.explanationZh: 节目先说伦敦和洛杉矶过去也有严重空气污染，接着又说这些城市后来因为新法律和技术变化而变得更安全。这个对比的作用，是说明中国并不是唯一案例，而且治理是可能的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文恰恰说这些城市“现在更干净更安全”，不是仍然最差。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目同时强调工厂、火车、汽车尾气等人为因素，不是说天气总是最重要。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有讨论票价或燃料价格。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这正是两个城市例子的功能：提供历史比较，并引出中国也可以通过政策和技术改善。

public.prompt: According to David Shukman, what are Beijing officials mainly trying to do?

options.text: Build more coal power stations and widen the roads
Close coal-burning power stations in the city and limit road traffic
Replace all buses with bicycles immediately
Move factories from Beijing to Shanghai

evidence.quote:
they're closing down any power stations within the city that burn coal, that's the
biggest source of pollution; next they're trying to limit road traffic – greener cars will get
priority.

private.explanationZh: David Shukman明确说两项主要措施是关闭市内燃煤电站，以及限制道路交通并让更环保的汽车优先，因此B准确复述了原文。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文说的是关闭燃煤电站，不是新建。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这与原文两项措施完全对应，是正确答案。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有提到立刻把公交车全部换成自行车。
optionRationalesZh.d: 也没有提到把工厂整体迁往上海。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about renewable energy from the discussion?

options.text: It is presented as a quick fix that will solve pollution immediately
It mainly helps make cars look environmentally friendly
It could reduce air pollution by decreasing the need to burn coal
It works only in cities that already have clean air

evidence.quote:
China is also going to use more renewable energy – this is power made by
natural materials that never get used up, such as wind and water. This would
reduce air pollution caused by burning coal.

private.explanationZh: 主持人解释renewable energy来自不会用尽的自然资源，并紧接着说这会减少燃煤造成的空气污染，所以可以推断它的价值在于减少对煤炭燃烧的依赖。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目还提醒这些改变需要很长时间，因此不是“立刻解决”的快速方案。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这里谈的是能源来源，不是汽车外观或宣传。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这正是文段明确支持的推断：少烧煤，就能少一些相关空气污染。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文没有说它只适用于已经空气洁净的城市。

reasonsZh.bbc-20140320-6min-male-female-brain-audi: 节目主题是中国城市空气污染为何严重，以及政府正在采取哪些治理措施。其余选项都缩窄或歪曲了讨论重点。
提到伦敦和洛杉矶，是为了说明别的城市过去也有严重烟雾问题，但后来通过法律和技术改善了空气质量，形成对照。
David Shukman明确说两项主要做法是关闭市内燃煤电站，以及限制道路交通，绿色汽车优先。
节目说更多使用renewable energy会减少燃煤，从而降低空气污染，因此可推断其作用是减少对煤炭燃烧的依赖。

public.prompt: What is the program mainly about?

options.text: Why secrecy is necessary in modern democracies
Different meanings of freedom and ways it can be limited or defended
How prison gardens help hostages recover emotionally
Why New Zealand ranks media systems better than other countries

evidence.quote: In today's programme, we're talking about freedom.

private.explanationZh: 节目开头就点出主题是freedom，后面又从政治自由、平等、监控、人质经历和社会抗争等角度展开，因此主旨是讨论自由的不同含义，以及它如何被限制或争取。
