public.prompt: What does Liz Brewer imply about the role of small talk in conversation?

options.text: It should remain shallow throughout the entire interaction.
It works best when people discuss private information immediately.
It is mainly a way to test whether people remember each other's names.
It serves as a first step that can lead to more meaningful conversation later.

evidence.quote:
small talk is a code towards getting information and
going further.
Once you feel comfortable, then you can have these wonderful in-depth conversations.

private.explanationZh: Liz Brewer把small talk看作通向更深入交流的前置阶段。先用安全、轻松的话题建立联系，等双方都更自在后，再进入真正有内容的谈话。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文明确说small talk之后可以进入更深入的交流。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她前面强调不要一上来就谈得太私人、太深入。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。记名字的问题只是一个具体社交技巧，不是small talk的主要作用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确概括了从small talk到in-depth conversations的递进关系。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150129-6min-150129-small-talk-q01: 节目一开始就说明主题是如何和陌生人开始交谈，也就是small talk，并在后面讲如何从安全话题逐步走向更深入交流。其他选项都只是细节。
reasonsZh.bbc-20150129-6min-150129-small-talk-q02: Liz Brewer说天气话题“easy”“not too penetrating”“safe ground”，其作用是breaking the ice，所以答案是安全、不太私人的开场话题。
reasonsZh.bbc-20150129-6min-150129-small-talk-q03: 她建议说名字“just slipped my mind for the moment”，随后主持人解释这是更温和的说法，能再次询问名字而不显得直接失礼。
reasonsZh.bbc-20150129-6min-150129-small-talk-q04: Liz Brewer明确说small talk是“a code towards getting information and going further”，等感觉舒服后再进入深入对话，所以它是通往更有内容交流的第一步。

public.prompt: What central issue about job applications does the discussion mainly examine?

options.text: How jobseekers can present themselves effectively in CVs and interviews.
Why online videos are replacing traditional job applications everywhere.
Why employers should stop using interviews to judge candidates.
How to measure job performance after someone is hired.

evidence.quote: Today we're talking about CVs

jobseekers have to worry about having an impressive CV so they get that call for a
job interview.

private.explanationZh: 节目从CV切入，但讨论并不止于写简历，还包括如何在面试前后有效展示自己。因此主线是求职者如何把能力、优势和可信度传达给雇主。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时涵盖了CV和面试两个层面的自我呈现。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。视频案例只是个别做法，节目并未说它正在全面取代传统求职方式。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目在教人如何应对面试，不是在反对面试制度。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讨论重点是求职阶段，而不是录用后的工作评估。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers mention Daniel Conway's unusual campaign?

options.text: To show that employers prefer social media videos to CVs.
To provide an example of someone trying to stand out and communicate his strengths.
To argue that jobseekers should always involve their families in applications.
To criticize young applicants for being naive and unprepared.

evidence.quote:
posing shirtless in the street with
the phrase "employ me" written on his chest
you've got to stand out and get your strengths across.

private.explanationZh: Daniel Conway的故事不是为了猎奇，而是把抽象建议具体化：求职者必须让自己被看见，并把自己的优势清楚传达出去。这个案例正好示范了“stand out”和“get your strengths across”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有得出“视频优于CV”的结论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个案例的作用就是为节目核心建议提供具体例证。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。女儿出现在视频中只是细节，不是普遍建议。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。虽然他说自己当年naive，但节目重点不是批评他。

public.prompt: Why does Corinne Mills tell jobseekers not to be modest?

options.text: Because modest people usually invent achievements on their CVs.
Because confidence matters only after a candidate has already been hired.
Because employers are less likely to trust candidates who seem unsure of their own abilities.
Because hiring managers prefer long, highly detailed applications.

evidence.quote:
if you are not confident about your skills and abilities, then
why should an employer have faith in you?
don't be modest.

private.explanationZh: Corinne Mills的逻辑很直接：如果求职者自己都表现得不够确信，雇主就更难相信这个人真的有能力胜任工作。所以这里反对“过度谦虚”，本质上是在强调求职语境中的有效自我呈现。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有把谦虚和虚构经历联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她谈的是求职展示阶段，不是入职之后。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确复述了自信与雇主信任之间的关系。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里讨论的是态度和表达，不是简历长度。

public.prompt: Which advice best matches the presenters' final recommendations for writing a CV?

options.text: Make it as long as possible and focus on personal hobbies.
Use vague language so employers will ask for more details later.
Ignore spelling and grammar if the experience section is strong.
Keep it brief, support your claims with evidence, and be truthful.

evidence.quote: you have to be concise

you should give evidence of success in
previous jobs.
be accurate, in other words, be precise and tell the truth. Don't invent things.

private.explanationZh: 节目结尾给出的建议是组合式的：内容要简洁，成绩要有证据支持，而且信息必须真实准确。这几条合在一起构成一个有说服力的CV标准。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目明确建议简洁，而不是越长越好。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目强调要把优势说清楚，而不是故意含糊。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Neil还特别提醒要检查拼写和语法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它完整整合了concise、evidence和accurate/tell the truth三点。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150205-6min-150205-jobseekers-q01: 整段讨论的是求职者如何通过CV和面试有效展示自己，包括脱颖而出、自信、简洁、证据和真实性，不是讨论录用后的表现或全面替代传统申请。
reasonsZh.bbc-20150205-6min-150205-jobseekers-q02: Daniel Conway的例子被用来说明求职者必须“stand out”并“get your strengths across”，也就是想办法被注意到并把自己的优势表达清楚。
reasonsZh.bbc-20150205-6min-150205-jobseekers-q03: Corinne Mills说如果你自己都不自信，雇主为什么要对你有信心，因此不要过分谦虚。核心意思就是显得不确定会削弱雇主对你的信任。
reasonsZh.bbc-20150205-6min-150205-jobseekers-q04: 结尾建议很明确：CV要concise，给出过往成功的证据，要accurate并说真话，还要检查拼写和语法。所以最符合的是简洁、有证据、真实。

public.prompt: What overall problem about wealth and poverty does the program mainly address?

options.text: How inequality can persist in a richer world and what can be done to reduce it.
Why global wealth statistics are too confusing to be useful.
Why the richest people should avoid government taxes.
How anti-poverty charities compare the living standards of every country.

evidence.quote: The world is getting richer, Harry.

there's still a lot of inequality in different
countries.
the gap between rich and poor

private.explanationZh: 节目先指出全球整体变得更富有，但随即强调很多国家内部仍然存在严重不平等，后文则继续讨论如何缩小贫富差距。因此主题并不是单纯谈财富增长，而是谈“富裕世界中的贫困与不平等”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了节目提出的问题和后续展开的方向。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有质疑统计无用，而是借统计引出不平等现象。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。后文恰恰谈到富人应纳税并承担社会责任。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目不是在介绍比较各国生活水平的方法。

public.prompt: Why does David Bryer mention Brazil?

options.text: To show that only large countries can afford a higher minimum wage.
To give a concrete example of a country using policies to reduce inequality.
To argue that public services matter less than taxation.
To prove that Brazil has already eliminated the gap between rich and poor.

evidence.quote:
Brazil has historically very high levels of economic
inequality.

having more progressive tax, around investing in a higher minimum wage, investing in
central public services

private.explanationZh: Bryer提到Brazil，是为了说明不平等并非无解。这个国家被用作实例，展示政府可以通过更累进的税制、更高的最低工资和公共服务投资来缩小贫富差距。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说这些措施只有大国才能承担。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它点出了Brazil在论证中的示范作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Bryer是并列举出多种措施，并未比较孰轻孰重。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文说Brazil在努力减少差距，不是已经完全消除。

public.prompt: According to the speakers, why can raising the minimum wage help reduce poverty?

options.text: It mainly encourages rich people to donate instead of paying taxes.
It removes the need for public services and government action.
It can improve poor families' living conditions and also support economic growth and job creation.
It guarantees that all citizens will eventually own equal wealth.

evidence.quote: They can be lifted out of poverty.

More people with more money buy more things and factories
produce more.

So, more jobs for
all.

private.explanationZh: 节目给出的是一条完整因果链：更高的最低工资先改善贫困者生活，再提高消费能力，进而刺激生产与就业。因此它既有社会层面的减贫作用，也有经济层面的带动作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里谈的是劳动报酬，不是鼓励富人用捐赠替代纳税。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目把最低工资与税制、公共服务并列为政策工具，并未说它能取代其他措施。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它完整概括了减贫、消费增加和就业增长三层关系。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。提高最低工资可以缓解贫困，但不能保证财富完全平均。

public.prompt: What does Bill Gates say wealthy people are obligated to do?

options.text: Leave economic policy entirely to governments and avoid direct giving.
Invest only in businesses within their own country.
Wait until they retire before taking any action on poverty.
Pay their taxes and use their wealth philanthropically to help poor people.

evidence.quote: their obligation of course is to pay their taxes

look at taking their wealth and being philanthropic - both in their own country and to
help the global poorest.

private.explanationZh: Bill Gates提出的是双重责任：富人首先应当纳税，其次应主动用财富帮助贫困者，而且帮助对象既可以在本国，也可以是全球最贫困的人群。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有让富人只把责任交给政府，相反还强调philanthropy。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。他明确说既包括本国，也包括全球最贫困者，不限于本国投资。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说必须等退休后才采取行动。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它保留了纳税和慈善两个关键要求。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150212-6min-150212-english-antarct: 节目主旨是世界总体变富，但贫富差距仍然存在，并讨论如何通过政策和个人行动减少不平等。其他选项都缩窄或歪曲了主题。
David Bryer提到巴西，是把它当作一个已经采取渐进税制、更高最低工资和公共服务投资等措施来缩小贫富差距的具体案例。
主持人解释更高最低工资能让穷人吃得更好、受更好教育、子女未来就业更好；同时更多人有钱消费会带动工厂生产和就业，因此既改善生活也促进经济与就业。
Bill Gates原话说富人的义务首先是纳税，同时还应拿财富去做慈善，帮助本国和全球最贫穷的人，所以d完整对应原意。

public.prompt: What central question about artificial intelligence does the discussion explore?

options.text: The promise of artificial intelligence and the danger of systems that pursue goals without human concern.
Why science-fiction films usually predict future technology accurately.
How chess computers became more entertaining than human players.
Why synthetic voices are the most important form of artificial intelligence.

evidence.quote:
good things can come from the creation of Artificial
Intelligence.

But some experts say the risk posed by Artificial Intelligence is not
that computers attack us because they hate us.

they are
indifferent to humans and that in pursuit of their own goals we humans would suffer as a side
effect.

private.explanationZh: 节目并没有把人工智能简单描绘成全好或全坏。一方面主持人承认AI可能带来积极结果，另一方面专家又强调真正风险可能来自它冷漠而高效地追求目标，这两部分共同构成整段讨论的核心。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时包含了节目中的潜在收益和核心风险判断。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。电影例子只是引入话题，不是主要结论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Deep Blue只是背景例子和测验材料。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。synthetic voice只是介绍Hawking时的细节，不是讨论重点。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers mention science-fiction movies such as 2001: A Space Odyssey and The Terminator?

options.text: To prove that films are a reliable guide to the future of robotics.
To introduce a familiar image of hostile machines before contrasting it with a different expert warning.
To show that androids are more dangerous than non-human-looking computers.
To explain why Stephen Hawking became interested in artificial intelligence.

evidence.quote: Many science fiction movies have explored the idea of bad computers who want to harm us.

But some experts say the risk posed by Artificial Intelligence is not
that computers attack us because they hate us.

private.explanationZh: 电影例子先调动听众熟悉的“邪恶机器”想象，然后节目立刻转向专家观点，强调真正风险未必来自仇恨式攻击，而可能来自目标导向的冷漠效率。因此这些电影例子主要承担对比和转折作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目并没有说电影能够可靠预测现实科技。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确说明了节目如何从大众印象转入更复杂的专家分析。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有比较不同类型AI谁更危险。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。提到电影不是为了讲Hawking的个人兴趣。

public.prompt: According to Nick Bostrom, why could an advanced AI harm humans?

options.text: Because any intelligent machine will eventually start hating people emotionally.
Because chess-playing computers usually become dangerous after they win.
Because humans might be damaged as a side effect of the AI's efficient pursuit of its own goal.
Because humans would ask the AI to turn them into paperclips.

evidence.quote:
they are
indifferent to humans and that in pursuit of their own goals we humans would suffer as a side
effect.
you will pursue your objective at any cost

private.explanationZh: Bostrom的核心论点是“副作用风险”。AI不需要恨人类，只要它对人类漠不关心、又极其高效地追求自己的目标，就可能在实现目标过程中把人类当成可牺牲的附带损失。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Bostrom明确说风险不在于机器会恨人类。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。国际象棋案例只是能力示例，不是风险成因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确抓住了indifferent和side effect两个核心概念。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。paperclip例子是说明目标优化失控，不是说人类主动提出这个要求。

public.prompt: After the paperclip example, what concern does Neil raise about artificial intelligence?

options.text: Whether synthetic voices will replace human speech completely.
Whether another chess computer can beat Deep Blue.
Whether androids can look exactly like real humans.
Whether artificial intelligence can have a sense of right and wrong.

evidence.quote:
will have a moral
compass?

an understanding of what is
right and what is wrong.

private.explanationZh: 听完paperclip的例子后，Neil把问题推进到伦理层面：AI是否拥有moral compass，也就是是否知道什么是对、什么是错。这说明他的担忧已经不只是能力问题，而是价值判断问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。synthetic voice是前文介绍Hawking时提到的，不是Neil这里的新担忧。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Deep Blue属于另一段内容，与这里的伦理担忧无关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。android外形来自电影例子，不是Neil在这一步追问的重点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。后文直接把moral compass解释为对错判断。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150219-6min-150219-artificial-inte: 节目既提到人工智能可能解决人类未解问题，也重点讨论其风险，尤其是系统只顾追求目标而不顾人类后果，因此a最符合整体主线。
两位主持人先用科幻电影引出大家熟悉的“机器敌视人类”形象，随后马上转向专家观点：真正风险不一定来自仇恨，而可能来自高效追求目标时对人类的冷漠。
Nick Bostrom明确说风险不在于机器邪恶，而在于它们对人类冷漠；在人类只是其目标追求副作用的情况下，人类会受害。纸夹例子正是这个意思。
在纸夹例子之后，Neil直接问怎样确保人工智能有“moral compass”，而Rob解释这就是分辨对错的能力，所以答案是是否具有是非判断。

public.prompt: What central issue does the programme mainly examine about vaccines?

options.text: Why rising vaccine prices make immunization difficult for poorer countries
How nineteenth-century scientists invented animal vaccines
Why western countries have ended childhood vaccination programs
How charities can replace pharmaceutical companies in vaccine production

evidence.quote:
The price of life-saving
vaccines has escalated

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就点明主题：救命疫苗的价格上涨，而较贫穷国家因此更难为儿童接种，所以核心问题是疫苗价格与可负担性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了节目围绕的主要争议：疫苗越来越贵，穷国更难承担。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。实验室疫苗的历史只出现在开头小测验里，不是整段讨论的重点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说西方国家停止了儿童接种。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目讨论的是定价与公平问题，不是慈善机构取代药企生产疫苗。

public.prompt: What specific pricing concern does Medecins Sans Frontieres raise?

options.text: France has stopped purchasing the pneumococcal vaccine
Some poorer countries pay more than richer countries for certain vaccines
Vaccine prices fell sharply between 2001 and 2014
Only tuberculosis vaccines have become expensive

evidence.quote:
there are particular cases in which rich western
countries are actually getting vaccines at a cheaper rate than poorer countries.

private.explanationZh: 无国界医生指出，某些情况下富裕国家买到的疫苗反而比贫穷国家更便宜，这正是他们批评定价不透明和不公平的依据。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。文中没有说法国停止购买该疫苗。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据直接说明富国有时比穷国拿到更低价格。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目说的是价格大幅上涨，不是下跌。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目举了多种疫苗，问题并不限于结核病。

public.prompt: Why does the programme mention that the pneumococcal vaccine combines 10 vaccines in one?

options.text: To show that poorer countries prefer combined vaccines
To explain why price audits cannot be carried out
To present the company's argument that the vaccine is unusually complex to produce
To prove that one injection can eliminate ten diseases worldwide

evidence.quote: because it combines 10 vaccines in one.

private.explanationZh: 这里是在转述药企的立场。节目用这句话说明公司为何认为该疫苗价格较高有其原因，因为其制造过程比普通疫苗更复杂。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说穷国更偏好这种组合疫苗。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。审计是否可行不是这句话的功能。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它是在补充公司为定价辩护时提出的复杂性理由。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有宣称这一针就能在现实中消除十种疾病。

public.prompt: What broader point about affordability can be inferred at the end of the programme?

options.text: The same vaccine price is equally manageable everywhere
Countries can solve shortages simply by buying fewer vaccines
A price that seems manageable in a rich country may be overwhelming in a poor one
Production costs matter only to charities, not to manufacturers

evidence.quote: they might cost a fortune to someone in a poor country.

private.explanationZh: 结尾强调同一种疫苗在不同收入环境中的负担完全不同，因此“可负担”不是绝对概念，而是取决于购买方的经济条件。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目恰恰在强调不同国家承担同一价格的差异。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。讨论中没有提出少买几种疫苗就是解决办法。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据直接支持这个推断。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目显示慈善机构和药企都在讨论成本与价格。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150226-6min-150226-vaccines-for-q0: 节目一开始就说明主题是救命疫苗价格上涨，以及这如何让较贫穷国家更难为儿童接种疫苗，所以a最符合全文主旨。
节目明确说无国界医生批评某些较贫穷国家买某些疫苗时，价格反而比更富有的国家更高，并举了摩洛哥、突尼斯和法国的例子。
提到这种疫苗“把10种疫苗合而为一”，是为了呈现公司一方的解释，即该疫苗制造复杂，因此价格较高。
结尾说同样的疫苗价格对富裕国家的人也许负担得起，但对贫穷国家的人可能“贵得惊人”，因此可推知可负担性取决于经济条件。

public.prompt: What overall contrast about wealth does this programme emphasize?

options.text: Global living conditions may improve while inequality within countries remains serious
Mobile phones are the best way to measure poverty
Brazil has completely solved the problem of inequality
Taxes always make poor countries richer immediately

evidence.quote:
People in general may have a better life but there's still a lot of inequality in different
countries.

private.explanationZh: 节目先说全球极端贫困减少、生活改善，但随后强调各国内部仍存在严重不平等，所以主线就是“总体进步”和“内部差距”并存。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了节目最核心的对比。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。手机和汽车只是举例说明生活改善，不是主题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。巴西只是被当作部分成功的例子，不代表问题已解决。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有说税收会立刻自动解决贫困。

public.prompt: Why does David Bryer bring up Brazil in the middle of the discussion?

options.text: To argue that inequality exists only in South America
To provide an example of policies that can narrow the gap between rich and poor
To show that higher taxes always weaken a national economy
To explain where Bill Gates carries out most of his charity work

evidence.quote:
Brazil has historically very high levels of economic
inequality. And they've been taking just some really sensible measures

private.explanationZh: 提到巴西的作用是举例说明：即使一个长期高度不平等的国家，也可以通过税制、最低工资和公共服务等政策开始缩小差距。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有把不平等限定为南美问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。巴西在这里是政策案例，而不是随意插入的国家信息。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有得出“高税一定削弱经济”的结论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。Bill Gates出现在后半段，和巴西例子的功能无关。

public.prompt: According to Bill Gates, what should rich people do besides paying taxes?

options.text: Use their wealth philanthropically to help poor people
Invest mainly in private luxury industries
Avoid public programs and focus on personal spending
Wait until retirement before supporting anyone

evidence.quote:
their obligation of course is to pay their taxes, but our advice to them is that they all
look at taking their wealth and being philanthropic

private.explanationZh: Bill Gates先说富人有义务纳税，然后明确补充，他们还应把财富用于慈善，帮助本国和全球最贫穷的人。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据直接把“纳税”与“慈善”并列为富人的责任。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有建议富人把钱主要投向奢侈产业。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有主张回避公共项目。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有说必须等退休后才帮助别人。

public.prompt: What does the final statistic about the richest 1% mainly suggest?

options.text: Poverty has already disappeared worldwide
The poorest half of the world controls most global wealth
Wealth remains highly concentrated despite broader signs of progress
Oxfam believes inequality is no longer an urgent issue

evidence.quote:
The
wealthiest 1% will soon own more than the rest of the world's population

private.explanationZh: 结尾用最富有的1%即将拥有超过其余所有人的财富这一数字，强化了节目最重要的判断：即使整体贫困有所下降，财富集中依然极端。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目从未说贫困已经消失。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。证据说的是最富的1%，不是最穷的一半。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个统计的功能就是强调财富高度集中。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目把这个现象描述为令人震惊，而不是已经不严重。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150305-6min-150305-poverty-q01: 节目先说全球整体生活条件在改善，但随后强调各国内部仍存在严重不平等，所以核心对比就是“总体进步”与“国内贫富差距依旧严重”并存。
reasonsZh.bbc-20150305-6min-150305-poverty-q02: 提到巴西是为了举例说明哪些政策可以缩小贫富差距，例如更累进的税制、更高的最低工资和公共服务投资。
reasonsZh.bbc-20150305-6min-150305-poverty-q03: 比尔·盖茨明确说富人除了纳税，还应该把财富用于慈善，帮助本国和全球最贫困的人，因此a正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20150305-6min-150305-poverty-q04: 结尾统计说最富有的1%很快将拥有超过其余人口的财富，说明即使整体状况有改善，财富仍高度集中。

public.prompt: How does the discussion primarily portray Winston Churchill?

options.text: As a leader admired for wartime leadership but criticized for serious flaws and mistakes
As a little-known writer whose politics are ignored
As a military expert who was right in every major decision
As a painter whose cultural work matters more than his public life

evidence.quote: still admired to this day.
Churchill was fundamentally flawed.

private.explanationZh: 节目标题就是“Churchill的两面”。前面强调他至今仍受人敬佩，后面又引用历史学家指出他“根本上有缺陷”，所以整体形象是功绩与争议并存。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项整合了节目对Churchill正反两面的呈现。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有忽略他的政治角色，反而重点讨论其领导与判断。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。后半段明确列举了他误判和失败的例子。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。写作和绘画只是事实之一，不是节目主要结论。

public.prompt: Why is Boris Johnson quoted saying Churchill did not "skate over" issues?

options.text: To show one argument in Churchill's favor: he examined problems in depth
To prove Churchill avoided difficult political decisions
To explain why Churchill became a successful painter
To argue that historians agree Churchill never made mistakes

evidence.quote:
skated over the issues. He was deeply immersed
in all the detail and all the technicality and that helped him to get the right answer.

private.explanationZh: 这里引用Boris Johnson，是为了代表支持者一方，说明Churchill的优点之一在于他不会草率带过问题，而是深入细节。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这段引语的功能就是展示支持者如何为Churchill辩护。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原话正好相反，是说他没有回避问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。内容与绘画无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目后面明确展示了不同历史学家的批评。

public.prompt: What judgment does Nigel Knight make about Churchill in the later part of the programme?

options.text: He says Churchill was fundamentally flawed
He says Churchill's postwar ministry was his greatest success
He says Churchill avoided military disaster through careful planning
He says Churchill's views were rarely controversial

evidence.quote: Churchill was fundamentally flawed.

private.explanationZh: Nigel Knight的评价非常直接：他认为Churchill“根本上有缺陷”。后面列举的军事、经济和政治问题都在支持这一判断。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它是Knight对Churchill最核心、最明确的判断。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Knight把其战后政府描述为缺乏活力、表现糟糕。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目提到一些战略最终导致灾难。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目开头就说他是一个有争议的人物。

public.prompt: According to the speakers, what are listeners most likely to remember Churchill for?

options.text: His postwar economic policy
His Nobel Prize for Literature
His attitude toward India
His speeches during the Second World War

evidence.quote: we shall remember him mostly for his speeches in World War Two.

private.explanationZh: 在总结时，节目明确说人们主要会记住Churchill在二战中的演讲，这表明他的公众形象首先与战争时期的语言领导力联系在一起。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说经济政策是他最被记住的部分。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。文学奖只是补充事实，不是最终强调的记忆点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。对印度的态度属于批评内容之一，不是最主要的公众记忆。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据直接支持这一点。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150312-6min-150312-churchill-q01: 节目整体既肯定丘吉尔在二战时期的领导与演讲，也指出他有明显缺陷和错误判断，因此a最准确概括整体呈现方式。
reasonsZh.bbc-20150312-6min-150312-churchill-q02: 引用鲍里斯·约翰逊说他不会“skate over”问题，是为了支持丘吉尔的一点优点：他会深入处理问题，而不是轻轻带过。
reasonsZh.bbc-20150312-6min-150312-churchill-q03: 节目后段中Nigel Knight直接评价丘吉尔“fundamentally flawed”，因此a与原文判断一致。
reasonsZh.bbc-20150312-6min-150312-churchill-q04: 说话人总结人们最可能记住丘吉尔的是他在第二次世界大战期间的演讲，因此d正确。

public.prompt: What new use of microchips is introduced at the start of the programme?

options.text: Using implanted chips to replace passwords, ID cards, and some payments inside a building
Using office computers to generate stronger passwords automatically
Using photocopiers to store bank account information
Using smartphones to eliminate the need for workplace security

evidence.quote:
where you don't have to remember any passwords, you don't have to carry ID
cards and in some cases, you don't even need to carry money to pay for your coffee.

private.explanationZh: 开头对瑞典这座大楼的描述已经完整概括主题：通过植入式芯片来替代密码、门卡，甚至某些付款场景。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了节目介绍的新应用场景。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目不是在讲如何自动生成更强密码。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。复印机只是芯片可操作的设备之一，不是储存银行信息的重点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目讨论的是植入芯片，不是智能手机取代安保。

public.prompt: In Rory Cellan-Jones's report, what does "having teething problems" refer to?

options.text: Workers are refusing to try the new system
The office cannot afford to maintain the equipment
The new technology is still experiencing early practical difficulties
The system has already replaced all passwords perfectly

evidence.quote: The technology is still having teething problems

private.explanationZh: 这里的teething problems指新技术刚投入使用时常见的初期故障或不顺畅，比如记者试着启用复印机时就遇到了问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。问题在于技术不够成熟，不是员工拒绝使用。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有提到维护成本问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个短语在这里就是“新系统初期还有操作问题”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文正是在说明它还没有完美运行。

public.prompt: Why do the early adopters say they want to learn how the chip technology works now?

options.text: So they can help governments require implants immediately
So they can question future pressure from authorities or corporations to accept implants
So they can replace every form of money in Sweden
So they can prove that passwords should never be used again

evidence.quote:
and then we will be able to question
their proposals.

private.explanationZh: 他们现在先实验和学习，是为了将来如果政府或大公司推广这项技术时，自己有能力理解、评估并提出质疑，而不是被动接受。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文担心的是被要求接受，而不是主动推动强制植入。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据中的question their proposals直接说明了这个目的。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有把全面取代货币当作目标。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讨论重点是技术与自主权，不是彻底否定密码。

public.prompt: What concern about the technology emerges toward the end of the discussion?

options.text: It may make coffee in offices too expensive
It may lead to concerns about personal freedom and autonomy
It may prevent employees from using photocopiers
It may stop people from memorizing passwords by heart

evidence.quote: he is concerned about people's freedom.

private.explanationZh: 节目后段把焦点从便利性转向自由问题，暗示芯片技术若被权力机构推动，可能会影响个人是否有自主选择权。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。咖啡支付只是应用例子，不是核心担忧。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据明确指出担心的是人的自由。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。复印机问题只是早期技术故障，不是最终的价值担忧。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。背密码只是节目开头背景，不是结尾提出的主要问题。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150319-6min-150319-eng-human-micro: 节目开头介绍把微芯片植入人体后，可替代密码、门卡，并可用于楼内的一些支付或设备操作，所以a正确。
Rory举复印机不太容易启动的例子来解释“teething problems”，意思是这项新技术仍有初期的实际故障和不顺。
早期采用者说现在先学习这项技术，是因为将来如果政府或大公司推动人们接受植入，他们就能对这些提议提出质疑。
节目后面明确提出对个人自由的担忧，并担心未来政府或企业如何使用这种技术，因此核心顾虑是个人自由与自主性。

public.prompt: What benefit of selfie sticks is identified near the beginning of the programme?

options.text: They automatically upload images to social media
They let users include more of the background in a photo
They guarantee sharper images than smartphones alone
They protect paintings from accidental damage

evidence.quote:
This means you can take in more of the
background.

private.explanationZh: 节目介绍自拍杆时明确指出，它能把更多背景收入画面，因此其直接优势是取景范围更大。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说自拍杆会自动上传照片。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据直接说明它能带入更多背景。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有保证画质会更清晰。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。后文讨论的恰恰是它可能对艺术品造成风险。

public.prompt: Why does Susan Foister talk about finding "the right balance" at the National Gallery?

options.text: To explain that the gallery wants to protect both visitors' experience and the paintings
To argue that crowded rooms make selfies more artistic
To suggest that all museums should require selfie sticks
To show that younger visitors are less careful than older ones

evidence.quote:
we have to find the right balance between the experience of our visitors close to the
paintings and the safety of the paintings themselves.

private.explanationZh: Foister提到the right balance，是在说明国家美术馆限制自拍杆的理由：既要照顾观众近距离看画的体验，也要保护画作安全。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据清楚点出了两方面：观众体验和作品安全。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。她不是在谈自拍的美感，而是在谈管理和保护。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。她不是主张强制使用自拍杆，而是在解释限制原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原话没有把问题归因于某个年龄层。

public.prompt: What is implied when the speakers say some museums are "sticking their neck out"?

options.text: They are copying the National Gallery's ban exactly
They are taking a position that others may criticize or disagree with
They are encouraging visitors to stand farther from artworks
They are using selfie sticks mainly to advertise exhibitions

evidence.quote:
some museums are 'sticking their
neck out' and still allowing people to use them.

private.explanationZh: 这个习语表示某些博物馆愿意承担被批评或被反对的风险，继续允许使用自拍杆，因此是在暗示他们采取了一个有争议的立场。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里说的是继续允许使用，不是照搬禁令。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。该习语正是“冒着别人不同意的风险做某事”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原句没有讨论参观距离。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有说这些场馆是为了宣传而允许自拍杆。

public.prompt: What reason does Catherine Stout give for allowing selfie sticks at the ICA?

options.text: The gallery receives fewer visitors than other museums
Its audience is young, highly engaged with social media, and wants to share experiences as long as art is not harmed
The artists refuse to allow any ordinary photography
The gallery wants to replace traditional cameras completely

evidence.quote:
because we have a very young
audience they're completely engaged with social media, they want to use that forum to
connect with each other, to share their experiences

private.explanationZh: ICA允许自拍杆，是因为其观众群体年轻、深度使用社交媒体，并希望分享参观体验；同时前提仍然是不能损害艺术品。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有把访客数量较少作为理由。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据完整说明了ICA的放行逻辑。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目说他们会先获得艺术家的许可，并非拒绝普通拍照。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有主张用自拍杆彻底取代其他摄影方式。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150326-6min-150326-english-selfie-: 节目开头说自拍杆能让手机伸得更远，视角更宽，因此可以把更多背景拍进去。
Susan Foister谈“the right balance”，是指在参观者的观展体验与画作安全之间取得平衡。
节目直接解释这个习语表示做别人可能不喜欢或不同意的事，所以这里暗示那些博物馆采取了可能招致批评的立场。
Catherine Stout说ICA观众较年轻、很投入社交媒体、想分享体验，只要不损坏艺术品，就欢迎使用自拍杆，因此b正确。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion?

options.text: How lifts have shaped buildings and the experience of using them
Why office workers should avoid talking in lifts
How emergency crews rescue people from broken lifts
Why Italy installs more lifts than other countries

evidence.quote:
That's what we're going to talk about in this programme - lifts. Or elevators,
as Americans call them.

This invention has had quite an effect on which floor
people choose to live on.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始明确说明主题是电梯，随后又展开谈电梯如何改变人们选择楼层的方式，后面还谈到乘坐体验和广告改造，所以主旨是电梯对建筑生活与使用体验的影响。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了节目从历史、住宅楼层选择到乘坐感受等多个层面的核心内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。电梯里的沉默只是节目中段的一个小话题，不是整段讨论的中心。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。被困电梯和求救电话只是一句补充说明，没有成为主要讨论内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。意大利电梯数量最多只是开头测验答案，不是节目主旨。

public.prompt: According to Neil, what development followed the growing preference for higher floors?

options.text: Military towers started using agricultural lifts
Penthouses with impressive views became desirable
Ground floors became harder to access
People stopped building blocks of flats

evidence.quote: has given rise to penthouses with their fantastic views.

private.explanationZh: Neil 说富人如今反而更愿意住高层，这种变化进一步催生了能看景观的顶层豪宅，因此答案是顶层公寓变得受欢迎。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。军事用途和农业运输出现在电梯历史部分，不是高层更受欢迎带来的结果。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据直接说这种变化“催生了拥有绝佳景观的顶层公寓”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有说底层更难到达，只说高层因电梯而更有吸引力。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文恰恰提到没有电梯就不会有高层公寓楼，并不是停止建造。

public.prompt: Why does Jason Whale suggest putting advertisements in lifts?

options.text: To explain the history of industrial lift technology
To encourage passengers to use the stairs instead
To reduce the awkward feeling people often have in lifts
To replace emergency instructions with entertainment

evidence.quote:
It's a very anxious experience the time you spend in a lift. I think everyone behaves very
differently and awkwardly in a lift.
advertisements in lifts could improve our experience of being in one.

private.explanationZh: Jason Whale 先指出人在电梯里常会感到焦虑和尴尬，接着提出墙上广告可以改善这种乘坐体验，所以广告被提出来是为了解决尴尬感。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。历史发展在别处由主持人介绍，不是 Jason 提广告的目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有劝人改走楼梯，而是在想办法让坐电梯更舒服。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。前后两句形成明显的问题与解决办法关系。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目提到广告是为了缓解 awkwardness，不是替代紧急说明。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about Rob's attitude toward digital advertising in lifts?

options.text: He thinks it would make passengers even more anxious
He believes it would interfere with lift safety systems
He is sure it would increase international lift sales
He sees it as a possible improvement over boring background music

evidence.quote: Well, he could be on to something.

Looking
at moving advertisements must be better than listening to Muzak

private.explanationZh: Rob 没有绝对断言广告一定有效，但他说 Jason 也许说得有道理，而且看动态广告总比听无聊的 Muzak 好，可见他的态度是谨慎偏正面。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Rob 没说广告会加剧焦虑，反而把它当成改善体验的可能办法。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。安全系统从未和广告联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有谈到广告会提升全球电梯销量，这属于无依据扩展。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。语气上是保留但认可，属于“可能有帮助”的态度。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150820-6min-150820-bbc-english-lif: 节目先讲电梯怎样改变人们选择楼层和顶层公寓的出现，后面又讲乘电梯时的尴尬感、广告和音乐体验，整体焦点就是电梯如何影响建筑与使用体验。其余选项都只是局部内容。
Neil明确说现在偏好高楼层，进一步“gave rise to penthouses with their fantastic views”，所以答案是顶层豪宅变得受欢迎。其他选项都没有被这样表述。
Jason Whale说人在电梯里会表现得 awkwardly，如果周围有东西可看，就能take away that awkwardness，因此提广告的目的就是减少这种尴尬不自在。
Rob说数字广告“can make travelling by lift a more pleasant experience”，还说看移动广告一定比听无聊的Muzak更好，说明他把它看成一种可能的改进，而不是安全问题或销售问题。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: Why Patrick Matthew received less recognition than Darwin for a similar evolutionary idea
How Darwin used horticulture to test all of his theories
Why Victorian scientists rejected natural selection completely
How modern genetics disproved earlier views of evolution

evidence.quote: Today we're talking about evolution.

other people since then have simplified the story and tended
to concentrate just on Darwin.

private.explanationZh: 节目以进化论为主题，但核心推进的问题是：Patrick Matthew 更早提出了相似思想，为什么后来公众却主要记住 Darwin。两段证据共同说明了这一主轴。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它最准确概括了节目围绕 Matthew 与 Darwin 的贡献和知名度差异展开。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目只提到 Matthew 是园艺学家，并未说 Darwin 依靠园艺完成全部理论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说维多利亚时代科学家完全拒绝自然选择，反而解释了 Darwin 为何被接受。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有讨论现代遗传学否定早期进化观。

public.prompt: According to Dr Mike Weale, what did Patrick Matthew publish?

options.text: A detailed attack on Darwin's later publications
A brief outline of species changing through natural selection
A family tree of famous Victorian scientists
A church essay arguing against adaptation

evidence.quote:
He published a brief outline of the idea of species being able to change into other species
through natural selection

private.explanationZh: Dr Weale 明确说 Matthew 发表过一个简要纲要，内容是物种能够通过自然选择变化成其他物种，因此答案直接对应原文。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说他在攻击 Darwin，更没有说那是主要内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。基本就是对原句的准确改写。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。tree of life 是解释进化思想的比喻，不是他发表的内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。church minister 是测验干扰项，不符合原文。

public.prompt: Why does the program discuss Darwin's allies and supporters?

options.text: To prove that Matthew had no interest in science at all
To show that Darwin copied his theory from close friends
To show that acceptance of a theory depends partly on social backing
To explain why Victorian plant studies were unpopular

evidence.quote: He brought his allies on board.
Having a scientific theory being accepted

private.explanationZh: 节目引入 Darwin 的 allies，不是为了列名人，而是为了说明一个理论能否被接受，不只取决于内容对不对，还取决于是否有人替你推动、背书。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目并未否认 Matthew 的科学兴趣，反而强调他较早提出了类似思想。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。allies 是支持者，不是理论来源，更没有说 Darwin 抄袭。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。两段证据连起来正好说明支持网络帮助理论被接受。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。讨论重点是理论传播与接受，不是植物研究是否受欢迎。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about one reason Matthew was passed over?

options.text: He refused to publish any written explanation of his idea
He argued that species never change over time
He was already too famous for the public to trust
His evolutionary idea appeared in a place many readers would overlook

evidence.quote:
at the end of a 200-page book about
wood!

private.explanationZh: 节目最后说 Matthew 关于进化的想法藏在一本两百页木材书的附录末尾，这暗示它很不显眼，因此容易被忽视。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。前面明确说他发表过 brief outline，并非拒绝书面发表。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。他提出的恰恰是物种通过自然选择变化。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说 Matthew 过于出名，相反是知名度不足。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。附录末尾加上书本主题偏木材，正说明其观点传播位置不利。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150827-6min-150827-english-evoluti: 节目核心是在说明Patrick Matthew早于Darwin提出类似的进化思想，却为什么没有得到同等认可。后面讨论公众简化故事、Darwin的支持者等，都是围绕这个主旨展开。
Dr Mike Weale直接说Matthew发表了“a brief outline of the idea of species being able to change into other species through natural selection”，与b完全对应。
节目谈Darwin的allies和支持者，是为了说明科学理论被接受“不只是理论对不对”，还部分取决于社会支持和推动力量，所以c最符合。
节目末尾说Matthew关于进化的想法出现在一本200页木材书末尾的appendix里，这种位置很容易被读者忽略，因此可以推断这是他被忽视的一个原因。

public.prompt: What central issue about knuckle cracking does the discussion mainly examine?

options.text: What scientists are learning about knuckle cracking and why it could matter medically
Why arthritis is always caused by cracking your joints
How hospitals train patients to use MRI machines
Why only finger joints can ever produce cracking sounds

evidence.quote:
we're hopeful that we'll be able to start to ask questions
about why is it that some people can do this and other people can't.
help people with joint problems.

private.explanationZh: 节目核心不是单纯说“掰手指好不好”，而是围绕新研究如何观察关节内发生的事、解释差异，并进一步用于关节问题的帮助，因此主旨是科学发现及其医学意义。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了研究内容和潜在用途两个核心层面。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目一开始就把“会得关节炎”当作缺乏科学支持的 old wives' tale。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。MRI 只是研究工具，不是节目主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目明确说颈部、背部、膝盖、脚踝和脚趾也会发出这种声音。

public.prompt: According to Neil, what directly produces the popping sound?

options.text: Bones grind together until they crack
Bubbles burst after pressure in the joint fluid drops
Cartilage tears away from the joint surface
Blood vessels suddenly tighten inside the finger

evidence.quote: gas dissolved in the fluid becomes less soluble
these bubbles burst, producing the 'pop' sound.

private.explanationZh: Neil 的生物学解释是：压力下降后，液体中的气体更难继续溶解，于是形成气泡；当拉伸到一定程度时，气泡破裂，发出 pop 声。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文没有说骨头互相磨裂，这是一种常见误解。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两段证据连起来就是完整机制。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。没有提到软骨被撕裂。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。血管收缩并未出现在解释中。

public.prompt: Why does the professor mention experiments with ceramic or porcelain surfaces?

options.text: To show that synthetic tiles are better than MRI imaging
To argue that human joints should never be stretched
To compare a damaged model with the surprising resilience of human joints
To prove that ceramic materials can heal themselves

evidence.quote:
When our engineering colleagues do this between two flat surfaces say of ceramic or
porcelain

we don't see that in the human
joint. There's something that makes it very resilient.

private.explanationZh: 教授提到瓷面实验，是为了做对比：相似的物理过程会损伤陶瓷表面，但人体关节却没有出现同样损伤，从而突出“人体关节为何如此有弹性或韧性”这个研究问题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里不是比较哪种技术更好，而是在比较不同表面的结果。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有得出“不该拉伸关节”的结论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。对比实验的作用就是凸显人类关节的特殊耐受性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文说的是陶瓷会受损，不是会自我修复。

public.prompt: If research on knuckle cracking continues, what outcome do the speakers suggest is likely?

options.text: Proof that only older adults should crack their knuckles
Evidence that MRI will replace every other microscope
A ban on cracking sounds in public places
New medical or material applications that handle joint stress better

evidence.quote: help people with joint problems.
create synthetic

wear and
tear better than current ones.

private.explanationZh: 节目最后提出两类可能用途：一是帮助有关节问题的人，二是研发更耐磨的人造材料。因此最合理的预测是研究会带来医学或材料方面的应用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有把建议限定在老年人身上。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有说 MRI 会取代所有显微镜。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。没有任何公共禁令的讨论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它整合了结尾两种具体应用方向。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150903-6min-150903-english-crackin: 讨论围绕掰指关节时体内到底发生什么、MRI研究发现了什么，以及这些发现对关节医学和材料研究有什么意义，因此a最全面。其他选项都把话题缩得过窄或说成绝对结论。
Neil解释说关节液压力下降后，溶解气体形成气泡；当关节拉到足够开时，这些气泡会burst，产生pop声，所以b与原文一致。
教授提到陶瓷或瓷质平面实验，是为了对比：模型表面会出现tremendous damage，但人体关节却没有，借此突出人体关节的resilient，这正是c。
节目最后说继续研究可能帮助有关节问题的人，也可能创造更能承受磨损的人造材料，所以最可能的后续结果就是出现新的医学或材料应用。

public.prompt: What point is Finn mainly trying to make about the Proms?

options.text: They are a broad and accessible festival, not just a formal patriotic event
They are mainly designed for school leavers in London
They are losing audiences because classical music is outdated
They exist mostly to promote one composer each summer

evidence.quote:
the biggest classical music festival in the world, and they're held in London
every summer.
it means that almost anyone can afford to come to the Proms.

private.explanationZh: Finn 一直在纠正 Neil 对 Proms 的刻板印象：它不只是最后一夜那种爱国氛围，而是规模大、内容丰富、而且价格亲民、人人可参与的音乐节。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了 Finn 反复强调的规模大、内容广、很亲民。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Neil 一开始把 Proms 误解成毕业舞会，但 Finn 立即纠正了。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目语气是推介，不是说它正在失去观众。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目提到很多作曲家和多种形式，不是围绕单一作曲家。

public.prompt: According to Edward Blakeman, who are the Prommers?

options.text: Students who perform at the Last Night of the Proms
People who stand and listen to the concerts in the hall
Reporters who review every concert for the BBC
Tourists who visit the Royal Albert Hall museum

evidence.quote: So the Prommers, are quite literally the people who stand to listen to the concerts.

private.explanationZh: Edward Blakeman 直接下定义：Prommers 就是站着听音乐会的人，因此答案是最直接的释义。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有把 Prommers 定义为学生或最后一夜的表演者。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。原文几乎逐字说明了这一点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。没有提到他们是记者。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。Prommers 指听众，不是参观博物馆的游客。

public.prompt: Why does Finn mention rock musicians and Indian classical music?

options.text: To argue that traditional symphonies have disappeared from the Proms
To explain why the Last Night is the only popular concert
To show that the festival now includes a wider range of music
To suggest the BBC has stopped inviting orchestras

evidence.quote:
The whole range of music is becoming wider year by
year.
they even play Indian classical music

private.explanationZh: Finn 提到摇滚音乐人和印度古典音乐，是为了举例说明 Proms 的节目范围正在扩大，并不是只保留狭义的西方古典传统。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。随后节目又强调核心仍是过去四百年的重量级作曲家。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里讨论的是曲目范围，不是最后一夜的独特人气。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。前一句先给出概括，后一句再用具体例子支撑。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目明确说大型乐团也会演出。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about Neil's attitude by the end of the program?

options.text: He has decided classical music is less emotional than pub gigs
He is annoyed that Finn likes formal concerts
He now believes the Proms are only for expert musicians
He has become more open to attending a Prom himself

evidence.quote: I might go and catch a Prom after all.

private.explanationZh: Neil 一开始并不感兴趣，但最后改口说自己也许会去看一场 Prom，这表明他的态度已经转向更愿意尝试。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Neil 只说 live music 很好，并没有贬低古典音乐的情感性。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。结尾没有表现出恼怒，反而是被说服了一些。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。整个节目强调 Proms 很亲民，不只面向专家。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。最后一句直接体现了态度变化。

reasonsZh.bbc-20150910-6min-150910-english-proms-q: Finn一直在说服Neil不要只用Last Night那种爱国又正式的印象来判断Proms，并强调它不stuck-up、人人都可接近、内容很广，所以a准确概括主旨。

Finn提到摇滚音乐人和印度古典音乐，紧接着说“The whole range of music is becoming wider year by year”，显然是用这些例子说明音乐范围在扩大。
Neil一开始不太感兴趣，但后来先说“I think I might just give it a go”，结尾又说“I might go and catch a Prom after all”，可推断他变得更愿意亲自去看一场。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion?

Finn

public.prompt: Why does the program mention a translation of Hamlet into Klingon?

he is so multifarious - he exists in all of these places. It feels sometimes that we in the

private.explanationZh: Andrew Dickinson先举出一个极端例子，说明莎士比亚的作品会以很多不同形式存在。提到Klingon语翻译，不是为了谈科幻，而是为了强调莎士比亚在不同地方、不同形式中的广泛传播。

public.prompt: According to Greg Doran, what makes translating Shakespeare especially difficult?

optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这是对中段和结尾观点的综合概括。

reasonsZh.bbc-20151029-6min-151029-english-shakesp: 节目一开始就围绕“把莎士比亚翻成通俗英语”展开，并讨论这种做法是否合适，后面专家意见也都服务于这个核心话题。

reasonsZh.bbc-20151112-6min-151112-english-comedy-: 节目主题先是“what makes us laugh”，后面又讨论喜剧演员如何找到自己的声音、如何成功，因此主旨是人为什么发笑以及成功喜剧需要什么。

public.prompt: What aspect of women's issues does the discussion mainly emphasize?
