Women's rights issues and the BBC's effort to discuss them through 100 Women

evidence.quote: The series is called 100 Women and it's the theme of this 6 Minute English.

private.explanationZh: 前面先谈女性权利和媒体报道不足，随后明确说明BBC推出100 Women系列来讨论影响全球一半人口的问题，所以整段讨论的核心就是女性议题以及BBC围绕这些议题展开的项目。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。女性杂志只是Catherine批评媒体关注点时提到的例子。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目主张更多讨论真实女性议题，而不是回避。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。联合国数据只在结尾作为测验答案出现。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确概括了主题和项目目的。

public.prompt: Why were packs sent to more than 100 women's groups around the world?

options.text: To help them take part in the live debates on the day of the event
To train them to produce BBC television programs
To persuade them to focus only on leadership issues
To collect private stories that would not be broadcast

evidence.quote:
sent out packs to over 100
women's groups around the world, so that on the day they all get involved.

private.explanationZh: Tammi Walker说寄出材料包是为了让全球一百多个妇女团体在当天都能参与进来，因此这些包的作用就是组织并促进同步参与直播讨论。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。原文直接说明材料包是为了让大家当天都参与进来。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。没有提到培训这些团体制作节目。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。当天有三个主题，不是只谈领导力。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目说的是参与辩论，不是秘密收集个人故事。

public.prompt: Why does Tammi Walker mention "the right to pee" in India?

options.text: To argue that toilet fees are the main cause of sexism worldwide
To show that some women's issues involve basic daily needs and public health
To explain why the BBC prefers humorous topics on social media
To compare India's media system with Britain's broadcasting rules

evidence.quote:
right to pee. For example in India, women
are charged 25 rupees to go to the toilet whereas men obviously can go anywhere for free.

private.explanationZh: 这里提出“上厕所的权利”不是为了搞笑，而是为了举出一个非常具体、贴近日常生活的例子，说明女性面对的某些问题非常基础，也与公共卫生直接相关。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这个例子说明一种不平等现象，但没有说它是全球性别歧视的主要原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它突出了女性议题并不抽象，而是涉及基本生活条件和公共健康。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Tammi不是在解释社交媒体选题风格。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里没有比较两国媒体制度。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the reaction to programs about sexism in Iran?

options.text: Audiences in Iran were already very familiar with the term sexism
The BBC decided to stop discussing sexism with younger audiences
Some people misunderstood sexism as sexual content rather than discrimination
The main debate in Iran was whether racism should be discussed first

evidence.quote:
confused it with sex. So some people were asking us: Air your programmes about sexism
after the watershed, after 9 pm, you know, so that children will not watch.

private.explanationZh: Sanam Dolatshahi说很多人把sexism和sex混淆，所以才会要求把节目放到晚上九点以后播出。由此可推断，一些观众并没有把sexism理解成性别歧视，而是误解成与性内容有关。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文恰恰说明很多人并不熟悉这个概念。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这是观众的反应，不是BBC决定停止讨论。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这正是该反应所暴露出的误解。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。提到racism只是为了说明人们更熟悉另一个“ism”词。

reasonsZh.bbc-20151119-6min-151119-english-100-wom: 节目围绕女性权利议题展开，并重点介绍BBC通过“100 Women”系列节目和辩论来讨论这些问题。
Tammi Walker明确说这些材料包寄给全球100多个妇女团体，是为了让她们在那一天都参与到现场辩论中。
提到印度“the right to pee”是为了说明女性面临的有些问题非常基础，涉及如厕这种日常需要，而且“it is a matter of public health too”。
Iran的反应是有人把sexism误解成和sex有关，所以要求节目放到晚上9点后播出，说明他们误把它当成性内容，而不是性别歧视。

public.prompt: What relationship issue is the main focus of the discussion?

options.text: How financial honesty and different money attitudes affect relationships
Why banks reject most mortgage applications from married couples
How to choose the right engagement ring for a first date
Why restaurants should always make couples split the bill

evidence.quote:
money matters can cause relationship
problems in couples – and that’s the subject of the show. Here’s today’s quiz question

private.explanationZh: Neil在开头直接点明，节目主题是金钱问题如何引发伴侣关系中的麻烦。后文围绕财务诚实、自我价值感、spender和saver差异展开，都是这个主线的展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了整期节目的主题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。房贷只是举例说明隐瞒债务可能带来的后果。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。戒指只是主持人争论中的例子，不是节目主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目也没有主张所有约会都必须AA。

public.prompt: Why does Arabella Russell say money is difficult to talk about?

options.text: Because most couples do not understand the meaning of budget
Because people usually hide their salaries from banks
Because money is tied to guilt, shame, and feelings of self-worth
Because modern payment systems have replaced hard cash

evidence.quote:
how we value ourselves, how we feel valued. It’s not just a simple case of
talking about hard cash.

private.explanationZh: Arabella的重点是，谈钱之所以难，不只是谈数字，而是会牵涉羞愧、内疚，以及“我怎么看自己、别人怎么看我”这种自我价值感，所以钱的话题很容易带上情绪。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。budget的定义是后面解释的，不是谈钱困难的根本原因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。原文没有说因为银行问题才难谈。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个选项概括了Arabella对情绪和自我价值感的说明。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。硬现金与否不是她强调的核心。

public.prompt: What advice does Arabella Russell give couples who handle money differently?

options.text: They should let the saver make every financial decision
They should stay honest, accept the difference, and still have a budget
They should avoid discussing money until they are married
They should keep separate secrets to prevent arguments

evidence.quote:
Accept the fact that in your relationship you might do money differently – there might be a
spender there might be a saver. It’s very tempting if your partner does things differently to
say they’re wrong. They might do it differently because they see money differently. Do it
differently but have a budget. Of course be honest about each other but recognise that you
might just have a different view of money.

differently but have a budget. Of course be honest about each other but recognise that you
might just have a different view of money.

private.explanationZh: Arabella的建议很具体：先接受双方处理金钱的方式不同，再保持诚实，同时仍然要有预算安排。也就是说，不是强求一致，而是在差异中建立透明和规则。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。她没有让某一方完全掌控财务。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个选项完整概括了“接受差异、保持诚实、制定预算”的建议。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。她强调的是开始这些对话，而不是拖延。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。隐瞒和秘密正是节目提醒会带来问题的做法。

public.prompt: What is the purpose of the speakers' argument about engagement rings near the end?

options.text: To prove that expensive gifts always improve a relationship
To show that marriage is impossible when couples have debt
To explain how to calculate a realistic wedding budget
To illustrate how a saver and a spender can interpret the same relationship issue differently

evidence.quote:
Calm down. I see money differently to you. I’m a saver and you’re a spender, and that’s all
there is to it.

agree to differ. And that means accept that we have different opinions
on this one!

private.explanationZh: 临近结尾，两人围绕订婚戒指的花费争起来，不是为了真的讨论购物技巧，而是把前面Arabella提到的“saver和spender看钱不同”演成一个具体场景。它用来说明，同一段关系中的同一问题，双方可能会因为金钱观不同而得出不同判断。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Alice虽然主张投入关系，但这段争论不是为了证明“贵就一定好”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里争的是消费观，不是债务问题导致婚姻不可能。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有在这里教观众计算婚礼预算。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。戒指争论正好把不同金钱观如何造成分歧具体化了。

reasonsZh.bbc-20151119-6min-151119-english-couples: 节目明确说主题是金钱问题如何导致伴侣关系中的麻烦，后面集中讨论财务诚实以及spender和saver的不同金钱观。
Arabella Russell说谈钱之所以困难，是因为它伴随guilt、shame，还关系到如何看待自己、是否被重视，所以不是单纯谈现金。
Arabella建议伴侣接受彼此处理金钱方式不同，保持诚实，并且“have a budget”，这与B完全对应。
戒指争论不是在教人买多贵，而是用具体例子展示Neil这个saver和Alice这个spender如何对同一关系问题作出不同解读。

public.prompt: Why does Alice say that Neil has watched many TV and film versions of Robin Hood but has not read the literature?

options.text: To argue that film versions are more accurate than written texts
To contrast familiar popular images with the older literary tradition the program will discuss
To explain why Neil should answer the quiz without listening further
To show that Robin Hood first became famous through children’s cartoons

evidence.quote:
OK, it sounds like you’ve watched a lot of TV and film versions but haven’t read
the literature.

private.explanationZh: Alice这样说，是为了把Neil熟悉的影视化罗宾汉形象，与接下来要讨论的中世纪文学传统区分开来，说明节目重点不是流行改编，而是文学来源和演变。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说影视版本更准确，反而暗示Neil了解的是流行改编而非文学原貌。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确答案，准确概括了Alice这句话的引入作用：从大众印象转向文学传统。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这句话不是在谈答题策略，而是在铺垫节目的讨论方向。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目提到迪士尼只是Neil后面的补充，不是Alice此处发言的目的。

public.prompt: According to Professor Hahn, what may disturb modern audiences about the ballad The Monk?

options.text: It treats Robin Hood mainly as a romantic hero
It focuses on children’s humor instead of action
It contains graphic violence comparable to gangster dramas
It avoids conflict and presents Robin as unusually gentle

evidence.quote:
quite disturbing in terms of its levels of violence. In terms of
trying to make some comparisons with popular culture it seems to me that it’s really at the

level of Sopranos in terms of things like dismembered bodies and actual violence and
assassinations.

private.explanationZh: Professor Hahn明确说《The Monk》对现代观众来说“令人不安”，原因是其暴力程度很高，还把它比作《黑道家族》那种包含肢解、暴力和暗杀的作品，因此核心信息就是暴力描写非常血腥。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文段强调的是暴力，不是把Robin Hood主要塑造成浪漫英雄。
optionRationalesZh.b: 教授没有说它面向儿童或以幽默为主，恰恰相反，他强调其令人不安的暴力。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确答案，直接对应教授把该作品与黑帮题材电视剧相比较的说明。
optionRationalesZh.d: 与原文相反，原文突出的是强烈暴力，而不是温和或回避冲突。

public.prompt: According to Professor Hahn, what quality do many people admire in Robin Hood stories?

options.text: His devotion to Maid Marian in the earliest ballads
His ability to turn violence into comedy
His respect for religious and political authority
His forceful independence and willingness to act by his own rules

evidence.quote:
What he represents I think is a kind of strong and forceful masculinity that operates on its
own terms and for its own interests and that’s I think what we admire in these stories.

private.explanationZh: 教授认为人们欣赏的是Robin Hood所代表的一种强势、独立、按自己规则行事的男性气质，因此可推断其吸引力来自独立反叛的形象，而不是具体情节元素。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目还特别说明早期歌谣里并没有Maid Marian，所以这不是核心可赞赏之处。
optionRationalesZh.b: 教授没有说观众欣赏的是把暴力变成喜剧，而是强调独立自主的气质。
optionRationalesZh.c: Robin Hood作为outlaw并不代表尊重权威，这与原意相反。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确答案，是对“operate on its own terms and for its own interests”的准确概括。

public.prompt: According to Alice, when do we find the first reference to Robin Hood in English literature?

options.text: In the 5th century
In the 10th century
In the 14th century
In the 15th century

evidence.quote:
The first reference occurs in the English poet
William Langland's book Piers Plowman written between 1370 and 1390.

private.explanationZh: Alice说最早的文学提及出现在Langland于1370到1390年间写成的《Piers Plowman》中，这一时间段属于14世纪，所以答案是14th century。

optionRationalesZh.a: 5世纪只是节目中提到的中世纪起点之一，不是Robin Hood首次被文学提及的时间。
optionRationalesZh.b: 10世纪是Neil的错误猜测，Alice随后明确否定。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确答案，因为1370-1390明确落在14世纪。
optionRationalesZh.d: 15世纪比原文给出的时间更晚，不符合证据。

reasonsZh.bbc-20151203-6min-151203-english-robin-h: Alice先让Neil说出他熟悉的影视化形象，随后指出他“看了很多电视和电影版本，但没读过文学作品”，紧接着转入中世纪文学与最早文献记载，作用是把大众熟悉的流行形象和节目将讨论的早期文学传统对照起来。
Professor Hahn明确说《The Monk》对现代受众而言令人不安，因为其暴力程度很高，可与《The Sopranos》相比，并提到“dismembered bodies”“actual violence”“assassinations”，所以是含有接近黑帮剧的血腥暴力。
Professor Hahn说Robin Hood代表一种“strong and forceful masculinity”，并且“operates on its own terms and for its own interests”，这正对应按自己的规则行事、具有强烈独立性的描述。
Alice最后公布答案时说最早的文学提及出现在William Langland于1370到1390年间写的《Piers Plowman》中，属于14世纪，因此选14th century。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the discussion?

options.text: Why university parties matter more than academic work
Why many students face mental-health stress and what may help them cope
Why only homesick students need counselling services
Why students should avoid leaving home for university

evidence.quote: the subject of today's show is student mental health.

there has been a rise in students using
counselling services.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就点明主题是“student mental health”，随后又说英国使用心理咨询服务的学生人数在上升，因此整段讨论的主线是大学生面临的心理压力以及可能的应对支持。

optionRationalesZh.a: Neil提到派对只是个人回忆，不是整段讨论的主题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确答案，准确整合了开头点题和后面对咨询、压力、适应问题的展开。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目并没有把问题缩小到“只有想家的人”，范围太窄。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目关注的是适应和支持，而不是主张学生不要离家上大学。

public.prompt: What does Kirsty’s description suggest was hardest about her first period at university?

options.text: Managing several new responsibilities at once while trying to fit in socially
Deciding which major subject to choose
Finding a job to cover her tuition costs
Adapting to strict rules set by university staff

evidence.quote:
I was trying to balance you know social success with
academic success whilst learning how to look after myself at quite a young age.

private.explanationZh: Kirsty把自己的处境概括为一种“balancing act”，同时要交朋友、兼顾学业、还要学会独立照顾自己，所以最难的是多重新任务同时压在一起。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确答案，准确概括了她所说的社交、学业和自我照顾三方面压力。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她没有提到专业选择问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有说她当时最主要的问题是打工赚钱。
optionRationalesZh.d: 她谈的是适应新生活，不是学校规章太严格。

public.prompt: According to Dr Ruth Caleb, which skill is extremely important in university life?

options.text: Memorizing lecture notes efficiently
Joining several social clubs quickly
Travelling home frequently on weekends
Budgeting your money carefully

evidence.quote: Budgeting is extremely important in university life.

private.explanationZh: Dr Ruth Caleb在列举洗衣、做饭等生活能力时，特别强调“Budgeting is extremely important”，因此她点名认为极其重要的技能是预算管理。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她讨论的是生活自理能力，不是记笔记技巧。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她没有把加入社团说成“extremely important”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 经常回家不在她给出的准备事项中。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确答案，直接对应原文。

public.prompt: Why does Alice return to the survey results near the end of the program?

options.text: To show that most students regret attending university
To argue that counselling has replaced teaching
To show that student stress is both widespread and often frequent
To explain why only Imperial College students feel anxious

evidence.quote:
The survey, completed by over a thousand
students, also found that almost 70% of those that suffer from stress do so at least once a
week, and 9% of students feel stressed constantly.

private.explanationZh: 结尾再次提到调查，并补充“70%至少每周一次”“9%持续感到压力”，作用是用数据强化前面主题：学生压力并非个别现象，而且出现得很频繁。

optionRationalesZh.a: 调查谈的是压力和心理健康，不是多数人后悔上大学。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有说咨询已经取代教学，这属于无根据夸大。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确答案，准确概括了这组数据在结尾的支撑作用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目引用Imperial College调查是为了说明普遍问题，不是把问题限定在这一所学校。

reasonsZh.bbc-20151210-6min-151210-english-univers: 节目开头直接说明主题是“student mental health”，后面又通过Kirsty和Dr Ruth Caleb讨论学生面临的压力、适应困难、做家务、做饭、budgeting等应对方式，因此主旨是很多学生会面临心理压力以及哪些因素有助于应对。
Kirsty说上大学初期是“a real balancing act”，要同时平衡交朋友、学业成功，以及年纪轻轻就学会照顾自己。最难的是同时处理多项新责任并融入社交环境。
Dr Ruth Caleb明确说“Budgeting is extremely important in university life.” 后面Alice也解释budgeting就是规划自己有多少钱以及怎么花，所以答案唯一明确。
Alice在结尾重提调查结果，不只说有3/4学生经历高压或心理健康问题，还补充近70%的人至少每周一次、9%持续焦虑，用来说明学生压力既普遍又经常发生。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: Why people often misjudge danger and how some people decide risks are worth taking
Why air travel is always more dangerous than driving
Why lottery statistics are impossible for most people to understand
Why extreme athletes should avoid all risky activities

evidence.quote: risk taking is the subject of today's show!
people who enjoy taking big risks – those thrill seekers out there?

private.explanationZh: 节目先总述“risk taking”是主题，随后既讨论人们为何错误评估风险，也讨论thrill seekers为什么主动追求高风险，因此主旨是风险判断与冒险动机两方面。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确答案，能够同时涵盖前半段的风险误判和后半段的主动冒险。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目用飞行和骑车只是举例，不是要证明飞行永远更危险。
optionRationalesZh.c: 彩票概率只是一个例子，不能代表整段的主要焦点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有主张极限运动者应该完全停止冒险。

public.prompt: Why does Andreas Wilkey mention bird-virus outbreaks and flu shots?

options.text: To show that bird flu is the most common danger people face
To illustrate that news about rare disasters can distract people from routine health risks
To argue that nuclear accidents are easier to predict than disease
To recommend avoiding travel to countries with outbreaks

evidence.quote:
Recently we have another increase in these birds' virus outbreaks
in South Korea. People read about that. And they may pay a lot of attention to that in the
news but they may forget to get their flu shot.

private.explanationZh: 这里的例子不是为了讨论韩国本身，而是说明媒体报道会放大罕见、灾难性的威胁感，让人忽略更日常、实际上更该处理的健康风险，比如接种流感疫苗。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有说鸟流感是最常见风险，只说人们会特别关注这类新闻。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确答案，准确概括了该例子的论证作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有比较核事故与疾病哪一个更容易预测。
optionRationalesZh.d: 说话者不是在给旅行建议。

public.prompt: What point does Finn make with his cigarette example?

options.text: Smokers are usually more afraid of flying than of illness
Most dangerous habits disappear when people learn statistics
People often ignore long-term risks because the harm is not immediate
Immediate dangers are always easier to prevent than chronic ones

evidence.quote:
But plenty of people are happy to smoke for years, and put off worrying about the health
risks for the future.

private.explanationZh: Finn拿香烟作对比，是为了说明如果危险不是立刻发生，人们就更容易拖延担忧、继续原来的行为，因此长期风险常被低估。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他没有比较吸烟者对飞行和疾病的恐惧程度。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有说只要懂统计，人们就会放弃危险习惯。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确答案，准确概括了“put off worrying”所体现的心理。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文强调的是人们对长期风险的忽视，不是做出绝对的难易比较。

public.prompt: According to Alice, what are the odds of both winning the national lottery from a single ticket and being killed during a one-mile bicycle journey?

options.text: 1 in 4 million
1 in 40 million
1 in 400 million
1 in 14 million

evidence.quote:
Yes. The odds are actually 1 in 14 million. You are as likely to win the national lottery from
a single ticket as you are to be knocked off your bicycle and killed during a one-mile journey.

private.explanationZh: Alice 在节目后段直接更正了 Finn 的回答，说正确概率是“1 in 14 million”，并说明买彩票中奖与在一英里骑行中被撞死这两件事的概率相同，所以正确答案是 d。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。1 in 4 million 只是前面竞猜题的一个选项，不是 Alice 最后公布的正确答案。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目中没有给出 1 in 40 million，这个数字是干扰项，不受原文支持。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这是 Finn 的作答，但 Alice 随后明确说他答错了。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。Alice 明确说“The odds are actually 1 in 14 million”，与题干所问完全一致。

reasonsZh.bbc-20151217-6min-151217-english-taking-: 节目围绕“why we're bad at assessing risk”展开，讲人们为何害怕小概率灾难、忽视慢性风险，以及像base jumper这类人为什么觉得风险值得，因此主焦点是人们如何误判危险，以及有些人为何仍认为冒险值得。
Andreas Wilkey提到韩国禽流感新闻后说，人们会非常关注这类新闻，却可能忘记去打普通流感疫苗，说明罕见但灾难性的新闻事件会分散人们对日常健康风险的注意。
Finn举香烟的例子时说，如果一抽就立刻致命，没人会抽；但很多人愿意抽很多年，把健康风险推到未来再担心。这里强调人们常忽视不会马上发生、而是长期累积的风险。
Alice揭晓答案时明确说，这两件事发生的几率“actually 1 in 14 million”，因此正确选项是14 million。
题目询问 Alice 明确给出的概率数值。她在公布答案时直接说“The odds are actually 1 in 14 million”，并进一步重述这两个事件的概率相同，因此只有 d 能被文本唯一支持，不存在歧义。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: How acts of Christmas generosity can help people who need support
Why holiday shopping is usually pointless
How Dickens created modern Christmas traditions by himself
Why children should stop believing in Santa Claus

evidence.quote:
the subject of today's show
is Christmas kindness!

private.explanationZh: 节目开头直接点出主题是“Christmas kindness”，后面所有例子都围绕圣诞期间的善意和帮助他人展开，因此主旨是圣诞善举如何帮助有需要的人。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确答案，准确概括了节目主题与后文例证。
optionRationalesZh.b: Neil抱怨购物只是引子，不是整段节目的中心论点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目提到《圣诞颂歌》只是小测验背景，不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有主张孩子不该相信Santa Claus。

public.prompt: Why does the program include Beatrice Cadell’s birthday story?

options.text: To show that older people usually do not want birthday presents
To give an example of turning personal gift-giving into help for needy children
To compare toy banks with hospital charities in detail
To argue that Christmas shopping should be replaced by birthdays

evidence.quote:
The toy bank then distributed
Beatrice's toys to parents who couldn't afford to buy presents for their children at
Christmas.

private.explanationZh: 节目讲Beatrice的故事，是为了给“Christmas kindness”提供一个具体实例：把本来属于自己的生日礼物需求转化成对买不起礼物家庭的帮助。

optionRationalesZh.a: 故事重点不是老年人普遍不想收礼物，而是她主动把善意转向他人。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确答案，准确概括了该故事的功能。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有把toy bank和hospital charity做细致对比。
optionRationalesZh.d: 故事并不是要否定圣诞购物本身。

public.prompt: What do the volunteer’s comments suggest about the kindness project?

options.text: It depends mainly on business donations rather than community effort
It works only when hospitals coordinate every part of it
It unites many different kinds of people around a shared goal
It succeeds because participants all share the same background

evidence.quote:
Lots of different groups of people come together under the banner of kindness – so
religious people come together, corporate people come together, young people, old people
– it seems to be something that stretches across many many many different demographics.

private.explanationZh: 志愿者强调宗教人士、企业人士、年轻人和老年人都参与其中，可推断这个项目的特点是跨越不同群体，以共同的善意目标把大家连接起来。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文列举了很多群体，不是说主要依靠企业捐助。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这段话重点不在医院组织，而在不同人群的共同参与。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确答案，是对“different groups... different demographics”的归纳。
optionRationalesZh.d: 原文恰恰强调背景不同，而不是相同。

public.prompt: According to Alice, what does Scrooge buy the Cratchit family for Christmas dinner?

options.text: A goose
A chicken
A ham
A turkey

evidence.quote: The answer is b) a turkey.

private.explanationZh: Alice在核对问题答案时明确说正确答案是“a turkey”，所以Scrooge买给Cratchit一家的圣诞晚餐主菜是火鸡。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是Neil的错误猜测，不是Alice最后公布的答案。
optionRationalesZh.b: chicken只是原先选项之一，不是正确项。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有提到ham。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确答案，直接对应Alice的核对。

reasonsZh.bbc-20151224-6min-151224-english-christm: 节目主题被Alice直接说成“Christmas kindness”，后文举玩具银行、给医院儿童送礼物、志愿者合作等例子，核心就是圣诞节的善举如何帮助有需要的人。
Beatrice过80岁生日时不要给自己送礼，而是请朋友买玩具，再把玩具捐给toy bank送给买不起圣诞礼物的家庭。这个故事的作用就是举例说明把个人收礼转化为帮助贫困儿童。
志愿者说不同群体的人都在“under the banner of kindness”聚到一起，包括religious、corporate、young、old，Alice随后也解释这是不同社会群体为共同理念联合行动，因此说明该项目把很多不同背景的人团结在共同目标下。
Alice揭晓测验答案时明确说Neil答错了，正确答案是“b) a turkey”，并补充Scrooge买的是“the prize turkey”。在本题四个选项排列里对应字母d。

public.prompt: What is the main idea of the discussion?

options.text: Fashion history proves that men care more about looks than women do
Appearance influences social judgments, but people should value more than looks alone
Physical attractiveness is the best sign of intelligence
Social media has made appearance unimportant in modern life

evidence.quote: The subject of today's show is appearance or the way we look.

Having a belief system or focussing on your values in life or
other attributes or personality characteristics or interests or anything else that's not to do
with appearance is really important.

private.explanationZh: 节目先讨论外表以及外表如何影响他人判断，结尾又强调人不能只按外貌来衡量自己，而应重视价值观、兴趣和人格等其他方面，因此主旨是“外貌重要，但不应只看外貌”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有得出“男性比女性更在意外表”的结论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是正确答案，既包含前半段的社会判断，也包含后半段的价值反思。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目说人们倾向于这样判断，但没有认同这种判断是正确的。
optionRationalesZh.d: Neil提到社交媒体自拍，恰恰说明外貌并未变得不重要。

public.prompt: Why does Alice say peahens prefer male peacocks with many eyespots on their tails?

options.text: Because such a tail may signal strong genes and good health
Because bright tails help peacocks hide from predators
Because female peacocks are trained to prefer unusual colors
Because a large tail makes the male sound more attractive

evidence.quote: one theory is that a male peacock's tail is a sign of his genes.

private.explanationZh: Alice解释说，大尾巴需要很多能量来生长和携带，因此只有最强壮、最健康的雄孔雀才“负担得起”，可推断雌孔雀把它视为基因和健康状况的信号。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这是正确答案，符合Alice后续关于强壮健康雄性的说明。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有说尾巴的作用是隐藏自己。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是无中生有，原文没有任何“训练偏好”的信息。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目提到的是视觉信号，不是声音。

public.prompt: Why does Amber Butchart mention men wearing corsets?

options.text: To argue that modern fashion is less restrictive than any earlier style
To prove that men once cared only about physical beauty
To show that appearance practices also displayed status and were tied to attractiveness
To explain why 18th-century men were expected to speak in higher voices

evidence.quote:
It was possibly concerned with displaying status and rank rather than what we
now define as physical attractiveness but I think the two were very bound up together.

private.explanationZh: Amber提到男性穿束身衣，并不是单纯讲奇闻，而是说明外表修饰既与社会地位展示有关，也和吸引力相互交织，因此这一历史例子服务于更大的论点：外貌与社会评价长期相连。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她没有把古今服饰限制程度做全面比较。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她明确说不仅关乎吸引力，也关乎status和rank，所以“only”错误。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是正确答案，最准确概括了这一例子的说明作用。
optionRationalesZh.d: Neil关于高嗓音只是玩笑，不是Amber举例的目的。

public.prompt: According to the poll Alice cites, which physical feature were men most likely to name as their favourite?

options.text: Their hair
Their face
Their shoulders
Their height

evidence.quote:
men were most likely to mention their
height - 21% of them in fact said it was their favourite physical attribute

private.explanationZh: Alice在公布民调结果时说，男性最可能提到自己最喜欢的外貌特征是height，而且21%的男性这样回答，所以答案是身高。

optionRationalesZh.a: Neil自己选了hair，但Alice说明这不是正确结果。
optionRationalesZh.b: face是题目选项之一，但不是最终公布的答案。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有提到shoulders。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这是正确答案，直接对应民调结果。

reasonsZh.bbc-20151231-6min-151231-english-appeara: 前半段谈人和动物都会根据外表快速判断，甚至认为好看的人更聪明；后半段又通过Philippa Dietrich强调不应只看外表，而应重视价值观、个性和其他特质，因此主旨是外貌影响社会判断，但人不应只以外貌来衡量。
Alice解释说，一种理论认为雄孔雀的大尾巴是其基因的信号，长出并拖着大尾巴需要很多能量，所以只有最强壮、最健康的雄性才能做到，这对应强基因和良好健康状况。
Amber Butchart提到男性过去穿紧身胸衣，不只是为了我们今天理解的外貌吸引力，更和展示“status and rank”有关，而且两者“very bound up together”。所以该例子的作用是说明外貌实践也和地位展示相关。
Alice最后公布民调结果时说，男性最可能提到的最喜欢身体特征是“their height”，并给出21%的数据，因此答案是height，在本题选项中对应d。

public.prompt: What overall point about fairy tales do the speakers mainly develop in this discussion?

options.text: How fairy tales moved from adult stories to children's tales and are now often being retold for older audiences again
How Walt Disney originally created the best-known European fairy tales
Why modern filmmakers should stop changing classic fairy-tale characters
How scholars translate lost Latin fairy tales into modern English

evidence.quote: originally fairy tales were intended for an adult audience.

we're going back to the
inception of fairy stories now with a more adult take on them.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。节目先说明童话最初是成年人讲给成年人听的，后来在维多利亚时期被改造成带有道德教育色彩的儿童故事，如今影视改编又把它们重新拉回更成熟的受众，因此核心是在讨论童话受众与风格的历史变化。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项完整概括了“原本偏成人、后来儿童化、现在又更成人化”的发展脉络。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目明确说迪士尼并没有发明这些故事，只是做了流行改编。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。说话人并没有主张停止改编，而是在分析改编反映出的受众变化。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目提到拉丁语和希腊语的早期书面版本，但重点不是翻译方法。

public.prompt: Why does Neil mention the Grimm version of The Frog Prince?

options.text: To show that Disney kept the oldest version unchanged
To illustrate how much darker early fairy tales could be than familiar children's versions
To prove that children prefer violent stories
To explain why frogs often appear in European legends

evidence.quote: Can you give us some examples of dark stories written by the brothers Grimm, Neil?
In The Frog Prince the princess doesn't kiss the frog

private.explanationZh: 本题考查举例目的。Sophie先要求Neil举出“黑暗”的格林童话例子，Neil随后提到《青蛙王子》里公主并不是亲吻青蛙，而是用更粗暴的方式对待它。这个例子不是在讲情节本身，而是在说明早期版本比后来常见的儿童版本更阴暗。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里不是为了说明迪士尼忠于原版，恰恰是在对比原版和后来较温和版本的差异。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。该例子正是为“早期童话更黑暗”这一论点服务。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说儿童更喜欢暴力故事。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。谈话并没有讨论青蛙在欧洲传说中的象征意义。

public.prompt: According to Dr Jamie Tehrani, what did his research suggest about some famous European fairy tales?

options.text: They were first written by Shakespeare for adult audiences
They were created by the Brothers Grimm in the Victorian era
They may have originated four to six thousand years ago
They disappeared until Hollywood remade them

evidence.quote:
probably sort of 4-6,000 years is the origin of many famous
European folk tales

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。Tehrani直接指出，许多著名欧洲民间故事的源头可以追溯到大约四千到六千年前，所以正确答案是关于这些故事年代非常久远。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Shakespeare只是被当作历史参照，不是这些故事的作者。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目明确说这些故事比格林兄弟和维多利亚时代都早得多。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个选项准确复述了研究结论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目谈的是长期口头传播，而不是故事一度消失。

public.prompt: According to Sophie, why are names like Sneezy and Dopey unsuitable for the newer Snow White film?

options.text: Because the remake had to follow a different copyright agreement
Because the dwarves were removed from the newer film entirely
Because the audience would not recognize the original characters
Because the newer film targets young adults rather than a childish style

evidence.quote: for young adults
too childish.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节理解。Sophie认为新版《白雪公主》面向的是年轻成人，因此像Sneezy、Dopey这类明显偏儿童化的名字不适合这种更成熟、更严肃的风格。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有提到版权安排。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目说的是名字不同，不是矮人角色被删除。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。说话人没有担心观众认不出角色。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原因就是新版受众更偏年轻成人，风格不再那么幼稚。

reasonsZh.bbc-20160317-6min-english-fairy-tales-q0: 讨论先说明童话原本是成人讲给成人听的，维多利亚时期才变成儿童道德故事，后来又出现面向年轻成人的黑暗改编，因此a最完整。
Neil提到《青蛙王子》里公主把青蛙砸向墙，是为了举例说明早期童话比大家熟悉的儿童版本更黑暗、更残酷。
Tehrani明确说许多著名欧洲民间故事的起源“大概是四千到六千年前”，所以c直接对应原文。
Sophie说新版是给young adults看的，是不同的film genre，因此Sneezy、Dopey这类名字显得太childish，d准确概括原因。

public.prompt: What broad point about gardening do the speakers mainly develop in this program?

options.text: Gardening can improve well-being and has benefits that help explain why many people value it
Professional tomato growing is the easiest path to stable employment in Britain
Insects are the main reason people avoid outdoor activity
British offices are designed in ways that reduce stress better than gardens do

evidence.quote: gardening is the subject of today's show!

82% saying it makes them feel
happier.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。整期节目围绕园艺展开，既谈到它让人更快乐、更放松，也谈到土壤细菌实验和戒酒康复中的治疗作用，因此重点是园艺为何能提升幸福感并受到广泛欢迎。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了节目关于园艺与幸福感、疗愈感和吸引力的整体讨论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有教授职业种植或就业路径。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。昆虫只是Neil抱怨园艺体验时的一个细节，不是节目中心。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目对比了花园和办公室偏好，但主旨不是办公室设计。

public.prompt: What result did Dr Christopher Lowry describe in his experiment?

options.text: Mice avoided soil after being exposed to the bacterium
Mice that received a placebo became calmer than the others
Mice injected with the bacterium swam longer than mice given a placebo
Mice lost interest in food after being placed in water

evidence.quote:
These mice that had been injected with bacterium swam during a 6-minute test for a
longer period of time than animals that just got a placebo.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查实验结果细节。Dr Lowry明确说，注射了土壤细菌的小鼠在六分钟测试中比安慰剂组游得更久，这被用来类比抗抑郁效果。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。实验谈的是游泳持续时间，不是是否回避土壤。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。更积极的结果出现在细菌组，而不是安慰剂组。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它准确对应实验结果。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有提到食欲变化。

public.prompt: Why does the program include Scot Stephenson's story?

options.text: To show that gardening is more profitable than other kinds of work
To provide an example of gardening being used in recovery and personal rebuilding
To argue that formal education is unnecessary for gardeners
To explain how British vocational qualifications are graded

evidence.quote:
I left school early or should I say I got expelled... came here, I got my NVQ level 2 which is
my first qualification and enjoyed it ever since.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查举例目的。Scot Stephenson的经历不是为了介绍学历制度，而是具体展示园艺如何帮助康复中的酒精成瘾者重新建立能力、信心和生活方向。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有把重点放在园艺收入上。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这个例子正是为了说明园艺具有治疗性和重建性作用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有否定正规教育，只是描述他后来获得了资格证。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。NVQ只是背景说明，不是举这个例子的主要目的。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the survey results Sophie reports at the end?

options.text: Most people in the UK consider gardening too physically demanding
People enjoy gardening only when they can do it professionally
Office workers in the UK rarely spend time outdoors
Gardening is widely linked with happiness and is appealing enough that many people would choose it over office work

evidence.quote:
82% saying it makes them feel
happier.
spend their working day in the garden

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。高比例的人表示园艺让他们更快乐，而且很多人如果可以选择，宁愿在花园里度过工作日而不是待在办公室，这说明园艺不仅与幸福感联系紧密，而且对很多人具有现实吸引力。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。数据反而显示多数人对园艺持积极态度。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。调查谈的是一般人的感受，不是职业园丁。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有得出“办公室人群很少户外活动”的结论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它综合了“更快乐”和“宁愿在花园工作”两层信息。

reasonsZh.bbc-20160324-6min-english-gardening-q01: 节目围绕园艺为何让人更快乐展开，涉及放松、土壤细菌带来的积极影响，以及对康复治疗的帮助，因此a最符合主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20160324-6min-english-gardening-q02: Lowry说注射土壤细菌的老鼠在六分钟测试中比注射安慰剂的老鼠游得更久，节目把这解释为类似抗抑郁效果，所以选c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20160324-6min-english-gardening-q03: Scot Stephenson的故事被用来说明园艺可以用于治疗和戒瘾恢复，帮助人成长重建生活，而不是讨论收益或学历。
reasonsZh.bbc-20160324-6min-english-gardening-q04: 结尾数据说82%的人因园艺更快乐，70%若能选择宁愿在花园工作而不是办公室，因此可推知园艺与幸福感广泛相关，而且很有吸引力。

public.prompt: What central issue about social media and devices do the speakers mainly explore?

options.text: How online dictionaries decide whether a new word should be accepted
How phone use can interrupt face-to-face interaction but may also reshape human connection
Why college students should stop bringing phones to dinner
How train passengers use tablets more politely than cafe customers

evidence.quote: social media and its impact on our daily lives.
there were a couple sharing a tablet.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。节目先批评设备打断面对面交流，随后又补充设备有时也能创造新的亲密互动，所以核心不是单纯谴责科技，而是讨论设备如何一方面干扰交流、另一方面重塑连接方式。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。词典是否收词只是结尾的一个小点，不是主线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它同时覆盖了“打断交流”和“形成新连接”两方面。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。学生晚餐礼仪只是Turkle例子中的一部分，不是整期节目目标。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有在比较火车乘客和咖啡馆顾客谁更礼貌。

public.prompt: According to Professor Sherry Turkle's rule of three, when is it acceptable to look down at your phone during dinner?

options.text: When at least three people in the group are already looking up in the conversation
When everyone at the table has finished eating
When the person next to you checks a message first
When you are sitting with fewer than three friends

evidence.quote:
you don't want to look down at your phone until you see that three
people, let's say you're six at dinner, are looking up in the conversation.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。Turkle解释的“rule of three”是：在聚餐时，只有当你看到有三个人已经没有参与当前谈话时，你才去看手机，这是一种新的社交礼仪。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这个选项准确复述了规则内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。规则与是否吃完饭无关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。原文没有说只要旁边的人先看手机就可以。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。人数少于三并不是规则条件，Neil只是拿这个规则开玩笑。

public.prompt: Why does Neil describe the couple sharing a tablet on the train?

options.text: To prove that tablets are more useful than smartphones
To show that train travel encourages deeper conversations than cafes do
To give a counterexample suggesting that devices can sometimes support intimacy rather than destroy it
To explain why public transport should offer better internet access

evidence.quote:
there were a couple sharing a tablet. They were
looking at the screen, and talking about what they saw there.
It was very intimate, and they

private.explanationZh: 本题考查举例目的。前面Sophie讲的是设备破坏面对面交流，Neil接着举火车上一对共享平板的情侣作反例，说明设备并不总是削弱关系，有时反而会支持一种新的亲密互动。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Neil不是在比较设备功能强弱。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。重点不在交通工具与咖啡馆环境的优劣比较。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这个例子就是为了反驳“设备只会破坏关系”的单一看法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有讨论公共交通网络服务。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Sophie's comment that phubbing is appearing in some online dictionaries?

options.text: The behavior has already disappeared from everyday life
The word was invented by professional lexicographers
Only academic researchers use the term seriously
The behavior is common or noticeable enough for the term to gain wider recognition

evidence.quote:
'phubbing' is starting to
appear in some online dictionaries.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。一个新词开始进入网络词典，说明它所描述的现象已经有了一定传播度和社会关注度，不再只是极少数人的临时说法。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。进入词典更像是现象被注意到，而不是消失。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目只解释了构词来源，并没有说词典编纂者创造了这个词。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。它出现在网络词典中，反而说明它正在走向更广泛使用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这是最合理的社会语言学推断。

reasonsZh.bbc-20160331-6min-english-social-media-q: 节目一方面批评手机打断面对面交流，另一方面又讨论设备可能带来新的亲密连接方式，因此b最能概括整体讨论。
Sherry Turkle说明rule of three是：和朋友吃饭时，只有当三个人都“looking up in the conversation”时，你才看手机，所以a正确。
Neil举火车上情侣共看平板并交流的例子，是为了反驳设备只会破坏关系的单一看法，说明设备有时也能支持亲密互动。
Sophie说phubbing开始出现在一些网络词典里，这可推知这种行为和这个词已经常见到足以被更广泛识别和记录，因此d最佳。

public.prompt: What main idea about brain training do the speakers develop in this program?

options.text: Activities such as language learning and other mental exercises may strengthen or protect the brain
Scientists disagree about whether the brain contains neurons
Chocolate is the most effective treatment for memory loss
Right-handed people should avoid using their left hand

evidence.quote:
learning a second
language can boost brainpower?
a form of brain training.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。节目围绕brain training展开，介绍学语言、做认知练习、建立cognitive reserve等做法如何增强或保护大脑功能，因此A最能概括全篇。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它覆盖了节目最核心的主题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。神经元数量只是一个小测验点，不是节目主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目只是顺带提到巧克力可能有帮助，并没有说它是最有效治疗。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。用非惯用手只是众多训练方式之一，不是核心结论。

public.prompt: According to Dr Catherine Loveday, why might bilingualism help protect the brain after a stroke?

options.text: Because it increases the total number of neurons in the brain
Because switching between languages exercises executive functions and makes those systems stronger
Because bilingual people never experience interruptions in blood flow to the brain
Because speaking two languages automatically improves physical fitness

evidence.quote:
protective factor is that [it] involves a
lot of switchings
stronger and fitter when it comes to resisting some kind of damage from the stroke.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。Dr Loveday的解释是，双语者不断在语言间切换，会锻炼执行功能相关的大脑部分，使其更强健，因此更能抵抗中风造成的损伤。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说双语会增加神经元总数。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了“频繁切换语言会锻炼执行功能，从而增强抵抗力”的逻辑。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。双语并不能阻止中风发生。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里讨论的是脑功能，不是身体体能。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Dr Loveday's comment about taking up languages or other activities later in life?

options.text: Only people who start in childhood can build cognitive reserve
Late learners improve only if they study every day for many hours
Starting mentally stimulating activities later can still provide some protection for the brain
Learning music is useful, but language learning later in life is not

evidence.quote: it will give them a degree of protection.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。Loveday明确表示，即使人在较晚阶段才开始学习语言或其他活动，也仍然会得到一定程度的保护，所以可以推断“晚开始仍然有价值”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。原文正好反驳这种“必须从小开始”的看法。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有给出这种强度要求。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这是对原话最直接、最合理的推断。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。原文把语言和其他活动并列看作有帮助的刺激。

public.prompt: Why does Neil end by saying he might memorize another shopping list in Japanese?

options.text: To show that he has decided to become fully bilingual immediately
To admit that the earlier quiz question about neurons was unfair
To reject Sophie's suggestions about brain training
To playfully combine the program's advice about memory practice and language learning

evidence.quote: this time in Japanese.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查结尾话语的作用。Neil把“记购物清单”和“学日语”这两个前面提到的训练方式合并成一句玩笑式总结，作用是轻松地呼应节目内容，而不是突然做出严肃决定。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里是轻松调侃，不是宣布立刻达到双语水平。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。与小测验是否公平无关。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。Neil并没有拒绝建议，反而是在顺着节目内容开玩笑。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它把两种脑力训练建议巧妙结合了起来。

reasonsZh.bbc-20160407-6min-english-brain-training: 节目核心是在讨论语言学习、换手做事、记忆训练等脑力活动如何刺激大脑并建立认知储备，从而增强或保护大脑，所以选a。
Loveday解释说双语者不断在语言之间切换，会训练executive functions，使这些系统更强，因此在中风后更能抵抗损伤。
Loveday明确说即使人们在晚年才开始学语言或其他活动，也会得到一定程度的保护，因此可推断晚开始仍然有帮助。
Neil最后说要用日语再记一个购物清单，是在开玩笑地把节目提出的两类建议结合起来：记忆练习加语言学习。

public.prompt: What main point about groupthink do the speakers emphasize in this discussion?

options.text: Fairy tales are useful tools for teaching children honesty
Group pressure can lead people to support views they do not genuinely believe
Decaffeinated coffee is becoming more popular in workplaces
People can avoid bad decisions simply by becoming stronger leaders

evidence.quote: Groupthink refers to the type of bad decisions we make when we are in a group.
submit to the will of the group.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查主旨。节目从Neil在会议上附和别人讲起，随后用心理学实验和社交媒体例子说明，群体压力会让人违背自己的真实判断去从众。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。童话故事只在结尾作为辅助例子出现，不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了“群体压力导致从众”的核心议题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。无咖啡因咖啡只是开场引子。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目重点不是个人领导力训练。

public.prompt: What happened in Solomon Asch's conformity experiment, as Sophie describes it?

options.text: People gave a wrong answer about an obvious line-length match because of group pressure
People refused to answer once they realized the test was unfair
Most participants secretly changed the cards before the test began
The participants were asked to explain fairy tales rather than compare lines

evidence.quote:
The right answer was plain to see, but the participants felt pressurized
into saying the wrong answer.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查细节。Sophie说明实验中正确答案其实很明显，但参与者因为群体压力而说出了错误答案，这正是从众现象的经典展示。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了实验发生的事情。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。参与者并不是因为觉得不公平而拒答。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有提到有人改动卡片。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。实验内容是比较线段长度，不是讲童话。

public.prompt: What concern do the speakers raise about social media?

options.text: It makes people less interested in reading fiction
It prevents unpopular opinions from ever being posted online
It can turn popularity into pressure, pushing people to conform and attack outsiders
It mainly causes confusion because users cannot understand trends

evidence.quote:
people adopt it simply because it's popular, not because they
really believe it.

using our
individual nonconformity to create a more conformist world.

private.explanationZh: 本题考查推断。Neil先说推特上的观点一旦流行就会凭借势头被人接受，Jon Ronson又指出，人们会以“非从众”的名义制造更从众的世界，因此可以推断说话人担心社交媒体把流行变成压力，并伤害边缘者。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有谈阅读兴趣。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目担心的是从众压力，不是技术上无法发帖。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它综合了“因流行而接受观点”和“群体围攻异见者”两层含义。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。重点不是“看不懂趋势”，而是趋势带来的社会压力。

public.prompt: Why does Neil bring up The Emperor's New Clothes at the end of the program?

options.text: To show that fairy tales are more persuasive than psychology experiments
To argue that children are naturally better at politics than adults
To explain why people enjoy stories about invisible clothing
To provide a familiar example of someone telling the truth when everyone else is conforming to an obvious falsehood

evidence.quote:
No one dares to say that he doesn't see
any suit of clothes
a child cries out

private.explanationZh: 本题考查举例目的。《皇帝的新装》中的孩子在所有人都附和谎言时说出真相，这个故事正好呼应节目关于群体压力与独立判断的主题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有说童话比心理实验更有说服力。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里强调的是说真话的行为，不是儿童更懂政治。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。重点不在故事情节的趣味性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个故事被用来形象说明“在群体从众中仍敢讲真话”的意义。

reasonsZh.bbc-20160414-6min-english-groupthink-q01: 节目反复强调group pressure会让人违背自己真实想法，去附和群体甚至明显错误的意见，因此b就是主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20160414-6min-english-groupthink-q02: Sophie描述Asch实验时说，参与者明知哪条线才匹配，却因为他人都给错答案而在压力下跟着答错，所以a正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20160414-6min-english-groupthink-q03: 他们担心社交媒体上的观点一旦流行就会形成动能，促使人们因流行而从众，并一起攻击圈外人，因此c最贴切。
reasonsZh.bbc-20160414-6min-english-groupthink-q04: Neil提《皇帝的新装》是为了给groupthink提供一个熟悉的类比：大家都顺从明显的谎言，只有一个人说出真相。

public.prompt: What is the discussion mainly about?

options.text: How physical injuries prevent professional writers from working
Writer's block as a creativity problem and several ways people explain inspiration
Why Dan Brown's exercise habits are typical of modern authors
How ancient poetry became more important than science

evidence.quote:
Creative juices means a
flow of ideas and the subject of today's show is creativity and writer's block – which
means not being able to write because of a psychological problem.

private.explanationZh: 开头直接点明本期讨论的主题是“创造力”和“写作障碍”。后面的内容也一直围绕人们如何理解灵感、障碍和创造性思维展开，所以主旨不是某一个例子或某个人，而是对写作障碍与创造力的整体讨论。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目开头还专门区分了心理问题和像网球肘那样的身体问题，重点不是身体受伤。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。题干问主旨，这一项准确概括了节目从写作障碍谈到缪斯、漫想和多巴胺等不同解释路径。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。Dan Brown 只是节目里的一个小测验例子，不是整段讨论的中心。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目提到古希腊缪斯是为了说明一种关于灵感的理解，不是在比较诗歌和科学的重要性。

public.prompt: Why does Alice mention the Ancient Greek Muses?

options.text: To prove that modern singers are treated like gods
To show that psychological treatment began in Greece
To introduce an older way of thinking about creative inspiration
To explain why Neil wanted to stop writing poems

evidence.quote:
in Ancient Greece, there were nine Muses – and depending on what type of creative thing
you did – philosophy, poetry, science and so on – you invoked – or called upon – that
particular Muse to inspire you.

private.explanationZh: 这里提到缪斯，不是为了考历史事实，而是为了引出一种较早的创造力观念：灵感仿佛来自外部启发，创作者需要“召唤”它。这与节目后面谈到漫想、开放心态等内容形成呼应。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目中提到 Beyoncé 只是开玩笑式过渡，不是在论证现代歌手被神化。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这里没有讨论心理治疗起源，而是在谈灵感来源的观念。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。缪斯的例子用于说明古人如何理解创作灵感，这是这段话的功能。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。Neil 是否继续写诗不是这里的重点，缪斯也不是为了解释他的个人决定。

public.prompt: According to Zachary Leader, how is being blocked different from drying up?

options.text: It treats the problem as an external obstacle that might be removed
It means a writer has permanently lost all creative ability
It suggests outside critics are the main cause of the problem
It refers to a physical illness rather than a metaphor

evidence.quote:
And the difference between being
blocked and drying up is that in the case of blockage the problem is externalised and
objectified – it's not yourself that's the problem – it's something that's outside you like an
obstacle or an impediment – something that you could really cut away, and as a
consequence a cure like a growth or a foreign body.

private.explanationZh: 教授明确说，所谓 blockage 的关键在于把问题外化成一个障碍物，好像它在你外面，可以被移除；而 drying up 则不是这种外部障碍的比喻。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。原文直接说 blockage 把问题 externalised and objectified，像外部障碍一样。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。文中没有说是永久失去创造力，只是在解释这个比喻如何构造问题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。这里没有把评论家或他人评价说成主要原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。整段都在谈比喻和心理解释，不是身体疾病。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from the final discussion of dopamine and the thalamus?

options.text: Creativity depends mainly on exercise and listening to music
Anyone with more dopamine will certainly become an artist
Mind wandering usually prevents useful thinking
Some people may stay more open to new ideas because their brains work differently

evidence.quote:
having more dopamine in the thalamus enables some people to see the world in a
different way – and they express this creatively – through science, music, the arts.

private.explanationZh: 原文并没有把创造力说成必然结果，而是说某些人大脑运作方式不同，因此更容易以不同方式看世界，并以创造性形式表达出来，所以合理推断是：这种脑部差异可能让人更开放、更容易产生新想法。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。运动和音乐只在前面的测验语境里出现，不是最后科学解释的核心结论。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文说 enables some people，并非绝对保证所有这类人都会成为艺术家。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。前文把 musing 描述为对新想法开放，不是无用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 对。这一项准确概括了“大脑差异与开放新想法之间可能有关联”的推断。

reasonsZh.bbc-20160811-6min-english-writers-block-: 节目开头直接说明主题是创造力与写作障碍，后面又讨论缪斯、走神和多巴胺等对灵感的解释，因此主旨是写作障碍这一创造力问题及人们如何理解灵感。
Alice提到古希腊缪斯后，马上解释古人会向不同缪斯祈求灵感，并引出关于缪斯象征意义的专家讲话，所以这里的作用是引入一种更早的关于创作灵感的理解方式。
Zachary Leader明确说，blocked与drying up的区别在于，blockage把问题外化为你之外的障碍或阻碍物，仿佛可以切除，因此a是对原意的准确转述。
结尾部分说，有些人更善于走神和接受新想法，因为他们的大脑工作方式不同，丘脑中的多巴胺更多，因此可推知某些人会因为大脑差异而更开放地接受新观点。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the discussion?

options.text: To argue that shoppers should ignore labels and trust their instincts
To explain why exercise-equivalent labels may help people make better food choices
To describe how supermarkets set pizza prices
To compare the health benefits of walking, running, and cycling

evidence.quote:
Writing in the
British Medical Journal, she argues that food should be labelled with the equivalent exercise
needed to burn off its calories. This would give consumers an immediate link between
foods' energy content and physical activity that might help to reduce obesity.

private.explanationZh: 节目核心不是单纯谈某种食物，而是讨论一种新的食品标识办法：把食物热量与需要消耗这些热量的运动时间联系起来，从而帮助消费者更直观地做决定。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目恰恰强调要帮助消费者更了解信息，而不是忽视标签。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。结尾总结明确指出，这种标签能把热量和运动联系起来，帮助做选择并可能减少肥胖。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有讨论超市如何定价。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。运动方式只是辅助语境，讨论重点是标签信息如何影响选择。

public.prompt: Why does the program include comments from people in London?

options.text: To show that some consumers might reconsider snacks if labels showed the exercise needed
To prove that Londoners prefer crisps to pizza
To demonstrate that most consumers already understand calorie labels
To show that exercise labels are unpopular with every shopper

evidence.quote:
Two people who would think twice about whether to buy crisps if they knew how long it
would take to burn off the calories.

private.explanationZh: 插入街头采访的作用是把抽象政策效果具体化：听众可以看到，如果包装上直接显示需要跑多久或走多久来消耗热量，有些人确实会重新考虑是否购买零食。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。两位受访者都表示看到运动时间后会“再想一想”，这正说明标签可能改变选择。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。受访者谈的是是否会重新考虑购买，不是在比较薯片和披萨偏好。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目之前还说人们不擅长估算热量，并非已经普遍理解。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。采访里没有显示所有人都反对，反而有人认为有帮助。

public.prompt: According to Alice, what are the current "traffic lights" on food packaging?

options.text: A list of ingredients ranked by weight
A symbol used only on unhealthy products
A color-coded guide to fat, sugar, and salt levels
A chart showing the prices of similar foods

evidence.quote:
It's where food content is colour-coded red, orange or green – like traffic lights –
depending on its percentage of fat, sugar, and salt. So you can see at a glance which pizza on
the supermarket shelf is better for you.

private.explanationZh: Alice 对 traffic lights 的解释很直接：它是把脂肪、糖和盐的含量用红橙绿三色标出来，让消费者一眼判断相对健康程度。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。这里没有说按重量排列配料表。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。traffic lights 不是只给不健康食品使用，而是一种信息呈现方式。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。原文明确说是 red, orange or green，并依据 fat, sugar, and salt 的比例。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有把它描述成价格比较图。

public.prompt: What point does Neil make with his example of diet cola and fresh orange juice?

options.text: Foods with more calories are always less healthy
Diet cola is healthier because it requires no exercise to burn off
People should stop paying attention to calories completely
Calorie information matters, but it does not fully determine how healthy a food is

evidence.quote:
a healthy diet isn't just about calories is it? I wouldn't need to cycle to work to burn off
a diet cola, but drinking a fresh orange juice would be a healthier choice – even though it
contains more calories.

private.explanationZh: Neil 的例子是在提醒听众：热量和健康之间不是简单画等号。某样东西热量更低，不代表它整体更健康，所以标签有用，但不能替代更全面的营养判断。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。Neil 恰恰用 orange juice 反驳了“热量越高越不健康”的简单看法。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文没有说 diet cola 更健康，只说它更容易“烧掉”热量。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目不是否定热量信息，而是强调不能只看热量。
optionRationalesZh.d: 对。这一项准确概括了 Neil 用这个对比想表达的限制性观点。

reasonsZh.bbc-20160818-6min-english-food-exercise-: 节目围绕食品包装上的运动等值标签展开，先谈现有标识，再谈伦敦路人的反应和公共卫生负责人的支持理由，因此主旨是解释这种标签为何可能帮助消费者作出更好的饮食选择。
节目插入伦敦路人的评论后，Alice总结说这两个人如果知道燃烧薯片热量需要跑多久，就会重新考虑是否购买，因此这些评论是为了显示部分消费者可能因此三思。
Alice明确解释traffic lights是把脂肪、糖和盐的含量用红橙绿三色编码，让消费者一眼看出哪种食物更健康，所以答案是颜色编码指南。
Neil说健不健康不只是看卡路里，健怡可乐虽然更容易“消耗掉”，但鲜橙汁尽管热量更高却更健康，说明热量信息重要，但不能单独决定食物是否健康。

public.prompt: What issue does the discussion mainly address?

options.text: Why online surveys are unreliable measures of housework
How unequal domestic chores can affect work opportunities and financial security
Why cooking is the most important home skill for men
How researchers measure dirt and untidiness in families

evidence.quote:
I hope you do, Neil, because a bit more give and take at home could help improve a
working mum's prospects of getting promoted at work and earning more.

both partners –
whether male or female – should protect their earnings capacity.

private.explanationZh: 节目不只是在抱怨谁做家务更多，而是把家务分工与职业晋升、收入能力和长期经济风险联系起来。两处证据一起说明，核心问题是家务不平等会影响工作机会与经济安全。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。网络调查只是引入话题的背景，不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。节目从家务分工一路谈到晋升、收入能力和关系破裂后的风险，这正是主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有把做饭说成最重要技能，也不是专门讨论男性厨艺。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。threshold 和脏乱只是在开头趣味引子，不是节目主要议题。

public.prompt: According to Sarah, why does Geoff do more chores in their household?

options.text: Because Geoff prefers housework to paid work
Because Sarah thinks men are naturally better at chores
Because Sarah finds chores boring and says she has less time
Because Geoff stays home full time with the children

evidence.quote:
The thing is, it’s a boring thing. You should just do the minimum amount you need to do
to get by. That is my policy. I would say I definitely work harder than you do, and therefore
have less time.

private.explanationZh: Sarah 给出的理由有两个：她觉得家务无聊，而且她认为自己工作更辛苦、时间更少。因此 Geoff 做更多家务。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。Sarah 没有说 Geoff 更喜欢家务，只是说自己更没时间。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。文中没有提出“男人更擅长家务”这种观点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。原文直接说明她嫌家务无聊，并且觉得自己工作更多、时间更少。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有说 Geoff 是全职在家带孩子。

public.prompt: Why does Alice list examples such as laundry, school organization, rubbish, and fixing a tap?

options.text: To illustrate how some household tasks are still divided along gender lines
To show that women never take out the rubbish
To argue that laundry requires more skill than repairs
To prove that Neil already shares chores equally

evidence.quote:
Some domestic tasks
can be strongly gendered – or specific to one sex. For example, doing the laundry,
organizing your children's school and social lives are often jobs that women do.

private.explanationZh: 这些例子不是随便列举，而是为了让“gendered”这个抽象概念具体化，说明很多家务仍然被社会习惯性地分配给某一性别。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。举例的功能就是把“家务具有性别化分工”这一点说清楚。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。Alice 还说自己家里就是她倒垃圾，这恰好否定了绝对化说法。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。这里不是在比较不同家务的技术难度。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。Neil 反而承认自己在家务上做得不够。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Professor Gershuny's comments?

options.text: Marriages usually fail when both partners work long hours
Only women need to protect their earning capacity
Housework matters mainly because it makes homes cleaner
Depending entirely on one partner's career can leave the other financially vulnerable

evidence.quote:
if under these circumstances
you've been out of the labour force looking after the kids while he builds up his earnings
capacity by working long hours at work and then he runs off with his secretary, well he
departs with his earnings power, and you're left with the baby.

private.explanationZh: 教授的例子强调的是风险分配：如果一个人为了家庭退出劳动力市场，而另一个人持续积累收入能力，一旦关系破裂，前者就会处于明显不利的位置。因此可以推断，不能把经济能力完全押在伴侣一方身上。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。教授并没有把婚姻失败归因于双方都工作太久。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目后面明确说无论男女，双方都应保护自己的 earning capacity。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。这里讨论的是经济后果，不是清洁效果。
optionRationalesZh.d: 对。这是对该例子最合理的概括性推断。

reasonsZh.bbc-20160825-6min-english-domestic-chore: 节目从家务分工谈起，但核心进一步转到女性做更多家务带来的严肃后果，如影响晋升、收入能力和离婚后的经济处境，因此主旨是家务不平等如何影响工作机会与经济安全。
Sarah说家务很无聊，只该做最低限度；她还说自己工作更努力，因此时间更少。随后Neil总结说Geoff做更多家务，是因为Sarah觉得家务无聊，而且她做更多有薪工作。
Alice列举洗衣、安排孩子学校和社交事务，以及倒垃圾、修水龙头，是为了说明某些家务仍然具有明显的性别分工特征，并非为了比较技能高低。
Gershuny指出，如果一方长期退出劳动力市场照顾孩子，而另一方积累赚钱能力，婚姻破裂时前者会处于不利地位，因此可推知完全依赖伴侣的事业会让自己在经济上变得脆弱。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: How police in London created church-based language rules
What slang is as group language and how speakers use it in real contexts
Why rude words are the most useful part of informal English
How formal English has remained unchanged for centuries

evidence.quote:
Slang – or informal language used by a particular group – is the subject of
today's show, and I was just demonstrating a couple of slang words that mean 'money'.
Dosh is a general term for 'money' and a smacker is a British pound or US dollar.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就定义了 slang，并马上用 money 的例子来展示它在真实交流中的用法，所以主旨是介绍俚语这种群体语言及其使用方式，而不是礼貌规范或历史细节本身。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目提到 Cockney Rhyming Slang 与警察的关系只是其中一个历史例子。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。定义加示例正好说明节目核心是在解释 slang 是什么、怎么被人使用。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目还特别区分了 slang 和 profanity，不是在鼓励粗话。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目讨论的正是语言变化与多样性，而不是“不变”。

public.prompt: According to the explanation of Cockney Rhyming Slang, how does it disguise a word like "money"?

options.text: It keeps only the first word from a longer rhyming phrase
It reverses the sounds of the hidden word
It replaces the word with a professional term
It adds a humorous suffix to the original word

evidence.quote:
Cockney Rhyming Slang uses just the first word of a phrase that
rhymes with a word we're trying to disguise. So 'money' becomes 'bread and honey' but we
just say 'bread'.

private.explanationZh: 节目最后明确解释了其机制：先找一个押韵短语，再只说短语的第一个词。因此 money 对应 bread and honey，实际只说 bread。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。原文就是这样解释的，是最直接的细节信息。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。这里不是音位倒置或倒读。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。professional term 属于 jargon 的范围，不是 Cockney Rhyming Slang 的构成方式。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。文中没有说要给原词加后缀。

public.prompt: Why does Neil list many expressions for drinking and being drunk?

options.text: To prove that alcohol is the main cause of language change
To criticize people who use humorous expressions
To demonstrate how inventive and abundant slang can be in one topic area
To show that all slang is acceptable in formal situations

evidence.quote:
there are hundreds of slang expressions to talk about drink and being drunk: 'on the
sauce', 'in your cups', 'half cut', 'hammered', 'squiffy', 'tipsy', 'wasted', 'legless', and many many
more that are far too rude to mention in this programme.

private.explanationZh: 这一长串例子不是为了让听众记单词，而是为了展示俚语在某一语义领域里可以非常丰富、活泼、富于创造性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目没有把饮酒说成语言变化的主因。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。列举这些表达的语气是说明和展示，不是批评。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。大量例子正是为了体现俚语的丰富性和创造性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目还特别说这些词在正式场合不一定合适。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers turn to jargon near the end of the program?

options.text: To replace the earlier topic with an unrelated discussion
To compare slang with another kind of group-specific language
To argue that jargon is more offensive than profanity
To show that radio presenters should avoid technical vocabulary

evidence.quote:
That's the idea, though – jargon is the technical language belonging to a specific group. And
to outsiders this jargon is often hard to understand.

private.explanationZh: 节目在后段转到 jargon，是为了把它和 slang 放在一起比较：两者都跟群体内部语言有关，但 jargon 更偏职业或专业场域。这个转折帮助听众区分相近概念。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。jargon 与前面的 slang 主题相关，并非无关话题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。这里的功能就是引入另一类群体语言，形成比较。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目把 profanity 定义为冒犯性语言，并没有说 jargon 更冒犯。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。后面 Neil 甚至开玩笑地使用了广播术语，并不是主张完全避免技术词汇。

reasonsZh.bbc-20160901-6min-english-slang-q01: 节目先定义slang是某一群体使用的非正式语言，随后讨论Cockney押韵俚语、俚语的社会功能、酒类表达以及jargon，因此核心是俚语作为群体语言以及人们如何在真实语境中使用它。
reasonsZh.bbc-20160901-6min-english-slang-q02: 节目最后直接解释，Cockney Rhyming Slang会先用一个与目标词押韵的短语，再只保留这个短语的第一个词，例如money变成bread and honey，再简称bread。
reasonsZh.bbc-20160901-6min-english-slang-q03: 在Alice说人们谈喝酒时很有创造力后，Neil一口气列出许多“喝醉”的俚语，明显是用实例展示同一语义领域里俚语既丰富又有创造性。
reasonsZh.bbc-20160901-6min-english-slang-q04: 节目在谈完俚语和脏话后转到jargon，并说明它也是某一个群体的内部语言，只是更偏职业和专业环境，因此这里是把俚语与另一种群体专属语言进行比较。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion?

options.text: Why digital media disappeared after the 1990s
Why older technologies still attract people for emotional and practical reasons
How nuclear computers produce better sound than vinyl
How to start a DVD rental business in the UK

evidence.quote:
There's been a resurgence – or new rise in –
record sales recently – here in the UK, and in the US. Some DJs have gone back to using
them, and new record stores have opened to meet the demand for vinyl.

We've survived because of the experience of coming into this store. Um… It's a very
human experience. It's human interaction. People come here because they want to talk to
somebody who's really knowledgeable about film. They want to have a recommendation or
a conversation about the films they just watch. You know, they really want to discuss those
things.

private.explanationZh: 两段证据合起来说明旧技术并未简单消失：一方面，黑胶复兴显示人们对旧媒介仍有兴趣；另一方面，DVD 租赁店的存活说明旧技术还承载着人际交流和体验价值。所以节目主旨是解释旧技术为何仍然有吸引力，而且原因既有情感也有实际层面。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。节目不是说数字媒体消失了，反而默认其已经普及。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对。黑胶复兴、五寸软盘系统和影碟店案例都在说明旧技术仍有多重吸引力。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有把核武系统和音质进行这种荒谬比较。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。影碟店只是一个案例，不是创业指南。

public.prompt: According to Jacqueline Springer, what did cassette tapes help people do?

options.text: Preserve government data more safely than computers
Avoid influence from parents and siblings
Shape their own identity by choosing and sharing music
Replace records because tapes always sounded better

evidence.quote:
They were audio love letters. They were ways in which you started to carve out your own
identity

private.explanationZh: Springer 把磁带称为“音频情书”，并说人们借此开始塑造自己的身份认同。也就是说，选择、整理和分享音乐本身就是自我定义的一部分。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。政府数据储存对应的是后面五寸软盘的例子，不是磁带。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。她反而说人们会从兄弟姐妹或父母那里获得影响，再自己筛选。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对。carve out your own identity 直接支持这一项。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。她没有说磁带总是音质更好，也没有把重点放在技术替代上。

public.prompt: Why does Neil mention the Pentagon's use of floppy disks?

options.text: To show that some old technology survives because replacing it is costly even if it seems outdated
To prove that floppy disks store more data than memory sticks
To argue that nostalgia is the only reason people keep old devices
To show that the Pentagon sells vintage computers to music fans

evidence.quote:
Well, a Pentagon spokesperson said it would be extremely expensive to update the system
and it still works.

private.explanationZh: 这个例子的重要性在于，它把“旧技术继续存在”的原因从怀旧扩展到现实考量：如果升级成本很高而旧系统还能运作，机构就可能继续使用旧技术。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对。原文明确给出的两个原因就是“升级太贵”和“系统仍然能用”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。节目恰恰说要存同样的数据需要很多软盘，不是说软盘更强。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。这个例子明显不是怀旧，而是成本和功能问题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 不对。节目没有提到出售设备给音乐迷。

public.prompt: What can be inferred from Tara Judah's explanation of why the DVD rental store survives?

options.text: DVD stores survive mainly by matching the speed of streaming services
Customers visit rental stores mostly to avoid talking to staff
Old media matter only to people who dislike the internet
Some people still value knowledgeable human interaction that digital services do not fully replace

evidence.quote:
We've survived because of the experience of coming into this store. Um… It's a very
human experience. It's human interaction. People come here because they want to talk to
somebody who's really knowledgeable about film. They want to have a recommendation or
a conversation about the films they just watch. You know, they really want to discuss those
things.

private.explanationZh: Tara Judah 强调的不是效率，而是人与人之间的交流、推荐和讨论。这说明即使数字服务很方便，仍有一些体验价值是线下、有人参与的环境更容易提供的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 不对。她说的优势不是速度，而是 human interaction。
optionRationalesZh.b: 不对。原文正好相反，顾客来这里就是想和懂电影的人交流。
optionRationalesZh.c: 不对。节目没有把顾客说成讨厌互联网，而是说明他们重视另一种体验。
optionRationalesZh.d: 对。这是对她整段说明最合理的概括性推断。

reasonsZh.bbc-20160908-6min-english-old-tech-q01: 节目讨论黑胶、磁带、软盘和DVD租赁店，既谈怀旧和身份认同，也谈更新成本高、人际互动体验等现实原因，因此主旨是旧技术为何仍因情感与实用原因吸引人。
reasonsZh.bbc-20160908-6min-english-old-tech-q02: Jacqueline Springer说磁带像“音频情书”，人们借此挑选音乐、形成自己的身份，Alice也总结说这一过程帮助塑造音乐和社会身份，因此c最符合原文。
reasonsZh.bbc-20160908-6min-english-old-tech-q03: Neil提到五角大楼仍使用软盘后，紧接着解释发言人说更新系统会非常昂贵，而且现有系统仍能运作，所以这个例子是为了说明有些旧技术即便过时也会因为替换成本高而继续存在。
reasonsZh.bbc-20160908-6min-english-old-tech-q04: Tara Judah说DVD店能存活，是因为顾客重视进店体验、人与人的互动，以及与懂电影的人交流和获得推荐。由此可推知，有些人仍重视数字服务无法完全替代的专业化人际交流。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the discussion?

options.text: To explain how penicillin transformed medicine and why resistance now threatens its usefulness
To describe the exact laboratory steps Fleming used to manufacture penicillin
To argue that handwashing has replaced antibiotics in modern treatment
To compare cancer drugs with antibiotics to show which is more dangerous

evidence.quote:
it's also essentially paved the way for
modern medicine, modern interventional medicine that we benefit from now.

These days we overuse penicillin both in agriculture and human medicine, which has
given bacteria the chance to adapt and fight back. So it's now up to scientists to adapt
penicillin to extend its lifespan, and to search for new types of antibiotics.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是a。节目先说明青霉素不仅能治疗感染，还为现代介入医学铺平了道路；后面又指出细菌耐药性正在上升，科学家必须改造青霉素并寻找新抗生素，所以核心是在讲青霉素的巨大医学意义以及它面临的耐药挑战。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对，完整概括了节目中段强调的医学革命性作用和后段强调的耐药危机。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错，节目只简要提到弗莱明的发现过程，没有讲制造步骤的实验细节。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错，洗手只是结尾顺带提到的预防建议，不是节目主旨，更没有说它取代抗生素。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错，节目提到化疗是为了说明抗生素让现代治疗成为可能，不是在比较哪种药更危险。

public.prompt: What did Fleming notice on the petri dish?

options.text: A ring around the mould where bacteria were not growing
A layer of dust that made the bacteria change colour
A cracked dish that exposed the bacteria to fresh air
A liquid that caused the mould to spread across the dish

evidence.quote: which had a halo – or circle – around it where no bacteria were growing.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是a。原文明确说培养皿上的霉菌周围有一个“光环”，那个区域没有细菌生长，这正是弗莱明注意到的现象。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对，准确复述了霉菌周围无细菌生长这一关键观察。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错，文中没有提到灰尘或细菌变色。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错，文中没有说培养皿破裂，也没有把发现归因于空气进入。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错，文中讲的是霉菌周围细菌不生长，不是液体让霉菌扩散。

public.prompt: Why does Alice explain what chemotherapy does?

options.text: To clarify why antibiotics are needed when treatments weaken the immune system
To suggest chemotherapy was developed before penicillin
To show that cancer is caused by bacteria
To argue that chemotherapy should replace antibiotics

evidence.quote:
Chemotherapy is a chemical treatment used to kill cancer cells that also suppresses – or
stops – the body's immune system from working. The immune system is our body's
defence against infection and disease. So cancer patients have to take antibiotics to prevent
infections that the body can't fight off by itself.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是a。Alice解释化疗会抑制免疫系统，是为了把前面专家的话说清楚：正因为这类病人更容易感染，抗生素才让这类现代治疗成为可能。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对，这正是这一解释在篇章中的作用：说明抗生素为何对化疗病人至关重要。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错，文中没有比较化疗和青霉素谁先出现。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错，文中没有说癌症由细菌引起。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错，Alice并不是主张用化疗替代抗生素，而是在说明二者的关系。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the future use of penicillin?

options.text: It will remain effective without any changes if doctors prescribe it carefully
Scientists will need to modify it and develop alternatives because bacteria are adapting
It will soon be used mainly to treat viral infections instead of bacterial ones
It is no longer valuable because most bacteria were always resistant to it

evidence.quote:
These days we overuse penicillin both in agriculture and human medicine, which has
given bacteria the chance to adapt and fight back. So it's now up to scientists to adapt
penicillin to extend its lifespan, and to search for new types of antibiotics.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是b。原文说过度使用给了细菌适应和反击的机会，因此科学家需要改造青霉素并寻找新型抗生素，这直接支持对未来使用方式的推断。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错，原文恰恰说细菌已经在适应，不能指望青霉素原样长期有效。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对，这是根据结尾总结作出的合理推断。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错，节目一直在谈细菌与抗生素，没有说它会转去治疗病毒。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错，节目明确强调青霉素曾经极其有效，只是耐药问题在增长。

reasonsZh.bbc-20160922-6min-english-antibiotics-q0: 节目先说明青霉素如何改变医学，再讨论耐药性如何威胁其效果；其余选项要么范围过窄，要么与原文不符。
原文明确说弗莱明看到培养皿里霉菌周围有一圈细菌没有生长的区域，因此只有a符合。
Alice解释化疗会抑制免疫系统，是为了说明这类治疗会让人更需要抗生素来预防感染。
结尾说细菌正在适应并产生耐药性，因此科学家必须改造青霉素并寻找新抗生素；其余选项都超出或违背原文。

public.prompt: According to the speakers, why does punctuation matter?

options.text: Because it helps make written meaning clearer even as usage changes
Because texting should completely avoid traditional punctuation marks
Because keyboard symbols were invented mainly for computer programming
Because public speaking became less persuasive after the Greeks

evidence.quote:
I am a stickler when people don't follow the rules of punctuation
because this makes written text ambiguous or difficult to understand.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是a。节目围绕“标点是否重要”展开，核心论点是标点能减少歧义、帮助理解；后面又谈到历史变化和网络交流，但都服务于这个主题。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对，准确概括了节目主线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错，Neil一开始表达这种态度，但节目整体并不是支持完全不用标点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错，节目谈的是书写与交流，不是计算机编程符号史。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错，节目说古希腊人用标点帮助演讲更易理解，不是说说服力下降。

public.prompt: Why does Alice add a comma in the sentence about Grandma?

options.text: To show that the sentence should be spoken more loudly
To show that the speaker is inviting Grandma to eat rather than eating her
To mark the end of a paragraph in the story
To make the sentence sound more formal than ordinary speech

evidence.quote:
And with a well-placed
comma it becomes: Let's eat, Grandma. Without proper punctuation the sentence is
ambiguous.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是b。加上逗号后，句子的意思从“把奶奶吃掉”变成“和奶奶一起吃饭”，Alice用这个例子说明标点如何消除歧义。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错，Alice讨论的是意义，不是音量。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对，这正是该例子的核心。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错，段落符号是另一件事，这里只是句内逗号。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错，例子的重点不是正式程度，而是避免误解。

options.text: It moves from a modern ambiguity example to the historical origins and functions of punctuation
It shifts from grammar rules to a detailed lesson on keyboard repair

evidence.quote:
They used a series of
dots to indicate different lengths of pauses.

No, it was about public speaking. The different dots indicated different lengths of pauses:

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是a。节目先用“Let’s eat, Grandma”说明现实中的歧义问题，随后转到古希腊，解释标点最初如何帮助停顿和理解，篇章推进非常清楚。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对，准确概括了例子之后的话题展开方式。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错，节目没有讨论键盘维修。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错，提到希腊是为了讲标点历史，不是转去讲政治。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错，节目没有进入小说比较。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the role of emoticons in online writing?

options.text: They were invented by the Ancient Greeks to replace commas

evidence.quote: because we only have text in front of us not intonation.
characters – we can signpost the feelings we intend.

reasonsZh.bbc-20160929-6min-english-punctuation-q0: 讨论核心是标点如何帮助表达更清楚，同时也承认用法会演变，所以a最完整准确。

public.prompt: What broad idea about identity is the program exploring?

options.text: How identity is shaped by appearance, ethnic background, culture, and other people's views

Our sense of who we are is always a response in part to how other

optionRationalesZh.a: 对，概括了外表、族裔、文化和他人眼光几个核心维度。

public.prompt: Why did people sometimes ask Sarah Jones whether she was adopted?

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是c。Sarah Jones说别人看到她母亲是白皮肤、而她从远处看是黑人，就会问她是不是被收养的，原因就是母女外貌差异明显。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错，文中没有提到她童年频繁搬家。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错，她其实很清楚自己复杂的家族背景。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about Alice's Turkish neighbours?

options.text: They have rejected English culture and kept completely separate habits
Living in England for a long time has led them to adopt some local customs
They recently arrived and still find everyday life confusing

they've lived in England for 45 years so they've

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是b。Alice举邻居的例子，说明他们在英国生活很久后喜欢英式肥皂剧、圣诞烤火鸡和加奶茶，这表明长期居住会让人吸收当地习惯。

optionRationalesZh.b: 对，这是根据具体生活习惯作出的直接推断。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错，45年显然不是“最近才来”。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错，节目没有说他们完全失去原有背景，只说他们发生了融合。

but

optionRationalesZh.c: 错，这个例子和统计调查方法无关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 对，这正是这个例子在论证中的功能。

reasonsZh.bbc-20161006-6min-english-identity-q01: 整期节目围绕身份认同展开，涉及外貌、族裔、文化融入以及他人看法如何塑造自我，所以a最贴切。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161006-6min-english-identity-q02: Sarah Jones明确说别人看到她母亲肤色白、而她从远处看是黑人，所以会问她是否被收养。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161006-6min-english-identity-q03: Alice说她的土耳其邻居在英国住了45年，已经融入当地文化，喜欢英式电视剧、圣诞火鸡和奶茶，说明他们吸收了当地习俗。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161006-6min-english-identity-q04: Neil举这位朋友的例子，是为了说明一个人的行为和气质也会表达身份认同，而不只是外貌。

public.prompt: What central issue about loneliness does the program examine?

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是b。节目从“孤独是一种社会性疼痛”谈起，后面又讨论基因、社交媒体证据和环境传播，因此主旨是孤独的生物与社会成因及其影响。
