optionRationalesZh.d: 对，例子正是为了具体化“环境因素”这一点。

reasonsZh.bbc-20161013-6min-english-loneliness-q01: 节目讨论孤独的基因因素、环境因素以及社交媒体和传染性等影响，核心就是其生物和社会层面的成因与后果。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161013-6min-english-loneliness-q02: Boomsma教授说这些基因之所以保留下来，可能是因为在某些情况下，能忍受较高程度的孤独是有优势的。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161013-6min-english-loneliness-q03: Klinenberg明确说没有很强的研究证据表明社交媒体让人更孤独，所以最合理的推断是这种说法证据不足。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161013-6min-english-loneliness-q04: Sophie提到每天遇到不友好的人，会让你在与别人互动时更消极，这是在举例说明环境因素如何传播与孤独相关的负面状态。

public.prompt: What main question about attraction does the program explore?

options.text: Which biological and social factors influence human attraction
Why romantic relationships should be based only on first impressions
How plastic surgery guarantees long-term love
Why smell is more important than health in every culture

evidence.quote:
I think you can be physically attracted to somebody straight away. I just don't think you can
fall in love with somebody so easily. Love is about other things: compatibility, personality
and a good sense of humour.

if they don't smile or react then that counts for much less. So
we can change our faces by interacting with others and doing so in a pleasant manner, and
that will aid our attractiveness and make others interested in us.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是a。节目先区分“立刻被吸引”和“真正相爱”，随后又谈腰臀比、气味、微笑和互动方式，所以主旨是在讨论影响吸引力的生物和社会因素。

optionRationalesZh.a: 对，能够概括节目整体内容。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错，节目并没有主张只靠第一印象建立关系。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错，专家明确说整形并不算决定性因素。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错，节目没有说气味在所有文化中都比健康更重要。

public.prompt: According to Dr Machin, why is a waist-hip ratio of 0.7 considered attractive?

options.text: Because it is the average ratio among British women
Because it is associated with fertility and good health
Because it makes a person seem more humorous
Because it shows that someone has an especially strong immune system

evidence.quote:
is 0.7 and that's really your classic hourglass
figure. And the reason why that universally is attractive is because it's tied to the health of
that individual.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是b。Dr Machin说0.7之所以普遍被认为有吸引力，是因为它和健康及生育能力相关。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错，文中没有说这是英国女性平均值。
optionRationalesZh.b: 对，直接符合专家解释。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错，幽默感是另一类吸引因素，不是腰臀比所显示的内容。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错，文中谈的是整体健康与生育相关线索，不是“特别强的免疫系统”。

public.prompt: Why does the program include Professor Perrett's comments about smiling and reacting pleasantly?

options.text: To argue that people should avoid judging appearance altogether
To prove that plastic surgery usually improves personality
To show that behaviour and interaction also affect attractiveness
To explain why men and women choose partners for identical reasons

evidence.quote:
if they don't smile or react then that counts for much less. So
we can change our faces by interacting with others and doing so in a pleasant manner, and
that will aid our attractiveness and make others interested in us.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是c。节目此前强调了生理线索，这里加入Perrett的观点，是为了补充说明吸引力不只来自外貌，还来自微笑、反应和愉快互动。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错，节目没有否定外貌作用，只是说它不是全部。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错，专家说整形的作用有限，并没有说它能改善人格。
optionRationalesZh.c: 对，这正是该引语在论证中的作用。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错，这里也没有讨论男女选择标准完全相同。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the speakers' view of love at first sight?

options.text: They believe love at first sight is purely a myth created by websites
They think people should ignore compatibility and humour when choosing partners
They conclude that only married people experience love at first sight
Immediate attraction may happen, but lasting love involves more than appearance alone

evidence.quote:
I think it takes time to get to know somebody well enough to know if you love
them.

I think you can be physically attracted to somebody straight away. I just don't think you can
fall in love with somebody so easily. Love is about other things: compatibility, personality
and a good sense of humour.

private.explanationZh: 正确答案是d。Sophie承认人可以立刻被外表吸引，但她明确区分了这种即时吸引和真正的爱情，认为爱情还需要相容性、个性和幽默感，因此可以推断节目并不把“一见钟情”等同于成熟的爱。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错，节目没有把它说成纯粹神话。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错，Sophie恰恰强调相容性和幽默感的重要性。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错，节目只说民调中已婚者比例更高，没有说只有已婚者才会这样。
optionRationalesZh.d: 对，这是综合开头两处表述得出的合理推断。

reasonsZh.bbc-20161020-6min-english-attraction-q01: 节目综合讨论了吸引力中的生物因素，如腰臀比和气味，也谈到微笑、互动和幽默等社会行为因素，因此a最全面。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161020-6min-english-attraction-q02: Dr Machin明确解释0.7之所以有吸引力，是因为它与生育能力和整体健康状况相关。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161020-6min-english-attraction-q03: Perrett教授强调，仅有外貌不够，微笑和愉快互动也会提升吸引力，所以这段话的作用是补充行为因素的重要性。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161020-6min-english-attraction-q04: 两位主持人都承认人可能会立刻被外表吸引，但真正的爱还涉及性格、契合度、幽默感和互动，因此d最符合整体立场。

public.prompt: Which central issue do the speakers explore in this program?

options.text: How social media companies secretly monitor private messages
How people present different versions of themselves in different online spaces
Why public online communication is disappearing in favor of private chat
How internet slang is replacing standard English everywhere

evidence.quote:
they're all different, and the question is, do we use different identities when we are using
different digital spaces? And what's all this tweeting, posting, and hashtagging doing to our
language and our psyche?

private.explanationZh: 节目开头直接提出核心问题：人在不同数字空间里是否会使用不同身份，以及这些线上表达如何影响语言和心理。因此主旨是网络身份与自我呈现，而不是平台技术或隐私设置。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有讨论公司如何监控私人信息，Neil提到的只是公开推文。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。题干中的核心问题就是不同平台上是否会出现不同的自我呈现。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有说公开交流正在消失，反而强调推文具有公开可见性。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目提到网络语言，但没有声称它会全面取代标准英语。

public.prompt: Why does Neil switch into street language when talking about Twitter?

options.text: To pressure Sophie into opening a Twitter account immediately
To show that Twitter users are more truthful than Facebook users
To demonstrate that he performs a different persona on that platform
To explain where urban slang originally came from

evidence.quote:
I think I'm cooler on Twitter than on Facebook. I talk more, you know,
street.

private.explanationZh: Neil先说自己在Twitter上比在Facebook上更酷，接着故意切换成street的说法，作用是举例说明同一个人会因平台不同而塑造不同的说话风格和身份形象。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他前面确实调侃过Sophie，但这里切换语体的主要目的不是劝她立刻注册。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有比较两个平台上的诚实程度。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这是用语言风格差异来具体展示不同平台上的不同人格面貌。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目解释了street的大致含义，但Neil这里不是在讲词源。

public.prompt: What do Dr Aleks Krotoski's comments suggest about posting to very large online audiences?

options.text: People want attention and also hope to connect with a group
People mainly post publicly to correct their friends
People post publicly because they want to avoid belonging anywhere
People prefer talking to strangers rather than to people they know

evidence.quote:
we're trying to get as much attention as we possibly
can, in order to get that tribe.

private.explanationZh: Aleks Krotoski指出，人们在线上努力吸引尽可能多的注意力，是为了找到自己的tribe，也就是所属群体。可以推断，面向巨大受众发声既有求关注的一面，也有寻求连接和归属的一面。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。证据同时包含attention和tribe两个动机。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有把公开发言的主要目的说成纠正朋友。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这与找到tribe的说法正好相反。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有比较陌生人与熟人哪一种更受偏好。

public.prompt: According to the quiz answer, what is typical of the average person?

options.text: Having three social media accounts
Spending 28 percent of each day on social media
Checking social media only in the morning
Having five social media accounts

evidence.quote:
The average person has 5 social media accounts
and spends around 1 hour and 40 minutes browsing these networks every day, accounting
for 28% of the total time spent on the internet.

private.explanationZh: 结尾公布测验答案时明确说明，普通人平均拥有5个社交媒体账号，还会每天花1小时40分钟浏览这些网络。选项d完整对应原文。

optionRationalesZh.a: 3是Sophie的猜测，不是公布的正确数据。
optionRationalesZh.b: 28%指的是上网总时间中的占比，不是一天24小时中的占比。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这是Neil个人的习惯，不是average person的统计。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。节目直接给出平均为5个账号。

reasonsZh.bbc-20161103-6min-english-online-persona: 节目开头明确提出主题：人们是否会在不同数字空间使用不同身份，以及这种发帖、转发等行为怎样影响语言和心理，所以 b 最能概括全文核心。
Neil 先说自己在 Twitter 上比在 Facebook 上“更酷”，随后故意用 street language 举例，作用是展示他在该平台上会表现出不同的自我形象，因此选 c。
Dr Aleks Krotoski 说人们上网既有“look at me”的求关注动机，也想找到自己的 tribe、与他人建立连接，所以 a 完整对应她的意思。
节目最后公布答案时明确说 average person has 5 social media accounts。其余选项要么数字不对，要么把 28% 误解成每天时间比例。

public.prompt: What aspect of bicycles is the discussion mainly designed to explain?

options.text: Why bicycle racing became safer than driving in the nineteenth century
How tyre companies competed to control the earliest bicycle market
Why modern cyclists want to return to old-fashioned clothing
How bicycles developed from an expensive novelty into a force for social change

evidence.quote: when manufacturing costs dropped, they became a vehicle for social change.

private.explanationZh: 节目并不只是在讲自行车零件或发明史，而是强调自行车在变得可负担之后，如何改变普通人的流动性、女性穿着和社会生活，因此主旨是它从新奇玩物变成社会变革工具。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有把重点放在自行车与汽车的安全比较上。
optionRationalesZh.b: 轮胎发明只在结尾作为小测答案出现，不是全篇主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 服装只是社会影响中的一个例子，不是整个讨论的中心。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。原文直接把自行车称为社会变革的载体。

public.prompt: Why does Robert Penn bring up rational dress?

options.text: To show that the earliest bicycles were mainly intended for children
To illustrate how cycling encouraged practical clothing changes and challenged social norms
To explain why women quickly gave up riding bicycles in public
To argue that trousers were immediately accepted everywhere

evidence.quote:
And rational dress was a better fitting jacket and pantaloon trousers
which were cinched below the knee, which meant that nothing got caught in the chain.

private.explanationZh: 提到rational dress的作用，是说明女性一旦骑车，就需要更实用的穿着；而这种改变又触发公众反感，显示自行车正在挑战当时的性别规范。这个例子既说明功能性，也说明社会冲突。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这里讨论的是女性穿着变化，不是儿童用车。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。该例同时体现了实用需求和社会规范受冲击。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文没有说女性因此停止骑车，反而说明骑车带来了改变。
optionRationalesZh.d: 后文明确说当时引发了outrage，并非马上被接受。

public.prompt: What does Robert Penn mean when he says bicycles shored up the gene pool?

options.text: They reduced the need for other kinds of transport
They inspired more people to become professional cyclists
They allowed people to travel farther for partners and mix populations more widely
They helped scientists study inherited diseases more effectively

evidence.quote:
It definitely shored up the gene pool in countries like Britain because it meant that people
could go further than they ever had before in order to find a partner for life and to mate.

private.explanationZh: 这里的意思不是科学研究，而是交通范围扩大后，人们能去更远的地方寻找伴侣，从而让原本较封闭的人群发生更多基因交流。

optionRationalesZh.a: 交通替代并不是shore up the gene pool这句话的核心含义。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文说的是寻找伴侣，不是培养职业骑手。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据把去更远的地方与找伴侣直接联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有讨论科学研究或遗传病。

public.prompt: According to the program, who developed the first pneumatic cycle tyre mentioned in the quiz?

options.text: John Boyd Dunlop
Charles Goodyear
Harvey Samuel Firestone
Robert Penn

evidence.quote:
Scottish inventor and veterinary surgeon John Boyd
Dunlop developed the pneumatic rubber tyre.

private.explanationZh: 节目最后核对测验答案时明确说，John Boyd Dunlop开发了充气橡胶轮胎，因此a是直接对应原文的信息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。结尾明确给出这个答案。
optionRationalesZh.b: 这是题目中的干扰项，不是节目给出的答案。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这也是干扰项，原文没有说他发明了这种自行车轮胎。
optionRationalesZh.d: Robert Penn是受访作者，不是轮胎发明者。

reasonsZh.bbc-20161110-6min-english-bicycles-q01: 节目先讲自行车最初是中产阶级的昂贵玩物，后来因成本下降而普及，并进一步谈到女性解放和社会流动，所以主旨是自行车如何从新奇物件变成社会变革力量，选 d。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161110-6min-english-bicycles-q02: Elizabeth Semmelhack 讨论 rational dress，是为了说明骑车让女性改穿更实用的服装，并因此引发公众震惊和社会规范冲突，所以 b 符合她举例的目的。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161110-6min-english-bicycles-q03: Robert Penn 说自行车让人们能去更远的地方寻找伴侣、生儿育女，主持人随后解释这会让 gene pool 更大、更健康，因此 c 是对这段话的正确推断。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161110-6min-english-bicycles-q04: 结尾核对 quiz 时，Sophie 明确说 John Boyd Dunlop 发明了题目所问的充气自行车轮胎，因此答案是 a。

public.prompt: What broader point about trainers anchors the discussion?

options.text: They matter mainly because athletes need increasingly specialized equipment
They are discussed primarily as museum artifacts from the industrial age
They are now worn widely as cultural and social symbols, not only for sport
They have become popular chiefly because they are cheaper than all other shoes

evidence.quote:
People wear them across
the globe – men, women, kids, teenagers and pensioners. And did you know that 85% of
people who buy trainers don't wear them for sport?

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就指出，训练鞋几乎人人都穿，而且绝大多数购买者并不是为了运动。这说明讨论重点是训练鞋如何成为广泛的社会文化用品，而不仅是体育装备。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目当然提到体育趋势，但主旨不是专业运动装备需求。
optionRationalesZh.b: 博物馆专家只是中间的一个支持例子，不是整段讨论的核心。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据同时说明了普及范围和非运动用途。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目没有把低价说成训练鞋流行的主要原因。

public.prompt: Why does Elizabeth Semmelhack discuss the emerging middle class?

options.text: To argue that nineteenth-century workers needed trainers for factory safety
To explain why wealthy people rejected sports clothing at first
To prove that shoe collectors created the first trainer market
To show that early trainers let people visibly display leisure and prosperity

evidence.quote:
the sneaker, in part, was invented to meet the needs of this new class, so that they could
very conspicuously show that they were playing.

private.explanationZh: Semmelhack提到新兴中产阶级，是为了说明训练鞋最初不仅有功能，还承担炫示休闲时间和经济成功的社会意义。穿上它，别人就能看出你有闲暇去玩。

optionRationalesZh.a: 她讨论的是休闲身份展示，不是工厂安全。
optionRationalesZh.b: 原文没有说富人最初拒绝运动服饰。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中提到sneakerheads在现代存在，但不是早期市场形成的原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据中的conspicuously show that they were playing正是这个意思。

public.prompt: What does Matt Powell's historical overview suggest about trainer fashion?

options.text: It changes only when manufacturers release limited editions
It tends to follow whichever sports capture public enthusiasm at a given time
It became less connected to sport after the 1970s
It has stayed centered on tennis without major shifts

evidence.quote:
jogging craze in the 70s –
everybody was out jogging, and then that became a full-fledged running business. The
basketball business really exploded in the mid late 80s with the emergence of Michael
Jordan.

private.explanationZh: Matt Powell依次提到网球、慢跑和篮球热潮，说明训练鞋时尚会随着某一时期最吸引大众的运动项目而转移。重点不是单一品牌，而是社会兴趣如何推动鞋类风格。

optionRationalesZh.a: limited edition只是前面提到的一类商品，不是这里的核心逻辑。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。不同年代对应不同热门运动，体现出潮流随运动热度变化。
optionRationalesZh.c: 恰恰相反，这里强调训练鞋与流行运动始终紧密相关。
optionRationalesZh.d: 证据清楚显示焦点从网球转向慢跑和篮球，不是一直停留在网球。

public.prompt: In the end-of-program quiz, which term is identified as slang for trainers?

options.text: kicks
wedges
flats
hoodies

evidence.quote:
I asked you: What is a slang term for trainers? Is it… a) kicks, b) wedges or c)
flats?

private.explanationZh: 节目回到测验问题时，明确把kicks、wedges和flats放在一起比较，其中真正表示训练鞋俚语的是kicks。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。题目本身把kicks作为训练鞋俚语候选项，后文也确认Neil答对了。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目后面解释wedges是一种楔形跟鞋，不是训练鞋俚语。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目后面解释flats是平底女鞋，不是训练鞋俚语。
optionRationalesZh.d: hoodies是前文提到的连帽衫，不属于该测验中的鞋类俚语。

reasonsZh.bbc-20161117-6min-english-trainers-q01: 节目一开始就说 85% 的人买 trainers 不是为了运动，后文又讨论身份、时尚、收藏和社会意义，所以核心观点是 trainers 被广泛穿着，已不只是运动用品，选 c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161117-6min-english-trainers-q02: Elizabeth Semmelhack 提到新兴中产阶级拥有闲暇和经济成功，并想通过穿着展示自己在 leisure time 中“playing”，所以她提中产阶级是为了说明早期 trainers 如何显示闲暇与富足，选 d。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161117-6min-english-trainers-q03: Matt Powell 按时间线讲 70 年代网球和慢跑热、80 年代篮球热，说明 trainer fashion 会跟随某一时期最受欢迎的运动变化，因此 b 最准确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161117-6min-english-trainers-q04: 节目末尾回到 quiz 时，Sophie 明确确认 slang term for trainers 是 kicks，所以选 a。

public.prompt: What does the program mainly explain about the suffragette movement?

options.text: How it fought for voting rights while also pushing for wider improvements in women's status
Why the BBC created a season devoted to famous women
How New Zealand's election system became a model for Britain
Why modern voters have lost trust in political campaigns

evidence.quote: the women who fought for the right to vote in the UK!

she in particular wanted wider reforms for women,
an improvement in women's status and position.

private.explanationZh: 节目先把suffragettes定义为争取投票权的女性，随后又借Julia Bush的话说明这场运动并不只关心议会选举，还追求更广泛的女性地位改善。因此主旨应同时覆盖投票权与更宽广的改革目标。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两个证据片段分别对应投票权和更广泛改革。
optionRationalesZh.b: 100 Women只是引入背景，不是整段节目的主要讨论对象。
optionRationalesZh.c: 新西兰只出现在测验答案里，不是节目主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目讨论的是历史上的妇女参政运动，不是现代选民的不信任。

public.prompt: Why does Alice imply that women in Britain had to wait so long for full voting rights?

options.text: Most women refused to participate in political activism
Parliament did not treat it as urgent and was still dealing with male suffrage issues
The movement focused mainly on education rather than on elections
Women were legally forbidden to speak in public spaces

evidence.quote:
Because Parliament didn't see votes for women as a priority. Then, 30% of men still didn't
have the vote either

private.explanationZh: Alice给出的原因有两层：第一，议会没有把女性投票权当作优先事项；第二，当时仍有相当一部分男性也没有选举权，所以政治人物先处理男性选举权问题。由此可推断，延迟并非因为运动不存在，而是政治优先顺序不在女性这一边。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有说大多数女性拒绝参与运动。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据同时给出了不被优先处理和男性选举权尚未解决这两层原因。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目明确说她们争取的就是投票权，并非主要转向教育。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目提到她们会在公共场所发声和打断活动，所以并非完全不能公开讲话。

public.prompt: Why does June Purvis describe interruptions in theatres, churches, and teashops?

options.text: To argue that the suffragettes preferred private meetings to public protest
To suggest that their earliest tactics were basically harmless entertainment
To prove that they copied campaign methods from male politicians
To show how they raised public visibility before moving toward stronger direct action

evidence.quote:
You have women interrupting theatre plays, getting thrown out of church services for
interrupting, getting thrown out of Lyons Corner House for standing up on chairs and
having little impromptu meetings.

private.explanationZh: June Purvis列举这些场景，不是为了渲染趣味性，而是具体展示suffragettes如何通过打断公共活动来提高能见度、引起注意，然后再发展到更激进的direct action。这个例子说明的是运动策略的升级过程。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这些行动发生在公开场所，恰恰不是偏好私人会议。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她的重点不是说这些行为只是娱乐，而是说明运动如何制造公共影响。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有提到模仿男性政治人物。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。例子展示了先提高曝光，再走向更强硬行动的过程。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about World War I from the way the speakers discuss women's suffrage?

options.text: The war ended the movement before it achieved meaningful change
Women immediately received voting rights equal to men as soon as the war ended
Women's wartime responsibilities made continued denial of the vote harder to justify
Parliament granted the vote mainly as a reward for military service

evidence.quote:
So when the war ended in 1918 women had proved how capable they were in so many
ways. To deny them the right to vote now seemed ridiculous.

private.explanationZh: 这里的逻辑是，战争期间女性承担了家庭、照护、经济与工作等多重职责，战后再说她们不具备政治资格就显得站不住脚。因此战争加强了女性争取投票权的正当性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说运动因此失败，反而说明形势变得更有利。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目明确说还要再过十年，所有女性才在与男性平等的条件下获得投票权。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据把证明能力与继续拒绝投票权显得荒谬直接联系起来。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这里强调的是社会能力得到证明，而不是把投票权简单当作奖励。

reasonsZh.bbc-20161124-6min-english-suffragism-q01: 节目不仅讲争取投票权，还借 Julia Bush 的话说明 Pankhurst 追求更广泛的女性地位改善，因此 a 最完整地概括了妇女参政运动在文中的意义。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161124-6min-english-suffragism-q02: Alice 直接解释说 Parliament 没把女性选举权视为优先事项，而且当时还有很多男性也没有投票权，政界想先处理那些问题，所以 b 正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161124-6min-english-suffragism-q03: June Purvis 先举在剧院、教堂和茶馆打断公共活动的例子，随后说 militancy 发展成更强的 direct action，说明这些例子是为了展示她们如何先提高曝光度，再升级为更激烈行动，选 d。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161124-6min-english-suffragism-q04: Neil 和 Alice 说一战期间女性承担了大量家庭与社会职责，战后再剥夺其投票权显得荒谬，因此可以推断战争让继续拒绝女性选举权更难以辩护，选 c。

public.prompt: What question about animal phobias is the program mainly addressing?

options.text: How irrational animal fears involve brain responses and possible survival-based tendencies
Why insects are more dangerous than most people think
How psychologists can quickly cure any animal phobia
Why dogs and moths are the most common fears in Britain

evidence.quote: phobia is a strong and irrational fear. That's a fear not based on reason.

private.explanationZh: 节目先定义phobia是不基于理性的强烈恐惧，随后继续解释大脑中的fight-or-flight机制，并讨论人类为何会自动注意到蜘蛛。因此主旨是动物恐惧症的心理和生物学机制，而不是单纯列举害怕什么。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了定义、脑部反应和进化解释三条主线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有证明昆虫普遍更危险，反而强调很多恐惧并不建立在现实危险之上。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有讨论快速治疗方法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 狗和飞蛾只是两位主持人的引入例子，不是主旨结论。

public.prompt: According to Dr Dean Burnett, what role does the amygdala play in a phobic reaction?

options.text: It helps a person remember where the fear first began
It immediately triggers the fight-or-flight response
It prevents adrenalin from moving through the body
It decides whether a fear is socially acceptable

evidence.quote: message directly to the amygdala, which immediately triggers the 'fight or flight' response

private.explanationZh: Dr Dean Burnett说明，感觉信息会直接传到amygdala，而它会立刻触发fight-or-flight response。重点在于这种反应速度快、偏本能。

optionRationalesZh.a: 原文没有把amygdala的作用说成回忆恐惧来源。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据中的immediately triggers直接说明了它的功能。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目后面解释这种反应会让身体进入警戒状态，而不是阻止adrenalin流动。
optionRationalesZh.d: 社会接受度不是这里讨论的大脑功能。

public.prompt: Why does the program include comments from Stephen and Reena?

options.text: To compare British spiders with Australian spiders
To show that most people can easily control their phobias
To provide vivid examples of how overwhelming a phobic reaction can feel
To prove that spiders are the most dangerous animals in the home

evidence.quote:
hear from Stephen and Reena now, two self-confessed arachnophobes. They have strong
emotional reactions just like you, Neil.

private.explanationZh: 节目插入Stephen和Reena的发言，是为了把抽象定义变成具体体验：胸口像被打中、完全瘫住、理性消失、只想逃跑。这些例子让听者切身感受到phobia的压倒性。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这段并没有进行英国与澳大利亚蜘蛛的比较。
optionRationalesZh.b: 他们的发言恰恰说明恐惧难以控制。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。两人的亲身描述让phobic reaction变得具体可感。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目说蜘蛛恐惧常见，但没有借此证明蜘蛛一定最危险。

public.prompt: Why does Sophie suggest humans may notice spiders more automatically than moths or flies?

options.text: Because spiders are larger and noisier than most other small creatures
Because modern homes usually contain more spiders than insects
Because people can recognize spiders only after someone warns them
Because early humans benefited from spotting spiders quickly in dangerous settings

evidence.quote: spot a spider as an early human on the plains of the African Savannah. A bite might kill you

private.explanationZh: Sophie的意思是，这种对蜘蛛的自动觉察可能来自进化压力：早期人类若能更快发现蜘蛛，就更容易避免致命咬伤或由此带来的其他危险。因此这种注意力偏向具有生存价值。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目没有说蜘蛛更大或更吵。
optionRationalesZh.b: 现代家庭里数量多少不是这里的解释重点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 原文强调的是无需思考的自动觉察，而不是依赖别人提醒。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据明确把自动识别能力与祖先生存优势联系起来。

reasonsZh.bbc-20161201-6min-english-animal-phobias: 节目主体围绕动物恐惧症的脑部机制、fight-or-flight 反应，以及人类对蜘蛛可能具有的生存性警觉展开，所以 a 最能概括主旨。
Dr Dean Burnett 明确说信息也会直接传到 amygdala，并立即触发 fight or flight response，因此答案是 b。
Stephen 和 Reena 的引语集中描写胸口像被击中、完全瘫住、理性消失、转身就跑，作用是生动展示恐惧症发作时那种压倒性的感受，因此选 c。
Sophie 说研究发现人类对蜘蛛有 reflexive awareness，Neil 进一步解释这对早期人类在危险环境中有生存价值，Sophie 也认可，所以 d 正确。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion about Cleopatra?

options.text: Why Cleopatra's family background mattered more than her rule of Egypt
How Cleopatra's reputation involved political skill, education, and personal appeal rather than beauty alone
Why Roman generals failed to understand Egyptian traditions
How modern museums reconstruct Cleopatra's daily life

evidence.quote:
Cleopatra was a wily politician. She made important alliances with Rome to protect her
country

But let's talk
some more about Cleopatra's attributes. She may not have been Hollywood gorgeous, but
she spoke many languages and was highly educated in philosophy, astronomy, mathematics,
and oratory.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查主旨。节目并不是单纯讨论克利奥帕特拉是否美貌，而是反复强调她的政治手腕、受教育程度和个人魅力，因此正确答案概括的是她名声背后的多重原因。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中提到家族出身只是结尾测验的一部分，不是整段讨论的核心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。前文说她是精明的政治人物，后文又说她受教育程度高，这正好概括了节目的主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目重点不是分析罗马将领对埃及文化的误解，而是分析克利奥帕特拉本人。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有讨论博物馆如何重建她的日常生活，这属于无中生有。

public.prompt: According to Susan Walker, what did Plutarch find especially remarkable about Cleopatra?

options.text: Her ability to defeat Roman armies in battle
Her family's long rule over Egypt
Her intelligence, voice, and charisma
Her wealth and luxurious clothing

evidence.quote:
Plutarch says that what was really remarkable about Cleopatra was not so much her beauty
as the intelligence of her company.

She had a very beautiful voice, which I think is a quality
that we perhaps underrate these days, in estimations of celebrity, and she clearly had
masses of charisma

private.explanationZh: Susan Walker转述普鲁塔克的看法时，明确说真正出众的不是美貌，而是她谈吐中的智慧、动听的声音和强大的个人魅力，所以应选概括这些特质的选项。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中这里没有说她靠军事胜利著称，讨论焦点是个人特质。
optionRationalesZh.b: 家族统治背景不是Susan Walker此处强调的内容。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。证据同时提到智慧、声音和charisma，和该选项完全对应。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有把她的财富或服饰当作普鲁塔克特别称赞的重点。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers mention Cleopatra arriving in a carpet and later on a golden barge?

options.text: To illustrate how skillfully she used spectacle and dramatic entrances
To prove that Roman stories about her were completely false
To compare Egyptian and Greek transportation methods
To show that she preferred luxury to politics

evidence.quote: Cleopatra was very skilled at making grand entrances.

Rolled up in a carpet for Caesar, or dressed up as Venus on board a shimmering golden
barge, for Mark Anthony.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查举例目的。说到地毯和金船，不是为了讲交通工具或考证真假，而是用两个生动例子说明她如何借助视觉效果制造“spectacle”和强烈登场感。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。前一句先给出总论，说她擅长grand entrances，后一句就是具体例证。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有说这些故事“完全是假的”，反而是在用它们说明她的表演性。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有比较交通方式，这完全偏题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这些例子说明的是公共形象经营，不等于她只重奢华不重政治。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about historical reports of Cleopatra's death?

options.text: Most historians agree that she drank poison from a cup
Her death was probably accidental rather than planned
Ancient writers ignored the public effect of her death
The exact method is uncertain, but the event appears to have been carefully staged

evidence.quote:
there's a certain amount of uncertainty about the exact method of her death. I think most
sources prefer the snake.

But she did it in a calculated – or
carefully planned – way.

private.explanationZh: 题目要求综合推断。材料一方面说她的具体死法并不确定，另一方面又明确说她的自杀方式是calculated，也就是精心策划的，因此最合理的结论是“方法不确定，但事件被有意设计过”。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中提到的不确定性恰好说明不能断定一定是毒酒。
optionRationalesZh.b: 证据明确说是calculated，和“意外”相反。
optionRationalesZh.c: 教授恰恰强调了死亡中的spectacle效果，因此不能说古代作者忽视这一点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这一选项同时涵盖了“不确定的方法”和“精心安排”的两个关键信息。

reasonsZh.bbc-20161208-6min-english-cleopatra-q01: 通篇围绕克利奥帕特拉的政治手腕、教育背景、语言能力、魅力与戏剧化形象展开，并明确弱化“只靠美貌”的说法，b最符合主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161208-6min-english-cleopatra-q02: Susan Walker明确说普鲁塔克认为她真正突出的不是美貌，而是陪伴时展现出的智慧、优美声音、个人魅力和迷人风采，因此c正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161208-6min-english-cleopatra-q03: 节目先提地毯出场，后提金色驳船，并紧接着说明法老重视spectacle、她擅长grand entrances，所以这些例子是为了说明她善于制造视觉效果和戏剧性出场。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161208-6min-english-cleopatra-q04: 关于自杀方式，节目说可能是蛇、毒发簪或毒药，方法并不确定；但专家又说她以calculated manner并再次聚焦spectacle来安排死亡，因此d最能概括。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the discussion?

options.text: To explain what circadian rhythms are and how the body's clock affects daily functions
To argue that morning people are healthier than everyone else
To describe how scientists first discovered jet lag
To compare human sleep cycles with those of plants in detail

evidence.quote:
today we're talking about the biological reason for this – it's all about circadian
rhythms. They are produced by a so-called body clock in our brains that regulates our body
functions

private.explanationZh: 开头直接点明主题：节目讨论的是昼夜节律以及大脑中的“身体时钟”如何调节体温、困倦、警觉和饥饿等功能，所以A最符合整体目的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了节目对circadian rhythms和body clock的介绍。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有主张“晨型人更健康”，只是举例说明不同人的作息差异。
optionRationalesZh.c: jet lag只是中间用来说明身体时钟为何重要的一个例子，不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中虽然 briefly 提到植物和微生物也有昼夜节律，但并未展开细致比较。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about totally blind people who have no light perception?

options.text: Their internal clocks stop working completely
They can synchronize their sleep patterns perfectly through social interaction alone
Their sleep-wake timing may drift because their clocks are no longer guided by light cues
Their circadian cycles become much shorter than those of sighted people

evidence.quote:
they've lost that connection between the light-dark circle and the
clock.

they don't get cues from the outside environment telling
them when to wake up, when to eat, when to feel sleepy.

private.explanationZh: 这里需要把两处信息合起来理解。教授说完全失明者失去了光暗循环与生物钟之间的联系，主持人又解释说他们得不到外界何时醒来、进食、困倦的提示，因此最合理的推断是作息时间会相对社会时间发生漂移。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中明确说clock本身没有问题，所以不是“完全停止工作”。
optionRationalesZh.b: 材料没有说仅靠社交互动就能完美同步，重点反而是缺乏关键的光照线索。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。失去光暗提示后，生物钟会free-running，因此睡眠时间可能漂移。
optionRationalesZh.d: 教授说完全失明者的正常范围与其他人相似，并没有说周期明显更短。

public.prompt: According to Debra Skene, what has the strongest influence on circadian timing?

options.text: Exercise
The light-dark cycle
Caffeine
Meal size

evidence.quote: not as strongly as the light-dark cycle.

private.explanationZh: 教授先提到运动、食物和咖啡因可能会产生一定影响，但马上补充说这些影响都“不如光暗循环强”，因此答案是光暗循环。

optionRationalesZh.a: 运动被说成可能有影响，但不是最强因素。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据直接说其他因素不如light-dark cycle强。
optionRationalesZh.c: 咖啡因也只是次要线索之一，不是最强影响。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中提到规律进餐有帮助，但没有说餐量是最强因素。

public.prompt: Why does Alice mention meals and naps near the end of the discussion?

options.text: To prove that light does not affect the body clock
To argue that medication is the best solution for shift workers
To summarize the history of circadian research
To give practical examples of habits that may help people adjust to unusual schedules

evidence.quote:
So eating three well-balanced meals at regular times each day can help your
body clock adapt to an unusual schedule.

Taking naps – or short sleeps – just before you start a night shift can help you feel more
alert.

private.explanationZh: 这里的作用是把前面的科学解释转化为实际建议。Alice列举规律吃饭和上夜班前小睡，不是为了讲理论历史，而是为了说明人们可以怎样在现实中帮助身体适应非常规作息。

optionRationalesZh.a: 前文明确说光暗循环仍然是最强影响，因此这不是她举例的目的。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有把药物作为最佳解决方案。
optionRationalesZh.c: 这些例子不是研究史回顾，而是具体做法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。两条建议都属于帮助人们调整不寻常日程的实用办法。

reasonsZh.bbc-20161215-6min-english-circadian-rhyt: 开头就定义circadian rhythms和body clock，并说明其调节睡意、体温、饥饿、激素等，后文再讨论失调和适应，a最准确概括整段目的。
专家说完全失明者失去了光暗循环与生物钟的连接，钟本身仍在运转；Neil进一步解释他们的钟是free-running，可能在错误时间困倦或清醒，所以c正确。
Debra Skene明确说运动、食物、咖啡因会有一些影响，但没有light-dark cycle强，因此最强影响是b。
结尾提到规律进餐、班前小睡和固定睡眠时间，都是在回答夜班人群如何帮助身体钟适应异常作息，属于实际建议，因此选d。

public.prompt: What question does the discussion mainly explore?

options.text: Why one puzzle became the world's best-selling toy
Why crazes spread quickly and attract many participants
How smartphone games replaced older toys
Why celebrity support is the only cause of every fad

evidence.quote:
Perhaps I should explain at this point that a craze or a fad is a sudden and widespread
enthusiasm for something, which only lasts a short time.

When a person or a group perceives an idea or a process or a product as being beneficial
to one person or to a group of people, they immediately want to experience that benefit
for themselves, which hooks into an ancient evolutionary fear of being left behind or
abandoned by our tribes, and so more people join in.

private.explanationZh: 节目先定义什么是craze/fad，随后重点解释人们为什么会加入并推动其扩散，因此主旨是“流行热潮为何会迅速传播并吸引参与者”。

optionRationalesZh.a: Rubik's Cube只是结尾提到的一个例子，不是整段核心问题。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。定义加上心理学解释共同指向“为什么热潮会传播”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 节目没有论证新游戏“取代”旧玩具，这个表述过度延伸。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中提到名人戴loom bands，但没有说名人支持是所有fad的唯一原因。

public.prompt: What does Alice suggest when she mentions emotional insecurity and herd mentality?

options.text: Only children are affected by social pressure during crazes
People usually join crazes after carefully studying the product
Peer influence and insecurity can help explain why some people follow crazes
Most crazes continue because they have clear educational value

evidence.quote:
people who are more emotionally insecure are far more likely to follow a
craze? You know, sort of, herd mentality?

Herd mentality describes how people are influenced by their peers to adopt certain
behaviours.

private.explanationZh: Alice在这里并不是单纯下定义，而是在反驳Neil把追随热潮看成纯粹“好玩”的说法。她提出情绪不安全感和从众心理，暗示追随craze往往和同伴压力、害怕落后有关。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有把这种现象限制为儿童，范围被缩小了。
optionRationalesZh.b: 材料强调的是情绪和群体因素，不是理性研究产品。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。两处证据一起说明了不安全感与同伴影响对跟风行为的解释力。
optionRationalesZh.d: 节目并没有说多数craze因为教育价值而持续。

public.prompt: Why is Cheong Choon Ng's story included in the discussion?

options.text: To show how a simple idea can grow into a craze once people copy and share it
To prove that all successful toys begin as school projects
To argue that celebrity fashion is more important than invention
To explain why internet videos usually fail to influence sales

evidence.quote: The idea of loom band came from my daughters.

I tried to impress them by making a thicker bracelet that
was made from a prototype loom that I fabricated. And it was a success.

He posted a video of his daughters making the bracelets and these went
viral on the internet.

private.explanationZh: 这个故事的功能是提供一个具体案例，说明热潮并不一定来自复杂产品：一个家庭中的小点子，经过原型制作和网络传播，也能迅速扩散成流行现象。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。故事从女儿的想法讲到原型成功，再到视频走红，完整展示了craze形成过程。
optionRationalesZh.b: 文中没有说所有成功玩具都起源于学校项目。
optionRationalesZh.c: 名人佩戴是在别处提到的辅助现象，这里重点是发明与传播。
optionRationalesZh.d: 这个故事恰恰说明视频可能带来巨大传播效果，而不是“通常失败”。

public.prompt: According to Neil, what helped make Pokémon Go so appealing at first?

options.text: It was cheaper than most other mobile games
It was designed mainly for classroom use
It depended on famous athletes to advertise it
It combined exciting technology with outdoor play at a good time of year

evidence.quote:
It uses augmented reality – and that's exciting new technology! It's fun to play outside – and
the game was released over the summer when people want to be outside.

private.explanationZh: Neil列举Pokémon Go受欢迎的原因时，强调的是增强现实这项新技术、到户外玩的乐趣，以及夏天发布的时机，因此D准确概括了他的观点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 文中没有提到价格因素。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目把它描述为大众游戏，而不是课堂工具。
optionRationalesZh.c: Neil没有把名人宣传当成它走红的主要原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。该选项概括了增强现实、户外活动和夏季发布这几个关键信息。

reasonsZh.bbc-20161222-6min-english-crazes-q01: 节目先定义craze/fad，再反复追问人们为何参与、为何Pokémon Go会迅速流行，并给出心理和传播层面的解释，所以主问题是b。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161222-6min-english-crazes-q02: Alice提到更情绪不安全的人更可能追随craze，并补充herd mentality即受同伴影响而模仿行为，说明从众压力和不安全感能解释流行追随，c正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161222-6min-english-crazes-q03: Cheong Choon Ng从女儿玩橡皮筋得到灵感，做出prototype，又通过视频传播走红，这个故事用来展示简单创意如何被复制分享并发展成热潮，因此选a。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161222-6min-english-crazes-q04: Neil明确说它用了令人兴奋的新技术、适合在户外玩，而且在夏天发布，人们本就想出去；这些因素组合起来对应d。

public.prompt: What is the main point of the discussion about introverts?

options.text: To challenge the idea that introversion is less valuable and to discuss how introverts can be supported
To prove that extroverts are better suited to modern life
To explain why personality types never change over time
To compare several famous psychologists in detail

evidence.quote:
You're reflecting the general view
that people seem to have these days, that being an introvert is somehow less valuable than
being an extrovert. Introverts have many valuable qualities.

schools are being asked to develop new strategies to support
their learning.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始先反驳“内向不如外向有价值”的社会偏见，后半段又讨论学校如何支持内向者，所以A最完整地概括了全文。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时覆盖了价值判断的纠偏和实际支持策略两个核心部分。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目并没有证明外向者更适合现代生活，反而提醒人们不要只按外向标准设计环境。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有讨论人格是否会随时间改变。
optionRationalesZh.d: 心理学家和研究者只是提供观点与案例，不是整段的比较对象。

public.prompt: According to Lisa Kaenzig, how do introverts typically recharge?

options.text: By speaking spontaneously in large groups
By spending time alone
By seeking stronger external stimulation
By avoiding reflection and acting quickly

evidence.quote: Introverts are people of any age who really get their energy from within themselves.

need some time to recharge their batteries by being by
themselves, and enjoy having alone time.

private.explanationZh: Lisa Kaenzig明确说，内向者的能量来自内在，而且他们需要独处来“recharge their batteries”，所以最准确的答案是独处。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这更像外向者偏好的行为，不符合证据。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。证据直接说他们通过独处恢复精力。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中说内向者往往不喜欢过强的外部刺激。
optionRationalesZh.d: Lisa强调的是先思考、喜欢独处，并不是避免反思。

public.prompt: Why does the discussion include Dr Peter Aloka's work with students in Kenya?

options.text: To show that introverts should always be separated from extroverts
To argue that personality differences matter only in Africa
To provide a real educational example of strategies that can help introverts participate
To prove that teachers should stop asking questions in class

evidence.quote:
schools are being asked to develop new strategies to support
their learning.

Whenever a teacher is teaching a question in class let them allow for wait time
for students to process the information before answering the question.

Thinking, pairing, and sharing: those three strategies will help
the introverts to speak out.

private.explanationZh: 这个案例的作用不是介绍某个地区本身，而是用真实教育场景说明：通过等待时间、配对和分享等策略，可以让内向学生更容易开口参与。

optionRationalesZh.a: Dr Aloka建议的是更小的组和合理配对，不是永远分开内外向者。
optionRationalesZh.b: 案例来自肯尼亚，但结论并不限于非洲。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。几条具体策略都说明这是一个现实中的教学应用案例。
optionRationalesZh.d: 他说的是提问后要给等待时间，而不是停止提问。

public.prompt: How is the discussion mainly organized?

options.text: By tracing the history of personality theory from Freud to Jung
By listing famous introverts and extroverts one by one
By comparing schools in different countries before defining introversion
By moving from definitions and contrasts to practical settings and then to specific support strategies

evidence.quote:
That's right. Introverts like to work alone or in small groups and need a quieter space to
perform well.

So the idea is that with fewer people – in a small group or a pair – the introvert will have
enough time and space to express their ideas.

private.explanationZh: 这题考查篇章结构。节目先说明内向与外向的区别，再谈学校和工作环境中的需求差异，最后给出更具体的课堂支持办法，因此D最符合信息展开顺序。

optionRationalesZh.a: 节目只在结尾 briefly 提到Jung，不是按理论史展开。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目没有逐个列举名人案例。
optionRationalesZh.c: 它不是先比较各国学校再定义introversion，顺序说反了。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。证据显示讨论从一般特征过渡到具体环境，再落实到可操作的支持策略。

reasonsZh.bbc-20161229-6min-english-introverts-q01: 节目先批评“内向不如外向有价值”的偏见，再说明内向者的优点，并讨论学校和工作场景如何支持他们，所以a最符合中心。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161229-6min-english-introverts-q02: Lisa Kaenzig明确说内向者需要独处来recharge their batteries，并且享受alone time，因此b是直接信息。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161229-6min-english-introverts-q03: 引入Peter Aloka是为了给出课堂支持策略实例，如小组、配对、等待时间和Think-Pair-Share，说明如何帮助内向学生发言参与，所以选c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20161229-6min-english-introverts-q04: 结构上先定义内外向及其差异，再谈学校和职场中的不适配，最后给出具体教学支持方法，d准确概括组织方式。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion?

options.text: How racing teams train professional drivers
Why people should never trust computers on the road
The benefits, concerns, and possible future of autonomous driving
How road accident statistics are collected worldwide

evidence.quote:
An autonomous – or independent and driverless – car is a vehicle that is capable of sensing
its environment and navigating without human input.

Well, the future is likely to be all about automated transport – planes, trains, buses, and
cars – and you simply won't have a choice, Neil.

private.explanationZh: 节目从介绍什么是autonomous car开始，接着讨论人类驾驶的乐趣、自动驾驶的安全与体验问题，最后展望自动化交通的未来，所以C最能概括全文。

optionRationalesZh.a: Eddie Jordan只是被采访的一个例子，不是节目主线。
optionRationalesZh.b: 节目呈现的是利弊讨论，不是单方面主张“永远不要信任电脑”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。定义、担忧、优势与未来预测都集中在自动驾驶这一主题上。
optionRationalesZh.d: 事故数据只是中间的背景信息，不是主要焦点。

public.prompt: Why did Eddie Jordan say he felt cheated when someone else drove him?

options.text: Because control and enjoyment were central to his experience of driving
Because he thought professional drivers were unsafe
Because he could not make phone calls in the car
Because he wanted to save money on transport

evidence.quote: When I get in a car I feel a different person, I feel that I'm now in control.

I always felt cheated because there was a driver there taking the pleasure
that I should be having.

private.explanationZh: 要从“in control”和“pleasure”两个表达里推断。Eddie Jordan重视的是自己掌控车辆并从驾驶中获得乐趣，所以别人代驾会让他觉得这种体验被夺走了。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。两处证据都指向控制感和驾驶快感。
optionRationalesZh.b: 他没有说职业司机不安全，问题不在安全，而在失去亲自驾驶的乐趣。
optionRationalesZh.c: 相反，他提到有司机时一个好处是可以打电话，所以这不是他感到cheated的原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 文中没有涉及省钱。

public.prompt: What surprised Brian Fung most about riding in Google's driverless car?

options.text: It could not handle pedestrians or parked cars
It quickly felt comfortable and surprisingly ordinary
It needed frequent help from a human driver
It moved much more slowly than a regular car

evidence.quote:
it takes about ten seconds for you to get pretty
comfortable.

it felt very similar to a regular car-driving experience. And I think that's one of the most
surprising and exciting things about it – it's just how mundane it was.

private.explanationZh: Brian Fung先说大约十秒就会感到舒服，后来又总结说最令人惊讶的是这种体验其实非常mundane，也就是普通得出人意料，所以B最准确。

optionRationalesZh.a: 他明确说车能识别行人并绕开停着的车。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。两个证据点都强调“很快适应”和“意外地普通”。
optionRationalesZh.c: 文中没有说它需要人频繁接管。
optionRationalesZh.d: 他说的是加速和刹车比普通车更猛烈一些，不是更慢。

public.prompt: What does Alice suggest is likely to happen in the future of transport?

options.text: People will reject automation and return to traditional driving
Autonomous cars will remain a small luxury product
Pilots will stop using autopilot systems entirely
Automated transport will become widespread, leaving people with less choice about driving themselves

evidence.quote:
Well, the future is likely to be all about automated transport – planes, trains, buses, and
cars – and you simply won't have a choice, Neil.

private.explanationZh: Alice对未来做了直接预测：自动化交通将覆盖飞机、火车、公交和汽车，而且Neil可能根本没有是否亲自驾驶的选择，因此D最符合她的意思。

optionRationalesZh.a: 这和Alice的预测相反。
optionRationalesZh.b: 她说的是广泛普及，而不是小众奢侈品。
optionRationalesZh.c: autopilot只是类比说明override，不是未来预测的重点。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。该选项完整复述了“自动化普及”和“个人选择变少”两层含义。

reasonsZh.bbc-20170105-driving-q01: 节目讨论无人驾驶汽车的定义、潜在安全优势、人们的不信任、体验反馈以及未来自动化交通，因此主焦点是c。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170105-driving-q02: Eddie Jordan说一开车就觉得自己在控制中，能享受驾驶乐趣；有人代驾时他觉得那份本该属于自己的快乐被拿走了，所以核心原因是控制感和乐趣，选a。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170105-driving-q03: Brian Fung说除了起步和刹车稍激进外，大约十秒就感到舒服，而且最令人惊讶、兴奋的是它其实很mundane，和普通驾驶很像，因此b正确。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170105-driving-q04: Alice明确说未来很可能是飞机、火车、公交和汽车都自动化，Neil甚至可能没有选择自己开车的权利，因此d最符合她的预测。

public.prompt: What is the main purpose of the discussion?

options.text: To argue that emojis should replace written language
To explain how emojis and emoticons help convey emotion in digital communication
To describe how mobile phones created online empathy
To compare emoji habits in different countries

evidence.quote: They enable us to express emotion and empathy in digital communication.

private.explanationZh: 节目核心在于解释表情符号为什么重要。专家明确指出，它们能在数字交流中帮助人们表达情绪与共情，这正是整段讨论反复展开的中心论点。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目说它们能补充文字信息，没有主张完全取代文字。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了节目对 emoji 和 emoticon 交流功能的说明。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。手机只是使用场景之一，不是“创造共情”的原因。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有比较不同国家的使用差异。

public.prompt: Which example does Dan give of an emoji?

options.text: A semicolon followed by a bracket
A keyboard colon
A bowl of noodles
A newspaper typo

evidence.quote:
a bowl of
noodles

private.explanationZh: Dan 在区分 emoji 和 emoticon 时列举了几个 emoji 例子，其中之一就是“一碗面”。这道题考查对具体例证的听辨。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这是早期 emoticon 的组成方式，不是 Dan 举的 emoji 例子。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。冒号属于键盘字符，用于构成 emoticon。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。Dan 说 emoji 可以是笑脸、跳舞女士，甚至一碗面。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。报纸排版错误是谈 1862 例子时提到的，不是 emoji 示例。

public.prompt: Why does Dan use the example about hitting his finger with a hammer?

options.text: To prove that texting about injuries is unreliable
To show that Neil often misunderstands jokes
To suggest that words are less useful than images
To show that emojis can function like non-verbal cues

evidence.quote:
The emoji is the
non-verbal cue

private.explanationZh: 这个锤子砸手指的例子不是在讨论受伤本身，而是在说明：同一句文字，如果后面跟不同 emoji，接收者会对说话人的情绪状态作出不同判断。因此 emoji 的作用像面对面交流中的非语言提示。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。重点不是短信不可靠，而是情绪线索不足。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。Neil 的反应只是配合示例，不是论点本身。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有主张图像比文字更有用，只说它们能补充文字。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。Dan 最后直接把 emoji 解释为 non-verbal cue。

public.prompt: What can be inferred about the 1862 Lincoln newspaper example?

options.text: It may have looked like an emoticon, but it was probably accidental
It became the official beginning of digital emoticons
Lincoln intentionally created it to mark jokes
It showed that newspapers preferred emojis to words

evidence.quote: Though people think this was sadly just a typographical error

private.explanationZh: 节目提到 1862 年的报纸里出现过类似眨眼脸的符号组合，但紧接着又说那很可能只是排版错误。所以合理推断是：它像 emoticon，却未必是有意创造出来的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它同时保留了“形式上像”和“可能并非有意”这两个要点。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目说通常把 1982 视为 emoticon 的正式起点。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有说 Lincoln 本人有意发明这种写法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里讨论的是一个历史巧合，不是报纸的传播偏好。

reasonsZh.bbc-20170323-the-rise-of-the-emoji-q01: 节目一开始就说明要讨论 emojis 和 emoticons，随后引用作者观点，核心一直在解释它们如何帮助人在数字交流中表达情感与共情。A、C、D都把范围缩窄或转向未讨论的主题。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170323-the-rise-of-the-emoji-q02: Dan 解释 emoji 时明确举了 actual image 的例子，包括笑脸、dancing lady 和 a bowl of noodles。A、B说的是用键盘符号组成的 emoticon，不是 emoji；D是后面提到的 typo。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170323-the-rise-of-the-emoji-q03: 锤子砸手指的短信例子用来说明，同一句话配不同 emoji 会传达不同情绪，因此 emoji 在数字交流里起到类似面部表情这类非语言线索的作用。其目的不是证明发短信不可靠，也不是贬低文字本身。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170323-the-rise-of-the-emoji-q04: 节目说 1862 年林肯相关报纸文本里确实出现过像眨眼脸的符号，但紧接着又说人们认为那只是排版或打字错误，所以最合理推断是“看起来像 emoticon，但多半并非有意创造”。其余选项都与原文相反。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the discussion?

options.text: Why airport security should replace machines with dogs
How dogs may be used to detect cancer and assist healthcare
How pet owners can keep children from getting sick
Why rescue dogs are more useful than guide dogs

evidence.quote:
currently assessing whether dogs can be used to detect early stage prostate
cancer.

private.explanationZh: 节目一开始就明确说明，英国 NHS 正在评估狗是否可以用于早期前列腺癌检测，因此整段讨论的主线是狗在医疗检测中的潜在用途。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。机场安检只是为了类比狗的嗅觉能力。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。这准确概括了节目中心话题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。孩子生病只是开场闲聊，不是主题。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有比较哪类工作犬“更有用”。

public.prompt: How does Dr Claire Guest describe the dog in this context?

options.text: As a replacement for all doctors
As a pet that comforts nervous patients
As a highly sophisticated biosensor
As an animal that learns by imitation

evidence.quote:
a highly sophisticated bio-
sensor.

private.explanationZh: Dr Claire Guest 直接把狗称为“高度复杂的生物传感器”，强调的是它们凭借嗅觉执行化学检测任务的能力。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目从未说狗能完全替代医生。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里讨论的是检测功能，不是安抚病人的陪伴功能。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这是发言中的原始核心表述。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目重点不是模仿学习，而是生理嗅觉优势。

public.prompt: Why do the speakers mention guide dogs, guard dogs, and rescue dogs?

options.text: To explain the historical origins of cancer research
To compare pet ownership rules across countries
To introduce vocabulary unrelated to the main topic
To show that dogs already support humans in several practical roles

evidence.quote:
rescue dogs. They are trained to search and find victims of natural disasters
such as avalanches or earthquakes.

private.explanationZh: 节目连续列举导盲犬、护卫犬和搜救犬，不是偏题，而是在搭建论证背景：既然狗早已在许多重要场景中帮助人类，那么把它们用于医学检测也更容易被理解和接受。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这里与癌症研究的历史无关。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有比较国家间的宠物管理规则。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这些例子与主话题直接相关，不是无关插入。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。列举不同类型工作犬的作用，是在支撑整体论证。

public.prompt: In the final vocabulary review, what does "orthodoxy" mean?

options.text: A generally accepted way of doing things
A medical test based on smell
A way to train rescue dogs
A punishment used to deter crime

evidence.quote: An orthodoxy is a generally accepted way of doing things.

private.explanationZh: 在结尾回顾词汇时，主持人把 orthodoxy 明确定义为“普遍被接受的做事方式”。这与前面 Jeremy Hunt 提到研究挑战 orthodoxies 的说法呼应。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。这就是节目结尾给出的定义。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这更接近 biosensor 的概念。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有把 orthodoxy 定义为训练方法。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。deterrent 才与阻止某人做某事有关。

reasonsZh.bbc-20170330-dog-detectors-q01: 开头就说要讨论狗被用于癌症检测，后面围绕嗅觉、biosensor、NHS评估和医疗应用展开，因此主旨是狗如何帮助检测癌症并服务医疗。A、C、D都只是局部内容或无关内容。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170330-dog-detectors-q02: Dr Claire Guest 明确说狗“in fact a highly sophisticated bio-sensor”。其他选项都不是她的表述：她没有说替代所有医生，也没有说主要作用是安抚病人或靠模仿学习。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170330-dog-detectors-q03: 提到 guide dogs、guard dogs 和 rescue dogs，是为了说明狗早已在多个实际场景中支持人类，因此把它们用于健康检测并非完全不可想象。不是讲癌症研究历史，也不是无关词汇展示。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170330-dog-detectors-q04: 在最后词汇回顾中，Neil 直接解释 orthodoxy 是“a generally accepted way of doing things”。这是原文明确定义，其他选项把词义误解成医疗测试、训练方式或惩罚。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the discussion?

options.text: How scientists count bacteria in the body
How food, gut bacteria, and mood may be connected
Why chocolate is better than traditional foods
Why travel around the Mediterranean improves mood

evidence.quote:
your mental state is connected to the
bacteria in your gut.

private.explanationZh: 节目把“情绪状态”和“肠道细菌”直接联系起来，随后又讨论发酵食物、地中海饮食以及情绪，因此主旨就是食物、肠道菌群与心情之间的关系。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。细菌数量只是节目后段的补充信息，不是主旨。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它抓住了整段讨论的科学主线。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目并没有支持巧克力优于传统食物。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目谈的是饮食模式，不是旅行本身。

public.prompt: According to the BBC program discussed here, what kind of food was best?

options.text: Chocolate desserts
Packaged sweet snacks
Homemade fermented food
Foods with no bacteria at all

evidence.quote:
homemade
fermented food was best.

private.explanationZh: 节目引用 BBC 的相关研究结果，明确说自制发酵食物最好。这一题考查对中段结论性细节的把握。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。巧克力泡芙在后面被拿来当反例。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目并未推荐甜的加工零食。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。节目直接给出了这一结论。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目讨论的是“好细菌”，不是完全没有细菌的食物。

public.prompt: Why does Dr Michael Mosley mention chocolate eclairs?

options.text: To show that sugary foods are part of the Mediterranean diet
To argue that fermented foods should taste sweeter
To prove that all desserts are harmful in the same way
To contrast a brief pleasure food with the healthier diet he recommends

evidence.quote:
That will make you feel good for about 30 seconds until you finish it and then you
feel terribly guilty.

private.explanationZh: Mosley 提到巧克力泡芙，是为了把“短暂满足但随后内疚”的高糖甜点，与他推荐的更稳定、更健康的饮食方案形成鲜明对比。这个例子起的是对照说明作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Mosley 推荐的是地中海饮食，不包括把高糖甜点当核心。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里不是在讨论发酵食物的口味。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目并没有把所有甜点都做绝对化判断。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它准确说明了这个例子在论证中的功能。

public.prompt: What does the comparison between bacterial cells and human cells mainly suggest?

options.text: Bacteria are present in the body in very large numbers, so their effects are worth studying
Human cells will soon be replaced by bacteria
Only unhealthy people carry many bacteria
Bacteria are too rare to influence mood

evidence.quote: around 39 trillion bacterial cells in the body

private.explanationZh: 节目最后给出人体内细菌数量极其庞大的信息，不是为了单纯报数字，而是强化前面讨论的合理性：既然菌群规模如此巨大，研究它们对健康和情绪的影响当然有意义。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它把数量信息和整段讨论的科学意义联系了起来。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有预测人类细胞会被细菌取代。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里说的是平均人体情况，不是病人特征。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。庞大的数量恰好支持其潜在影响值得关注。

reasonsZh.bbc-20170406-food-and-mood-q01: 节目从“food can influence your mood”切入，再讲肠道细菌、焦虑、发酵食品和地中海饮食，整体主题是食物、肠道细菌与情绪之间可能存在的联系。A只抓住一个细节，C、D都偏离主旨。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170406-food-and-mood-q02: Neil 概述 BBC 节目研究结果时明确说“they found that homemade fermented food was best”。这是直接信息。A、B与节目建议相反，D也不符合“good bacteria”这一核心。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170406-food-and-mood-q03: Mosley 先推荐 Mediterranean diet，又说 chocolate eclairs 只会让人短暂开心几十秒，之后还会内疚，因此提它是为了和更健康、长期更好的饮食建议形成对比。不是说它属于地中海饮食，也不是证明所有甜点都一样有害。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170406-food-and-mood-q04: 节目最后说人体平均约有 39 trillion bacterial cells，而 human cells 约 30 trillion。这个比较主要说明细菌数量非常多，因此它们对人体和情绪的影响值得认真研究。B、C、D都超出了或违背原文。

public.prompt: What is the main topic of the program?

options.text: The history of surnames related to spies
The growing trend of portfolio careers and having more than one job
How childhood dreams shape adult employment
Why art portfolios matter in business hiring

evidence.quote: more than one job

private.explanationZh: 节目开头就明确说他们要讨论的是“拥有不止一份工作”的趋势，后文围绕 portfolio career 的定义、吸引力和代价展开，因此主旨十分清楚。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。Bond 姓氏来源只是结尾的小测验答案。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它准确概括了整段节目的中心。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。童年理想只是导入，不是主线。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。这里的 portfolio career 不是艺术作品集。

public.prompt: Which example does Emily Wapnick give of a portfolio career?

options.text: A guard who becomes a spy
A footballer who teaches art full time
A psychotherapist who also makes violins
A corporate manager with one secure office job

evidence.quote: psychotherapist and a luthier

private.explanationZh: Emily 给出的具体例子，是一个人同时做心理治疗师和制琴师。这是节目中最明确的 portfolio career 实例。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。这是把 Bond 小测验的内容混进来了。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有提到这种职业组合。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。它直接对应节目中的原始例子。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。单一稳定岗位恰好不符合 portfolio career 的定义。

public.prompt: What does the phrase "add another string to your bow" mean in this discussion?

options.text: To make one job harder than it needs to be
To avoid changing careers in the future
To focus only on artistic work
To learn additional skills that may be useful later

evidence.quote: add another string to your bow.

private.explanationZh: 前文说工作世界变化很快，因此会做不止一件事更明智；紧接着 Dan 解释这个短语指学习未来可能用得上的新技能。它强调的是提升适应力，而不是把事情复杂化。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目中这个短语是正面含义，不是增加无谓难度。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。恰恰相反，它是在为未来变化做准备。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。它不局限于艺术领域。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。它与节目给出的解释完全一致。

public.prompt: What does the explanation of the surname Bond mainly illustrate?

options.text: Occupational surnames can preserve older meanings that differ from modern associations
Most English surnames came from secret agents
People named Bond usually worked as guards
Modern jobs are easier to trace than older ones

evidence.quote:
Bonds were originally peasant farmers who were bound to
work for a particular lord.

private.explanationZh: 节目先借 James Bond 制造“间谍”联想，最后却说明 Bond 原本指被束缚在领主土地上劳动的农民。这个反差说明，职业类姓氏会保留古老含义，而不一定符合现代直觉。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它概括了这个结尾例子的真正作用。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。节目没有说大多数英文姓氏来自间谍。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。guards 是 Neil 的错误猜测。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目并没有比较古今职业追溯难度。

reasonsZh.bbc-20170413-multiple-careers-q01: 节目一开始就说 specifically looking at the trend for having more than one job，并持续围绕 portfolio careers 展开，所以主旨是这种多重职业趋势。其余选项都只是节目中的边缘内容或错误方向。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170413-multiple-careers-q02: Emily Wapnick 举的具体例子是一个人既是 psychotherapist，又是 luthier，也就是做小提琴的人。这个信息在引述和主持人复述中都很明确。其他选项都不是原文例子。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170413-multiple-careers-q03: Dan 紧接着直接解释这个短语“means to learn new skills you can use in the future”，所以答案就是学习未来可用的新技能。A、B、C都不符合原解释。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170413-multiple-careers-q04: 节目先让人联想到 James Bond 的现代含义，但揭晓时说明 Bond 原本指受束缚为领主工作的农民，这主要说明职业姓氏会保留较早的意义，而这些意义可能与现代联想完全不同。B、C、D都不是这一点。

public.prompt: What is the main focus of the program?

options.text: How to control breathing during public speaking
Why sighing happens and how it helps keep mammals alive
Why people lose mobile phones when stressed
How scientists measure intelligence in mice

evidence.quote: why we sigh, and learn how sighing keeps us alive!

private.explanationZh: 节目开头就点明要讨论“我们为什么叹气”以及“叹气如何让我们活着”，后面的科学解释和生活例子都围绕这一点展开。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目不是关于演讲技巧。
optionRationalesZh.b: 正确。它完整概括了节目的主题。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。手机只是“如释重负”的一个例子。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。老鼠只被用来说明叹气频率。

public.prompt: According to the program, how does sighing help the body survive?

options.text: It speeds up the heart during emergencies
It removes frustration from the brain
It resets the lungs by keeping the alveoli working properly
It stops people from breathing too deeply

evidence.quote:
keeps the tiny sacs of air in our lungs, called
alveoli, working properly.

private.explanationZh: 节目解释说，叹气其实是一种特殊的深呼吸，它能让肺里的微小气囊 alveoli 保持正常运作，从而起到“重置肺部”的生理作用。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。节目没有把叹气的作用说成加快心跳。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。frustration 是情绪语境，不是叹气的生理功能。
optionRationalesZh.c: 正确。这正是节目给出的科学解释。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。叹气本身就是一种很深的呼吸，而不是阻止深呼吸。

public.prompt: Why does Catherine suddenly say "Boo!" to Dan?

options.text: To show that Dan sighs too often
To interrupt the scientific explanation
To make fun of Dan's vocabulary knowledge
To demonstrate what a reflex is

evidence.quote: you jumped! And that was a fabulous reflex, Dan!

private.explanationZh: Catherine 突然吓 Dan 一下，是为了让 Dan 出现不由自主的反应，然后立刻把这个反应解释成 reflex。这个动作把抽象概念变成了可感知的现场示范。

optionRationalesZh.a: 错误。它不是在统计 Dan 叹气次数。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。虽然暂时打断了说明，但目的仍然是服务于说明。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。这里不是在嘲笑词汇知识，而是在做概念演示。
optionRationalesZh.d: 正确。这个插曲的作用就是示范 reflex。

public.prompt: Why do mice sigh more often than humans, according to the program?

options.text: Their faster hearts mean they need to regulate their lungs more often
They become emotionally frustrated more easily
Their lungs are damaged by laboratory air
They are trained to sigh during experiments

evidence.quote: regulate their lungs more than humans.

private.explanationZh: 节目在揭晓答案后补充说，老鼠心跳更快，因此比人类更需要频繁调节肺部。也就是说，叹气频率更高是由生理调节需求带来的。

optionRationalesZh.a: 正确。它准确概括了节目给出的因果解释。
optionRationalesZh.b: 错误。这里的原因是生理，不是情绪。
optionRationalesZh.c: 错误。节目没有提到实验环境损伤肺部。
optionRationalesZh.d: 错误。节目没有说这是训练结果。

reasonsZh.bbc-20170420-sighing-q01: 开头就说要看 why we sigh，并学习 sighing keeps us alive，后文围绕 survival mechanism、lungs、alveoli 和 mammals 展开，因此主旨正是叹气为何发生以及它如何帮助生存。其他选项都不是节目中心。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170420-sighing-q02: Dan 解释 sighing can reset the lungs，随后说明深呼吸能让 alveoli 保持正常工作，否则会塌陷，所以正确答案是通过让肺部小气囊正常运作来重置肺。A、B、D都与原文机制不符。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170420-sighing-q03: Catherine 先喊“Boo!”，Dan 被吓得跳起来，她马上说这就是 reflex，因此这个插曲的作用是现场示范什么叫 reflex。不是为了打断说明或取笑 Dan。
reasonsZh.bbc-20170420-sighing-q04: 节目最后直接解释 mice 的 hearts beat faster，所以它们 need to regulate their lungs more than humans。这正是为什么老鼠叹气更频繁。其他选项都没有文本支持。
